^14^And in the second month, in the twenty-seventh day of the month, the
earth dried up.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap8_14.jpg){height="46" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 8:15*](#Contents.html_rgen8_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And God spoke to Noah, saying,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap8_15.jpg){height="22" width="212"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 8:16*](#Contents.html_rgen8_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^"Go out from the ark, you and your wife and your sons and your sons'
wives with you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap8_16.jpg){height="43" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 8:17*](#Contents.html_rgen8_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^Bring out with you all the living things that are with you, of all
flesh, of the birds and of the domestic animals and of all the creeping
animals that creep on the earth, and they will swarm in the earth and be
fruitful and multiply on the earth."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap8_17.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/8page_41.jpg){height="25" width="79"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 8:18*](#Contents.html_rgen8_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Noah went out, and his sons and his wife and his sons' wives
with him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap8_18.jpg){height="21" width="281"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 8:19*](#Contents.html_rgen8_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^All the living things, all the creeping animals and all the birds,
all that creep on the earth went out from the ark by their families.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap8_19.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 8:20*](#Contents.html_rgen8_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Noah built an altar to YHWH, and he took some of each of the
pure animals and of each of the pure birds, and he offered sacrifices on
the altar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap8_20.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 8:21*](#Contents.html_rgen8_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And YHWH smelled the pleasant smell, and YHWH said to His heart, "I
won't curse the ground on account of humankind again, because the
inclination of the human heart is bad from their youth, and I won't
strike all the living again as I have done.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap8_21.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 8:22*](#Contents.html_rgen8_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^All the rest of the earth's days, seed and harvest, and cold and
heat, and summer and winter, and day and night will not cease."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap8_22.jpg){height="46" width="288"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter09.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:1*](#Contents.html_rgen9_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **9** {.h2a}

^1^And God blessed Noah and his sons and said to them, "Be fruitful and
multiply and fill the earth.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_01.jpg){height="46" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:2*](#Contents.html_rgen9_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And fear of you and dread of you will be on every living thing of the
earth and on every bird of the skies, in every one that will creep on
the earth and in all the fish of the sea. They are given into your hand.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_02.jpg){height="96" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:3*](#Contents.html_rgen9_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^Every creeping every animal that is alive will be yours for food;
I've given every one to you like a plant of vegetation,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_03.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:3. **Every creeping animal**. Elsewhere this term refers only to
certain animals that crawl on the ground, but here it is understood to
refer to all animals: "I've given one to you like a plant"---meaning
that humans are permitted to eat animals in addition to plants. Do
humans eat animals before this? Noah makes burnt offerings
(*![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11"
width="7"}l![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11" width="7"}t*) after
the flood, but that is a type of sacrifice that is not eaten. Abel
brings a different kind of offering
(*min![Image](images/hdot.jpg){.block height="15"
width="7"}![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}h*)
to God, which is a type of sacrifice that is understood later in the
Torah to be eaten by humans. But, prior to Genesis 9, all statements
from the deity to humans concerning eating refer only to plants (1:29;
2:16; 3:18). It is therefore commonly understood that humans are not
permitted to eat animals until after the flood. Some say that God now
permits it as a concession to human appetites. I see no grounds in the
text for this view. A more likely explanation for the change in divine
requirements is that humans have saved all animal species by taking them
in the ark. All animals now owe their lives to humankind, and this may
be why humans are now permitted to take their lives for food.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:4*](#Contents.html_rgen9_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^except you shall not eat flesh in its life, its blood,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_04.jpg){height="46" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:5*](#Contents.html_rgen9_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^and except I shall inquire for your blood, for your lives. I shall
inquire for it from the hand of every animal and from the hand of a
human. I shall inquire for a human's life from the hand of each man for
his brother.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_05.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:5. **I shall inquire for your blood, for your lives**. This expression
means that it is forbidden to take a human life unlawfully. It does not
necessarily mean that one may never take a human life; for example, it
may not forbid taking a life in self-defense. Rather, God will
inquire---that is, require an accounting---and hold the guilty
responsible, whether human or animal.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:6*](#Contents.html_rgen9_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^One who sheds a human's blood: by a human his blood will be shed,
because He made the human in the image of God.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_06.jpg){height="47" width="242"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:7*](#Contents.html_rgen9_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And you, be fruitful and multiply, swarm in the earth and multiply in
it."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_07.jpg){height="19" width="302"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:8*](#Contents.html_rgen9_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And God said to Noah and to his sons with him, saying,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_08.jpg){height="22" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:9*](#Contents.html_rgen9_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^"And I: here, I'm establishing my covenant with you and with your
seed after you

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_09.jpg){height="46" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:9. **covenant**. The flood event concludes with a contract between the
deity and humans: a covenant. The Noahic covenant is the first of four
major covenants that provide the structure in which nearly all of the
Bible is framed. (The exceptions, most notably the book of Job, are
relatively few.) The Noahic covenant promises the security of the
cosmos. The Abrahamic covenant (Genesis 15 and 17) promises the land to
Abraham's descendants and makes YHWH their God. The Israelite (or Sinai,
or Mosaic) covenant (Exodus 20; 34; Deuteronomy 5; 7:12--15) promises
well-being in the promised land. And the Davidic covenant (2 Samuel 7;
Psalms 89; 132) promises kingship over Jerusalem and Judah to David and
his descendants.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:10*](#Contents.html_rgen9_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^and with every living being that is with you, of the birds, of the
domestic animals, and of all the wild animals of the earth with you,
from all those coming out of the ark to every living thing of the earth.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_10.jpg){height="69" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:11*](#Contents.html_rgen9_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And I shall establish my covenant with you, and all flesh will not
be cut off again by the floodwaters, and there will not be a flood again
to destroy the earth."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_11.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:12*](#Contents.html_rgen9_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And God said, "This is the sign of the covenant that I'm giving
between me and you and every living being that is with you for eternal
generations:

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_12.jpg){height="70" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:13*](#Contents.html_rgen9_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^I've put my rainbow in the clouds, and it will become a covenant
sign between me and the earth.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_13.jpg){height="47" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:13. **rainbow**. It is called this (in Hebrew and in English) because
its shape is like a bow that one uses to shoot arrows. The rainbow is
the sign of the covenant that promises that God will not destroy the
earth again. I heard Rabbi Martin Lawson say that the rainbow symbolizes
this because it is "a bow pointed away from the earth."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:14*](#Contents.html_rgen9_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And it will be when I bring a cloud over the earth, and the rainbow
will appear in the cloud,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_14.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:15*](#Contents.html_rgen9_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^and I'll remember my covenant that is between me and you and every
living being of all flesh, and the waters will not become a flood to
destroy all flesh again.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_15.jpg){height="92" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:16*](#Contents.html_rgen9_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And the rainbow will be in the cloud, and I'll see it, to remember
an eternal covenant between God and every living being of all flesh that
is on the earth."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_16.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:17*](#Contents.html_rgen9_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And God said to Noah, "This is the sign of the covenant that I've
established between me and all flesh that is on the earth."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_17.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:18*](#Contents.html_rgen9_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Noah's sons who went out from the ark were Shem and Ham and
Yaphet. And Ham: he was the father of Canaan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_18.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:19*](#Contents.html_rgen9_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^These three were Noah's sons, and all the earth expanded from these.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_19.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:20*](#Contents.html_rgen9_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Noah began to be a man of the ground, and he planted a vineyard.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_20.jpg){height="22" width="255"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:21*](#Contents.html_rgen9_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And he drank from the wine and was drunk. And he was exposed inside
his tent.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_21.jpg){height="22" width="293"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:22*](#Contents.html_rgen9_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw his father's nakedness and told
his two brothers outside.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_22.jpg){height="46" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:23*](#Contents.html_rgen9_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And Shem and Yaphet took a garment and put it on both their
shoulders and went backwards and covered their father's nakedness. And
they faced backwards and did not see their father's nakedness.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_23.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:24*](#Contents.html_rgen9_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And Noah woke up from his wine, and he knew what his youngest son
had done to him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_24.jpg){height="46" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:25*](#Contents.html_rgen9_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And he said, "Canaan is cursed. He'll be a servant of servants to
his brothers."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_25.jpg){height="42" width="260"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:26*](#Contents.html_rgen9_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And he said, "Blessed is YHWH, God of Shem, and may Canaan be a
servant to them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_26.jpg){height="30" width="360"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:27*](#Contents.html_rgen9_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^May God enlarge Yaphet, and may he dwell in the tents of Shem, and
may Canaan be a servant to them."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_27.jpg){height="50" width="261"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:28*](#Contents.html_rgen9_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And Noah lived after the flood three hundred years and fifty years,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_28.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 9:29*](#Contents.html_rgen9_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^and all of Noah's days were nine hundred years and fifty years. And
he died.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap9_29.jpg){height="56" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter10.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:1*](#Contents.html_rgen10_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **10** {.h2a}

^1^And these are the records of Noah's sons, Shem, Ham, and Yaphet: And
children were born to them after the flood.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_01.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:2*](#Contents.html_rgen10_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^Yaphet's children were Gomer and Magog and Madai and Yawan and Tubal
and Meshech and Tiras.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_02.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:2. **Yawan**. Greece (Ionia).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:3*](#Contents.html_rgen10_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Gomer's children were Ashkenaz and Riphath and Togarmah.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_03.jpg){height="22" width="235"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:4*](#Contents.html_rgen10_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Yawan's children were El-isha and Tarshish, Kittim and Dodanim.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_04.jpg){height="23" width="276"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:4. **Tarshish**. Where Jonah tries to sail from the Mediterranean
port of Joppa. Perhaps Gibraltar or Sardinia.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:5*](#Contents.html_rgen10_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^The islands of the nations in their lands were dispersed out from
these, each by its language, by their families within their nations.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_05.jpg){height="66" width="360"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:5. **The islands of the nations**. This has been understood to mean
seacoast peoples.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:6*](#Contents.html_rgen10_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Ham's children were Cush and Egypt and Put and Canaan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_06.jpg){height="23" width="264"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:7*](#Contents.html_rgen10_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Cush's children were Seba and Havilah and Sabtah and Raamah and
Sabteca. And Raamah's children were Sheba and Dedan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_07.jpg){height="56" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:7. **Sheba**. The Sabeans (of southwest Arabia) whose queen would
make a famous journey to meet Israel's King Solomon generations later.
The confusing thing is that Sheba is traced to both Ham (10:7) and Shem
(v. 28). (And this is further confused by a reference to Sheba as
descended from Abraham in Gen 25:3.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:8*](#Contents.html_rgen10_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Cush fathered Nimrod. He began being a man of power in the earth.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_08.jpg){height="46" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:9*](#Contents.html_rgen10_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^He was a powerful hunter before YHWH. On account of this it is said:
"A powerful hunter before YHWH like Nimrod!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_09.jpg){height="58" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:10*](#Contents.html_rgen10_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And the beginning of his kingdom was Babylon and Erech and Akkad and
Calneh in the land of Shinar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_10.jpg){height="75" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:11*](#Contents.html_rgen10_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^Asshur came out of that land and built Nineveh and Rehovoth-Ir and
Calah,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_11.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:12*](#Contents.html_rgen10_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^and Resen between Nineveh and Calah. It is the big city.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_12.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:13*](#Contents.html_rgen10_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Egypt fathered Ludim and Anamim and Lehabim and Naphtuhim

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_13.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:14*](#Contents.html_rgen10_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^and Pathrusim and Casluhim, from which the Philistines came out, and
Caphtorim.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_14.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:14. **Casluhim, from which the Philistines came out, and Caphtorim**.
But cf. Amos 9:7, which says that the Philistines come from Caphtor. In
any case, this first reference to the Philistines in the Torah already
identifies them as---unlike the other residents of Canaan---coming from
across the Mediterranean. Still, it separates them from the Greeks.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:15*](#Contents.html_rgen10_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Canaan fathered Sidon, his firstborn, and Heth

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_15.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:16*](#Contents.html_rgen10_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^and the Jebusite and the Amorite and the Gir-gashite

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_16.jpg){height="26" width="305"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:17*](#Contents.html_rgen10_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^and the Hivite and the Arkite and the Sinite

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_17.jpg){height="19" width="252"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:18*](#Contents.html_rgen10_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^and the Arvadite and the Zemarite and the Hamathite. And later the
families of the Canaanites were dispersed.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_18.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:19*](#Contents.html_rgen10_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And the Canaanite border was from Sidon, as you come to Gerar, up to
Gaza, as you come to Sodom and Gomorrah and Admah and Zeboim, up to
Lasha.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_19.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:20*](#Contents.html_rgen10_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^These are Ham's children, by their families, by their languages, in
their lands, within their nations.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_20.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:21*](#Contents.html_rgen10_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^Children were born to Shem, him as well, father of all of Eber's
children, older brother of Yaphet.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_21.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:22*](#Contents.html_rgen10_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^Shem's children were Elam and Asshur and Arpachshad and Lud and
Aram.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_22.jpg){height="26" width="343"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:23*](#Contents.html_rgen10_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And Aram's children were Uz and Hul and Gether and Mash.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_23.jpg){height="22" width="243"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 10:24*](#Contents.html_rgen10_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And Arpachshad fathered Shelah, and Shelah fathered Eber.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap10_24.jpg){height="22" width="338"}

[]{#Chapter11.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 11:1*](#Contents.html_rgen11_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **11** {.h2a}

^1^And it was: all the earth was one language and the same words.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap11_01.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 11:2*](#Contents.html_rgen11_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And it was when they were traveling from the east: and they found a
valley in the land of Shinar and lived there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap11_02.jpg){height="64" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 11:3*](#Contents.html_rgen11_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And they said to one another, "Come on, let's make bricks and fire
them." And they had brick for stone, and they had bitumen for mortar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap11_03.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 11:4*](#Contents.html_rgen11_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And they said, "Come on, let's build ourselves a city and a tower,
and its top will be in the skies, and we'll make ourselves a name, or
else we'll scatter over the face of all the earth."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap11_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:4. **we'll make ourselves a name.** The story of the tower of Babylon
has its ambiguity at its center. It tells of an attempt by humanity to
build a tower whose top is in the sky, but it never tells what they will
do when they finish the tower, what is meant by "make ourselves a name,"
or why they fear being scattered if they do not have this tower. When
YHWH sees the tower He is described as concerned that "now nothing that
they'll scheme to do will be precluded from them." Their actions appear
to be an act of rebellion of some sort, and in this sense the episode
fits with those of Adam and Eve, Cain, and the flood, i.e., that humans
as a species are continuously in conflict with the initial "good" state
of creation. And so God disperses them and gives them languages that
make them less intelligible to one another and thereby less united. The
story thus prepares the way for a shift in the narrative away from
dealing with the fate of the species and, instead, dealing with
individuals. At the same time, this narrative provides the etiology of
languages and of the dispersion of humans all over the earth. It also
provides a Hebrew etiology for the name Babylon (Hebrew *bbl*) "because
YHWH babbled (Hebrew *Ml*) the language of all the earth" (11:9). This
would have come as a surprise to the people of Babylon, who understood
the name to derive from *bab-ilu*, the "Gate of God."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 11:5*](#Contents.html_rgen11_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And YHWH went down to see the city and the tower that the children of
humankind had built.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap11_05.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 11:6*](#Contents.html_rgen11_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And YHWH said, "Here, they're one people, and they all have one
language, and this is what they've begun to do. And now nothing that
they'll scheme to do will be precluded from them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap11_06.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 11:7*](#Contents.html_rgen11_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^Come on, let's go down and babble their language there so that one
won't understand another's language."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap11_07.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 11:8*](#Contents.html_rgen11_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

and they stopped building the city.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap11_08.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

the language of all the earth there, and YHWH scattered them from there

![Image](images/Gen_Chap11_09.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter12.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:1*](#Contents.html_rgen12_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## G[O]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **12** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH said to Abram, "Go from your land and from your birthplace
and from your father's house to the land that I'll show you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12.jpg){height="35" width="76"}

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_01.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:1. **Go**. Hebrew *lek l![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block
height="12" width="7"}k![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}*. Much has been made of the second word in this phrase,
*l![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}k![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}*,
which means "for you." No translation quite captures the sense of the
Hebrew ("Go you," "Get you," "Go for yourself"). The second element,
*l![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}k![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}*
(or *l![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}kem*),
occasionally follows verbs in the *Tanak*. For example, in Lev 26:1 we
have *l![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11" width="7"}'
ta'![Image](images/a1.jpg){.block height="12"
width="8"}![Image](images/s.jpg){.block height="12"
width="6"}![Image](images/u.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}
l![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}kem* (you
shall not make for yourselves; you shall make you no ...), and in Num
14:25 we have *s![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}'![Image](images/u.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}
l![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}kem* (travel
you; travel for yourselves). I believe it is better to use no English
term than to use any of the possible equivalents, all of which are
clumsy English and do not convey the Hebrew.

12:1. **your land, your birthplace**. But he is in Haran. He has already
left Ur of the Chaldeans, which was his land and birthplace. Rashi tries
to solve the problem by saying that this means "Go even farther from
your land and birthplace." Ibn Ezra tries to solve it by saying that it
means "God *had* said \[i.e., earlier, back in Ur\], 'Go from your land
...'" But these stretch the meaning of the Hebrew beyond what is
possible. And besides, Abraham later sends his servant to get a wife for
Isaac, saying, "Go to my land and my birthplace" (24:4), and the servant
goes to *Haran* ("to Aram Naharaim, to the city of Nahor," 24:10; which
is identified as Haran, 27:43). On these grounds Ramban rejects Rashi's
and Ibn Ezra's solutions. Ramban's own solution, though, falls short as
well, requiring that Abraham's land and birthplace are Haran, not Ur.
This is further complicated by the fact that the Abrahamic covenant
begins with God saying to Abraham, "I brought you out of Ur ..." (15:7).
The truth is that this is a case in which the contradiction is a result
of the fact that the Torah was composed from several sources. In this
case, one of its sources had Abraham coming from Ur, and another had him
coming from Haran. The aim of this commentary is to deal with the Torah
in its final, combined form and not to analyze its sources. (I have done
that elsewhere: in *Who Wrote the Bible?* and *The Hidden Book in the
Bible*.) Usually, the combination of sources has produced a richer, more
complex story in the Torah. But this passage is one of a few instances
in which the combination has produced an irreconcilable contradiction.
In such cases it is better to acknowledge the problem than to stretch to
make forced interpretations.

12:1. **your land, your birthplace, and your father's house**. But if he
has left his land, then *of course* he has left his birthplace and his
father's house. This is geographically backwards. Therefore, the point
of this order is not geographical. It is emotional. The three steps are
arranged in ascending order of difficulty for Abraham. It is hard to
leave one's land, harder if it is where one was born, and harder still
to leave one's family. And where is he to go? To "the land that I'll
show you." That is, he must leave his homeland without knowing for what
he is giving it up. The wording seems designed to make it hard for
Abraham. This pattern of testing will be repeated when he is commanded
to sacrifice Isaac, thus: "Take your son, your only one, whom you love,
Isaac." And where is he to sacrifice him? "On one of the mountains that
I'll say to you." (See the comment on 22:2.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:2*](#Contents.html_rgen12_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And I'll make you into a big nation, and I'll bless you and make your
name great. And *be* a blessing!

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_02.jpg){height="46" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:3*](#Contents.html_rgen12_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And I'll bless those who bless you, and those who affront you I'll
curse. And all the families of the earth will be blessed through you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_03.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:3. **all the families of the earth will be blessed through you**. The
*Tanak* is so focused on the people of Israel that one can underestimate
the overwhelming significance of its opening section. The first eleven
chapters (Parashat Bereshit and Parashat Noah) are about the
relationship between God and the entire human community. That
relationship does not go well, and after ten generations the deity
decides to destroy the mass and start over with a single virtuous man's
family. But it turns out that choosing a virtuous individual does not
guarantee that this individual's descendants will be virtuous as well.
Another ten generations pass, and humans in general are not a
planet-full of Noahs. So once again the focus narrows to a single
virtuous person, Abraham. We must keep in mind what has happened up to
this point when we read this, or else we will lose the significance of
what is happening here. Wiping out everyone but a virtuous person did
not work. So God leaves the species alive but chooses an individual who
will produce a family that will ultimately bring blessing to *all the
families of the earth*.

To make sure we get it, it is the final point of God's first revelation
to Abraham: Go to the land I'll show you. I'll make you a big nation.
I'll bless you. "And all the families of the earth will be blessed
through you" (Gen 12:3).

To make sure we do not forget it, it is repeated four times---and always
in crucial moments of revelation: during the appearance of the three
visitors to Abraham (Gen 18:17--18); in the blessing following the
near-sacrifice of Isaac (22:16--18); in God's first appearance to Isaac
(26:2--4); and in Jacob's first encounter with God in the dream of the
ladder at Beth-El (28:10--14).

It is *important*. In some way, at some time, the result of the divine
choice of Abraham is supposed to be some good for all humankind. We are
never told what this good is supposed to be. Is it that Abraham's
descendants are supposed to bring blessing by being "a light to the
nations"---setting an example, showing how a community can live: caring
for one another, not cheating one another, not enslaving one another,
not lending to each other for profit, and so on and on? Or is it that
they are to bring blessing by doing things that benefit the species:
inventions, cures, literature, music, learning? It does not say. But at
minimum it must mean that the people of Israel do not live alone or
apart. Their destiny---our destiny---whatever it is, must be bound up in
the destiny of all humankind.

This adds a dimension, an additional layer of significance, to every
story that will follow in the Torah. When Abraham travels to Canaan, we
might imagine it from a God's-eye view above the earth: the tiny
movement of a man and his family along the globe is a first step in a
process that is to bring benefit for all the earth. When Abraham travels
to Egypt, this is the first in a series of encounters that he and his
descendants will have with the peoples of the world. When he and Lot are
forced to part, this is a step in distinguishing his destiny from that
of the families of his brothers Haran and Nahor, though he and his
descendants will continue to interact with them. When he joins in a
battle among kings in order to rescue Lot (Genesis 14), he is being
drawn into world events, and it is another first step, an anticipation
of all the times that his descendants, the people of Israel, will be
drawn into contact and interaction with nations. When Abraham covenants
with God (Genesis 15 and 17) the ceremony includes specific references
to Ur of the Chaldees, the land from which Abraham has come; to Egypt,
the land where his descendants will be enslaved; and to ten peoples of
the land that Abraham is promised. It also includes the announcement of
his coming son Isaac, the key to the fulfillment of the destiny to be a
blessing to all the families of the earth---as will be confirmed
explicitly following the Aqedah. And in between the two chapters
concerning the Abrahamic covenant comes the story of the birth of
Abraham's son Ishmael, whose descendants, the Ishmaelites, will be among
Israel's related neighbors.

All of these episodes are conveying the formative stages of a
development that begins with that narrowing of attention to Abraham. The
interpretive point is that we must understand every section of the Torah
with awareness of what has preceded it and what will come after it. The
social and moral point is that Abraham's descendants are not to live by
themselves or only for themselves. Whether dealing with non-Jews who
live in Israel or dealing with non-Jews who are their neighbors in the
countries in which they reside, the Jews are a community that connects
its birth with a prediction (?), a promise (?), an obligation (?), a
destiny (?) to be a blessing to them all.

12:3. **will be blessed through you**. Rashi takes the plain meaning of
this phrase to mean that non-Israelites will bless their children with
words such as "May you be like Abraham." His proof for this reading is
the wording of Jacob's blessing of his grandsons Ephraim and Manasseh:
"Israel will bless with you, saying: 'May God make you like Ephraim and
Manasseh'" (Gen 48:20). But that passage uses an active form of the verb
(the *Piel*), whereas all the occurrences of this phrase in terms of the
nations being blessed use forms that are passive or reflexive (*Niphal*
and *Hitpael*), which is a different matter. If we take the meaning as
passive, then it is as I have translated it here: "the earth's families
*will be blessed* through you." If we take it as reflexive, then it can
mean "they *will bless themselves* through you," which is Rashi's
understanding; but a reflexive can also mean "they will get blessing
through you." Interpreters are split on this. I believe that *context*
settles the question. The issue in the story until now has been the
course of relations between God and all the families of the earth. Now
God makes a special bond with Abraham's family and lets him know that
this is for the eventual benefit of all families. (See the preceding
comment.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:4*](#Contents.html_rgen12_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Abram went as YHWH had spoken to him, and Lot went with him. And
Abram was seventy-five years old when he went out from Haran.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_04.jpg){height="66" width="343"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:4. **Abram went as YHWH had spoken to him**. Traditionally the
emphasis has been on faith as the mark of Abraham's character. But the
narrative conveys far more that the mark of Abraham is obedience. Leave
your land: "And Abram went." Sacrifice your son: "And Abram got up
early." The tests that Abraham undergoes are not necessarily tests of
faith but are certainly tests of obedience. At this stage in relations
between God and humans, God appears to single out a human who will do
what he is told. This will change gradually in the *Tanak*, as humans
grow and mature and God cedes more responsibility for the world to them.
But for now, obedience is sought.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:5*](#Contents.html_rgen12_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Abram took Sarai, his wife, and Lot, his brother's son, and all
their property that they had accumulated and the persons whom they had
gotten in Haran; and they went out to go to the land of Canaan.

And they came to the land of Canaan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_05.jpg){height="96" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:6*](#Contents.html_rgen12_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Abram passed through the land as far as the place of Shechem, as
far as the oak of Moreh. And the Canaanite was in the land then.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_06.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:7*](#Contents.html_rgen12_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And YHWH appeared to Abram and said, "I'll give this land to your
seed." And he built an altar there to YHWH who had appeared to him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:8*](#Contents.html_rgen12_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And he moved on from there to the hill country east of Beth-El and
pitched his tent---Beth-El to the west and Ai to the east---and he built
an altar there to YHWH and invoked the name YHWH.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_08.jpg){height="70" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:8. **Beth-El to the west and Ai to the east**. At this site, Abram
invokes the name YHWH for the first time, and he builds an altar there.
Later Abraham returns to this site and invokes the divine name again
(13:3). No explanation of the significance of this place between the two
cities---Beth-El and Ai---is given in the Torah. But we learn in the
book of Joshua that, many generations later, God delivers Ai into the
Israelites' hands by means of an ambush, and it takes place "between
Beth-El and Ai, to the west of Ai" (Josh 8:9,12; cf. 12:9). This appears
to be a case of "the merit of the fathers." That is, the pious acts of
Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob have consequences for their descendants. In
this case, the Israelites are successful in the age of settlement of the
land thanks to what Abraham had done on the very spot centuries earlier.
There will be enough cases of this phenomenon to establish it as a major
theme and message of the Torah: that the good acts that we do have
implications for the lives of our children and many generations
thereafter: as an example, as a source of strength, and in subtle ways
that are impossible to calculate or foresee. This phenomenon of the
merit of the parents also acts as a preparation for and a balance to the
opposite phenomenon that will be developed in the Torah later: that our
sins are visited on the lives of our children and descendants as well.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:9*](#Contents.html_rgen12_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Abram traveled on, going to the Negeb.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_09.jpg){height="22" width="254"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:10*](#Contents.html_rgen12_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And there was a famine in the land, and Abram went down to Egypt to
reside there because the famine was heavy in the land.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_10.jpg){height="47" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:11*](#Contents.html_rgen12_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And it was when he was close to coming to Egypt: and he said to
Sarai, his wife, "Here, I know that you're a beautiful woman.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_11.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:11--13. **Here, I know**. Abraham's words to Sarah contain the first
two occurrences of the Hebrew word *n****a'***. It is sometimes rendered
"now" in English and sometimes rendered "please." But neither is quite
right. It is an untranslatable particle that is a sign of polite speech.
Its most striking occurrence is in God's words to Abraham in the story
of the binding of Isaac: "Take *n****a'*** your son ... and offer him"
(Gen 22:2). I do not translate it with any English word, because it is
closer to the Hebrew to use *no* word than to use such words as "please"
or "now."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:12*](#Contents.html_rgen12_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And it will be when the Egyptians see you that they'll say, 'This is
his wife,' and they'll kill me and keep you alive.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_12.jpg){height="92" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:13*](#Contents.html_rgen12_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^Say you're my sister so it will be good for me on your account and
I'll stay alive because of you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_13.jpg){height="47" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:13. **Say you're my sister ... and I'll stay alive**. All
commentators have agonized over this. Abraham constructs a lie and puts
Sarah in a compromising position. This may mean that he thinks that he
simply has no real choice: it is either lie or die. Or it may convey
that Abraham---like other biblical heroes---is not perfect. We cannot
know. In either of these cases, he cannot be faulted for choosing to put
Sarah in a compromising position, because, in his understanding, Sarah
would be taken either way. The concern is that, without the lie, they
would also kill him. (In fact he turns out to be wrong. There is no
evidence in the Bible that the Egyptians or anyone else ever did what
Abraham fears they would do. Here and in two other cases in which
Abraham and his son Isaac claim that their wives are their sisters, the
Egyptian and Philistine kings send the couple away when they find out
that she is his wife.)

12:13. **my sister**. For a while it was common to cite tablets from
Nuzi as evidence that there was a Near Eastern practice of adopting
one's wife as a sister. But the Nuzi case involved a single family, and
scholars recently have questioned whether it is justified to make so
much of it. Abraham's actions here (and in Genesis 20; and Isaac's
actions in Genesis 26) are treated as an unusual case. They require no
Near Eastern legal precedent.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:14*](#Contents.html_rgen12_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And it was as Abram came to Egypt, and the Egyptians saw the woman,
that she was very beautiful.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_14.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:15*](#Contents.html_rgen12_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Pharaoh's officers saw her and praised her to Pharaoh, and the
woman was taken to Pharaoh's house.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_15.jpg){height="50" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:16*](#Contents.html_rgen12_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And he was good to Abram on her account, and he had a flock and oxen
and he-asses and servants and maids and she-asses and camels.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_16.jpg){height="47" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:17*](#Contents.html_rgen12_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And YHWH plagued Pharaoh and his house, big plagues, over the matter
of Sarai, Abram's wife.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_17.jpg){height="91" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:18*](#Contents.html_rgen12_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Pharaoh called Abram and said, "What is this you've done to me?
Why didn't you tell me that she was your wife?

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_18.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:19*](#Contents.html_rgen12_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^Why did you say, 'She's my sister,' so I took her for a wife for
myself! And now, here's your wife. Take her and go."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_19.jpg){height="50" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 12:20*](#Contents.html_rgen12_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Pharaoh commanded people over him, and they let him and his wife
and all he had go.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap12_20.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter13.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:1*](#Contents.html_rgen13_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **13** {.h2a}

^1^And Abram went up from Egypt, he and his wife and all he had and Lot
with him, to the Negeb.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_01.jpg){height="53" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:1. **went up from Egypt ... to the Negeb**. Abraham's movements
prefigure the future fate of the Israelites. As he returns from Egypt,
going up into the Negeb, so the Israelites will return from Egypt, and
Moses will send the first Israelites (the spies) to reenter the land
with the words "Go up there in the Negeb" (Num 13:17). (See the next
comment.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:2*](#Contents.html_rgen13_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Abram was very heavy with livestock and silver and gold.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_02.jpg){height="50" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:3*](#Contents.html_rgen13_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And he went on his travels from the Negeb as far as Beth-El, as far
as the place where his tent was at the start, between Beth-El and Ai,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_03.jpg){height="73" width="345"}

![Image](images/13page_53.jpg){height="21" width="79"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:3. **he went on his travels**. This expression continues the
prefiguring of the fate of Abraham's descendants, who went "on their
travels" when they left Egypt (Exod 17:1; Num 10:12).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:4*](#Contents.html_rgen13_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^to the place of the altar that he had made there at the beginning.
And Abram invoked the name YHWH there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_04.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:5*](#Contents.html_rgen13_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Lot, who was going with Abram, also had a flock and oxen and
tents.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_05.jpg){height="47" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:6*](#Contents.html_rgen13_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And the land did not suffice them to live together, because their
property was great, and they were not able to live together.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_06.jpg){height="73" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:7*](#Contents.html_rgen13_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And there was a quarrel between those who herded Abram's livestock
and those who herded Lot's livestock. And the Canaanite and the
Perizzite lived in the land then.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_07.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:8*](#Contents.html_rgen13_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Abram said to Lot, "Let there be no quarreling between me and you
and between my herders and your herders, because we're brothers.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_08.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:9*](#Contents.html_rgen13_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^Isn't the whole land before you? Separate from me: if left then I'll
go right, and if right then I'll go left."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_09.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:10*](#Contents.html_rgen13_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Lot raised his eyes and saw all the plain of the Jordan, that
all of it was well watered (before YHWH's destroying Sodom and Gomorrah)
like YHWH's garden, like the land of Egypt, as you come to Zoar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_10.jpg){height="97" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:11*](#Contents.html_rgen13_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Lot chose all the plain of the Jordan for himself, and Lot
traveled east. And they separated, each from his brother.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_11.jpg){height="79" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:12*](#Contents.html_rgen13_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^Abram lived in the land of Canaan, and Lot lived in the cities of
the plain and tented as far as Sodom.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_12.jpg){height="48" width="355"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:13*](#Contents.html_rgen13_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And the people of Sodom were very bad and sinful to YHWH.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_13.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:14*](#Contents.html_rgen13_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And YHWH said to Abram after Lot's separation from him, "Lift your
eyes and see from the place where you are to the north and south and
east and west.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_14.jpg){height="96" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:15*](#Contents.html_rgen13_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^For all the land that you see, I'll give it to you and to your seed
forever.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_15.jpg){height="49" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:16*](#Contents.html_rgen13_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And I'll make your seed like the dust of the earth, so that if a man
could count the dust of the earth then your seed also could be counted.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_16.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:17*](#Contents.html_rgen13_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^Get up, go around in the land to its length and its width, because
I'm giving it to you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_17.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 13:18*](#Contents.html_rgen13_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Abram took up his tent and came and lived among the oaks of
Mamre which are in Hebron, and he built an altar to YHWH there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap13_18.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter14.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:1*](#Contents.html_rgen14_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **14** {.h2a}

^1^ And it was in the days of Amraphel, king of Shinar, Arioch, king of
Ellasar, Chedor-laomer, king of Elam, and Tidal, king of Goiim:4

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_01.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:2*](#Contents.html_rgen14_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^They made war with Bera, king of Sodom, and Birsha, king of Gomorrah,
Shinab, king of Admah, and Shemeber, king of Zeboiim, and the king of
Bela. (That is Zoar.)

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_02.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/14page_54.jpg){height="25" width="92"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:3*](#Contents.html_rgen14_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^All these were allied at the Siddim Valley. (That is the Dead Sea.)

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_03.jpg){height="50" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:4*](#Contents.html_rgen14_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^Twelve years they served Chedorlaomer, and the thirteenth year they
revolted,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:5*](#Contents.html_rgen14_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^and in the fourteenth year Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with
him came, and they struck the Rephaim in Ashteroth Karnaim and the Zuzum
in Ham and the Emim in Shaveh Kiriataim

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_05.jpg){height="97" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:6*](#Contents.html_rgen14_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^and the Horites in their mountain Seir to El Paran, which is by the
wilderness.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_06.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:7*](#Contents.html_rgen14_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And they came back and came to En Mishpat (that is Kadesh) and struck
the area of the Amalekites and also the Amorites who live in Hazazon
Tamar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_07.jpg){height="72" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:8*](#Contents.html_rgen14_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And the king of Sodom and the king of Gomorrah and the king of Admah
and the king of Zeboiim and the king of Bela (that is Zoar) went out and
aligned with them for war in the Siddim Valley

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_08.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/14page_55.jpg){height="26" width="84"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:9*](#Contents.html_rgen14_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^with Chedorlaomer, king of Elam, and Tidal, king of Goiim, and
Amraphel, king of Shinar, and Arioch, king of Ellasar---four kings with
five.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_09.jpg){height="76" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:10*](#Contents.html_rgen14_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Siddim Valley was pits, pits of bitumen, and the kings of Sodom
and Gomorrah fled, and they fell there; and those who were left fled to
the mountain.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_10.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:11*](#Contents.html_rgen14_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And they took all the property of Sodom and Gomorrah and all their
food, and they went.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_11.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:12*](#Contents.html_rgen14_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And they took Lot, Abram's brother's son, and his property when they
went. And he had been living in Sodom.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_12.jpg){height="48" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:13*](#Contents.html_rgen14_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And an escapee came and told Abram the Hebrew, and he was tenting
among the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and brother of
Aner; and they were covenant partners of Abram.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_13.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:13. **the Hebrew**. This is an unusual use of the word "Hebrew."
Elsewhere in biblical stories it is used to identify Israelites only
when one is speaking among foreigners. It is not the standard term for
the people, which is rather "Israelite" at first and "Jew" later.
Perhaps it is used here because there are not yet any other Israelites
around, and Abraham himself is the foreigner. (Regarding the term
"Hebrew slave," see the comment on Exod 21:2.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:14*](#Contents.html_rgen14_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Abram heard that his brother had been taken prisoner, and he had
his trained men, born in his house, unsheathe: three hundred eighteen.
And he pursued as far as Dan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_14.jpg){height="74" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:15*](#Contents.html_rgen14_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And he divided against them by night, he and his servants, and he
struck them and pursued them as far as Hobah, which is at the left of
Damascus.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_15.jpg){height="52" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:16*](#Contents.html_rgen14_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And he brought back all the property, and he also brought back Lot,
his brother, and his property, and also the women and the people.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_16.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:17*](#Contents.html_rgen14_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And the king of Sodom came out to him after he came back from
striking Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him at the Shaveh
Valley. (That is the valley of the king.)

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_17.jpg){height="100" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:18*](#Contents.html_rgen14_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Melchizedek, king of Salem, had brought out bread and wine. And
he was a priest of El the Highest.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_18.jpg){height="52" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:18. **the Highest**. Hebrew *'ely![Image](images/o.jpg){.block
height="11" width="7"}n*. This is how God is known at the end of the
Torah also (Deut 32:8). There (and in Ps 82:6) this is the epithet that
is used for God in connection with the formation of the nations of the
earth. That makes it notable that here the priest who is associated with
El the Highest is not from Abraham's family but from another group.
Abraham is not pictured as the only worshiper of this God on earth.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:19*](#Contents.html_rgen14_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And he blessed him and said, "Blessed is Abram to El the Highest,
creator of skies and earth.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_19.jpg){height="49" width="294"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:20*](#Contents.html_rgen14_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And blessed is El the Highest, who delivered your foes into your
hand." And he gave him a tithe from everything.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_20.jpg){height="50" width="308"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:21*](#Contents.html_rgen14_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And the king of Sodom said to Abram, "Give the persons to me, and
take the property for you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_21.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:22*](#Contents.html_rgen14_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Abram said to the king of Sodom, "I've lifted my hand to YHWH,
El the Highest, creator of skies and earth,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_22.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:23*](#Contents.html_rgen14_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^that, from a thread to a shoelace, I won't take anything that is
yours, so you won't say, *'I* made Abram rich.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_23.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 14:24*](#Contents.html_rgen14_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^Except only what the boys have eaten and the share of the people who
went with me: Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre. *They* shall take their share."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap14_24.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter15.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:1*](#Contents.html_rgen15_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **15** {.h2a}

^1^After these things YHWH's word came to Abram in a vision, saying,
"Don't fear, Abram. I'm a shield for you. Your reward is very much."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_01.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:2*](#Contents.html_rgen15_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Abram said, "My Lord, YHWH, what would you give me when I go
childless and my household is an acquired person!" (That is Damascus
Eliezer.)

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_02.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:3*](#Contents.html_rgen15_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Abram said, "Here, you haven't given me seed. And, here, a member
of my household is taking possession of what is mine."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_03.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:3. **a member of my household is taking possession**. The word for
"taking possession" means to inherit or acquire. Why would Nahor or Lot
not inherit what is Abraham's? Because they are not there. Abraham's
brother Nahor did not make the trip to Canaan with Abraham, and Lot
moved away to the east. So, if they were to come to claim Abraham's
possessions, who would enforce their claim? There is no central
government of Canaan. We are told over and over that it is a land of
Canaanites, Jebusites, Perizzites, and so on---a variety of communities
in separate cities.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:4*](#Contents.html_rgen15_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And, here, YHWH's word came to him, saying, "This one won't take
possession from you, but rather one who will come out of your in-sides:
*he* will take possession from you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_04.jpg){height="72" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:3--4. **take possession**. This term occurs three times here,
emphasizing that Abraham's own offspring will someday take possession of
everything that is promised to him. It then occurs over sixty times in
Deuteronomy as the Israelites are about to enter the land, leaving no
doubt of the point that this promise is to be fulfilled.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:5*](#Contents.html_rgen15_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
