^5^And He brought him outside and said, "Look at the skies and count the
stars---if you'll be able to count them." And He said to him, "That is
how your seed will be."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_05.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:6*](#Contents.html_rgen15_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And he trusted in YHWH, and He considered it for him as virtue.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_06.jpg){height="22" width="240"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:6. **virtue**. We were not told the reason for God's choosing
Abraham. We must derive it from Abraham's later behavior: his virtue,
his combination of obedience and willingness to question, humility as
well as boldness, trust in his God as well as caring about his fellow
human beings.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:7*](#Contents.html_rgen15_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And He said to him, "I am YHWH, who brought you out of Ur of the
Chaldees to give you this land, to possess it."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_07.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:8*](#Contents.html_rgen15_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And he said, "My Lord YHWH, how will I know that I'll possess it?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_08.jpg){height="43" width="330"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:9*](#Contents.html_rgen15_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And He said to him, "Take a three-year-old heifer and a
three-year-old she-goat and a three-year-old ram and a dove and a pigeon
for me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_09.jpg){height="70" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:10*](#Contents.html_rgen15_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And he took all of these for Him and split them up the middle and
set each half opposite its other half, but he did not split the birds.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_10.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:10. **he took all of these for Him and split them**. This is the
beginning of a covenant ceremony. Such use of animals is well known in
ancient Near Eastern covenant ceremonies. The Abrahamic covenant takes
up the rest of this chapter and then is added to in Genesis 17.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:11*](#Contents.html_rgen15_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And birds of prey came down on the carcasses, and Abram retrieved
them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_11.jpg){height="22" width="336"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:11. **Abram retrieved them**. This is usually taken to mean that
Abram drove the birds of prey away, but the Hebrew plural must refer to
the split carcasses, not the birds (which are identified by a singular
noun, meaning either a single bird or a group of birds for which a
collective noun is used).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:12*](#Contents.html_rgen15_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And the sun was about to set, and a slumber had come over Abram;
and, here, a big, dark terror was coming over him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_12.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:13*](#Contents.html_rgen15_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And He said to Abram, "You shall *know* that your seed will be an
alien in a land that is not theirs, and they will serve them, and they
will degrade them four hundred years.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_13.jpg){height="94" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:13. **alien**. No English term is quite equivalent to the Hebrew
*g![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14" width="6"}r*. The English
term "alien" has a variety of connotations, but most commonly it means a
person residing in a place where he is not a regular citizen, which is
what a *g![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14" width="6"}r* is. In
rabbinic Hebrew, *g![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14"
width="6"}r* is the word for a convert to Judaism, but that is not its
meaning in the Torah.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:14*](#Contents.html_rgen15_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^But I'll judge the nation whom they'll serve as well, and after that
they'll go out with much property.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_14.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:15*](#Contents.html_rgen15_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And you: you'll come to your fathers in peace. You'll be buried at a
good old age.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_15.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:16*](#Contents.html_rgen15_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And a fourth generation will come back here, because the Amor-ite's
crime is not yet complete."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_16.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:17*](#Contents.html_rgen15_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And the sun was setting, and there was darkness, and here was an
oven of smoke and a flame of fire that went between these pieces.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_17.jpg){height="46" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:17. **a flame of fire**. The presence of God is expressed through
fire here in the covenant with Abraham. Fire will be the expression of
God's presence many more times, including most notably the miraculous
burning bush when Moses first meets God, and the fire on Sinai in the
covenant with Israel.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:18*](#Contents.html_rgen15_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^In that day YHWH made a covenant with Abram, saying, "I've given
this land to your seed, from the river of Egypt to the big river, the
river Euphrates:

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_18.jpg){height="96" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:18. **river of Egypt**. This is generally understood to refer to the
Wadi el-'Arîsh.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:19*](#Contents.html_rgen15_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^the Cainites and the Kenizzites and the Kad-monites

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_19.jpg){height="22" width="269"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:20*](#Contents.html_rgen15_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^and the Hittites and the Perizzites and the Rephaim

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_20.jpg){height="19" width="269"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 15:21*](#Contents.html_rgen15_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^and the Amorites and the Canaanites and the Girgashites and the
Jebu-sites."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap15_21.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter16.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:1*](#Contents.html_rgen16_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **16** {.h2a}

^1^And Abram's wife Sarai had not given birth by him. And she had an
Egyptian maid, and her name was Hagar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_01.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:2*](#Contents.html_rgen16_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Sarai said to Abram, "Here, YHWH has held me back from giving
birth. Come to my maid. Maybe I'll get 'childed' through her." And Abram
listened to Sarai's voice.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_02.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:3*](#Contents.html_rgen16_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Sarai, Abram's wife, took Hagar, the Egyptian, her maid, at the
end of ten years of Abram's living in the land of Canaan, and gave her
to Abram, her husband, as a wife to him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_03.jpg){height="74" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:4*](#Contents.html_rgen16_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And he came to Hagar, and she became pregnant. And she saw that she
had become pregnant, and her mistress was lowered in her eyes.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:5*](#Contents.html_rgen16_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Sarai said to Abram, "My injury is on you. I, *I*, placed my maid
in your bosom, and she saw that she had become pregnant, and I was
lowered in her eyes. Let YHWH judge between me and you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_05.jpg){height="73" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:6*](#Contents.html_rgen16_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Abram said to Sarai, "Here, your maid is in your hand. Do to her
whatever is good in your eyes." And Sarai degraded her, and she fled
from her.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_06.jpg){height="24" width="241"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:6. **And Sarai degraded her, and she fled**. Sarai's treatment of the
Egyptian Hagar foreshadows (or is reversed in, or governs) Israel's
experience in Egypt. These exact words recur there: Egypt degraded them
(Exod 1:12), and they fled (14:5). And note that, like Israel, Hagar
fled to the *wilderness* (v. 7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:7*](#Contents.html_rgen16_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And an angel of YHWH found her at a spring of water in the
wilderness, by the spring on the way to Shur,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_07.jpg){height="73" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:8*](#Contents.html_rgen16_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^and said, "Hagar, Sarai's maid, from where have you come, and where
will you go?"

And she said, "I'm fleeing from Sarai, my mistress."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_08.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:9*](#Contents.html_rgen16_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And the angel of YHWH said to her, "Go back to your mistress, and
suffer the degradation under her hands."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_09.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:10*](#Contents.html_rgen16_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And the angel of YHWH said, "I'll *multiply* your seed, and it won't
be countable because of its great number."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_10.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:11*](#Contents.html_rgen16_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And the angel of YHWH said to her, "Here, you're pregnant and will
give birth to a son, and you shall call his name Ishmael, for YHWH has
listened to your suffering.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_11.jpg){height="73" width="317"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:12*](#Contents.html_rgen16_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And he'll be a wild ass of a man, his hand against everyone, and
everyone's hand against him, and he'll tent among all his brothers."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_12.jpg){height="72" width="326"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:12. **he'll tent**. The verb is denominative; that is, the verb "to
tent" (Hebrew root *škn*) derives from the word
*mišk![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}n*, a
tent or tabernacle. In the blessing of Noah's son Yaphet, it refers
explicitly to dwelling in tents (Gen 9:27). In Moses' blessing of
Benjamin (Deut 33:12) it is connected to God's "sheltering" (Hebrew
*![Image](images/hdot.jpg){.block height="15"
width="7"}![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11"
width="7"}p![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14" width="6"}p*,
related to *![Image](images/hdot.jpg){.block height="15"
width="7"}upp![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}h*, a canopy). Most important, it will be used (in its *Piel*
form) for the way in which God tents the Ark of the Testimony inside the
Tabernacle. Occasionally it can refer to "dwelling" in general, not
necessarily in tents (e.g., Gen 49:13).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:13*](#Contents.html_rgen16_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And she called the name of YHWH who spoke to her "You are El-roi,"
for she said, "Have I also seen after the one who sees me here?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_13.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:14*](#Contents.html_rgen16_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^On account of this the well was called "the well Lahai-roi." Here it
is between Kadesh and Bered.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_14.jpg){height="74" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:15*](#Contents.html_rgen16_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Hagar gave birth to a son for Abram, and Abram called the name
of his son whom Hagar had borne Ishmael.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_15.jpg){height="52" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 16:16*](#Contents.html_rgen16_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And Abram was eighty years and six years old when Hagar gave birth
to Ishmael for Abram.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap16_16.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter17.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:1*](#Contents.html_rgen17_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **17** {.h2a}

^1^And Abram was ninety years and nine years old, and YHWH appeared to
Abram and said to him, "I am El Shadday. Walk before me and be
unblemished,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_01.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:1. **El Shadday**. Meaning: El, the One of the Mountain. (The old
translation "God Almighty" cannot be justified.) The association of gods
in general, and YHWH in particular, with mountains is well known. And
there are gods called *šadu rabu* ("big mountains") in Akkadian, and
gods called *šdyn* in the Deir 'Alla text (an ancient plaster text found
in Jordan in 1967).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:2*](#Contents.html_rgen17_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^and let me place my covenant between me and you, and I'll make you
very, very numerous."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_02.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:3*](#Contents.html_rgen17_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Abram fell on his face, and God spoke with him, saying,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_03.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:4*](#Contents.html_rgen17_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^"*I:* here, my covenant is with you, and you'll become a father of a
mass of nations.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_04.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:5*](#Contents.html_rgen17_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And your name will not be called Abram anymore, but your name will be
Abraham, because I've set you to be a father of a mass of nations.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_05.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:6*](#Contents.html_rgen17_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And I'll make you very, very fruitful and make you into nations. And
kings will come out of you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_06.jpg){height="51" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:7*](#Contents.html_rgen17_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And I'll establish my covenant between me and you and your seed after
you for their generations as an eternal covenant, to become God to you
and to your seed after you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_07.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:8*](#Contents.html_rgen17_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And I'll give you and your seed after you the land where you're
residing, all the land of Canaan, as an eternal possession, and I'll
become a God to them."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_08.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:9*](#Contents.html_rgen17_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And God said to Abraham, "And you: you shall observe my covenant, you
and your seed after you through their generations.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_09.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:10*](#Contents.html_rgen17_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^This is my covenant that you shall observe between me and you and
your seed after you: every male is to be circumcised among you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_10.jpg){height="48" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:11*](#Contents.html_rgen17_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And you shall be circumcised at the flesh of your foreskin, and it
will become a sign of a covenant between me and you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_11.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:12*](#Contents.html_rgen17_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And at eight days old every male shall be circumcised among you
through your generations: homeborn or purchased with money from any
foreigner who is not from your seed.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_12.jpg){height="72" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:12. **purchased with money**. A slave.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:13*](#Contents.html_rgen17_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^Your homeborn and the one purchased with your money *will be
circumcised*. And my covenant will become an eternal covenant in your
flesh.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_13.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:14*](#Contents.html_rgen17_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And an uncircumcised---a male the flesh of whose foreskin will not
be circumcised---that person will be cut off from his people. He has
broken my covenant."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_14.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:14. **cut off from his people**. No one is certain what this meant.
It refers to some sort of punishment produced by God, not humans. It may
mean to die without heirs or perhaps not to be buried in the ancestral
tomb. (In Gen 49:29 Jacob on his deathbed says, "I'm being gathered to
my people." The next thing is that he asks to be buried in the ancestral
tomb!) Or it may mean not to join one's ancestors in an afterlife. Its
meaning was already unknown for certain by early rabbinic times.

The word has a double meaning here in the context of circumcision: *krt*
is the term for Zipporah's circumcision of Gershom in Exod 4:25.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:15*](#Contents.html_rgen17_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And God said to Abraham, "Sarai, your wife: you shall not call her
name Sarai, because her name is Sarah.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_15.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:16*](#Contents.html_rgen17_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And I'll bless her, and I'll also give you a son from her. And I'll
bless her, and she'll become nations. Kings of peoples will be from
her."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_16.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:17*](#Contents.html_rgen17_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And Abraham fell on his face and laughed and said in his heart,
"Will he be born to someone who's a hundred years old?! And will Sarah,
who's ninety years old, give birth?!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_17.jpg){height="72" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:18*](#Contents.html_rgen17_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Abraham said to God, "If only Ishmael will live before you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_18.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:18. **If only Ishmael will live before you**. Abraham is content with
Ishmael as his successor, but it is God who decides the succession, and
God declares that it will be Isaac. Compare this to the development one
generation later, in which it is not God but humans who determine the
succession: Rebekah and Jacob manipulate the birthright and blessing so
that Jacob and not Esau will be Isaac's successor. That is one of many
cases in which humans' control of their own destiny increases through
the Torah and through the entire *Tanak*.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:19*](#Contents.html_rgen17_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And God said, "But Sarah, your wife, is giving birth to a son for
you. And you shall call his name Isaac. And I'll establish my covenant
with him as an eternal covenant for his seed after him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_19.jpg){height="76" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:19. **you shall call his name Isaac**. Isaac's father Abram/Abraham
and his son Jacob/Israel have their names changed, but Isaac does not.
The reason appears to be that Abraham's and Jacob's names are changed by
*God*, but Isaac's name is given here by God in the first place.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:20*](#Contents.html_rgen17_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And I've listened to you about Ishmael. Here, I've blessed him, and
I'll make him fruitful and make him very, very numerous. He'll father
twelve chieftains, and I'll make him into a big nation.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_20.jpg){height="97" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:20. **I'll make him into a big nation**. People sometimes speak of
Ishmael as if the Bible were picturing him as the ancestor of the Arabs,
but the Bible pictures Ishmael only as the ancestor of the Ishmaelites.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:21*](#Contents.html_rgen17_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^But I'll establish my covenant with Isaac, whom Sarah will bear for
you at this appointed time in the next year."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_21.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:22*](#Contents.html_rgen17_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And He finished speaking with him, and God went up from Abraham.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_22.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:23*](#Contents.html_rgen17_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And Abraham took Ishmael, his son, and all his homeborn and everyone
purchased with his money, every male among the men of Abraham's house,
and he circumcised the flesh of their foreskin in that very day, as God
had spoken with him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_23.jpg){height="97" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:23. **Ishmael, his son**. The triple repetition of "his son" conveys
concern for Ishmael and feeling for him as Abraham's son. Thus it
teaches us about compassion and acceptance. There is no attempt here to
minimize Ishmael or his descendants, the Ishmaelites, or to cast
aspersions on them.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:24*](#Contents.html_rgen17_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And Abraham was ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised, the
flesh of his foreskin,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_24.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:24. **ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised**. But God has
known Abraham for years, so why does God not command this when Abraham
is younger? Why not command it at the beginning of their relationship?
Because the issue is not the circumcision. It is the *covenant*, of
which circumcision is just the sign. So why not make the covenant right
at the beginning? Because Abraham has to go through certain things and
*merit* a covenant.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:25*](#Contents.html_rgen17_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^and Ishmael, his son, was thirteen years old when he was circumcised
at the flesh of his foreskin.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_25.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:26*](#Contents.html_rgen17_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^In that very day Abraham was circumcised, and Ishmael, his son;

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_26.jpg){height="44" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 17:27*](#Contents.html_rgen17_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^and all the men of his house, homeborn and purchased with money from
a foreigner, were circumcised with him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap17_27.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter18.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:1*](#Contents.html_rgen18_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## A[ND]{.smallcaps1} H[E]{.smallcaps1} A[PPEARED]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **18** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH appeared to him at the oaks of Mamre. And he was sitting at
the tent entrance in the heat of the day,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18.jpg){height="20" width="49"}

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_01.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:2*](#Contents.html_rgen18_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^and he raised his eyes and saw, and here were three people standing
over him. And he saw and ran toward them from the tent entrance and
bowed to the ground.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_02.jpg){height="75" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:2. **bowed**. The word in Hebrew refers to bowing prostrate, nose to
the ground.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:3*](#Contents.html_rgen18_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And he said, "My Lord, if I've found favor in your eyes don't pass on
from your servant.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_03.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:3. **My Lord**. Why does Abraham run to the three people and bow to
them but then speak to God? Is he meeting God, people, or angels?
Several of the biblical stories involving angels contain confusions such
as this, i.e., confusions between when it is the deity and when it is
the angel who is speaking or doing something. But it is confusing only
so long as we imagine angels as beings who are independent or separate
from God. These texts indicate that angels are rather conceived of here
as expressions of God's presence. The consistent biblical conception of
God is that God cannot possibly be seen by a human ("A human will not
see me and live," Exod 33:20) and cannot possibly be contained in any
known space ("The heavens ... will not contain you," 1 Kings 8:27). God,
in this conception, can nonetheless make Himself known to humans by a
sort of emanation from the Godhead that is visible to human eyes. It is
a hypostasis, a concrete expression of the divine presence, which is
otherwise inexpressible to human beings. What the human sees when such a
hypostasis is in front of him or her looks like "people," like a "man."
And the word for such a thing is "angel." Thus Jacob can encounter an
angel and still say "I've seen God face-to-face" (Gen 32:31). And
Abraham can face three angels and address "my Lord." And an angel can
speak God's words in first person or can speak about God in third
person. All this is so because in some ways an angel is an identifiable
thing itself, and in some ways it is merely a representation of divine
presence in human affairs. (See *The Hidden Face of God, pp*. 10--11.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:4*](#Contents.html_rgen18_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^Let a little water be gotten, and wash your feet and relax under a
tree,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_04.jpg){height="48" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:5*](#Contents.html_rgen18_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^and let me get a bit of bread, and satisfy your heart. Afterward
you'll pass on, for that's why you've passed by your servant."

And they said, "Do that, as you've spoken."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_05.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:6*](#Contents.html_rgen18_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

measures of fine flour. Knead it and make cakes."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_06.jpg){height="71" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:7*](#Contents.html_rgen18_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

gave it to a servant, and he hurried to prepare it.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_07.jpg){height="72" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:8*](#Contents.html_rgen18_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

placed them in front of them, and he was standing over them under the
tree, and they ate.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_08.jpg){height="70" width="310"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Genesis 18:9*](#Contents.html_rgen18_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

And he said, "Here in the tent."

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:10*](#Contents.html_rgen18_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

here, Sarah, your wife, will have a son."

And Sarah was listening at the tent entrance, which was behind him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_10.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:11*](#Contents.html_rgen18_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:12*](#Contents.html_rgen18_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:13*](#Contents.html_rgen18_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_13.jpg){height="46" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:15*](#Contents.html_rgen18_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^since Abraham *will* become a big and powerful nation, and all the

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_19.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:20*](#Contents.html_rgen18_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And YHWH said, "The cry of Sodom and Gomorrah: how great it is. And
their sin: how very heavy it is.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_20.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:21*](#Contents.html_rgen18_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^Let me go down, and I'll see if they've done, all told, like the cry
that has come to me. And if not, let me know."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_21.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:22*](#Contents.html_rgen18_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And the people turned from there and went to Sodom.

And Abraham was still standing before YHWH,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_22.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:23*](#Contents.html_rgen18_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^and Abraham came over and said, "Will you also annihilate the
virtuous with the wicked?

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_23.jpg){height="40" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:24*](#Contents.html_rgen18_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^Maybe there are fifty virtuous people within the city. Will you also
annihilate and not sustain the place for the fifty virtuous who are in
it?

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_24.jpg){height="92" width="360"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:25*](#Contents.html_rgen18_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^Far be it from you to do a thing like this, to kill virtuous with
wicked---and it will be the same for the virtuous and the wicked---far
be it from you. Will the judge of all the earth not do justice?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_25.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:25. **Far be it from you to do a thing like this**. This is the first
time in the Bible that a human questions a divine decision. Moses will
take this even farther on at least three occasions. Concerning the
exchange between God and Abraham, see the comment on Gen 22:3.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:26*](#Contents.html_rgen18_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And YHWH said, "If I find in Sodom fifty virtuous people within the
city, then I'll sustain the whole place for their sake."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_26.jpg){height="46" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:27*](#Contents.html_rgen18_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

my Lord, and I'm dust and ashes.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_27.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:28*](#Contents.html_rgen18_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

destroy the whole city for the five?"

And He said, "I won't destroy if I find forty-five there."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_28.jpg){height="101" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:29*](#Contents.html_rgen18_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And he went on again to speak to Him and said, "Maybe forty will be
found there."

And He said, "I won't do it for the sake of the forty."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_29.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:30*](#Contents.html_rgen18_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And he said, "May my Lord not be angry, and let me speak. Maybe
thirty will be found there."

And He said, "I won't do it if I find thirty there."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_30.jpg){height="92" width="361"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:31*](#Contents.html_rgen18_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And he said, "Here I've undertaken to speak to my Lord. Maybe twenty
will be found there."

And He said, "I won't destroy for the sake of the twenty."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_31.jpg){height="92" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 18:32*](#Contents.html_rgen18_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And he said, "May my Lord not be angry, and let me speak just this
one time. Maybe ten will be found there."

And He said there, "I won't destroy for the sake of the ten."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_32.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Genesis 18:33*](#Contents.html_rgen18_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

went back to his place.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap18_33.jpg){height="52" width="325"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter19.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:1*](#Contents.html_rgen19_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **19** {.h2a}

^1^And the two angels came to Sodom in the evening, and Lot was sitting
at Sodom's gate, and Lot saw and got up toward them, and he bowed, nose
to the ground.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_01.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:2*](#Contents.html_rgen19_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And he said, "Here, my lords, turn to your servant's house and spend
the night and wash your feet, and you'll get up early and go your way."

And they said, "No, we'll spend the night in the square."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_02.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:3*](#Contents.html_rgen19_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And he pressed them very much, and they turned to him and came to his
house, and he made a feast and baked unleavened bread for them, and they
ate.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_03.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:4*](#Contents.html_rgen19_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^They had not yet lain down, and the people of the city, the people of
Sodom, surrounded the house, from youth to old man, all the people, from
the farthest reaches.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_04.jpg){height="47" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:5*](#Contents.html_rgen19_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And they called to Lot and said to him, "Where are the people who
came to you tonight? Bring them out to us, and let's know them!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_05.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:5. **let's know them**. The word "know" sometimes has the meaning of
sexual intimacy, as in the case of "Cain knew his wife, and she became
pregnant." That is how it is commonly understood in this passage: the
people are threatening sexual abuse of the guests. This is possible, and
even likely (in light of a parallel story in Judges 19). But this
episode is also commonly understood to be about homosexual rape. I see
no basis for this whatever. The text says that two *people* come to
Sodom, and that *all* of the *people* of Sodom come and say, "Let's know
them." The homosexuality interpretation apparently comes from
misunderstanding the Hebrew word '*![Image](images/a1.jpg){.block
height="12" width="8"}n![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}šîm* to mean "men," instead of "people."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:6*](#Contents.html_rgen19_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Lot went out to them at the entrance and closed the door behind
him,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_06.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:7*](#Contents.html_rgen19_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^and he said, "Don't do bad, my brothers.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_07.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:8*](#Contents.html_rgen19_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^Here I have two daughters who haven't known a man. Let me bring them
out to you, and do to them as is good in your eyes. Only don't do
anything to these people, because that is why they came under the shadow
of my roof."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_08.jpg){height="95" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:8. **I have two daughters\.... do to them as is good in your eyes**.
Some say that this is a matter of ancient Near Eastern hospitality: Lot
as the host must do *anything* to protect his guests. Some say that
daughters were held in low esteem in that world's values. Ramban and
others say that this shows that Lot had an evil heart. It seems to me
that it is not the Near Eastern tradition of hospitality but of
*bargaining* that accounts for what is going on here. When Abraham goes
to buy a tomb for Sarah, the seller says to him, "I've *given* it to
you!" (Gen 23:11). But he does *not* give it. Then Abraham says, "I've
given the *money*" (23:13). Then the seller says, "Land worth four
hundred shekels of silver: what's that between me and you?!" (23:15) as
if this were a small sum. But it is a very large sum. And Abraham pays
it without another word. Interpreters have frequently noted the
traditions of negotiating there: deliberate overstatement, saying things
one does not mean: "gracious insincerity." I suggest that they apply
here as well. Lot is supposed to make an extraordinary gesture. He
offers his own daughters. But no one is supposed to take him up on it.
And then, in this horrible town, the gesture does not work anyway. The
people only become angry. (In the parallel story in Judges 19, in a
similar circumstance a host offers his own daughter and his guest's
concubine to the crowd. But the guest gives them the concubine. The host
does not give his daughter.) For another example of these Near Eastern
conventions, see the comment on Gen 33:9.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:9*](#Contents.html_rgen19_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And they said, "Come over here," and they said, "This one comes to
live, and then he *judges!* Now we'll be worse to you than to them." And
they pressed the man, Lot, very much and came over to break down the
door.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_09.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:10*](#Contents.html_rgen19_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And the people reached their hand out and brought Lot in to them in
the house, and they closed the door.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_10.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:11*](#Contents.html_rgen19_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And they struck the people who were at the house's entrance with
blindness, from smallest to biggest, and they wearied themselves with
finding the entrance.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_11.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:12*](#Contents.html_rgen19_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And the people said to Lot, "Who else do you have here---son-in-law
and your sons and your daughters and all that you have in the city-take
them out from the place,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_12.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:13*](#Contents.html_rgen19_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^because we're destroying this place, because its cry has grown big
before YHWH's face, and YHWH has sent us to destroy it."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_13.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:14*](#Contents.html_rgen19_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Lot went out and spoke to his sons-in-law, who had married his
daughters, and said, "Get up, get out of this place, for YHWH is
destroying the city." And he was like a joker in his sons-in-law's eyes.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_14.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:15*](#Contents.html_rgen19_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And as the dawn rose the angels urged Lot, saying, "Get up, take
your wife and your two daughters who are present, or else you'll be
annihilated for the city's crime."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_15.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:16*](#Contents.html_rgen19_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And he delayed. And the people took hold of his hand and his wife's
hand and his two daughters' hands because of YHWH's compassion for him,
and they brought him out and set him outside the city.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_16.jpg){height="70" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:17*](#Contents.html_rgen19_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And it was as they were bringing them outside, and He said, "Escape
for your life. Don't look behind you and don't stop in all of the plain.
Escape to the mountain or else you'll be annihilated."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_17.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:18*](#Contents.html_rgen19_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Lot said to them, "Let it not be, my Lord.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_18.jpg){height="27" width="238"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:19*](#Contents.html_rgen19_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^Here, your servant has found favor in your eyes, and you've
magnified your kindness that you've done for me, keeping my soul alive,
and I'm not able to escape to the mountain, in case the bad thing will
cling to me and I'll die.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_19.jpg){height="101" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:20*](#Contents.html_rgen19_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^Here, this city is close to flee there, and it's small. Let me
escape there---isn't it small?---and my soul will live."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_20.jpg){height="75" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:21*](#Contents.html_rgen19_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And He said to him, "Here, I've granted you this thing, too, that I
won't overturn the city of which you spoke.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_21.jpg){height="76" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:22*](#Contents.html_rgen19_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^Quickly, escape there, because I can't do a thing until you get
there."

On account of this the city's name was called Zoar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_22.jpg){height="76" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:22. **Zoar**. This name has the same root as the word "small," which
Lot uses twice in his plea to go there (19:20).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:23*](#Contents.html_rgen19_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^The sun rose on the earth, and Lot came to Zoar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_23.jpg){height="52" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:24*](#Contents.html_rgen19_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And YHWH rained brimstone and fire on Sodom and on Gomorrah, from
YHWH out of the skies.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_24.jpg){height="51" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:25*](#Contents.html_rgen19_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And He overturned these cities and all of the plain and all of the
residents of the cities and all the growth of the ground.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_25.jpg){height="76" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:26*](#Contents.html_rgen19_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And his wife looked behind him, and she was a pillar of salt!

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_26.jpg){height="50" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:27*](#Contents.html_rgen19_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And Abraham got up early in the morning to the place where he had
stood in YHWH's presence,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_27.jpg){height="52" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:28*](#Contents.html_rgen19_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^and he gazed at the sight of Sodom and Gomorrah and at the sight of
all the land of the plain. And he saw: and, here, the smoke of the land
went up like the smoke of a furnace.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_28.jpg){height="74" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:29*](#Contents.html_rgen19_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And it was, when God destroyed the cities of the plain, that God
remembered Abraham and let Lot go from inside the overthrow: at the
overthrowing of the cities in which Lot lived.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_29.jpg){height="76" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:30*](#Contents.html_rgen19_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And Lot went up from Zoar and lived in the mountain, and his two
daughters with him, because he feared to live in Zoar, and he lived in a
cave, he and his two daughters.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_30.jpg){height="79" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:31*](#Contents.html_rgen19_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And the firstborn said to the younger one, "Our father is old, and
there's no man in the earth to come to us in the way of all the earth.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_31.jpg){height="52" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:32*](#Contents.html_rgen19_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^Come on, let's make our father drink wine, and let's lie with him
and make seed live from our father."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_32.jpg){height="25" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:33*](#Contents.html_rgen19_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And they made their father drink wine in that night, and the
firstborn came and lay with her father. And he did not know of her lying
down and her getting up.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_33.jpg){height="75" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:34*](#Contents.html_rgen19_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And it was on the next day, and the firstborn said to the younger
one, "Here, I lay with my father last night. Let's make him drink wine
tonight as well, and you come and lie with him, and we'll make seed live
from our father."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_34.jpg){height="78" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:35*](#Contents.html_rgen19_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And they made their father drink wine in that night as well, and the
younger one got up and lay with him, and he did not know of her lying
down and her getting up.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_35.jpg){height="52" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:36*](#Contents.html_rgen19_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And Lot's two daughters became pregnant by their father.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_36.jpg){height="26" width="276"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:37*](#Contents.html_rgen19_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^And the firstborn gave birth to a son and called his name Moab. He
is the father of Moab to this day.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_37.jpg){height="78" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 19:38*](#Contents.html_rgen19_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^And the younger one, she too gave birth to a son and called his name
ben-Ammi. He is the father of the children of Ammon to this day.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap19_38.jpg){height="49" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter20.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:1*](#Contents.html_rgen20_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **20** {.h2a}

^1^And Abraham traveled from there to the Negeb country and lived
between Kadesh and Shur and resided in Gerar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_01.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:2*](#Contents.html_rgen20_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Abraham said of Sarah, his wife, "She's my sister." And Abimelek,
king of Gerar, sent and took Sarah.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_02.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:3*](#Contents.html_rgen20_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And God came to Abimelek in a night dream and said to him, "Here,
you're dead over the woman whom you took, for she's a man's wife!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_03.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:4*](#Contents.html_rgen20_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Abimelek had not come close to her, and he said, "My Lord, will
you kill a virtuous nation as well?

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_04.jpg){height="45" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:5*](#Contents.html_rgen20_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^Didn't he say to me, 'She's my sister'? And she---she, too---said,
'He's my brother.' I did this in my heart's innocence and in my hands'
cleanness."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_05.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:6*](#Contents.html_rgen20_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And God said to him in the dream, "I, too, knew that you did this in
your heart's innocence; and I, too, held you back from sinning against
me. On account of this, I didn't let you touch her.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_06.jpg){height="97" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:7*](#Contents.html_rgen20_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And now, give the man's wife back, because he's a prophet, and he'll
pray for you. And live! And if you don't give back, know that you will
*die*, you and all that you have."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

20:7. **prophet**. This is the first and only occurrence of the word
"prophet" in Genesis. Prophecy as a formal institution begins in Exodus
20. (See the comment on Exod 20:16.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:8*](#Contents.html_rgen20_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Abimelek got up in the morning and called to all his servants and
spoke all these things in their ears, and the people were very afraid.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_08.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:9*](#Contents.html_rgen20_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Abimelek called Abraham and said to him, "What have you done to
us, and how did I sin against you, that you brought a big sin over me
and over my kingdom? You've done things with me that are not done!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_09.jpg){height="92" width="360"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:10*](#Contents.html_rgen20_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Abimelek said to Abraham, "What did you see, that you did this
thing?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_10.jpg){height="45" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:11*](#Contents.html_rgen20_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Abraham said, "Because I said, There just isn't the fear of God
in this place, and they'll kill me on account of my wife.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_11.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:12*](#Contents.html_rgen20_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And also she is, in fact, my sister, my father's daughter but not my
mother's daughter, and she became a wife to me.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_12.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:13*](#Contents.html_rgen20_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And it was when God had me roam from my father's house, and I said
to her, This will be your kindness that you'll do with me: to every
place where we come, say about me, "He's my brother."'"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_13.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

20:13. **had me roam**. The Hebrew verb that is used in connection with
God here is a plural, which is extremely unusual. Possibly this
formulation is attributed to Abraham because he is speaking to a pagan
king, and this king has not told him that Abraham's God has appeared to
him. (The Samaritan text has the singular verb here.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:14*](#Contents.html_rgen20_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Abimelek took sheep and oxen and servants and maids, and he gave
them to Abraham, and he gave Sarah, his wife, back to him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_14.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:15*](#Contents.html_rgen20_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Abimelek said, "Here's my land in front of you. Live wherever
it's good in your eyes."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_15.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:16*](#Contents.html_rgen20_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And to Sarah he said, "Here, I've given a thousand weights of silver
to your brother. Here, it's a covering of eyes for you with everyone
with you, and you're justified with everyone."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_16.jpg){height="65" width="351"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:17*](#Contents.html_rgen20_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And Abraham prayed to God, and God healed Abimelek and his wife and
his maids, and they gave birth,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_17.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 20:18*](#Contents.html_rgen20_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^because YHWH *had held* back every womb of Abimelek's house on
account of Sarah, Abraham's wife!

![Image](images/Gen_Chap20_18.jpg){height="67" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter21.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:1*](#Contents.html_rgen21_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **21** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH took account of Sarah as He had said, and YHWH did to Sarah
as He had spoken.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_01.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:2*](#Contents.html_rgen21_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Sarah became pregnant and gave birth to a son for Abraham in his
old age at the appointed time that God had spoken.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_02.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:3*](#Contents.html_rgen21_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Abraham called the name of his son who was born to him, to whom
Sarah had given birth for him: Isaac.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_03.jpg){height="71" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:4*](#Contents.html_rgen21_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Abraham circumcised Isaac, his son, at eight days old, as God had
commanded him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_04.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:5*](#Contents.html_rgen21_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Abraham was a hundred years old when Isaac, his son, was born to
him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_05.jpg){height="69" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:6*](#Contents.html_rgen21_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Sarah said, "God has made laughter for me. Everyone who hears
will laugh for me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_06.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:7*](#Contents.html_rgen21_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And she said, "Who would have said to Abraham, 'Sarah has nursed
children'? Yet I've given birth to a son in his old age."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_07.jpg){height="69" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:8*](#Contents.html_rgen21_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And the boy grew and was weaned. And Abraham made a big feast in the
day that Isaac was weaned.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_08.jpg){height="97" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:9*](#Contents.html_rgen21_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Sarah saw the son of Hagar, the Egyptian, whom she had borne to
Abraham, fooling around.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_09.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

21:9. **fooling around**. The Greek text has "fooling around with her
son Isaac," which can connote either playing with Isaac or mocking him.
The word in Hebrew is ![Image](images/page71-1.jpg){.block height="17"
width="41"}, which is a pun on the name Isaac. It will occur again in
the story of Isaac, Re-bekah, and Abimelek (Gen 26:8).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:10*](#Contents.html_rgen21_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And she said to Abraham, "Drive this maid out---and her son, because
the son of this maid will not inherit with my son, with Isaac."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_10.jpg){height="75" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:11*](#Contents.html_rgen21_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And the thing was very bad in Abraham's eyes in regard to his son.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_11.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

21:11. **the thing was very bad in Abraham's eyes in regard to his
son**. The Israelite who wrote this text---which favors his own
ancestor, Isaac---still expressed sympathy for Ishmael. He wrote of
Abraham's affection for him and God's promise for him.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:12*](#Contents.html_rgen21_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And God said to Abraham, "Let it not be bad in your eyes about the
boy and about your maid. Everything that Sarah tells you: listen to her
voice; because seed for you will be called by Isaac.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_12.jpg){height="71" width="382"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

21:12. **seed for you will be called by Isaac**. Meaning: Abraham's
best-known line will later be identified as children of
Abraham-Isaac-Jacob, rather than Abraham-Ishmael or Abraham-Midian or
Abraham-Zimran.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:13*](#Contents.html_rgen21_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And I'll make the maid's son into a nation as well, because he's
your seed."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_13.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

21:13. **I'll make the maid's son into a nation as well**. Namely, the
Ishmaelites. Their genealogy appears in Gen 25:13--18, and they will
figure in the Joseph story (37:25--28; 39:1). They do not appear in the
Torah after that.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:14*](#Contents.html_rgen21_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Abraham got up early in the morning and took bread and a bottle
of water and gave to Hagar---he put them on her shoulder---and the boy
and he sent her off. And she went and strayed in the Beer-sheba
wilderness.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_14.jpg){height="70" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------
