[*Genesis 21:15*](#Contents.html_rgen21_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And the water was finished from the bottle, and she thrust the boy
under one of the shrubs,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_15.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:16*](#Contents.html_rgen21_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^and she went and sat opposite, going as far as a bow-shot, because
she said, "Let me not see the boy's death." And she sat opposite and
raised her voice and wept.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_16.jpg){height="71" width="343"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:17*](#Contents.html_rgen21_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And God heard the boy's voice. And an angel of God called to Hagar
from the heavens and said to her, "What trouble do you have, Hagar?
Don't be afraid. Because God has heard the boy's voice where he is.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_17.jpg){height="95" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:18*](#Contents.html_rgen21_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^Get up. Carry the boy and hold him up in your hand, because I shall
make him into a big nation."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_18.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

21:18. **I shall make him into a big nation**. These words are spoken by
the angel, but they certainly appear to be the words of God, not the
angel itself. Angels do not make great nations in the *Tanak*. This is
another demonstration that angels are not independent beings in the
Hebrew Bible but are rather expressions of God's presence. (See the
comment on Gen 18:3.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:19*](#Contents.html_rgen21_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And God opened her eyes, and she saw a water well, and she went and
filled the bottle with water and had the boy drink.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_19.jpg){height="50" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:20*](#Contents.html_rgen21_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And God was with the boy, and he grew, and he lived in the
wilderness and was a bowman.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_20.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:21*](#Contents.html_rgen21_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And he lived in the Paran wilderness, and his mother took him a wife
from the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_21.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:22*](#Contents.html_rgen21_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And it was at that time, and Abimelek and Phichol, the commander of
his army, said to Abraham, saying, "God is with you in everything that
you do.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_22.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:23*](#Contents.html_rgen21_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And now, swear to me here by God that you won't act falsely to me
and to my offspring and to my posterity Like the kindness that I've done
with you, you'll do with me and with the land in which you've resided."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_23.jpg){height="92" width="351"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:24*](#Contents.html_rgen21_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And Abraham said, "I'll swear,"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_24.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:25*](#Contents.html_rgen21_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^and Abraham criticized Abimelek about a water well that Abimelek's
servants had seized.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_25.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:26*](#Contents.html_rgen21_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And Abimelek said, "I don't know who did this thing; and also, you
didn't tell me; and also, I didn't hear of it except for today."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_26.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:27*](#Contents.html_rgen21_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And Abraham took sheep and oxen and gave to Abimelek, and the two of
them made a covenant.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_27.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:28*](#Contents.html_rgen21_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And Abraham stood seven ewes of the sheep by themselves.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_28.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:29*](#Contents.html_rgen21_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And Abimelek said to Abraham, "What are these seven ewes that you've
stood by themselves?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_29.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:30*](#Contents.html_rgen21_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And he said, "Because you'll take these seven ewes from my hand so
that it will be evidence for me that I dug this well."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_30.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:31*](#Contents.html_rgen21_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^On account of this he called that place Beer-sheba, because the two
of them swore there

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_31.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:32*](#Contents.html_rgen21_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^and made a covenant in Beer-sheba. And Abimelek and Phichol, the
commander of his army, got up and went to the land of the Philistines.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_32.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:33*](#Contents.html_rgen21_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And he planted a tamarisk in Beer-sheba and he called the name of
YHWH El Olam.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_33.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 21:34*](#Contents.html_rgen21_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And Abraham resided in the land of the Philistines many days.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap21_34.jpg){height="65" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter22.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:1*](#Contents.html_rgen22_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **22** {.h2a}

^1^And it was after these things, and God tested Abraham.

And He said to him, "Abraham."

And he said, "I'm here."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_01.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

22:1. **He said to him, "Abraham."** The Greek and Samaritan versions
have his name called twice here: "Abraham, Abraham." This parallels the
call by the angel later (v. 11), by which Abraham is stopped from
sacrificing Isaac. Thus the instruction to sacrifice him and the
instruction to hold back are given equal weight. This also parallels the
repetition of Moses' name in God's first words to him at the burning
bush (Exod 3:4) and the repetition of Samuel's name the first time God
speaks to him (1 Sam 3:10). This appears to be a mark of divine
communication at significant moments in the biblical narrative. What
stands out about Abraham is that it comes here in the Aqedah rather than
the first time that God speaks to him. This marks the near-sacrifice as
a defining event in Abraham's life and in the destiny of his
descendants.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:2*](#Contents.html_rgen22_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And He said, "Take your son, your only one, whom you love, Isaac, and
go to the land of Mo-riah and make him a burnt offering there on one of
the mountains that I'll say to you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_02.jpg){height="71" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

22:2. **Take your son, your only one, whom you love, Isaac**. If the
issue were only a matter of identification, just the name Isaac would
have been sufficient; but the issue, we are told explicitly in the first
verse of the story, is the test. The fourfold, heartrending
identification creates background for all that is to come. Now Abraham's
unquestioning obedience is understood against this background: "your
son, your only one, whom you love, Isaac." The otherwise minor temporal
note that "Abraham got up early in the morning" to do the deed becomes a
fact worthy of wonder and interpretation against the background of "your
son, your only one, whom you love, Isaac." The notation that he puts the
wood for the sacrificial fire on Isaac himself to carry becomes an
ironic image. Abraham's words to the servants who accompany them---"I
and the boy: we'll go over there, and we'll bow, and we'll come back to
you"---become not only enigmatic but emotionally charged. (Does his
saying *"we'll* come back" suggest extraordinary faith? one last hope?
Or is it constructed so as not to frighten Isaac?) The words of the
dialogue between the father and son become charged by this background as
well, as Isaac adds the phrase "my father" in his question addressed to
Abraham, and Abraham adds "my son" in each sentence in response. (The
words "son" and "father" occur twelve times in the story and are, in
almost every case, unnecessary for identification.) The dialogue
moreover begins and ends with the words "and the two of them went
together," another mundane phrase turned into a remarkable one by what
has preceded.

Most remarkable of all is the exchange between Abraham and Isaac over
the obvious absence of an animal to be sacrificed. Isaac says, "Here are
the fire and the wood, but where is the sheep for the burnt offering?"
To appreciate the artistry of the wording of Abraham's answer, we must
keep in mind that the Hebrew has no punctuation. The text says: "God
will see to the sheep for the burnt offering my son"---which can be
understood in two ways. The last two words can be read as a touching
epithet:

"God will see to the sheep for the burnt offering, my son."

But they can also be read as a fearful irony:

"God will see to the sheep for the burnt offering: *My son*."

---subtly conveying the truth that Isaac himself is to be the sheep for
the sacrifice.

And so the short passage, which like the entire chapter expresses no
emotions explicitly, reads like a tightly packed container whose
contents may burst in a moment:

::: quote
        And the two of them went together.

        And Isaac said to Abraham his father; he said, "My father."

        And he said, "I'm here, my son."

        And he said, "Here are the fire and the wood, but where is the
sheep for the burnt offering?"

        And Abraham said, "God will see to the sheep for the burnt
offering my son." And the two of them went together.
:::

The denouement of the story, in which Isaac is spared, is no less
charged by all of this expressed and unexpressed background. Indeed,
Abraham's feelings (relief? gratitude? reverence? confusion?) are
likewise not a part of the narrative. There is rather a reminiscence of
the original wording, as God twice says to Abraham that now all is well
because "You didn't hold back *your son, your only one*" (22:12,16; cf.
22:2). As biblical interpreters noted centuries ago, the wording
suggests that the reward at the end is in proportion to the difficulty
of the test as expressed at the beginning.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:3*](#Contents.html_rgen22_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Abraham got up early in the morning and harnessed his ass and
took his two boys with him and Isaac, his son. And he cut the wood for
the burnt offering, and he got up and went to the place that God had
said to him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_03.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

22:3. **Abraham got up early in the morning**. Abraham fights for the
lives of the people of Sodom and Gomorrah but not for the life of Isaac.
That is a strange fact itself, and then these two stories are juxtaposed
by being placed in the same parashah so that they are regularly read
together, which makes the ironic relationship between them even harder
to ignore. When God tells Abraham, "The cry of Sodom and Gomorrah: how
great it is. And their sin: how very heavy it is. Let me go down, and
I'll see if they've done, all told, like the cry that has come to me.
And if not, let me know" (18:20--21), Abraham's response is to be the
first human in the Bible's story to challenge a divine decision. But
when God tells him, "Take your son, your only one, whom you love, Isaac
... and offer him as a burnt offering," the report is: And Abraham got
up early in the morning!

The text does not tell us what Abraham's motive is for questioning his
God about the fate of Sodom. Is it because his nephew, Lot, lives there?
Or is it Abraham's sense of justice? Or is it his compassion for people
who are facing a divine catastrophe? Two of the above? All of the above?
Even if it is mainly his concern for Lot, still, that's a nephew. Isaac
is his son! Even if it is his compassion for those morally-challenged
people of Sodom, still, there must also be compassion for his son. And
if it is his sense of justice, is there less of a case for justice in
connection with sacrificing an innocent Isaac than in the matter of the
destruction of those very guilty people?

One possible answer: The mark of Abraham's personality is *obedience*.
He will obey anything that his God commands him to do. Leave your land.
Leave your birthplace. Leave your father's house. Circumcise yourself.
Even if he is commanded to sacrifice his child, he will do it. There are
no arguments or even questions. There is only immediate compliance. But
the case of Sodom and Gomorrah is different because in this case Abraham
is not commanded to do anything. He is not the one who is to execute
judgment on the cities. It is even intriguing that the deity chooses to
inform Abraham of the divine concern over the cries from Sodom and
Gomorrah in the first place. Whatever reason one imagines for God to
tell Abraham about this, the fact is that by informing Abraham of the
divine intention to look into the situation in Sodom and Gomorrah, God
opens the door for Abraham to speak up. Commands, on the other hand,
leave no room for discussion.

A second answer *(d![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}bar 'a![Image](images/hdot.jpg){.block height="15"
width="7"}![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14" width="6"}r*): The
reason that Abraham speaks up for the more distant relative and for all
the unrelated populace of Sodom and Gomorrah but does not speak up for
his own son is precisely *because* it is his own son. He is sufficiently
distant from Lot and the populace of Sodom that he is in a position to
argue whether their destruction would be just. And so he argues: "Will
the judge of all the earth not do justice?!" But in the case of Isaac,
he is not what we would call an unbiased observer. He has a personal
interest in the case, an intensely personal interest. And, as such, he
does not have the same standing to argue the justice or injustice of the
case.

A third answer *(d![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}bar 'ah![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14"
width="6"}r*): Another possible explanation is that the outcome of the
Sodom and Gomorrah matter itself is what causes Abraham to stay silent
in the matter of Isaac. In the famous dialogue between God and Abraham
over the fate of the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah, the exchange is
subtle as God responds not merely to what Abraham says but apparently to
what is in Abraham's heart. Thus: when Abraham learns that God means to
judge these cities, he says, "Maybe there are fifty virtuous people
within the city" (18:24). God responds, "If I find in Sodom fifty
virtuous people within the city, then I'll sustain the whole place for
their sake" (18:26). Abraham's next bargaining position will be the
sparing of the place for forty-five righteous, but he articulates his
question in such a way as to make it seem a small request. He does not
say "forty-five." He says:

::: quote
Maybe the fifty virtuous people will be short by five.

Will you destroy the whole city for *the five?*
:::

To which God replies, "I won't destroy if I find *forty-five* there."
Thus God would appear to be conveying to Abraham that there is not much
point to being cagey in one's words when one is arguing with someone who
is omniscient. After this, Abraham words his positions more
straightforwardly: What if there are forty? thirty? etc. Abraham gets
the message that he may as well say directly what is on his mind because
that is what is going to be responded to in any case.

Even then, Abraham succeeds in getting down to ten. God agrees to
sustain the cities for ten virtuous people. But not even ten are to be
found, and the cities are destroyed. So even though Abraham's dialogue
with God is important in several ways---including: it provides Abraham
with an opportunity to articulate the principle of justice for future
generations; and it is an important step in the growth of humankind's
independence and development of responsibility---the fact remains that
nothing is changed for Sodom and Gomorrah by this. Abraham learns that,
since God knows what is in one's heart, why argue? Since God knows the
situation and its necessary outcome, why speak? After the destruction of
Sodom and Gomorrah, Abraham never argues with God again. Moses will
argue with God, Jonah will, Jeremiah will, but not Abraham. When the
creator tells Abraham to listen to Sarah and send Hagar and Ishmael
away, "Abraham got up early in the morning" (21:14). When the creator
tells him to sacrifice his son, "Abraham got up early in the morning"
(22:3).

But Abraham's obedience is what saves Isaac in the end. What an irony:
Abraham's silence over Isaac is more effective than his argument over
Sodom and Gomorrah.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:4*](#Contents.html_rgen22_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^On the third day: and Abraham raised his eyes and saw the place from
a distance.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_04.jpg){height="65" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:5*](#Contents.html_rgen22_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Abraham said to his boys, "Sit here with the ass; and I and the
boy: we'll go over there, and we'll bow, and we'll come back to you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_05.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:6*](#Contents.html_rgen22_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Abraham took the wood for the burnt offering and put it on Isaac,
his son, and took the fire and the knife in his hand.

And the two of them went together.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_06.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:7*](#Contents.html_rgen22_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Isaac said to Abraham, his father; and he said, "My father."

And he said, "I'm here, my son."

And he said, "Here are the fire and the wood, but where is the sheep for
the burnt offering?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_07.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:8*](#Contents.html_rgen22_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Abraham said, "God will see to the sheep for the burnt offering,
my son."

And the two of them went together.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_08.jpg){height="75" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:9*](#Contents.html_rgen22_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And they came to the place that God had said to him. And Abraham
built the altar there and arranged the wood, and he bound Isaac, his
son, and put him on the altar on top of the wood.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_09.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:10*](#Contents.html_rgen22_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Abraham put out his hand and took the knife to slaughter his
son.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_10.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:11*](#Contents.html_rgen22_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And an angel of YHWH called to him from the skies and said,
"Abraham! Abraham!"

And he said, "I'm here."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_11.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:12*](#Contents.html_rgen22_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And he said, "Don't put your hand out toward the boy, and don't do
anything to him, because now I know that you fear God, and you didn't
withhold your son, your only one, from me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_12.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:13*](#Contents.html_rgen22_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Abraham raised his eyes and saw, and here was a ram behind,
caught in the thicket by its horns. And Abraham went and took the ram
and made it a burnt offering instead of his son.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_13.jpg){height="70" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

22:13. **behind**. Some manuscripts read "one" ram, i.e., Hebrew
![Image](images/page22-1.jpg){.block height="13" width="31"}, rather
than "behind," Hebrew ![Image](images/page22-2.jpg){.block height="13"
width="31"}. "One" makes plainer sense, but this may very well be a case
that suggests the principle of *lectio difficilior praeferenda est* (the
more difficult reading is preferable). That is, scribes tend to simplify
an unusual reading more often than they make a change that turns a
simple reading into a more difficult one. The matter remains unsolved.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:14*](#Contents.html_rgen22_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Abraham called the name of that place "YHWH Yir'eh," as is said
today: "In YHWH's mountain it will be seen."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_14.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

22:14. **YHWH Yir'eh**. Meaning "YHWH Will See." (Perhaps it should be
pointed as YHWH Yera'eh, "YHWH Will Appear"---which is how it appears
later in the same verse.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:15*](#Contents.html_rgen22_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And an angel of YHWH called to Abraham a second time from the skies

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_15.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:16*](#Contents.html_rgen22_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^and said, "I swear by me---word of YHWH---that because you did this
thing and didn't withhold your son, your only one,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_16.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:17*](#Contents.html_rgen22_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^that I'll *bless* you and *multiply* your seed like the stars of the
skies and like the sand that's on the seashore, and your seed will
possess its enemies' gate.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_17.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:18*](#Contents.html_rgen22_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And all the nations of the earth will be blessed through your seed
because you listened to my voice."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_18.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

22:18. **all the nations of the earth will be blessed through your
seed**. This is the third occurrence of this promise. (See the comment
on Gen 12:3.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:19*](#Contents.html_rgen22_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And Abraham went back to his boys, and they got up and went together
to Beer-sheba, and Abraham lived in Beer-sheba.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_19.jpg){height="46" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

22:19. **Abraham went back to his boys**. The text does not mention
Isaac going back. (And Abraham had specifically told the boys, "*We' ll*
come back to you.") Both midrash and critical scholarship have dared to
raise the possibility that in one version of the story Abraham actually
sacrifices Isaac (discussed in *Who Wrote the Bible?* p. 257). But in
the present form of the text itself, Isaac clearly is spared. And that
makes this verse extraordinarily powerful. Following the Aqedah, Isaac
and Abraham do not go back together. Moreover: following the Aqedah,
Isaac and Abraham are never quoted as speaking to each other.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:20*](#Contents.html_rgen22_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And it was after these things, and it was told to Abraham, saying,
"Here Milcah has given birth, she also, to sons for your brother Nahor:

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_20.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:21*](#Contents.html_rgen22_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^Uz, his firstborn, and Buz his brother, and Kemuel, the father of
Aram,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_21.jpg){height="50" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:22*](#Contents.html_rgen22_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^and Chesed and Hazo and Pildash and Jidlaph and Bethuel.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_22.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:23*](#Contents.html_rgen22_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And Bethuel fathered Rebekah." Milcah gave birth to these eight for
Nahor, Abraham's brother.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_23.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 22:24*](#Contents.html_rgen22_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And his concubine, whose name was Reumah, she too gave birth, to
Tebah and Gaham and Tahash and Maacah.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap22_24.jpg){height="47" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter23.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:1*](#Contents.html_rgen23_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## S[ARAH'S LIFE]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **23** {.h2a}

^1^And Sarah's life was a hundred years and twenty years and seven
years: the years of Sarah's life.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23.jpg){height="19" width="94"}

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_01.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:2*](#Contents.html_rgen23_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Sarah died in Kiriath Arba---it is Hebron---in the land of
Canaan. And Abraham came to grieve for Sarah and to weep for her.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_02.jpg){height="76" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:3*](#Contents.html_rgen23_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Abraham got up from in front of his dead, and he spoke to the
children of Heth, saying,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_03.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:4*](#Contents.html_rgen23_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^"I'm an alien and a visitor with you. Give me a possession for a tomb
with you so I may bury my dead from in front of me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_04.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

23:4. **alien**. (On the meaning of this term, see the comment on
15:13.) Here it is Abraham who is the alien in the land. This is ironic
because one day this land will belong to his descendants, and so they
are commanded some fifteen times in the Torah to treat aliens well. That
is, the Israelites must be fair to non-Israelites who live in Israel
because once *they* were the aliens there, and because they were aliens
in Egypt: "You know the alien's *soul*, because you were aliens in the
land of Egypt" (Exod 23:9).

23:4. **visitor**. Meaning: a sojourner, a person who is staying in a
place temporarily. With the hendiadys of "an alien and a sojourner,"
Abraham emphasizes to the Hittites that he recognizes that he is an
outsider in their territory. Even though the deity has already told
Abraham that the land of the Hittites will one day belong to his
descendants, Abraham deals with them respectfully and courteously.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:5*](#Contents.html_rgen23_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And the children of Heth answered Abraham, saying to him,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_05.jpg){height="22" width="277"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:6*](#Contents.html_rgen23_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^"Listen to us, my lord. You're a chieftain of God among us. Bury your
dead in the choice of our tombs. Not a man of us will hold back his tomb
from you, from burying your dead."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_06.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:7*](#Contents.html_rgen23_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Abraham got up and bowed to the people of the land, to the
children of Heth,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_07.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:8*](#Contents.html_rgen23_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^and he spoke with them, saying, "If it's acceptable to you to bury my
dead from in front of me, listen to me, and intercede for me with
Ephron, son of Zohar,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_08.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:9*](#Contents.html_rgen23_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^that he'll give me the cave of Machpelah which he has, that is at the
edge of his field. For full price let him give it to me among you as a
possession for a tomb."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_09.jpg){height="75" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:10*](#Contents.html_rgen23_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Ephron was sitting among the children of Heth, and Ephron, the
Hittite, answered Abraham in the ears of the children of Heth, for all
who were coming to his city's gate, saying,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_10.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:11*](#Contents.html_rgen23_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^"No, sir. Listen to me: I've *given* the field to you, and I've
given the cave that's in it to you. I've given it to you before the eyes
of the children of my people. Bury your dead."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_11.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

23:11. **I've *given* the field to you**. But he has *not* given it to
him. They are bargaining, and Ephron follows the practice of adopting an
extraordinarily gracious opening position. But one is not supposed to
take him up on it, and Abraham in fact does not. This in turn sheds
light on the extraordinary offer that Lot makes, to give his daughters
to the people of Sodom for abuse (see the comment on Gen 19:8).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:12*](#Contents.html_rgen23_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And Abraham bowed in front of the people of the land,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_12.jpg){height="22" width="241"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:13*](#Contents.html_rgen23_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^and he spoke to Ephron in the ears of the people of the land,
saying, "Just if you'll listen to me: I've given the money for the
field. Take it from me so I may bury my dead there."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_13.jpg){height="98" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:14*](#Contents.html_rgen23_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Ephron answered Abraham, saying to him,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_14.jpg){height="22" width="262"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:15*](#Contents.html_rgen23_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^"Sir, listen to me. Land worth four hundred shekels of silver:
what's that between me and you?! And bury your dead."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_15.jpg){height="50" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

23:15. **four hundred shekels of silver: what's that between me and
you?!** He says it as if this were a paltry sum. But it is in fact a
great deal.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:16*](#Contents.html_rgen23_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And Abraham listened to Ephron, and Abraham weighed out to Ephron
the money that he had spoken in the ears of the children of Heth: four
hundred shekels of silver, at the merchant's current rate.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_16.jpg){height="94" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:17*](#Contents.html_rgen23_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And Ephron's field that was in Machpelah, which faces Mamre, the
field and the cave that was in it and every tree that was in the field,
that was in all of its border all around, was established

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_17.jpg){height="71" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:18*](#Contents.html_rgen23_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^for Abraham as a purchase in the eyes of the children of Heth, among
all who were coming to his city's gate.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_18.jpg){height="49" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:19*](#Contents.html_rgen23_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And after that Abraham buried Sarah, his wife, at the cave of the
field of Machpelah, facing Mamre---it is Hebron---in the land of Canaan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_19.jpg){height="71" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

23:19. **it is Hebron**. Why does the story of Sarah's death and burial
begin and end with a notice that it is at Hebron (23:2,19)? Because
Abraham's purchase of this land establishes unquestionable title to
property at Hebron, and this is a basis of Israel's return to the land
after the years of slavery in Egypt. Thus, when Moses sends spies into
the land, they go to Hebron, which is singled out among all cities of
Canaan with a verse of description (Num 13:22).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 23:20*](#Contents.html_rgen23_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And the field and the cave that was in it were established for
Abraham as a possession for a tomb from the children of Heth.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap23_20.jpg){height="46" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter24.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:1*](#Contents.html_rgen24_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **24** {.h2a}

^1^And Abraham was old, well along in 24 days, and YHWH had blessed
Abraham in everything.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_01.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:2*](#Contents.html_rgen24_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Abraham said to his servant, the elder of his house, who was in
charge of all that he had, "Place your hand under my thigh,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_02.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:3*](#Contents.html_rgen24_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^and I'll have you swear by YHWH, God of the skies and God of the
earth, that you won't take a wife for my son from the daughters of the
Canaanite among whom I live,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_03.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:4*](#Contents.html_rgen24_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^but you'll go to my land and my birthplace and take a wife for my
son, for Isaac."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_04.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:5*](#Contents.html_rgen24_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And the servant said to him, "Maybe the woman won't be willing to
follow me to this land. Shall I *take back* your son to the land you
came from?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_05.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:6*](#Contents.html_rgen24_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Abraham said to him, "Watch yourself, that you don't take my son
back there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_06.jpg){height="49" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:7*](#Contents.html_rgen24_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^YHWH, God of the skies, who took me from my father's house and from
the land of my birth and who spoke to me and who swore to me, saying,
'I'll give this land to your seed,' He'll send His angel ahead of you,
and you shall take a wife for my son from there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_07.jpg){height="119" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:8*](#Contents.html_rgen24_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And if the woman won't be willing to go after you, then you'll be
freed from this oath of mine. Only you are not to take my son back
there."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_08.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:9*](#Contents.html_rgen24_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And the servant placed his hand under his lord Abraham's thigh and
swore to him about this thing.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_09.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:10*](#Contents.html_rgen24_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And the servant took ten camels of his lord's camels and went, and
all of his lord's best things were in his hand. And he got up and went
to Aram Naharaim, to the city of Nahor,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_10.jpg){height="76" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:11*](#Contents.html_rgen24_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^and he had the camels kneel outside the city at the water well at
evening time, the time that the women went out to draw water.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_11.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:12*](#Contents.html_rgen24_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And he said, "YHWH, God of my lord Abraham, make something happen in
front of me today and show kindness to my lord Abraham:

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_12.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

24:12. **make something happen**. The Hebrew includes the particle
*n![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}'*,
conveying that this is a polite request. Even as a request, though, it
is remarkable: the servant himself asks for a miraculous sign from God,
and he himself names what that sign should be. No human thus far,
including Abraham himself, has gone this far. Abraham had told this
servant that his God "will send His angel ahead of you," and this is
presumably what gives the servant the confidence to do this. Still, it
is noteworthy as one of a chain of steps that humans are taking toward
increasing control of miracle in the *Tanak*.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:13*](#Contents.html_rgen24_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^Here I am, standing over the spring of water, and the daughters of
the people of the city are going out to draw water.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_13.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:14*](#Contents.html_rgen24_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And let it be that the girl to whom I'll say, 'Tip your jar so I may
drink,' and she'll say, 'Drink, and I'll water your camels, too,' she'll
be the one you've pointed out for your servant, for Isaac, and I'll know
by this that you've shown kindness to my lord."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_14.jpg){height="96" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:15*](#Contents.html_rgen24_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And he had not even finished speaking, and here was Rebekah---who
was born to Bethuel son of Milcah, wife of Nahor, Abraham's
brother---coming out, and her jar was on her shoulder.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_15.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:16*](#Contents.html_rgen24_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And the girl was very good-looking, a virgin, and no man had known
her. And she went down to the spring and filled her jar and went up.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_16.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:17*](#Contents.html_rgen24_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And the servant ran toward her and said, "Give me a little water
from your jar."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_17.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:18*](#Contents.html_rgen24_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And she said, "Drink, my lord," and she hurried and lowered her jar
on her arm and let him drink.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_18.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:19*](#Contents.html_rgen24_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And she finished letting him drink, and she said, "I'll draw water
for your camels, too, until they finish drinking."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_19.jpg){height="50" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:20*](#Contents.html_rgen24_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And she hurried and emptied her jar into the trough and ran again to
the well to draw water, and she drew water for all his camels.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_20.jpg){height="72" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:21*](#Contents.html_rgen24_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And the man, astonished at her, was keeping quiet so as to know if
YHWH had made his trip successful or not.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_21.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:22*](#Contents.html_rgen24_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And it was when the camels finished drinking: and the man took a
gold ring---its weight was a beqa---and two bracelets for her
arms---their weight was ten of gold.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_22.jpg){height="97" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

24:22. **beqa**. Half a shekel (see Exod 38:26).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:23*](#Contents.html_rgen24_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And he said, "Tell me, whose daughter are you? Is there a place at
your father's house for us to spend the night?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_23.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:24*](#Contents.html_rgen24_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And she said to him, "I'm a daughter of Bethuel, Milcah's son whom
she bore to Nahor."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_24.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:25*](#Contents.html_rgen24_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And she said to him, "We also have plenty of both straw and fodder,
also a place to spend the night."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_25.jpg){height="51" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:26*](#Contents.html_rgen24_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And the man knelt and bowed to YHWH

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_26.jpg){height="25" width="229"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:27*](#Contents.html_rgen24_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^and said, "Blessed is YHWH, God of my lord Abraham, whose kindness

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_27.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:28*](#Contents.html_rgen24_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And the girl ran and told her mother's household about these things.

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:30*](#Contents.html_rgen24_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And it was when he saw the ring and the bracelets on his sister's
hands and when he heard his sister Rebekah's words, saying, "The man
spoke like this to me." And he came to the man, and here he was standing
by the camels at the spring.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_30.jpg){height="95" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:31*](#Contents.html_rgen24_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And he said, "Come, blessed one of YHWH. Why do you stand outside
when I've prepared the house and a place for the camels!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_31.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:32*](#Contents.html_rgen24_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:33*](#Contents.html_rgen24_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And \[bread\] was set in front of him to eat.

And he said, "I won't eat until I've said what I have to say."

And he said, "Speak."

![Image](images/24page_83.jpg){height="30" width="88"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:34*](#Contents.html_rgen24_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And he said, "I am Abraham's servant.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_34.jpg){height="22" width="202"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Genesis 24:35*](#Contents.html_rgen24_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:37*](#Contents.html_rgen24_37)

son from the daughters of the Canaanite in whose land I live,

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Genesis 24:38*](#Contents.html_rgen24_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:39*](#Contents.html_rgen24_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:40*](#Contents.html_rgen24_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^And he said to me, 'YHWH, before whom I have walked, will send His
angel with you and make your trip successful, and you shall take a wife

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_40.jpg){height="98" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:41*](#Contents.html_rgen24_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^Then you'll be freed from my oath: when you'll come to my family,
and if they won't give her to you, then you'll be free from my oath.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_41.jpg){height="54" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:42*](#Contents.html_rgen24_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^And I came to the spring today and I said, 'YHWH, God of my lord
Abraham, if you're making my trip on which I'm going successful,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_42.jpg){height="73" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:43*](#Contents.html_rgen24_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^here I am, standing at the spring of water, and let it be that the
young woman who goes to draw water and I say to her, "Let me drink a
little water from your jar,"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_43.jpg){height="76" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:44*](#Contents.html_rgen24_44)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^44^and she says to me, "Both drink yourself and I'll draw water for
your camels, too," she will be the woman whom YHWH has designated for my
lord's son.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_44.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:45*](#Contents.html_rgen24_45)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^45^I hadn't even finished speaking in my heart, and here was Rebekah
coming out, and her jar was on her shoulder, and she went down to the
spring and drew water. And I said to her, 'Give me a drink.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_45.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:46*](#Contents.html_rgen24_46)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^46^And she hurried and lowered her jar from on her and said, 'Drink,
and I'll water your camels, too.' And I drank, and she watered the
camels, too.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_46.jpg){height="76" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:47*](#Contents.html_rgen24_47)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^47^And I asked her and said, 'Whose daughter are you?' And she said,
'The daughter of Bethuel, son of Nahor, whom Milcah bore for him.' And I
put the ring on her nose and the bracelets on her hands,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_47.jpg){height="96" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:48*](#Contents.html_rgen24_48)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^48^and I knelt and bowed to YHWH and blessed YHWH, my lord Abraham's
God, who had led me in a faithful way to take a daughter of my lord's
brother for his son.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_48.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:49*](#Contents.html_rgen24_49)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^49^And now, if you're exercising kindness and faithfulness with my
lord, tell me; and if not, tell me; so I'll turn to right or to left."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_49.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:50*](#Contents.html_rgen24_50)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^50^And Laban and Bethuel answered, and they said, "The thing has come
from YHWH. We can't speak bad or good to you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_50.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:51*](#Contents.html_rgen24_51)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^51^Here is Rebekah before you. Take her and go, and let her be a wife
to your lord's son as YHWH has spoken."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_51.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:52*](#Contents.html_rgen24_52)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^52^And it was, when Abraham's servant heard their words, that he bowed
to the ground to YHWH.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_52.jpg){height="49" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:53*](#Contents.html_rgen24_53)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^53^And the servant brought out silver articles and gold articles and
garments and gave them to Rebekah and gave precious things to her
brother and to her mother.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_53.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:54*](#Contents.html_rgen24_54)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^54^And they ate and drank, he and the people who were with him, and
spent the night.

And they got up in the morning, and he said, "Send me back to my lord."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_54.jpg){height="69" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:55*](#Contents.html_rgen24_55)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^55^And her brother and her mother said, "Let the girl stay with us a
few days---or ten. After that, she'll go."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_55.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:56*](#Contents.html_rgen24_56)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^56^And he said to them, "Don't hold me back, since YHWH has made my
trip successful. Send me away, so I may go to my lord."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_56.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:57*](#Contents.html_rgen24_57)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^57^And they said, "We'll call the girl and ask her from her own mouth."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_57.jpg){height="24" width="280"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:58*](#Contents.html_rgen24_58)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^58^And they called Re-bekah and said to her, "Will you go with this
man?"

And she said, "I'll go."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_58.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:59*](#Contents.html_rgen24_59)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^59^And they sent their sister Rebekah and her nurse and Abraham's
servant and his people.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_59.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:60*](#Contents.html_rgen24_60)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^60^And they blessed Rebekah and said to her, "You're our sister. Become
thousands of ten-thousands. And may your seed possess the gate of those
who hate him."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_60.jpg){height="72" width="228"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:61*](#Contents.html_rgen24_61)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^61^And Rebekah and her maids got up and rode on the camels and followed
the man, and the servant took Rebekah and went.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_61.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:62*](#Contents.html_rgen24_62)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^62^And Isaac was coming from the area of the well Lahai-roi, and he was
living in the territory of the Negeb,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_62.jpg){height="48" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:63*](#Contents.html_rgen24_63)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^63^and Isaac went to meditate in a field toward evening, and he raised
his eyes and saw, and here were camels coming.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_63.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:64*](#Contents.html_rgen24_64)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^64^And Rebekah raised her eyes and saw Isaac, and she fell from the
camel.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_64.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:65*](#Contents.html_rgen24_65)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^65^And she said to the servant, "Who is that man who's walking in the
field toward us?"

And the servant said, "He's my lord." And she took a veil and covered
herself.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_65.jpg){height="73" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:66*](#Contents.html_rgen24_66)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^66^And the servant told Isaac all the things that he had done.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_66.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 24:67*](#Contents.html_rgen24_67)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^67^And Isaac brought her to his mother Sarah's tent. And he took
Rebekah, and she became his wife, and he loved her.

And Isaac was consoled after his mother.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap24_67.jpg){height="79" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter25.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:1*](#Contents.html_rgen25_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **25** {.h2a}

^1^And Abraham went on and took a wife, and her name was Keturah,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_01.jpg){height="43" width="330"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:1. **Keturah**. The most ignored significant person in the Torah.
Rashi follows an old rabbinic idea that she is Hagar. But there is no
basis for this in the text, and other traditional commentators reject it
(Ibn Ezra, Ramban, Rashbam). Keturah is the mother of tribes located
along the route of incense trade, and her name is related to the word
for incense. Notably, the Midianites are among the children of Abraham
and Keturah, and the influence of the Midianite priest Jethro on Moses,
his son-in-law, is understood to be substantial. And the line of Levites
who are descended from Moses thus---alone among the Israelites---derive
from Abraham through both Sarah and Keturah.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:2*](#Contents.html_rgen25_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^and she gave birth to Zimran and Jokshan and Medan and Midian and
Ishbak and Shuah for him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_02.jpg){height="48" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:3*](#Contents.html_rgen25_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Jokshan fathered Sheba and Dedan. And the children of Dedan were
Ashurim and Letushim and Leummim.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_03.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:4*](#Contents.html_rgen25_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And the children of Midian: Ephah and Epher and Hanoch and Abida and
Eldaah. All these were children of Keturah.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_04.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:5*](#Contents.html_rgen25_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Abraham gave everything that he had to Isaac.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_05.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:6*](#Contents.html_rgen25_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Abraham gave gifts to the children of the concubines that Abraham
had, and he sent them away from Isaac, his son, while he was still
living: east, to the land of the East.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_06.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:7*](#Contents.html_rgen25_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And these are the days of the years of Abraham's life that he lived:
a hundred years and seventy years and five years.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:8*](#Contents.html_rgen25_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And he expired. And Abraham died at a good old age, old and full, and
was gathered to his people.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_08.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:9*](#Contents.html_rgen25_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Isaac and Ishmael, his sons, buried him at the cave of Machpelah
at the field of Ephron, son of Zohar, the Hittite, facing Mamre,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_09.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:10*](#Contents.html_rgen25_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^the field that Abraham bought from the children of Heth. Abraham was
buried there---and Sarah, his wife.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_10.jpg){height="44" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:11*](#Contents.html_rgen25_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And it was after Abraham's death, and God blessed Isaac, his son.
And Isaac lived at the well Lahai-roi.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_11.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:11. **Isaac lived at the well Lahai-roi**. He lives at the place
where Hagar was told by an angel that she would give birth to Ishmael,
Isaac's brother (Gen 16:14). Ishmael remains significant in the
background. It is as if Isaac recognizes that his miraculous birth and
his being spared from sacrifice have given him everything that otherwise
would have been Ishmael's. Living at Lahai-roi, he is reminded that
Ishmael's life, too, was divinely determined.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:12*](#Contents.html_rgen25_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And these are the records of Ishmael, son of Abraham, to whom Hagar,
the Egyptian, Sarah's maid, gave birth for Abraham:

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_12.jpg){height="49" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:13*](#Contents.html_rgen25_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And these are the names of Ishmael's sons, by their names, according
to their records: Ishmael's firstborn was Nebaioth, and Kedar and Adbeel
and Mibsam

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_13.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:14*](#Contents.html_rgen25_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^and Mishma and Dumah and Massa,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_14.jpg){height="23" width="158"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:15*](#Contents.html_rgen25_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^Hadar and Tema, Jetur, Naphish and Kedmah.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_15.jpg){height="42" width="172"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:16*](#Contents.html_rgen25_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^These are they, Ishmael's sons, and these are their names in their
settlements and in their encampments, twelve chieftains by their clans.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_16.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:17*](#Contents.html_rgen25_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And these are the years of Ishmael's life: a hundred years and
thirty years and seven years. And he expired and died and was gathered
to his people.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_17.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:18*](#Contents.html_rgen25_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And they tented from Havilah to Shur, which faces Egypt, as you come
toward Asshur. He fell facing all of his brothers.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_18.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:18. **He fell facing all of his brothers**. Meaning: his family's
places of residence were located among the territories of all their
kindred peoples (presumably Israelites, Moabites, Ammonites, Edomites).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:19*](#Contents.html_rgen25_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## R[ECORDS]{.smallcaps1} {.h2}

^19^And these are the records of Isaac, son of Abraham: Abraham had
fathered Isaac.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25.jpg){height="26" width="72"}

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_19.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:19. **these are the records of Isaac**. Isaac's life is different
from his father Abraham's and his son Jacob's. Unlike them: He never
leaves the promised land. He never fights. He has only one wife. His
name is not changed. He is pictured going to meditate. He lives the
longest. There are fewer stories about him. He is a relatively passive
figure, acted on by his father and his wife and his son. Why is he
different? Presumably because he has once lain on an altar as a
sacrifice to God. Even though the sacrifice is not consummated, his life
is now consecrated, and his life is distinct after this. The actions
that parents take regarding their children impact on their children's
lives ever after.
