------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:20*](#Contents.html_rgen25_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Isaac was forty years old when he took Rebekah, daughter of
Bethuel, the Aramean, from Paddan Aram, sister of Laban, the Aramean, to
him as a wife.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_20.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:21*](#Contents.html_rgen25_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Isaac prayed to YHWH for his wife because she was infertile, and
YHWH was prevailed upon by him, and his wife Rebekah became pregnant.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_21.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:22*](#Contents.html_rgen25_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And the children struggled inside her, and she said, "If it's like
this, why do I exist?" And she went to inquire of YHWH.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_22.jpg){height="72" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:23*](#Contents.html_rgen25_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And YHWH said to her,

::: quote1
        Two nations are in your womb,

        and two peoples will be dispersed from your insides,

        and one people will be mightier than the other people,

        and the older the younger will serve.
:::

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_23.jpg){height="70" width="302"}

![Image](images/25page_87.jpg){height="30" width="81"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:23. **YHWH said to her**. Rebekah hears the voice of God before Isaac
does. (God first speaks to Isaac in the next chapter, 26:2.) In matters
of revelation, man is not more important than woman. The *message* is
what is important. Rebekah hears first because her *need* arises first.
She is troubled because of the struggling twins in her womb.

25:23. **the older the younger will serve**. As I explained in *The
Hidden Face of God*, people have usually taken this to mean that YHWH
tells Rebekah that her younger son, Jacob, will dominate her older one,
Esau. Some have thought, therefore, that Rebekah is not really
manipulating the succession when she sends Jacob to pose as Esau.
Rather, she is simply fulfilling God's will. The decision of who is
number-one-son thus is still God's. However, this understanding is based
on a misunderstanding of the subtle, exquisitely ambiguous biblical
wording. The text does not in fact say that the elder son will serve the
younger son. In biblical Hebrew, the subject may either precede or
follow the verb, and the object likewise may either precede or follow
the verb. What that means is that sometimes it is impossible to tell
which word in a biblical verse is the subject and which is the object,
especially if the verse is in poetry. That is the case in this oracle to
Rebekah, which is in poetry. It can mean:

"the elder will serve the younger"

But it equally can mean:

"the elder, the younger will serve"

Like the Delphic oracles in Greece, this prediction contains two
opposite meanings, and thus the person who receives it---Rebekah---can
hear whatever she wants (consciously or subconsciously) to hear. It can
be understood to mean that Jacob will serve Esau or that Esau will serve
Jacob. I learned this from my senior colleague, David Noel Freedman.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:24*](#Contents.html_rgen25_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And her days to give birth were completed, and here were twins in
her womb.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_24.jpg){height="24" width="285"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:25*](#Contents.html_rgen25_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And the first came out all ruddy, like a hairy robe, and they called
his name Esau.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_25.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:26*](#Contents.html_rgen25_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And after that his brother came out, and his hand was holding Esau's
heel, and he called his name Jacob. And Isaac was sixty years old at
their birth.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_26.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:27*](#Contents.html_rgen25_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And the boys grew up, and Esau was a man who knew hunting, a man of
the field, and Jacob was a simple man, living in tents.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_27.jpg){height="51" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:27. **Esau**. Numerous attempts have been made to denigrate Esau in
midrash and even in current biblical interpretation. It is not justified
according to the text. The motive is understandable: Jacob's behavior in
the matters of the birthright and the blessing is an embarrassment. Even
small children express surprise at what this patriarch does to his
brother---and his father. The Torah itself neither denigrates Esau nor
justifies Jacob. On the contrary, one of the great qualities of the
*Tanak* is precisely that none of its heroes is perfect. Moreover, Jacob
suffers some corresponding recompense for every deception he commits, as
do Laban, Rachel, Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, and Joseph's other
brothers. (See the relevant comments below.) The lesson, therefore, is
not only that people are not perfect, but also that acts of deception
can fester in a family, and that they have consequences. Those who try
to excuse Jacob and turn Esau into a villain are unfortunately hiding
these essential lessons of the Torah.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:28*](#Contents.html_rgen25_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And Isaac loved Esau because he put game meat in his mouth, and
Rebekah loved Jacob.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_28.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:29*](#Contents.html_rgen25_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And Jacob made a stew, and Esau came from the field, and he was
exhausted.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_29.jpg){height="51" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:30*](#Contents.html_rgen25_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And Esau said to Jacob, "Will you feed me some of the red stuff,
this red stuff, because I'm exhausted." (On account of this he called
his name "Edom.")

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_30.jpg){height="76" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:31*](#Contents.html_rgen25_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And Jacob said, "Sell your birthright to me, today."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_31.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:32*](#Contents.html_rgen25_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And Esau said, "Here I'm going to die, and what use is this, that I
have a birthright?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_32.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 25:33*](#Contents.html_rgen25_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And Jacob said, "Swear to me, today."

And he swore to him and sold his birthright to Jacob. And Jacob gave
bread and lentil stew to Esau. And he ate and drank and got up and went.
And Esau disdained the birthright.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap25_33.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter26.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:1*](#Contents.html_rgen26_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **26** {.h2a}

^1^And there was a famine in the land (other than the first famine,
which was in Abraham's days), and Isaac went to Abimelek, king of the
Philistines, at Gerar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_01.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:2*](#Contents.html_rgen26_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And YHWH appeared to him and said, "Don't go down to Egypt. Reside in
the land that I say to you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_02.jpg){height="68" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:3*](#Contents.html_rgen26_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^Stay on in this land, and I'll be with you and bless you, for I'll
give all these lands to you and your seed, and I'll uphold the oath that
I swore to Abraham your father,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_03.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:4*](#Contents.html_rgen26_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^and I'll multiply your seed like the stars of the skies and give to
your seed all these lands, and all the nations of the earth will be
blessed through your seed

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

26:4. **all the nations of the earth will be blessed through your
seed**. This prediction was made to Abraham in his first communication
from God. It was repeated to him in Isaac's presence at the Aqedah. Now
God gives the same message to Isaac himself. Now that Isaac has grown up
and had children himself, the renewal of the message confirms that it is
through his descendants that this is to occur.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:5*](#Contents.html_rgen26_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^because Abraham listened to my voice and kept my watch, my
commandments, my laws, and my instructions."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_05.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:6*](#Contents.html_rgen26_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And so Isaac lived in Gerar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_06.jpg){height="19" width="151"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:7*](#Contents.html_rgen26_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And the people of the place asked about his wife, and he said, "She's
my sister," because he was afraid to say, "My wife," or else "the people
of the place will kill me for Rebekah, because she's good-looking."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_07.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:8*](#Contents.html_rgen26_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And it was when his days extended there: and Abimelek, king of the
Philistines, gazed through the window, and he saw: and here was Isaac
"fooling around" with Rebekah his wife!

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_08.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

26:8. **fooling around**. Hebrew ![Image](images/page90-1.jpg){.block
height="18" width="42"}, a pun on the name Isaac.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:9*](#Contents.html_rgen26_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Abimelek called Isaac and said, "But, here, she's your wife! And
how could you say, 'She's my sister'?"

And Isaac said to him, "Because I said, 'Or else I'll die over her.'"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_09.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:10*](#Contents.html_rgen26_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Abimelek said, "What is this you've done to us? One of the
people nearly could have lain with your wife, and you would have brought
guilt on us."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_10.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:11*](#Contents.html_rgen26_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Abimelek commanded all the people saying, "He who touches this
man or his wife will be put to *death!*"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_11.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:12*](#Contents.html_rgen26_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And Isaac planted seed in that land, and he harvested a hundredfold
in that year. And YHWH blessed him,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_12.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

26:12. **blessed him, and the man became great**. This should be read as
one connected thought even though it has been broken into two verses.
Compare Gen 24:34, where the servant says about Isaac's father Abraham,
"YHWH has blessed my lord very much, and he has become great." The point
appears to be that the becoming great is the expression of divine
blessing.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:13*](#Contents.html_rgen26_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^and the man became great, and he went on getting greater until he
was very great

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_13.jpg){height="67" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:14*](#Contents.html_rgen26_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^and had livestock of flocks and livestock of herds and a large
number of servants. And the Philistines envied him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_14.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:15*](#Contents.html_rgen26_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And the Philistines stopped up all the wells that his father's
servants had dug in his father Abraham's days, and they filled them with
dirt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_15.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:16*](#Contents.html_rgen26_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And Abimelek said to Isaac, "Go from among us, because you've become
much mightier than we are."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_16.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:17*](#Contents.html_rgen26_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And Isaac went from there and camped in the wadi of Gerar and lived
there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_17.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:18*](#Contents.html_rgen26_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Isaac went back and dug the water wells that they had dug in the
days of his father Abraham and that the Philistines had stopped up after
Abraham's death, and he called them by names, like the names that his
father had called them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_18.jpg){height="116" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:19*](#Contents.html_rgen26_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And Isaac's servants dug in the wadi and found a well of fresh water
there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_19.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:20*](#Contents.html_rgen26_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And the shepherds of Gerar quarreled with Isaac's shepherds, saying,
"The water is ours." And he called the name of the well Esek because
they tangled with him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_20.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:21*](#Contents.html_rgen26_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And they dug another well, and they quarreled over it also, and he
called its name Sitnah.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_21.jpg){height="50" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:22*](#Contents.html_rgen26_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And he moved on from there and dug another well, and they did not
quarrel over it, and he called its name Rehovot and said, "Because now
YHWH has widened for us, and we've been fruitful in the land."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_22.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:23*](#Contents.html_rgen26_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And he went up from there to Beer-sheba.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_23.jpg){height="21" width="193"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:24*](#Contents.html_rgen26_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And YHWH appeared to him that night and said, "I'm your father
Abraham's God. Don't be afraid, because I'm with you, and I'll bless you
and multiply your seed on account of Abraham, my servant."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_24.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:25*](#Contents.html_rgen26_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And he built an altar there and invoked the name YHWH. And he
pitched his tent there, and Isaac's servants dug a well there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_25.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:26*](#Contents.html_rgen26_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And Abimelek and Ahuzat, his companion, and Phicol, the commander of
his army, went to him from Gerar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_26.jpg){height="46" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:27*](#Contents.html_rgen26_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And Isaac said to them, "Why have you come to me, since you hated me
and sent me away from you?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_27.jpg){height="78" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:28*](#Contents.html_rgen26_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And they said, "We've *seen* that YHWH has been with you; and we
say, 'Let there be an oath between us, between us and you, and let us
make a covenant with you

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_28.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:29*](#Contents.html_rgen26_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^that you won't do bad toward us as we haven't touched you and as
we've done only good toward you and sent you away in peace. You are now
blessed by YHWH.'"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_29.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:30*](#Contents.html_rgen26_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And he made a feast for them, and they ate and drank.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_30.jpg){height="22" width="260"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:31*](#Contents.html_rgen26_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And they got up early in the morning and swore, each man to his
brother, and Isaac sent them away, and they went from him in peace.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_31.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:32*](#Contents.html_rgen26_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And it was in that day and Isaac's servants came and told him about
the well that they had dug and said to him, "We found water."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_32.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:33*](#Contents.html_rgen26_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And he called it Seven. On account of this the name of the city is
Beer-sheba to this day.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_33.jpg){height="51" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:34*](#Contents.html_rgen26_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And Esau was forty years old, and he took a wife: Judith, daughter
of Beeri, the Hittite, and Basemath, daughter of Elon, the Hittite.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_34.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 26:35*](#Contents.html_rgen26_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And they were a bitterness of spirit to Isaac and to Re-bekah.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap26_35.jpg){height="50" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter27.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:1*](#Contents.html_rgen27_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **27** {.h2a}

^1^And it was when Isaac was old and his eyes were too dim for seeing:
and he called Esau, his older son, and said to him, "My son."

And he said to him, "I'm here."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_01.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:2*](#Contents.html_rgen27_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And he said, "Here, I've become old. I don't know the day of my
death.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_02.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:3*](#Contents.html_rgen27_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And now, take up your implements: your quiver and your bow, and go
out to the field and hunt me some game

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_03.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/27page_92.jpg){height="18" width="75"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:4*](#Contents.html_rgen27_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^and make me the kind of delicacies that I love and bring them to me
and let me eat, so my soul will bless you before I die."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:5*](#Contents.html_rgen27_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Rebekah was listening as Isaac was speaking to Esau, his son. And
Esau went to the field to hunt game to bring.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_05.jpg){height="68" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

27:5. **to bring**. The Septuagint reads "to bring *to his father*,"
translating ![Image](images/page92-1.jpg){.block height="12" width="20"}
![Image](images/page92-2.jpg){.block height="17" width="85"}. The second
word appears to have been omitted from the Masoretic Text by the scribe
because of its similarity to the preceding word.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:6*](#Contents.html_rgen27_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Rebekah said to Jacob, her son, saying, "Here I've heard your
father speaking to Esau, your brother, saying,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_06.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:7*](#Contents.html_rgen27_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^'Bring me game and make me delicacies so I may eat, and I'll bless
you in the presence of YHWH before my death.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_07.jpg){height="90" width="356"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:8*](#Contents.html_rgen27_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And now, my son, listen to my voice, to what I'm commanding you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_08.jpg){height="22" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:9*](#Contents.html_rgen27_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^Go to the flock and take two good goat kids for me from there, and
I'll make them the kind of delicacies that your father loves.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_09.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:10*](#Contents.html_rgen27_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And bring them to your father, and he'll eat, so he'll bless you
before his death."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_10.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:11*](#Contents.html_rgen27_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Jacob said to Rebekah, his mother, "Here, Esau, my brother, is a
hairy man and I'm a smooth man.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_11.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:12*](#Contents.html_rgen27_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^Maybe my father will feel me, and I'll be like a trickster in his
eyes, and I'll bring a curse on me and not a blessing."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_12.jpg){height="91" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:13*](#Contents.html_rgen27_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And his mother said to him, "Let your curse be on me, my son; just
listen to my voice and go take them for me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_13.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:14*](#Contents.html_rgen27_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And he went and took and brought them to his mother, and his mother
made the kind of delicacies that his father loved.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_14.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:15*](#Contents.html_rgen27_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Rebekah took the finest clothes of Esau, her older son, that
were with her in the house and put them on Jacob, her younger son,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_15.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:16*](#Contents.html_rgen27_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^and put the skins of the goat kids on his hands and on the smooth
part of his neck.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_16.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:17*](#Contents.html_rgen27_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And she put the delicacies and the bread that she'd made in the hand
of Jacob, her son.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_17.jpg){height="43" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:18*](#Contents.html_rgen27_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And he came to his father.

And he said, "My father."

And he said, "I'm here. Who are you, my son?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_18.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:19*](#Contents.html_rgen27_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And Jacob said to his father, "I'm Esau, your firstborn. I did as
you spoke to me. Get up, sit, and eat some of my game so your soul will
bless me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_19.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:20*](#Contents.html_rgen27_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Isaac said to his son, "What's this? You were quick to find it,
my son."

And he said, "Because YHWH your God made it happen for me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_20.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:21*](#Contents.html_rgen27_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Isaac said to Jacob, "Come over, and I'll feel you, my son: is
this you, my son Esau, or not?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_21.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:22*](#Contents.html_rgen27_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Jacob came over to Isaac, his father, and he felt him. And he
said, "The voice is the voice of Jacob, and the hands are the hands of
Esau."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_22.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:23*](#Contents.html_rgen27_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And he did not recognize him because his hands were hairy like the
hands of Esau, his brother, and he blessed him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_23.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:24*](#Contents.html_rgen27_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And he said, "Is this you, my son Esau?"

And he said, "I am."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_24.jpg){height="22" width="295"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:25*](#Contents.html_rgen27_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And he said, "Bring it over to me and let me eat some of my son's
game so that my soul will bless you. And he brought it over to him, and
he ate; and he brought him wine, and he drank.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_25.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:26*](#Contents.html_rgen27_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And Isaac, his father, said to him, "Come over and kiss me, my son."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_26.jpg){height="22" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:27*](#Contents.html_rgen27_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And he came over and kissed him, and he smelled the aroma of his
clothes and blessed him and said, "See, my son's aroma is like the aroma
of a field that YHWH has blessed.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_27.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:28*](#Contents.html_rgen27_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And may God give you from the dew of the skies and from the fat of
the earth and much grain and wine.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_28.jpg){height="71" width="223"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

27:28. **from**. David Noel Freedman notes that there is play on words
here, as the blessings of the two sons hinge on the term *min*. In the
blessing given to Jacob he is promised access to and a rich share of the
rain and the dew, with *min* being used partitively In Esau's blessing
it is used spatially to mean that Esau will be denied access to fertile
lands and the fruits of the soil. So what was a blessing for Jacob
becomes in effect a kind of curse for Esau, and the words are
practically identical.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:29*](#Contents.html_rgen27_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^May peoples serve you and nations bow to you. Be your brothers'
superior, and may your mother's sons bow to you. May those who curse you
be cursed and those who bless you be blessed."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_29.jpg){height="70" width="275"}

![Image](images/27page_94.jpg){height="18" width="100"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:30*](#Contents.html_rgen27_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And it was as Isaac finished blessing Jacob, and it was: Jacob had
*just gone out* from the presence of Isaac, his father!---and Esau, his
brother, came from his hunting.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_30.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:31*](#Contents.html_rgen27_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And he, too, had made delicacies and brought them to his father. And
he said to his father, "Let my father get up and eat some of his son's
game so you yourself will bless me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_31.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:32*](#Contents.html_rgen27_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And Isaac, his father, said to him, "Who are you?"

And he said, "I'm your son, your firstborn, Esau."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_32.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:33*](#Contents.html_rgen27_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And Isaac trembled---a very big trembling. And he said, "Who then is
the one who hunted game and brought it to me, and I ate some of it all
before you came, and I blessed him? He will in fact be blessed."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_33.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

27:33. **a very big trembling ... a very big and bitter cry**. The
sensitivity that is shown here for the feelings of Isaac and of Esau
indicates how wrong it is to try to excuse Jacob and sully Esau in our
interpretations. The text reveals that Rebekah's plan and Jacob's
actions have caused a deep hurt. Rather than excuse them, the story will
show how these things are resolved later through maturing and
forgiveness.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:34*](#Contents.html_rgen27_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^When Esau heard his father's words he cried---a very big and bitter
cry. And he said to his father, "Bless me, also me, my father."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_34.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:35*](#Contents.html_rgen27_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And he said, "Your brother came with deception, and he took your
blessing."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_35.jpg){height="23" width="301"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:36*](#Contents.html_rgen27_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And he said, "Was his name really called Jacob! And he's usurped me
two times now. He's taken my birthright, and, here, now he's taken my
blessing." And he said, "Haven't you saved a blessing for me?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_36.jpg){height="77" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

27:36. **Jacob! And he's usurped me**. Esau makes a new play on the name
Jacob, which was derived previously from the word for "heel" (Gen
25:26). Now he derives it from a word meaning "to usurp" or "to catch."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:37*](#Contents.html_rgen27_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^And Isaac answered, and he said to Esau, "Here I've made him your
superior, and I've given all his brothers as servants, and I've endowed
him with grain and wine. And for you: where, what, will I do, my son?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_37.jpg){height="85" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:38*](#Contents.html_rgen27_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^And Esau said to his father, "Is it one blessing that you have, my
father? Bless me, also me, my father." And Esau raised his voice and
wept.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_38.jpg){height="84" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:39*](#Contents.html_rgen27_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^And Isaac, his father, answered, and he said to him, "Here, away
from the fat of the earth will be your home, and from the dew of the
skies from above.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_39.jpg){height="98" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:40*](#Contents.html_rgen27_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^And you'll live by your sword. And you'll serve your brother. And it
will be that when you get dominion you'll break his yoke from your
neck."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_40.jpg){height="53" width="311"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

27:40. **when you get dominion**. This refers to the distant future,
when Jacob's descendants, Israel, will dominate Esau's descendants,
Edom, for a time, after which Edom will become independent. See Num
24:18--19, which speaks of Israel's dominion over Edom. King David
conquers Edom, which remains under Judean control until the reign of
Jehoram, when Edom breaks free.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:41*](#Contents.html_rgen27_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^And Esau despised Jacob because of the blessing with which his
father blessed him, and Esau said in his heart, "The days of mourning
for my father will be soon, and then I'll kill Jacob, my brother."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_41.jpg){height="81" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:42*](#Contents.html_rgen27_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^And the words of Esau, her older son, were told to Rebekah, and she
sent and called to Jacob, her younger son, and said to him, "Here, Esau,
your brother, consoles himself regarding you with the idea of killing
you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_42.jpg){height="102" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:43*](#Contents.html_rgen27_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^And now, my son, listen to my voice and get up, flee to Laban, my
brother, at Haran

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_43.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:44*](#Contents.html_rgen27_44)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^44^and live with him for a number of days until your brother's fury
will turn back,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_44.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:45*](#Contents.html_rgen27_45)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^45^until your brother's anger turns back from you and he forgets what
you did to him. And I'll send and take you from there. Why should I be
bereaved of the two of you as well in one day?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_45.jpg){height="83" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 27:46*](#Contents.html_rgen27_46)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^46^And Rebekah said to Isaac, "I'm disgusted with my life because of
the daughters of Heth! If Jacob takes a wife from the daughters of Heth
like these daughters of the land, why do I have a life!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap27_46.jpg){height="58" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter28.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:1*](#Contents.html_rgen28_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **28** {.h2a}

^1^And Isaac called Jacob, and he blessed him and commanded him, and he
said to him, "You shall not take a wife from the daughters of Canaan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_01.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:2*](#Contents.html_rgen28_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^Get up. Go to Paddan Aram, to the house of Bethuel, your mother's
father, and take a wife from there, from the daughters of Laban, your
mother's brother.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_02.jpg){height="77" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:3*](#Contents.html_rgen28_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And may El Shadday bless you and make you fruitful and multiply you,
so you'll become a community of peoples,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_03.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:4*](#Contents.html_rgen28_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^and may He give you the blessing of Abraham, to you and to your seed
with you, for you to possess the land of your residences, which God gave
to Abraham."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_04.jpg){height="78" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:5*](#Contents.html_rgen28_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Isaac sent Jacob, and he went to Paddan Aram, to Laban, son of
Bethuel, the Aramean, brother of Rebekah, mother of Jacob and Esau.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_05.jpg){height="78" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:6*](#Contents.html_rgen28_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him to Paddan Aram
to take a wife for himself from there when he blessed him, and he had
commanded him, saying, "You shall not take a wife from the daughters of
Canaan,"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_06.jpg){height="102" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:6. **sent**. Reading the verb as Hebrew *Qal* ("and he sent him") as
in v. 5, rather than as *Piel* ("and he let him go") with the MT.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:7*](#Contents.html_rgen28_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^and Jacob had listened to his father and to his mother and had gone
to Paddan Aram.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_07.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:8*](#Contents.html_rgen28_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Esau saw that the daughters of Canaan were bad in his father
Isaac's eyes.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_08.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:9*](#Contents.html_rgen28_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Esau went to Ishmael and took Mahalath, daughter of Ishmael, son
of Abraham, sister of Nebaioth, in addition to his wives as a wife for
him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_09.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:10*](#Contents.html_rgen28_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## A[ND HE LEFT]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

^10^And Jacob left Beer-sheba and went to Haran.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28.jpg){height="23" width="55"}

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_10.jpg){height="22" width="278"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:11*](#Contents.html_rgen28_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And he happened upon a place and stayed the night there because the
sun was setting. And he took from the stones of the place and set it as
his headrest and lay down in that place.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_11.jpg){height="77" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:12*](#Contents.html_rgen28_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And he dreamed. And here was a ladder, set up on the earth, and its
top reaching to the skies. And here were angels of God, going up and
going down by it.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_12.jpg){height="53" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:13*](#Contents.html_rgen28_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And here was YHWH standing over him, and He said, "I am YHWH, your
father Abraham's God and Isaac's God. The land on which you're lying:
I'll give it to you and to your seed.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_13.jpg){height="77" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:14*](#Contents.html_rgen28_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And your seed will be like the dust of the earth, and you'll expand
to the west and east and north and south, and all the families on the
earth will be blessed through you and through your seed.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_14.jpg){height="77" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:14. **all the families on the earth**. The prediction that God made
to Abraham and Isaac that all humankind will be blessed through them is
now repeated to Jacob as well. The confirmation of this prediction to
each of the three patriarchs individually highlights its tremendous
importance.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:15*](#Contents.html_rgen28_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And here I am with you, and I'll watch over you everywhere that
you'll go, and I'll bring you back to this land, for I won't leave you
until I've done what I've spoken to you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_15.jpg){height="96" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:16*](#Contents.html_rgen28_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And Jacob woke from his sleep and said, "YHWH is actually in this
place, and I didn't know!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_16.jpg){height="78" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:17*](#Contents.html_rgen28_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And he was afraid, and he said, "How awesome this place is! This is
none other than God's house, and this is the gate of the skies!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_17.jpg){height="52" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:18*](#Contents.html_rgen28_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Jacob got up early in the morning and took the stone that he had
set as his headrest and set it as a pillar and poured oil on its top.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_18.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:19*](#Contents.html_rgen28_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And he called that place's name Beth-El, though in fact Luz was the
name of the city at first.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_19.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:20*](#Contents.html_rgen28_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Jacob made a vow, saying, "If God will be with me and watch over
me in this way that I'm going and give me bread to eat and clothing to
wear,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_20.jpg){height="92" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:21*](#Contents.html_rgen28_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^and I come back in peace to my father's house, then YHWH will become
my God,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_21.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 28:22*](#Contents.html_rgen28_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^and this stone that I set as a pillar will be God's house, and
everything that you'll give me I'll *tithe* to you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap28_22.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter29.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:1*](#Contents.html_rgen29_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **29** {.h2a}

^1^And Jacob lifted his feet and went to the land of the people of the
east.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_01.jpg){height="27" width="328"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:2*](#Contents.html_rgen29_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And he looked, and here was a well in the field, and here were three
flocks of sheep lying by it, because they watered the flocks from that
well, and the stone on the mouth of the well was big,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_02.jpg){height="78" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:3*](#Contents.html_rgen29_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^and all the flocks would be gathered there, and they would roll the
stone from the mouth of the well and would water the sheep and then
would put the stone back in its place on the mouth of the well.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_03.jpg){height="105" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:4*](#Contents.html_rgen29_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

4And Jacob said to them, "My brothers, where are you from?"

And they said, "We're from Haran."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_04.jpg){height="51" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:5*](#Contents.html_rgen29_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And he said to them, "Do you know Laban, son of Nahor?"

And they said, "We know."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_05.jpg){height="53" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:6*](#Contents.html_rgen29_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And he said to them, "Is he well?"

And they said, "Well. And here's Rachel, his daughter, coming with the
sheep."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_06.jpg){height="79" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:7*](#Contents.html_rgen29_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And he said, "Here, it will still be daytime for a long time, not the
time for gathering the livestock. Water the sheep and go, pasture them."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_07.jpg){height="52" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:8*](#Contents.html_rgen29_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And they said, "We can't until all the flocks will be gathered and
they'll roll the stone from the mouth of the well, and then we'll water
the sheep."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_08.jpg){height="75" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:9*](#Contents.html_rgen29_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^He was still speaking with them, and Rachel came with her father's
sheep, because she was a shepherdess.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_09.jpg){height="77" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:10*](#Contents.html_rgen29_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And it was when Jacob saw Rachel, daughter of Laban, his mother's
brother, and the sheep of Laban, his mother's brother: and he went over
and rolled the stone from the mouth of the well and watered the sheep of
Laban, his mother's brother.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_10.jpg){height="103" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:11*](#Contents.html_rgen29_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Jacob kissed Rachel and raised his voice and wept.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_11.jpg){height="75" width="379"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:12*](#Contents.html_rgen29_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And Jacob told Rachel that he was her father's kin and that he was
Rebekah's son, and she ran and told her father.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_12.jpg){height="50" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:13*](#Contents.html_rgen29_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And it was, when Laban heard the news of Jacob, his sister's son,
that he ran to him and embraced him and kissed him and brought him to
his house. And he told Laban all these things.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_13.jpg){height="52" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:14*](#Contents.html_rgen29_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Laban said to him, "You are indeed my bone and my flesh." And he
stayed with him a month.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_14.jpg){height="53" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:15*](#Contents.html_rgen29_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Laban said to Jacob, "Is it right because you're my brother that
you should work for me for free? Tell me what your pay should be."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_15.jpg){height="74" width="305"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:16*](#Contents.html_rgen29_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And Laban had two daughters. The older one's name was Leah, and the
younger one's name was Rachel.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_16.jpg){height="63" width="357"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:17*](#Contents.html_rgen29_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And Leah's eyes were tender, and Rachel had an attractive figure and
was beautiful.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_17.jpg){height="52" width="361"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:18*](#Contents.html_rgen29_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Jacob loved Rachel.

And he said, "I'll work for you seven years for Rachel, your younger
daughter."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_18.jpg){height="54" width="360"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:19*](#Contents.html_rgen29_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And Laban said, "Better for me to give her to you than for me to
give her to another man. Live with me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_19.jpg){height="75" width="361"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:20*](#Contents.html_rgen29_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Jacob worked seven years for Rachel, and they were like a few
days in his eyes because of his loving her.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_20.jpg){height="49" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:21*](#Contents.html_rgen29_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Jacob said to Laban, "Give me my wife, because my days have been
completed, and let me come to her."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_21.jpg){height="77" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:22*](#Contents.html_rgen29_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Laban gathered all the people of the place and made a feast.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_22.jpg){height="50" width="357"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:23*](#Contents.html_rgen29_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And it was in the evening, and he took Leah, his daughter, and
brought her to him. And he came to her.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_23.jpg){height="75" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:24*](#Contents.html_rgen29_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And Laban gave her his maid Zilpah---to Leah, his daughter, as a
maid.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_24.jpg){height="57" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:25*](#Contents.html_rgen29_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And it was in the morning, and here she was: Leah! And he said to
Laban, "What is this you've done to me? Didn't I work with you for
Rachel? And why have you deceived me?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_25.jpg){height="75" width="362"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:26*](#Contents.html_rgen29_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And Laban said, "It's not done like that in our place, to give the
younger one before the firstborn.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_26.jpg){height="51" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:26. **the firstborn**. Not the younger before the "older" (as many
translations mistakenly have it) but the younger before the *firstborn*.
Jacob, who appropriated his brother's birthright, now suffers because of
the birthright of his beloved's sister!

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:27*](#Contents.html_rgen29_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^Complete this week, and this one will be given to you as well---for
the work that you'll do with me: another seven years."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_27.jpg){height="77" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:28*](#Contents.html_rgen29_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And Jacob did so; and he completed this week, and he gave him
Rachel, his daughter, for a wife.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_28.jpg){height="48" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:29*](#Contents.html_rgen29_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And Laban gave his maid Bilhah to Rachel, his daughter---to her as a
maid.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_29.jpg){height="49" width="360"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:30*](#Contents.html_rgen29_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And he also came to Rachel. And he also loved Rachel more than Leah.
And he worked with him another seven years.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_30.jpg){height="53" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:31*](#Contents.html_rgen29_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And YHWH saw that Leah was hated, and He opened her womb, and Rachel
was infertile.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_31.jpg){height="69" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:32*](#Contents.html_rgen29_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And Leah became pregnant and gave birth to a son and called his name
Reuben because she said, "Because YHWH looked at my suffering, so that
now my man will love me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_32.jpg){height="94" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:32. **and \[she\] called his name Reuben**. Jacob's two wives name
their own children and those born by their maids. In other cases the
fathers name the children. What is the convention, and what are the
exceptions? The record of who names whom in Genesis is:

::: quote
Eve names Cain.

The text does not say who names Abel.

Eve and Adam are both said to have named Seth (4:25; 5:3).

Lamech names Noah.

Lot's elder daughter names Moab.

Lot's younger daughter names Ben-ammi (Ammon).

An angel tells Hagar to name Ishmael, but Abraham names him.

Abraham (obeying God) names Isaac.

"They" (meaning both Isaac and Rebekah?) name Esau.

"He" (meaning Esau himself? or Jacob?) calls Esau Edom.

"He" (meaning Isaac?) names Jacob.

Leah names Reuben, Simeon, Judah, Gad, Asher, Issachar, Zebulun, Dinah.

"He" (meaning Jacob?) names Levi.

Rachel names Dan, Naphtali, Joseph, and Ben-oni, but Jacob names him
Benjamin.

Judah names Er, Perez, and Zerah.

Judah's first wife names Onan and Shelah.

Joseph names Ephraim and Manasseh.
:::

Here are two possible explanations of this picture: (1) There is no
clear pattern here whatever, and we can learn from this that we do not
have to squeeze a great lesson out of every single distribution of
wording in the Torah. (2) We can observe that the person who does the
naming in each story is the person on whom that particular story is
primarily focused. This works in every case except the naming of Levi,
Onan, and Shelah (and arguably Cain): not 100 percent, but enough cases
to be worth noting. Thus the rivalry between Leah and Rachel is the
focus of this story, and so it should not come as a surprise that the
naming is ascribed to them.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:33*](#Contents.html_rgen29_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And she became pregnant again and gave birth to a son and said,
"Because YHWH listened because I was hated and gave me this one, too."
And she called his name Simeon.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_33.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:34*](#Contents.html_rgen29_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And she became pregnant again and gave birth to a son, and she said,
"Now, this time my man will become bound to me because I've given birth
to three sons for him." On account of this his name was called Levi.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_34.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 29:35*](#Contents.html_rgen29_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And she became pregnant again and gave birth to a son and said,
"This time I'll praise YHWH." On account of this she called his name
Judah. And she stopped giving birth.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap29_35.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter30.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:1*](#Contents.html_rgen30_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **30** {.h2a}

^1^And Rachel saw that she had not given birth for Jacob, and Rachel was
jealous of her sister, and she said to Jacob, "Give me children. And if
not I'm dying!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_01.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:2*](#Contents.html_rgen30_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Jacob's anger flared at Rachel, and he said, "Am I in place of
God, who has held back the fruit of the womb from you?!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_02.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:3*](#Contents.html_rgen30_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And she said, "Here's my maid, Bilhah. Come to her, and she'll give
birth on my knees, and I too will get a child through her."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_03.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:4*](#Contents.html_rgen30_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And she gave him Bilhah, her maid, as a wife, and Jacob came to her.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_04.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:5*](#Contents.html_rgen30_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Bilhah became pregnant and gave birth to a son for Jacob.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_05.jpg){height="26" width="235"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:6*](#Contents.html_rgen30_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Rachel said, "God has judged me and heard my prayer as well and
has given me a son." On account of this she called his name Dan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_06.jpg){height="52" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:7*](#Contents.html_rgen30_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Bilhah, Rachel's maid, became pregnant again and gave birth to a
second son for Jacob.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_07.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:8*](#Contents.html_rgen30_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Rachel said, "I've had Godlike struggles with my sister. I've
also prevailed." And she called his name Naphtali.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_08.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:9*](#Contents.html_rgen30_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Leah saw that she had stopped giving birth, and she took Zilpah,
her maid, and gave her to Jacob as a wife.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_09.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:10*](#Contents.html_rgen30_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Zilpah, Leah's maid, gave birth to a son for Jacob.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_10.jpg){height="51" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:11*](#Contents.html_rgen30_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Leah said, "With fortune!" and called his name Gad.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_11.jpg){height="26" width="302"}

![Image](images/30page_101.jpg){height="26" width="94"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:12*](#Contents.html_rgen30_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And Zilpah, Leah's maid, gave birth to a second son for Jacob.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_12.jpg){height="25" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:13*](#Contents.html_rgen30_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Leah said, "With my happiness!---because daughters will wish me
happiness," and called his name Asher.
