![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_13.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:14*](#Contents.html_rgen30_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And in the days of wheat harvest Reuben went and found mandrakes in
the field and brought them to Leah, his mother. And Rachel said to Leah,
"Give me some of your son's mandrakes."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_14.jpg){height="112" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:14. **mandrakes**. This flowering plant has been believed to have
medicinal or magical properties. It is supposed to look like a small
human being, with exaggerated sexual features, and to serve as an
aphrodisiac, with magical powers to produce conception.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:15*](#Contents.html_rgen30_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And she said to her, "Is your taking my man a small thing? And
you're taking my son's mandrakes, too?!"

And Rachel said, "Then let him lie with you tonight in return for your
son's mandrakes."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_15.jpg){height="96" width="357"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:16*](#Contents.html_rgen30_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And Jacob came from the field in the evening, and Leah went out to
him and said, "You are to come to me, because I've *hired* you with my
son's mandrakes." And he lay with her that night.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_16.jpg){height="92" width="336"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:17*](#Contents.html_rgen30_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And God listened to Leah, and she became pregnant and gave birth to
a fifth son for Jacob.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_17.jpg){height="69" width="357"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:18*](#Contents.html_rgen30_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Leah said, "God has given my hire because I gave my maid to my
man," and she called his name Issachar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_18.jpg){height="72" width="336"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:19*](#Contents.html_rgen30_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And Leah became pregnant again and gave birth to a sixth son for
Jacob.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_19.jpg){height="25" width="276"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:20*](#Contents.html_rgen30_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Leah said, "God has given me a good gift. This time my man will
value me highly, because I've given birth to six sons for him," and she
called his name Zebulun.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_20.jpg){height="72" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:21*](#Contents.html_rgen30_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And after that she gave birth to a daughter and called her name
Dinah.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_21.jpg){height="66" width="357"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:22*](#Contents.html_rgen30_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And God remembered Rachel, and God listened to her and opened her
womb,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_22.jpg){height="44" width="336"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:23*](#Contents.html_rgen30_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^and she became pregnant and gave birth to a son. And she said, "God
has taken away my humiliation"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_23.jpg){height="48" width="336"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:24*](#Contents.html_rgen30_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^and called his name Joseph, saying, "May YHWH add another son to
me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_24.jpg){height="54" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:25*](#Contents.html_rgen30_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And it was when Rachel had given birth to Joseph: and Jacob said to
Laban, "Send me away so I may go to my place and my land.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_25.jpg){height="73" width="336"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:26*](#Contents.html_rgen30_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^Give me my wives and my children for whom I've worked for you and
let me go; because *you* know my work that I've done for you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_26.jpg){height="70" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:27*](#Contents.html_rgen30_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And Laban said to him, "If I've found favor in your eyes, I've
divined that YHWH has blessed me because of you,"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_27.jpg){height="47" width="336"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:28*](#Contents.html_rgen30_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^and he said, "Designate your pay for me, and I'll give it."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_28.jpg){height="77" width="129"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:29*](#Contents.html_rgen30_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And he said to him, "*You* know how I've worked for you and how your
cattle have become with me,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_29.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:30*](#Contents.html_rgen30_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^that a little that you had before me expanded into a lot, and YHWH
blessed you wherever I set foot. And now, when shall *I* also take care
of *my* house?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_30.jpg){height="137" width="380"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:31*](#Contents.html_rgen30_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And he said, "What shall I give you?"

And Jacob said, "You won't *give* me anything---if you'll do this thing
for me: Let me go back; I'll tend and watch over your flock.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_31.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:32*](#Contents.html_rgen30_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^I'll pass among all your flock today removing from there every
speckled and spotted lamb and every brown lamb among the sheep and every
spotted and speckled one among the goats---and that will be my pay.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_32.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:33*](#Contents.html_rgen30_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And my virtue will answer for me in a future day: when you'll come
upon my pay, it will be in front of you. Any one that isn't speckled and
spotted among the goats and brown among the sheep, it's stolen with me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_33.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:34*](#Contents.html_rgen30_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And Laban said, "Here, let it be according to your word."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_34.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:35*](#Contents.html_rgen30_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And in that day he removed the he-goats that were streaked and
spotted and the she-goats that were speckled and spotted, every one that
had white in it, and every brown one among the sheep, and set them in
his sons' hand

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_35.jpg){height="102" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:36*](#Contents.html_rgen30_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^and put three days' distance between him and Jacob. And Jacob was
tending Laban's remaining sheep.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_36.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:37*](#Contents.html_rgen30_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^And Jacob took a rod of fresh poplar and one of almond tree and one
of plane tree, and he peeled white stripes in them, exposing the white
that was on the rods.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_37.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:38*](#Contents.html_rgen30_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^And he set up the rods that he had peeled in the channels in the
watering troughs at which the flock came to drink, facing the flock. And
they copulated when they came to drink.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_38.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:39*](#Contents.html_rgen30_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^And the flock mated at the rods, and the flock gave birth to
streaked, speckled, and spotted ones.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_39.jpg){height="80" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:39. **the flock mated at the rods and ... gave birth to streaked,
speckled, and spotted ones**. Jacob sets up the peeled (phallic?) sticks
where the animals mate. When they mate in front of the sticks, they have
offspring that have patterns like those on the peeled sticks. It is
unclear if this was believed to work genetically, or was thought to be a
practice of sympathetic magic, or was thought to be miraculous. Jacob
later claims to his wives that it happened through God's power, but it
is unclear from his wording if he is supposed to be modifying what has
happened as he presents it to them.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:40*](#Contents.html_rgen30_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^And Jacob separated the sheep and had the flock face the streaked
and every brown one among Laban's flock, and he set his own droves apart
and did not set them by Laban's flock.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_40.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:41*](#Contents.html_rgen30_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^And it was that whenever the fittest sheep would copulate Jacob
would put the rods in the channels before the sheep's eyes so that they
would copulate by the rods,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_41.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:42*](#Contents.html_rgen30_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^and when the sheep were feebler he would not put them, so the feeble
ones became Laban's and the fitter ones became Jacob's.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_42.jpg){height="49" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:42. **the feeble ones became Laban's and the fitter ones became
Jacob's**. By thus manipulating Laban's flock, Jacob pays Laban back for
the Rachel-Leah deception. The sign of the connection between Jacob's
deception and Laban's is when Jacob later speaks to Laban about his
"ewes" and "rams"---which plays on the names of Rachel and Leah. (See
the comment on 31:38.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 30:43*](#Contents.html_rgen30_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^And the man expanded very, very much, and he had many sheep and
female and male servants and camels and asses.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap30_43.jpg){height="69" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter31.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:1*](#Contents.html_rgen31_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **31** {.h2a}

^1^And he heard Laban's sons' words, saying, "Jacob has taken everything
that was our father's, and he has made all this wealth from what was our
father's."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_01.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:2*](#Contents.html_rgen31_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Jacob saw Laban's face; and, here, he was not like the day before
yesterday with him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_02.jpg){height="54" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:3*](#Contents.html_rgen31_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And YHWH said to Jacob, "Go back to your fathers' land and to your
birthplace, and I'll be with you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_03.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:4*](#Contents.html_rgen31_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah to the field, to his flock,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_04.jpg){height="50" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:5*](#Contents.html_rgen31_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^and said to them, "I see your father's face, that he's not like the
day before yesterday to me. And my father's God has been with me,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_05.jpg){height="74" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:6*](#Contents.html_rgen31_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^and you know that I served your father with all my might,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_06.jpg){height="51" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:7*](#Contents.html_rgen31_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^and your father has toyed with me and changed my pay ten times, and
God hasn't let him do bad with me.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_07.jpg){height="53" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:8*](#Contents.html_rgen31_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^If he would say this: 'The speckled will be your pay', then all the
flock gave birth to speckled. And if he would say this: 'The streaked
will be your pay', then all the flock gave birth to streaked.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_08.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:9*](#Contents.html_rgen31_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And God has delivered your father's livestock and given them to me.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_09.jpg){height="55" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:10*](#Contents.html_rgen31_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And it was at the time of the flock's being in heat, and I raised my
eyes and saw in a dream: and here were the he-goats that were going up
on the flock: streaked, speckled, and spotted.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_10.jpg){height="76" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:11*](#Contents.html_rgen31_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^"And an angel of God said to me in the dream, 'Jacob.'

"And I said, 'I'm here.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_11.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:12*](#Contents.html_rgen31_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^"And he said, 'Raise your eyes and see all the he-goats that are
going up on the flock: streaked, speckled, and spotted. Because I've
seen everything that Laban is doing to you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_12.jpg){height="76" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:13*](#Contents.html_rgen31_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^I am the God at Beth-El, where you anointed a pillar, where you made
a vow to me. Now get up, go from this land, and go back to the land of
your birth.'"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_13.jpg){height="101" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:13. **the God at Beth-El**. The Hebrew construction is unclear. The
Septuagint reads: "I am the God who appeared to you at Beth-El," which
is presumably what the Hebrew means.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:14*](#Contents.html_rgen31_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Rachel and Leah answered, and they said to him, "Do we still
have a portion and legacy in our father's house?

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_14.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:15*](#Contents.html_rgen31_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^Aren't we thought of as foreigners by him, because he sold us and
has *eaten up* our money as well?!

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_15.jpg){height="93" width="380"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:16*](#Contents.html_rgen31_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^Because all the wealth that God has delivered from our father: it's
ours and our children's. And now, do everything that that God has said
to you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_16.jpg){height="75" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:17*](#Contents.html_rgen31_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And Jacob got up and carried his children and his wives on the
camels.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_17.jpg){height="70" width="363"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:18*](#Contents.html_rgen31_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And he drove all his cattle and all his property that he had
acquired, the cattle that were in his possession that he had acquired in
Paddan Aram, to come to Isaac, his father, at the land of Canaan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_18.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:19*](#Contents.html_rgen31_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And Laban had gone to shear his flock. And Rachel stole the teraphim
that her father had.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_19.jpg){height="81" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:19. **teraphim**. These are icons (Septuagint has *eidolon*, meaning
"image"), which may relate to ancestor worship. Ancestor veneration was
common in Israel at least until the reign of King Hezekiah (c. 700
[B.C.E.]{.smallcaps}). King Josiah burns teraphim along with other items
related to communicating with the dead
(![Image](images/page105-1.jpg){.block height="12" width="41"} and
![Image](images/page105-2.jpg){.block height="11" width="52"}) in 2
Kings 23:24.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:20*](#Contents.html_rgen31_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Jacob stole the heart of Laban, the Aramean, by not telling him
that he was fleeing.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_20.jpg){height="49" width="277"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:20. **stole the heart of Laban ... by not telling him**. Note the
pun, a string of plays on the consonants of the name Laban:
![Image](images/page105-3.jpg){.block height="20" width="186"}. Some
puns in the Torah convey subtle or ironic messages. This pun may have no
purpose other than the artistry for its own sake, or it may suggest a
comeuppance: the play on language here describes a deception of Laban, a
person who uses language to deceive others.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:21*](#Contents.html_rgen31_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And he fled, he and everyone he had, and he got up and crossed the
river and set his face toward the mountain of Gilead.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_21.jpg){height="94" width="362"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:22*](#Contents.html_rgen31_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And it was told to Laban on the third day that Jacob had fled.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_22.jpg){height="53" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:22. **And it was told to Laban ... that Jacob had fled**. **And he
took ... and pursued ... and caught up**. Compare the words describing
Egypt's pursuit of Israel to the Red Sea: "And it was told to the king
of Egypt that the people had fled ... and he took ... and he pursued...
. And they caught up" (Exod 14:5--9). The Laban episode prefigures the
flight from Egypt, and it hints once again that the merit of the
patriarchs lies in the background when Israel is saved from dangers.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:23*](#Contents.html_rgen31_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And he took his brothers with him and pursued him seven days'
journey and caught up to him in the mountain of Gilead.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_23.jpg){height="51" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:24*](#Contents.html_rgen31_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And God came to Laban, the Aramean, in a night dream and said to
him, "Watch yourself in case you speak with Jacob: from good to bad."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_24.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:25*](#Contents.html_rgen31_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And Laban caught up to Jacob, and Jacob had set up his tent in the
mountain, and Laban set up with his brothers in the mountain of Gilead.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_25.jpg){height="55" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:26*](#Contents.html_rgen31_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And Laban said to Jacob, "What have you done, that you've stolen my
heart and driven off my daughters like prisoners by the sword?

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_26.jpg){height="76" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:27*](#Contents.html_rgen31_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^Why did you hide so as to flee, and you stole from me, and you
didn't tell me? And I would have sent you off with happiness and with
songs and with a drum and with a lyre.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_27.jpg){height="75" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:28*](#Contents.html_rgen31_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And you didn't permit me to kiss my sons and my daughters. Now
you've been foolish to do this.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_28.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:28. **to do this**. The Hebrew ![Image](images/page106-1.jpg){.block
height="13" width="27"} puns on the name of Esau
(*![Image](images/page106-2.jpg){.block height="13" width="27"}*), who
returns to the story in the next chapter (32:4).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:29*](#Contents.html_rgen31_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^The god at my hand has the means to do bad to you. But your father's
God said to me yesterday saying, 'Watch yourself from speaking with
Jacob: from good to bad.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_29.jpg){height="72" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:29. **your father's God**. Hebrew
![Image](images/page106-3.jpg){.block height="13" width="46"}. "Your" is
a plural here, and "father" is singular. The text as it is does not
appear to make sense. To whom would the plural "your" refer? Is it Jacob
and his wives? But then "your father" would be Laban himself. Is it
Jacob and his sons? But then "your father" would be Isaac. But why would
Laban formulate it this way, since he has the same relationship as Isaac
with Jacob's sons? We would rather have expected Hebrew
![Image](images/page106-4.jpg){.block height="18" width="46"}, "the God
of your fathers." This may possibly be one of the very few grammatical
errors to be transmitted in the biblical text. Presumably, the wrong
element of the word has been pluralized. Or: Perhaps the error should be
understood to be *Laban's!* As he tries to make a dignified speech,
claiming the moral high ground in this matter, calling Jacob the foolish
one, he undermines his pretensions by making a clumsy mistake in the
middle of his sentence.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:30*](#Contents.html_rgen31_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And now, you *went*, because you *longed* for your father's house.
Why did you steal my gods?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_30.jpg){height="71" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:31*](#Contents.html_rgen31_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And Jacob answered, and he said to Laban, "Because I was afraid.
Because I said, 'In case you'll seize your daughters from me.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_31.jpg){height="73" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:32*](#Contents.html_rgen31_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^Let the one with whom you'll find your gods not live. In front of
our brothers, recognize and take what of yours is with me." And Jacob
did not know that Rachel had stolen them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_32.jpg){height="70" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:32. **Let the one with whom you'll find your gods not live**. By
saying this, Jacob unwittingly puts a curse on Rachel, who has the
teraphim.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:33*](#Contents.html_rgen31_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And Laban came in Jacob's tent and Leah's tent and the two maids'
tent, and he did not find them. And he came out from Leah's tent and
came in Rachel's tent.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_33.jpg){height="75" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:34*](#Contents.html_rgen31_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And Rachel had taken the teraphim and put them in the camel's saddle
and sat on them. And Laban felt around the whole tent and did not find
them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_34.jpg){height="76" width="343"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:34. **felt around**. This is the same verb that Jacob used in the
context of deceiving his father in order to get Esau's blessing: "Maybe
my father will *feel* me." The treatment he receives from his wives'
father here is the first hint that his deception of his own father still
remains unsettled. Recompense for that deception is coming (Genesis 37).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:35*](#Contents.html_rgen31_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And she said to her father, "Let it not offend in my lord's eyes
that I'm not able to get up before you, because I have the way of
women." And he searched and did not find the teraphim.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_35.jpg){height="102" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:35. **I have the way of women**. Rachel tells her father that she is
menstruating, which keeps him away, so he does not search where she is
sitting. But she is deceiving him. She cannot in fact be menstruating,
because we find out below that she must be pregnant at this time. Her
deception is a recompense for Laban's having substituted Leah for her
and made her share her husband with her sister.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:36*](#Contents.html_rgen31_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And Jacob was angered, and he quarreled with Laban, and Jacob
answered, and he said to Laban, "What is my offense? What is my sin,
that you *blazed* after me,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_36.jpg){height="77" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:37*](#Contents.html_rgen31_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^that you *felt around* all my belongings? What did you find out of
all of your house's belongings? Set it here, in front of my brothers and
your brothers, and let them judge between the two of us.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_37.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:38*](#Contents.html_rgen31_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^This twenty years I've been with you, your ewes and your she-goats
haven't lost their offspring, and I haven't eaten your flock's rams.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_38.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:38. **ewes, rams**. In Hebrew, a ewe is a
*r![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}k![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14" width="6"}l*,
i.e., a "Rachel." And a ram is an '*ayil*, which is a play on "Leah."
Jacob's words to Laban thus have a double meaning. He has protected
Laban's animals and has done right by his daughters as well. Moreover,
they give Laban a hint that Jacob has paid him back for the Rachel-Leah
deception---just as Laban's words to Jacob ("We don't give the younger
before the *firstborn*") once gave Jacob a hint that he was being paid
back for getting his brother's birthright.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:39*](#Contents.html_rgen31_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^I haven't brought you one torn up. I would miss it: you would ask it
from my hand, be it stolen by day or stolen by night.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_39.jpg){height="49" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:40*](#Contents.html_rgen31_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^I was ... In the daytime heat ate me up, and ice in the night. And
my sleep fled from my eyes.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_40.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:40. **I was ... In the daytime heat**. The first words of the verse,
"I was," have no referent or continuation in the rest of the sentence.
Commentators and translators have taken it to mean "Thus I was: in the
daytime heat ate me up," etc. But perhaps the confusion that results
from this break in the sentence is precisely the point. Jacob is
speaking up after twenty years. He is rattling off a list of his
complaints as fast and as strongly as he can. And, as people often do in
such bursts of anger, he starts a point and then interrupts himself and
says something else that occurs to him. Indeed, it is at this juncture
that Jacob changes directions. He has been listing his merits; now he
suddenly switches and points out the abuses that he has been suffering.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:41*](#Contents.html_rgen31_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^This twenty years I've had in your house: I worked for you fourteen
years for your two daughters and six years for your flock, and you
changed my pay ten times.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_41.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:42*](#Contents.html_rgen31_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^If I hadn't had my father's God, the God of Abraham and Awe of
Isaac, by now you would have sent me away empty-handed. God saw my
degradation and my hands' exhaustion, and He pointed it out last night."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_42.jpg){height="101" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:43*](#Contents.html_rgen31_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^And Laban answered, and he said to Jacob, "The daughters are my
daughters, and the sons are my sons, and the flock is my flock, and
everything that you see: it's mine. But what shall I do to my daughters,
to these, today, or to their children to whom they've given birth?

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_43.jpg){height="99" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:44*](#Contents.html_rgen31_44)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^44^So now, come and let's make a covenant, I and you, and let it be a
witness between me and you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_44.jpg){height="49" width="293"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:45*](#Contents.html_rgen31_45)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^45^And Jacob took a stone and set it up as a pillar,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_45.jpg){height="47" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:46*](#Contents.html_rgen31_46)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^46^and Jacob said to his brothers, "Collect stones." And they took
stones and made a pile and ate there at the pile.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_46.jpg){height="79" width="351"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:47*](#Contents.html_rgen31_47)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^47^And Laban called it *y![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block
height="12" width="7"}gar s![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block
height="11" width="8"}h![Image](images/a1.jpg){.block height="12"
width="8"}d![Image](images/u.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}ta*', and
Jacob called it *gal-'![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14"
width="6"}d*.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_47.jpg){height="52" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:48*](#Contents.html_rgen31_48)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^48^And Laban said, "This pile is a witness between me and you today."
On account of this he called its name Gal-Ed.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_48.jpg){height="49" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:49*](#Contents.html_rgen31_49)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

(^49^And Mizpah, because he said, "May YHWH observe between me and you
when one is hidden from the other.")

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_49.jpg){height="74" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:50*](#Contents.html_rgen31_50)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^50^"If you degrade my daughters and if you take wives in addition to my
daughters, no *man* is with us. See: *God* is witness between me and
you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_50.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:51*](#Contents.html_rgen31_51)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^51^And Laban said to Jacob, "Here is this pile, and here is the pillar
that I've cast between me and you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_51.jpg){height="69" width="362"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:52*](#Contents.html_rgen31_52)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^52^This pile is a witness, and the pillar is a witness that I won't
cross this pile to you and you won't cross this pile and this pillar to
me for bad.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_52.jpg){height="102" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:53*](#Contents.html_rgen31_53)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^53^Let Abraham's God and Nahor's gods, their father's gods, judge
between us."

And Jacob swore by the Awe of his father, Isaac.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_53.jpg){height="95" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:53. **Abraham's God and Nahor's gods, their father's gods**. Since
the Hebrew word for God, *'![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block
height="12" width="7"}l![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11"
width="7"}hîm*, can be singular or plural, and since there is no
capitalization to distinguish between references to the God of the
patriarchs and the pagan gods, Laban's meaning is uncertain.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 31:54*](#Contents.html_rgen31_54)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^54^And Jacob made a sacrifice in the mountain and called his brothers
to eat bread, and they ate bread and spent the night in the mountain.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap31_54.jpg){height="78" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter32.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:1*](#Contents.html_rgen32_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **32** {.h2a}

^1^And Laban got up early in the morning and kissed his sons and his
daughters and blessed them and went. And Laban went back to his place,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_01.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:2*](#Contents.html_rgen32_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^and Jacob went his way. And angels of God came upon him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_02.jpg){height="25" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:3*](#Contents.html_rgen32_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Jacob said when he saw them, "This is a camp of God," and he
called that place's name Mahanaim.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_03.jpg){height="68" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:4*](#Contents.html_rgen32_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## A[ND HE SENT]{.smallcaps1} {.h2}

^4^And Jacob sent messengers ahead of him to Esau, his brother, to the
land of Seir, the territory of Edom,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32.jpg){height="28" width="74"}

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_04.jpg){height="44" width="311"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:5*](#Contents.html_rgen32_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^and commanded them saying, "You shall say this: 'To my lord, to Esau,
your servant Jacob said this, "I've stayed with Laban and delayed until
now,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_05.jpg){height="76" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

32:5. **I've stayed with Laban**. Rashi conveys a tradition that this
means: "I lived temporarily with him, but I learned nothing from his bad
ways." But Jacob does in fact learn from Laban. The deceiver meets a
deceiver. He learns how it feels to *receive* an injustice, not just how
it feels to dish it out. He learns that it hurts. It is a step in his
development as a man. And it apparently brings about the honorable way
in which he behaves toward Esau when he returns.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:6*](#Contents.html_rgen32_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^and ox and ass and sheep and male and female servant have become
mine. And I'm sending to tell my lord so as to find favor in your
eyes."'"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_06.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:7*](#Contents.html_rgen32_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And the messengers came back to Jacob saying, "We came to your
brother, to Esau, and also he's coming to you---and four hundred men
with him."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_07.jpg){height="97" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:8*](#Contents.html_rgen32_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Jacob was very afraid, and he had anguish. And he divided the
people who were with him and the sheep and the oxen and the camels into
two camps.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_08.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:9*](#Contents.html_rgen32_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And he said, "If Esau will come to one camp and strike it, then the
camp that is left will survive."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_09.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:10*](#Contents.html_rgen32_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Jacob said, "God of my father Abraham and God of my father
Isaac, YHWH, who said to me, 'Go back to your land and to your
birthplace, and I'll deal well with you,'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_10.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:11*](#Contents.html_rgen32_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^I'm not worthy of all the kindnesses and all the faithfulness that
you've done with your servant, because I crossed this Jordan with just
my rod, and now I've become two camps.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_11.jpg){height="99" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:12*](#Contents.html_rgen32_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^Save me from my brother's hand, from Esau's hand, because I fear
him, in case he'll come and strike me, mother with children.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_12.jpg){height="49" width="343"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:13*](#Contents.html_rgen32_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And *you' ve* said, 'I'll do *well* with you, and I'll make your
seed like the sand of the sea, that it won't be countable because of its
great number.'"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_13.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:14*](#Contents.html_rgen32_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And he spent that night there.

And he took an offering for Esau, his brother, from what had come into
his hand:

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_14.jpg){height="51" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:15*](#Contents.html_rgen32_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^two hundred she-goats and twenty he-goats, two hundred ewes and
twenty rams,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_15.jpg){height="52" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:16*](#Contents.html_rgen32_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^thirty nursing camels and their offspring, forty cows and ten bulls,
twenty she-asses and ten he-asses.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_16.jpg){height="73" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:17*](#Contents.html_rgen32_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And he placed them in his servants' hands, each herd by itself, and
he said to his servants, "Pass on in front of me, and keep a distance
between each herd and the next."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_17.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:18*](#Contents.html_rgen32_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And he commanded the first, saying, "When my brother, Esau, meets
you and asks you, saying, 'To whom do you belong, and where are you
going, and to whom do these in front of you belong?'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_18.jpg){height="77" width="351"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:19*](#Contents.html_rgen32_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^then you'll say, 'To your servant, to Jacob. It's an offering sent
to my lord, to Esau. And here he is behind us as well.'"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_19.jpg){height="77" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:20*](#Contents.html_rgen32_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And he also commanded the second, also the third, also all of those
who were going behind the herds, saying, "You'll speak this way to Esau
when you find him,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_20.jpg){height="74" width="348"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:21*](#Contents.html_rgen32_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^and you'll say, 'Here is your servant, Jacob, behind us as well.'"
Because he said, "Let me appease his face with the offering that's going
in front of me, and after that I'll see his face; maybe he'll raise my
face."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_21.jpg){height="101" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

32:21. **his face ... in front of me ... I'll see his face ... my face
... ahead of him**. The word "face" recurs five times in this line and
all through these two chapters (as does the word "raise"). It also is
part of the term that is translated as the preposition "in front of" or
"ahead of" (in Hebrew, *l![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}p![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}n![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}y*). The repetition conveys the force of this juncture in
Jacob's life. He must face his past. He must face his brother, whom he
wronged. And in the middle of the account of his facing his brother will
come the account of his most immediate contact with God in his life, his
struggle after which he will say, "I've seen God face-to-face." And the
two encounters, first with his God and then with his brother, will then
be brought together as he says to Esau, "I've seen your face---like
seeing God's face!" (33:10).

This is also ironic because Jacob left twenty years earlier in the wake
of his deception, which worked because his weak-eyed father could not
see his face!

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:22*](#Contents.html_rgen32_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And the offering passed ahead of him. And he spent that night in the
camp.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_22.jpg){height="52" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:23*](#Contents.html_rgen32_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And he got up in that night and took his two wives and his two maids
and his eleven boys and crossed the Jabbok ford.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_23.jpg){height="95" width="373"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

32:23. **his eleven boys**. His daughter, Dinah, is not mentioned.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:24*](#Contents.html_rgen32_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And he took them and had them cross the wadi, and he had everything
that was his cross.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_24.jpg){height="71" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:25*](#Contents.html_rgen32_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And Jacob was left by himself.

And a man wrestled with him until the dawn's rising.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_25.jpg){height="48" width="194"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

32:25. **a man wrestled with him**. With whom does he wrestle? It says
"a man." But he is able against the man, and later the man names him
*yi![Image](images/s1.jpg){.block height="11"
width="7"}r![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}-'![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14" width="6"}l*,
which is interpreted to mean "struggles with God," and says to him,
"You've struggled with God and with people and were able." And Jacob
names the place Peni-El, meaning "face of God," and says, "I've seen God
face-to-face." This all indicates that he has wrestled with God in human
form. But the prophet Hosea refers to this moment and says, "He fought
with God, and he fought with an angel and was able" (Hos 12:5--6). This
is consistent with the description of angels that I gave in the matter
of the three people who visit Abraham (18:3). Angels are material
expressions of God's presence (emanations from the Godhead, hypostases).
When they are in front of a human, they look like a "man," like
"people." That is why Hosea can say "He fought with God" and then say in
poetic parallel about the same event "he fought with an angel."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:26*](#Contents.html_rgen32_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And he saw that he was not able against him, and he touched the
inside of his thigh, and the inside of Jacob's thigh was dislocated
during his wrestling with him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_26.jpg){height="78" width="351"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

32:26. **dislocated**. This is the term that is used in Num 25:4 for a
punishment that is inflicted on the Israelites following a heresy at
Baal Peor. Its meaning is uncertain both there and here. There it may
mean to hang or impale, in which case here it might mean that the thigh
bone is hanging out of joint.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:27*](#Contents.html_rgen32_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And he said, "Let me go, because the dawn has risen."

And he said, "I won't let you go unless you bless me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_27.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

32:27. **I won't let you go unless you bless me**. Jacob *demands* a
blessing. Is it usual to demand a blessing from someone with whom we
have been fighting?! The struggle with God takes such an exertion of
one's strength, and takes such a toll, that the human who does it has to
live with the wounds thereafter (Jacob limps after this struggle). And
so he is in need of blessing---and has earned it.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:28*](#Contents.html_rgen32_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And he said to him, "What is your name?" And he said, "Jacob."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_28.jpg){height="51" width="350"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:29*](#Contents.html_rgen32_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And he said, "Your name won't be said 'Jacob' anymore but 'Israel,'
because you've struggled with God and with people and were able."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_29.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

32:29. **Israel**. There is little character development in Adam, Eve,
Noah, Abraham, Sarah, Isaac, or Rebekah, all of whom remain basically
constant figures through the stories about them. But Jacob changes, and
the matter of deception is intimately related to that development. As
Esau points out, Jacob's very name connotes deception: to catch. And
Jacob starts out as a manipulator. But Jacob is changed after his
experiences in Mesopotamia. He has been the deceiver and the deceived.
He has hurt and been hurt. He is now a husband and a father, a man who
has struggled and prospered. For the rest of the story he is no longer
pictured as a man of action but, more often, as a relatively passive
man, like his father Isaac, seeking to appease his brother, avoiding
strife and risk. And precisely at the juncture that marks this change in
Jacob's character he has his encounter with God at Penuel. God blesses
him in a remarkable etiology/etymology: "Your name shall no longer be
called Jacob, but Israel *\[yi![Image](images/s1.jpg){.block height="11"
width="7"}r![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}-'![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14" width="6"}l*,
understood here to mean 'he struggles with God'\], because you've
struggled with God and with people and were able. Is this divine
encounter the signpost of the change in Jacob's character, or the cause?
Either way, as his character changes, and he ceases to be the deceiver,
just then he sheds the name Jacob (the one who catches) and becomes
instead Israel (the one who struggles with God).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:30*](#Contents.html_rgen32_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And Jacob asked, and he said, "Tell your name."

And he said, "Why is this that you ask my name?" And he blessed him
there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_30.jpg){height="54" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:31*](#Contents.html_rgen32_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And Jacob called the place's name Peni-El, "because I've seen God
face-to-face, and my life has been delivered."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_31.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:32*](#Contents.html_rgen32_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And the sun rose on him as he passed Penuel, and he was faltering on
his thigh.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_32.jpg){height="75" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 32:33*](#Contents.html_rgen32_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^On account of this the children of Israel to this day will not eat
the tendon of the vein that is on the inside of the thigh, because he
touched the inside of Jacob's thigh, the tendon of the vein.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap32_33.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter33.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:1*](#Contents.html_rgen33_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **33** {.h2a}

^1^And Jacob raised his eyes and looked, and here was Esau coming, and
four hundred men with him. And he divided the children among Leah and
Rachel and the two maids;

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_01.jpg){height="94" width="357"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

33:1. **Jacob**. Abraham and Sarah have their names changed permanently,
but Jacob is still called Jacob many times after his name is changed to
Israel. Rabbi Simcha Weis-er teaches that this shows that "Israel"
(struggle with God) is something to be attained. It is a long process in
one's life.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:2*](#Contents.html_rgen33_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^and he placed the maids and their children first, and Leah and her
children following, and Rachel and Joseph following,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_02.jpg){height="102" width="357"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:3*](#Contents.html_rgen33_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^and he passed in front of them. And he bowed to the ground seven
times until he came up to his brother.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_03.jpg){height="102" width="357"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:4*](#Contents.html_rgen33_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Esau ran to him and embraced him and fell on his neck and kissed
him. And they wept.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_04.jpg){height="66" width="357"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

33:4. **fell on his neck**. Jacob deceived his father and displaced Esau
by putting skins "on the smooth part of his *neck*" (27:16). Then there
was a hint that Esau would be compensated for his loss one day by the
use of the metaphor that "you'll break his yoke from your *neck*"
(27:40). And now, the reconciliation between the twins is conveyed as
Esau runs and embraces Jacob and "fell on his *neck*."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:5*](#Contents.html_rgen33_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And he raised his eyes and saw the women and the children, and he
said, "Who are these whom you have?"

And he said, "The children with whom God has graced your servant."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_05.jpg){height="87" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:6*](#Contents.html_rgen33_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And the maids came over, they and their children, and bowed.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_06.jpg){height="36" width="307"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:7*](#Contents.html_rgen33_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Leah and her children, too, came over, and they bowed. And then
Joseph and Rachel came over, and they bowed.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_07.jpg){height="83" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

33:7. **Joseph and Rachel came over**. Leah, Bilhah, and Zilpah are
mentioned before their children, but Joseph is mentioned before Rachel,
yielding a picture of him approaching ahead of her. In his favor, we
might take this as a protective gesture toward his mother.
Alternatively, we might take this as consistent with the next picture we
shall have of his early years: seeing himself in a special status
relative to his family.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:8*](#Contents.html_rgen33_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And he said, "Who is all this camp of yours that I met?"

And he said, "To find favor in my lord's eyes."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_08.jpg){height="62" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:9*](#Contents.html_rgen33_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Esau said, "I have a great deal, my brother. Let what's yours be
yours."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_09.jpg){height="41" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

33:9. **Let what's yours be yours**. Esau graciously refuses Jacob's
gift. Then Jacob presses him to accept it. Then Esau takes it. As in the
episode of Abraham's purchase of the tomb for Sarah, and Lot's offering
his daughters to the people of Sodom, Jacob and Esau are going through
the classic Near Eastern conventions, which continue to this day.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:10*](#Contents.html_rgen33_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Jacob said, "Don't. If I've found favor in your eyes, then
you'll take my offering from my hand, because on account of this I've
seen your face---like seeing God's face!---and you've accepted me.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_10.jpg){height="114" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

33:10. **I've seen your face---like seeing God's face**. Esau has no
idea just how literally this is meant. Jacob had prayed for help because
he was afraid of Esau. Then he had the encounter after which he said,
"I've seen God face-to-face." And this, presumably, enabled him to face
his brother. Esau may take Jacob's words as a great compliment, but they
actually convey that, after facing God, one is certainly able to face
any human.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:11*](#Contents.html_rgen33_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^Take my blessing that's been brought to you, because God has been
gracious to me and because I have everything." And he pressed him, and
he took it.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_11.jpg){height="65" width="357"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

33:11. **Take my blessing**. It was Jacob's taking of Esau's blessing
that led to the brothers' separation. His description now of what he is
offering as "my blessing" suggests that it is an act of compensation for
what he did to Esau twenty years earlier. Unlike biblical interpreters
who try to defend Jacob's earlier actions, Jacob himself is pictured as
(1) not trying to make any excuses, and (2) trying to make amends.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:12*](#Contents.html_rgen33_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And he said, "Let's travel, and let's go. And let me go alongside
you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_12.jpg){height="36" width="289"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:13*](#Contents.html_rgen33_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And he said to him, "My lord knows that the children are weak and
the nursing sheep and oxen are with me, and they'll drive them one day
and all the sheep will die.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_13.jpg){height="83" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:14*](#Contents.html_rgen33_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^Let my lord pass on in front of his servant; and I, let me move
along at my pace required by the task that's before me and required by
the children until I come to my lord at Seir."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_14.jpg){height="83" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:15*](#Contents.html_rgen33_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Esau said, "Let me set with you some of the people who are with
me."

And he said, "Why have I found favor in my lord's eyes?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_15.jpg){height="86" width="355"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

33:15. **Why have I found favor in my lord's eyes?** Jacob seems to be
saying, "No, no, I don't deserve this kindness from you," which appears
to be an extremely polite way of declining Esau's offer. Understandably,
this offer to post some men with Jacob is not attractive to him: Jacob
has just told Esau that he will meet him in Seir, but in fact the next
two verses report that Esau goes back to Seir but Jacob goes to Sukkot!

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:16*](#Contents.html_rgen33_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And Esau went back on his way to Seir that day,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_16.jpg){height="41" width="290"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:17*](#Contents.html_rgen33_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^and Jacob traveled to Sukkot. And he built a house for himself and
made booths for his cattle. On account of this he called the place's
name Sukkot.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_17.jpg){height="88" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:18*](#Contents.html_rgen33_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Jacob came, safe, to the city of Shechem, which was in the land
of Canaan, when he was coming from Paddan Aram, and camped in front of
the city.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_18.jpg){height="87" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:19*](#Contents.html_rgen33_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And he bought the section of field in which he pitched his tent from
the hand of the sons of Hamor, father of Shechem, for a hundred qesita.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_19.jpg){height="60" width="357"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 33:20*](#Contents.html_rgen33_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And he set up an altar there and called it "El, God of Israel."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap33_20.jpg){height="60" width="357"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter34.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:1*](#Contents.html_rgen34_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **34** {.h2a}

^1^And Dinah, Leah's daughter, whom she had borne to Jacob, went out to
see the daughters of the land.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_01.jpg){height="73" width="360"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:2*](#Contents.html_rgen34_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Shechem, son of Hamor, the Hivite, the chieftain of the land, saw
her. And he took her and lay with her and degraded her.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_02.jpg){height="73" width="360"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

34:2. **he took her and lay with her and degraded her**. This is
commonly treated as a story of a rape. Some Bibles even put a heading
over it: "The Rape of Dinah." But, the nature of the act is not in fact
clear from this wording. The three key terms here (took, lay, degraded)
appear to suggest force, but all three are used without such a meaning
in the Torah's laws concerning marriage and rape. To "take"
(*l![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}qah*) a
woman is used even to mean marriage (Deut 22:13); and to "lie with"
(*š![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}kab*) and
to "degrade" (*'inn![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}h*) are both used in a case that is specifically ruled *not*
to be a rape (Deut 22:23--24). The following case there adds the words
"he took hold of her" (22:25), and that case is ruled to be a rape. Also
the story of Amnon and Tamar (2 Samuel 13) is comparable to the Dinah
story in a notable number of details and terms. In that story there is
unquestionably a rape, and it uses the terms "lay with her" and
"degraded her," but it also adds "he took hold of her" (13:11--12,14).
That determining verb in Deuteronomy and in the Tamar story is not
present here in the Dinah story. It may be a rape, but we cannot be
sure. What we can say, at minimum, is that whether it is a rape, a
seduction, or even consensual intercourse, Shechem's act, taking place
before the request for marriage, is regarded as disgraceful by Dinah's
family. This leads to Simeon's and Levi's violent deception, and this in
turn has consequences ultimately for the destiny of the family (see the
next comment).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:3*](#Contents.html_rgen34_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And his soul clung to Dinah, Jacob's daughter, and he loved the girl
and spoke on the girl's heart.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_03.jpg){height="60" width="356"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:4*](#Contents.html_rgen34_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Shechem said to Hamor, his father, "Get me this girl for a wife."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_04.jpg){height="57" width="355"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:5*](#Contents.html_rgen34_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Jacob heard that he had defiled Dinah, his daughter; and his sons
were with his cattle in the field, and Jacob kept quiet until they came.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_05.jpg){height="72" width="356"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:6*](#Contents.html_rgen34_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Hamor, Shechem's father, went out to Jacob to speak with him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_06.jpg){height="32" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:7*](#Contents.html_rgen34_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Jacob's sons came from the field when they heard, and the men
were pained, and they were very furious, for he had done a foolhardy
thing among Israel, to lie with Jacob's daughter, and such a thing is
not done.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_07.jpg){height="117" width="353"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------
