[*Genesis 34:8*](#Contents.html_rgen34_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Hamor spoke with them, saying, "Shechem---my son---his soul longs
for your daughter. Give her to him as a wife,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_08.jpg){height="87" width="353"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:9*](#Contents.html_rgen34_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^and marry with us; give your daughters to us and take our daughters
to you,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_09.jpg){height="64" width="353"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:10*](#Contents.html_rgen34_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^and live with us; and the land will be before you: live and go
around in it and take possession in it."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_10.jpg){height="64" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:11*](#Contents.html_rgen34_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Shechem said to her father and to her brothers, "Let me find
favor in your eyes, and I'll give whatever you say to me.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_11.jpg){height="64" width="353"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:12*](#Contents.html_rgen34_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^Make a bride-price and gift on me very great, and let me give
whatever you say to me, and give me the girl as a wife."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_12.jpg){height="89" width="355"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:13*](#Contents.html_rgen34_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Jacob's sons answered Shechem and Hamor, his father, with
deception as they spoke because he had defiled Dinah, their sister.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_13.jpg){height="96" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:14*](#Contents.html_rgen34_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And they said to them, "We aren't able to do this thing, to give our
sister to a man who has a foreskin, because that's a disgrace to us.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_14.jpg){height="85" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:15*](#Contents.html_rgen34_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^Only this way will we consent to you: if you'll be like us, every
male among you to be circumcised.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_15.jpg){height="48" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:16*](#Contents.html_rgen34_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And we'll give our daughters to you and take your daughters to us,
and we'll live with you, and we'll become one people.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_16.jpg){height="80" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:17*](#Contents.html_rgen34_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And if you won't listen to us, to be circumcised, then we'll take
our daughter and go."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_17.jpg){height="62" width="355"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:18*](#Contents.html_rgen34_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And their words were good in Hamor's eyes and in Hamor's son
Shechem's eyes.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_18.jpg){height="58" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:19*](#Contents.html_rgen34_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And the boy did not delay to do the thing, for he desired Jacob's
daughter. And he was more respected than all his father's house.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_19.jpg){height="61" width="356"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:20*](#Contents.html_rgen34_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Hamor and his son Shechem came to the gate of their city and
spoke to the people of their city, saying,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_20.jpg){height="72" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:21*](#Contents.html_rgen34_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^"These people are peaceable with us, and they'll live in the land
and go around in it, and the land, here, has enough breadth for them.
Let's take their daughters for us as wives and give our daughters to
them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_21.jpg){height="108" width="356"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:22*](#Contents.html_rgen34_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^Only in this way will these people consent to us to live with us, to
be one people: if every male among us is circumcised as they are
circumcised.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_22.jpg){height="85" width="356"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:23*](#Contents.html_rgen34_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^Their cattle and possessions and all their animals: won't they be
ours? Only let's consent to them, and they'll live with us."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_23.jpg){height="59" width="355"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:24*](#Contents.html_rgen34_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And everyone who went out of the gate of his city listened to Hamor
and to his son Shechem; and every male, everyone who went out of the
gate of his city, was circumcised.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_24.jpg){height="58" width="356"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:25*](#Contents.html_rgen34_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And it was on the third day, when they were hurting, and two of
Jacob's sons, Simeon and Levi, Dinah's brothers, each took his sword,
and they came upon the city stealthily, and they killed every male.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_25.jpg){height="111" width="356"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:26*](#Contents.html_rgen34_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And they killed Hamor and his son Shechem by the sword and took
Dinah from Shechem's house and went out.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_26.jpg){height="85" width="355"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:27*](#Contents.html_rgen34_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^Jacob's sons had come upon the corpses and despoiled the city
because they had defiled their sister.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_27.jpg){height="59" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:28*](#Contents.html_rgen34_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^They took their sheep and their oxen and their asses and what was in
the city and what was in the field.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_28.jpg){height="91" width="362"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:29*](#Contents.html_rgen34_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And they captured and despoiled all their wealth and all their
infants and their wives and everything that was in the house.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_29.jpg){height="62" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:30*](#Contents.html_rgen34_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And Jacob said to Simeon and to Levi, "You've caused me anguish,
making me odious to those who live in the land, to the Canaanite and to
the Per-izzite, and I'm few in number, and they'll be gathered against
me and strike me, and I'll be destroyed, I and my house."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_30.jpg){height="94" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 34:31*](#Contents.html_rgen34_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And they said, "Shall he treat our sister like a prostitute?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap34_31.jpg){height="24" width="274"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

34:31. Shall he treat our sister like a prostitute? Simeon and Levi seem
to have the last word here, but the recompense for their violence comes
many years later. In his deathbed blessing of his sons, Jacob criticizes
them, demotes them from the order of birthright, and condemns them to
being scattered (Gen 49:5--7). This comes true, as the tribes of Levi
and Simeon became landless.

[]{#Chapter35.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:1*](#Contents.html_rgen35_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **35** {.h2a}

^1^And God said to Jacob, "Get up. Go up to Beth-El and live there and
make an altar there to God, who appeared to you when you were fleeing
from Esau, your brother."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_01.jpg){height="77" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:2*](#Contents.html_rgen35_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Jacob said to his house and to everyone who was with him, "Put
away the foreign gods that are among you and be purified and change your
clothes;

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_02.jpg){height="77" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:3*](#Contents.html_rgen35_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^and let's get up and go up to Beth-El, and I'll make an altar there
to God, who answered me in a day of my trouble, and He was with me in
the way that I went."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_03.jpg){height="69" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:4*](#Contents.html_rgen35_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And they gave all the foreign gods that were in their hand and the
rings that were in their ears to Jacob, and Jacob stashed them under the
oak that was by Shechem.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_04.jpg){height="77" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:5*](#Contents.html_rgen35_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And they traveled, and God's terror was on the cities that were
around them, and they did not pursue the children of Jacob.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_05.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:6*](#Contents.html_rgen35_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Jacob came to Luz, which is in the land of Canaan---it is
Beth-El---he and all the people who were with him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_06.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:7*](#Contents.html_rgen35_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And he built an altar there and called the place El of Beth-El
because God was revealed to him there when he was fleeing from his
brother.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_07.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:8*](#Contents.html_rgen35_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Deborah, Rebekah's nurse, died and was buried beneath Beth-El,
beneath an oak, and he called its name Oak of Weeping.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_08.jpg){height="60" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:9*](#Contents.html_rgen35_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And God appeared to Jacob again when he was coming from Paddan Aram,
and He blessed him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_09.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:10*](#Contents.html_rgen35_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And God said to him, "Your name is Jacob. Your name will not be
called Jacob anymore, but rather Israel will be your name." And He
called his name Israel.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_10.jpg){height="94" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:11*](#Contents.html_rgen35_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And God said to him, "I am El Shadday Be fruitful and multiply. A
nation and a community of nations will be from you, and kings will come
out from your hips.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_11.jpg){height="78" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:12*](#Contents.html_rgen35_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And the land that I gave to Abraham and to Isaac: I'll give it to
you, and I'll give the land to your seed after you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_12.jpg){height="78" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:13*](#Contents.html_rgen35_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And God went up from on him in the place where He had spoken with
him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_13.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:14*](#Contents.html_rgen35_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Jacob set up a pillar in the place where He had spoken with him,
a stone pillar, and he poured a libation on it and spilled oil on it.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_14.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:15*](#Contents.html_rgen35_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Jacob called the name of the place where God had spoken to him
there Beth-El.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_15.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:16*](#Contents.html_rgen35_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And they traveled from Beth-El, and there was still the span of the
land to come to Ephrat, and Rachel gave birth, and she had difficulty in
her labor.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_16.jpg){height="67" width="360"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:17*](#Contents.html_rgen35_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And it was when she was having difficulty in her labor, and the
midwife said to her, "Don't be afraid, because this, too, is a son for
you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_17.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:18*](#Contents.html_rgen35_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And it was as her soul was going out---because she died---and she
called his name Ben-oni. And his father called him Benjamin.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_18.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:19*](#Contents.html_rgen35_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And Rachel died. And she was buried on the road to Ephrat. It is
Bethlehem.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_19.jpg){height="45" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

35:19. **Rachel died**. This is the saddest and most ironic consequence
of all the deceptions in this narrative. Jacob had unwittingly put a
curse on her by telling Laban, "Let the one with whom you'll find your
gods not live" (31:32). Rachel had told her father that she was
menstruating so as to keep him from searching her (31:35). Now she dies
in childbirth, which both reveals her deception---that she could not
have been menstruating---and is the horrible fulfillment of Jacob's
words.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:20*](#Contents.html_rgen35_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Jacob set up a pillar on her grave. It is the pillar of Rachel's
grave to this day.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_20.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:21*](#Contents.html_rgen35_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Israel traveled and pitched his tent past Migdal-Eder.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_21.jpg){height="49" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:22*](#Contents.html_rgen35_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And it was when Israel was tenting in that land: and Reuben went and
lay with Bilhah, his father's concubine. And Israel heard.

And Jacob's sons were twelve.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_22.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

35:22. **Israel heard**. The story seems to end unfinished, with Reuben
suffering no consequence for taking his father's concubine. But, as with
Simeon and Levi (see the comment on Gen 34:31), Reuben pays dearly in
his father's deathbed blessing of his sons. Jacob criticizes Reuben and
demotes him from his birthright, "for you ascended your father's bed"
(Gen 49:3--4).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:23*](#Contents.html_rgen35_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^The sons of Leah were Jacob's firstborn, Reuben, and Simeon and Levi
and Judah and Issachar and Zebulun.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_23.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:24*](#Contents.html_rgen35_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^The sons of Rachel were Joseph and Benjamin.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_24.jpg){height="21" width="179"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:25*](#Contents.html_rgen35_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And the sons of Bilhah, Rachel's maid, were Dan and Naphtali.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_25.jpg){height="25" width="294"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:26*](#Contents.html_rgen35_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And the sons of Zilpah, Leah's maid, were Gad and Asher. These were
Jacob's sons, who were born to him in Paddan Aram.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_26.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:27*](#Contents.html_rgen35_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And Jacob came to Isaac, his father, at Mamre, at Kiriath Arba---it
is Hebron---where Abraham and Isaac had resided.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_27.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:28*](#Contents.html_rgen35_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And Isaac's days were a hundred years and eighty years,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_28.jpg){height="42" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 35:29*](#Contents.html_rgen35_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^and Isaac expired. And he died and was gathered to his people, old
and full of days. And Esau and Jacob, his sons, buried him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap35_29.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

35:29. **Isaac expired**. Isaac's life is markedly different from the
lives of his father Abraham and his son Jacob. They both spend time in
the two great centers of civilization at that time, Mesopotamia and
Egypt, but Isaac never leaves the land of the covenant. They both have
multiple wives and concubines, but Isaac has only Rebekah. They both
have their names changed, but Isaac does not (see the comment on Gen
17:19). They both fight, but Isaac does not. There are fewer stories
about Isaac, and he is a fairly passive figure in them. Regarding the
two most famous stories of Isaac: in the story of his near-sacrifice he
is acted upon by his father, and in the story of Jacob's appropriation
of Esau's blessing he is acted upon by his son. Perhaps the explanation
for his being different is that he once lay on the altar as a sacrifice,
and so his life is consecrated in a singular way.

35:29. **Esau and Jacob, his sons**. It is hard to miss the order here:
Esau before Jacob. It is not simply a matter of birth order, because
Isaac is listed before Ishmael in the corresponding passage (25:9). This
seems rather to be a sign of recognition, compassion, and compensation
to Esau for his displacement by Jacob.

[]{#Chapter36.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 36:1*](#Contents.html_rgen36_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **36** {.h2a}

^1^And these are the records of Esau: He is Edom.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap36_01.jpg){height="22" width="236"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 36:2*](#Contents.html_rgen36_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^Esau had taken his wives from the daughters of Canaan: Adah, daughter
of Elon, the Hittite, and Aholibamah, daughter of Anah, daughter of
Zibeon, the Hivite,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap36_02.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

36:2--3. **Adah, daughter of Elon ... Aholibamah, daughter of Anah ...
Basemath, daughter of Ishmael**. These names are in conflict with the
names of Esau's wives given earlier (26:34; 28:9). Rashi gives midrashic
explanations for this confusion, Ibn Ezra suggests that they each had
two names, and Ramban says that it is possible that Esau's first two
wives died, he married two others, and he renamed his third wife. The
character and variety of their solutions indicate that they did not know
what the reason was for this confusion in the text. Critical scholars
have not solved it in terms of standard source divisions either. It may
be a combination of scribal and source reasons. It remains a classic
puzzle to be solved.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 36:3*](#Contents.html_rgen36_03)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^and Basemath, daughter of Ishmael, sister of Nebaioth.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap36_03.jpg){height="22" width="288"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 36:4*](#Contents.html_rgen36_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Adah gave birth for Esau to Eliphaz, and Basemath gave birth to
Reuel,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap36_04.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 36:5*](#Contents.html_rgen36_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^and Aholibamah gave birth to Jeush and Jalam and Korah. These are
Esau's sons, who were born to him in the land of Canaan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap36_05.jpg){height="46" width="341"}

![Image](images/36page_121a.jpg){height="18" width="75"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 36:6*](#Contents.html_rgen36_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Esau took his wives and his sons and his daughters and all the
persons of his household and his cattle and all of his animals and all
of his possessions that he had acquired in the land of Canaan, and he
went to a land, from the presence of Jacob, his brother,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap36_06.jpg){height="115" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

36:6. **a land**. The name of the land is not given. It may have been
omitted due to a scribal error. Or: some understand the verse to mean
that he went to a land that was away from Jacob. The Septuagint reads,
"he went out of the land of Canaan," which makes sense.

the land of their residences was not able to suffice them because of
their cattle.

[]{#Chapter37.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:1*](#Contents.html_rgen37_01)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## A[ND HE LIVED]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **37** {.h2a}

^1^And Jacob lived in the land of his father's residences, in the land
of Canaan.

![Image](images/Gen_37.jpg){height="22" width="56"}

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_01.jpg){height="22" width="310"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:2*](#Contents.html_rgen37_02)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^These are the records of Jacob: Joseph, at seventeen years old, had
been tending the sheep with his brothers, and he was a boy with the sons
of Bilhah and the sons of Zilpah, his father's wives; and Joseph brought
a bad report of them to their father.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_02.jpg){height="100" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:3*](#Contents.html_rgen37_03)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Israel had loved Joseph the most of all his children because he
was a son of old age to him. And he made him a coat of many colors.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_03.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

37:3. **coat of many colors**. We have no idea what the Hebrew means, so
I have retained the traditional "many colors." Some have tried to derive
something about its meaning from the story of Tamar, who is wearing one
when her brother Amnon rapes her (2 Samuel 13). But this is difficult
because these two stories appear to be intentionally connected. (I
brought evidence that they come from the same source in *The Hidden Book
in the Bible*.) It is hardly coincidental that the two people who wear a
coat of many colors in the Bible are both victims of violence by their
brothers, and that both coats are torn. The significance of the coat,
therefore, is as a symbol of injustice among siblings. This significance
is doubled when we see the fate of the coat. See the comment on 37:31.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:4*](#Contents.html_rgen37_04)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And his brothers saw that their father loved him the most of all his
brothers. And they hated him. And they were not able to speak a greeting
to him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_04.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

37:4. **to speak a greeting to him**. Literally "to speak to him of
peace," it appears to refer to the standard Hebrew greetings: one says
*š![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}lôm*
(peace/well-being) for hello; and one says
*h![Image](images/a1.jpg){.block height="12"
width="8"}š![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}lôm* *lô* (does he have peace?) for "How is he?" (Gen 29:6).
That is, the brothers cannot even bid him "hello" or ask how he is. This
is rendered ironic by the fact that Joseph's father will soon send him
to check on his brothers, telling him, "See how your brothers are
*\[š![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}lôm\]*."
It is rendered doubly ironic later when Joseph tells the Pharaoh that it
is possible to interpret Pharaoh's dream because "God will answer
regarding Pharaoh's well-being *\[š![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block
height="11" width="8"}lôm\]*," suggesting that God shows even more care
for the Pharaoh than Joseph's brothers are able to show him. (And see
the comment on 43:27.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:5*](#Contents.html_rgen37_05)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Joseph had a dream and told it to his brothers, and they went on
to hate him more.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_05.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:6*](#Contents.html_rgen37_06)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And he said to them, "Listen to this dream that I had:

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_06.jpg){height="45" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:7*](#Contents.html_rgen37_07)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^and here we were binding sheaves in the field; and here was my sheaf
rising and standing up, too; and, here, your sheaves surrounded and
bowed to my sheaf."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_07.jpg){height="74" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:8*](#Contents.html_rgen37_08)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And his brothers said to him, "Will you *rule* over us?! Will you
*dominate* us?!" And they went on to hate him more because of his dreams
and because of his words.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_08.jpg){height="75" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:9*](#Contents.html_rgen37_09)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And he had yet another dream and told it to his brothers. And he
said, "Here, I've had another dream, and here were the sun and the moon
and eleven stars bowing to me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_09.jpg){height="101" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:10*](#Contents.html_rgen37_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And he told it to his father and to his brothers.

And his father was annoyed at him and said to him, "What is this dream
that you've had? Shall *we* come, I and your mother and your brothers,
to bow to you to the ground?!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_10.jpg){height="120" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

37:10. **What is this dream that you've had?** No one seems to know that
the dreams are prophetic: not the brothers, not Jacob, not even Joseph
himself. Joseph will be able to interpret other people's dreams---but
not his own. Like many of us, he will learn what every psychoanalyst
knows: that seeing the meaning of one's own dreams is the hardest.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:11*](#Contents.html_rgen37_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And his brothers were jealous of him, and his father took note of
the thing.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_11.jpg){height="43" width="169"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:12*](#Contents.html_rgen37_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And his brothers went to feed their father's sheep in Shechem.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_12.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:13*](#Contents.html_rgen37_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Israel said to Joseph, "Aren't your brothers feeding in Shechem?
Come on and I'll send you to them."

And he said to him, "I'm here."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_13.jpg){height="74" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:14*](#Contents.html_rgen37_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And he said to him, "Go, see how your brothers are and how the sheep
are and bring me back word." And he sent him from the valley of Hebron.

And he came to Shechem.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_14.jpg){height="95" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:15*](#Contents.html_rgen37_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And a man found him, and here he was straying in a field. And the
man asked him, saying, "What are you looking for?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_15.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:16*](#Contents.html_rgen37_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And he said, "I'm looking for my brothers. Tell me, where are they
feeding?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_16.jpg){height="50" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:17*](#Contents.html_rgen37_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And the man said, "They traveled on from here. Because I heard them
saying, 'Let's go to Dothan.'"

And Joseph went after his brothers and found them in Dothan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_17.jpg){height="96" width="361"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:18*](#Contents.html_rgen37_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And they saw him from a distance, and before he came close to them
they conspired against him: to kill him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_18.jpg){height="52" width="343"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:19*](#Contents.html_rgen37_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And the brothers said to one another, "Here comes the dream-master,
that one there!

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_19.jpg){height="72" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:20*](#Contents.html_rgen37_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And now, come on and let's kill him and throw him in one of the
pits, and we'll say a wild animal ate him, and we'll see what his dreams
will be!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_20.jpg){height="91" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

37:20 **wild animal**. Literally, a *bad* animal---not just any wild
animal. This expression will occur one more time in the Torah: the
blessings of the covenant at Sinai will include a divine promise to
eliminate wild (bad) animals from the land. The value of that blessing
is rendered more visible by the recollection of Jacob's pain when he
believes that such a creature has taken the life of his child.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:21*](#Contents.html_rgen37_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Reuben heard, and he saved him from their hand. And he said,
"Let's not take his life."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_21.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:22*](#Contents.html_rgen37_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Reuben said to them, "Don't spill blood. Throw him into this pit
that's in the wilderness, and don't put out a hand against him"---in
order to save him from their hand, to bring him back to his father.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_22.jpg){height="100" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:23*](#Contents.html_rgen37_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And it was when Joseph came to his brothers: and they took off
Joseph's coat, the coat of many colors, which he had on.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_23.jpg){height="68" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:24*](#Contents.html_rgen37_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And they took him and threw him into the pit. And the pit was empty;
there was no water in it.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_24.jpg){height="74" width="369"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

37:24. **the pit was empty; there was no water in it**. Rashi takes this
to be redundant: if it is empty, of course there is no water in it. He
concludes that there are snakes and scorpions in it. But it is not
redundant. Rather, two things are conveyed: It is empty. This conveys
that he is alone and helpless. There is no water in it. This conveys
that his survival is in danger.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:25*](#Contents.html_rgen37_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And they sat down to eat bread.

And they raised their eyes and saw, and here was a caravan of
Ishmaelites coming from Gilead, and their camels were carrying spices
and balsam and myrrh, going to bring them down to Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_25.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:26*](#Contents.html_rgen37_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And Judah said to his brothers, "What profit is there if we kill our
brother and cover his blood?

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_26.jpg){height="52" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

37:26. **What profit is there**. Judah's motives are a mystery. Is he
really concerned with money ("let's sell him to the Ishmaelites") and
avoiding performing fratricide ("let our hand not be on him, because
he's our brother")? Or is his motive to save Joseph's life? Judah's
later behavior toward Benjamin and his great reward at the end (see the
comment on 49:8) suggest that his behavior here is positive. Reuben's
failed plan leads Joseph to the pit; Judah's plan leads him to Egypt.
And Abraham's unions with Hagar and Keturah produced the Ishmaelites and
the Midianites, who are now crucial to Joseph's going from the pit to
Egypt! Perhaps the point is how complex and fragile our fate is.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:27*](#Contents.html_rgen37_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^Come on and let's sell him to the Ishmaelites, and let our hand not
be on him, because he's our brother, our flesh." And his brothers
listened.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_27.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:28*](#Contents.html_rgen37_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And Midianite people, merchants, passed, and they pulled and lifted
Joseph from the pit. And they sold Joseph to the Ishmaelites for twenty
weights of silver. And they brought Joseph to Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_28.jpg){height="100" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:29*](#Contents.html_rgen37_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And Reuben came back to the pit, and here: Joseph was not in the
pit. And he tore his clothes.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_29.jpg){height="54" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:30*](#Contents.html_rgen37_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And he went back to his brothers and said, "The boy's gone! And I,
where can I go?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_30.jpg){height="53" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:31*](#Contents.html_rgen37_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And they took Joseph's coat and slaughtered a he-goat and dipped the
coat in the blood.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_31.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

37:31. **they took Joseph's coat and slaughtered a he-goat**. Jacob was
ironically paid back for appropriating his brother's birthright: he had
to work an additional seven years for Rachel on account of her sister's
birthright (see the comment on Gen 29:26). Now he is likewise paid back
for appropriating his brother's blessing: He deceived his father using
his brother's clothing and the meat and skins of a goat. Now his own
sons deceive him using their brother's clothing and the blood of a goat.
The Torah does not excuse Jacob's behavior. It rather teaches that such
acts have consequences.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:32*](#Contents.html_rgen37_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And they sent the coat of many colors and brought it to their father
and said, "We found this. Recognize: is it your son's coat or not?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_32.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:33*](#Contents.html_rgen37_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And he recognized it and said, "My son's coat. A wild animal ate
him. Joseph is *torn up!*"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_33.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:34*](#Contents.html_rgen37_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And Jacob ripped his clothes and wore sackcloth on his hips and
mourned over his son many days.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_34.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:35*](#Contents.html_rgen37_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And all his sons and all his daughters got up to console him, and he
refused to be consoled, and he said, "Because I'll go down mourning to
my son at Sheol," and his father wept for him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_35.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 37:36*](#Contents.html_rgen37_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And the Medanites sold him to Egypt, to Potiphar, an official of
Pharaoh, chief of the guards.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap37_36.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter38.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:1*](#Contents.html_rgen38_01)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **38** {.h2a}

^1^And it was at that time, and Judah went *3* O down from his brothers
and turned to an Adullamite man, and his name was Hirah.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_01.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

38:1. **Judah**. The eponymous ancestor of the Jews, Judah is the most
prominent of the brothers in the Joseph stories, and here he is the only
one of Jacob's sons besides Joseph to have a separate story about him.
Some say that the word ![Image](images/page127-1.jpg){.block height="12"
width="40"} means "to be thankful." Its derivation is unknown.

38:1. **Judah went down.** The next chapter begins with "Joseph had been
brought down." The contrast is blatant (even more so in the Hebrew)
between Judah's independence and Joseph's weakness: Judah *went* down,
and Joseph *was brought* down.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:2*](#Contents.html_rgen38_02)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Judah saw a daughter of a Canaanite man there, and his name was
Shua. And he took her and came to her.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_02.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:3*](#Contents.html_rgen38_03)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And she became pregnant and gave birth to a son, and he called his
name Er.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_03.jpg){height="22" width="282"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:4*](#Contents.html_rgen38_04)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And she became pregnant again and gave birth to a son, and she called
his name Onan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_04.jpg){height="22" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:5*](#Contents.html_rgen38_05)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And she proceeded again to give birth to a son, and she called his
name Shelah. (And he was at Chezib when she gave birth to him.)

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_05.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:6*](#Contents.html_rgen38_06)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Judah took a wife for Er, his firstborn, and her name was Tamar.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_06.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:7*](#Contents.html_rgen38_07)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Er, Judah's firstborn, was bad in YHWH's eyes, and YHWH killed
him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_07.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:8*](#Contents.html_rgen38_08)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Judah said to Onan, "Come to your brother's wife and couple as a
brother-in-law with her and raise seed for your brother."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_08.jpg){height="79" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

38:8. **raise seed for your brother**. This is known as the law of
levirate marriage, from the Latin *levir*, meaning brother-in-law. See
Deuteronomy 25 and the comments on it.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:9*](#Contents.html_rgen38_09)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Onan knew that the seed would not be his. And it was when he came
to his brother's wife: and he spent on the ground so as not to give seed
for his brother.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_09.jpg){height="79" width="348"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:10*](#Contents.html_rgen38_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And what he did was bad in YHWH's eyes, and He killed him, too.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_10.jpg){height="50" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:11*](#Contents.html_rgen38_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Judah said to Tamar, his daughter-in-law, "Live as a widow at
your father's house until my son Shelah grows up" (because he said, "Or
else he, too, will die like his brothers"). And Tamar went and lived at
her father's house.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_11.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:12*](#Contents.html_rgen38_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And the days were many, and Judah's wife, the daughter of Shua,
died. And Judah was consoled. And he went up to his sheepshearers, he
and his friend Hirah, the Adullamite, to Timnah.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_12.jpg){height="75" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:13*](#Contents.html_rgen38_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And it was told to Tamar, saying, "Here, your father-in-law is going
up to Timnah to shear his sheep."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_13.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:14*](#Contents.html_rgen38_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And she took off her widowhood clothes from on her and covered
herself with a veil and wrapped herself and sat in a visible place that
was on the road to Timnah, because she saw that Shelah had grown up and
she had not been given to him as a wife.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_14.jpg){height="102" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:15*](#Contents.html_rgen38_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Judah saw her and thought her to be a prostitute because she had
covered her face.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_15.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:16*](#Contents.html_rgen38_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And he turned to her by the road.

And he said, "Come on. Let me come to you," because he did not know that
she was his daughter-in-law.

And she said, "What will you give me when you come to me?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_16.jpg){height="97" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:17*](#Contents.html_rgen38_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And he said, "I'll have a goat kid sent from the flock."

And she said, "If you'll give a pledge until you send it."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_17.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:18*](#Contents.html_rgen38_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And he said, "What is the pledge that I'll give you?"

And she said, "Your seal and your cord and your staff that's in your
hand." And he gave them to her, and he came to her, and she became
pregnant by him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_18.jpg){height="96" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:19*](#Contents.html_rgen38_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And she got up and went and took off her veil from on her and put on
her widowhood clothes.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_19.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:20*](#Contents.html_rgen38_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Judah sent the goat kid by the hand of his friend the Adullamite
to take back the pledge from the woman's hand, and he did not find her.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_20.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:21*](#Contents.html_rgen38_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And he asked the people of her place, saying, "Where is the sacred
prostitute? She was visibly by the road."

And they said, "There was no sacred prostitute here."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_21.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

38:21. **sacred prostitute**. On sacred prostitution, see the comment on
Deut 23:18. The immediate point in this episode is that Judah is
described as thinking that Tamar is a prostitute
(*z![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11"
width="7"}n![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}h*)
(38:15), but now his friend uses what is seemingly a more refined word,
"sacred prostitute" (*q![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}d![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14"
width="6"}š![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}h)---* which may also denote a higher level of
prostitute---when he discreetly inquires about her.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:22*](#Contents.html_rgen38_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And he came back to Judah and said, "I didn't find her, and also the
people of the place said There was no sacred prostitute here.'"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_22.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:23*](#Contents.html_rgen38_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And Judah said, "Let her take them or else we'll be a disgrace.
Here, I've sent this goat kid, and you didn't find her."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_23.jpg){height="93" width="362"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:24*](#Contents.html_rgen38_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And it was about three months, and it was told to Judah, saying,
"Your daughter-in-law Tamar has whored, and, here, she's pregnant by
whoring as well."

And Judah said, "Bring her out and let her be burned."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_24.jpg){height="94" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:25*](#Contents.html_rgen38_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^She was brought out. And she sent to her father-in-law, saying, "I'm
pregnant by the man to whom these belong," and she said, "Recognize: to
whom do these seal and cords and staff belong?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_25.jpg){height="92" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

38:25. **Recognize**. Tamar lays down the evidence and uses the same
word that Joseph's brothers used when they laid down the evidence of
Joseph's demise (the bloodstained coat of many colors) in front of
Jacob. Like father, like son: Judah might well feel a chill down his
back when he hears the word and knows that he was a guilty party on both
occasions. We might even imagine that this double sense of his own
errors is what moves him to declare: "She's more right than I am."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:26*](#Contents.html_rgen38_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And Judah recognized and said, "She's more right than I am, because
of the fact that I didn't give her to my son Shelah." And he did not go
on to know her again.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_26.jpg){height="73" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:27*](#Contents.html_rgen38_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And it was at the time that she was giving birth, and here were
twins in her womb.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_27.jpg){height="54" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:28*](#Contents.html_rgen38_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And it was as she was giving birth, and one put out his hand, and
the midwife took a scarlet thread and tied it on his hand, saying, "This
one came out first."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_28.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:29*](#Contents.html_rgen38_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And it was as he pulled his hand back, and here his brother came
out. And she said, "What a breach you've made for yourself!" And he
called his name Perez.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_29.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 38:30*](#Contents.html_rgen38_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And his brother who had the scarlet thread on his hand came out
after. And he called his name Zerah.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap38_30.jpg){height="49" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter39.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:1*](#Contents.html_rgen39_01)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **39** {.h2a}

^1^And Joseph had been brought down to Egypt. And an Egyptian man,
Potiphar, an official of Pharaoh, chief of the guards, bought him from
the hand of the Ishmaelites who had brought him down there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_01.jpg){height="78" width="351"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:2*](#Contents.html_rgen39_02)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And YHWH was with Joseph, and he was a successful man, and he was in
his Egyptian lord's house.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_02.jpg){height="48" width="351"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:3*](#Contents.html_rgen39_00)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And his lord saw that YHWH was with him, and YHWH made everything he
was doing successful in his hand.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_03.jpg){height="92" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:4*](#Contents.html_rgen39_04)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Joseph found favor in his eyes, and he attended him, and he
appointed him over his house and put everything he had in his hand.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_04.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:5*](#Contents.html_rgen39_05)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And it was from the time that he appointed him in his house and over
all that he had that YHWH blessed the Egyptian's house because of
Joseph, and YHWH's blessing was in everything that he had, in the house
and in the field.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_05.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:6*](#Contents.html_rgen39_06)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And he left everything that he had in Joseph's hand and did not know
a thing about what he had except the bread that he was eating.

And Joseph had an attractive figure and was handsome.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_06.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:7*](#Contents.html_rgen39_07)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And it was after these things, and his lord's wife raised her eyes to
Joseph and said, "Lie with me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_07.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:8*](#Contents.html_rgen39_08)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And he refused, and he said to his lord's wife, "Here, my lord
doesn't know what he has with me in the house, and he's put everything
that he has in my hand.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_08.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:9*](#Contents.html_rgen39_09)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^No one is bigger than I am in this house, and he hasn't held back a
thing from me except you because you're his wife, and how could I do
this great wrong? And I would sin against God."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_09.jpg){height="101" width="343"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:10*](#Contents.html_rgen39_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And it was, when she spoke to Joseph day after day, that he didn't
listen to her, to lie by her, to be with her.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_10.jpg){height="68" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:11*](#Contents.html_rgen39_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And it was on a day like this, and he came to the house to do his
work, and not one of the people of the house was there in the house.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_11.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:12*](#Contents.html_rgen39_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And she grasped him by his garment, saying, "Lie with me!" And he
left his garment in her hand and fled and went outside.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_12.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:13*](#Contents.html_rgen39_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And it was, when she saw that he had left his garment in her hand
and run off outside,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_13.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:14*](#Contents.html_rgen39_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^that she called to the people of her house and said to them, saying,
"See, he brought us a Hebrew man to fool with us. He came to me, to lie
with me, and I called in a loud voice.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_14.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

39:14 **to fool with us ... to fool with me**. She says "to fool with
*us*" when she tells the household, but she says "to fool with *me*"
when she tells her husband. Deceivers formulate their words carefully to
persuade their particular audience.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:15*](#Contents.html_rgen39_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And it was when he heard that I raised my voice and called that he
left his garment by me and fled and went outside."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_15.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:16*](#Contents.html_rgen39_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And she laid his garment down by her until his lord came to his
house.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_16.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:17*](#Contents.html_rgen39_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And she spoke things like these to him, saying, "The Hebrew slave,
whom you brought us, came to me, to fool with me.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_17.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

39:17. **whom you brought us**. She uses the same device that Adam had
used when trying to exonerate himself in Eden when he said, "The woman,
whom *you* placed with me ..." It is an attempt to help one's position
by putting part of the blame on one's judge. It works better for
Potiphar's wife, using it on a human authority, than it did for Adam,
who tried to use it on God.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:18*](#Contents.html_rgen39_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And it was when I raised my voice and called that he left his
garment by me and fled outside."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_18.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:19*](#Contents.html_rgen39_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And it was, when his lord heard his wife's words that she spoke to
him, saying, "Your servant did things like these to me," that his anger
flared.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_19.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:20*](#Contents.html_rgen39_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Joseph's lord took him and put him in prison, a place where the
king's prisoners were kept.

And he was there in the prison,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_20.jpg){height="70" width="339"}

![Image](images/39page_131.jpg){height="25" width="91"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:21*](#Contents.html_rgen39_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^and YHWH was with Joseph and extended kindness to him and gave him
favor in the eyes of the warden.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_21.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:22*](#Contents.html_rgen39_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And the warden put all the prisoners who were in the prison into
Joseph's hand, and he was doing all the things that they do there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_22.jpg){height="73" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 39:23*](#Contents.html_rgen39_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^The warden was not seeing anything in his hand because YHWH was with
him, and YHWH would make whatever he did successful.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap39_23.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter40.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:1*](#Contents.html_rgen40_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **40** {.h2a}

^1^And it was, after these things, the drink-steward of the king of
Egypt and the baker sinned against their lord, against the king of
Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_01.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:2*](#Contents.html_rgen40_02)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Pharaoh was angry at his two officers, at the chief of the
drink-stewards and at the chief of the bakers,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_02.jpg){height="50" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:3*](#Contents.html_rgen40_03)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^and he put them under watch at the house of the chief of the guards,
into prison, the place where Joseph was held.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_03.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:4*](#Contents.html_rgen40_04)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And the chief of the guards assigned Joseph with them, and he
attended them.

And they were under watch for days.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_04.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:5*](#Contents.html_rgen40_05)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And the two of them had a dream, each his own dream, in one night,
each with his own dream's meaning, the drink-steward and the baker whom
the king of Egypt had, who were held in the prison.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_05.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:6*](#Contents.html_rgen40_06)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Joseph came to them in the morning and saw them, and here they
were upset.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_06.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:7*](#Contents.html_rgen40_07)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And he asked Pharaoh's officers who were with him under watch at his
lord's house, saying, "Why are your faces bad today?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_07.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:8*](#Contents.html_rgen40_08)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And they said to him, "We had a dream, and there's no one who can
tell the meaning of it."

And Joseph said to them, "Don't meanings belong to God? Tell me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_08.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:9*](#Contents.html_rgen40_09)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And the chief of the drink-stewards told his dream to Joseph, and he
said to him, "In my dream: And here was a vine in front of me,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_09.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:10*](#Contents.html_rgen40_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^and in the vine were three branches, and it was like it was
blooming: its blossom came up; its clusters produced grapes.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_10.jpg){height="46" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:11*](#Contents.html_rgen40_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
