^11^And Pharaoh's cup was in my hand. And I took the grapes and pressed
them into Pharaoh's cup and set the cup on Pharaoh's hand."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_11.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:12*](#Contents.html_rgen40_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And Joseph said to him, "This is its meaning: The three branches,
they're three days.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_12.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

40:12. **This is its meaning**. Joseph's ability to interpret dreams is
the first time in the Torah that a human possesses a divine power. (The
only preceding case is Jacob's manipulation of the coloring of Laban's
flocks, but we cannot be certain if that was understood to be a
miraculous power or rather a case of wisdom or cleverness, because we do
not know what notions of heredity and miracle are presumed there.) The
significance of dreams in human experience---and the significance of
dream *interpretation* especially---is reflected by this fact: the first
divine power possessed by a human in the *Tanak* is the interpretation
of dreams.

We should also note that something has changed in Joseph. At the
beginning of his story he appeared not to be aware of the prophetic
import of his own dreams, but now he is able to interpret the dreams of
others, and next he will be able to interpret the dreams of Pharaoh,
affecting the destiny of countries and of his own family. We shall see
other changes in Joseph as he grows older.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:13*](#Contents.html_rgen40_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^In three more days Pharaoh will lift your head up, and he'll put you
back at your station, and you'll set Pharaoh's cup in his hand---like
the original manner when you were his drink-steward.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_13.jpg){height="89" width="362"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:14*](#Contents.html_rgen40_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^So if you'll remember me with you when it will be good for you, and
you'll practice kindness with me, then you'll bring up the memory of me
to Pharaoh, and you'll bring me out of this house.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_14.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

40:14. **bring me out of this house**. Joseph's words to describe his
liberation from his enslavement in Egypt prefigure the description of
the children of Israel's liberation from enslavement in Egypt in the
opening words of the Ten Commandments: "who *brought you out* from the
land of Egypt, *from a house* of slaves."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:15*](#Contents.html_rgen40_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^Because I was *stolen* from the land of the Hebrews; and here, too,
I haven't done anything that they should have put me in the pit."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_15.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:16*](#Contents.html_rgen40_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And the chief of the bakers saw that he told a good meaning, and he
said to Joseph, "Me, too. In my dream: And here were three baskets of
white bread on my head,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_16.jpg){height="68" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:17*](#Contents.html_rgen40_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^and in the highest basket were some of all of Pharaoh's foods, baked
goods. And the birds were eating them from the basket, from on my head."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_17.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:18*](#Contents.html_rgen40_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Joseph answered, and he said, "This is its meaning: The three
baskets, they're three days.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_18.jpg){height="46" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:19*](#Contents.html_rgen40_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^In three more days Pharaoh will lift your head up from you, and
he'll hang you on a tree, and the birds will eat your flesh from you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_19.jpg){height="70" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:20*](#Contents.html_rgen40_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And it was in the third day, Pharaoh's birthday, and Pharaoh made a
feast for all his servants. And he held up the head of the chief of the
drink-stewards and the head of the chief of the bakers among his
servants.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_20.jpg){height="99" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:21*](#Contents.html_rgen40_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And he put the chief of the drink-stewards back over his
stewardship, and he set the cup on Pharaoh's hand;

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_21.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:22*](#Contents.html_rgen40_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^and he hanged the chief of the bakers---as Joseph had told the
meaning for them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_22.jpg){height="52" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 40:23*](#Contents.html_rgen40_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And the chief of the drink-stewards did not remember Joseph. And he
forgot him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap40_23.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter41.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:1*](#Contents.html_rgen41_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## A[T THE END]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **41** {.h2a}

^1^And it was at the end of two years'time, and Pharaoh was dreaming:
and here he was standing by the Nile.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41.jpg){height="37" width="59"}

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_01.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:2*](#Contents.html_rgen41_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And here, coming up from the Nile, were seven cows, beautiful-looking
and fat-fleshed, and they fed in the reeds.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_02.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:3*](#Contents.html_rgen41_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And here were seven other cows coming up after them from the Nile,
bad-looking and thin-fleshed, and they stood by the cows that were by
the bank of the Nile.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_03.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:4*](#Contents.html_rgen41_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And the bad-looking and thin-fleshed cows ate the seven
beautiful-looking and fat cows---and Pharaoh woke up.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_04.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:5*](#Contents.html_rgen41_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And he slept and dreamed a second time, and here were seven ears of
grain coming up on one stalk, fat and good.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_05.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:6*](#Contents.html_rgen41_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And here were seven ears of grain, thin and scorched by the east
wind, growing after them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_06.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:7*](#Contents.html_rgen41_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And the thin ears of grain swallowed the seven fat and full ears of
grain---and Pharaoh woke up, and here it was a dream.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:8*](#Contents.html_rgen41_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And it was in the morning, and his spirit was moved, and he sent and
called all of Egypt's magicians and all its wise men. And Pharaoh told
them his dream, and there was no one who could tell the meaning of them
to Pharaoh.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_08.jpg){height="97" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:9*](#Contents.html_rgen41_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And the chief of the drink-stewards spoke to Pharaoh, saying, "I'm
recalling my sins today:

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_09.jpg){height="54" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:10*](#Contents.html_rgen41_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^Pharaoh had been angry at his servants, and he put me under watch at
the house of the chief of the guards, me and the chief of the bakers.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_10.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:11*](#Contents.html_rgen41_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And we had a dream in one night, I and he; we each dreamed with his
own dream's meaning.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_11.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:12*](#Contents.html_rgen41_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And there, with us, was a Hebrew boy, a slave of the chief of the
guards; and we told him, and he told us the meaning of our dreams. He
told the meaning according to each one's dream.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_12.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:13*](#Contents.html_rgen41_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And it was---as he told the meaning for us, so it was: me he put
back at my station, and him he hanged."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_13.jpg){height="49" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:14*](#Contents.html_rgen41_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Pharaoh sent and called Joseph, and they rushed him from the
pit, and he shaved and changed his clothing and came to Pharaoh.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_14.jpg){height="51" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:15*](#Contents.html_rgen41_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Pharaoh said to Joseph, "I've had a dream, and there's no one
who can tell the meaning of it, and I've heard about you, saying you
hear a dream so as to tell the meaning of it."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_15.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:16*](#Contents.html_rgen41_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And Joseph answered Pharaoh, saying, "Not I. God will answer
regarding Pharaoh's well-being."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_16.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:17*](#Contents.html_rgen41_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And Pharaoh spoke to Joseph, "In my dream, here I was standing by
the bank of the Nile.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_17.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:18*](#Contents.html_rgen41_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And here, coming up from the Nile, were seven cows, fat-fleshed and
beautiful-figured, and they fed in the reeds.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_18.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:19*](#Contents.html_rgen41_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And here were seven other cows coming up after them, weak and very
bad-figured and scrawny-fleshed. I haven't seen any like these in all
the land of Egypt for bad!

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_19.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

41:19. **scrawny**. There is confusion in the wording in regard to both
the ears of grain and the cows, going back and forth between the word
"thin" and the word "scrawny" (see 41:3,4,19,20,27). This minor point is
instructive because it is a good example of a well-known scribal
phenomenon, the confusion of the letters *dalet* and *resh*, which
looked similar in many periods. The two words in Hebrew are
![Image](images/page135-1.jpg){.block height="17" width="35"} (thin) and
![Image](images/page135-2.jpg){.block height="18" width="37"} (scrawny).
An error is easily made. And the chance of error is increased because
the word is sometimes followed by ![Image](images/page135-3.jpg){.block
height="12" width="36"} (bad), and this alliteration further draws a
scribe's eye to see a *resh* in place of a *dalet*.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:20*](#Contents.html_rgen41_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And the cows that were scrawny and bad ate the first seven cows that
were fat,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_20.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:21*](#Contents.html_rgen41_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^and they came inside, and it wasn't known that they had come inside!
And their appearance was bad, as at first. And I woke up.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_21.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:22*](#Contents.html_rgen41_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^"And I saw in my dream, and here were seven ears of grain coming up
on one stalk, full and good.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_22.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:23*](#Contents.html_rgen41_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And here were seven ears of grain, dried up, thin, scorched by the
east wind, growing after them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_23.jpg){height="47" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:24*](#Contents.html_rgen41_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And the thin ears of grain swallowed the seven good ears of grain.

"And I said it to the magicians, and there's no one who can tell me."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_24.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:25*](#Contents.html_rgen41_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And Joseph said to Pharaoh, "Pharaoh's dream: it is *one*. It has
told to Pharaoh what God is doing.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_25.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

41:25. **Pharaoh's dream: it is** ***one*.** **It has told to Pharaoh
what God is doing**. The pronoun is unclear. The verse could mean that
*God* has told Pharaoh what He is doing or that the dream has told
Pharaoh what God is doing. Even on the latter meaning, Joseph has
already said that "meanings belong to God" and that "God will answer
regarding Pharaoh's well-being." But the question is still whether God
has provided both the dream and the interpretation of it---or just the
interpretation, while the dream itself may derive from elsewhere. This
question remains unanswered in the text! (The same applies to 41:28, "It
has shown Pharaoh what God is doing.")

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:26*](#Contents.html_rgen41_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^The seven good cows: they're seven years. And the seven good ears of
grain: they're seven years. It's one dream.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_26.jpg){height="78" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:27*](#Contents.html_rgen41_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And the seven cows that were scrawny and bad that were coming up
after them: they're seven years. And the seven ears of grain that were
scrawny, scorched by the east wind, will be seven years of famine.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_27.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:28*](#Contents.html_rgen41_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^That is the thing that I spoke to Pharaoh: it has shown Pharaoh what
God is doing.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_28.jpg){height="56" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:29*](#Contents.html_rgen41_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^Here, seven years are coming: big bountifulness in all the land of
Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_29.jpg){height="43" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:30*](#Contents.html_rgen41_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And seven years of famine will rise after them, and all the
bountifulness in the land of Egypt will be forgotten, and the famine
will finish off the land.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_30.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:31*](#Contents.html_rgen41_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And the bountifulness won't be known in the land on account of that
famine afterwards because it will be very heavy.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_31.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:32*](#Contents.html_rgen41_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And about the recurrence of the dream to Pharaoh two times: because
the thing is right from God, and God is hurrying to do it.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_32.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

41:32. **right from God**. This term for "right" (Hebrew *nakôn*) will
be recalled to play an important part in the story of the exodus (Exod
8:22; see the text and comment there).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:33*](#Contents.html_rgen41_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And now, let Pharaoh look out for an understanding and wise man and
set him over the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_33.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:34*](#Contents.html_rgen41_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^Let Pharaoh do it and appoint overseers over the land and five-out
the land of Egypt in the seven years of bountifulness.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_34.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

41:34. **five-out**. This perhaps means to divide the land into portions
of five and save one-fifth for the bad years, but the meaning is
uncertain because this is the only occurrence of this verb in the Hebrew
Bible.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:35*](#Contents.html_rgen41_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And let them gather all the food of these coming good years and pile
up grain under Pharaoh's hand, food in the cities, and watch over it.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_35.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:36*](#Contents.html_rgen41_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And the food will become a deposit for the land for the seven years
of famine that will be in the land of Egypt so the land won't be cut off
in the famine."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_36.jpg){height="93" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:37*](#Contents.html_rgen41_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^And the thing was good in Pharaoh's eyes and in all his servants'
eyes.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_37.jpg){height="22" width="331"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:38*](#Contents.html_rgen41_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^And Pharaoh said to his servants, "Will we find such a man as this,
that God's spirit is in him?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_38.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:39*](#Contents.html_rgen41_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^And Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Since God has made you know all this,
there's no one as understanding and wise as you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_39.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

41:39. **understanding and wise**. Some interpreters identify the Joseph
story as a wisdom tale. It is treated as comparable to wisdom tales from
numerous cultures about a wise young man who is successful in a royal
court because of his cleverness. The story of Mordecai in the book of
Esther and the story of Daniel are compared in this context as well. I
would caution that there is a danger here of overdefining the word
"wisdom." The term was commonly used to refer to the books of Proverbs,
Job, and Ecclesiastes, works similar to philosophy; composed, like the
pre-Socratics, in poetry; sometimes set in very loose story contexts
(Job, Ecclesiastes), but basically dealing with issues outright, not
developing them through a prose narrative fabric. Now, in the first
place, if we redefine wisdom to include the Joseph cycle, and thus the
books of Daniel and Esther, we shall be forced to include a number of
other comparable sections of the Hebrew Bible under this heading as
well. We shall be left with a category of works with more elements of
variety than commonality (Ecclesiastes and Esther?), and the term
"wisdom" will be rendered less descriptive and useful. Second, the facts
of the Joseph story do not really fit this model in any case. Joseph is
not successful initially because of his cleverness or his sage advice.
He succeeds because he can interpret dreams, apparently by revelation
rather than by his own insight, since even the most brilliant Freudian
analyst would be unlikely to guess that a dream of cows eating cows
means that there will be prosperity followed by famine in the Near East.
Joseph is described as stating that it is God and not he who directs
events and sheds light on dreams (40:8; 41:16,25; 50:20). One might
suggest that this story is still based on the wisdom tale model but has
been adapted to fit the biblical author's literary and theological
requirements. That would be an interesting phenomenon to pursue, but it
would be a study of adaptation, not of wisdom. To understand biblical
wisdom, we are better advised to study wisdom literature as it has
classically been understood: Proverbs, Job, and Ecclesiastes.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:40*](#Contents.html_rgen41_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^You shall be over my house, and at your mouth all my people shall
conform. I shall be greater than you only in the throne."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_40.jpg){height="49" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

41:40. **conform**. The verb here, which usually means "to kiss," is a
problem for all translators. It may reflect a scribal problem or an
idiom that is no longer familiar to us.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:41*](#Contents.html_rgen41_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^And Pharaoh said to Joseph, "See, I've put you over all the land of
Egypt."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_41.jpg){height="49" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:42*](#Contents.html_rgen41_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^And Pharaoh took off his ring from his hand and put it on Joseph's
hand, and he had him dressed in linen garments, and he set a gold chain
on his neck,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_42.jpg){height="93" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:43*](#Contents.html_rgen41_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^and he had him driven in the chariot of the second-in-command whom
he had, and they called in front of him, "Kneel," so putting him over
all the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_43.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

41:43. **Kneel**. Hebrew *'abr![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14"
width="6"}k*. Its meaning is unknown. Some see a derivation from an
Egyptian term. One rabbinic interpretation takes it to mean "Bend the
knee."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:44*](#Contents.html_rgen41_44)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^44^And Pharaoh said to Joseph, "I am Pharaoh, and without you a man
won't lift his hand and his foot in all the land of Egypt."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_44.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:45*](#Contents.html_rgen41_45)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^45^And Pharaoh called Joseph's name Zaphe-nath-paneah. And he gave him
Asenath, daughter of Poti-phera, priest of On, as a wife. And Joseph
went out over the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_45.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

41:45. **Asenath, daughter of Poti-phera, priest of On**. Like Moses,
Joseph marries the daughter of a priest. The Pharaoh would naturally
provide Joseph with a prestigious marriage, but he would be disinclined
to let Joseph, a foreigner, marry anyone from the royal house. Marriage
to a woman from a distinguished priestly family would be the next best
thing.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:46*](#Contents.html_rgen41_46)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^46^And Joseph was thirty years old when he stood in front of Pharaoh,
king of Egypt. And Joseph went out from in front of Pharaoh and passed
through all the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_46.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:47*](#Contents.html_rgen41_47)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^47^And the land produced in the seven years of bountifulness by
fistfuls.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_47.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:48*](#Contents.html_rgen41_48)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^48^And he gathered all the food of the seven years, which were in the
land of Egypt, and he put food in cities; he put the food of the city's
fields that were around it inside of it.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_48.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:49*](#Contents.html_rgen41_49)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^49^And Joseph piled up grain like the sand of the sea, very much, until
he stopped counting, because it was without number.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_49.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:50*](#Contents.html_rgen41_50)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^50^And two sons were born to Joseph before the year of famine came---to
whom Asenath, daughter of Poti-phera, priest of On, gave birth for him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_50.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:51*](#Contents.html_rgen41_51)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^51^And Joseph called the firstborn's name Manasseh, "because God has
made me forget all of my trouble and all of my father's house."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_51.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:52*](#Contents.html_rgen41_52)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^52^And he called the second one's name Ephraim, "because God has made
me fruitful in the land of my degradation."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_52.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:53*](#Contents.html_rgen41_53)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^53^And the seven years of the bountifulness that was in the land of
Egypt finished,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_53.jpg){height="43" width="309"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:54*](#Contents.html_rgen41_54)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^54^and the seven years of famine started to come, as Joseph had said.
And there was famine in all the lands, and in all the land of Egypt
there was bread.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_54.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:55*](#Contents.html_rgen41_55)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^55^And all the land of Egypt hungered, and the people cried to Pharaoh
for the bread, and Pharaoh said to all Egypt, "Go to Joseph. Do what
he'll tell you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_55.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:56*](#Contents.html_rgen41_56)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^56^And the famine was on all the face of the earth. And Joseph opened
everything that was in them and sold to Egypt. And the famine was strong
in the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_56.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

41:56. **everything that was in them**. In what? The Septuagint reads,
"Joseph opened all the grain storehouses"
(![Image](images/page139-1.jpg){.block height="12" width="74"}), which
is consistent with 41:49.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 41:57*](#Contents.html_rgen41_57)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^57^And all the earth came to Egypt to buy, to Joseph, because the
famine was strong in all the earth.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap41_57.jpg){height="92" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter42.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:1*](#Contents.html_rgen42_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 42 {.h2a}

^1^And Jacob saw that there was grain in Egypt, and Jacob said to his
sons, "Why do you look at each other?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_01.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

42:1. **Jacob saw**. The text says that "Jacob *saw* that there was
grain in Egypt.... And he said, "I've *heard* that there's grain in
Egypt." How could he have *seen* it? Rashi says that he had a vision.
This rather reflects the metaphor of *seeing* as meaning to know, to
learn, to find out, to comprehend. It shows the high value we place on
sight over the other senses. The Torah will convey this in many ways,
including a progression: Isaac cannot see on his deathbed, and so he is
deceived. Jacob cannot see on his deathbed, but he has more insight than
he did when he was young and could see. (He sees his grandsons'
destinies, when earlier he could not see what was going on among his
sons.) And then Moses'"eye was not dim" up to the time of his death at
the age of 120.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:2*](#Contents.html_rgen42_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And he said, "Here I've heard that there's grain in Egypt. Go down
there and buy grain for us from there, and we'll live and not die."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_02.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:3*](#Contents.html_rgen42_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And ten of Joseph's brothers went down to buy grain from Egypt,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_03.jpg){height="22" width="313"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:4*](#Contents.html_rgen42_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^and Jacob did not send Joseph's brother Benjamin with his brothers
because, he said, "In case some harm will happen to him."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_04.jpg){height="47" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:5*](#Contents.html_rgen42_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And the sons of Israel came to buy among those who were coming,
because the famine was in the land of Canaan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_05.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

42:5. **sons of Israel**. Until now they have been called "sons of
Jacob." Now they are called "sons of Israel" for the first time just as
they come to Egypt. One explanation is that it is in Egypt that they
will grow and become the nation that will be known as Israel. (The
nation is called "children of Israel" 350 times in the Torah but never
called "children of Jacob.") Another explanation: it is now, as they
enter Egypt, that they will themselves have a struggle with God
(*yi![Image](images/s.jpg){.block height="12"
width="6"}r![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}-'![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14" width="6"}l*).
They will unknowingly confront Joseph, who has risen in Egypt through
the divine power of interpreting dreams (41:16). And in the end Joseph
will tell them that, although they thought they were struggling with
Joseph, God had been in control all along (45:8; 50:20).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:6*](#Contents.html_rgen42_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Joseph was the one in charge over the land; he was the one who
sold grain to all the people of the land. And Joseph's brothers came and
bowed to him, noses to the ground.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_06.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:7*](#Contents.html_rgen42_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Joseph saw his brothers and recognized them, but he made himself
unrecognizable to them, and he spoke with them in hard tones. And he
said to them, "From where have you come?"

And they said, "From the land of Canaan, to buy food."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_07.jpg){height="93" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:8*](#Contents.html_rgen42_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Joseph recognized his brothers, but they did not recognize him,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_08.jpg){height="22" width="288"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:9*](#Contents.html_rgen42_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^and Joseph remembered the dreams that he had had about them. And he
said to them, "You're spies. You came to see the land exposed."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_09.jpg){height="70" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:10*](#Contents.html_rgen42_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And they said to him, "No, my lord, your servants came to buy food.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_10.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:11*](#Contents.html_rgen42_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^We're all sons of one man. We're honest men. Your servants weren't
spying."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_11.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:12*](#Contents.html_rgen42_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And he said to them, "No, but you've come to see the land exposed."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_12.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:13*](#Contents.html_rgen42_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And they said, "Your servants are twelve brothers. We're sons of one
man in the land of Canaan, and, here, the youngest is with our father
today, and one is no more."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_13.jpg){height="95" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

42:13. **one is no more**. We must keep in mind that the brothers do not
*know* what happened to Joseph.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:14*](#Contents.html_rgen42_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Joseph said to them, "It's as I spoke to you, saying: you're
spies.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_14.jpg){height="46" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:15*](#Contents.html_rgen42_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^By this you'll be tested: by Pharaoh's life, you won't go out of
here except by your youngest brother's coming here.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_15.jpg){height="68" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:16*](#Contents.html_rgen42_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^Send one from among you, and let him get your brother, and you
remain in prison, and your words will be tested: is the truth with you?
And if not, then by Pharaoh's life you're spies."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_16.jpg){height="71" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:17*](#Contents.html_rgen42_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And he gathered them under watch for three days.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_17.jpg){height="46" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:18*](#Contents.html_rgen42_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Joseph said to them on the third day, "Do this and live, as I
fear God:

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_18.jpg){height="46" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:19*](#Contents.html_rgen42_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^If you're honest, one brother from among you will be held at the
place where you're under watch; and you, go, bring grain for the famine
in your houses.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_19.jpg){height="47" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:20*](#Contents.html_rgen42_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And you will bring your youngest brother to me, and your words will
be confirmed, and you won't die." And they did so.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_20.jpg){height="47" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:21*](#Contents.html_rgen42_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And they said, each to his brother, "But we're guilty over our
brother, because we saw his soul's distress when he implored us and we
didn't listen. On account of that this distress has come to us!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_21.jpg){height="95" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

42:21. **his soul's distress ... this distress has come to us ... and
here was each man's bundle.** Note the pun on
![Image](images/sdot.jpg){.block height="11" width="6"}*rh* (trouble)
and ![Image](images/sdot.jpg){.block height="11" width="6"}*rr*
(bundle), which conveys the irony of the recompense that is happening to
them though they do not yet know it.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:22*](#Contents.html_rgen42_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Reuben answered them, saying, "Didn't I say to you, saying,
'Don't sin against the boy'? And you didn't listen. And his blood, too,
is required here!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_22.jpg){height="71" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:23*](#Contents.html_rgen42_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And they did not know that Joseph was listening, because an
interpreter was between them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_23.jpg){height="22" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:24*](#Contents.html_rgen42_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And he turned from them and wept. And he came back to them and spoke
to them and took Simeon from them and shackled him before their eyes.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_24.jpg){height="71" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

42:24. **Simeon**. Since Reuben, the oldest, had originally opposed
selling Joseph and had not been present at the time of the deed, it was
*Simeon*, the second oldest, who was responsible, so his imprisonment is
a hidden recompense.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:25*](#Contents.html_rgen42_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And Joseph commanded that they fill their containers with grain and
to put their silver back in each man's sack and to give them provisions
for the road. And he did that for them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_25.jpg){height="74" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:26*](#Contents.html_rgen42_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And they loaded their grain on their asses and went from there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_26.jpg){height="22" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:27*](#Contents.html_rgen42_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And one opened his sack to give fodder to his ass at a lodging
place, and he saw his silver, and here it was in the mouth of his bag.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_27.jpg){height="71" width="350"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:28*](#Contents.html_rgen42_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And he said to his brothers, "My silver's been put back, and here it
is in my bag, too."

And their heart went out, and the brothers trembled to one another,
saying, "What is this that God has done to us?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_28.jpg){height="96" width="348"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:29*](#Contents.html_rgen42_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And they came to Jacob, their father, to the land of Canaan, and
told him all the things that happened to them, saying,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_29.jpg){height="66" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:30*](#Contents.html_rgen42_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^"The man, the lord of the land, spoke with us hard and accused us of
spying on the land.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_30.jpg){height="47" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:31*](#Contents.html_rgen42_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And we said to him, 'We're honest. We weren't spying.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_31.jpg){height="43" width="350"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:32*](#Contents.html_rgen42_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^We're twelve brothers, our father's sons; one is no more, and the
youngest is with our father in the land of Canaan today'.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_32.jpg){height="71" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:33*](#Contents.html_rgen42_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And the man, the lord of the land, said to us, 'By this I'll know
that you're honest: leave one brother from among you with me, and take
for the famine in your houses and go,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_33.jpg){height="71" width="350"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:34*](#Contents.html_rgen42_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^and bring your youngest brother to me, so I may know that you aren't
spies, that you're honest. I'll give you your brother, and you'll go
around in the land.'"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_34.jpg){height="94" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:35*](#Contents.html_rgen42_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And it was: they were emptying their sacks, and here was each man's
bundle of silver in his sack, and they saw the bundles of their silver,
they and their father, and they were afraid.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_35.jpg){height="71" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:36*](#Contents.html_rgen42_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And their father, Jacob, said to them, "You've bereaved me! Joseph
is gone, and Simeon's gone, and you'll take Benjamin. All these have
happened to me!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_36.jpg){height="74" width="352"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:37*](#Contents.html_rgen42_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^And Reuben said to his father, saying, "Kill my two sons if I don't
bring him back to you. Put him in my hand, and I'll bring him back to
you."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_37.jpg){height="95" width="348"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 42:38*](#Contents.html_rgen42_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^And he said, "My son will not go down with you. Because his
brother's dead, and he's left by himself, and if some harm would happen
to him on the way in which you're going then you'll bring down my gray
hair in anguish to Sheol."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap42_38.jpg){height="70" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter43.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:1*](#Contents.html_rgen43_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 43 {.h2a}

^1^And the famine was heavy in the land.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_01.jpg){height="20" width="191"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:2*](#Contents.html_rgen43_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And it was when they had finished eating the grain that they had
brought from Egypt: and their father said to them, "Go back. Buy us a
little food."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_02.jpg){height="95" width="361"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:3*](#Contents.html_rgen43_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Judah said to him, saying, "The man *certified* to us, saying,
'You won't see my face unless your brother is with you.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_03.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:4*](#Contents.html_rgen43_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^If you're sending our brother with us, we'll go down and buy food for
you,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_04.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:5*](#Contents.html_rgen43_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^and if you're not sending, we won't go down, because the man said to
us, 'You won't see my face unless your brother is with you.'"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_05.jpg){height="52" width="350"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:6*](#Contents.html_rgen43_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Israel said, "Why have you done me wrong, to tell the man that
you have another brother?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_06.jpg){height="92" width="360"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:7*](#Contents.html_rgen43_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And they said, "The man *asked* about us and about our birthplace,
saying, 'Is your father still alive? Do you have a brother?' And we told
him about these things. Could we have *known* that he would say, 'Bring
your brother down'?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_07.jpg){height="99" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:8*](#Contents.html_rgen43_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Judah said to Israel, his father, "Send the boy with me, so we
may get up and go, and we'll live and not die, we and you and our
infants as well.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_08.jpg){height="93" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:9*](#Contents.html_rgen43_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^I'll be security for him. You'll seek him from my hand. If I don't
bring him to you and set him before you, then I'll have sinned against
you for all time.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_09.jpg){height="96" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:10*](#Contents.html_rgen43_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^For, if we hadn't delayed, by now we would have come back twice."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_10.jpg){height="51" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:11*](#Contents.html_rgen43_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Israel, their father, said to them, "If that's how it is, then
do this: Take some of the best fruit of the land in your containers and
take a gift down to the man, a little balm and a little honey, gum and
myrrh, pistachios and almonds.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_11.jpg){height="100" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:12*](#Contents.html_rgen43_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And take double the silver in your hand, and take back in your hand
the silver that was put back in the mouth of your bags. Maybe it was a
mistake.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_12.jpg){height="49" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:13*](#Contents.html_rgen43_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And take your brother. And get up, go back to the man.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_13.jpg){height="26" width="299"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:14*](#Contents.html_rgen43_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And may El Shadday give you mercy before the man, so he'll send your
other brother and Benjamin to you. And I: if I'm bereaved I'm bereaved!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_14.jpg){height="103" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:15*](#Contents.html_rgen43_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And the men took this gift and took double the silver in their hand
and Benjamin and got up and went down to Egypt and stood before Joseph.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_15.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:16*](#Contents.html_rgen43_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And Joseph saw Benjamin with them and said to the one who was over
his house, "Bring the men to the house, and slaughter and prepare an
animal, because the men will eat with me at noon."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_16.jpg){height="119" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:17*](#Contents.html_rgen43_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And the man did as Joseph said, and the man brought the men to
Joseph's house.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_17.jpg){height="53" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:18*](#Contents.html_rgen43_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And the men were afraid because they were brought to Joseph's house,
and they said, "We're being brought on account of the silver that came
back in our bags the first time, in order to roll over us and to fall
upon us and to take us as slaves---and our asses."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_18.jpg){height="125" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:19*](#Contents.html_rgen43_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And they went over to the man who was over Joseph's house and spoke
to him at the entrance of the house

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_19.jpg){height="50" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:20*](#Contents.html_rgen43_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^and said, "Please, my lord, we *came down* the first time to buy
food,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_20.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:21*](#Contents.html_rgen43_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^and it was when we came to the lodging place, and we opened our
bags, and here was each man's silver in the mouth of his bag, our money
in its full weight. And we've brought it in our hand,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_21.jpg){height="74" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:22*](#Contents.html_rgen43_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^and we've brought down additional silver in our hand to buy food. We
don't know who put our silver in our bags."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_22.jpg){height="46" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:23*](#Contents.html_rgen43_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And he said, "Peace to you. Don't be afraid. Your God and your
father's God put treasure in your bags for you. Your silver came to me."
And he brought Simeon out to them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_23.jpg){height="102" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:24*](#Contents.html_rgen43_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And the man brought the men to Joseph's house, and he gave water,
and they washed their feet, and he gave fodder for their asses.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_24.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:25*](#Contents.html_rgen43_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And they prepared the gift until Joseph's arrival at noon because
they heard that they would eat bread there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_25.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:26*](#Contents.html_rgen43_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And Joseph came to the house, and they brought the gift for him that
was in their hand to the house, and they bowed to him to the ground.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_26.jpg){height="52" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:27*](#Contents.html_rgen43_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And he asked if they were well, and he said, "Is your old father
whom you mentioned well? Is he still alive?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_27.jpg){height="76" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

43:27. **he asked if they were well**. Recall that the brothers were
described earlier as being unable to speak to Joseph *of
š![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}lôm*, that
is, to say "hello" or "how are you?" (see the comment on 37:4). Now they
and Joseph finally speak of *š![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block
height="11" width="8"}lbm* (vv. 23,27--28). Even though it is unwitting,
it is the beginning of their reconciliation with their brother.

43:27. **Is he still alive?** Here is an exceptional example of the
emotional power that looms in the background in the Torah's stories.
From the point of view of the brothers and the Egyptians who are
present, Joseph is just making polite conversation, graciously asking
about "your old father whom you mentioned." But, inside, Joseph is about
to find out---with anticipation, dread, even guilt?---whether his own
father, who loved him the most, is alive or dead.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:28*](#Contents.html_rgen43_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And they said, "Your servant, our father, is well. He's still
alive." And they knelt and bowed.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_28.jpg){height="68" width="359"}

![Image](images/43page_144.jpg){height="25" width="106"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

43:28. **He's still alive**. In the Septuagint and Samaritan, Joseph
responds to this news about Jacob. They read: "And he said, 'Blessed of
God is that man.'" This line presumably was lost from the Masoretic Text
as a result of haplography when a scribe's eye jumped from the *ivy* at
the beginning of *wayy'![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11"
width="7"}mer* to the beginning of
*wayyiqq![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}d![Image](images/u.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}*.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:29*](#Contents.html_rgen43_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And he raised his eyes and saw Benjamin, his brother, his mother's
son, and said, "Is this your youngest brother whom you mentioned to me?"
And he said, "May God be gracious to you, my son."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_29.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:30*](#Contents.html_rgen43_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

boiling, and he looked for a place to weep and came to his room and wept
there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_30.jpg){height="22" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:31*](#Contents.html_rgen43_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And he washed his face and went out and restrained himself and said,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_31.jpg){height="51" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:32*](#Contents.html_rgen43_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

and for the Egyptians who were eating with him by themselves, because

offensive thing to Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_32.jpg){height="100" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:33*](#Contents.html_rgen43_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And they sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright
and the youngest according to his youth. And the men looked amazed at
one another.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_33.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 43:34*](#Contents.html_rgen43_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And he conveyed portions from before him to them, and he made

![Image](images/Gen_Chap43_34.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter44.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:1*](#Contents.html_rgen44_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 44 {.h2a}

^1^And he commanded the one who was over his house, saying, "Fill the
men's bags with as much food as they can carry and put each man's silver
in the mouth of his bag,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_01.jpg){height="76" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

44:1. **each man's silver**. The first time, when ten brothers come to
Egypt, Joseph imprisons Simeon, and he has their silver placed back in
their *nine* sacks. The second time, when they return with Benjamin,
Joseph releases Simeon, and he has their silver placed back in their
*eleven* sacks. The total number of portions of silver returned is
*twenty*, corresponding to the price that was paid for Joseph (37:28).
It is yet another case of a hidden link between acts of deception and
their payback in later events.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:2*](#Contents.html_rgen44_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^and put my cup, the silver cup, in the mouth of the youngest one's
bag, and the silver for his grain." And he did according to Joseph's
word that he spoke.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_02.jpg){height="93" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:3*](#Contents.html_rgen44_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^The morning was light: and the men had been sent away, they and their
asses.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_03.jpg){height="52" width="343"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:4*](#Contents.html_rgen44_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

you'll catch up with them and say to them, 'Why did you pay back bad for
good?

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_04.jpg){height="99" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_16.jpg){height="101" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

## A[ND HE WENT OVER]{.smallcaps1} {.h2}

^18^And Judah went over to him and said, "Please, my lord, let your
servant speak something in my lord's ears, and let your anger not flare
at your servant, because you're like Pharaoh himself.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44.jpg){height="22" width="49"}

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_18.jpg){height="77" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:20*](#Contents.html_rgen44_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
