![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_20.jpg){height="76" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:21*](#Contents.html_rgen44_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And you said to your servants, 'Bring him down to me, so I may set
my eye on him.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_21.jpg){height="54" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:22*](#Contents.html_rgen44_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And we said to my lord, The boy can't leave his father; if he left
his father he'd die.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_22.jpg){height="50" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:23*](#Contents.html_rgen44_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And you said to your servants, 'If your youngest brother doesn't
come down with you, you won't see my face again.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_23.jpg){height="72" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:24*](#Contents.html_rgen44_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And it was, when we went up to your servant, my father, and told him
my lord's words,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_24.jpg){height="50" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:25*](#Contents.html_rgen44_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^that our father said, 'Go back. Buy us a little food.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_25.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:26*](#Contents.html_rgen44_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And we said, 'We can't go down. If our youngest brother is with us
then we'll go down, because we can't see the man's face if our youngest
brother isn't with us.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_26.jpg){height="77" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:27*](#Contents.html_rgen44_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And your servant, my father, said to us, 'You know that my wife gave
birth to two for me,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_27.jpg){height="48" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:28*](#Contents.html_rgen44_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^and one went away from me, and I said he's surely *torn up*, and I
haven't seen him since.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_28.jpg){height="51" width="343"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

44:28; **since**. It is hard to convey the irony in the wording in
English translation. Jacob says, "I haven't seen him since" (literally,
"up to here," ![Image](images/page147-1.jpg){.block height="12"
width="54"} and later Joseph says, "You didn't send me here"
![Image](images/page147-2.jpg){.block height="12" width="25"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:29*](#Contents.html_rgen44_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And if you take this one from me as well and some harm happens to
him, then you'll bring down my gray hair in wretchedness to Sheol.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_29.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:30*](#Contents.html_rgen44_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And now, when I come to your servant, my father, and the boy isn't
with us, and he's bound to him soul to soul,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_30.jpg){height="46" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:31*](#Contents.html_rgen44_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^it will be, when he sees that the boy isn't there, that he'll die.
And your servants will have brought down your servant our father's gray
hair in anguish to Sheol,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_31.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:32*](#Contents.html_rgen44_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^because your servant offered security for the boy to my father,
saying, 'If I don't bring him to you, then I'll have sinned against my
father for all time.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_32.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:33*](#Contents.html_rgen44_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And now, let your servant stay as my lord's servant in place of the
boy, and let the boy go up with his brothers---

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_33.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 44:34*](#Contents.html_rgen44_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^for how could I go up to my father and the boy isn't with me---or
else I'll see the wretchedness that will find my father."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap44_34.jpg){height="68" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter45.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:1*](#Contents.html_rgen45_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 45 {.h2a}

^1^And Joseph was not able to restrain himself in front of everyone who
was standing by him, and he called, "Take everyone out from my
presence." And not a man stood with him when Joseph made himself known
to his brothers.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_01.jpg){height="113" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

45:1. **not able to restrain himself**. He has toyed with his brothers
long enough. Judah's words, "the wretchedness that will find my father,"
are more than Joseph can bear, and so at last he reveals himself to
them.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:2*](#Contents.html_rgen45_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And he wept out loud. And Egypt heard, and Pharaoh's house heard.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_02.jpg){height="66" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:3*](#Contents.html_rgen45_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Joseph said to his brothers, "I'm Joseph. Is my father still
alive?" And his brothers were not able to answer him, because they were
terrified in front of him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_03.jpg){height="90" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

45:3. **Is my father still alive?** But he *knows* that his father is
still alive. He already asked his brothers this (43:27). Even the major
traditional commentators do not address this. In critical scholarship it
is understood that these two seemingly conflicting passages come from
two different sources. But even if this is so, how shall we understand
Joseph's question in the context of the narrative as it stands? It seems
to me that the key is that when he asks them the first time, it is in
the role that he is playing as an Egyptian official, with his real
identity hidden from them. But now he puts together his revelation of
who he really is with his question about Jacob: "I'm Joseph. Is my
father still alive?" For all he knows, his brothers were lying to him as
the Egyptian official, but now he asks them: tell me, your brother,
Joseph, is he really alive?

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:4*](#Contents.html_rgen45_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Joseph said to his brothers, "Come over to me." And they went
over. And he said, "I'm Joseph, your brother, whom you sold to Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_04.jpg){height="93" width="357"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:5*](#Contents.html_rgen45_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And now, don't be sad and let there be no anger in your eyes because
you sold me here, because God sent me before you to preserve life.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_05.jpg){height="82" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:6*](#Contents.html_rgen45_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^For it's two years that the famine is in the land, and for five more
years there'll be no plowing and harvest.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_06.jpg){height="56" width="351"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:7*](#Contents.html_rgen45_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And God sent me ahead of you to provide a remnant for you in the
earth and to keep you alive as a big, surviving community.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_07.jpg){height="62" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:8*](#Contents.html_rgen45_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And now, it wasn't you who sent me here, but God, and he made me into
a father to Pharaoh and a lord to all his house and a ruler in all the
land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_08.jpg){height="107" width="355"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

45:8. **it wasn't you who sent me here, but God**. Is this the same
Joseph who spoke so naively to his brothers about his dreams at the
beginning of these events? Like the character of his father Jacob,
Joseph's character develops through the events of his life. Here,
immediately after revealing himself to them, he goes into a chain of
assurances, responding to every fear they would have: come close to me;
don't be sad or angry or afraid of me; it wasn't your fault; I'll
provide for you during the famine; your families will be safe; bring our
father. He has become a wise, sensitive man. And, like his father, the
change in him came during the period in which he had contact with the
divine, when he was given the divine power to interpret dreams.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:9*](#Contents.html_rgen45_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^Hurry, and go up to my father and say to him: 'Your son Joseph said
this: God has made me a lord to all Egypt. Come down to me. Don't stand
back.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_09.jpg){height="79" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:10*](#Contents.html_rgen45_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And you'll live in the land of Goshen, and you'll be close to me,
you and your children and your children's children and your flock and
your oxen and everything you have.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_10.jpg){height="89" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:11*](#Contents.html_rgen45_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And I'll provide for you there, because there are five more years of
famine, or else you and your house and everything you have will be
impoverished.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_11.jpg){height="60" width="352"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:12*](#Contents.html_rgen45_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And here your eyes see, and the eyes of my brother Benjamin, that
it's my mouth that speaks to you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_12.jpg){height="59" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:13*](#Contents.html_rgen45_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And you'll tell my father of all my glory in Egypt and of all that
you've seen, and hurry and bring my father down here."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_13.jpg){height="87" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:14*](#Contents.html_rgen45_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And he fell on his brother Benjamin's neck and wept, and Benjamin
wept on his neck.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_14.jpg){height="57" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:15*](#Contents.html_rgen45_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And he kissed all his brothers and wept over them. And after that
his brothers spoke with him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_15.jpg){height="82" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:16*](#Contents.html_rgen45_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And the report was heard at Pharaoh's house, saying, "Joseph's
brothers have come." And it was good in Pharaoh's eyes and in his

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_16.jpg){height="60" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:17*](#Contents.html_rgen45_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Say to your brothers, 'Do this: load
your beasts and go; come to the land of Canaan,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_17.jpg){height="80" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:18*](#Contents.html_rgen45_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^and take your father and your households and come to me, and I'll
give you the best of the land of Egypt; and eat the fat of the land.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_18.jpg){height="82" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:19*](#Contents.html_rgen45_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And you are commanded, 'Do this: Take wagons from the land of Egypt
for your infants and for your wives, and carry your father and come.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_19.jpg){height="82" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:20*](#Contents.html_rgen45_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And let your eye not care about your possessions, for the best of
all the land of Egypt is yours.'"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_20.jpg){height="62" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:21*](#Contents.html_rgen45_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And the children of Israel did so. And Joseph gave them wagons by
order of Pharaoh and gave them provisions for the road.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_21.jpg){height="85" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Genesis 45:22*](#Contents.html_rgen45_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_22.jpg){height="67" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 45:23*](#Contents.html_rgen45_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Genesis 45:25*](#Contents.html_rgen45_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/Gen_Chap45_27.jpg){height="107" width="338"}

[]{#Chapter46.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 46:1*](#Contents.html_rgen46_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 46 {.h2a}

^1^And Israel and everyone he had traveled and came to Beer-sheba, and
he offered sacrifices to the God of his father, Isaac.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap46_01.jpg){height="52" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 46:2*](#Contents.html_rgen46_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And God said to Israel in night visions, and He said, "Jacob, Jacob."

And he said, "I'm here."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap46_02.jpg){height="76" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

46:2. **"Jacob, Jacob." And he said, "I'm here."** This is the second of
three times that this pattern occurs. When Abraham is about to sacrifice
Isaac, an angel calls, "Abraham! Abraham!" And he said, "I'm here"
(22:11). There it is to save a life. Here it is to assure Jacob that he
has nothing to fear in Egypt. Both cases are about reunions of
patriarchs with their sons. And this forms the background to the third
time the pattern will occur. When Moses comes to the burning bush, God
says, "Moses, Moses." And he said, "I'm here" (Exod 3:4). Like Abraham,
Moses is told that Abraham's children are to be saved. Like Jacob, he is
taught not to be afraid. Like both, this is about reunion of the people
with the land where the patriarchs are (see comment on Gen. 22:1).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 46:3*](#Contents.html_rgen46_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And He said, "I am God, your father's God. Don't be afraid of going
down to Egypt, because I'll make you into a big nation there.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap46_03.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 46:4*](#Contents.html_rgen46_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^*I* shall go down with you to Egypt, and *I* shall also *bring you
up*. And Joseph will set his hand on your eyes."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap46_04.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 46:5*](#Contents.html_rgen46_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Jacob, their father, and their infants and their wives in the wagons

![Image](images/Gen_Chap46_05.jpg){height="93" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Genesis 46:28*](#Contents.html_rgen46_28)

[*Genesis 46:29*](#Contents.html_rgen46_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And Joseph hitched his chariot and went up to Israel, his father, at

![Image](images/Gen_Chap46_29.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 46:30*](#Contents.html_rgen46_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/Gen_Chap46_30.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 46:31*](#Contents.html_rgen46_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And Joseph said to his brothers and to his father's house, "Let me
go up and tell Pharaoh and say to him, 'My brothers and my father's
house that were in the land of Canaan have come to me.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap46_31.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 46:32*](#Contents.html_rgen46_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And the people are shepherds, because they were livestock people,
and they've brought their flock and their oxen and all they have.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap46_32.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 46:33*](#Contents.html_rgen46_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And it will be, when Pharaoh will call you and say, 'What is your
occupation?'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap46_33.jpg){height="45" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 46:34*](#Contents.html_rgen46_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^that you'll say, 'Your servants were livestock people from our youth
until now, both we and our fathers,' so that you'll live in the land of
Goshen, because any shepherd is an offensive thing to Egypt."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap46_34.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter47.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:1*](#Contents.html_rgen47_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 47 {.h2a}

^1^And Joseph came and told Pharaoh and said, "My father and my brothers
and their flock and their oxen and all that they own have come from the
land of Canaan, and here they are in the land of Goshen."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_01.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:2*](#Contents.html_rgen47_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And he took several of his brothers, five men, and set them before
Pharaoh.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_02.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:3*](#Contents.html_rgen47_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Pharaoh said to his brothers, "What is your work?"

And they said to Pharaoh, "Your servants are shepherds, both we and our
fathers,"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_03.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

47:3. **Your servants are shepherds**. Joseph had just told them not to
answer Pharaoh that they are shepherds (46:33--34), yet they go ahead
and say it! Some might surmise that this seeming contradiction is the
result of the combination of two sources into one story. But that is not
correct. This has nothing to do with the sources that are identified in
critical biblical scholarship. This is one continuous passage (all from
the source known in scholarship as J). The difficulty in the brothers'
words must therefore be understood as being a part of the story itself.
The brothers simply do not follow Joseph's instructions. He has told
them to say that they are cowherds, not shepherds, because Egyptians
disdain shepherds; but they are not willing to misrepresent themselves
in this way. Are they right? On one hand, Pharaoh does permit them to
settle and offers them the best of Egypt's land. But, on the other hand,
Pharaoh stops speaking directly to them. He switches to speaking about
them in the third person to Joseph. And he offers to have some of them
serve as officers over his *cattle* even though they have just said they
are shepherds, not cattlemen.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:4*](#Contents.html_rgen47_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^and they said to Pharaoh, "We came to reside in the land because
there's no pasture for your servants' flock because the famine is heavy
in the land of Canaan. And now may your servants live in the land of
Goshen."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_04.jpg){height="104" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:5*](#Contents.html_rgen47_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Pharaoh said to Joseph, saying, "Your father and your brothers
have come to you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_05.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:6*](#Contents.html_rgen47_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^The land of Egypt is before you. Settle your father and your brothers
in the best of the land. Let them live in the land of Goshen. And if you
know---and if there are among them---worthy men, then you shall make
them livestock officers over those that I have."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_06.jpg){height="99" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:7*](#Contents.html_rgen47_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Joseph brought Jacob, his father, and stood him in front of
Pharaoh. And Jacob blessed Pharaoh.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_07.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:8*](#Contents.html_rgen47_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Pharaoh said to Jacob, "How many are the days of the years of
your life?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_08.jpg){height="69" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:9*](#Contents.html_rgen47_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Jacob said to Pharaoh, "The days of the years of my residences
are a hundred thirty years. The days of the years of my life have been
few and bad, and they haven't attained the days of the years of my
fathers' lives, in the days of their residences."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_09.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

47:9. **few and bad, and ... haven't attained**. Jacob is critical of
his life, in itself and in comparison with Abraham's and Isaac's lives.
This is consistent with the telling of the story until now, which has
not excused or sanitized Jacob the way some Sunday schools, early
commentaries, and modern interpreters do. The biblical writers seem to
have been quite content to leave their heroes imperfect. Why? Why even
conceive of such a story? Perhaps the author thus pointed to deception
in the world and said, "It comes back." Or perhaps the author conceived
it in literary protest against the ancient Near Eastern practice of
glorifying national heroes. As has frequently been observed, to read the
ancient reports one would think that no Near Eastern king ever lost a
battle. A clear majority of the biblical authors, however, show their
heroes with weaknesses and imperfections, making errors and committing
offenses. For whatever purpose the author of the Jacob cycle conceived
this story, the fact is that this author exhibited an historical,
realistic impulse, a sense of the psychological complexity of families:
sibling rivalry, fathers and sons in conflict, mothers finding channels
of influence in male family structures, women torn between fathers and
husbands. Further, it may be said that by not glorifying its human
heroes the text glorifies its other central figure, the deity. The
message here may well be that God can work through anyone: through an
all-obedient man, a passive, dim-eyed patriarch, or a deceiver.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:10*](#Contents.html_rgen47_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Jacob blessed Pharaoh and went out from in front of Pharaoh.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_10.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:11*](#Contents.html_rgen47_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Joseph settled his father and his brothers and gave them a
possession in the land of Egypt, in the best of the land, in the land of
Rameses, as Pharaoh had commanded.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_11.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:12*](#Contents.html_rgen47_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And Joseph supported his father and his brothers and all of his
father's household, bread by the number of infants.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_12.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:13*](#Contents.html_rgen47_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And there was no bread in all the land of Egypt, because the famine
was very heavy, and the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan languished
because of the famine.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_13.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:14*](#Contents.html_rgen47_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Joseph collected all the silver that was found in the land of
Egypt and in the land of Canaan for the grain that they were buying, and
Joseph brought the silver to Pharaoh's house.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_14.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:15*](#Contents.html_rgen47_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And the silver came to an end from the land of Egypt and from the
land of Canaan. And all of Egypt came to Joseph, saying, "Give us bread!
And why should we die in front of you? Because there's no more silver!"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_15.jpg){height="97" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:16*](#Contents.html_rgen47_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And Joseph said, "Give your livestock, and I'll give it to you for
your livestock if there's no more silver."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_16.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:17*](#Contents.html_rgen47_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And they brought their livestock to Joseph, and Joseph gave them
bread for the horses and for the livestock of the flocks and for the
livestock of the oxen and for the asses, and he sustained them with
bread for all their livestock in that year.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_17.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:18*](#Contents.html_rgen47_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And that year came to an end, and they came to him in the second
year and said to him, "We won't conceal from my lord that the silver has
come to an end and the cattle livestock have gone to my lord. Nothing is
left in front of my lord except our body and our land.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_18.jpg){height="99" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:19*](#Contents.html_rgen47_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^Why should we die before your eyes, both we and our land? Buy us and
our land for bread, and we and our land will be servants to Pharaoh; and
give seed so we'll live and not die, and the land won't be devastated."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_19.jpg){height="127" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:20*](#Contents.html_rgen47_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Joseph bought all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh, because Egypt,
each man, sold his field, because the famine was strong on them. And the
land became Pharaoh's.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_20.jpg){height="104" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:21*](#Contents.html_rgen47_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And the people: he moved them to cities, from one edge of Egypt's
border to its other edge.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_21.jpg){height="49" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:22*](#Contents.html_rgen47_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^Only the priests' land he did not buy, because it was a law for the
priests from Pharaoh, and they ate their statutory share that Pharaoh
had given them. On account of this they did not sell their land.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_22.jpg){height="76" width="363"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:23*](#Contents.html_rgen47_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And Joseph said to the people, "Here, I've bought you and your land
for Pharaoh today. Look: seed for you. And you'll sow the land;

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_23.jpg){height="99" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:24*](#Contents.html_rgen47_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^and it will be, at the harvests, that you'll give a fifth to
Pharaoh, and the four parts will be yours for field seed and for you to
eat and for whoever is in your households and for your infants to eat."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_24.jpg){height="78" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:25*](#Contents.html_rgen47_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And they said, "You've kept us alive! Let us find favor in my lord's
eyes, and we'll be servants to Pharaoh."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_25.jpg){height="47" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:26*](#Contents.html_rgen47_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And Joseph set it as a law to this day on Egypt's land: to Pharaoh
the fifth. Only the land of the priests alone was not Pharaoh's.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_26.jpg){height="75" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:27*](#Contents.html_rgen47_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And Israel lived in Egypt in the land of Goshen, and they held
property in it. And they were fruitful and multiplied very much.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_27.jpg){height="75" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:28*](#Contents.html_rgen47_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## A[ND HE LIVED]{.smallcaps1} {.h2}

^28^And Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years. And Jacob's
days, the years of his life, were seven years and a hundred forty years.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47.jpg){height="43" width="67"}

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_28.jpg){height="74" width="358"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:29*](#Contents.html_rgen47_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And Israel's days to die drew close. And he called his son, Joseph,
and said to him, "If I've found favor in your eyes, place your hand
under my thigh and practice kindness and faithfulness with me: don't
bury me in Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_29.jpg){height="101" width="362"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:30*](#Contents.html_rgen47_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And I'll lie with my fathers, and you'll carry me out of Egypt and
bury me in their burial place."

And he said, "I'll do according to your word."

And he said, "Swear to me."

And he swore to him. And Israel bowed at the head of the bed.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_30.jpg){height="50" width="361"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 47:31*](#Contents.html_rgen47_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/Gen_Chap47_31.jpg){height="50" width="361"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Chapter48.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:1*](#Contents.html_rgen48_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 48 {.h2a}

^1^And it was after these things, and one said to Joseph, "Here, your
father is sick." And he took his two sons with him, Man-asseh and
Ephraim.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_01.jpg){height="80" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:2*](#Contents.html_rgen48_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And one told Jacob and said, "Here, your son Joseph is coming to
you." And Israel fortified himself and sat up on the bed.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_02.jpg){height="58" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:3*](#Contents.html_rgen48_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Jacob said to Joseph, "El Shadday appeared to me in Luz in the
land of Canaan, and He blessed me

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_03.jpg){height="61" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:4*](#Contents.html_rgen48_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^and said to me, 'Here, I'm making you fruitful and multiplying you,
and I'll make you into a community of peoples, and I'll give this land
to your seed after you, an eternal possession.'

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_04.jpg){height="79" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

48:4. **I'm making you fruitful and multiplying you, and I'll make you**
... But God did not say, "*I'll make you* fruitful..." God said, *"Be*
fruitful ..." (35:11). Why does Jacob tell Joseph that God promised to
do it when God actually told *him* to do it? Perhaps it is because Jacob
has only one more son (Benjamin) after God tells him this, and so the
becoming fruitful must refer to the births of his grandchildren and
great-grandchildren. Jacob would not see this as in his power but rather
as God's doing, and so he understands God's words not as a command but
as a promise and a blessing.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:5*](#Contents.html_rgen48_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And now, your two sons who were born to you in the land of Egypt by
my arrival to you at Egypt: they're mine. Ephraim and Man-asseh will be
like Reuben and Simeon to me.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_05.jpg){height="109" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:6*](#Contents.html_rgen48_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And your offspring that you'll have after them will be yours. They
shall be called by their brothers' names with regard to their
inheritance.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_06.jpg){height="59" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:7*](#Contents.html_rgen48_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And I: when I was coming from Paddan Aram, Rachel died by me in the
land of Canaan on the way when there was still a span of land to come to
Ephrat, and I buried her there on the Ephrat road. That's Bethlehem."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_07.jpg){height="106" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:8*](#Contents.html_rgen48_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Israel saw Joseph's sons and said, "Who are these?"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_08.jpg){height="37" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:9*](#Contents.html_rgen48_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Joseph said to his father, "They're my sons, whom God has given
me here."

And he said, "Bring them to me, and I'll bless them."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_09.jpg){height="90" width="356"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:10*](#Contents.html_rgen48_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Israel's eyes were heavy from old age. He was not able to see.
And he brought them close to him, and he kissed them and embraced them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_10.jpg){height="87" width="356"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

48:10. **Israel's eyes were heavy from old age**. The analogy to his
father is obvious. When Isaac had been old, on his deathbed, unable to
see, Jacob had come and appropriated his brother Esau's blessing. Now
Jacob himself is old, on his deathbed, unable to see, and he favors the
younger son, Ephraim, in his blessing.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:11*](#Contents.html_rgen48_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Israel said to Joseph, "I didn't expect to see your face; and,
here, God has shown me your seed as well."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_11.jpg){height="57" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:12*](#Contents.html_rgen48_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And Joseph brought them out from between his knees, and he bowed,
his nose to the ground.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_12.jpg){height="62" width="354"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:13*](#Contents.html_rgen48_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Joseph took the two of them, Ephraim in his right hand, at
Israel's left; and Manasseh in his left hand, at Israel's right; and he
brought them over to him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_13.jpg){height="87" width="356"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:14*](#Contents.html_rgen48_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Israel put out his right hand and placed it on Ephraim's
head---and he was the younger---and his left hand on Manasseh's head. He
crossed his hands---because Manasseh was the firstborn.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_14.jpg){height="84" width="355"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:15*](#Contents.html_rgen48_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And he blessed Joseph and said, "The God before whom my fathers,
Abraham and Isaac, walked, who shepherded me from my start to this day,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_15.jpg){height="101" width="326"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

48:15. **before whom my fathers walked**. Note here and in Gen 6:9 and
17:1 that this is technical covenant terminology known from ancient Near
Eastern documents to mean loyalty to one's partner in a covenant.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:16*](#Contents.html_rgen48_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^the angel who redeemed me from all bad, may He bless the boys. And
may my name be called on them, and the name of my fathers, Abraham and
Isaac. And may they spawn into a great number within the earth."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_16.jpg){height="104" width="310"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:17*](#Contents.html_rgen48_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And Joseph saw that his father had placed his right hand on
Ephraim's head, and it was bad in his eyes, and he held up his father's
hand to turn it from on Ephraim's head onto Manasseh's head,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_17.jpg){height="80" width="348"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:18*](#Contents.html_rgen48_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^and Joseph said to his father, "Not like that, my father, because
this is the firstborn. Set your right hand on his head."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_18.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:19*](#Contents.html_rgen48_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And his father refused and said, "I know, my son, I know. He, too,
will become a people; and he, too, will be great. But in fact his little
brother will be greater than he, and his seed will be full-fledged of
nations."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_19.jpg){height="82" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

48:19. **full-fledged of nations**. The unusual phrase
*m![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}l![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11"
width="7"}'-hagg![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11" width="7"}yim*
is usually taken to mean a multitude of nations, but that makes no
particular sense in terms of the fate of the single tribe of Ephraim.
Elsewhere *m![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}l![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11" width="7"}'* can
mean a full unit among a group (as in 2 Sam 8:2). It may mean here that
Ephraim will be thought of as a nation, for Ephraim later comes to
dominate the kingdom of Israel, and the name Ephraim is sometimes used
to refer to the entire Israelite kingdom (Isa 7:2--17; Hos 5:3; 6:10;
7:1).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:20*](#Contents.html_rgen48_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And he blessed them in that day, saying, "Israel will bless with
you, saying: 'May God make you like Ephraim and like Manasseh.'" And he
set Ephraim before Manasseh.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_20.jpg){height="111" width="251"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 48:21*](#Contents.html_rgen48_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Israel said to Joseph, "Here, I'm dying. And God will be with
you and will bring you back to your fathers' land. And I've given you
one shoulder over your brothers, which I took from the Amorite's hand
with my sword and my bow."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap48_21.jpg){height="83" width="359"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

48:21. **one shoulder over your brothers**. This expression appears to
refer to Joseph's getting two tribes while each of his brothers gets
only one. But it also puns on the word for shoulder, Hebrew *š
![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12" width="7"}kem*
(Shechem). Shechem is the name of the city that will one day be the
capital of the kingdom of Israel, and it is located in one of the Joseph
tribes (Manasseh).

[]{#Chapter49.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:1*](#Contents.html_rgen49_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 49 {.h2a}

^1^And Jacob called to his sons and said: Gather, and I'll tell you what
will happen to you in the future days.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_01.jpg){height="71" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:2*](#Contents.html_rgen49_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^Assemble and listen, sons of Jacob, and listen to Israel, your
father.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_02.jpg){height="25" width="372"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:3*](#Contents.html_rgen49_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^Reuben, you're my firstborn, my power, and the beginning of my might,
preeminent in bearing and preeminent in strength.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_03.jpg){height="45" width="273"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:4*](#Contents.html_rgen49_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^Unstable as water, you'll not be preeminent, for you ascended your
father's bed; then you defiled, going up to my couch.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_04.jpg){height="46" width="318"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:5*](#Contents.html_rgen49_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^Simeon and Levi are brothers: Implements of violence are their tools
of trade.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_05.jpg){height="22" width="279"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:6*](#Contents.html_rgen49_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^Let my soul not come in their council; let my glory not be united in
their society. For in their anger they killed a man, and by their will
they crippled an ox.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_06.jpg){height="44" width="314"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:7*](#Contents.html_rgen49_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^Cursed is their anger, for it's strong, and their wrath, for it's
hard. I'll divide them in Jacob, and I'll scatter them in Israel.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_07.jpg){height="47" width="270"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:8*](#Contents.html_rgen49_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^Judah: You, your brothers will praise you. Your hand on your enemies'
neck, your father's sons will bow to you.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_08.jpg){height="47" width="310"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

49:8. **Judah**. Reuben is demoted from firstborn preeminence (49:4);
Simeon and Levi are condemned to be scattered (49:7). The fourth-born
Judah is the one to receive praise, success, and dominion. The
fulfillment will come when Judah becomes the largest of the tribes, and
its royal family, the kings descended from David, rule for centuries and
hold the messianic promise. Judah was the brother who saved Joseph's
life, who promised to be the protector of Benjamin, whose relationship
with Tamar resulted in the birth of the clan of the future kings of
Israel. The patriarchs will all be buried in Hebron, the capital city of
the tribe of Judah. Here at the end of Genesis we find the denouement of
the stories of the brothers, and we are made aware of their significance
for the future.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:9*](#Contents.html_rgen49_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^A lion's whelp is Judah; from prey, my son, you've risen. He bent,
crouched, like a lion; and, like a feline, who will rouse him?

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_09.jpg){height="46" width="261"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:10*](#Contents.html_rgen49_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^The scepter won't depart from Judah or a ruler from between his legs
until he comes to Shiloh, and peoples' obedience is his.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_10.jpg){height="53" width="312"}

![Image](images/49page_159.jpg){height="25" width="83"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:11*](#Contents.html_rgen49_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^Tying his ass to the vine and his she-ass's foal to the choice vine,
he washed in wine his clothing and in blood of grapes his garment,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_11.jpg){height="46" width="264"}

![Image](images/49page_159b.jpg){height="52" width="87"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:12*](#Contents.html_rgen49_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^eyes darker than wine and teeth whiter than milk.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_12.jpg){height="22" width="303"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:13*](#Contents.html_rgen49_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^Zebulun will dwell by seashores: and he'll be a shore for boats, and
his border at Sidon.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_13.jpg){height="47" width="307"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:14*](#Contents.html_rgen49_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^Issachar is a strong ass crouching between the saddle-packs:

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_14.jpg){height="41" width="300"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:15*](#Contents.html_rgen49_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^and he saw rest, that it was good, and the land, that it was
pleasant, and he leaned his shoulder to bear and became a work-company
servant.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_15.jpg){height="44" width="295"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:16*](#Contents.html_rgen49_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^Dan will judge his people as one of the tribes of Israel:

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_16.jpg){height="22" width="251"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

49:16. **as one of the tribes of Israel**. David Noel Freedman says: "as
one of the tribes of Israel" is too banal to merit utterance.
'*![Image](images/hdot.jpg){.block height="15" width="7"}d* must mean
"first" as in the opening chapter of Genesis. Now why should Dan be
first among the tribes? Because it is always mentioned first, when
anyone describes the territory of Israel: from Dan to Beer-sheba.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:17*](#Contents.html_rgen49_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^Dan will be a snake on a road, a venomous snake on a path, that
bites a horse's heels, and its rider falls backward.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_17.jpg){height="46" width="283"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:18*](#Contents.html_rgen49_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^I wait for your salvation, YHWH.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_18.jpg){height="29" width="168"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:19*](#Contents.html_rgen49_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^Gad: a troop will trap him, and he'll trap their heel.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_19.jpg){height="19" width="261"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:20*](#Contents.html_rgen49_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^Asher: his bread will be rich, and he'll provide a king's delights.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_20.jpg){height="22" width="314"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

49:20. **Asher**. The MT says *"from* Asher," but that is a scribal
mistake. The Hebrew letter *mem* (meaning "from") has been displaced
from the end of the preceding word, *'![Image](images/a1.jpg){.block
height="12" width="8"}q![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14"
width="6"}b![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}m*
(where it means "their" heel).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:21*](#Contents.html_rgen49_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^Naphtali: a hind let loose, who gives lovely words.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_21.jpg){height="42" width="327"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:22*](#Contents.html_rgen49_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^A fruitful bough is Joseph, a fruitful bough over a spring, branches
running over a wall:

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_22.jpg){height="47" width="232"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:23*](#Contents.html_rgen49_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And archers bitterly attacked him, shot at him, and despised him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_23.jpg){height="22" width="269"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:24*](#Contents.html_rgen49_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And his bow stayed strong, and his forearms were nimble, from the
hands of the Mighty One of Jacob, from there, the shepherd, the rock of
Israel,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_24.jpg){height="46" width="293"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:25*](#Contents.html_rgen49_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^from your father's God, and He'll strengthen you, and Shadday, and
He'll bless you, blessings of skies from above, blessings of deep,
crouching below, blessings of breast and womb.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_25.jpg){height="70" width="304"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

49:25. **Shadday**. The MT reads just Shadday, but evidence from other
versions indicates that the line should read El Shadday. (The MT reads
*'et* where it should read '*![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14"
width="6"}l*.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 49:26*](#Contents.html_rgen49_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^blessings of your father, the mighty and most high, blessings of the
mountains of old desired object of the hills of antiquity. They'll be on
Joseph's head, on the top of the head of the one separate from his
brothers.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap49_26.jpg){height="71" width="297"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

49:26. **the mighty and most high**. This is based on a reconstruction
proposed by David Noel Freedman, reading
![Image](images/page161-1.jpg){.block height="16" width="60"} instead of
![Image](images/page161-2.jpg){.block height="16" width="60"}.

49:26. **mountains of old**. This understanding involves an emendation
of the text based on the parallel with "hills of antiquity" in the next
line and on comparison with Moses' blessing of Joseph: "from the top of
the ancient mountains, and from the most precious of the hills of
antiquity" (Deut 33:15).

[*Genesis 50:1*](#Contents.html_rgen50_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 50 {.h2a}

^1^And Joseph fell upon his father's face and wept over him and kissed
him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_01.jpg){height="22" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:2*](#Contents.html_rgen50_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Joseph commanded his servants, the physicians, to embalm his
father. And the physicians embalmed Israel,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_02.jpg){height="50" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:3*](#Contents.html_rgen50_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^and they spent forty days on him, because that is the number of days
they spent on the embalming, and Egypt mourned him seventy days.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_03.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:4*](#Contents.html_rgen50_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And his mourning days passed, and Joseph spoke to Pharaoh's house,
saying, "If I've found favor in your eyes, speak in Pharaoh's ears,
saying,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_04.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:5*](#Contents.html_rgen50_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^'My father had me swear, saying, "Here I'm dying. In my tomb, which I
dug for me in the land of Canaan: you shall bury me there." And now, let
me go up so I may bury my father and come back.'"

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_05.jpg){height="114" width="356"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:6*](#Contents.html_rgen50_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Pharaoh said, "Go up and bury your father as he had you swear."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_06.jpg){height="48" width="343"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:7*](#Contents.html_rgen50_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Joseph went up to bury his father, and all of Pharaoh's servants,
the elders of his house, and all the elders of the land of Egypt,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_07.jpg){height="51" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:8*](#Contents.html_rgen50_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^and all of Joseph's house and his brothers and his father's house.
Only their infants and their flock and their oxen they left in the land
of Goshen.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_08.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:9*](#Contents.html_rgen50_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And both chariots and horsemen went up with him. It was a very heavy
camp.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_09.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:10*](#Contents.html_rgen50_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And they came to the threshing floor of Atad, which is across the
Jordan, and they had a very big and heavy funeral, and he made a
mourning for his father or seven days.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_10.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:11*](#Contents.html_rgen50_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And the Canaanite residents of the land saw the mourning at the
threshing floor of Atad and said, "This is a heavy mourning to Egypt."
On account of this its name was called Abel of Egypt, which is beyond
the Jordan.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_11.jpg){height="74" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:12*](#Contents.html_rgen50_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And his sons did so for him, as he had commanded them.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_12.jpg){height="22" width="216"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:13*](#Contents.html_rgen50_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And his sons carried him to the land of Canaan and buried him in the
cave of the field of Machpelah, as Abraham had bought the field as a
possession for a tomb from Ephron, the Hittite, facing Mamre.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_13.jpg){height="115" width="356"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:14*](#Contents.html_rgen50_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And, after his burial of his father, Joseph came back to Egypt, he
and his brothers and all those who went up with him to bury his father.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_14.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:15*](#Contents.html_rgen50_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Joseph's brothers saw that their father was dead, and they said,
"If Joseph will despise us he'll *pay us back* all the bad that we dealt
to him."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_15.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:16*](#Contents.html_rgen50_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And they commanded to Joseph, saying, "Your father had commanded
before his death, saying,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_16.jpg){height="49" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

50:16. **they commanded**. Why would they *command?* The problem appears
to be scribal. The Septuagint appears to be translating Hebrew
![Image](images/page162-1.jpg){.block height="13" width="32"}, meaning
"they approached," instead of the MT
![Image](images/page162-2.jpg){.block height="12" width="32"}, meaning
"they commanded." A scribe apparently changed the word under the
influence of the word "command" which comes up later in the verse.

50:16. **Your father had commanded before his death**. We never find out
whether Joseph---or his brothers---ever told Jacob what his brothers did
to him. The brothers *claim* that Jacob commanded that Joseph should
forgive them, but we do not know if they are making this up or not.
Either way, it is the right message: after a parent's death, the
children should try to heal any old wounds and draw close.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:17*](#Contents.html_rgen50_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^'You shall say this to Joseph: Please, bear your brothers' offense
and their sin, because they dealt you bad.' And now bear the offense of
the servants of your father's God."

And Joseph wept when they spoke to him.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_17.jpg){height="77" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:18*](#Contents.html_rgen50_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And his brothers also went and fell in front of him and said, "Here,
we're yours as slaves."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_18.jpg){height="22" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

50:18. **we're yours as slaves**. This is the final ironic recompense in
the chain of deceptions in this family. The brothers who once sold
Joseph as a slave now say that they will be *his* slaves.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:19*](#Contents.html_rgen50_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And Joseph said to them, "Don't be afraid, because am I in God's
place?

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_19.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:20*](#Contents.html_rgen50_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And *you* thought bad against me. *God* thought for good: in order
to do as it is today, to keep alive a numerous people.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_20.jpg){height="74" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:21*](#Contents.html_rgen50_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And now, don't be afraid. I'll provide for you and your infants."
And he consoled them. And he spoke on their heart.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_21.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

50:21. **he spoke on their heart**. We have seen that each act of
deception since Jacob led to another deception that came as a
recompense. Thus deceptions and hurts within a family can go on in a
perpetual cycle. In order to bring it to an end, one member of the
family who is entitled to retribution must stop the cycle and forgive
instead. That is what Joseph does here.

This beautiful end of the story of deceptions and retributions completes
an intricately embroidered narrative. Many interpreters have treated the
Joseph cycle as if it were a separate entity, referring to it as a
"novella." Insofar as that term connotes an independent work, it is
misleading, cutting off what is an integral part of this connected
narrative. Symbols, ironies, and relationships of characters and events
are lost when one performs this surgery and separates the Joseph cycle
from the rest of the story.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:22*](#Contents.html_rgen50_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Joseph lived in Egypt, he and his father's house. And Joseph
lived a hundred ten years,

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_22.jpg){height="50" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:23*](#Contents.html_rgen50_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^and Joseph saw children of Ephraim's of the third generation. Also
children of Machir, son of Manasseh, were born on Joseph's knees.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_23.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:24*](#Contents.html_rgen50_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And Joseph said to his brothers, "I'm dying. And God *will* take
account of you and will bring you up from this land to the land that He
swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_24.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:25*](#Contents.html_rgen50_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And Joseph had the children of Israel swear, saying, "God *will*
take account of you, and you'll bring up my bones from here."

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_25.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Genesis 50:26*](#Contents.html_rgen50_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And Joseph died, a hundred ten years old, and they embalmed him and
set him in a coffin in Egypt.

![Image](images/Gen_Chap50_26.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#exotitle.html}

![Image](images/Exo_title.jpg){height="302" width="527"}

[]{#Exofrontmatter.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[COMMENTARY ON THE TORAH](#Contents.html_rexo_fr)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[T]{.dropcap}he book of Exodus tells the story of the birth of a nation
in slavery and ends with the nation's establishment of its own center,
leaders, and symbols in freedom. Genesis involves a continuous narrowing
