------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:3*](#Contents.html_rExo5_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And they said, "The God of the Hebrews has communicated with us. Let
us go on a trip of three days in the wilderness so we may sacrifice to
YHWH, our God, or else He'll strike us with an epidemic or with the
sword."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_03.jpg){height="98" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

5:3. **Let us go on a trip of three days**. Moses and Aaron do not ask
for the Israelites' liberation from Egypt. They ask only to make a
three-day sacrificial pilgrimage. They just do not mention the fact that
they do not plan to return!

5:3. **strike**. The word here (Hebrew *pg'*) is the same as the word
for the Israelites' "meeting" Moses later in v. 20. The range of
meanings of this root makes a wordplay that is enhanced by clustering
with the blatant pun in the next verse.

5:3. **strike us**. Moses and Aaron say that God may strike "us," the
Israelites, if they don't go to sacrifice to Him, but the Hebrew suffix
has a subtle double meaning: it can also mean "strike him"---which could
apply to Pharaoh himself or to a nonspecific person, that is, to the
Egyptians who will in fact suffer the plagues. The double meaning here
is not merely playful but meaningful to the context.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:4*](#Contents.html_rExo5_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And the king of Egypt said to them, "Why, Moses and Aaron, do you
turn the people loose from its work? Go to your burdens!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_04.jpg){height="70" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

5:4. **turn loose**. With the Hebrew root *pr'*, this is a pun on
"Pharaoh." An even stronger play on this word will occur later, in the
middle of the golden calf episode (Exod 32:25).

5:4. **Go to your burdens!** He talks down to Moses and Aaron, telling
them to get back to work, and thus conveying to them that they are just
slaves like everyone else.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:5*](#Contents.html_rExo5_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Pharaoh said, "Here, the people of the land are now many, and
you've made them cease from their burdens."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_05.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

5:5. **cease**. This is the term that describes God's ceasing on the
seventh day of creation, Hebrew *![Image](images/scirc.jpg){.block
height="12" width="8"}bt*, cognate to the word "Sabbath."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:6*](#Contents.html_rExo5_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Pharaoh commanded the taskmasters among the people and its
officers in that day, saying,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_06.jpg){height="47" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:7*](#Contents.html_rExo5_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^"You shall not continue to give straw to the people to make bricks as
yesterday and the day before. *They* shall go and collect straw for
themselves.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_07.jpg){height="49" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:8*](#Contents.html_rExo5_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And you shall impose on them the quota of the bricks that they were
making yesterday and the day before. You shall not subtract from it.
Because they're lazy. On account of this they're crying, saying, 'Let us
go and sacrifice to our God.'

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_08.jpg){height="94" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:9*](#Contents.html_rExo5_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^Let the work be heavy on the people, and let them do it, and let them
not pay attention to words that are a lie!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_09.jpg){height="49" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

5:9. **Let the work be heavy**. The idiom of Moses' "heavy mouth and
heavy tongue" initiates a chain of puns on the various shades of meaning
of the word "heavy" (Hebrew *kbd,* meaning weighty, difficult, or
substantial). Here Pharaoh orders that the work of the Israelites be
made heavy. The hardening of the Pharaoh's heart is expressed several
times through this term (7:14; 8:11,28; 9:7,34; 10:1). Four of the
plagues on Egypt are described as heavy: insects (8:20), pestilence
(9:3), hail (9:18,24), and locusts (10:14). The Israelites leave with a
heavy supply of livestock (12:38). As Moses holds up his arms in support
of the Israelites while they battle the Amalekites, his arms become
heavy (17:12). Moses' father-in-law warns him that his efforts to manage
the entire nation on his own are foolish, "because the thing is too
heavy for you" (18:18). The chain of puns culminates at Sinai, as a
heavy cloud is on the mountain while the people hear the voice of God
(19:16). It is not that the narrator's vocabulary is too limited to
produce other adjectives. Rather, the construction of such chains of
wordplay is a technique of biblical narrative, deployed in other
biblical books as well. Here the chain contributes to the narrative's
unity, rather than the narrative's appearing to be a series of loosely
bound episodes.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:10*](#Contents.html_rExo5_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And the people's taskmasters and its officers went out and said to
the people, saying, "Pharaoh said this: 'I am not giving you straw.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_10.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:11*](#Contents.html_rExo5_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^You, go, take straw for yourselves from wherever you'll find it,
because not a thing is subtracted from your work.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_11.jpg){height="49" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:12*](#Contents.html_rExo5_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And the people scattered through all the land of Egypt to collect
stubble for straw.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_12.jpg){height="52" width="324"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:13*](#Contents.html_rExo5_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And the taskmasters were prodding, saying, "Finish your work, the
day's thing in that very day, as when there was the straw."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_13.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:14*](#Contents.html_rExo5_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And the officers of the children of Israel whom Pharaoh's
taskmasters had set over them were beaten---saying, "Why haven't you
finished your requirement to make brick like the day before yesterday,
also yesterday, also today?"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_14.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:15*](#Contents.html_rExo5_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And the officers of the children of Israel came and cried to
Pharaoh, saying, "Why do you do a thing like this to your servants?

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_15.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:16*](#Contents.html_rExo5_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^Straw isn't given to your servants, and they say to us: Make bricks.
And here, your servants are beaten, and it's your people's sin."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_16.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:17*](#Contents.html_rExo5_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And he said, "You're lazy, lazy! On account of this you're saying,
'Let us go and sacrifice to YHWH.'

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_17.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:18*](#Contents.html_rExo5_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And now go, work! And straw will not be given to you, and you shall
give the quota of bricks!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_18.jpg){height="46" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:19*](#Contents.html_rExo5_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And the officers of the children of Israel saw themselves in a bad
state---saying, "You shall not subtract from your bricks, the day's
thing in that very day."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_19.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:20*](#Contents.html_rExo5_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And they met Moses and Aaron, standing opposite them when they came
out from Pharaoh,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_20.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:21*](#Contents.html_rExo5_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^and they said to them, "May YHWH look on you and judge, that you've
made our smell odious in Pharaoh's eyes and in his servants' eyes, to
give a sword in their hand to kill us!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_21.jpg){height="76" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

5:21. **our smell odious in Pharaoh's eyes**. People smell with their
noses, not their eyes! This may be just an expression or even the
author's oversight, but I rather think that the contradiction is
purposeful: the people's officers misspeak because they are upset. We
*frequently* do that.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:22*](#Contents.html_rExo5_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Moses went back to YHWH and said, "My Lord, why have you done
bad to this people? Why did you send me here?

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_22.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 5:23*](#Contents.html_rExo5_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And since I came to Pharaoh to speak in your name he has done bad to
this people, and you *haven't* rescued your people!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap5_23.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus06.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:1*](#Contents.html_rExo6_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **6** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH said to Moses, "Now you'll see what I shall do to Pharaoh,
because with a strong hand he'll let them go, and with a strong hand
he'll drive them from his land."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_1.jpg){height="72" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

6:1. **with a strong hand he'll let them go**. But with *whose* strong
hand? God's or Moses' own? The probable meaning is: God's. But the
ambiguity is rich because either meaning is instructive.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:2*](#Contents.html_rExo6_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## A[ND]{.smallcaps1} I [APPEARED]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

^2^And God spoke to Moses and said to him, "I am YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_2a.jpg){height="20" width="56"}

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_2.jpg){height="25" width="360"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:3*](#Contents.html_rExo6_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And I appeared to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob as El Shadday, and
I was not known to them by my name, YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_3.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

6:3. **I was not known to them by my name, YHWH**. But God *was* known
to them by this name (Gen 13:4; 15:2; 22:14,16; 26:22,25; 28:13). I have
discussed the significance of the understanding of this problem in
critical scholarship elsewhere (*Who Wrote the Bible?*). It has to do
with the case for multiple sources from which the Torah was composed.
But in terms of the final composition, the full Torah as we know it, how
shall we understand this verse? Perhaps covenantally: In the Noahic
covenant (Gen 9:8--9), God is called
*'![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}l![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11" width="7"}hîm*. In
the Abrahamic covenant, God is known as El Shadday (Gen 17:1--2). But
the covenant that will be made with this generation at Sinai will be
with the use of the name YHWH (Exod 20:2). In support of this
understanding: the very next words here are "And I also established my
*covenant* with them."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:4*](#Contents.html_rExo6_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And I also established my covenant with them, to give them the land
of Canaan, the land of their residences, in which they resided.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_4.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:5*](#Contents.html_rExo6_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And also: I've heard the cry of the children of Israel as Egypt is
enslaving them, and I've remembered my covenant.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_5.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:6*](#Contents.html_rExo6_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^Therefore, say to the children of Israel: 'I am YHWH, and I shall
bring you out from under Egypt's burdens, and I shall rescue you from
their toil, and I shall redeem you with an outstretched arm and with
tremendous judgments,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_6.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:7*](#Contents.html_rExo6_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^and I shall take you to me as a people, and I shall become your God,
and you'll know that I am YHWH, your God, who is bringing you out from
under Egypt's burdens.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_7.jpg){height="98" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

6:7. **you'll know that I am YHWH, your God, who is bringing you out**.
The dominant theme of Exodus, YHWH's becoming known, is connected
explicitly here to the rescue from slavery and to the "judgments" that
will occur in Egypt. It will continue to be stated, several times, as a
reason for the miraculous plagues.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:8*](#Contents.html_rExo6_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And I shall bring you to the land that I raised my hand to give to
Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, and I shall give it to you as a
possession. I am YHWH.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_8.jpg){height="73" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:9*](#Contents.html_rExo6_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Moses spoke this to the children of Israel, and they did not
listen to Moses because of shortage of spirit and because of hard work.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_9.jpg){height="69" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:10*](#Contents.html_rExo6_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_10.jpg){height="25" width="216"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:11*](#Contents.html_rExo6_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^"Come, speak to Pharaoh, king of Egypt, that he should let the
children of Israel go from his land."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_11.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:12*](#Contents.html_rExo6_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And Moses spoke in front of YHWH, saying, "Here, the children of
Israel didn't listen to me, and how will *Pharaoh* listen to me?! And
I'm uncircumcised of lips!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_12.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

6:12,30. **uncircumcised of lips**. Does this shed light on the meaning
of "heavy of tongue and heavy of mouth"? It seems rather to be even
harder to interpret. Uncircumcision is used elsewhere in the Torah as a
metaphor as well. Lev 26:41 and Deut 10:16 refer to the people's
uncircumcised heart. (The prophets Jeremiah and Ezekiel use this image
also.) There it is a strong image, criticizing the people who understand
that their covenant requires circumcision of the skin that covers the
penis, but who do not appreciate that they must likewise remove the
impediment to their hearts. But the meaning of the image here is less
clear. We still do not know what the impediment is. All we can say is
that Moses is again pictured as unconfident, feeling inadequate to the
task. The repeated emphasis on this point will make the eventual success
of the exodus that much more dramatic.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:13*](#Contents.html_rExo6_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And YHWH spoke to Moses and to Aaron and commanded them regarding
the children of Israel and regarding Pharaoh, king of Egypt, to bring
out the children of Israel from the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_13.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:14*](#Contents.html_rExo6_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^These are the heads of their fathers' houses:

The sons of Reuben, Israel's firstborn: Hanoch and Pallu, Hezron, and
Carmi. These are the families of Reuben.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_14.jpg){height="74" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:15*](#Contents.html_rExo6_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And the sons of Simeon: Jemuel and Jamin and Ohad and Jachin and
Zohar and Saul, son of a Canaanite woman. These are the families of
Simeon.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_15.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:16*](#Contents.html_rExo6_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And these are the names of the sons of Levi by their records:
Gershon and Kohath and Merari. And the years of Levi's life were a
hundred thirty-seven years.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_16.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:17*](#Contents.html_rExo6_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^The sons of Gershon: Libni and Shimei, by their families.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_17.jpg){height="25" width="254"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:18*](#Contents.html_rExo6_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And the sons of Kohath: Amram and Izhar and Hebron and Uzziel. And
the years of Kohath's life were a hundred thirty-three years.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_18.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:19*](#Contents.html_rExo6_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And the sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of
Levi by their records.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_19.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:20*](#Contents.html_rExo6_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Amram took Jochebed, his aunt, as a wife; and she gave birth to
Aaron and Moses for him. And the years of Amram's life were a hundred
thirty-seven years.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_20.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

6:20. **she gave birth to Aaron and Moses**. Until now, it has not been
clear that Moses and Aaron are brothers. There is no reference to a
brother in the story of Moses' birth, and at the bush Aaron is referred
to as "your Levite brother," which sounds like a fellow Levite, not an
actual brother. The list in which this appears (6:13--25) seems strange:
it comes in the middle of a conversation between God and Moses, and it
covers only Reuben, Simeon, and Levi, and then leaves out the rest of
the tribes. But it appears that the point of the list was to get to
Aaron: to identify him as Moses' brother, to state that he is married to
the sister of the chieftain of the tribe of Judah, and to name his son
and grandson who will be his successors. The order also indicates that
Aaron is the firstborn; this is confirmed in Exod 7:7.

6:20. **Aaron and Moses**. And what about Miriam? One Hebrew manuscript
and the Septuagint and Samaritan texts have the words "and their sister
Miriam." The scribe appears to have accidentally omitted these words
here.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:21*](#Contents.html_rExo6_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And the sons of Izhar: Korah and Nepheg and Zichri.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_21.jpg){height="20" width="230"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:22*](#Contents.html_rExo6_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And the sons of Uzziel: Mishael and Elzaphan and Sithri.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_22.jpg){height="23" width="284"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:23*](#Contents.html_rExo6_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And Aaron took Elisheba, daughter of Amminadab, sister of Nahshon as
a wife; and she gave birth to Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar for
him.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_23.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

6:23. **daughter of Amminadab, sister of Nahshon**. Aaron marries the
sister of the chieftain of the tribe of Judah. It is noteworthy that the
priestly and political elite marry (and this suggests that such
marriages occured later between the royal and high priestly families of
Israel).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:24*](#Contents.html_rExo6_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And the sons of Korah: Assir and Elkanah and Abiasaph. These are the
families of the Korahites.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_24.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:25*](#Contents.html_rExo6_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And Eleazar son of Aaron had taken one of the daughters of Putiel as
a wife, and she gave birth for him to Phinehas. These are the heads of
the fathers of the Levites according to their families.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_25.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:26*](#Contents.html_rExo6_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^That is Aaron and Moses, to whom YHWH said, "Bring out the children
of Israel from the land of Egypt by their masses."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_26.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

6:26--27. **Aaron and Moses ... Moses and Aaron**. Both word orders are
given: Aaron first, then Moses first, suggesting that each is important
in a different way. The recognition of Aaron here is especially
significant because it is natural to think of Moses first, but this
notice follows a genealogy that has listed Aaron as the firstborn and
identified his wife and children. (The Septuagint and Qumran texts have
the order in v. 27 reversed, which unfortunately loses this point.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:27*](#Contents.html_rExo6_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^They were the ones speaking to Pharaoh, king of Egypt, to bring out
the children of Israel from Egypt. That is Moses and Aaron.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_27.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:28*](#Contents.html_rExo6_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And it was in the day that YHWH spoke to Moses in the land of Egypt,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_28.jpg){height="45" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:29*](#Contents.html_rExo6_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^and YHWH spoke to Moses, saying, "I am YHWH. Speak to Pharaoh, king
of Egypt, everything that I speak to you."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_29.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 6:30*](#Contents.html_rExo6_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And Moses said in front of YHWH, "Here, I'm uncircumcised of lips,
and how will Pharaoh listen to me?!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap6_30.jpg){height="46" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus07.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:1*](#Contents.html_rExo7_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **7** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH said to Moses, "See, I've made you a god to Pharaoh, and
Aaron, your brother, will be your prophet.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_1.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:2*](#Contents.html_rExo7_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^You shall speak everything that I'll command you; and Aaron, your
brother, shall speak to Pharaoh, that he let the children of Israel go
from his land.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_2.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:3*](#Contents.html_rExo7_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And I, I'll harden Pharaoh's heart, and I'll multiply my signs and my
wonders in the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_3.jpg){height="67" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:4*](#Contents.html_rExo7_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Pharaoh won't listen to you, and I'll set my hand in Egypt and
bring out my masses, my people, the children of Israel, from the land of
Egypt with great judgments.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_4.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:5*](#Contents.html_rExo7_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Egypt will know that I am YHWH when I reach out my hand on Egypt,
and I'll bring out the children of Israel from among them."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_5.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:6*](#Contents.html_rExo7_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Moses and Aaron did as YHWH had commanded them. They did so.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_6.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:7*](#Contents.html_rExo7_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Moses was eighty years old, and Aaron was eighty-three years old
when they spoke to Pharaoh.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_7.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:8*](#Contents.html_rExo7_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And YHWH said to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_8.jpg){height="22" width="292"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:9*](#Contents.html_rExo7_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^"When Pharaoh will speak to you, saying, 'Produce a wonder!' then say
to Aaron, 'Take your staff and throw it in front of Pharaoh. Let it
become a serpent.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_9.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:9. **a serpent**. Not a snake. This is different from the *snake*
(Hebrew *n![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}h![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}![Image](images/scirc.jpg){.block height="12" width="8"}*)
that *Moses'* staff became in Exod 4:3. Moses performed that miracle for
the Israelite elders (4:30). Now, in front of Pharaoh, *Aaron's* staff
becomes a *tannîn*. This is the term that is used for the big sea
serpents that God makes on the fifth day of creation (Gen 1:21). They
are not merely snakes, as people have often pictured them. They are
extraordinary creatures from a seemingly unearthly realm.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:10*](#Contents.html_rExo7_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Moses and Aaron came to Pharaoh and did so, as YHWH had
commanded, and Aaron threw his staff in front of Pharaoh and in front of
his servants, and it became a serpent.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_10.jpg){height="95" width="348"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:11*](#Contents.html_rExo7_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Pharaoh, too, called the wise men and the sorcerers; and they,
too, Egypt's magicians, did so with their charms:

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_11.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:12*](#Contents.html_rExo7_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^and they each threw his staff, and they became serpents. And Aaron's
staff swallowed their staffs!

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_12.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:13*](#Contents.html_rExo7_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Pharaoh's heart was strong, and he did not listen to them---as
YHWH had spoken.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_13.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:14*](#Contents.html_rExo7_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And YHWH said to Moses, "Pharaoh's heart is heavy. He has refused to
let the people go.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_14.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:14. **Pharaoh's heart is heavy**. The stream of descriptions of
elements as "heavy" that began in Exod 4:10 with Moses' heavy mouth and
heavy tongue continues to flow here and in what follows.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:15*](#Contents.html_rExo7_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^Go to Pharaoh in the morning---here, he'll be going out to the
water---and you'll stand opposite him on the bank of the Nile, and you
shall take in your hand the staff that was changed into a snake.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_15.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:16*](#Contents.html_rExo7_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And you'll say to him, 'YHWH, the God of the Hebrews, sent me to
you, saying: "Let my people go so they may serve me in the wilderness."
And here, you haven't listened so far.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_16.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:17*](#Contents.html_rExo7_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^YHWH said this: "By this you'll know that I am YHWH." Here, I'm
striking with the staff that's in my hand on the waters that are in the
Nile, and they'll be changed into blood.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_17.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:17. **By this you'll know that I am YHWH**. This is the story of the
introduction of the deity *by name* to the world, so this statement can
be understood not only as establishing the existence of the named deity
to Pharaoh, but also as establishing what the name *conveys*. Thus: By
this (a miracle involving a change in nature) you'll know that I am *the
one who causes being*.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:18*](#Contents.html_rExo7_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And the fish that are in the Nile will die, and the Nile will stink,
and Egypt will weary themselves to drink water from the Nile.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_18.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:18. **will stink**. Hebrew ![Image](images/f0193-01.jpg){.block
height="13" width="30"} (*b![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block
height="11" width="8"}'![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14"
width="6"}![Image](images/scirc.jpg){.block height="12" width="8"}*).
This is the same word that the people used metaphorically in their
complaint to Moses earlier: "You've made our smell odious in Pharaoh's
eyes" (5:21). They now see the divine power giving the Egyptians
something *literally* odious to smell. This in turn demonstrates how
mistaken they were when they judged the situation so early in the
process.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:19*](#Contents.html_rExo7_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And YHWH said to Moses, "Say to Aaron, 'Take your staff and reach
your hand over Egypt's waters, over their rivers, over their canals, and
over their pools, and over every concentration of their waters.' And
they will be blood! And blood will be in all the land of Egypt---and in
the trees and in the stones!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_19.jpg){height="119" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:20*](#Contents.html_rExo7_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Moses and Aaron did so, as YHWH had commanded. And he raised the
staff and struck the waters that were in the Nile before Pharaoh's eyes
and before his servants' eyes, and all the waters that were in the Nile
were changed into blood.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_20.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:21*](#Contents.html_rExo7_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And the fish in the Nile died, and the Nile had an odor, and Egypt
was not able to drink water from the Nile. And the blood was in all the
land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_21.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:22*](#Contents.html_rExo7_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Egypt's magicians did so with their charms. And Pharaoh's heart
was strong, and he did not listen to them---as YHWH had spoken.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_22.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:23*](#Contents.html_rExo7_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And Pharaoh turned and came into his house and did not pay heed to
this as well.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_23.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:24*](#Contents.html_rExo7_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And all Egypt dug around the Nile for water to drink, because they
were not able to drink from the Nile's waters.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_24.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:25*](#Contents.html_rExo7_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And seven days were filled after YHWH's striking the Nile.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_25.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:26*](#Contents.html_rExo7_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And YHWH said to Moses, "Come to Pharaoh, and you'll say to him,
'YHWH said this: Let my people go, so they may serve me.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_26.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:27*](#Contents.html_rExo7_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And if you refuse to let go, here, I'm plaguing all your border with
frogs,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_27.jpg){height="50" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:28*](#Contents.html_rExo7_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^and the Nile will teem with frogs, and they'll go up and come in
your house and in your bedroom and on your bed and in your servants'
house and among your people and in your ovens and in your bowls,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_28.jpg){height="94" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 7:29*](#Contents.html_rExo7_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^and the frogs will go up on you and on your people and on all your
servants.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap7_29.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus08.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:1*](#Contents.html_rExo8_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **8** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH said to Moses, "Say to Aaron, 'Reach out your hand with your
staff over the rivers, over the canals, and over the pools, and bring up
the frogs on the land of Egypt.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_1.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:2*](#Contents.html_rExo8_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Aaron reached out his hand over Egypt's waters, and the frogs
came up and covered the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_2.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:3*](#Contents.html_rExo8_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And the magicians did so with their charms. And they brought up the
frogs on the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_3.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:4*](#Contents.html_rExo8_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron and said, "Pray to YHWH that He
will take away the frogs from me and from my people so I may let the
people go, and they'll sacrifice to YHWH."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_4.jpg){height="96" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:5*](#Contents.html_rExo8_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Moses said to Pharaoh, "Be honored over me as to when I'll pray
for you and for your servants and for your people to cut off the frogs
from you and from your houses---they'll be left only in the river."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_5.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:6*](#Contents.html_rExo8_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And he said, "As of tomorrow."

And he said, "According to your word: so that you'll know that there's
none like YHWH, our God.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_6.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:7*](#Contents.html_rExo8_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And the frogs will turn away from you and from your houses and from
your servants and from your people---they'll be left only in the river."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_7.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:8*](#Contents.html_rExo8_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Moses and Aaron went out from Pharaoh. And Moses cried to YHWH
over the matter of the frogs that he had set upon Pharaoh.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_8.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:9*](#Contents.html_rExo8_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And YHWH did according to Moses' word, and the frogs died from the
houses, from the yards, and from the fields.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_9.jpg){height="69" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:10*](#Contents.html_rExo8_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And they piled them up, heaps and heaps, and the land smelled.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_10.jpg){height="44" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:10. **they piled them up**. This term was used twice for Joseph's
piling up the grain (Gen 41:35,49), and these are its only occurrences
in biblical prose. The wordplay appears to make the point that the good
that Joseph did for Egypt was repaid with bad by the subsequent
Pharaohs, and this makes the current plague more clearly a punishment to
suit the crime. Also, the term for "were left" in 8:5,7 plays on the
"bowls" (both have root *![Image](images/scirc.jpg){.block height="12"
width="8"}'r*) in 7:28. And this whole paragraph is filled with words
using the Hebrew letter *![Image](images/sdot.jpg){.block height="11"
width="6"}adeh*, perhaps playing on *![Image](images/sdot.jpg){.block
height="11" width="6"}![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}pard![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14" width="6"}‛a*
(frogs).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:11*](#Contents.html_rExo8_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Pharaoh saw that there was a break, and he made his heart heavy,
and he did not listen to them---as YHWH had spoken.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_11.jpg){height="75" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:12*](#Contents.html_rExo8_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And YHWH said to Moses, "Say to Aaron, 'Reach out your staff and
strike the dust of the earth, and it will become lice in all the land of
Egypt.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_12.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:13*](#Contents.html_rExo8_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And they did so, and Aaron reached out his hand with his staff and
struck the dust of the earth, and the lice were in the humans and in the
animals. All the dust of the earth was lice in all the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_13.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:14*](#Contents.html_rExo8_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And the magicians did so with their charms, to bring out the lice,
and they were not able. And the lice were in the humans and in the
animals.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_14.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:15*](#Contents.html_rExo8_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And the magicians said to Pharaoh, "It's the finger of God!" And
Pharaoh's heart was strong, and he did not listen to them---as YHWH had
spoken.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_15.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:16*](#Contents.html_rExo8_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And YHWH said to Moses, "Get up early in the morning and stand up in
front of Pharaoh. Here, he's going out to the water. And you'll say to
him, 'YHWH said this: Let my people go so they may serve me.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_16.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:17*](#Contents.html_rExo8_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^Because if you're not letting my people go, here, I'm causing an
insect swarm to be let go on you and on your servants and on your people
and on your houses. And the houses of Egypt will be filled with the
insect swarm, and also the ground that they're on.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_17.jpg){height="93" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:18*](#Contents.html_rExo8_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And I shall distinguish in that day the land of Goshen, on which my
people is standing, for no insect swarm to be there, so that you will
know that I, YHWH, am within the land.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_18.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:18. **I, YHWH, am within the land**. As earlier (7:17), this can also
be understood in terms of the *meaning* of the divine name, thus meaning
"So that you will know that *I am the one who causes being* in the
land."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:19*](#Contents.html_rExo8_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And I shall set a distinction between my people and your people.
This sign will be tomorrow.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_19.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:19. **distinction**. The Hebrew reads
*p![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}d![Image](images/ubar.jpg){.block height="10" width="7"}t*,
"ransom?" The Greek and the Vulgate read "distinction," which makes more
apparent sense and relates to the distinguishing that is predicted in
the preceding verse.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:20*](#Contents.html_rExo8_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And YHWH did so. And a heavy insect swarm came to Pharaoh's house
and his servants' house, and in all the land of Egypt the land was
corrupted because of the insect swarm.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_20.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:20. **heavy**. Starting with the insect swarm, several plagues now
join the stream of elements in the narrative that are described as
"heavy."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:21*](#Contents.html_rExo8_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron. And he said, "Go. Sacrifice to
your God *in the land*."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_21.jpg){height="68" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:21. **Sacrifice to your God *in the land***. Pharaoh is not fooled by
the request for a "trip of three days." He takes a step back from his
tough stance, and he turns to negotiation: You can sacrifice to your
God, but do it here in Egypt, not out in the wilderness. A process of
bargaining will follow, but note that, really, all the bargaining is on
Pharaoh's part. Moses' position never changes, and in the end Pharaoh
must capitulate completely.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:22*](#Contents.html_rExo8_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Moses said, "It's not right to do so, because we'll be
sacrificing to YHWH, our God, an offensive thing to Egypt. Here, we'll
be sacrificing an offensive thing to Egypt before their eyes; and will
they not stone us?!

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_22.jpg){height="88" width="422"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:22. **It's not right**. "Right"
(n![Image](images/a-image.jpg){height="12" width="8"}kôn) is the word
Joseph used to tell Pharaoh that the repetition of his dream meant that
the seven good and seven bad years were certainly coming (Gen 41:32). In
that same speech, Joseph used the word "heavy" for the famine (41:31).
As with the "piling up" in Exod 8:10, the cluster of language from the
Joseph story suggests that the good that Joseph did for Egypt was repaid
with bad by the subsequent Pharaohs, and this, again, suggests that the
current plague suits the crime.

8:22. **an offensive thing to Egypt**. As we have seen in the Joseph
story, there are matters relating to the Israelites' food that are
pictured as being "an offensive thing to Egypt." There the Egyptians
will not eat with Israelites (Gen 43:32), and they disdain the Israelite
consumption of sheep. Now Moses uses this fact---which worked against
the Israelites in Joseph's day---in the Israelites' favor.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:23*](#Contents.html_rExo8_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^We'll go on a trip of three days in the wilderness, and we'll
sacrifice to YHWH, our God, whatever He'll say to us."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_23.jpg){height="57" width="422"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:24*](#Contents.html_rExo8_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And Pharaoh said, "I'll let you go, and you'll sacrifice to YHWH,
your God, in the wilderness. Only you shall not go *far*. Pray for me."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_24.jpg){height="88" width="422"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:25*](#Contents.html_rExo8_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And Moses said, "Here, I'm going out from you, and I'll pray to
YHWH, and the insect swarm will turn away from Pharaoh and from his
servants and from his people tomorrow. Only let Pharaoh not continue to
toy so as not to let the people go to sacrifice to YHWH."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_25.jpg){height="118" width="422"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:25. **let Pharaoh not continue**. The Hebrew word for "continue" here
is *y![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11"
width="7"}s![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14"
width="6"}p*---Joseph---thus culminating the string of connections of
this story to the Joseph story.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:26*](#Contents.html_rExo8_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And Moses went out from Pharaoh, and he prayed to YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_26.jpg){height="55" width="422"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:27*](#Contents.html_rExo8_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And YHWH did according to Moses' word, and the insect swarm turned
away from Pharaoh and from his servants and from his people. Not one was
left.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_27.jpg){height="58" width="424"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 8:28*](#Contents.html_rExo8_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And Pharaoh made his heart heavy this time as well, and he did not
let the people go.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap8_28.jpg){height="58" width="422"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus09.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:1*](#Contents.html_rExo9_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **9** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH said to Moses, "Come to Pharaoh and speak to him: 'YHWH, God
of the Hebrews, said this: Let my people go, so they may serve me.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_1.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:2*](#Contents.html_rExo9_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^Because if you refuse to let go and you are still holding on to them,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_2.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:3*](#Contents.html_rExo9_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^here, YHWH's hand is on your livestock that are in the field, on the
horses, on the asses, on the camels, on the oxen, and on the flock---a
very heavy epidemic.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_3.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:4*](#Contents.html_rExo9_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And YHWH will distinguish between Israel's livestock and Egypt's
livestock, and not a thing out of all that Israel has will die.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_4.jpg){height="69" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:5*](#Contents.html_rExo9_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And YHWH has set an appointed time, saying: Tomorrow YHWH will do
this thing in the land.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_5.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:6*](#Contents.html_rExo9_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And YHWH did this thing on the next day, and all Egypt's livestock
died, and of all the livestock of the children of Israel not one died.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_6.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:7*](#Contents.html_rExo9_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Pharaoh sent; and, here, not even one of Israel's livestock had
died. And Pharaoh's heart was heavy, and he did not let the people go.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_7.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:8*](#Contents.html_rExo9_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And YHWH said to Moses and to Aaron, "Take handfuls of furnace ash,
and let Moses fling it to the skies before Pharaoh's eyes.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_8.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:8. **furnace**. The mention of a furnace is ominous because the only
use of the word until now was to describe the smoke of the destruction
of Sodom and Gomorrah (Gen 19:28). Now it comes again as a harbinger of
something awful. But the next time it will be mentioned will be in
connection with God's descent onto Mount Sinai (Exod 19:18). Then it
will still be frightening, but it will be transformed from something
ominous into something reflecting the closest that God ever comes to a
human community in the Bible. And then the word will never occur again
in the Bible. The point: humans must overcome their fear in order to
come close to the divine.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:9*](#Contents.html_rExo9_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And it will become a powder on all the land of Egypt, and it will
become a boil breaking out in sores on the humans and on the animals in
all the land of Egypt."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_9.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:10*](#Contents.html_rExo9_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And they took the furnace ash and stood in front of Pharaoh, and
Moses flung it to the skies, and it was a boil breaking out in sores in
the humans and in the animals.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_10.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:11*](#Contents.html_rExo9_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And the magicians were not able to stand in front of Moses on
account of the boil, because the boil was in the magicians and in all
Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_11.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:12*](#Contents.html_rExo9_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And YHWH strengthened Pharaoh's heart, and he did not listen to
them---as YHWH had spoken to Moses.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_12.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:12. **YHWH strengthened**. Until this point the text has said
"Pharaoh's heart was strong." But now that a plague has struck even the
magicians themselves, one might expect that Pharaoh would begin to
weaken, and so God bolsters Pharaoh's resolve so as to achieve a
particular outcome. See the comment on Exod 10:1. (For more on the
meaning of this expression, see the comment on Deut 5:26.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:13*](#Contents.html_rExo9_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And YHWH said to Moses, "Get up early in the morning and stand in
front of Pharaoh, and you'll say to him, 'YHWH, God of the Hebrews, said
this: Let my people go so they may serve me.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_13.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:14*](#Contents.html_rExo9_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^Because this time I am sending all my plagues at your heart and at
your servants and at your people, so that you'll know that there is none
like me in all the land.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_14.jpg){height="93" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:15*](#Contents.html_rExo9_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^Because by now I could have put out my hand and struck you and your
people with an epidemic, and you would have been obliterated from the
land.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_15.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:16*](#Contents.html_rExo9_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And in fact I established you for this purpose, for the purpose of
showing you my power---and in order to tell my name in all the earth.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_16.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:17*](#Contents.html_rExo9_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^You are still elevating yourself against my people by not letting
them go.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_17.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:18*](#Contents.html_rExo9_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^Here, I'll be showering at this time tomorrow a very heavy hail,
that there hasn't been one like it in Egypt from the day of its founding
until now.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_18.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:19*](#Contents.html_rExo9_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And now send, protect your livestock and everything that you have in
the field. Every human and animal that will be found in the field and
will not be gathered to the house: the hail will come down on them, and
they'll die.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_19.jpg){height="96" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:20*](#Contents.html_rExo9_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^Whoever feared YHWH's word among Pharaoh's servants had his servants
and his livestock flee to the houses.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_20.jpg){height="69" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:21*](#Contents.html_rExo9_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And whoever did not pay heed to YHWH's word left his servants and
his livestock in the field.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_21.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:22*](#Contents.html_rExo9_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And YHWH said to Moses, "Reach out your hand at the skies, and let
there be hail in all the land of Egypt: on human and on animal and on
all vegetation of the field in the land of Egypt."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_22.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:23*](#Contents.html_rExo9_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And Moses reached out his staff at the skies, and YHWH gave out
thunders and hail, and lightning went to the ground. And YHWH showered
hail on the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_23.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:23. **his staff**. Plagues are initiated with Aaron's staff, Moses'
staff, and Moses' hand, or directly by God---four different
ways---showing that it is not the staff or the hand that is doing it.
They only usher in the miracles.

9:23. **thunders**. Literally, "sounds."

9:23. **lightning**. Literally, "fire."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:24*](#Contents.html_rExo9_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And it was hail with lightning flashing in the hail, very heavy,
that there had not been anything like it in all the land of Egypt since
the time it became a nation.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_24.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:25*](#Contents.html_rExo9_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And the hail struck in all the land of Egypt everything that was in
the field, from human to animal, and the hail struck all the vegetation
of the field and shattered every tree of the field.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_25.jpg){height="98" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:26*](#Contents.html_rExo9_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^ Only in the land of Goshen, where the children of Israel were,
there was no hail.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_26.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:27*](#Contents.html_rExo9_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And Pharaoh sent and called Moses and Aaron and said to them, "I
sinned this time. YHWH is the virtuous one, and I and my people are the
wicked ones.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_27.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:28*](#Contents.html_rExo9_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^Pray to YHWH, and enough of there being God's thunders and hail, so
I may let you go, and you won't continue to stay."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_28.jpg){height="70" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:29*](#Contents.html_rExo9_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And Moses said to him, "As I go out of the city I'll spread my hands
to YHWH, the thunders will stop, and the hail won't be anymore, so that
you'll know that the earth is YHWH's.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_29.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:29. **the earth is YHWH's**. Hebrew
*'ere![Image](images/sdot.jpg){height="11" width="6"}* can mean the
"earth" or the "land." It is usually apparent which it means in context.
But here either is possible. It may mean that all the world belongs to
God. Or it may be a direct message to Pharaoh that the land of Egypt
belongs to God and not to him.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:30*](#Contents.html_rExo9_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And you and your servants: I know that you don't yet fear in front
of YHWH, God."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_30.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:31*](#Contents.html_rExo9_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

(^31^And the flax and the barley were struck, because the barley was
fresh and the flax was in bud.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_31.jpg){height="44" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:32*](#Contents.html_rExo9_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And the wheat and the spelt were not struck, because they were late
crops.)

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_32.jpg){height="43" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:33*](#Contents.html_rExo9_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And Moses went out from Pharaoh, from the city, and spread his hands
to YHWH, and the thunders and the hail stopped, and a shower did not
pour to the earth.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_33.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:34*](#Contents.html_rExo9_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And Pharaoh saw that the shower and the hail and the thunder
stopped, and he continued to sin, and he made his heart heavy, he and
his servants.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_34.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 9:35*](#Contents.html_rExo9_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And Pharaoh's heart was strong, and he did not let the children of
Israel go---as YHWH had spoken by Moses' hand.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap9_35.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus10.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:1*](#Contents.html_rExo10_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## C[OME]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **10** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH said to Moses, "Come to Pharaoh, because I have made his
heart and his servants' heart heavy for the purpose of my setting these
signs of mine among them

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10.jpg){height="20" width="31"}

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_01.jpg){height="73" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:1. **I have made his heart heavy**. Earlier the text says that
*Pharaoh* made his heart heavy. Now it says that God claims to have done
it. This verse may refer only to the immediate condition; that is, that
Pharaoh hardened his own heart before, but now it is God doing it. Or it
may suggest dual causation; that is, even when the human (Pharaoh)
decides himself, it is still God who is causing this decision. It is in
the theme of YHWH's becoming known that we may find the explanation of
the contradiction that has occurred even to little children: why does
God harden Pharaoh's heart against releasing Israel and then plague
Egypt for not letting them go?! The fact is that the Torah does not
present the plagues as punishment of the Egyptians for enslaving the
Israelites, but rather as signs by which YHWH will become known. Thus it
pictures God stating unambiguously here:

I have made his heart and his servants' heart heavy *for the purpose of*
my setting these signs of mine among them and for the purpose that you
will tell in the ears of your son and your son's son about how I abused
Egypt and about my signs that I set among them, *and you will know that
I am YHWH*.

Even more dramatically, YHWH tells Moses to inform the Pharaoh that "And
in fact I established you for this purpose, for the purpose of showing
you my power---and in order to tell my name in all the earth" (9:16).
Dispersed through the account are frequent reminders that this is the
purpose of the hardening of Pharaoh's heart (7:3--5; 11:9--10;
14:4,17--18) and that this is the purpose of the plagues (7:17; 8:6,18;
9:14,29). The Egyptians' suffering in the plagues may be understood to
be justified, a consequence of their cruelty; but, still, the *purpose*
of the plagues is not punishment but rather to make YHWH known.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:2*](#Contents.html_rExo10_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^and for the purpose that you will tell in the ears of your son and
your son's son about how I abused Egypt and about my signs that I set
among them, and you will know that I am YHWH."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_02.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}
