------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:3*](#Contents.html_rExo10_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Moses and Aaron came to Pharaoh and said to him, "YHWH, God of
the Hebrews, said this: 'How long do you refuse to be humbled in front
of me? Let my people go, so they may serve me.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_03.jpg){height="96" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:4*](#Contents.html_rExo10_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^Because if you refuse to let my people go, here, I'm bringing a
locust swarm in your border tomorrow,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_04.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:5*](#Contents.html_rExo10_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^and it will cover the eye of the land, and one won't be able to see
the land! And it will eat the remains of what has survived that is left
to you from the hail, and it will eat every tree that is growing for you
from the field.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_05.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:5. **cover the eye of the land**. Meaning: the locust swarm will be
so thick as to make the land invisible.

10:5. **the remains of what has survived that is left**. The phrase is a
triple redundancy: *yeter happ![Image](images/e-image.jpg){height="12"
width="7"}l![Image](images/e1.jpg){height="14"
width="6"}t![Image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11" width="8"}h hanniš
'eret*. Its purpose may be to develop the pun on the name of Jether,
Moses' father-in-law (Exod 4:18).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:6*](#Contents.html_rExo10_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And they'll fill your houses and the houses of all your servants and
the houses of all Egypt, which your fathers and your fathers' fathers
did not see, from the day they were on the land until this day'." And he
turned and went out from Pharaoh.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_06.jpg){height="96" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:7*](#Contents.html_rExo10_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Pharaoh's servants said to him, "How long will this be a trap for
us? Let the people go, so they may serve YHWH, their God. Don't you know
yet that Egypt has perished?!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_07.jpg){height="95" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:7. **Egypt has perished**. They speak as if the country is already
destroyed. Interpreters and translators have tried to make their
statement more logical by understanding it to mean "Egypt is lost" or
"ruined." But I think that Pharaoh's servants are pictured here as
speaking with deliberate hyperbole. The Israelites themselves will use
this very same hyperbole later: following the Korah rebellion, they say,
"We have expired. We have perished" (Num 17:27).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:8*](#Contents.html_rExo10_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Moses and Aaron were brought back to Pharaoh. And he said to
them, "Go. Serve YHWH, your God. Who are the ones who are going?"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_08.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:9*](#Contents.html_rExo10_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Moses said, "We'll go with our young and with our old, we'll go
with our sons and with our daughters, with our sheep and with our oxen,
because we have a festival of YHWH."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_09.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:10*](#Contents.html_rExo10_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And he said to them, "YHWH *would* be with you like that, when I
would let you *and your infants* go, see, because bad is in front of
your faces.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_10.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:10. **YHWH *would* be with you like that**. It is difficult to convey
the Pharaoh's cynicism that is expressed in the Hebrew jussive. He says
"Let it be so" in the sense that there is no chance at all that it could
be so.

10:10. ***and your infants***. Pharaoh is still negotiating, trying to
give the minimum. First he refused to let the people have their
religious festival. His second position was that they could have it in
the land rather than leave Egypt. Now he says that they may leave, but
their children must stay---thus guaranteeing that the people will
return.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:11*](#Contents.html_rExo10_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^It is *not* like that. Go---the men!---and serve YHWH, because that
is what you're asking." And he drove them out from Pharaoh's face.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_11.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:11. **the men**. The women must stay as well.

10:11. **he drove them out from Pharaoh's face**. Meaning: from
Pharaoh's presence.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:12*](#Contents.html_rExo10_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And YHWH said to Moses, "Reach out your hand at the land of Egypt
for the locust, and it will come up on the land of Egypt and eat all the
land's vegetation, everything that the hail has left."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_12.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:13*](#Contents.html_rExo10_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Moses reached out his staff at the land of Egypt, and YHWH drove
an east wind through the land all that day and all the night. It was the
morning: and the east wind had carried the locust swarm.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_13.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:14*](#Contents.html_rExo10_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And the locust swarm came up over all the land of Egypt and lingered
in all of Egypt's border, very heavy: before it there was no such locust
swarm like it, and after it there will not be such.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_14.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:15*](#Contents.html_rExo10_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And it covered the eye of all the land, and the land was dark; and
it ate all the land's vegetation and the fruit of every tree that the
hail had left, and not any plant was left, in the tree and in the
field's vegetation, in all the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_15.jpg){height="101" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:16*](#Contents.html_rExo10_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And Pharaoh hurried to call Moses and Aaron, and he said, "I've
sinned against YHWH, your God, and against you.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_16.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:17*](#Contents.html_rExo10_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And now, bear my sin just this one time and pray to YHWH, your God,
that He'll turn just this death away from me."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_17.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:18*](#Contents.html_rExo10_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And he went out from Pharaoh and prayed to YHWH,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_18.jpg){height="22" width="253"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:19*](#Contents.html_rExo10_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^and YHWH turned back a very strong west wind, and it picked up the
locust swarm and blew it to the Red Sea. Not one locust was left in all
of Egypt's border.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_19.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:19. **Red Sea**. Not the so-called "Reed" Sea. See the comment on
Exod 13:18.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:20*](#Contents.html_rExo10_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And YHWH strengthened Pharaoh's heart, and he did not let the
children of Israel go.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_20.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:21*](#Contents.html_rExo10_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And YHWH said to Moses, "Reach out your hand at the skies, and let
there be darkness on the land of Egypt, and one will *feel* darkness."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_21.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:21. **one will *feel* darkness**. Like the other plagues, this one is
explicitly identified as different from what is found in nature at other
times. The hail and locusts are unlike anything that has ever happened
in Egypt. The darkness can be *felt*. There is no chance whatever that
the Egyptians---or any interpreter of this text---can understand these
as being chance occurrences of nature.

Note also that the word for "felt," Hebrew
*y![Image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11"
width="8"}m![Image](images/e1.jpg){height="14" width="6"}š*, plays on
the name Moses, *m![Image](images/o.jpg){height="11" width="7"}šeh*.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:22*](#Contents.html_rExo10_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Moses reached out his hand at the skies, and there was dismal
darkness in all the land of Egypt---three days.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_22.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:22. **three days**. This is further indication that no natural
occurrence, such as an eclipse, is intended here; no eclipse lasts three
days.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:23*](#Contents.html_rExo10_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^Each man did not see his brother, and each man did not get up from
under it---three days. And for all the children of Israel there was
light in their homes.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_23.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:23. **from under it**. The Hebrew
(![Image](images/page204-01.jpg){height="" width=""}) can mean "from
under it" or "from his place." Everyone else makes it "from his place"
or some equivalent, but I think that the meaning "from under it" is
possible, even preferable. It conveys a much more vivid picture:

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:24*](#Contents.html_rExo10_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And Pharaoh called Moses, and he said, "Go. Serve YHWH. Only your
sheep and your oxen will stay put. Your infants will go with you as
well."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_24.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:24. **your sheep and your oxen will stay put**. Still negotiating,
Pharaoh now agrees to let the children go but wants the livestock to
stay. It is not as strong a guarantee to force Israel to return as
holding the children was, but it would mean that the Israelites would
not have sufficient food to make a long journey, or at least it would
mean that the Egyptians would get the livestock.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:25*](#Contents.html_rExo10_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And Moses said, "*You* shall put sacrifices and offerings in our
hand as well, so we'll do them for YHWH, our God.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_25.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:26*](#Contents.html_rExo10_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And our livestock will go with us as well. Not a hoof will be left.
Because we'll take from it to serve YHWH, our God, and we won't know how
we'll serve YHWH until we come there."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_26.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:27*](#Contents.html_rExo10_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And YHWH strengthened Pharaoh's heart, and he was not willing to let
them go.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_27.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:28*](#Contents.html_rExo10_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And Pharaoh said to him, "Go away from me! Watch yourself! Don't
continue to see my face. Because in the day you see my face you'll die!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_28.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:28,29; 11:6. **continue**. The word---Hebrew root *ysp*, meaning to
do again, add, continue---occurs three times in succession, a final
reminder of Joseph (*yôs![Image](images/e1.jpg){height="14"
width="6"}p*), whom the oppressing Pharaohs did not know.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 10:29*](#Contents.html_rExo10_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And Moses said, "So you've spoken: I won't continue to see your face
anymore."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap10_29.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus11.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 11:1*](#Contents.html_rExo11_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **11** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH said to Moses, "I'll bring one more plague on Pharaoh and on
Egypt. After that he'll let you go from here. When he lets go completely
he'll *drive you out* from here!

![Image](images/Exo_Chap11_01.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 11:2*](#Contents.html_rExo11_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^Speak in the people's ears that each man will ask of his neighbor and
each woman of her neighbor items of silver and items of gold."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap11_02.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 11:3*](#Contents.html_rExo11_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And YHWH put the people's favor in the Egyptians' eyes. Also, the man
Moses was very big in the land of Egypt in the eyes of Pharaoh's
servants and in the people's eyes.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap11_03.jpg){height="91" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 11:4*](#Contents.html_rExo11_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Moses said, "YHWH said this: In the middle of the night I am
going out through Egypt,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap11_04.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:4. **I am going out through Egypt**. YHWH Himself. Later Jewish
tradition, presumably unable to bear the thought of God personally
passing through Egypt and causing the deaths, introduced the horrible
concept of the "Angel of Death." But there is no such thing as an Angel
of Death in the Bible. The text is explicit that God personally passes
through Egypt. The subtlety comes in the question of the relationship
between the deity and the agent of the destruction: on one hand, God
says, "I strike Egypt" (12:13), and, on the other hand, it appears that
God halts at the door and prevents this destroyer
(*maš![Image](images/hdot.jpg){height="15" width="7"}ît*) from coming
into the house. Is the *maš![Image](images/hdot.jpg){height="15"
width="7"}ît* a sickness? a force? or a personified being? The wording
of 12:13 ("a plague among you as a destroyer") indicates that the
destroyer is the plague itself and not some person or angel. In any
case, God clearly is responsible for the creation of it and can
therefore say "*I* strike."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 11:5*](#Contents.html_rExo11_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^and every firstborn in the land of Egypt will die, from the firstborn
of Pharaoh who is sitting on his throne to the firstborn of the maid who
is behind the mill and every firstborn of an animal.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap11_05.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 11:6*](#Contents.html_rExo11_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And there will be a big cry in all the land of Egypt, that there has
been none like it and there won't continue to be like it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap11_06.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 11:7*](#Contents.html_rExo11_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^But not a dog will move its tongue at any of the children of Israel,
from man to animal, so that you'll know that YHWH will distinguish
between Egypt and Israel.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap11_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 11:8*](#Contents.html_rExo11_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And all these servants of yours will come down to *me*, and they'll
bow to *me*, saying: 'Go out, you and all the people who are at your
feet.' And after that I'll go out!" And he went out from Pharaoh in a
flaring of anger.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap11_08.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:8. **they'll bow to *me***. Read this speech carefully. At the
beginning, when Moses uses the first person he is quoting God: "*I* am
going out through Egypt . . ." But here at the end, the first person
refers to himself: "will come down to *me*, and they'll bow to
*me. . . *. And after that *I'll* go out!" It is hard to say where in
the text he stops quoting God and starts referring to himself. This is
tremendously important, a turning point in Moses' life. His fear of
Pharaoh and his lack of confidence are gone. And he has shifted
Pharaoh's attention from God to himself. He has not stepped over the
line and failed to credit God, but he will do that years later in his
sin at Meribah (Numbers 20). This moment in the Pharaoh's court is thus
a step in the long process of Moses' evolving relationship with his God
and his people, which will have a tragic aspect in the end.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 11:9*](#Contents.html_rExo11_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And YHWH said to Moses, "Pharaoh won't listen to you---in order to
multiply my wonders in the land of Egypt."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap11_09.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 11:10*](#Contents.html_rExo11_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Moses and Aaron had done all these wonders in front of Pharaoh,
and YHWH strengthened Pharaoh's heart, and he did not let the children
of Israel go from his land.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap11_10.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus12.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:1*](#Contents.html_rExo12_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **12** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH said to Moses and to Aaron in the land of Egypt, saying,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_01.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:2*](#Contents.html_rExo12_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"This month is the beginning of months for you. It is first of the
months of the year for you.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_02.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:2. **first of the months of the year**. According to the Torah, the
new year begins in spring, not in the fall as Jews celebrate it now. The
holiday that comes on the first day of fall is never referred to in the
Torah as Rosh Hashanah (Lev 23:23--25).

12:2--11. **This month ...** This is sometimes thought to be the
instruction for the annual observance of Passover for all time. But the
wording and context seem rather to apply only to the night before the
actual exodus from Egypt. The Passover instructions for future
generations come later, starting at 12:14.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:3*](#Contents.html_rExo12_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^Speak to all of the congregation of Israel, saying: On the tenth of
this month, let them each take a lamb for the fathers' houses, a lamb
per house.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_03.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:4*](#Contents.html_rExo12_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And if the household will be too few for a lamb, then he and his
neighbor who is close to his house will take it according to the count
of persons; you shall count each person according to what he eats for
the lamb.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_04.jpg){height="92" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:5*](#Contents.html_rExo12_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^You shall have an unblemished, male, year-old lamb; you shall take it
from the sheep or from the goats.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_05.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:6*](#Contents.html_rExo12_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And it will be for you to watch over until the fourteenth day of this
month. And all the community of the congregation of Israel will
slaughter it 'between the two evenings.'

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_06.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:6. **between the two evenings**. Understood to mean the early evening
hours: "dusk" or "twilight."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:7*](#Contents.html_rExo12_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And they will take some of the blood and place it on the two
doorposts and on the lintel on the houses in which they will eat it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:8*](#Contents.html_rExo12_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And they will eat the meat in this night; they will eat it
fire-roasted and with unleavened bread on bitter herbs.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_08.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:9*](#Contents.html_rExo12_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^Do not eat any of it raw or *cooked* in water, but fire-roasted: its
head with its legs and with its innards.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_09.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:10*](#Contents.html_rExo12_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And do not leave any of it until morning; and you shall burn what is
left of it until morning in fire.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_10.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:11*](#Contents.html_rExo12_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And you shall eat it like this: your hips clothed, your shoes on
your feet, and your staff in your hand; and you shall eat it in haste.
It is YHWH's Passover.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_11.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:12*](#Contents.html_rExo12_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^"And I shall pass through the land of Egypt in this night, and I
shall strike every firstborn in the land of Egypt, from human to animal,
and I shall make judgments on all the gods of Egypt. I am YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_12.jpg){height="92" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:12. **judgments on all the gods of Egypt**. The best-known deity of
Egyptian religion is the sun, and Egyptian religion was profoundly
concerned with death. The plagues culminate with "judgments" on these.
In the ninth plague there is darkness for three days. And in the final
plague death itself is shown to be in YHWH's control, as only firstborn
humans and animals die. (The first plague turns Egypt's waters to blood,
and this too undermines Egyptian deities.) Some readers may see in this
an implicit recognition of the existence of the pagan deities, who must
be thought to exist in order to be thus defeated. The text, however,
does not present these "judgments" as a *defeat* of the gods. The
plagues are rather a show of where power resides, namely, outside the
gods (which is to say, outside of nature), beyond them. The forces of
nature are not personified in the plagues narrative, and they do not
confront or challenge the God of Israel. They are merely manipulated in
the course of events.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:13*](#Contents.html_rExo12_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And the blood will be as a sign for you on the houses in which you
are, and I shall see the blood, and I shall halt at you, and there won't
be a plague among you as a destroyer when I strike in the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_13.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:13,23,27. **halt**. Hebrew *p![Image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11"
width="8"}sa![Image](images/hdot.jpg){height="15" width="7"}* does not
mean "to pass over." That wording has led people to images of the deity
floating over houses. The verb means "to halt" or "to walk in a halting
manner"; it can refer to limping (2 Sam 4:4). The noun form
*piss![Image](images/e1.jpg){height="14"
width="6"}a![Image](images/hdot.jpg){height="15" width="7"}* means a
cripple (2 Sam 9:13). Admittedly, this verb occurs in Isaiah in a verse
that pictures God defending Jerusalem "like birds flying" (31:5). Still,
"halting" fits with the context here in Exodus, especially in 12:23,
where it suggests a conception of the deity moving along through Egypt,
spotting the blood on the doorposts, and coming to an abrupt stop. God
"halts on the threshold" and does not allow the destroying force to
enter the house. "Passing over" the threshold does not really fit with
this picture of blocking or preventing the destroyer.

12:13,23. **destroyer**. The Hebrew pun conveys the message: the
Israelites are to slaughter (root
![Image](images/page207-01.jpg){height="17" width="37"}) the lamb so as
to keep out the destroyer (root
![Image](images/page207-02.jpg){height="16" width="38"}).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:14*](#Contents.html_rExo12_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^"And this day will become a commemoration for you, and you shall
celebrate it, a festival to YHWH; you shall celebrate it through your
generations, an eternal law:

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_14.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:15*](#Contents.html_rExo12_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread. Indeed, on the first day
you shall make leaven *cease* from your houses. Because anyone who eats
leavened bread: that person will be cut off from Israel---from the first
day to the seventh day.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_15.jpg){height="98" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:15. **make leaven *cease***. In the preceding verse it is said that
the Passover festival is to be celebrated as a commemoration of what
happened in Egypt. The wording now conveys this: The command to "make
leaven cease" recalls Pharaoh's first meeting with Moses, in which he
says, "You've *made them cease* from their burdens" (Exod 5:5).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:16*](#Contents.html_rExo12_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And you will have a holy assembly on the first day and a holy
assembly on the seventh day. Not any work will be done on them. Just
what will be eaten by each person: that alone will be done for you.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_16.jpg){height="99" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:17*](#Contents.html_rExo12_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And you shall observe the unleavened bread, because in this very day
I brought out your masses from the land of Egypt, and you shall observe
this day through your generations, an eternal law.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_17.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:17. **observe the unleavened bread**. Meaning: observe the
commandment concerning the unleavened bread. The Septuagint and the
Samaritan have "the commandment." The confusion may owe to the fact that
the words "unleavened bread" and "commandment" look the same in Hebrew
consonants: ![Image](images/page208-01.jpg){height="14" width="44"}.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:18*](#Contents.html_rExo12_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, in the
evening, you shall eat unleavened bread, until the twenty-first day of
the month, in the evening.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_18.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:19*](#Contents.html_rExo12_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^Seven days leaven shall not be found in your houses, because anyone
who eats something leavened: that person will be cut off from the
congregation of Israel, whether the alien or the citizen of the land.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_19.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:20*](#Contents.html_rExo12_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^You shall not eat anything leavened; in all your homes you shall eat
unleavened bread."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_20.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:21*](#Contents.html_rExo12_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Moses called all Israel's elders, and he said to them, "Pull out
and take a sheep for your families and slaughter the Passover.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_21.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:21. **Passover**. I have retained the English translation "Passover"
rather than using some form of the word "halt." Although the latter
would preserve the connection between this word and the story of God's
halting, the fact remains that Passover is now the established, famous
name of the holiday, and there is no point in ignoring this in a
translation.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:22*](#Contents.html_rExo12_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And you'll take a bunch of hyssop and dip in the blood that is in a
basin and touch some of the blood that is in the basin to the lintel and
to the two doorposts. And you shall not go out, each one, from his
house's entrance until morning.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_22.jpg){height="97" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:23*](#Contents.html_rExo12_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And YHWH will pass to strike Egypt, and He'll see the blood on the
lintel and on the two doorposts, and YHWH will halt at the entrance and
will not allow the destroyer to come to your houses to strike.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_23.jpg){height="104" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:24*](#Contents.html_rExo12_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And you shall observe this thing as a law for you and your children
forever.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_24.jpg){height="49" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:25*](#Contents.html_rExo12_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And it will be, when you will come to the land that YHWH will give
to you as He has spoken, that you shall observe this service.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_25.jpg){height="77" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:26*](#Contents.html_rExo12_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And it will be, when your children will say to you, 'What is this
service to you?'

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_26.jpg){height="50" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:27*](#Contents.html_rExo12_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^that you shall say, 'It is the Passover sacrifice to YHWH, because
He halted at the houses of the children of Israel in Egypt when He
struck Egypt, and He saved our houses.'"

And the people knelt and bowed.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_27.jpg){height="78" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:28*](#Contents.html_rExo12_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And the children of Israel went and did as YHWH had commanded Moses
and Aaron. They did so.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_28.jpg){height="77" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:29*](#Contents.html_rExo12_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And it was in the middle of the night, and YHWH struck every
firstborn in the land of Egypt, from the firstborn of Pharaoh who was
sitting on his throne to the firstborn of the prisoner who was in the
prison house and every firstborn of an animal.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_29.jpg){height="101" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:30*](#Contents.html_rExo12_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And Pharaoh got up at night, he and all his servants and all Egypt,
and there was a big cry in Egypt, because there was not a house in which
there was not one dead.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_30.jpg){height="78" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:31*](#Contents.html_rExo12_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And he called Moses and Aaron at night, and he said, "Get up. Go out
from among my people, both you and the children of Israel, and go, serve
YHWH as you spoke.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_31.jpg){height="104" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:31. **he called Moses and Aaron**. It is not clear whether this means
that they go back to him or that they just receive a message from him.
The issue is that Pharaoh had said, "Don't continue to see my face.
Because in the day you see my face you'll die!" And Moses had answered,
"So you've spoken: I won't continue to see your face anymore"
(10:28--29). So if Pharaoh sees Moses now, it means that his threat has
proved empty; and Moses' answer to him must be cynical: "So *you've*
spoken!" Or, if Pharaoh and Moses do not see each other now, then Moses'
answer must be understood to be ironic: "You're right, I won't see you
again, because we'll be gone!"

12:31. **as you spoke**. What we have seen here is a gradual
overwhelming of the power of Egypt through twists of events and
personalities, stymieing the Pharaoh's initial confidence and leading
him to surrender unconditionally. In the first meeting he is unbending.
In the second meeting and through the first two plagues, he stands firm
as his magicians perform the wonders as well. The magicians cannot
perform the third plague, and so the Pharaoh, no longer commanding
cosmic forces himself, negotiates on the fourth ("Only don't go too
far"). He gets away with his reneging on the negotiation, and so he
stands firm again through the fifth plague. His magicians themselves
suffer the sixth plague, and, following this indication that the
opposing power can even encroach on his forces, the Pharaoh talks to
Moses again on the seventh, generously sharing the blame with his
subjects ("I *and my people* are wrong ..."). When he reneges yet again,
and an eighth plague strikes, his own servants urge him to give up
resisting ("Don't you know yet that Egypt has perished?"). He then
negotiates again ("just the men"). When this only leads to a ninth
plague, darkening Egypt's divine sun, he negotiates again, allowing the
children, but not the livestock, to go. When Moses insists on the
livestock as well, the Pharaoh returns to his original position,
refusing to let the people go and harshly telling Moses to get out and
never return. The horror of the tenth plague then comes, and the Pharaoh
capitulates utterly. It becomes clear that this had never been a matter
of negotiation at all, but rather an agonizing, gradual drawing of the
Pharaoh to a decision that had been inescapable from the start.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:32*](#Contents.html_rExo12_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^Take your sheep also, your oxen also, as you spoke, and go. And
you'll bless me as well!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_32.jpg){height="54" width="283"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:33*](#Contents.html_rExo12_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And Egypt was forceful on the people to hurry to let them go from
the land, because they said, "We're all dead!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_33.jpg){height="77" width="370"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:34*](#Contents.html_rExo12_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And the people carried off its dough before it leavened, their bowls
being wrapped in their garments on their shoulder.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_34.jpg){height="48" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:34. **before it leavened**. My colleague William Propp asks: Is the
bread on the night of the exodus leavened or unleavened? He answers that
it is leavened. The reason it does not rise is that the people carry off
the dough before it has time to rise. The ban on eating leaven on
Passover forever after this is a commemoration of this occurrence: "You
shall eat unleavened bread ... because you went out from the land of
Egypt in haste" (Deut 16:3). He may be right, or perhaps we should
understand this verse to mean that the people carry off the dough before
they have time to put in the leaven, or that they deliberately do not
leaven the dough because they anticipate leaving in a hurry. In any
case, the historical origin of not eating leaven on Passover has
associations beyond this etiological story. See the comment on Exod
29:2.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:35*](#Contents.html_rExo12_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And the children of Israel had done according to Moses' word, and
they asked items of silver and items of gold and garments from Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_35.jpg){height="75" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:36*](#Contents.html_rExo12_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And YHWH had put the people's favor in the Egyptians' eyes, and they
lent to them, and they despoiled Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_36.jpg){height="50" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:37*](#Contents.html_rExo12_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^And the children of Israel traveled from Rameses to Succoth, about
six hundred thousand on foot---men, apart from infants.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_37.jpg){height="52" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:38*](#Contents.html_rExo12_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^And also a great mixture had gone up with them, and sheep and oxen,
a very heavy livestock.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_38.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:38. **heavy**. The word that described the Pharaoh's oppression and
the force of the plagues now recurs to describe, for the first time,
something good: the substantial quantity of possessions that the people
are able to take with them. It is as if this good is in proportion to
the bad that they have experienced.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:39*](#Contents.html_rExo12_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^And they baked the dough that they brought out of Egypt: cakes of
unleavened bread, because it had not leavened, because they were driven
from Egypt and were not able to delay, and they also had not made
provisions for themselves.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_39.jpg){height="103" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:40*](#Contents.html_rExo12_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^And the duration of the children of Israel that they lived in Egypt
was thirty years and four hundred years.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_40.jpg){height="77" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:41*](#Contents.html_rExo12_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^And it was at the end of thirty years and four hundred years, and it
was in that very day: all of YHWH's masses went out from the land of
Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_41.jpg){height="78" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:42*](#Contents.html_rExo12_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^It is a night to be observed for YHWH for bringing them out from the
land of Egypt. It is this night, to be observed for YHWH for all the
children of Israel through their generations.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_42.jpg){height="77" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:43*](#Contents.html_rExo12_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^And YHWH said to Moses and Aaron, "This is the law of the Passover:
any foreigner shall not eat from it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_43.jpg){height="51" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:44*](#Contents.html_rExo12_44)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^44^And every slave of a man, purchased with money: you shall circumcise
him; then he shall eat from it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_44.jpg){height="51" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:44. **slave**. It is extraordinary that, just two verses after
reporting that Israel went free from Egypt, there is a reference to the
possibility of an Israelite having a slave! The Torah does not forbid
slavery. Nor does it criticize the Egyptians for having slaves, but
rather for *maltreating* their slaves. The Torah rather initiates a
process that eventually is to bring about an end to all slavery on
earth, for Israelites and for everyone else. See the comments on Lev
25:43.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:45*](#Contents.html_rExo12_45)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^45^A visitor and an employee shall not eat from it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_45.jpg){height="48" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:45. **A visitor and an employee shall not eat from it**. These are
non-Israelites, who do not partake of the Passover sacrificial meal.
This does not apply to the Seder meal, which has been celebrated in
place of the sacrificial meal ever since the destruction of the Temple
in 70 [C.E.]{.smallcaps} brought an end to all sacrifices in Judaism.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:46*](#Contents.html_rExo12_46)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^46^It shall be eaten in one house; you shall not take any of the meat
from the house outside. And you shall not break a bone from it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_46.jpg){height="77" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:47*](#Contents.html_rExo12_47)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^47^All of the congregation of Israel shall do it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_47.jpg){height="24" width="224"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:48*](#Contents.html_rExo12_48)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^48^And if an alien will reside with you and will make a Passover to
YHWH, let him be circumcised, every male, and then he may come forward
to do it, and he will be like a citizen of the land, but everyone who is
uncircumcised shall not eat from it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_48.jpg){height="101" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:49*](#Contents.html_rExo12_49)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^49^There shall be one instruction for the citizen and for the alien who
resides among you."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_49.jpg){height="50" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:49. **one instruction for the citizen and for the alien**.
"Instruction" is the translation of Hebrew
*Tôr![Image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11" width="8"}h* because it
denotes both "teaching" and "law." This is the first occurrence of the
word "Torah" in the Torah. It is impressive beyond description that its
first appearance is in a verse declaring that a foreigner residing among
the people of Israel has the same legal status as an Israelite. The
context here concerns the Passover statute, but this principle of
treating a resident alien the same as any citizen will be repeated many
(about fifteen) times in the Torah.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:50*](#Contents.html_rExo12_50)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^50^And all the children of Israel did as YHWH had commanded Moses and
Aaron. They did so.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_50.jpg){height="72" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 12:51*](#Contents.html_rExo12_51)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^51^And it was in this very day: YHWH brought out the children of Israel
from the land of Egypt by their masses.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap12_51.jpg){height="51" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus13.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:1*](#Contents.html_rExo13_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **13** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_01.jpg){height="24" width="260"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:2*](#Contents.html_rExo13_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Consecrate every firstborn for me. The first birth of every womb of
the children of Israel, of a human and of an animal: it is mine."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_02.jpg){height="77" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:3*](#Contents.html_rExo13_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Moses said to the people, "Remember this day in which you went
out from Egypt, from a house of slaves, because YHWH brought you out of
here by strength of hand: And no leavened bread shall be eaten.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_03.jpg){height="78" width="370"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:4*](#Contents.html_rExo13_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^Today you are going out, in the month of Abib.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_04.jpg){height="48" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:4. **the month of Abib**. This is the name of the first month of the
year in the Torah, the month in which spring begins. Later, the name
Nisan is used instead.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:5*](#Contents.html_rExo13_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And it will be, when YHWH will bring you to the land of the Canaanite
and the Hittite and the Amorite and the Hivite and the Jebusite, which
He swore to your fathers to give you, a land flowing with milk and
honey, that you will perform this service in this month.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_05.jpg){height="127" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:6*](#Contents.html_rExo13_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^You shall eat unleavened bread seven days, and on the seventh day is
a festival to YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_06.jpg){height="50" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:7*](#Contents.html_rExo13_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^Unleavened bread will be eaten for the seven days, and leavened bread
shall not be seen for you, and leaven shall not be seen for you within
all your borders.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_07.jpg){height="52" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:8*](#Contents.html_rExo13_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And you shall tell your child in that day, saying, 'Because of that
which YHWH did for me when I went out from Egypt.'

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_08.jpg){height="50" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:9*](#Contents.html_rExo13_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And it will become a sign on your hand and a reminder between your
eyes for you so that YHWH's instruction will be in your mouth, because
YHWH brought you out from Egypt with a strong hand.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_09.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:10*](#Contents.html_rExo13_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And you shall observe this law at its appointed time, regularly.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_10.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:10. **regularly**. Literally, "from days to days," an expression
meaning "from year to year."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:11*](#Contents.html_rExo13_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And it will be, when YHWH will bring you to the land of the
Canaanite as He swore to you and to your fathers and will give it to
you,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_11.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:11. **He swore to you**. But God did not swear it to them. Could it
mean *for* you? Probably not. Rashi, citing the Mechilta, says He swore
when He said, "And I'll bring you to the land that I raised my hand ..."
(Exod 6:8). But there is no swearing of an oath to the people there.
Ramban suggests that the word of God itself may constitute an oath, but
that is not correct. The *Tanak* definitely distinguishes the cases in
which a divine oath is sworn. Ramban also takes the earlier words "He
swore to your fathers to give to you" (13:5) to constitute an oath to
the people. If an oath to the ancestors is in fact the explanation of
this, then I believe we should focus on the words that God says to
Abraham in the middle of their covenant ceremony. There the fire that
expresses the presence of God passes between the parts of animals, which
constitutes an oath ceremony; and the stated purpose of this ceremony is
to confirm that Abraham will possess the land. God states that after a
period of slavery "a fourth generation will come back here" (Gen
15:7--16). Or, more generally, we may say that the promise to Abraham is
repeated to his descendants, the later patriarchs, showing that the oath
carries through the lineage to the descendants. Since the whole people
of Israel is a descendant of Abraham, the oath may be regarded as made
to them as well. I am not certain of the answer to this problem. I point
it out in order to make it known and in the hope that someone else may
solve it.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:12*](#Contents.html_rExo13_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^that you will pass every first birth of a womb to YHWH; and every
first birth, offspring of an animal, that you will have---the males---is
YHWH's.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_12.jpg){height="54" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:13*](#Contents.html_rExo13_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And you shall redeem every first birth of an ass with a lamb, and if
you will not redeem, then you shall break its neck. And you shall redeem
every human firstborn among your sons.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_13.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:14*](#Contents.html_rExo13_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And it will be, when your child will ask you tomorrow, saying, 'What
is this?' that you'll say to him, 'With strength of hand YHWH brought us
out from Egypt, from a house of slaves.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_14.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:15*](#Contents.html_rExo13_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And it was when Pharaoh hardened against letting us go, and YHWH
killed every firstborn in the land of Egypt, from firstborn of a human
to firstborn of an animal. On account of this I am sacrificing to YHWH
every first birth of a womb---the males---and I shall redeem every
firstborn of my sons.'

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_15.jpg){height="123" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:16*](#Contents.html_rExo13_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And it will become a sign on your hand and bands between your eyes,
because with strength of hand YHWH brought us out from Egypt."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_16.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:16. **a sign on your hand and bands between your eyes**. The context
of this and the similar expression in v. 9 indicates clearly that this
is a metaphor, meaning that the Passover observance is to become a
vivid, conscious concern of the people of Israel. The occurrence of the
similar expression in Deut 6:8 came to be taken literally, referring to
the practice of wrapping one's hand and forehead in phylacteries
(*tephillin*).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:17*](#Contents.html_rExo13_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## W[HEN HE LET GO]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

^17^And it was, when Pharaoh let the people go, that God did not lead
them by way of the Philistines' land---because it was close---because
God said, "In case the people will be dissuaded when they see war, and
they'll go back to Egypt."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13.jpg){height="25" width="65"}

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_17.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:17. **Philistines**. Philistines are present along the coast by the
time of Ramesses III, who mentions them in "The War against the Sea
Peoples" (c. 1188 [B.C.E.]{.smallcaps}).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:18*](#Contents.html_rExo13_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And God turned the people by way of the wilderness of the Red Sea.
And the children of Israel went up armed from the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_18.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:18. **by way of the wilderness of the Red Sea**. They are not sent
through Philistine territory because they might be afraid and turn back.
But then they are sent to the Red Sea! Is that better? And, in fact, at
the Red Sea they *will* be afraid and wish they were back in Egypt!
(14:10--12). The difference is psychological. They have been slaves for
generations. Decision making, willpower, and responsibility have not
been part of their lives. One does not acquire responsibility instantly
when one becomes free. Facing opponents and having to take arms and
fight would terrify them, and their natural reaction would be to turn
and flee. But the Red Sea situation is different. With the Egyptian army
behind them and an opened sea in front of them, they do not need to make
a decision, exert willpower, or take responsibility. They have no choice
but to go forward.

13:18. **Red Sea**. Recent commentators and translators have called this
the "Sea of Reeds," a different body of water from the Red Sea; but
there is no such body of water. They say this because (1) the sea is
called *yam sûp* in the Hebrew, and *sûp* elsewhere means a reed (Exod
2:3); (2) reeds do not grow by the Red Sea; and (3) they conceive of a
smaller body of water than the Red Sea being subject to some sort of
drying or splitting, as opposed to the larger, deeper Red Sea. But none
of these considerations outweighs the simple fact that the *Tanak*
refers to the *eastern* arm of the sea---the body of water now known as
the Gulf of Eilat or the Gulf of Aqaba---as *yam sûp* as well (Num
14:25; 21:4; Deut 1:40; 2:1; Judg 11:16; 1 Kings 9:26; Jer 49:21). There
is therefore no doubt that the body of water that is pictured in the
story of the splitting of the sea in the Torah is the body of water that
is known in English as the Red Sea. I have returned to this traditional
English translation so it will be clear what body of water is meant. I
have no objection to translations that use "Reed Sea" or "Sea of Suph"
as long as it is clear that it is this body of water. (As for its Hebrew
name, *yam sûp,* we just do not know how that came to be its name.)

13:18. **armed**. Or prepared to fight, or resolute (Propp). But the
text has just said that God does not want them to have to face a fight
with the Philistines. So why note that they are armed at this point?
Answer: (1) because they will fight the Amalekites a short time later
(see the comment on 17:9); and (2) in recognition of the fact that one
day they will have to fight for the promised land.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:19*](#Contents.html_rExo13_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And Moses took Joseph's bones with him, because he had had the
children of Israel *swear,* saying, "God *will take account* of you, and
you'll bring up my bones from here with you."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_19.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:20*](#Contents.html_rExo13_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And they traveled from Succoth and camped in Etham at the edge of
the wilderness.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_20.jpg){height="44" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:21*](#Contents.html_rExo13_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And YHWH was going in front of them by day in a column of cloud to
show them the way, and by night in a column of fire to shed light for
them, so as to go by day and by night.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_21.jpg){height="70" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:21. **a column of cloud and a column of fire**. The cloud and fire
have a two-sided function, both conveying God's presence and reflecting
God's mysteriousness. The narrative reports that YHWH Himself goes in
front of the Israelites in the wilderness, but they do not see the deity
because He travels in the daytime in the column of cloud and by night in
the column of fire. Exodus must be read with a consciousness that the
cloud or the fire is present at all times, a constant miracle, an
introduction of the cosmic in history. One must have a sense of the awe
involved in this phenomenon, particularly expressed in fire. It is a
miraculously burning bush that first draws Moses. Now the people
experience a similar phenomenon: a continuous, miraculous fire.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 13:22*](#Contents.html_rExo13_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^The column of cloud by day and the column of fire by night did not
depart in front of the people.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap13_22.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus14.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:1*](#Contents.html_rExo14_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **14** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_01.jpg){height="22" width="231"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:2*](#Contents.html_rExo14_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Speak to the children of Israel that they should go back and camp in
front of Pi-Hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea, in front of
Baal-Zephon. You shall camp facing it, by the sea.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_02.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:3*](#Contents.html_rExo14_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Pharaoh will say about the children of Israel: 'They're muddled
in the land! The wilderness has closed them in.'

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_03.jpg){height="49" width="323"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:4*](#Contents.html_rExo14_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And I'll strengthen Pharaoh's heart, and he'll pursue them, and I'll
be glorified against Pharaoh and against all of his army, and Egypt will
know that I am YHWH." And they did so.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:5*](#Contents.html_rExo14_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And it was told to the king of Egypt that the people had fled, and
the heart of Pharaoh and his servants was changed toward the people. And
they said, "What is this that we've done, that we let Israel go from
serving us?!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_05.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:5. **and the heart of Pharaoh ... was changed**. The word that was
used for the stick's changing into a snake and the water's changing into
blood and the changing of the wind that carried away the locusts is now
used to describe Pharaoh's change of heart. Those past cases, plus the
passive (*Niphal*) form of the verb here, indicate that it is not
Pharaoh himself but God who causes the change.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:6*](#Contents.html_rExo14_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And he hitched his chariot and took his people with him.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_06.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:7*](#Contents.html_rExo14_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And he took six hundred chosen chariots---and all the chariotry of
Egypt---and officers over all of it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_07.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:8*](#Contents.html_rExo14_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And YHWH strengthened the heart of Pharaoh, king of Egypt, and he
pursued the children of Israel. And the children of Israel were going
out with a high hand.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_08.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:9*](#Contents.html_rExo14_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Egypt pursued them, and they caught up to them, camping by the
sea---every chariot horse of Pharaoh and his horsemen and his army---at
Pi-Hahiroth, in front of Baal-Zephon.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_09.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:10*](#Contents.html_rExo14_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Pharaoh came close! And the children of Israel raised their
eyes, and here was Egypt coming after them, and they were very afraid.
And the children of Israel cried out to YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_10.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:11*](#Contents.html_rExo14_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And they said to Moses, "Was it because of an absence---none!---of
graves in Egypt that you took us to die in the wilderness?! What is this
that you've done to us to bring us out of Egypt?

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_11.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:11. **an absence---none!---of graves**. Their words are redundant.
The prophet Elijah uses the same expression: "Is it because of an
absence---none!---of a God in Israel that you go to inquire of Baal?!"
(2 Kings 1:3,6,16). The redundancy dramatizes the question in each case,
as if to say, "Was it because of a complete and utter lack ..."---the
way people speak when accusing someone of doing something completely
senseless.

14:11. ***you've* done to us**. As with Joseph, people focus on the
human, Moses, as if he and not God were the one who brought this about.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:12*](#Contents.html_rExo14_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^Isn't this the thing that we spoke to you in Egypt, saying: Stop
from us! And let's serve Egypt. Because serving Egypt is better for us
than our dying in the wilderness!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_12.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:13*](#Contents.html_rExo14_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Moses said to the people, "Don't be afraid. Stand still and see
YHWH's salvation that He'll do for you today. For, as you've seen Egypt
today, you'll never see them again, ever.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_13.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:14*](#Contents.html_rExo14_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^*YHWH* will fight for you, and *you'll* keep quiet!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_14.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:15*](#Contents.html_rExo14_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And YHWH said to Moses, "Why do you cry out to me? Speak to the
children of Israel that they should move!

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_15.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:16*](#Contents.html_rExo14_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And you, lift your staff and reach your hand out over the sea---and
split it! And the children of Israel will come through the sea on the
dry ground.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_16.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:17*](#Contents.html_rExo14_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And I, here, I'm strengthening Egypt's heart, and they'll come after
them, and I'll be glorified against Pharaoh and against all of his army,
against his chariots and against his horsemen.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_17.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:18*](#Contents.html_rExo14_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Egypt will know that I am YHWH when I'm glorified against
Pharaoh, against his chariots and against his horsemen."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_18.jpg){height="43" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:19*](#Contents.html_rExo14_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And the angel of God who was going in front of the camp of Israel
moved and went behind them, and the column of cloud went from in front
of them and stood behind them.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_19.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:20*](#Contents.html_rExo14_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
