^20^And it came between the camp of Egypt and the camp of Israel. And
there was the cloud and darkness \[for the Egyptians\], while it \[the
column of fire\] lit the night \[for the Israelites\], and one did not
come near the other all night.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_20.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:21*](#Contents.html_rExo14_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Moses reached his hand out over the sea, and YHWH drove back the
sea with a strong east wind all night and turned the sea into dry
ground, and the sea was split.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_21.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:22*](#Contents.html_rExo14_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And the children of Israel came through the sea on the dry ground.
And the water was a wall to them at their right and at their left.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_22.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:23*](#Contents.html_rExo14_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And Egypt pursued and came after them, every horse of Pharaoh, his
chariots and his horsemen, through the sea.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_23.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:24*](#Contents.html_rExo14_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And it was in the morning watch, and YHWH gazed at Egypt's camp
through a column of fire and cloud and threw Egypt's camp into tumult,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_24.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:25*](#Contents.html_rExo14_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^and He turned its chariots' wheel so that it drove it with
heaviness.

And Egypt said, "Let me flee from Israel, because YHWH is fighting for
them against Egypt!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_25.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:25. **heaviness**. The recurrence of the word "heavy" to describe yet
another event conveys that the plagues section of the story and the Red
Sea section are united. The event at the sea is not just one more
episode; it is a climax to what has been happening.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:26*](#Contents.html_rExo14_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And YHWH said to Moses, "Reach your hand out over the sea, and the
water will go back over Egypt, over his chariots and over his horsemen."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_26.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:27*](#Contents.html_rExo14_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And Moses reached his hand out over the sea, and the sea went back
to its strong flow toward morning, and Egypt was fleeing toward it. And
YHWH tossed the Egyptians into the sea.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_27.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:28*](#Contents.html_rExo14_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And the waters went back and covered the chariots and the
horsemen---all of Pharaoh's army who were coming after them in the sea.
Not even one of them was left.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_28.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:29*](#Contents.html_rExo14_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And the children of Israel had gone on the dry ground through the
sea, and the water had been a wall to them at their right and at their
left.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_29.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:30*](#Contents.html_rExo14_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And YHWH saved Israel from Egypt's hand that day. And Israel saw
Egypt dead on the seashore,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_30.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 14:31*](#Contents.html_rExo14_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^and Israel saw the big hand that YHWH had used against Egypt, and
the people feared YHWH, and they trusted in YHWH and in Moses His
servant.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap14_31.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:31. **they trusted**. This word is also translated often as
"believed" (Hebrew *wayya'![Image](images/a1.jpg){height="12"
width="8"}mînû*). It does not have the meaning here that it has in later
religious concepts. That is, it does not function in the sense of
believing that a God exists. This notion of belief *in* does not occur
in Biblical Hebrew (nor in other ancient Near Eastern languages). In
pagan religion the gods, being observable forces in nature (e.g., the
sun, the sky, the storm wind), are not a matter of belief but of
knowledge. So in the conception of YHWH in Exodus, God becomes *known;*
God's existence and power are a matter of knowledge, not belief. When
one has seen ten plagues and a sea split and has a column of cloud and
fire visible at all times, one does not ask, "Do you believe in God?" As
the term is used in the Hebrew Bible, it means not belief *in*, but
belief *that;* that is, it means that if YHWH says He will do something
one can *trust* that He will do it.

[]{#Exodus15.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:1*](#Contents.html_rExo15_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **15** {.h2a}

^1^Then Moses and the children of Israel sang this song to YHWH. And
they said, saying:

Let me sing to YHWH, for He *triumphed!* Horse and its rider He cast in
the sea.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_01.jpg){height="96" width="336"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:1. **rider**. This refers to those who are riding in the chariots.
Cavalry was not used in Egypt in this period.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:2*](#Contents.html_rExo15_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^ My strength and song are Yah, and He became a salvation for me. This
is my God, and I'll praise Him, my father's God, and I'll hail Him.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_02.jpg){height="51" width="256"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:3*](#Contents.html_rExo15_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^ YHWH is a warrior. YHWH is His name.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_03.jpg){height="22" width="208"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:4*](#Contents.html_rExo15_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^ Pharaoh's chariots and his army He plunged in the sea and the choice
of his troops drowned in the Red Sea.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_04.jpg){height="47" width="270"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:5*](#Contents.html_rExo15_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^ The deeps covered them. They sank in the depths like a stone.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_05.jpg){height="22" width="275"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:6*](#Contents.html_rExo15_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^ Your right hand, YHWH, awesome in power, your right hand, YHWH,
crushed the foe.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_06.jpg){height="46" width="170"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:7*](#Contents.html_rExo15_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^ And in your triumph's greatness you threw down your adversaries. You
let go your fury: it consumed them like straw.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_07.jpg){height="44" width="189"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:8*](#Contents.html_rExo15_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^ And by wind from your nostrils water was massed, surf piled up like
a heap, the deeps congealed in the heart of the sea.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_08.jpg){height="47" width="308"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:9*](#Contents.html_rExo15_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^ The enemy said, "I'll pursue! I'll catch up! I'll divide spoil! My
soul will be sated! I'll unsheathe my sword! My hand will deprive them!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_09.jpg){height="71" width="253"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:10*](#Contents.html_rExo15_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^ You blew with your wind. Sea covered them. They sank like lead in
the awesome water.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_10.jpg){height="44" width="208"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:11*](#Contents.html_rExo15_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^ Who is like you among the gods, YHWH! Who is like you: awesome in
holiness! fearsome with splendors! making miracles!

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_11.jpg){height="71" width="162"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:12*](#Contents.html_rExo15_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^ You reached your right hand: earth swallowed them.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_12.jpg){height="47" width="257"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:13*](#Contents.html_rExo15_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^ You led, in your kindness, the people you redeemed; you ushered, in
your strength, to your holy abode.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_13.jpg){height="46" width="199"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:14*](#Contents.html_rExo15_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^ Peoples heard---they shuddered. Shaking seized Philistia's
residents.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_14.jpg){height="22" width="298"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:14. **Peoples heard**. This is not just about Egypt. As in the matter
of the plagues, the object is not only to defeat or punish the
Egyptians. It is to make YHWH known in the world. So the Song of the Sea
declares that others hear of this event: Philistia, Edom, Moab, *all* of
Canaan's residents.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:15*](#Contents.html_rExo15_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^ Then Edom's chiefs were terrified. Moab's chieftains: trembling
seized them. All Canaan's residents melted.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_15.jpg){height="71" width="252"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:16*](#Contents.html_rExo15_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^ Terror and fear came over them. At the power of your arm they're
silent like stone. 'Til your people passed, YHWH, 'til the people you
created passed.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_16.jpg){height="71" width="310"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:17*](#Contents.html_rExo15_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^ You'll bring them and plant them in your legacy's mountain, your
throne's platform, that you made, YHWH; a holy place, Lord, that your
hands reared.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_17.jpg){height="71" width="310"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:18*](#Contents.html_rExo15_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^ YHWH will reign forever and ever!

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_18.jpg){height="46" width="269"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:19*](#Contents.html_rExo15_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^Because Pharaoh's horses with his chariots and with his horsemen
came in the sea, and YHWH brought back the water of the sea over them,
and the children of Israel had gone on the dry ground through the sea.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_19.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:20*](#Contents.html_rExo15_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Miriam, the prophetess, Aaron's sister, took a drum in her hand,
and all the women went out behind her with drums and with dances.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_20.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:20. **Miriam, the prophetess**. Here we are informed that she is a
prophetess. This will be crucial later, when she and Aaron will speak
against Moses, saying, "Has YHWH only just spoken through Moses? Hasn't
He also spoken through us?" (Num 12:2).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:21*](#Contents.html_rExo15_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Miriam sang to them:

Sing to YHWH for He *triumphed!*

Horse and its rider He cast in the sea.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_21.jpg){height="71" width="203"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:21. **Miriam sang**. This song is therefore known both as the Song of
the Sea and as the Song of Miriam. My teacher Frank Moore Cross and my
senior colleague David Noel Freedman demonstrated together that this is
one of the oldest---if not the oldest---passages in the Bible. The Red
Sea episode was thus an early and decisive element of Israel's story.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:22*](#Contents.html_rExo15_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Moses had Israel travel from the Red Sea, and they went out to
the wilderness of Shur. And they went three days in the wilderness. And
they did not find water.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_22.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:22. **they went three days in the wilderness**. The original request
they made of Pharaoh---a three-day trip in the wilderness---is now
fulfilled. And they are not returning!

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:23*](#Contents.html_rExo15_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And they came to Marah, and they were not able to drink water from
Marah because it was bitter. On account of this he called its name
Marah.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_23.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:24*](#Contents.html_rExo15_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And the people complained at Moses, saying, "What shall we drink?"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_24.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:24. **the people complained at Moses**. The narrative has proceeded
from the confrontation between Moses and the deity to that between Moses
and the Pharaoh, and now to the ongoing confrontation between Moses and
the people. And, as I indicated earlier, each new stage of confrontation
carries with it the background of what has come before, so that Moses'
experiences with the people---involving sometimes wisdom, sometimes
rage, and sometimes affection---must be read with some appreciation of
the growth that has taken place in his personality through his earlier
encounters with divinity and royalty. Having defeated the leader of
Egypt, Moses now finds himself to be the leader of a people, a people
who never chose him to lead them. Through a series of crises, the people
doubt his competence and rebel against his authority.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:25*](#Contents.html_rExo15_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And he cried to YHWH, and YHWH showed him a tree, and he threw it
into the water, and the water sweetened.

He set law and judgment for them there, and He tested them there.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_25.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:26*](#Contents.html_rExo15_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And He said, "If you'll *listen* to the voice of YHWH, your God, and
you'll do what is right in His eyes and turn your ear to His
commandments and observe all His laws, I won't set on you any of the
sickness that I set on Egypt, because I, YHWH, am your healer."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_26.jpg){height="120" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 15:27*](#Contents.html_rExo15_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And they came to Elim. And twelve springs of water and seventy palm
trees were there. And they camped there by the water.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap15_27.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus16.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:1*](#Contents.html_rExo16_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **16** {.h2a}

^1^And they traveled from Elim, and all of the congregation of the
children of Israel came to the wilderness of Sîn, which is between Elim
and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after their exodus
from the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_01.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:2*](#Contents.html_rExo16_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And all of the congregation of the children of Israel complained at
Moses and at Aaron in the wilderness.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_02.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/f0222-01.jpg){height="27" width="79"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:2. **at Moses and at Aaron**. This is one of the most explicit cases
in the Bible of the way humans focus on the visible human figure rather
than on God. They complain at Moses and Aaron. They say, "*You* brought
us out" (16:3). Moses answers that they will soon see that "*YHWH*
brought you out" (16:6). And he tells them that their complaints are "at
*YHWH*," and he adds, "What are *we*, that you complain at *us?!*"
(16:8). The miracle of manna follows, confirming the message that Moses
has just told them in three different ways. But by the very next
chapter: "the people complained at *Moses* and said, 'Why did *you*
bring us up?'" (17:3). They continue to resist the fact that they are
experiencing divine power, that they are actually encountering God.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:3*](#Contents.html_rExo16_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And the children of Israel said to them, "Who would make it so that
we had died by YHWH's hand in the land of Egypt, when we sat by a pot of
meat, when we ate bread to the full! Because you brought us out to this
wilderness to kill this whole community with starvation!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_03.jpg){height="119" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:4*](#Contents.html_rExo16_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And YHWH said to Moses, "Here, I'm raining bread from the skies for
you, and let the people go out and gather the daily ration in its day in
order that I can test them: will they go by my instruction or not.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:5*](#Contents.html_rExo16_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And it will be that on the sixth day they'll prepare what they bring
in, and it will be twice as much as they'll gather on regular days."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_05.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:5. **on regular days**. Hebrew *yôm yôm*; meaning that the sixth
day's yield will be double what is gathered on an ordinary day of the
week (Sunday through Thursday).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:6*](#Contents.html_rExo16_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Moses and Aaron said to all the children of Israel, "At evening
you'll know that *YHWH* brought you out from the land of Egypt,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_06.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:7*](#Contents.html_rExo16_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^and in the morning you'll see YHWH's glory, because He has heard your
complaints about *YHWH*. And what are *we*, that you complain at *us?!*"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_07.jpg){height="69" width="337"}

![Image](images/f0223-01.jpg){height="27" width="79"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:7. **YHWH's glory**. The glory surrounds God when in the presence of
humans. It is some sort of indescribable substance that veils or masks
the deity, who cannot be seen. "A human will not see me and live." Even
the glory itself is seen through a cloud.

This is the first mention of the divine glory, and a few verses later it
is seen for the first time. It then does not occur again until the
revelation at Sinai. It is impressive, but also sad, that its first
appearance comes in response to the people's complaints over food rather
than as the surrounding of the Sinai event. It suggests the extreme
seriousness of what is taking place in this first period following the
exodus. The people are unprepared, vulnerable, and frightened; and they
are apparently in need of manifest evidence that the power that brought
them out of Egypt is still present, that the exodus was just the
beginning, that what comes next is the acquaintance and continuing
relationship with God.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:8*](#Contents.html_rExo16_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Moses said, "When YHWH gives you meat to eat in the evening and
bread to the full in the morning, because YHWH has heard your complaints
that you're making at *Him!* And what are *we*? Your complaints are not
at us but at YHWH!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_08.jpg){height="96" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:9*](#Contents.html_rExo16_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Moses said to Aaron, "Say to all of the congregation of the
children of Israel, 'Come close in front of YHWH, because He has heard
your complaints.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_09.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:10*](#Contents.html_rExo16_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And it was as Aaron was speaking to all of the congregation of the
children of Israel: and they turned to the wilderness, and here was
YHWH's glory appearing in a cloud.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_10.jpg){height="65" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:11*](#Contents.html_rExo16_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_11.jpg){height="22" width="212"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:12*](#Contents.html_rExo16_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^"I've heard the complaints of the children of Israel. Speak to them,
saying, 'Between the two evenings you shall eat meat, and in the morning
you shall be filled with bread, and you will know that I am YHWH, your
God.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_12.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:13*](#Contents.html_rExo16_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And it was in the evening, and quail went up and covered the camp.
And in the morning there was a layer of dew around the camp.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_13.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:14*](#Contents.html_rExo16_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And the layer of dew went up, and here, on the face of the
wilderness: scaly thin, thin as frost on the ground.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_14.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:15*](#Contents.html_rExo16_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And the children of Israel saw, and each said to his brother, "What
is it?"---because they did not know what it was.

And Moses said to them, "That is the bread that YHWH has given you for
food.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_15.jpg){height="69" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:15. **What is it?** Hebrew *m![Image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11"
width="8"}n hû*!. This is a play on words, because these words can also
mean "It is manna." It thus explains the origin of the word "manna" as
coming from their not having a name for something new. The famous comedy
routine of "Who's on first" in English is actually an equivalent that
conveys what is happening here. They say, "It's what?" And people then
take this not as a *question* but as a *statement:* "It's 'What'!" And
it is known thereafter as "What."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:16*](#Contents.html_rExo16_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^This is the thing that YHWH has commanded: Collect some of it, each
according to what he eats; you shall take an omer per head, the number
of your persons, each for whoever is in his tent."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_16.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:17*](#Contents.html_rExo16_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And the children of Israel did so. And they collected---the one who
took the most and the one who took the least.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_17.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:18*](#Contents.html_rExo16_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And they measured by the omer, and the one who took the most had not
exceeded, and the one who took the least had not fallen short. Each had
collected according to what he eats.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_18.jpg){height="74" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:19*](#Contents.html_rExo16_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And Moses said to them, "Let no one leave any of it over until
morning."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_19.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:20*](#Contents.html_rExo16_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And they did not listen to Moses, and people left some of it over
until morning, and it yielded worms and stank. And Moses was angry at
them.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_20.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:21*](#Contents.html_rExo16_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And they collected it morning by morning, each by what he eats, and
when the sun was hot it melted.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_21.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:22*](#Contents.html_rExo16_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And it was: on the sixth day they collected double bread, two times
the omer for each one, and all the chiefs of the congregation came and
told Moses.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_22.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:23*](#Contents.html_rExo16_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And he said to them, "That is what YHWH spoke. Tomorrow is a
ceasing, a holy Sabbath to YHWH. Bake what you'll bake, and cook what
you'll cook, and leave what is left over for keeping until the morning."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_23.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:24*](#Contents.html_rExo16_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And they left it until the morning, as Moses had commanded, and it
did not stink, and there was not a worm in it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_24.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:24. **as Moses had commanded**. Until now, the text has always said
"as YHWH had commanded" or "as God had commanded." Here, for the first
time, it attributes the commanding to Moses---even though Moses has said
explicitly in the preceding verse that this is what YHWH has spoken.
Moses still acts only by God's direction, but the wording participates
in the gradual impression through the *Tanak* that humans' share in the
responsibility for their fate is growing.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:25*](#Contents.html_rExo16_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And Moses said, "Eat it today, because today is a Sabbath to YHWH.
Today you won't find it in the field.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_25.jpg){height="73" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:26*](#Contents.html_rExo16_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^Six days you shall collect it. And on the seventh day is a Sabbath;
there will not be any in it."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_26.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:27*](#Contents.html_rExo16_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And it was: on the seventh day some of the people went out to
collect, and they did not find any.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_27.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:28*](#Contents.html_rExo16_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And YHWH said to Moses, "How long do you refuse to observe my
commandments and my instructions?

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_28.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:29*](#Contents.html_rExo16_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^See that YHWH has given you the Sabbath. On account of this He is
giving you two days' bread on the sixth day. Stay, each in his place.
Let no man go out from his place in the seventh day."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_29.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:30*](#Contents.html_rExo16_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And the people ceased in the seventh day.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_30.jpg){height="20" width="216"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:31*](#Contents.html_rExo16_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And the house of Israel called its name "manna." And it was like a
coriander seed, white, and its taste was like a wafer in honey.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_31.jpg){height="68" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:32*](#Contents.html_rExo16_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And Moses said, "This is the thing that YHWH has commanded: 'an
omer-ful of it is for watching over through your generations so that
they will see the bread that I fed you in the wilderness when I brought
you out from the land of Egypt.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_32.jpg){height="96" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:33*](#Contents.html_rExo16_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And Moses said to Aaron, "Take one jar and place an omer-ful of
manna there and lay it in front of YHWH for watching over through your
generations,"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_33.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:34*](#Contents.html_rExo16_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^as YHWH had commanded Moses. And Aaron laid it in front of the
Testimony for watching over.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_34.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:34. **the Testimony**. This term is introduced later as referring to
the words of the Ten Commandments on the "tablets of the Testimony"
(Exod 31:18), which are kept in the "Ark of the Testimony" (25:22),
which is in turn housed in the "Tabernacle of the Testimony" (38:21). It
seems chronologically out of place to report here that Aaron placed the
manna there, when the tablets, ark, and Tabernacle have not yet been
made. This problem has nothing to do with questions of authorship in
critical biblical scholarship. Nor is it to be explained by the
traditional rabbinic principle of "There is no early or late in the
Torah." Rather, there is a series of items that are reported to have
been placed by the Testimony in the Tabernacle: the manna, the incense,
Aaron's staff. The placement of each is reported in the context of the
story or law that relates to it. Since the story of the manna is told
here, the report that Aaron placed it at the Testimony is included here
as well. To have saved this report for a later, more chronologically
appropriate, point in the Torah would have meant cutting it off from the
rest of the manna story. The report in the next verse that they ate the
manna for forty years likewise goes chronologically past the present
moment in the story. It thus further confirms that the purpose of the
present context is to cover the entire matter of the manna in one place.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:35*](#Contents.html_rExo16_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And the children of Israel ate the manna forty years until they came
to settled land. They ate the manna until they came to the edge of the
land of Canaan.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_35.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 16:36*](#Contents.html_rExo16_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^(And an omer is a tenth of an ephah.)

![Image](images/Exo_Chap16_36.jpg){height="22" width="229"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus17.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:1*](#Contents.html_rExo17_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **17** {.h2a}

^1^And all of the congregation of the children of Israel traveled from
the wilderness of Sîn on their travels by YHWH's word, and they camped
in Rephidim. And there was no water for the people to drink.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_01.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:2*](#Contents.html_rExo17_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And the people quarreled with Moses. And they said, "Give us water,
and let us drink."

And Moses said to them, "Why do you quarrel with me? Why do you test
YHWH?!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_02.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:2. **test**. In each of the two chapters that precede this, God is
described as testing the people (15:25; 16:4). (It is the same word that
is used for God's testing Abraham in ordering him to sacrifice
Isaac---Gen 22:1.) Now the people dare to test God, and Moses asks them
why they do that. It is a rhetorical question: the point is that there
is no reason to test God. It is presumptuous, because God can be counted
upon. This is significant enough that at the end of the episode the
place is named Massah, meaning "Test," because of what the people dared
to do there.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:3*](#Contents.html_rExo17_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And the people thirsted for water there, and the people complained at
Moses and said, "Why is this that you brought us up from Egypt: to kill
me and my children and my cattle with thirst?!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_03.jpg){height="94" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:4*](#Contents.html_rExo17_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Moses cried to YHWH, saying, "What shall I do to this people? A
little more and they'll stone me!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_04.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:5*](#Contents.html_rExo17_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And YHWH said to Moses, "Pass in front of the people and take some of
Israel's elders with you, and take your staff with which you struck the
Nile in your hand, and you'll go.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_05.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:6*](#Contents.html_rExo17_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^Here, I'll be standing in front of you there on a rock at Horeb. And
you'll strike the rock, and water will come out of it, and the people
will drink." And Moses did so before the eyes of Israel's elders.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_06.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:6. **on a rock at Horeb**. The word "rock" refers not to a small
stone but to a crag of the mountain. God stands at a crag of Mount
Horeb/Sinai.

17:6. **at Horeb**. Here is the story's geography: Moses and the elders
are to go on ahead of the people to Mount Horeb (Sinai). The miracle of
the water will occur *there*, not at the site of the quarrel and
testing. Only the elders see it, not the people (17:6). The people
receive the water somewhere near Horeb, and then they return to the camp
in the region of Rephidim (or perhaps the water flows all the way to
where the people are). The site of their quarrel there is named Massah
and Meribah. Amalek attacks and fights them there (17:8). Then Jethro
goes to meet Moses at Horeb, where Moses is now encamped (18:5). Moses
comes out to meet him and Moses' wife and children there (18:7). Then
Aaron and the elders join them for a sacred meal there. The people come
there later from Rephidim (19:2). The important thing, often overlooked,
is that the miracle of the water from the rock takes place at Mount
Sinai. I learned this from William Propp.

17:6. **you'll strike the rock, and water will come out of it**. Moses,
who was drawn from water, and whose name stands for drawing from water,
now draws water from the rock. He also met his wife by defending her
right to draw water; the first plague he introduced involved turning the
waters (from which he had once been drawn) to blood; he initiated the
splitting of the sea; and he acted to sweeten the bitter water at Marah.
There will be several more incidents involving water in Moses' life. It
seems to be a key element in his destiny, which will culminate in his
tragic act when he strikes a rock to get water again at a second Meribah
(Numbers 20). For this act he will be forbidden to enter the promised
land. His life will end, as it began, at a river, the Jordan, which he
will not be permitted to cross.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:7*](#Contents.html_rExo17_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And he called the place's name Massah and Meribah because of the
quarrel of the children of Israel and because of their testing YHWH,
saying, "Is YHWH among us or not?"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:7. **he called the place's name Massah and Meribah**. That is, he
named it Testing and Quarrel.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:8*](#Contents.html_rExo17_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Amalek came and fought with Israel in Rephidim.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_08.jpg){height="26" width="310"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:9*](#Contents.html_rExo17_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Moses said to Joshua, "Choose men for us and go, fight against
Amalek. Tomorrow I'll be standing up on the hilltop, and the staff of
God in my hand."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_09.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:9. **fight against Amalek**. Israel was not sent by way of the
Philistines' land because they would be afraid of war (13:17), but now,
just a few chapters later, they are sent to war against the Amalek! And
Israel prevails! How do we explain this? Apparently the intervening
events have made a change in the Israelites so that now they have the
confidence and fortitude to fight. What are the intervening events: Red
Sea, Marah, manna, Meribah. They have received four miraculous signs
that their God is still with them even after they have left Egypt.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:10*](#Contents.html_rExo17_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Joshua did as Moses said to him, to fight against Amalek; and
Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the hilltop.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_10.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:11*](#Contents.html_rExo17_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And it was: when Moses would lift his hand, then Israel would
predominate; and when he would rest his hand, then Amalek would
predominate.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_11.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:12*](#Contents.html_rExo17_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And Moses' hands were heavy. And they took a stone and set it under
him, and he sat on it, and Aaron and Hur held up his hands, one from
this side and one from this side, and his hands were supported until the
sunset.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_12.jpg){height="93" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:13*](#Contents.html_rExo17_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Joshua defeated Amalek and his people by the sword.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_13.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:14*](#Contents.html_rExo17_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And YHWH said to Moses, "Write this---a memorial---in a scroll and
set it in Joshua's ears, because I shall *wipe out* the memory of Amalek
from under the skies!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_14.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:15*](#Contents.html_rExo17_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Moses built an altar and called its name "YHWH is my standard."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_15.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 17:16*](#Contents.html_rExo17_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And he said, "Because---hand on YH's throne---YHWH has war against
Amalek from generation to generation."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap17_16.jpg){height="70" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:16. **hand on YH's throne**. No one is sure what this means. It may
be an oath formula. The word for "throne," Hebrew
*k![Image](images/e-image.jpg){height="12" width="7"}s*, may be a form
of *kiss![Image](images/e-image.jpg){height="12" width="7"}'*, as some
versions have understood it.

[]{#Exodus18.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:1*](#Contents.html_rExo18_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## J[ETHRO]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **18** {.h2a}

^1^And Jethro, priest of Midian, Moses' father-in-law, heard everything
that God had done for Moses and for Israel, His people, that YHWH had
brought Israel out from Egypt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18.jpg){height="19" width="48"}

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_01.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:2*](#Contents.html_rExo18_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, took Zipporah, Moses' wife, after
her being sent off,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_02.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:3*](#Contents.html_rExo18_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^and her two sons, of whom one's name was Gershom, because he said, "I
was an alien in a foreign land,"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_03.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:4*](#Contents.html_rExo18_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^and one's name was Eliezer, "because my father's God was my help and
rescued me from Pharaoh's sword."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_04.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:5*](#Contents.html_rExo18_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, and his sons and his wife came to
Moses, to the wilderness in which he was camping, at the Mountain of
God.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_05.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:6*](#Contents.html_rExo18_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And he said to Moses, "I, your father-in-law, Jethro, have come to
you, and your wife and her two sons with her."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_06.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:7*](#Contents.html_rExo18_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Moses went out to his father-in-law, and he bowed, and he kissed
him, and they asked each other how they were, and they came to the tent.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_07.jpg){height="69" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:7. **Moses went out to his father-in-law**. Jethro arrives with
Moses' wife and sons, but only Moses' meeting with Jethro is described.
There is nothing about his reunion with his family. The Bible's silences
are frustrating (where did Cain find his wife? how did Sarah feel about
her son's being sacrificed?), but I think that we should learn to live
with them. Often they are very loud silences---which is to say: very
powerful. Why does the Torah announce the arrival of Moses' wife and
children but not describe a personal meeting scene between them and
Moses? Presumably, this is because it was important to let us know that
his wife and children were present with the Israelites during the
wilderness period, but it was not deemed important to describe their
family relations. Family relations are described in the age of the
patriarchs and in the age of the kings because, there, they play a role
in the destiny of the people. They are described in the cases of
Jephthah and Samson because they propel the life events of those
persons. But in Moses' case the family episodes are few: his mother and
sister protect him as a baby, his wife has a confrontation with him at
the inn on the way to Egypt, Aaron and Miriam criticize his Cushite wife
and are rebuked by God. The rest of Moses' family life is personal. His
wife speaks three sentences in the Torah. His sons speak none. That
tells us, at the very least, that his particular life mission is
primarily about him and God, on the one hand, and him and the people, on
the other. And the nature of that mission is such that his family
situation is not permitted to impinge upon it. The missions of Abraham,
David, and the rest of us are not like that.

It is our frustration with the silences and our hunger for more Torah
that give birth to midrash. So I was once asked to make a midrash on
what might have happened between Moses and his wife and sons. I
suggested: He told them everything---the confrontations with Pharaoh,
the snakes, the plagues, the splitting of the sea. And his sons asked
him why he had sent them away and thus kept them from seeing all that.
And he told them that it was dangerous, and he was protecting them. Did
that show insufficient faith on his part that things would go well in
Egypt? Of course, he says. But he was just starting out and did not yet
know the divine power---or the divine character. And so a father's love
was his first motive. But now, he assured them, they would stay with him
and get to see an even greater event: Sinai.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:8*](#Contents.html_rExo18_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Moses told his father-in-law everything that YHWH had done to
Pharaoh and to Egypt with regard to Israel, all the hardship that had
found them on the way, and YHWH rescued them.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_08.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:9*](#Contents.html_rExo18_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Jethro rejoiced over all the good that YHWH had done for Israel,
that He had rescued it from Egypt's hand.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_09.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:10*](#Contents.html_rExo18_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Jethro said, "Blessed is YHWH, who rescued you from Egypt's hand
and from Pharaoh's hand, who rescued the people from under Egypt's hand:

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_10.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:11*](#Contents.html_rExo18_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^now I know that YHWH is bigger than all the gods, because of the
thing that they plotted against them."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_11.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:11. **the thing that they plotted against them**. Meaning: Egypt's
plot to control the Israelites by enslaving them.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:12*](#Contents.html_rExo18_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And Jethro, Moses' father-in-law, took a burnt offering and
sacrifices to God. And Aaron and all of Israel's elders came to eat
bread with Moses' father-in-law before God.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_12.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:13*](#Contents.html_rExo18_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And it was the next day, and Moses sat to judge the people. And the
people stood by Moses from the morning to the evening.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_13.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:14*](#Contents.html_rExo18_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Moses' father-in-law saw all that he was doing for the people,
and he said, "What's this thing that you're doing for the people? Why
are you sitting by yourself, and the entire people is standing up by you
from morning until evening?"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_14.jpg){height="119" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:15*](#Contents.html_rExo18_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Moses said to his father-in-law, "Because the people come to me
to inquire of God.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_15.jpg){height="50" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:16*](#Contents.html_rExo18_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^When they have a matter, it comes to me, and I judge between each
one and his companion, and I make known God's laws and His
instructions."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_16.jpg){height="77" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:17*](#Contents.html_rExo18_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And Moses' father-in-law said to him, "The thing that you're doing
isn't good.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_17.jpg){height="50" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:18*](#Contents.html_rExo18_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^You'll be *worn out*, both you and this people who are with you,
because the thing is too heavy for you. You won't be able to do it by
yourself.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_18.jpg){height="78" width="370"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:18. **too heavy for you**. "Heavy" again. See the comment on Exod
5:9. And see Num 11:14.

18:18. **able to do it by yourself**. The convergence of the terms for
"to be by oneself" and "to be able" (twice: vv. 18 and 23) in this
passage is striking because they both appear in the story of Jacob
wrestling with God (Gen 32:24--33), which begins with the notice that
"he was left by himself" and later notes (twice) that Jacob "was able"
in the struggle. It is remarkable that Jacob was pictured as able to
struggle with God by himself but Moses is pictured as not being able to
manage the people by himself. Does a person stand a better chance of
being successful in a personal struggle with God than in taking on a
community of humans?

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:19*](#Contents.html_rExo18_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^Now listen to my voice, and I'll advise you, and may God be with
you. *You* be for the people toward God, and *you* will bring the
matters to God.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_19.jpg){height="104" width="370"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:20*](#Contents.html_rExo18_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And you'll enlighten them with the laws and the instructions, and
you'll make known to them the way in which they'll go and the thing that
they'll do.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_20.jpg){height="79" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:21*](#Contents.html_rExo18_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And *you* will envision, out of all the people, worthy men, who fear
God, men of truth, who hate bribery, and you'll set chiefs of thousands,
chiefs of hundreds, chiefs of fifties, and chiefs of tens over them.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_21.jpg){height="105" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:22*](#Contents.html_rExo18_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And they'll judge the people at all times. And it will be: they'll
bring every matter that is big to you, and *they* will judge every
matter that is small. And make it lighter on you, and they'll bear it
with you.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_22.jpg){height="79" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:23*](#Contents.html_rExo18_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^If you'll do this thing, and YHWH will command you, then you'll be
able to stand, and also this entire people will come to its place in
peace."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_23.jpg){height="75" width="370"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:24*](#Contents.html_rExo18_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And Moses listened to his father-in-law's voice and did everything
that he had said.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_24.jpg){height="45" width="407"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:25*](#Contents.html_rExo18_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And Moses chose worthy men out of all of Israel and made them heads
over the people: chiefs of thousands, chiefs of hundreds, chiefs of
fifties, and chiefs of tens.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_25.jpg){height="103" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:26*](#Contents.html_rExo18_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And they judged the people at all times. They would bring the matter
that was hard to Moses, and *they* would judge every matter that was
small.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_26.jpg){height="77" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 18:27*](#Contents.html_rExo18_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And Moses let his father-in-law go, and he went to his land.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap18_27.jpg){height="50" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus19.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:1*](#Contents.html_rExo19_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **19** {.h2a}

^1^In the third month after the exodus of the children of Israel from
the land of Egypt, on this day, they came to the wilderness of Sinai.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_01.jpg){height="51" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:2*](#Contents.html_rExo19_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And they traveled from Rephidim and came to the wilderness of Sinai
and camped in the wilderness. And Israel camped there opposite the
mountain.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_02.jpg){height="74" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:3*](#Contents.html_rExo19_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Moses had gone up to God. And YHWH called to him from the
mountain, saying, "This is what you shall say to the house of Jacob and
tell to the children of Israel:

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_03.jpg){height="78" width="370"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:3. **Moses had gone up to God**. While "going up to God" is an
intriguing image, the Septuagint text is more probably correct. It reads
"up to *the mountain of* God," which fits better with the words that
follow: "And YHWH called to him from the mountain."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:4*](#Contents.html_rExo19_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^'You've seen what I did to Egypt, and I carried you on eagles' wings
and brought you to me.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_04.jpg){height="78" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:4. **on eagles' wings**. The eagle is the highest-flying bird, so
there is no threat from above. Eagles fly with their young on their
backs.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:5*](#Contents.html_rExo19_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And now, if you'll *listen* to my voice and observe my covenant, then
you'll be a treasure to me out of all the peoples, because all the earth
is mine.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_05.jpg){height="77" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:5. **if**. This first occurrence of the word *segullah* (treasure) is
not ambiguous in context: The people of Israel will be a treasured
possession of God *only if* they listen and fulfill their covenant.
Their status is not based on some intrinsic quality but on their
behavior. God is about to make a covenant with them. If they keep their
part of it, then they will be *segullah*. (For more on the matter of
being chosen and a treasured people, see the comment on Deut 7:6.)

19:5. **a treasure to me out of all the peoples**. Since the verse says
explicitly that this treasured status is based on their behavior, and
not on their being superior to anyone else, what then is the purpose of
their being chosen to observe this covenant? The reason was stated, no
less explicitly, to their ancestor Abraham: to be a blessing to all the
families of the earth.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:6*](#Contents.html_rExo19_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And you'll be a kingdom of priests and a holy nation to me.' These
are the words that you shall speak to the children of Israel."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_06.jpg){height="74" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:6. **holy**. For a long time we have taught that this word, Hebrew
*q![Image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11"
width="8"}d![Image](images/o.jpg){height="11" width="7"}š*, does not
refer to some sacred, elevated condition but rather that it denotes
being separate, set aside for some particular purpose. But, really, it
does in fact refer to a special consecrated state. When God tells Moses
at the bush, "Take off your shoes from your feet, because the place on
which you're standing: it's holy ground," this means more than that the
ground is "set aside." It means "holy" in just the way that people
naturally understand it on first reading. The ground has a special
quality deriving from the realm of the divine which makes shoes
inappropriate there. And in the present context, "holy nation" does not
mean that Israel will be separate from other nations. It means that
Israel will be *consecrated* if the people will live the life that their
divine covenant requires of them. (See also the comment on Exod 29:37.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:7*](#Contents.html_rExo19_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Moses came and called the people's elders and set before them all
these words that YHWH had commanded him.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_07.jpg){height="77" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:8*](#Contents.html_rExo19_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And all the people responded together, and they said, "We'll do
everything that YHWH has spoken." And Moses brought back the people's
words to YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_08.jpg){height="77" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:9*](#Contents.html_rExo19_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And YHWH said to Moses, "Here, I am coming to you in a mass of cloud
for the purpose that the people will hear when I am speaking with you,
and they will believe in you as well forever." And Moses told the
people's words to YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_09.jpg){height="104" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:10*](#Contents.html_rExo19_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And YHWH said to Moses, "Go to the people and consecrate them today
and tomorrow; and they shall wash their clothes

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_10.jpg){height="52" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:11*](#Contents.html_rExo19_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^and be ready for the third day, because on the third day YHWH will
come down on Mount Sinai before the eyes of all the people.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_11.jpg){height="74" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:12*](#Contents.html_rExo19_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And you shall limit the people all around, saying, 'Watch yourselves
about going up in the mountain and touching its edge. Anyone who touches
the mountain shall be *put to death*.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_12.jpg){height="78" width="370"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:13*](#Contents.html_rExo19_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^A hand shall not touch him, but he shall be *stoned* or *shot*.
Whether animal or man, he will not live.' At the blowing of the horn
they shall go up the mountain."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_13.jpg){height="78" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:14*](#Contents.html_rExo19_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Moses went down from the mountain to the people. And he
consecrated the people, and they washed their clothes.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_14.jpg){height="55" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:15*](#Contents.html_rExo19_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And he said to the people, "Be ready for three days. Don't come
close to a woman."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_15.jpg){height="50" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:15. **he said to the people, "Don't come close to a woman."** If he
is speaking to "the people," which includes both men and women, why does
he say, "Don't come close to a woman"? Like many passages, this may have
two opposite interpretations. It may be a case of male chauvinism in
language, in which an address to "the people" means *the men*. Or it may
reflect a perception that a command to abstain from sex for three days
needs to be particularly directed to men because men are more likely
than women to violate the instruction.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:16*](#Contents.html_rExo19_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And it was on the third day, when it was morning, and it was:
thunders and lightning and a heavy cloud on the mountain, and a sound of
a horn, very strong. And the entire people that was in the camp
trembled.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_16.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:16. **a heavy cloud**. The word "heavy" recurs, but this time it
refers to something divine.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:17*](#Contents.html_rExo19_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And Moses brought out the people toward God from the camp, and they
stood up at the bottom of the mountain.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_17.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:18*](#Contents.html_rExo19_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Mount Sinai was all smoke because YHWH came down on it in fire,
and its smoke went up like the smoke of a furnace, and the whole
mountain trembled greatly.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_18.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:19*](#Contents.html_rExo19_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And the sound of the horn was getting much stronger. Moses would
speak, and God would answer him in a voice.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_19.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:19. **in a voice**. Here, Hebrew
*b![Image](images/e-image.jpg){height="12" width="7"}qôl* can mean "in a
voice" or "with sound" (i.e., aloud) or "with thunder." In v. 16 the
word refers first to thunder sounds and then to the sound of a horn. But
the divine statement in v. 9 that "the people will hear when I am
speaking with you" suggests that the people will hear spoken words
between God and Moses, and that is what now happens in v. 19.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:20*](#Contents.html_rExo19_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And YHWH came down on Mount Sinai, at the top of the mountain, and
YHWH called to Moses at the top of the mountain, and Moses went up.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_20.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:21*](#Contents.html_rExo19_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And YHWH said to Moses, "Go down. Warn the people in case they break
through to YHWH, to see, and many of them fall.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap19_21.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 19:22*](#Contents.html_rExo19_22)
