[*Exodus 28:10*](#Contents.html_rExo28_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^six of their names on one stone and the names of the remaining six
on the second stone, according to their birth orders.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_10.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:11*](#Contents.html_rExo28_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^By a stone engraver's work you shall inscribe the two stones with
signet inscriptions according to the names of the children of Israel.
You shall make them surrounded by settings of gold.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_11.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:12*](#Contents.html_rExo28_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And you shall set the two stones on the ephod's shoulder-pieces,
commemorative stones for the children of Israel, and Aaron shall carry
their names in front of YHWH on his two shoulders as a commemoration.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_12.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:13*](#Contents.html_rExo28_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^"And you shall make settings of gold

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_13.jpg){height="20" width="156"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:14*](#Contents.html_rExo28_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^and two chains of pure gold. You shall make them twisted, rope work,
and put the rope chains on the settings.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_14.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:15*](#Contents.html_rExo28_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^"And you shall make a breastplate of judgment, designer's work. You
shall make it like the work of the ephod. You shall make it gold, blue,
and purple and scarlet and woven linen.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_15.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:16*](#Contents.html_rExo28_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^It shall be square when doubled, its length a span and its width a
span.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_16.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:2*](#Contents.html_rExo28_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And you shall put a mounting of stones in it: four rows of stone. A
row of a carnelian, a topaz, and an emerald: the one row.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_17.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:18*](#Contents.html_rExo28_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And the second row: a ruby, a sapphire, and a diamond.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_18.jpg){height="22" width="235"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:19*](#Contents.html_rExo28_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And the third row: a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_19.jpg){height="25" width="272"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:20*](#Contents.html_rExo28_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And the fourth row: a beryl and an onyx and a jasper. They shall be
set in gold in their mountings.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_20.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:21*](#Contents.html_rExo28_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And the stones shall be with the names of the children of Israel,
twelve with their names; they shall be signet engravings, each with its
name, for twelve tribes.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_21.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:22*](#Contents.html_rExo28_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And you shall make twisted chains, rope work, of pure gold, on the
breastplate.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_22.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:23*](#Contents.html_rExo28_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And you shall make two rings of gold on the breastplate and put the
two rings on the two ends of the breastplate

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_23.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:24*](#Contents.html_rExo28_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^and put the two ropes of gold on the two rings at the ends of the
breastplate.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_24.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:25*](#Contents.html_rExo28_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And you shall put the two ends of the two ropes on the two settings,
and you shall put them on the ephod's shoulder-pieces opposite the front
of it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_25.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:26*](#Contents.html_rExo28_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And you shall make two rings of gold and set them on the
breastplate's two ends on its side that is adjacent to the ephod,
inward.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_26.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:27*](#Contents.html_rExo28_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And you shall make two rings of gold and put them on the ephod's two
shoulder-pieces below, opposite the front of it, by its connection,
above the ephod's designed belt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_27.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:28*](#Contents.html_rExo28_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And they shall attach the breastplate from its rings to the ephod's
rings with a blue string so it will be on the ephod's designed belt and
the breastplate will not be detached from the ephod.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_28.jpg){height="92" width="338"}

![Image](images/f0268-01.jpg){height="26" width="107"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:29*](#Contents.html_rExo28_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And Aaron shall carry the names of the children of Israel in the
breastplate of judgment on his heart when he comes into the Holy, as a
commemorative in front of YHWH always.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_29.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:30*](#Contents.html_rExo28_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And you shall put the Urim and Tummim in the breastplate of
judgment, and they shall be on Aaron's heart when he comes in front of
YHWH, and Aaron shall carry the judgment of the children of Israel on
his heart in front of YHWH always.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_30.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:30. **Urim and Tummim**. We have pondered for centuries what these
are. They have something to do with inquiring of God for an answer to a
question. They may contain letters that can spell out long answers, or
they may provide only a "yes" or "no," or they may also provide a third
option of "no response." They are mentioned four times in the Torah,
twice in 1 Samuel (14:41, where the Greek text differs from the
Masoretic Text by a full line; and 25:6), and then never again in
Israel's history until the narrative of Ezra and Nehemiah, where it is
noted that answers through the Urim and Tummim are not available (Ezra
2:63; Neh 7:65). We must admit it: we just do not know what they are.
What is important is that several different biblical sources indicate
that there was a belief that it was possible to ask questions of God and
get an answer, and that this was done through a priest, not a prophet.
It was a mechanism other than prophecy to learn the will of God. And it
is known as a practice only in Israel's earliest era.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:31*](#Contents.html_rExo28_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^"And you shall make the robe of the ephod all of blue.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_31.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:32*](#Contents.html_rExo28_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And its head-opening shall be within it. It shall have a binding for
its opening all around, weaver's work. It shall be like the opening of a
coat of mail for him; it will not be torn.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_32.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:33*](#Contents.html_rExo28_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And you shall make on its skirts pomegranates of blue and purple and
scarlet, on its skirts all around, and bells of gold within them all
around:

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_33.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:34*](#Contents.html_rExo28_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^a golden bell and a pomegranate, a golden bell and a pomegranate, on
the robe's skirts all around.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_34.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:35*](#Contents.html_rExo28_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And it shall be on Aaron for ministering: and the sound of it will
be heard when he comes to the Holy in front of YHWH and when he goes
out, so he will not die.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_35.jpg){height="68" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:36*](#Contents.html_rExo28_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^"And you shall make a plate of pure gold and make signet
inscriptions on it: 'Holiness to YHWH.'

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_36.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:37*](#Contents.html_rExo28_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^And you shall set it on a blue string, and it shall be on the
headdress. It shall be opposite the front of the headdress.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_37.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:38*](#Contents.html_rExo28_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^And it shall be on Aaron's forehead, and Aaron shall bear the crime
of the holy things that the children of Israel will sanctify, for all
their holy gifts. And it shall be on his forehead always for acceptance
for them in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_38.jpg){height="95" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:38,43. **bear a crime**. This difficult expression is thought to have
a range of meanings. In these passages it conveys that the priests bear
an enormous responsibility on account of their closeness to the holy.
Coming close to the holy objects or to the altar, they risk violation of
the sacred if they act incorrectly. The seriousness of this matter will
become manifest when two of Aaron's sons violate the holy and die
(Leviticus 10). After the destruction of the first Temple in Jerusalem
and the loss of the ark, tablets, Tabernacle, and Urim and Tummim, much
of the feeling for the sacred must have been lost. And since the
destruction of the second Temple, our sense of the sacred has diminished
even more. So it has become ever harder to comprehend this aspect of the
biblical world and to appreciate the power and awe that were associated
with sacred objects, sacred places, and the priesthood.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:39*](#Contents.html_rExo28_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^And you shall weave the coat in a pattern of linen and make a
headdress of linen. And you shall make a sash, embroiderer's work,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_39.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:40*](#Contents.html_rExo28_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^and you shall make coats for Aaron's sons, and you shall make sashes
for them, and you shall make hats for them---for glory and for beauty.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_40.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:41*](#Contents.html_rExo28_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^And you shall dress them---Aaron, your brother, and his sons with
him---and you shall anoint them and fill their hand and sanctify them,
and they shall function as priests for me.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_41.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:41. **anoint them**. The Hebrew word for anointing is
*mš![Image](images/hdat.jpg){.block height="17" width="14"}*. Its
English form is messiah. Later it is used for kings. Kings Saul and
David are each referred to as the messiah (1 Sam 12:3; 24:7; 2 Sam
19:22). And, later still, Jewish ideas of a coming messiah (Hebrew
*m![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}šîa![Image](images/hdat.jpg){.block height="17" width="14"}*)
developed out of this royal role of anointing. But here in its first
occurrence in the Torah, it is understood that the original use of
anointing is for ordaining the high priests.

28:41; 29:9. **fill their hand**. In the Torah and elsewhere in the
Bible, this expression, "to fill one's hand," means to take on the role
of priest (Lev 8:33; 16:32; 21:10; Num 3:3; 1 Kings 13:33). In Leviticus
it is usually related to donning the priestly garments.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:42*](#Contents.html_rExo28_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^And make shorts of linen for them, to cover naked flesh; they shall
be from hips to thighs.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_42.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 28:43*](#Contents.html_rExo28_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^And they shall be on Aaron and on his sons when they come to the
Tent of Meeting or when they come close to the altar to minister in the
Holy, so they will not bear a crime and die. It is an eternal law for
him and for his seed after him.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap28_43.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus29.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:1*](#Contents.html_rExo29_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **29** {.h2a}

^1^"And this is the thing that you shall do to them to sanctify them to
function as priests for me: Take one bull of the cattle and two
unblemished rams

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_1.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:2*](#Contents.html_rExo29_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^and unleavened bread and unleavened cakes mixed with oil and
unleavened wafers with oil poured on them---you shall make them of fine
flour of wheat.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_2.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:2. **unleavened bread**. Unleavened bread is the food of the priests.
It is eaten with the meat of the sacrifices. Leaven is seen as something
inappropriate for the holy realm, perhaps because its power in the
process of making bread rise is seen as something mysterious.
Biologically, yeast is classified as distinct, not belonging to either
the plant or the animal kingdom. This singular status of yeast
presumably has to do with the origin of the prohibition of leaven on
Passover as well. On Passover, all Israelites behave like priests,
eating no leaven.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:3*](#Contents.html_rExo29_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And you shall put them on one basket and bring them forward in the
basket---and the bull and the two rams.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_3.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:4*](#Contents.html_rExo29_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And you shall bring Aaron and his sons forward to the Tent of Meeting
and wash them with water.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_4.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:5*](#Contents.html_rExo29_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And you shall take the clothes and dress Aaron with the coat and with
the ephod's robe and with the ephod and with the breastplate, and you
shall put on the ephod for him with the ephod's designed belt.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_5.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:6*](#Contents.html_rExo29_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And you shall set the headdress on his head and put the crown of
Holiness on the headdress.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_6.jpg){height="67" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:6. **crown of Holiness**. It is clarified later (Exod 39:30; Lev 8:9)
that this is the golden plate that has the words "Holiness to YHWH"
inscribed on it, as described in Exod 28:36. Like a king (2 Sam 1:10; 2
Kings 11:12), the high priest wears a crown.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:7*](#Contents.html_rExo29_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And you shall take the anointing oil and pour it on his head and
anoint him.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_7.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:8*](#Contents.html_rExo29_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And you shall bring his sons forward, and you shall dress them in
coats

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_8.jpg){height="45" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:9*](#Contents.html_rExo29_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^and you shall belt them with a sash, Aaron and his sons, and put hats
on for them. And they shall have priesthood as an eternal law. And you
shall fill Aaron's hand and his sons' hand.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_9.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:10*](#Contents.html_rExo29_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And you shall bring the bull forward in front of the Tent of
Meeting, and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the bull's
head.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_10.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:11*](#Contents.html_rExo29_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And you shall slaughter the bull in front of YHWH at the entrance of
the Tent of Meeting.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_11.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:12*](#Contents.html_rExo29_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And you shall take some of the bull's blood and put it on the horns
of the altar with your finger, and you shall spill all of the blood at

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_12.jpg){height="70" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:13*](#Contents.html_rExo29_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And you shall take all of the fat that covers the inside and the
appendage on the liver and the two kidneys and the fat that is on them,
and you shall burn them into smoke at the altar.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_13.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:14*](#Contents.html_rExo29_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And you shall burn the meat of the bull and its skin and its dung in
fire outside the camp. It is a sin offering.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_14.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:15*](#Contents.html_rExo29_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And you shall take the one ram, and Aaron and his sons shall lay
their hands on the ram's head.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_15.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:16*](#Contents.html_rExo29_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And you shall slaughter the ram and take its blood and fling it on
the altar all around.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_16.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:17*](#Contents.html_rExo29_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And you shall cut up the ram into its parts, and you shall wash its
inside and its legs and put them on its parts and on its head.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_17.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:18*](#Contents.html_rExo29_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And you shall burn all of the ram to smoke at the altar. It is a
burnt offering to YHWH. It is a pleasant smell, an offering by fire to
YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_18.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:19*](#Contents.html_rExo29_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And you shall take the second ram, and Aaron and his sons shall lay
their hands on the ram's head.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_19.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:20*](#Contents.html_rExo29_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And you shall slaughter the ram and take some of its blood and put
it on Aaron's earlobe and on his sons' earlobe, the right one, and on
the thumb of their right hand and on the big toe of their right foot.
And you shall fling the blood on the altar all around.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_20.jpg){height="118" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:21*](#Contents.html_rExo29_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And you shall take some of the blood that is on the altar and some
of the anointing oil and sprinkle it on Aaron and on his clothes and on
his sons and on his sons' clothes with him. And he and his clothes and
his sons and his sons' clothes with him will be holy.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_21.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:22*](#Contents.html_rExo29_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And you shall take the fat from the ram and the *fat tail* and the
fat that covers the inside and the appendage of the liver and the two
kidneys and the fat that is on them and the right thigh---because it is
a ram of ordination---

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_22.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:22. **ordination**. The root meaning of "filling" relates back to the
term "to fill the hand," further conveying that this term means to
assume the role of priest (as in 28:41; 29:9).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:23*](#Contents.html_rExo29_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^and one loaf of bread and one cake of bread with oil and one wafer
from the basket of unleavened bread that is in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_23.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:33. **outsider**. The word, Hebrew
*z![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}r*, refers
to a person who is not a member of the group that is covered by a law.
If the law applies to priests, the term refers to laypersons. If it
applies to brothers, the term refers to anyone who is outside their
family (Deut 25:5).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:24*](#Contents.html_rExo29_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And you shall put it all on Aaron's hands and on his sons' hands and
*elevate* them as an elevation offering in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_24.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:25*](#Contents.html_rExo29_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And you shall take them from their hand and burn them to smoke at
the altar in addition to the burnt offering as a pleasant smell in front
of YHWH. It is an offering by fire to YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_25.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:26*](#Contents.html_rExo29_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And you shall take the breast from the ram of ordination that is
Aaron's and elevate it as an elevation offering in front of YHWH, and it
shall be a portion for you.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_26.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:27*](#Contents.html_rExo29_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And you shall sanctify the breast that is an elevation offering and
the thigh that is a donation, that is elevated and that is donated, from
the ram of ordination, from Aaron's and from his sons'.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_27.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:28*](#Contents.html_rExo29_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And it shall be Aaron's and his sons' as an eternal law from the
children of Israel, because it *is* a donation, and it *will be* a
donation from the children of Israel from the sacrifices of their peace
offerings, their donation to YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_28.jpg){height="99" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:29*](#Contents.html_rExo29_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And the holy clothes that are Aaron's shall be his sons' after him,
to be anointed in them and to fill their hand in them.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_29.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:30*](#Contents.html_rExo29_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^The priest in his place from among his sons, who will come to the
Tent of Meeting to minister in the Holy, shall wear them for seven days.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_30.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:31*](#Contents.html_rExo29_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And you shall take the ram of ordination and cook its meat in a holy
place,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_31.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:32*](#Contents.html_rExo29_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^and Aaron and his sons shall eat the meat of the ram and the bread
that is in the basket at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_32.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:33*](#Contents.html_rExo29_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And those who have acquired atonement by them shall eat them, to
fill their hand, to make them holy; and an outsider shall not eat,
because they are holy.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_33.jpg){height="76" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:33. **outsider**. The word, Hebrew
*z![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11" width="8"}r*, refers
to a person who is not a member of the group that is covered by a law.
If the law applies to priests, the term refers to laypersons. If it
applies to brothers, the term refers to anyone who is outside their
family (Deut 25:5).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:34*](#Contents.html_rExo29_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And if some of the meat of the ordination or some of the bread will
be left until the morning, then you shall burn what is left in fire. It
shall not be eaten, because it is holy.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_34.jpg){height="76" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:35*](#Contents.html_rExo29_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^"And you shall do thus to Aaron and to his sons, according to all
that I have commanded you. You shall fill their hand for seven days.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_35.jpg){height="76" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:36*](#Contents.html_rExo29_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And you shall do a bull sin offering per day for atonement, and you
shall make a sin offering on the altar when you make atonement for it,
and you shall anoint it to make it holy.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_36.jpg){height="87" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:37*](#Contents.html_rExo29_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^Seven days you shall make atonement on the altar and make it holy,
and the altar shall be holy of holies. Anyone who touches the altar will
be holy.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_37.jpg){height="77" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:37. **holy of holies**. Like the Holy of Holies in the Tabernacle,
this phrase means that this altar is the holiest of all.

29:37. **Anyone who touches the altar will be holy**. The quality of
being holy can spread. Coming into contact with the ark, which is in a
state of holiness, renders one holy as well. The condition of impurity
may spread by contact, too. (This will be a concern in Leviticus.) The
Torah involves a belief that there are certain conditions which are
invisible but which have an effect on persons and objects. The spreading
of holiness by contact also confirms that the word "holy" does not refer
to just being "apart" or "separate," as we have often taught in the
past. Holiness is a powerful condition related to closeness to the
divine. (See the comment on Exod 19:6.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:38*](#Contents.html_rExo29_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^"And this is what you shall do on the altar: year-old lambs, two per
day, always.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_38.jpg){height="60" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:39*](#Contents.html_rExo29_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^You shall do the one sheep in the morning and do the second sheep
between the two evenings.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_39.jpg){height="50" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:40*](#Contents.html_rExo29_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^And a tenth of a measure of fine flour mixed with a fourth of a hin
of pressed oil and a libation of a fourth of a hin of wine for the first
lamb.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_40.jpg){height="50" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:41*](#Contents.html_rExo29_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^And you shall do the second lamb between the two evenings. You shall
do it like the morning grain offering and like its libation for a
pleasant smell, an offering by fire to YHWH,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_41.jpg){height="80" width="370"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:42*](#Contents.html_rExo29_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^a continual burnt offering through your generations at the entrance
of the Tent of Meeting in front of YHWH, where I shall meet with you to
speak to you there.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_42.jpg){height="82" width="370"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:43*](#Contents.html_rExo29_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^And I shall meet there with the children of Israel, and it will be
made holy through my glory.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_43.jpg){height="48" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:44*](#Contents.html_rExo29_44)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^44^And I shall make the Tent of Meeting and the altar holy, and I shall
make Aaron and his sons holy to function as priests for me.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_44.jpg){height="75" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:45*](#Contents.html_rExo29_45)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^45^And I shall tent among the children of Israel, and I shall be God to
them.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_45.jpg){height="53" width="218"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 29:46*](#Contents.html_rExo29_46)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^46^And they will know that I am YHWH, their God, who brought them out
from the land of Egypt for me to tent among them. I am YHWH, their God.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap29_46.jpg){height="76" width="370"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:45--46. **I shall tent\.... And they will know**. In case anyone
might get lost in all the details of these many chapters on the
Tabernacle and the priesthood, this powerful statement comes in the
middle of the section. Once these things are established, then: YHWH
will meet with Moses and the people there, YHWH will be present among
the people, YHWH will be their God, and they will know that YHWH is
their God. It is one of the most crucial passages in the Torah. It is
the basis of all of the law and most of the episodes that will come in
Leviticus, Numbers, and Deuteronomy.

[]{#Exodus30.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:1*](#Contents.html_rExo30_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 30 {.h2a}

^1^"And you shall make an altar for burning incense. You shall make it
of acacia wood.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_1.jpg){height="48" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:1. **an altar for burning incense**. The incense altar goes in the
Holy, so why is it not treated with the ark, table, and menorah?
Elsewhere it is in fact listed together with them (Exod 31:7--8;
35:11--15; 39:36--38). So why is it separated here by other matters for
four chapters? The reason that it comes out of order here appears to be
connected with the fact that it now follows the section concerning the
inauguration of Aaron and his sons as priests. The burning of the
incense is now listed first among their priestly functions. And they are
commanded not to offer "unfitting incense." But that is exactly what
Aaron's sons Nadab and Abihu will do in the frightful story of the
events on the day of their inauguration (Lev 10:1). The order of the
commands concerning the incense altar here sets the stage for those
coming events. And it establishes the extreme importance of precise
obedience to the ritual commandments, especially by those who are
closest to the realm of the holy: the priests.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:2*](#Contents.html_rExo30_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^Its length a cubit and its width a cubit, it shall be square, and its
height two cubits, its horns a part of it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_2.jpg){height="50" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:3*](#Contents.html_rExo30_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And you shall plate it with pure gold, its roof and its walls all
around and its horns. And you shall make a border of gold for it all
around.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_3.jpg){height="76" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:4*](#Contents.html_rExo30_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And you shall make two rings of gold for it below its rim on its two
sides; you shall make it on its two sidewalls, and it will be for
housings for poles with which to carry it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_4.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:5*](#Contents.html_rExo30_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And you shall make the poles of acacia wood and plate them with gold.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_5.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:6*](#Contents.html_rExo30_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

the Testimony, in front of the atonement dais that is over the
Testimony, where I shall meet with you.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_6.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:7*](#Contents.html_rExo30_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And Aaron shall burn incense of fragrances on it. Morning by morning,
when he attends to the lamps, he shall burn it,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_7.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:8*](#Contents.html_rExo30_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^and when Aaron puts up the lamps between the two evenings he shall
burn it: a continual incense in front of YHWH through your generations.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_8.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:9*](#Contents.html_rExo30_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^You shall not offer unfitting incense or a burnt offering or a grain
offering on it, and you shall not pour a libation on it.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_9.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:10*](#Contents.html_rExo30_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Aaron shall make atonement on its horns once per year; he shall
make atonement on it with the blood of the sin offering of atonement
once per year through your generations. It is holy of holies to YHWH."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_10.jpg){height="82" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:11*](#Contents.html_rExo30_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## W[HEN YOU ADD]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

^11^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30.jpg){height="22" width="85"}

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_11.jpg){height="22" width="226"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:12*](#Contents.html_rExo30_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^"When you add up the heads of the children of Israel by their
counts, each of them shall give a ransom for his life to YHWH when
counting them, so there will not be a plague among them when counting
them.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_12.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:12. **add up the heads**. Meaning: when you take a census.

30:12. **so there will not be a plague among them when counting them**.
Why would there be a plague when the Israelites are being counted? In
biblical Israel a census is regarded as a negative thing. It gives a
central leader control: for conscription, corvée (forced labor), and
taxation. When King David takes a census, the result is a plague (2
Samuel 24). And so here a ransom must be taken for everyone counted in
the census so there will not be a plague.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:13*](#Contents.html_rExo30_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^Everyone who passes through the counts shall give this: half of a
shekel, by the shekel of the Holy (the shekel is twenty gerah); half of
a shekel as a donation to YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_13.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:14*](#Contents.html_rExo30_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^Everyone who passes through the counts, from twenty years old and
up, shall give YHWH's donation.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_14.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:15*](#Contents.html_rExo30_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^The rich one shall not multiply, and the poor one shall not diminish
from the half of a shekel, to give YHWH's donation, to make atonement
for your lives.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_15.jpg){height="70" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:16*](#Contents.html_rExo30_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

and give it for the service of the Tent of Meeting, and it shall become

atonement for your lives."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_16.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:17*](#Contents.html_rExo30_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_17.jpg){height="22" width="210"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:18*](#Contents.html_rExo30_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^"And you shall make a basin of bronze and its stand of bronze for

and put water there.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_18.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Exodus 30:19*](#Contents.html_rExo30_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_19.jpg){height="43" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:20*](#Contents.html_rExo30_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

and they will not die---or when they come near to the altar to minister,
to burn an offering by fire to YHWH to smoke.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_20.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:21*](#Contents.html_rExo30_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And they shall wash their hands and their feet, and they will not
die. And it will be an eternal law for them---for him and for his seed
through their generations."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_21.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:22*](#Contents.html_rExo30_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_22.jpg){height="21" width="217"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:23*](#Contents.html_rExo30_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^"And you, take spices, choice: flowing myrrh, five hundred; and
aromatic cinnamon, half as much, two hundred fifty; and aromatic reed,
two hundred fifty;

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_23.jpg){height="90" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:24*](#Contents.html_rExo30_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^and cassia, five hundred---by the shekel of the Holy---and olive
oil, a hin.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_24.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:25*](#Contents.html_rExo30_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And you shall make it oil of holy anointing, an ointment mixture,
the work of an ointment-maker. It shall be oil of holy anointing.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_25.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:26*](#Contents.html_rExo30_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And you shall anoint the Tent of Meeting and the Ark of the
Testimony with it,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_26.jpg){height="22" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:27*](#Contents.html_rExo30_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^and the table and all its equipment and the menorah and its
equipment and the incense altar

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_27.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Exodus 30:28*](#Contents.html_rExo30_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^and the altar of burnt offering and all its equipment and the basin
and its stand.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_28.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:29*](#Contents.html_rExo30_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And you shall make them holy, and they shall be holy of holies.
Anyone who touches them will be holy.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_29.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:29. **Anyone who touches them will be holy**. As in 29:37 above, the
quality of being holy can spread. Coming into contact with something
that is in a state of holiness renders one holy as well.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:30*](#Contents.html_rExo30_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And you shall anoint Aaron and his sons and make them holy to
function as priests for me.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_30.jpg){height="59" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:31*](#Contents.html_rExo30_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And you shall speak to the children of Israel, saying, 'This shall
be oil of holy anointing for me through your generations.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_31.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:32*](#Contents.html_rExo30_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^It shall not be poured on human flesh, and you shall not make
anything else like it in its quantities. It is a holy thing. It shall be
a holy thing to you.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_32.jpg){height="82" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:33*](#Contents.html_rExo30_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^Anyone who will make a compound like it and who will give some of it
to an outsider: he will be cut off from his people.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_33.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:33. **cut off from his people**. The meaning of this phrase is not
known for certain. See the comment on Gen 17:14.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:34*](#Contents.html_rExo30_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And YHWH said to Moses, "Take fragrances---stacte and onycha and
galbanum---fragrances and clear frankincense; it shall be part for part.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_34.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:34. **part for part**. Meaning: there are to be equal portions of
each of the ingredients in the compound.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:35*](#Contents.html_rExo30_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And you shall make it ointment incense, the work of an
ointment-maker: salted, pure, holy.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_35.jpg){height="47" width="226"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:36*](#Contents.html_rExo30_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And you shall pound some of it, making it thin, and put some of it
in front of the Testimony in the Tent of Meeting where I shall meet with
you. It shall be holy of holies to you.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_36.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:37*](#Contents.html_rExo30_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^And the incense that you will make: you shall not make for
yourselves in its quantities. It shall be a holy thing to you for YHWH.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_37.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:37. **you shall not make for yourselves in its quantities**. One
cannot make ordinary incense to burn in one's home out of the same
substances and same proportions as the Tabernacle incense.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 30:38*](#Contents.html_rExo30_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^Anyone who will make anything like it to make a fragrance with it
will be cut off from his people."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap30_38.jpg){height="44" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Exodus31.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:1*](#Contents.html_rExo31_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **31** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_1.jpg){height="22" width="258"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:1. **Bezalel**. Bezalel is the first artist, and he is summoned by
name. The irony is that immediately after this a work of art, the golden
calf, is made! The special importance of the artist and his or her
relationship to the work of art is conveyed as Aaron makes the calf
(32:4) but then, when he has to explain it to Moses, speaks as if it
somehow came to exist by itself: "I threw it into the fire, and this
calf came out!"

Some have taken the name Bezalel to mean "in the shadow of God"
(*b![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}![Image](images/sdot.jpg){.block height="11"
width="6"}![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14" width="6"}l
'![Image](images/e1.jpg){.block height="14" width="6"}l*). To me it also
intimates "in the image of God" (*b![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block
height="12" width="7"}![Image](images/sdot.jpg){.block height="11"
width="6"}elem '![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}l![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11" width="7"}hîm*)
from the creation story (Gen 1:27). (On the absence of the letter *mem*,
compare the name Noah being related to the root
*n![Image](images/hdot.jpg){.block height="15" width="7"}m*, and the
name Samuel being related to the root *![Image](images/s1.jpg){.block
height="11" width="7"}'l*. See the comment on Gen 5:29.) And Bezalel is
filled with the "spirit of God," which also comes from the creation
story (1:2). The allusions to creation are attractive because Bezalel,
after all, as the great artist of the Torah, is the creative one, who
fashions the Tabernacle and its contents, including the ark. Being
creative is the ultimate *imitatio Dei*. And the conclusion of the
account of the construction of the Tabernacle uses some of the same
language as the conclusion of the creation account. (See the comment on
Exod 39:32.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:2*](#Contents.html_rExo31_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"See, I've called by name Bezalel, son of Uri, son of Hur, of the
tribe of Judah.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_2.jpg){height="66" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:3*](#Contents.html_rExo31_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And I've filled him with the spirit of God in wisdom and in
understanding and in knowledge and in every kind of work,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_3.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:4*](#Contents.html_rExo31_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^to form conceptions to make in gold and in silver and in bronze

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_4.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:5*](#Contents.html_rExo31_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^and in cutting stone for setting and in cutting wood---for making
things in every kind of work.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_5.jpg){height="44" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:6*](#Contents.html_rExo31_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And I: here, I've put Oholiab, son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan,
with him. And in the heart of every wise-hearted person I've put wisdom.
And they'll make everything that I've commanded you:

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_6.jpg){height="93" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:7*](#Contents.html_rExo31_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^the Tent of Meeting and the Ark of the Testimony and the atonement
dais that is on it and all the equipment of the tent

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_7.jpg){height="53" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:8*](#Contents.html_rExo31_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^and the table and its equipment and the pure menorah and all its
equipment and the incense altar

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_8.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:9*](#Contents.html_rExo31_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^and the burnt offering altar and all its equipment and the basin and
its stand

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_9.jpg){height="44" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:10*](#Contents.html_rExo31_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^and the fabric clothes and the holy clothes for Aaron, the priest,
and his sons' clothes for functioning as priests

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_10.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:11*](#Contents.html_rExo31_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^and the anointing oil and the incense of fragrances for the Holy.
According to everything that I've commanded you, they shall do."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_11.jpg){height="50" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:12*](#Contents.html_rExo31_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And YHWH said to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_12.jpg){height="26" width="215"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:13*](#Contents.html_rExo31_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^"And you, speak to the children of Israel, saying, 'Just: you shall
observe my Sabbaths, because it is a sign between me and you through
your generations: to know that I, YHWH, make you holy.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_13.jpg){height="99" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:13. **Just**. The force of this word here seems to be to say that the
section preceding this tells what the skilled craftsmen will do, and
what God will do, to produce the holy place and all the holy objects;
and now it will be specified what the people must do to become holy
themselves. Why just the Sabbath out of all the commandments? Because
"it is a sign between me and you." As the sign of the Noahic covenant is
the rainbow and the sign of the Abrahamic covenant is circumcision, so
the sign of the Israelite covenant is the Sabbath. *A sign participates
in the thing that it symbolizes*. (Thus, at first a flag is just a
country's symbol, but later people will say, "I'd die for the flag!")
And so its fulfillment is of exceptional importance. The rainbow sign is
provided by God, and so it is certain. The signs of the other two
covenants must be performed by humans, and so they are commanded with
extreme emphasis and strictness. Both declare that failure to perform
the sign will result in "cutting off" (Gen 17:14; Exod 31:14).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:14*](#Contents.html_rExo31_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And you shall observe the Sabbath, because it is a holy thing to
you. One who desecrates it shall be *put to death*. Because anyone who
does work in it: that person will be cut off from among his people.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_14.jpg){height="77" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:14,15. ***put to death***. The extreme punishment of execution for
violation of the Sabbath must be understood in the context of the
singular importance of the Sabbath: as sign of the covenant and as
*imitatio Dei*---see the previous comment. Also, as I discussed in my
comments on Genesis 1, the special place of the Sabbath in the creation
of the universe marks it as having cosmic significance as well as
historical significance. Combining the cosmic with history, it sets the
history of human events that begins with Genesis 1 in the context of the
very nature of the universe. And it makes time holy. Thus this long list
of holy *objects* concludes with this reminder that periods of *time*
are holy as well. The Sabbath is thus so phenomenally important that a
violation of the Sabbath bears an extraordinary punishment.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:15*](#Contents.html_rExo31_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^Six days work shall be done, and in the seventh day is a Sabbath, a
ceasing, a holy thing to YHWH. Anyone who does work in the Sabbath day
shall be *put to death*.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_15.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:16*](#Contents.html_rExo31_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And the children of Israel shall observe the Sabbath, to make the
Sabbath through their generations, an eternal covenant.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_16.jpg){height="55" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:17*](#Contents.html_rExo31_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^Between me and the children of Israel it is a sign forever, because
for six days YHWH made the skies and the earth, and in the seventh day
He ceased and was refreshed.'"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_17.jpg){height="102" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:17. **refreshed**. This verb occurs only twice in the Torah: here,
referring to God's Sabbath rest; and in Exod 23:12, where it refers to
the Sabbath rest of "your maid's son and the alien." It is remarkable
that the parallel cases are the son of a slave and an alien. And this
comes in the middle of one of the most blatant cases of *imitatio Dei*
in the Torah: humans are to cease on the Sabbath because God ceased
creating on the Sabbath. This now conveys that every human participates
in this divine phenomenon. One cannot rest but still require his or her
slave to work. One cannot rest but still count on non-Israelites in the
community to do the work.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 31:18*](#Contents.html_rExo31_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And when He finished speaking with him in Mount Sinai, He gave the
two tablets of the Testimony to Moses, tablets of stone, written by the
finger of God.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap31_18.jpg){height="77" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:18. **two tablets of the Testimony**. There are two tablets of
testimony/witness, and there must always be at least two witnesses to
give testimony in law. And Moses names two witnesses, heaven and earth,
concerning the people's future at the end of the Torah (Deut 31:28;
32:1).

[]{#Exodus32.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 32:1*](#Contents.html_rExo32_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **32** {.h2a}

^1^And the people saw that Moses was delaying to come down from the
mountain, and the people assembled at Aaron and said to him, "Get up.
Make gods for us who will go in front of us, because this Moses, the man
who brought us up from the land of Egypt: we don't know what has become
of him!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap32_1.jpg){height="121" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

32:1. **this Moses, the man who brought us up from the land of Egypt**.
They talk about Moses as if it is necessary to identify who he is. That
is, they minimize him and push him into the past tense, to make the case
to Aaron that he should act.

32:1. **the man who brought us up from the land of Egypt**. Less than
forty days earlier they heard the divine voice itself say that *God*
brought them out of Egypt, but, like the wine steward and Joseph's
Pharaoh, they still focus on the human rather than on the deity.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 32:2*](#Contents.html_rExo32_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Aaron said to them, "Take off the gold rings that are in your
wives', your sons', and your daughters' ears, and bring them to me."

![Image](images/Exo_Chap32_2.jpg){height="52" width="336"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 32:3*](#Contents.html_rExo32_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And all the people took off the gold rings that were in their ears
and brought them to Aaron.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap32_3.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 32:4*](#Contents.html_rExo32_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And he took them from their hand and fashioned it with a stylus and
made it a molten calf.

And they said, "These are your gods, Israel, who brought you up from the
land of Egypt!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap32_4.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

32:4. **calf**. "Calf" is a bit misleading. It is a young bull. I
retained the word "calf" in the translation because the episode is now
famous as the "golden calf" story.

32:4--5. **These are your gods\.... And Aaron saw ... A festival to YHWH
tomorrow**. So many questions: Why do they use the plural, "These are
your gods," when there is only one calf? What did Aaron see? Why does he
proclaim a festival to *YHWH* in the middle of a pagan heresy? I gave
the critical explanation of these problems elsewhere (see *Who Wrote the
Bible?*). Here I want to give a *pesat* explanation of them as they
appear in the text. The plural "gods" does not appear to refer to the
calf. In the pagan religions of the ancient Near East, people did not
worship animals, and they did not worship idols. They worshiped gods who
were represented or honored by statues. The gods were sometimes pictured
as enthroned on a platform, and the platform was commonly composed of
figures of cherubs or of young bulls. Since there is only one young bull
here, together with plural "gods," we must understand the people to be
perceiving the bull as a throne platform for gods whom they will
worship. Note that the calf itself is not referred to as an idol.

What Aaron sees is this behavior on the people's part. The text does not
reveal what Aaron has in mind when he makes the golden calf: does he
mean it as a throne platform for such pagan deities, or as a throne
platform for YHWH?! All we know is that, after he sees the people
associating it with gods and claiming that these gods brought them out
of Egypt, he declares that the worship that is to take place is "A
festival to *YHWH!*" (Indeed, the word for "And Aaron *saw*" is Hebrew
![Image](images/f0280-01.jpg){.block height="12" width="29"}. In the
original Hebrew text, before the vowels were added, it can also have
meant "And Aaron *was afraid*"! That would make it even clearer why he
would redirect the worship back to YHWH.) Aaron is not completely
innocent in the matter of the golden calf, as we can see from the
defense he offers to Moses afterward (32:24); but it may be that he is
to be credited as a person who recognizes that he has made a dangerous
mistake and tries to turn it to something good. In recognizing his own
error, and in trying to steer a people who have gone wrong onto a
different course, Aaron becomes an extraordinarily valuable model---and
the story teaches an extraordinarily valuable lesson.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 32:5*](#Contents.html_rExo32_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Aaron saw, and he built an altar in front of it, and Aaron
called, and he said, "A festival to YHWH tomorrow!"

![Image](images/Exo_Chap32_5.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 32:6*](#Contents.html_rExo32_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And they got up early the next day, and they made burnt offerings and
brought over peace offerings, and the people sat down to eat and drink,
and they got up to fool around.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap32_6.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

32:6. **fool around**. Some of the occurrences of this word involve sex
(Gen 26:8; 39:14,17), and some do not (Gen 19:14; 21:9; Judg 16:25). It
is not justified, therefore, to assume that sexual play is implied here.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 32:7*](#Contents.html_rExo32_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And YHWH spoke to Moses: "Go. Go down. Because your people, whom you
brought up from the land of Egypt, has corrupted.

![Image](images/Exo_Chap32_7.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

32:7. ***your* people, whom *you* brought up from the land of Egypt**.
The issue of who brought the people out, which came up in v. 1, now has
an extraordinary twist. Like a parent who says to one's spouse about a
naughty child, "Guess what *your* child did today!"---so God now says to
Moses, "*Your* people, whom *you* brought up from the land of Egypt, has
corrupted!" But look at Moses' words in response. He seeks to appeal to
God's compassion and feelings for the people, and so he turns the
pronoun around, saying, "Why should your anger flare at *your* people,
whom *you* brought out from the land of Egypt? ... Relent about the bad
to *your* people." And the text conveys that Moses' case is successful,
because it concludes: "YHWH relented about the bad that He had spoken,
to do to *His* people"!

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Exodus 32:8*](#Contents.html_rExo32_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^They've turned quickly from the way that I commanded them. They've
made themselves a molten calf, and they've bowed to it and sacrificed to
it and said, 'These are your gods, Israel, who brought you up from the
land of Egypt!'"
