[*Leviticus 5:14*](#Contents.html_rlev5_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap5_14.jpg){height="22" width="236"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 5:15*](#Contents.html_rlev5_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^"A person who will make a breach and sin by mistake among YHWH's
holy things: he shall bring his guilt offering to YHWH, an unblemished
ram from the flock by your evaluation of silver by shekels, by the
shekel of the Holy, as a guilt offering.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap5_15.jpg){height="91" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

5:15. **make a breach**. The term, Hebrew *ma'al,* refers to trespassing
into a zone that is forbidden. Here it refers to a layperson's
mistakenly taking some of the holy objects, something that is no longer
available, which would in most cases mean food that was meant for the
priests. (Lev 22:14--16 refers explicitly to a layperson mistakenly
eating the holy things, and the penalty is the same: add a fifth.) In
Joshua 7:1, this term, "breach," refers to Achan's taking some of the
spoils of Jericho, which the Israelites had been forbidden to take under
the law of *![Image](images/hdot.jpg){.block height="15" width="7"}erem*
(complete destruction in wars commanded by God; see, e.g., Deut 13:18).
In Num 5:6, it refers to unlawful acquisition of another person's
property. In Num 5:12, a woman's adultery is referred to as a *ma'al*
against her husband. In Lev 26:40, it refers generally to a breach of
faith toward God by the community.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 5:16*](#Contents.html_rlev5_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And for what he sinned from the Holy he shall pay and add to it a
fifth of it and give it to the priest, and the priest shall make
atonement over him with the ram of the guilt offering, and it shall be
forgiven for him.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap5_16.jpg){height="99" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 5:17*](#Contents.html_rlev5_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^"And if a person who will sin and commit one of any of YHWH's
commandments that are not to be done and did not know and became guilty,
then he shall bear his crime.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap5_17.jpg){height="70" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 5:18*](#Contents.html_rlev5_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And he shall bring an unblemished ram from the flock by your
evaluation as a guilt offering to the priest, and the priest shall make
atonement over him, over his mistake that he made and had not known, and
it shall be forgiven for him.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap5_18.jpg){height="70" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 5:19*](#Contents.html_rlev5_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^It is a guilt offering. He *is* guilty to YHWH."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap5_19.jpg){height="44" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 5:20*](#Contents.html_rlev5_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap5_20.jpg){height="46" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 5:21*](#Contents.html_rlev5_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^"A person who will sin and make a breach against YHWH and tell a lie
against his fellow in a matter of a deposit or something set in hand or
by robbery, or has exploited his fellow

![Image](images/Lev_Chap5_21.jpg){height="74" width="343"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 5:22*](#Contents.html_rlev5_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^or has found something that was lost and has lied about it or sworn
to a falsehood---for one of any of these that a human will do to sin:

![Image](images/Lev_Chap5_22.jpg){height="69" width="343"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 5:23*](#Contents.html_rlev5_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^it will be, when he sins and is guilty, that he shall bring back the
thing that he robbed or the thing that he coerced or the thing that was
deposited with him or the lost thing that he found

![Image](images/Lev_Chap5_23.jpg){height="99" width="349"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 5:24*](#Contents.html_rlev5_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^or anything about which he swore as a falsehood, and he shall pay it
in its worth and add to it a fifth of it. He shall give it to the one
whose it is on the day of his being guilty,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap5_24.jpg){height="72" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 5:25*](#Contents.html_rlev5_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^and he shall bring his guilt offering to YHWH: an unblemished ram
from the flock by your evaluation as a guilt offering, to the priest.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap5_25.jpg){height="68" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 5:26*](#Contents.html_rlev5_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And the priest shall atone over him in front of YHWH, and it will be
forgiven for him, for any one out of all that he will do to become
guilty through it."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap5_26.jpg){height="49" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Lev_Chapter06.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:1*](#Contents.html_rlev6_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## C[OMMAND]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **6** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6.jpg){height="19" width="26"}

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_01.jpg){height="22" width="242"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:2*](#Contents.html_rlev6_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Command Aaron and his sons, saying: This is the instruction for the
burnt offering. It is the one that goes up on its place of burning on
the altar all night until morning, and the altar's fire shall be kept
burning through it.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_02.jpg){height="91" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

6:2. **the one that goes up on its place of burning**. Unlike the
sacrifices that are eaten, the "burnt offering"---literally "the one
that goes up"---is completely burned.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:3*](#Contents.html_rlev6_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And the priest shall wear his linen garment and shall wear linen
drawers on his flesh. And he shall lift the ashes to which the fire will
consume the burnt offering on the altar, and he shall set them beside
the altar.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_03.jpg){height="96" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:4*](#Contents.html_rlev6_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And he shall take off his clothes and wear other clothes, and he
shall take the ashes outside the camp, to a pure place.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_04.jpg){height="74" width="348"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

6:4. **he shall take off his clothes and wear other clothes**. The
priest wears the special priestly garments when he gathers the ashes in
the Tabernacle courtyard, but he changes to other clothes when he
carries the ashes out of the camp. Why? Because the sacred garments can
be worn only in the sacred place. This is another case of zones of
holiness.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:5*](#Contents.html_rlev6_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And the fire on the altar shall be kept burning through it. It shall
not go out. And the priest shall burn wood on it morning by morning, and
he shall arrange the burnt offering on it, and he shall burn the fat of
the peace offerings to smoke on it.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_05.jpg){height="94" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:6*](#Contents.html_rlev6_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^Fire always shall be kept burning on the altar. It shall not go out.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_06.jpg){height="38" width="343"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

6:6. **Fire always shall be kept burning on the altar**. **It shall not
go out**. If it will always be kept burning, of course it will not go
out. This is redundant, and this makes it emphatic: this ritual law has
special significance because it brings together space and time. YHWH
tells Moses to command Aaron and his sons to keep an eternal fire
burning at the altar. This law thus provides for a visual expression of
the idea that the ritual structure that is starting here is to prevail
for all time to come. This fire that is not to be quenched is also
reminiscent of the account in Exodus of Moses' encounter with YHWH at a
bush that burns without being consumed. Like the laws of distinction
that are reminiscent of the creation in Genesis 1 (see the comment on
Lev 10:10), this passage serves to connect the law and the Torah's
story. In both cases, the priests are required by the law to perform an
act that is imitative of something that God has done in the
narrative---which further points to the bond between law and history in
the Torah.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:7*](#Contents.html_rlev6_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^"And this is the instruction for the grain offering. The sons of
Aaron shall bring it forward in front of YHWH to the front of the altar.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_07.jpg){height="50" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:8*](#Contents.html_rlev6_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And he shall lift some of it in his fist, some of the fine flour of
the grain offering and some of its oil and all of the frankincense that
is on the grain offering, and he shall burn a representative portion of
it to smoke at the altar as a pleasant smell to YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_08.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:9*](#Contents.html_rlev6_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Aaron and his sons shall eat the remainder of it. It shall be
eaten as unleavened bread, in a holy place. They shall eat it in the
courtyard of the Tent of Meeting.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_09.jpg){height="78" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:10*](#Contents.html_rlev6_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it as their portion
from my offerings by fire. It is the holy of holies, like the sin
offering and like the guilt offering.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_10.jpg){height="46" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:11*](#Contents.html_rlev6_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^Every male among the sons of Aaron shall eat it---an eternal law
through your generations---from YHWH's offerings by fire. Anything that
will touch them will become holy."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_11.jpg){height="67" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:12*](#Contents.html_rlev6_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_12.jpg){height="22" width="223"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:13*](#Contents.html_rlev6_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^"This is the offering of Aaron and his sons that they shall bring to
YHWH in the day of his being anointed: a tenth of an ephah of fine flour
as a continual grain offering, half of it in the morning and half of it
in the evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_13.jpg){height="98" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:14*](#Contents.html_rlev6_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^It shall be made on a griddle in oil. You shall bring it well mixed.
You shall offer it as cakes of a grain offering of pieces, a pleasant
smell to YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_14.jpg){height="71" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:15*](#Contents.html_rlev6_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And the anointed priest in his place from among his sons shall do
it, an eternal law, for YHWH: it shall be burned to smoke entirely.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_15.jpg){height="46" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:16*](#Contents.html_rlev6_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And every grain offering of a priest shall be done entirely. It
shall not be eaten."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_16.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:17*](#Contents.html_rlev6_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_17.jpg){height="44" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:18*](#Contents.html_rlev6_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^"Speak to Aaron and to his sons, saying: This is the instruction for
the sin offering. In the place where the burnt offering will be
slaughtered, the sin offering shall be slaughtered, in front of YHWH. It
is the holy of holies.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_18.jpg){height="94" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:19*](#Contents.html_rlev6_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^The priest who makes it a sin offering shall eat it. It shall be
eaten in a holy place, in the courtyard of the Tent of Meeting.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_19.jpg){height="50" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:20*](#Contents.html_rlev6_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^Anything that will touch the meat of it will become holy. And if
some of its blood will spatter on clothing, you shall wash the thing on
which it will spatter in a holy place.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_20.jpg){height="74" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:21*](#Contents.html_rlev6_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And a clay container in which it will be boiled shall be broken. And
if it was boiled in a brass container then it shall be scoured and
rinsed with water.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_21.jpg){height="75" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:22*](#Contents.html_rlev6_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^Every male among the priests shall eat it. It is the holy of holies.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_22.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 6:23*](#Contents.html_rlev6_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And every sin offering some of whose blood will be brought to the
Tent of Meeting for atoning in the Holy shall not be eaten. It shall be
burned in fire.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap6_23.jpg){height="65" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Lev_Chapter07.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:1*](#Contents.html_rlev7_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **7** {.h2a}

^1^"And this is the instruction for the guilt offering. It is the holy
of holies.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_01.jpg){height="20" width="314"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:2*](#Contents.html_rlev7_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^They shall slaughter the guilt offering in the place where they
slaughter the burnt offering. And he shall fling its blood on the altar
all around.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_02.jpg){height="50" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:3*](#Contents.html_rlev7_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And he shall bring forward from it all its fat, the fat tail and the
fat that covers the innards,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_03.jpg){height="69" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:4*](#Contents.html_rlev7_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^and the two kidneys and the fat that is on them, that is on the
loins; and the lobe on the liver: he shall take it away with the
kidneys.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_04.jpg){height="74" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:5*](#Contents.html_rlev7_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And the priest shall burn them to smoke at the altar, an offering by
fire to YHWH. It is a guilt offering.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_05.jpg){height="46" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:6*](#Contents.html_rlev7_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^Every male among the priests shall eat it. It shall be eaten in a
holy place. It is the holy of holies.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_06.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:7*](#Contents.html_rlev7_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^As with the sin offering, so with the guilt offering: they have one
instruction. The priest who will make atonement with it: it shall be
his.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_07.jpg){height="50" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:8*](#Contents.html_rlev7_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And the priest who brings forward a man's burnt offering: the skin of
the burnt offering that he brought forward is for the priest; it shall
be his.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_08.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:9*](#Contents.html_rlev7_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And every grain offering that will be baked in an oven and every one
made in a pan and on a griddle shall be for the priest who brings it; it
shall be his.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_09.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:10*](#Contents.html_rlev7_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And every grain offering mixed with oil or dry shall be for all the
sons of Aaron, each like his brother.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_10.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:11*](#Contents.html_rlev7_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^"And this is the instruction of the peace-offering sacrifice that
one shall bring forward to YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_11.jpg){height="50" width="310"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:12*](#Contents.html_rlev7_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^If he will bring it forward out of thanks then he shall bring
forward with the sacrifice of thanks unleavened cakes mixed with oil and
unleavened wafers with oil poured on them and fine flour well mixed,
cakes, mixed with oil.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_12.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:13*](#Contents.html_rlev7_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^He shall make his offering with cakes of leavened bread, with his
peace-offering sacrifice of thanks.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_13.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:14*](#Contents.html_rlev7_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And he shall bring forward from it one of each offering as a
donation for YHWH. For the priest who flings the blood of the peace
offerings: it shall be his.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_14.jpg){height="74" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:15*](#Contents.html_rlev7_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And the meat of his peace-offering sacrifice shall be eaten on the
day of his offering. He shall not leave any of it until morning.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_15.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:16*](#Contents.html_rlev7_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And if his sacrificial offering is a vow or a contribution, it shall
be eaten on the day that he brings forward his sacrifice, and on the
next day what is left of it may be eaten.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_16.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:17*](#Contents.html_rlev7_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And what is left of the meat of the sacrifice on the third day shall
be burned in fire.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_17.jpg){height="22" width="336"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:18*](#Contents.html_rlev7_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And if any of the meat of his peace-offering sacrifice *will* be
eaten on the third day, it shall not be acceptable. The one who brings
it forward: it shall not be counted for him. It shall be a repugnant
thing. And the person who eats any of it shall bear his crime.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_18.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:19*](#Contents.html_rlev7_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And meat that will touch any impure thing shall not be eaten. It
shall be burned in fire. And meat: everyone who is pure may eat meat,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_19.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:20*](#Contents.html_rlev7_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^but the person who will eat meat from the peace-offering sacrifice
that is YHWH's while his impurity is on him, that person will be cut off
from his people.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_20.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:21*](#Contents.html_rlev7_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And a person who will touch any impure thing, human impurity or an
impure animal or any impure detestable thing, and will eat some of the
meat of the peace-offering sacrifice that is YHWH's: that person will be
cut off from his people."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_21.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:22*](#Contents.html_rlev7_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_22.jpg){height="21" width="220"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:23*](#Contents.html_rlev7_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^"Speak to the children of Israel, saying: You shall not eat any fat
of an ox or sheep or goat.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_23.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:24*](#Contents.html_rlev7_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And fat of a carcass and fat of a torn animal may be used for any
work, but you shall not *eat* it.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_24.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:24. **carcass**. The body of an animal that died on its own.

7:24. **torn animal**. The body of an animal that was killed by another
animal.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:25*](#Contents.html_rlev7_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^Because anyone who eats fat from an animal from which one would
bring forward an offering by fire to YHWH, the person who eats it will
be cut off from his people.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_25.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:26*](#Contents.html_rlev7_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And you shall not eat any blood in all of your homes, a bird's or an
animal's.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_26.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:27*](#Contents.html_rlev7_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^Any person who will eat any blood, that person will be cut off from
his people."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_27.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:28*](#Contents.html_rlev7_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_28.jpg){height="22" width="220"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:29*](#Contents.html_rlev7_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^"Speak to the children of Israel, saying: The one who brings forward
his peace-offering sacrifice to YHWH shall bring his offering to YHWH
from his peace-offering sacrifice:

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_29.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:30*](#Contents.html_rlev7_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^his hands shall bring YHWH's offerings by fire. He shall bring it,
the fat with the breast: the breast so as to elevate it, an elevation
offering in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_30.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:31*](#Contents.html_rlev7_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And the priest shall burn the fat to smoke at the altar, and the
breast shall be Aaron's and his sons'.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_31.jpg){height="50" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:32*](#Contents.html_rlev7_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And you shall give the right thigh as a donation to the priest from
your peace-offering sacrifices.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_32.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:33*](#Contents.html_rlev7_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^The one from the sons of Aaron who brings forward the blood of the
peace offerings and the fat: the right thigh shall be his as a portion.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_33.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:34*](#Contents.html_rlev7_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^Because I have taken the breast of the elevation offering and the
thigh of the donation from the children of Israel, from their
peace-offering sacrifices, and I have given them to Aaron the priest and
to his sons as an eternal law from the children of Israel."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_34.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:35*](#Contents.html_rlev7_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^This is the anointing of Aaron and the anointing of his sons from
YHWH's offerings by fire in the day He brought them forward to function
as priests for YHWH,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_35.jpg){height="70" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:35. **the anointing**. Meaning: the share of the sacrificial offerings
that they acquire by virtue of being anointed as Israel's priests.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:36*](#Contents.html_rlev7_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^which YHWH commanded to give to them in the day of His anointing
them from among the children of Israel, an eternal law through their
generations.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_36.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:37*](#Contents.html_rlev7_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^This is the instruction of the burnt offering, of the grain
offering, and of the sin offering and of the guilt offering and of the
ordination and of the peace-offering sacrifice,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_37.jpg){height="49" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:37. **This is the instruction of the burnt offering** ... This
concludes seven chapters of laws about sacrifices. Because it has been
nearly two millennia since the Temple sacrifices ended, what can
substitute for sacrifice as something that can give people some of the
things that sacrifices provided: a feeling of fulfillment, closeness to
God, sacredness of life, a link to Israel's history? My friend Rabbi
Lawson says: charity. A parallel question: what can substitute for
ancestor veneration, which was well known in the biblical world? Lawson
says: charity in memory of persons who have died. Many would say that we
have prayer as well as charity to compensate for the lack of sacrifice.
And we have other acts of atonement, such as fasting. All of this may be
true, but I note that people had prayer and charity and fasting in
biblical times, too. So these are not replacements for sacrifice. They
existed beside sacrifice all along. My question is: what do we have now
that they did *not* have? Answer: *study.* What we have that no one in
the biblical world (until Ezra) had is: the Torah. What we have that no
one at all in biblical times had is the full *Tanak.* Our task is to
study it, to shine our own new light on it, and to shine its light on
us. This is our task individually, in groups, and as a community.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 7:38*](#Contents.html_rlev7_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^which YHWH commanded Moses in Mount Sinai in the day that He
commanded the children of Israel to make their offerings to YHWH in the
wilderness of Sinai.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap7_38.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Lev_Chapter08.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:1*](#Contents.html_rlev8_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **8** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_01.jpg){height="22" width="234"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:2*](#Contents.html_rlev8_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Take Aaron and his sons with him and the clothes and the anointing
oil and the bull for the sin offering and the two rams and the basket of
unleavened bread,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_02.jpg){height="92" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:2. **Take Aaron and his sons**. Leviticus has only two narrative
accounts. The first is the story of the consecration of Aaron and his
sons to the priesthood of Israel (Leviticus 8--10). It is a lengthy and
detailed account. Most of the ceremonies that it describes have already
been presented in Exodus 29 as commands that YHWH gives to Moses, and
now the fulfillment of these commands is narrated. The details of the
rituals are impressive, picturing Moses placing the priestly garments
one by one on Aaron, then picturing him anointing, first, the Tabernacle
and its contents, then the altar, then Aaron. Next he has Aaron's sons
clothed. Then the inaugural sacrifices of the priesthood are described,
with details of Moses' slaughtering of a bull and two rams and his
dabbing of their blood on the altar and on the right ears, thumbs, and
big toes of Aaron and his sons. Moses directs Aaron and his sons to eat
the sacrificial meat at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting, from which
they are not permitted to depart for seven days. On the eighth day,
another series of sacrifices is pictured, but it is no longer Moses who
performs them, but rather Aaron and his sons. Then Aaron blesses the
people and, for the first time, enters the Tent of Meeting alongside
Moses. When they come out, they bless the people together, the glory of
YHWH appears to the people, divine fire comes and consumes the offering,
and the people shout and fall to the ground.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:3*](#Contents.html_rlev8_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^and assemble all of the congregation to the entrance of the Tent of
Meeting."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_03.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:4*](#Contents.html_rlev8_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Moses did as YHWH commanded him. And the congregation was
gathered to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_04.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:5*](#Contents.html_rlev8_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_09.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:11*](#Contents.html_rlev8_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Leviticus 8:12*](#Contents.html_rlev8_12)

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_12.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:13*](#Contents.html_rlev8_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Moses brought forward Aaron's sons and dressed them in coats and

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_13.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:14*](#Contents.html_rlev8_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And he brought up the bull for the sin offering. And Aaron and his
sons laid their hands on the head of the bull for the sin offering.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_14.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:15*](#Contents.html_rlev8_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Moses slaughtered it and took the blood and put it on the
altar's horns all around with his finger and purified the altar from
sin, and he poured the blood at the base of the altar, and he
consecrated it to make atonement on it.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_15.jpg){height="96" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:15. **Moses slaughtered it**. Moses personally performs all the
sacrifices in this ceremony of ordaining Aaron and his sons as priests.
After this day, all sacrifices will be performed by Aaron and his sons,
never again by Moses.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:16*](#Contents.html_rlev8_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_16.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:17*](#Contents.html_rlev8_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And he burned the bull and its skin and its meat and its dung in
fire outside the camp, as YHWH had commanded Moses.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_17.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:18*](#Contents.html_rlev8_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And he brought forward the ram for the burnt offering. And Aaron and
his sons laid their hands on the ram's head.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_18.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:19*](#Contents.html_rlev8_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And he slaughtered it, and Moses flung the blood on the altar all
around.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_19.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:20*](#Contents.html_rlev8_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And he cut the ram into its parts, and Moses burned the head and the
parts and the suet to smoke.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_20.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:21*](#Contents.html_rlev8_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And he washed the innards and the legs with water. And Moses burned
all of the ram to smoke at the altar. It was a burnt offering as a
pleasant smell. It was an offering by fire to YHWH, as YHWH had
commanded Moses.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_21.jpg){height="94" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:22*](#Contents.html_rlev8_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And he brought forward the second ram, the ram of ordination. And
Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the ram's head.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_22.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:23*](#Contents.html_rlev8_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And he slaughtered it, and Moses took some of its blood and put it
on Aaron's right earlobe and on the thumb of his right hand and on the
big toe of his right foot.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_23.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:24*](#Contents.html_rlev8_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And he brought Aaron's sons forward, and Moses put some of the blood
on their right earlobe and on the thumb of their right hand and on the
big toe of their right foot. And Moses flung the blood on the altar all
around.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_24.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:25*](#Contents.html_rlev8_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And he took the fat and the fat tail and all the fat that was on the
innards and the lobe of the liver and the two kidneys and their fat and
the right thigh.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_25.jpg){height="96" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:26*](#Contents.html_rlev8_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And from the basket of unleavened bread that was in front of YHWH he
took one cake of unleavened bread and one cake of bread with oil and one
wafer, and he set them on the fats and on the right thigh.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_26.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:27*](#Contents.html_rlev8_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And he put it all on Aaron's hands and on his sons' hands and
elevated them, an elevation offering in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_27.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:28*](#Contents.html_rlev8_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And Moses took them from on their hands and burned them to smoke at
the altar with the burnt offering. They were an ordination offering as a
pleasant smell. It was an offering by fire to YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_28.jpg){height="70" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:29*](#Contents.html_rlev8_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And Moses took the breast and elevated it, an elevation offering in
front of YHWH from the ram of ordination. It was Moses' for a portion,
as YHWH had commanded Moses.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_29.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:30*](#Contents.html_rlev8_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that
was on the altar and sprinkled them on Aaron, on his clothes, and on his
sons and on his sons' clothes with him, and he consecrated Aaron, his
clothes, and his sons and his sons' clothes with him.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_30.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:31*](#Contents.html_rlev8_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And Moses said to Aaron and to his sons, "Cook the meat at the
entrance of the Tent of Meeting and eat it and the bread that's in the
basket of ordination there, as I commanded, saying, 'Aaron and his sons
shall eat it.'

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_31.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:31. **as I commanded**. But it was God, not Moses, who commanded this
(Exod 29:31--32). Therefore, this should be read as the Hebrew passive
(*Pual*) as in Lev 8:35 and 10:13 rather than as the active
(*Piel*)---the two look the same in the Hebrew text without the vowel
points---and it should be understood to mean "as I was commanded."

Another explanation: Twice Moses says "as *I* commanded" when we would
have expected him to say "as *God* commanded"---here and in Lev 10:18
(cf. 10:13)---and in both cases he is speaking to his brother, Aaron.
This therefore may be related back to two occasions on which God states
that there is to be a special relationship between Moses and Aaron: in
Exod 4:16, "he will become a mouth for you, and you will become a god
for him!" and in Exod 7:1, "I've made you a god to Pharaoh, and Aaron,
your brother, will be your prophet." The relationship between Moses and
Aaron is like no other in the Bible. And this, too, is part of the
preparation for the climactic moment of their lives when Moses strikes
the rock at Meribah (Numbers 20). Although Moses says the words and
performs the deed while Aaron does not speak a word, Aaron shares the
same fate as Moses: death before entering the promised land. Aaron's
life is inseparably bound to the path of his younger brother, whose
words and acts have authority and consequences for him.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:32*](#Contents.html_rlev8_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And you shall burn what is left of the meat and of the bread in
fire.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_32.jpg){height="22" width="269"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:33*](#Contents.html_rlev8_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And you shall not go out from the Tent of Meeting for seven days,
until the day of completion of the days of your ordination, because He
will fill your hand for seven days.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_33.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:33. **fill your hand**. See the comment on Exod 29:29.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:34*](#Contents.html_rlev8_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^What has been done on this day YHWH commanded to do, to make
atonement over you.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_34.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:35*](#Contents.html_rlev8_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And you shall sit at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting day and
night, seven days, and you shall keep YHWH's charge, and you will not
die, because that is what I was commanded."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_35.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:35. **seven days**. This parashah
(![Image](images/f337-01.jpg){height="13" width="15"}, Command) ends
with Aaron and his sons sitting for seven days at the Tabernacle's
entrance, preparing for the climax that is coming in the next week,
building anticipation for the inauguration of the priesthood. And so, if
we follow the weekly cycle of readings of the Torah, we emulate what is
happening in the text: we wait for seven days! To be sure that we get
the connection, the next parashah is titled
*š![image](images/e-image.jpg)mînî* Eighth. If we pay proper attention
to this, we come to the story of the inauguration with anticipation, so
we appreciate the story, and we appreciate the power of what goes wrong
in the story: the catastrophe of Nadab and Abihu.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 8:36*](#Contents.html_rlev8_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And Aaron and his sons did all the things that YHWH commanded by
Moses' hand.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap8_36.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Lev_Chapter09.html}

## **9** {.h2a}

^1^And it was, on the eighth day, Moses called to Aaron and to his sons

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Leviticus 9:3*](#Contents.html_rlev9_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

for a sin offering and a calf and a lamb, unblemished one-year-olds, for
a burnt offering

![Image](images/Lev_Chap9_03.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 9:4*](#Contents.html_rlev9_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

![Image](images/Lev_Chap9_07.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Leviticus 9:8*](#Contents.html_rlev9_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 9:9*](#Contents.html_rlev9_9)

finger in the blood and put it on the altar's horns, and he poured the

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 9:10*](#Contents.html_rlev9_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And he burned the fat and the kidneys and the lobe from the liver

![Image](images/Lev_Chap9_10.jpg){height="69" width="321"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 9:11*](#Contents.html_rlev9_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And he burned the meat and the skin in fire outside the camp.

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Leviticus 9:12*](#Contents.html_rlev9_12)

[*Leviticus 9:15*](#Contents.html_rlev9_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And he made the people's offering: and he took the goat for the sin

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:15. **like the first one**. That is, he prepares the sin offering for
the people in the same place as the burnt offering that immediately
preceded it (9:12--14). This is in fulfillment of the commandment in
6:18.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 9:16*](#Contents.html_rlev9_16)

[*Leviticus 9:17*](#Contents.html_rlev9_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 9:19*](#Contents.html_rlev9_19)
