------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And the priest shall put some of the remaining oil that is on his
hand on the right earlobe of the one being purified and on the thumb of
his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot, over the blood of
the guilt offering.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_17.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:18*](#Contents.html_rlev14_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And he shall put what is left of the oil that is on the priest's
hand on the head of the one being purified. And the priest shall make
atonement over him in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_18.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:19*](#Contents.html_rlev14_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And the priest shall make the sin offering and make atonement over
the one being purified from his impurity, and after that he shall
slaughter the burnt offering.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_19.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:20*](#Contents.html_rlev14_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And the priest shall make the burnt offering and the grain offering
at the altar. And the priest shall make atonement over him, and he will
be pure.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_20.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:20. **and he will be pure**. My old classmate Danny Siegel says
concerning Parashat Me![Image](images/sdot.jpg){.block height="11"
width="6"}ora that it is about bringing people who are infirm back into
the community. As derivative practices in our own time he gives as
examples: (1) synagogues that have installed ramps to the *bimah* for
handicapped persons; and (2) balloons for the deaf so they can
experience the vibrating sound of the shofar on Rosh Hashanah. As we
were once responsible to return and include those who were impure to the
congregation, so we may learn to include the handicapped as well. It is
inspirational that we can derive this essential principle from a
parashah in the Torah that seems so remote to readers in this age.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:21*](#Contents.html_rlev14_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^"And if he is poor, and his hand cannot attain enough, then he shall
take one lamb, a guilt offering, for elevation, for making atonement
over him, and one-tenth of a measure of fine flour mixed with oil for a
grain offering and a log of oil

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_21.jpg){height="71" width="345"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:22*](#Contents.html_rlev14_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^and two turtledoves or two pigeons, whichever his hand can attain,
and one shall be a sin offering and one a burnt offering.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_22.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:23*](#Contents.html_rlev14_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And he shall bring them on the eighth day of his purification to the
priest, to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting, in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_23.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:24*](#Contents.html_rlev14_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And the priest shall take the lamb of the guilt offering and the log
of oil, and the priest shall elevate them, an elevation offering in
front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_24.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:25*](#Contents.html_rlev14_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And he shall slaughter the lamb of the guilt offering, and the
priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering and put it on
the right earlobe of the one being purified and on the thumb of his
right hand and on the big toe of his right foot.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_25.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:26*](#Contents.html_rlev14_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And the priest shall pour some of the oil on the priest's left hand,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_26.jpg){height="43" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:27*](#Contents.html_rlev14_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^and the priest shall sprinkle some of the oil that is on his left
hand with his right finger seven times in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_27.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:28*](#Contents.html_rlev14_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And the priest shall put some of the oil that is on his hand on the
right earlobe of the one being purified and on the thumb of his right
hand and on the big toe of his right foot, over the place of the blood
of the guilt offering.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_28.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:29*](#Contents.html_rlev14_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And what is left of the oil that is on the priest's hand he shall
put on the head of the one being purified to make atonement over him in
front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_29.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:30*](#Contents.html_rlev14_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And he shall make one of the turtledoves or the pigeons, from those
which his hand can attain---

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_30.jpg){height="65" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:31*](#Contents.html_rlev14_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^whichever his hand will attain---one a sin offering and one a burnt
offering, with the grain offering, and the priest shall make atonement
over the one being purified in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_31.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:32*](#Contents.html_rlev14_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^"This is the instruction for one who has an affliction of leprosy in
him, whose hand cannot attain enough for his purification."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_32.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:33*](#Contents.html_rlev14_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And YHWH spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_33.jpg){height="22" width="310"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:34*](#Contents.html_rlev14_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^"When you will come to the land of Canaan, which I am giving to you
as a possession, and I shall put an affliction of leprosy in a house at
the land of your possession,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_34.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:35*](#Contents.html_rlev14_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^and the one whose house it is will come and tell the priest, saying,
'It appears like an affliction to me in the house,'

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_35.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:36*](#Contents.html_rlev14_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^then the priest shall command, and they shall clear the house before
the priest will come to see the affliction, so everything that is in the
house will not become impure. And, after that, the priest shall come to
see the house.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_36.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:36. **they shall clear the house**. They clear the house before the
priest comes to examine the house "so everything that is in the house
will not become impure." One might ask why the house's contents are
exempted from the examination? What if the impurity has already spread?
But when the priest comes to examine the house, if he finds an
affliction in the walls he has the house sealed for a week. It is when
the house is thus sealed that the affliction can spread. (Thus the
people who have been in the house prior to the sealing are not rendered
impure, but anyone who enters the house during the week that it is
sealed *is* rendered impure; v. 46.) Therefore, they clear the house
before the priest's examination.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:37*](#Contents.html_rlev14_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^And he shall see the affliction, and, here, the affliction in the
walls of the house is greenish or reddish streaks, and their appearance
is deeper than the wall;

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_37.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:38*](#Contents.html_rlev14_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^then the priest shall come out from the house, to the entrance of
the house, and he shall shut the house up seven days.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_38.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:39*](#Contents.html_rlev14_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^And the priest shall go back on the seventh day and see, and, here,
the affliction has spread in the walls of the house;

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_39.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:40*](#Contents.html_rlev14_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^then the priest shall command, and they shall extract the stones in
which is the affliction, and they shall throw them outside the city, to
an impure place.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_40.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:41*](#Contents.html_rlev14_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^And he shall have the house scraped inside, all around, and they
shall spill the dust that they have scraped outside the city, to an
impure place.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_41.jpg){height="52" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:42*](#Contents.html_rlev14_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^And they shall take other stones and bring them in place of the
stones, and he shall take other dust and coat the house.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_42.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:43*](#Contents.html_rlev14_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^And if the affliction will come back and develop in the house after
he has extracted the stones and after scraping the house and after being
coated,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_43.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:44*](#Contents.html_rlev14_44)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^44^then the priest shall come and see, and, here, the affliction has
spread in the house, it is malignant leprosy in the house. It is impure.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_44.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:45*](#Contents.html_rlev14_45)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^45^And he shall demolish the house: its stones and its wood and all the
dust of the house; and he shall take it outside the city, to an impure
place.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_45.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:46*](#Contents.html_rlev14_46)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^46^And one who comes into the house all the days that it is shut up
will be impure until evening,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_46.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:47*](#Contents.html_rlev14_47)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^47^and one who lies in the house shall wash his clothes, and one who
eats in the house shall wash his clothes.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_47.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:48*](#Contents.html_rlev14_48)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^48^And if the priest will come and see, and, here, the affliction has
not spread in the house after coating the house, then the priest shall
identify the house as pure, because the affliction has been healed.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_48.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:49*](#Contents.html_rlev14_49)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^49^And, to decontaminate the house, he shall take two birds and
cedarwood and scarlet and hyssop,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_49.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:50*](#Contents.html_rlev14_50)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^50^and he shall slaughter one of the birds in a clay container over
living water.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_50.jpg){height="43" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:51*](#Contents.html_rlev14_51)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^51^And he shall take the cedarwood and the hyssop and the scarlet and
the live bird and dip them in the blood of the slaughtered bird and in
the living water, and he shall sprinkle it at the house seven times.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_51.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:52*](#Contents.html_rlev14_52)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^52^And he shall decontaminate the house with the bird's blood and with
the living water and with the live bird and with the cedarwood and with
the hyssop and with the scarlet.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_52.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:53*](#Contents.html_rlev14_53)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^53^And he shall let the live bird go outside the city at the open
field. And he shall make atonement over the house, and it will be pure.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_53.jpg){height="70" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:54*](#Contents.html_rlev14_54)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^54^"This is the instruction for every affliction of leprosy and for a
scab

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_54.jpg){height="22" width="285"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:55*](#Contents.html_rlev14_55)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^55^and for leprosy of clothing and for a house

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_55.jpg){height="22" width="187"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:56*](#Contents.html_rlev14_56)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^56^and for a swelling and for a rash and for a bright spot,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_56.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 14:57*](#Contents.html_rlev14_57)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^57^to instruct on the day of the impure and on the day of the pure.
This is the instruction for leprosy."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap14_57.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Lev_Chapter15.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:1*](#Contents.html_rlev15_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **15** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_01.jpg){height="22" width="309"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:2*](#Contents.html_rlev15_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Speak to the children of Israel, and you shall say to them: When any
man will have an emission from his flesh, his emission: it is impure.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_02.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:2. **an emission from his flesh**. A spontaneous discharge from the
penis.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:3*](#Contents.html_rlev15_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And this shall be his impurity when he has an emission: his flesh
leaks with his emission, or his flesh is obstructed from his emission.
It is his impurity.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_03.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:3. **this shall be his impurity**. That is, the following shall be
the nature of his impure condition.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:4*](#Contents.html_rlev15_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^ Any bed on which the one with the emission will lie will be impure,
and any item on which he will sit will be impure.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_04.jpg){height="70" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:5*](#Contents.html_rlev15_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And a man who will touch his bed shall wash his clothes and shall
wash in water and will be impure until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_05.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:6*](#Contents.html_rlev15_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And one who sits on an item on which the one with the emission has
sat shall wash his clothes and shall wash in water and will be impure
until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_06.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:7*](#Contents.html_rlev15_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And one who touches the flesh of the one with the emission shall wash
his clothes and shall wash in water and will be impure until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_07.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:8*](#Contents.html_rlev15_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And if the one with the emission will spit on one who is pure, then
he shall wash his clothes and shall wash in water and will be impure
until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_08.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:9*](#Contents.html_rlev15_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And any conveyance on which the one with the emission will ride will
be impure.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_09.jpg){height="45" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:10*](#Contents.html_rlev15_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And anyone who touches anything that has been under him will be
impure until evening. And one who carries them shall wash his clothes
and shall wash in water and will be impure until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_10.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:11*](#Contents.html_rlev15_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And anyone whom the one with the emission will touch when he has not
rinsed his hands in water shall wash his clothes and shall wash in water
and will be impure until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_11.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:12*](#Contents.html_rlev15_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And a clay container that the one with the emission will touch shall
be broken, and any wood container shall be rinsed in water.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_12.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:13*](#Contents.html_rlev15_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And when the one with the emission will be purified from his
emission, he shall count seven days for his purification and shall wash
his clothes and shall wash his flesh in living water and will be pure.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_13.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:14*](#Contents.html_rlev15_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And on the eighth day he shall take two turtledoves or two pigeons
and shall come in front of YHWH, to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting,
and shall give them to the priest.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_14.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:15*](#Contents.html_rlev15_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And the priest shall do them: one a sin offering and one a burnt
offering, and the priest shall make atonement over him in front of YHWH
from his emission.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_15.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:16*](#Contents.html_rlev15_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^"And when a man's intercourse seed will come out from him: he shall
wash all his flesh in water and will be impure until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_16.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:16. **intercourse seed will come out from him**. This refers to a
spontaneous flow of semen, as in a nocturnal emission.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:17*](#Contents.html_rlev15_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And any clothing and any leather on which there will be intercourse
seed: it shall be washed with water and will be impure until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_17.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:18*](#Contents.html_rlev15_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And a woman with whom a man will lie---an intercourse of seed---they
shall wash with water and will be impure until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_18.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:19*](#Contents.html_rlev15_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^"And when a woman will have an emission---her emission will be blood
in her flesh---she shall be in her impurity seven days. And anyone who
touches her will be impure until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_19.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:20*](#Contents.html_rlev15_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And anything on which she will lie during her impurity will be
impure, and anything on which she will sit will be impure.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_20.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:21*](#Contents.html_rlev15_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And anyone who touches her bed shall wash his clothes and shall wash
in water and will be impure until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_21.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:22*](#Contents.html_rlev15_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And anyone who touches any item on which she will sit shall wash his
clothes and shall wash in water and will be impure until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_22.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:23*](#Contents.html_rlev15_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And if he is on the bed or on the item on which she sits while he is
touching it, he will be impure until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_23.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:23. **he is on the bed or on the item on which she sits while he is
touching it**. Most other translators make it: "if he is on the bed or
on the item on which she sits, when he touches it he is impure." That
is, they make it that the impurity starts when he touches it. I think
that the text is rather saying that even if he was on the bed first, and
then she sat down on it, he is still rendered impure. (The Masoretic
Text's placement of the *etna![Image](images/hdot.jpg){height="15"
width="7"},*the symbol that marks the end of the phrase, agrees with my
reading; that is, it comes after the words "he touches it.") Now, since
he was there first, there is no question but that she has not had direct
contact with him and that she has not touched the part of the bed on
which he is sitting. But *still* he is impure---indicating that her
contact with the bed or seat makes it impure (1) *entirely* and (2)
*instantly.* This tells us something about the nature of impurity. It
spreads throughout a person or object. And it is not any kind of
creatures, like bacteria. It is a pervasive condition.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:24*](#Contents.html_rlev15_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And if a man will *lie* with her, and her impurity will be on him,
then he will be impure seven days. And any bed on which he will lie will
be impure.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_24.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:25*](#Contents.html_rlev15_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^"And when a woman will have an emission of her blood many days when
it is not the time of her impurity, or when she will have an emission
beyond her impurity, for all the days of her impure emission she shall
be like the days of her impurity. She is impure.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_25.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:26*](#Contents.html_rlev15_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^Any bed on which she will lie all the days of her emission will be
like a bed of her impurity for her, and any item on which she will sit
will be impure like the impurity of her menstruation.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_26.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:27*](#Contents.html_rlev15_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And anyone who touches them will be impure. And he shall wash his
clothes and shall wash with water and will be impure until evening.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_27.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:28*](#Contents.html_rlev15_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^"And if she has become purified from her emission, then she shall
count seven days, and after that she will be pure.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_28.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:29*](#Contents.html_rlev15_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And on the eighth day she shall take two turtledoves or two pigeons,
and she shall bring them to the priest at the entrance of the Tent of
Meeting.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_29.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:29. **at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting**. It has been noted
that the instruction is the same for a man and for a woman except that a
man is said to bring this offering "in front of YHWH," whereas these
words do not occur here in the instruction about a woman. And it has
been suggested that women's "cultic access" is thus not equal to men's.
But the words "in front of YHWH" appear to apply regularly to the place
at the entrance of the Tent. Both women and men have access to this
place. The absence of the extra phrase here must therefore mean
something else. (Or, since the phrase is not used in every reference to
the entrance of the Tent, perhaps its absence here means nothing in
particular.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:30*](#Contents.html_rlev15_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And the priest shall make one a sin offering and one a burnt
offering, and the priest shall make atonement over her in front of YHWH
from her impure emission.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_30.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:31*](#Contents.html_rlev15_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^"And you shall alert the children of Israel regarding their impurity
so they will not die through their impurity when they defile my
Tabernacle that is among them.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_31.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:32*](#Contents.html_rlev15_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^This is the instruction for one who has an emission and for one from
whom seed of intercourse will come out, so as to become impure by it,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_32.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 15:33*](#Contents.html_rlev15_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^and one who is in her menstrual impurity and one who has an
emission---for a male and for a female---and for a man who will lie with
a woman who is impure."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap15_33.jpg){height="68" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Lev_Chapter16.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:1*](#Contents.html_rlev16_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## A[FTER THE DEATH]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **16** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses after the death of Aaron's two sons---when
they came forward in front of YHWH and died.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16.jpg){height="20" width="109"}

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_01.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:1. **after death**. The title of this parashah is
*'a![Image](images/hdot.jpg){height="15"
width="7"}![Image](images/a1.jpg){.block height="12" width="8"}rê môt,*
meaning "after death." The title of the following week's parashah is
*q![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}d![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11" width="7"}šîm,*
meaning "holy" (19:2). In some years these two portions are read
together, and so their titles merge as
*'a![Image](images/hdot.jpg){height="15"
width="7"}![Image](images/a1.jpg){.block height="12" width="8"}rê môt
q![Image](images/e-image.jpg){.block height="12"
width="7"}d![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11" width="7"}šîm,*
meaning "after death: holy." My grandfather wrote a letter to my father
when my father was about to be married, and he noted a traditional
comment on this merger: that after people die we make them holy,
speaking of them as if they were saints. "But," my grandfather told my
father, "you have always shown me respect while I am still alive." And
so one of the greatest lessons of these portions of the Torah is the
result of a merger of titles that are three chapters apart and that were
inserted centuries after the Torah was completed. Thus wisdom can emerge
in a variety of ways if the reader is wise and sensitive.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:2*](#Contents.html_rlev16_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And YHWH said to Moses, "Speak to Aaron, your brother, and let him
not come at all times to the Holy, inside the pavilion, in front of the
atonement dais that is over the ark, so he will not die, because I shall
appear in a cloud over the atonement dais.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_02.jpg){height="118" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:2. **inside the pavilion**. The Hebrew is *mibbêt
lapp![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}r![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11" width="7"}ket.* The
use of the term *bêt* (house, building) may be further evidence that the
*p![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}r![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11" width="7"}ket* is a
four-sided structure, not a curtain. See the comments on Tabernacle
architecture at Exodus 26.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:3*](#Contents.html_rlev16_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^Aaron shall come to the Holy with this: with a bull of the cattle for
a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_03.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:4*](#Contents.html_rlev16_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^He shall wear a holy linen coat, and linen drawers shall be on his
flesh, and he shall be belted with a linen sash, and he shall wear a
linen headdress. They are holy clothes: and he shall wash his flesh with
water and put them on.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_04.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:5*](#Contents.html_rlev16_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And he shall take from the congregation of the children of Israel two
goats for a sin offering and one ram for a burnt offering.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_05.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:6*](#Contents.html_rlev16_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Aaron shall bring forward the bull of the sin offering that is
his and shall make atonement for himself and for his house.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_06.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:6. **his house**. Meaning: his family, his household.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:7*](#Contents.html_rlev16_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And he shall take the two goats and stand them in front of YHWH, at
the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_07.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:8*](#Contents.html_rlev16_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Aaron shall cast lots on the two goats, one lot for YHWH and one
lot for Azazel.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_08.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:8. **lots**. Why use lots? Lots are used frequently in the *Tanak.*
In some cases they are understood to reflect divine determination, and
in some they are not. They reflect the hand of God in the case of the
lot that identifies Jonah to the sailors (1:7), the lot that identifies
Achan as the Israelite who has violated the law of *herem* and taken
spoils for himself at Jericho (Joshua 7), the lot that identifies Saul
as the first king of Israel (1 Sam 10:17--21), and the lot that
identifies Saul's son Jonathan as the one who has unwittingly violated
an oath by his father (1 Sam 14:40--42). They appear to be a matter of
chance rather than divine direction here in the case of the goats, in
the case of the apportionment of the promised land to the Israelites
(Num 26:52--56; Joshua 14--19; Judg 1:3), in assigning priests to their
divisions (1 Chronicles 24), in drafting Israelites for a battle (Judg
20:9), in assigning population to Jerusalem (Nehemiah 11), and in
sharing in spoils and captives (Joel 4:3; Obadiah 11; Nah 3:10; Ps
22:19). (The case of Haman's lots in Esther is anomalous and requires
separate explanation.)

What is the distinction between these two groups of cases? It appears to
be that all the cases that involve divine determination are about
identifying a single individual in a specific situation. All the cases
that do not imply divine determination are about assigning things that
relate to groups or the community. The issue in the former cases is
finding the right person. The issue in the latter cases is
fairness---showing no favor in assignments. The goats are the first case
of such assignment by lot in the *Tanak,* and they are the only case
that is an annual event. All the others are onetime occurrences. The
ritual of the goats thus sets the principle and the model for all
subsequent cases involving lotteries for fairness in assignments.

It has been said that this is a denial of "Divine Providence." But this
is an act by the *community,* seeking atonement for things that *they*
have done. This is not a situation calling for "Divine Providence." On
the contrary, the point here is precisely to convey the will of the
community to express their atonement to God. The deity is the recipient,
not the controller, of this message.

16:8. **Azazel**. This word occurs only in this chapter and nowhere else
in the *Tanak.* There have been many attempts to explain it (the common
view being that it refers to some sort of desert demon), but the simple
fact is that we do not know who or what it is.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:9*](#Contents.html_rlev16_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Aaron shall bring forward the goat on which the lot for YHWH
arose, and he shall make it a sin offering.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_09.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:10*](#Contents.html_rlev16_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And the goat on which the lot for Azazel arose shall be stood alive
in front of YHWH to make atonement over it, to let it go to Azazel, into
the wilderness.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_10.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:11*](#Contents.html_rlev16_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Aaron shall bring forward the bull of the sin offering that is
his and shall make atonement for him and for his house, and he shall
slaughter the bull of the sin offering that he has.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_11.jpg){height="75" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:12*](#Contents.html_rlev16_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And he shall take a fire-holder-full of coals of fire from on the
altar, from in front of YHWH, and handfuls of fine incense of
fragrances, and he shall bring it inside the pavilion

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_12.jpg){height="77" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:13*](#Contents.html_rlev16_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^and put the incense on the fire in front of YHWH so that the cloud
of the incense will cover the atonement dais that is over the Testimony,
and he will not die.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_13.jpg){height="73" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:14*](#Contents.html_rlev16_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And he shall take some of the bull's blood and sprinkle with his
finger on the front of the atonement dais eastward, and he shall
sprinkle some of the blood with his finger in front of the atonement
dais seven times.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_14.jpg){height="102" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:15*](#Contents.html_rlev16_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And he shall slaughter the goat of the sin offering that is the
people's and shall bring its blood inside the pavilion and shall do its
blood as he did to the bull's blood: and he shall sprinkle it on the
atonement dais and in front of the atonement dais.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_15.jpg){height="102" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:16*](#Contents.html_rlev16_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And he shall make atonement over the Holy from the impurities of the
children of Israel and from their offenses, for all their sins, and he
shall do so for the Tent of Meeting that tents with them among their
impurities.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_16.jpg){height="76" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:17*](#Contents.html_rlev16_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And no human shall be in the Tent of Meeting when he comes to make
atonement in the Holy until he comes out. And he shall make atonement
for him and for his house and for all the community of Israel.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_17.jpg){height="103" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:18*](#Contents.html_rlev16_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^"And he shall go out to the altar that is in front of YHWH and make
atonement over it, and he shall take some of the bull's blood and some
of the goat's blood and put it on the altar's horns all around.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_18.jpg){height="77" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:19*](#Contents.html_rlev16_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And he shall sprinkle some of the blood on it with his finger seven
times and purify it and make it holy from the impurities of the children
of Israel.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_19.jpg){height="76" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:20*](#Contents.html_rlev16_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^"And when he will finish making atonement for the Holy and the Tent
of Meeting and the altar, then he shall bring forward the live goat.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_20.jpg){height="50" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:21*](#Contents.html_rlev16_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Aaron shall lay his two hands on the live goat's head and
confess over it all the crimes of the children of Israel and all their
offenses, for all their sins, and he shall put them on the goat's head
and let it go by the hand of an appointed man to the wilderness.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_21.jpg){height="106" width="367"}

![Image](images/16page_369.jpg){height="28" width="84"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:22*](#Contents.html_rlev16_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And the goat will carry all their crimes on it to an inaccessible
land. And he shall let the goat go in the wilderness.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_22.jpg){height="74" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:23*](#Contents.html_rlev16_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^"And Aaron shall come to the Tent of Meeting and take off the linen
clothes that he wore when he came to the Holy, and he shall leave them
there.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_23.jpg){height="76" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:24*](#Contents.html_rlev16_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And he shall wash his flesh with water in a holy place and put on
his clothes, and he shall go out and make his burnt offering and the
people's burnt offering and make atonement for him and for the people.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_24.jpg){height="75" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:25*](#Contents.html_rlev16_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And he shall burn the fat of the sin offering into smoke at the
altar.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_25.jpg){height="48" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:26*](#Contents.html_rlev16_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And the one who let the goat go to Azazel shall wash his clothes and
wash his flesh with water, and after that he shall come to the camp.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_26.jpg){height="75" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:27*](#Contents.html_rlev16_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And he shall take the bull of the sin offering and the goat of the
sin offering, whose blood was brought to make atonement in the Holy,
outside the camp; and they shall burn their skin and their meat and
their dung in fire.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_27.jpg){height="103" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:28*](#Contents.html_rlev16_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And the one who burns them shall wash his clothes and wash his flesh
with water, and after that he shall come to the camp.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_28.jpg){height="49" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:29*](#Contents.html_rlev16_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^"And it shall be an eternal law for you: in the seventh month, on
the tenth of the month, you shall degrade yourselves, and you shall not
do any work: the citizen and the alien who resides among you.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_29.jpg){height="102" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:29. **degrade yourselves**. The verb here is the same word that is
used to describe what the Egyptians did to the Israelites (Exod
1:11--12): degradation, affliction. It has been understood to mean that
one does not eat or drink or bathe or wear fine shoes on the Day of
Atonement; rather, one humbles oneself.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:30*](#Contents.html_rlev16_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^Because on this day he shall make atonement over you, to purify you.
You will be pure from all your sins in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_30.jpg){height="50" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:31*](#Contents.html_rlev16_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^It is a Sabbath, a ceasing, to you. And you shall degrade
yourselves---an eternal law.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_31.jpg){height="50" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:32*](#Contents.html_rlev16_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And the priest whom one will anoint and who will fill his hand to
function as a priest in his father's place shall make atonement. And he
shall wear the linen clothes, the holy clothes.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_32.jpg){height="77" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:32. **fill his hand to function as a priest**. See comment on Exod
29:29.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:33*](#Contents.html_rlev16_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And he shall make atonement for the holy place of the Holy, and he
shall make atonement for the Tent of Meeting and for the altar, and he
shall make atonement over the priests and over all the people of the
community.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_33.jpg){height="79" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 16:34*](#Contents.html_rlev16_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And this shall become an eternal law for you, to make atonement over
the children of Israel from all their sins, once in the year."

And he did as YHWH had commanded Moses.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap16_34.jpg){height="76" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Lev_Chapter17.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:1*](#Contents.html_rlev17_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **17** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_01.jpg){height="23" width="248"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:2*](#Contents.html_rlev17_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Speak to Aaron and to his sons and to all the children of Israel and
say to them: This is the thing that YHWH has commanded, saying:

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_02.jpg){height="76" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:3*](#Contents.html_rlev17_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^Any man from the house of Israel who slaughters an ox or a sheep or a
goat in the camp or who slaughters outside the camp

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_03.jpg){height="74" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:3. **Any man ... who slaughters**. Most sacrifices involve the ritual
slaughter of an animal that is to be used for food. In Leviticus, YHWH
forbids the slaughter of animals outside of a formal ritual setting. For
Leviticus there is no such occupation as butcher. If one wants to eat
meat, one must bring the animal to the priest, who sacrifices it in the
prescribed manner at the altar, retains a portion of it for the
sustenance of the priesthood, and gives back the rest for consumption.
The priests eat their portion according to prescription as well:
specifically, priests eat meat with unleavened bread. As with the laws
of Exodus, the reasons for requiring ritual slaughter are not stated
explicitly, but there appears to be more at stake than the support of
the priesthood. In the initial state of creation in Genesis, humans are
vegetarian. Genesis depicts animals, but not plants, as sharing with
humans the quality of being living beings (*nepeš
![Image](images/hdot.jpg){height="15"
width="7"}ayy![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}h,*Gen 1:20,21,24,30; 2:7,19). Following the flood, animals
are permitted as food. Now the sacrificial laws of Leviticus recognize
that this involves the taking of life, and they decree that life cannot
be taken as a secular act of slaughter. It must be done in a setting
that reflects sensitivity to what is at stake. An indicator that this is
the orientation of the Levitical law is that it permits one to hunt game
animals such as deer, which one cannot easily lead to an altar, but then
one must perform the act of pouring off the blood of the animal because,
it says, "the flesh's life (*nepeš*) is in the blood" (17:11).

At several places in my comments on Leviticus, I have noted that we have
lost the feeling of ritual as it was known in the biblical world. Here I
would add that if we still had a greater feeling for ritual we would
have more of a feeling for the life of an animal. For most humans in
this age, we are farther removed from the life and death of the animals
(and plants!) that we eat than in any previous generation. We buy meat
in sealed packages, so that it no longer resembles the animal from which
it came. We have lost the awareness of how this food came to be on our
table. Part of life is the consumption of life-forms by other
life-forms. But whether one is vegetarian or carnivorous, one will
enrich one's life---and understand better one's place in the world---if
one connects a ritual with that consumption. If it can no longer be
sacrifice, then perhaps it can be saying grace, or a blessing, or
occasionally visiting a farm or ranch and observing the animals and
plants: watching, touching, listening to them.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:4*](#Contents.html_rlev17_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^and has not brought it to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting to
bring forward an offering to YHWH in front of YHWH's Tabernacle: blood
will be counted to that man. He has spilled blood. And that man will be
cut off from among his people.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_04.jpg){height="103" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:4. **Tent of Meeting**. There is a long additional line here in the
Septuagint, Qumran, and Samaritan texts, which has been lost in the
Masoretic Text. The verse reads: "and has not brought it to the entrance
of the Tent of Meeting *to make it a burnt offering or peace offering to
YHWH to be acceptable for you as a pleasant smell, and he slaughters it
outside and has not brought it to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting*
..." The eye of the scribe writing the Masoretic Text apparently skipped
the line because his eye jumped from one occurrence of the phrase
"brought it to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting" to the next. This
shows how much of a text can be changed by scribal error and is a
warning to take account of all the available versions of the text. (It
is also a reminder that systems of seeking patterns or codes by counting
the text's letters are erroneous. See the comment on Exod 1:5.)

17:4. **blood will be counted to that man**. **He has spilled blood**.
This person has slaughtered an animal, but the text speaks of it as an
equivalent of murdering a human! Why does this act merit such
extraordinary severity? Because it merges two of the most essential
commandments of the Torah. First, it involves taking a life without
respect for life's sanctity---as discussed in the comment on 17:3. And,
second, it violates the law of centralization of worship, which is
declared for the first time in this chapter. According to the law, one
can sacrifice only at one place on earth: at the altar at the entrance
of the Tent of Meeting. If one wants to eat lamb or beef, one must bring
one's sheep or cow to that central altar. In the wilderness it is at the
Tent. Later, in the land, it is at the Temple. (The Tent of Meeting is
inside the Temple. See the comment on Exod 26:30.) But the all-important
point is centralization of worship. As I commented earlier (Exod 26:6),
centralization and monotheism go together: one God, one altar. Each town
in Israel does not have its own altar or its own Tabernacle. This is
explicitly forbidden here. In the books of Kings, it is the standard for
judging *every* king of Israel and Judah: did he allow "high places"
(Hebrew *b![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}môt*) outside of the Jerusalem Temple or not? The only two
kings to get nearly perfect ratings (Hezekiah and Josiah) are the two
kings who most zealously enforce centralization. Today, one does not see
a multiplicity of synagogues, churches, or mosques and assume that this
means that a multiplicity of gods is being worshiped. But in the
biblical world, when monotheism is a new idea, the principle of one
God--one Temple--one altar is presented as supremely important.

17:4. **cut off from among his people**. This may mean to die without
heirs or not to be buried in the ancestral tomb or not to join one's
ancestors in an afterlife. See the comment on Exod 30:33.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:5*](#Contents.html_rlev17_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^So that the children of Israel will bring their sacrifices---which
they are making at the open field---and will bring them to YHWH, to the
entrance of the Tent of Meeting, to the priest, and make them sacrifices
of peace offerings to YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_05.jpg){height="129" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:6*](#Contents.html_rlev17_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And the priest shall fling the blood on YHWH's altar at the entrance
of the Tent of Meeting and burn the fat to smoke as a pleasant smell to
YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_06.jpg){height="75" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:7*](#Contents.html_rlev17_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And they shall not make their sacrifices anymore to the satyrs, after
whom they are whoring. This shall be an eternal law to them through
their generations.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_07.jpg){height="79" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:7. **satyrs**. It is not certain what these are. The term "satyrs" is
used because the Hebrew term has to do with goats. They are mentioned in
Isa 13:21; 34:14 and in 2 Chr 11:15.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:8*](#Contents.html_rlev17_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^"And you shall say to them: Any man from the house of Israel and from
the aliens who will reside among them who will make a burnt offering or
a sacrifice

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_08.jpg){height="75" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:9*](#Contents.html_rlev17_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^and will not bring it to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting to make
it to YHWH: that man will be cut off from his people.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_09.jpg){height="50" width="365"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:10*](#Contents.html_rlev17_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^"And any man from the house of Israel and from the aliens who will
reside among them who will eat any blood: then I shall set my face
against the person who eats the blood, and I shall cut him off from
among his people.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_10.jpg){height="77" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:10. **person**. This is very difficult to capture in translation. The
word for "person," Hebrew *nepeš,* is the same word that means "life"
three times in v. 11. In the Hebrew, therefore, it is more obvious that
the issue is that a living creature is eating blood, which bears the
life of another living creature.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:11*](#Contents.html_rlev17_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^Because the *flesh's* life is in the blood, and *I* have given it to
you on the altar to make atonement over *your* lives, because it is the
blood that makes atonement for life.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_11.jpg){height="75" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:12*](#Contents.html_rlev17_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^On account of this I have said to the children of Israel: every
person among you shall not eat blood, and the alien who resides among
you shall not eat blood.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_12.jpg){height="75" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:13*](#Contents.html_rlev17_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^"And any man from the children of Israel and from the aliens who
reside among them who will hunt game, animal or bird, that may be eaten:
he shall spill out its blood and cover it with dust.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_13.jpg){height="102" width="364"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:14*](#Contents.html_rlev17_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^Because all flesh's life: its blood is one with its life. So I say
to the children of Israel: you shall not eat the blood of all
flesh---because all flesh's life: it is its blood. Everyone of those who
eat it will be cut off.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_14.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:15*](#Contents.html_rlev17_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And any living being, whether a citizen or an alien, who will eat
carcass or a torn animal shall wash his clothes and shall wash with
water and will be impure until evening and then will be pure.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_15.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:15. **carcass or a torn animal**. See the comments on Lev 7:24.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 17:16*](#Contents.html_rlev17_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And if he will not wash them and will not wash his flesh, then he
shall bear his crime."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap17_16.jpg){height="49" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Lev_Chapter18.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 18:1*](#Contents.html_rlev18_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **18** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap18_01.jpg){height="21" width="231"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 18:2*](#Contents.html_rlev18_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Speak to the children of Israel, and you shall say to them: I am
YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap18_02.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 18:3*](#Contents.html_rlev18_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^You shall shall not do like what is done in the land of Egypt, in
which you lived; and you shall not do like what is done in the land of
Canaan, to which I'm bringing you; and you shall not go by their laws.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap18_03.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 18:4*](#Contents.html_rlev18_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^You shall do *my* judgments, and you shall observe *my* laws, to go
by them. I am YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap18_04.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:4. **You shall do *my* judgments, and you shall observe *my* laws**.
Why is this said now, after we have already had seventeen chapters of
laws in Leviticus? This juncture at the beginning of chapter 18 is a
turning point: Until now the laws of Leviticus have had to do with
*ritual:* sacrifices, permitted and forbidden food, leprosy. But the
rest of Leviticus (especially chapter 19) will give *ethical* laws as
well---not separately, but interspersed with ritual laws. Turning to the
ethical laws, we find that, on one hand, they are easier to deal with
than ritual laws, first, simply because they are fewer and, second,
because the reasons behind ethical law tend to be more readily apparent
than these of ritual. We are inclined to question why cows are permitted
for food and pigs are not, but when we read a command that "You shall
not steal, and you shall not lie, and you shall not act false" (19:11),
we do not generally feel the need to ask, "Why not?" On the other hand,
what is harder about dealing with ethical laws is that they are not as
easily grouped by types, and they in fact usually do not occur in long
sections of related laws as do the sacrificial, food, purity, or leprosy
laws. The exceptions are the lists of forbidden sexual relations
(18:6--30; 20:10--20) and a group of economic laws (Leviticus 25). The
ethical laws include such diverse matters as leaving portions of
harvests for the poor, judging cases without bias (toward the rich or
toward the poor), honoring the aged, fearing one's parents, not injuring
the blind or deaf, not gossiping, not making one's daughter a
prostitute, and not committing adultery. If there is any common
denominator to these diverse laws it is: feeling empathy with other
human beings and attributing value to the lives of all of them.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 18:5*](#Contents.html_rlev18_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And you shall observe my laws and my judgments, which, when a human
will do them, he'll live through them! I am YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap18_05.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:5. **when a human will do them, he'll live through them!** This way
of picturing the laws, as a path to *life,* begins here. It returns as
the climax of the Torah in Deuteronomy. The path to the Tree of Life is
blocked at the Torah's beginning, and the way to recover it is
emphasized at the Torah's end. The laws are not presented as a burden
but as a blessing. Those who have characterized the law as a weight that
no human can possibly bear, as a curse from which one needs to be saved,
may insufficiently appreciate the positive, fulfilling quality of the
law as experienced by those who live it. It is presented here as
something that humans *can* do. And those who add so much to the law
that it does become a weight, forcing those who observe it into
separation from fellow Jews and from the world, risk diminishing the
positive force of the law and its value as being ultimately a source of
blessing for all the families of the earth.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 18:6*](#Contents.html_rlev18_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^"Any man, to any close relative of his: you shall not come close to
expose nudity. I am YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap18_06.jpg){height="77" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:6. **expose nudity**. This means sexual intimacy. In 18:14 the text
indicates that, if one has sexual relations with one's aunt, one exposes
one's *uncle's* nudity. So to "expose nudity" must be understood broadly
as meaning to enter the zone of another person's sexuality.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 18:7*](#Contents.html_rlev18_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^You shall not expose your father's nudity and your mother's nudity.
She is your mother. You shall not expose her nudity.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap18_07.jpg){height="52" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 18:8*](#Contents.html_rlev18_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^You shall not expose your father's wife's nudity. It is your father's
nudity.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap18_08.jpg){height="49" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 18:9*](#Contents.html_rlev18_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^Your sister's nudity---your father's daughter or your mother's
daughter, born home or born outside---you shall not expose their nudity.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap18_09.jpg){height="81" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 18:10*](#Contents.html_rlev18_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^The nudity of your son's daughter or your daughter's daughter---you
shall not expose their nudity, because they are *your* nudity.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap18_10.jpg){height="72" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 18:11*](#Contents.html_rlev18_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^The nudity of your father's wife's daughter, born of your father:
she is your sister; you shall not expose her nudity.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap18_11.jpg){height="54" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 18:12*](#Contents.html_rlev18_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
