^8^He shall not eat a carcass or a torn animal, to become impure by it.
I am YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_08.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:9*](#Contents.html_rlev22_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^"And they shall keep my charge so they will not bear sin over it and
die through it when they desecrate it. I, YHWH, make them holy.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_09.jpg){height="46" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:10*](#Contents.html_rlev22_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^"And any outsider shall not eat what is holy: a priest's visitor and
an employee shall not eat what is holy.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_10.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

22:10. **outsider**. See comment on Exod 29:33.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:11*](#Contents.html_rlev22_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^But when a priest buys a person as a possession by his money, he may
eat it; and one who is born in his household: they may eat his bread.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_11.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:12*](#Contents.html_rlev22_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And a priest's daughter who will become an outsider's: she shall not
eat the donated holy things;

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_12.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:13*](#Contents.html_rlev22_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^but a priest's daughter who will be a widow or a divorcée and has no
seed and returns to her father's house as in her youth, she may eat from
her father's bread. But any outsider shall not eat it.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_13.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:14*](#Contents.html_rlev22_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And a man who eats what is holy by mistake shall add to it a fifth
of it and shall give the holy thing to the priest.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_14.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:15*](#Contents.html_rlev22_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And they shall not desecrate the holy things of the children of
Israel that they will donate to YHWH

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_15.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:16*](#Contents.html_rlev22_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^so that they would make them bear a crime requiring a guilt offering
by their eating their holy things, because I, YHWH, make them holy."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_16.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:17*](#Contents.html_rlev22_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_17.jpg){height="22" width="219"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:18*](#Contents.html_rlev22_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^"Speak to Aaron and to his sons and to all the children of Israel,
and you shall say to them: Any man from the house of Israel or from the
aliens in Israel who will make his offering---for all their vows and all
their contributions that they bring forward to YHWH as a burnt
offering---

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_18.jpg){height="120" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:19*](#Contents.html_rlev22_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^for acceptance for you it is to be unblemished, male of the cattle,
of the sheep, or of the goats.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_19.jpg){height="43" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:20*](#Contents.html_rlev22_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^You shall not bring forward any that has an injury in it, because it
will not be acceptable for you.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_20.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:21*](#Contents.html_rlev22_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And a man who will bring forward a peace-offering sacrifice to YHWH
to express a vow or as a contribution of the cattle or of the flock: it
shall be unblemished for acceptance; there shall not be any injury in
it.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_21.jpg){height="97" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:22*](#Contents.html_rlev22_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^Blind or broken or slashed or sore or scabbed or scurvied: you shall
not bring these forward to YHWH, and you shall not give an offering by
fire of them on the altar to YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_22.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:23*](#Contents.html_rlev22_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^An ox or a sheep that has an elongated limb or a short limb: you may
make it a contribution, but it shall not be accepted for a vow.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_23.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:24*](#Contents.html_rlev22_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And you shall not bring forward to YHWH one that is bruised,
crushed, torn, or cut. And you shall not do it in your land,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_24.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:25*](#Contents.html_rlev22_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^and you shall not bring forward your God's bread from any of these
from a foreigner's hand, because their flaw is in them, an injury is in
them; they shall not be accepted for you."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_25.jpg){height="76" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:26*](#Contents.html_rlev22_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_26.jpg){height="26" width="226"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:27*](#Contents.html_rlev22_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^"When an ox or a sheep or a goat will be born, it will be under its
mother seven days, and from the eighth day on it will be accepted as an
offering, an offering by fire to YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_27.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:28*](#Contents.html_rlev22_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And an ox or a sheep: you shall not slaughter it and its child on
one day.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_28.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

22:28. **it and its child on one day**. It is possible that this is a
parallel to the commandment against cooking a kid in its mother's milk.
This case is more commonly understood to be a moral matter---a respect
for the animals, accepting that although we eat animals we must
appreciate that they are living beings---whereas the case of the kid
also has a ritual dimension, reflected in a Ugaritic text. See comment
on Exod 23:19.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:29*](#Contents.html_rlev22_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And if you will make a sacrifice of thanks to YHWH, you shall
sacrifice it so it will be acceptable for you.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_29.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:30*](#Contents.html_rlev22_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^It shall be eaten on that day. Do not leave any of it until morning.
I am YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_30.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:31*](#Contents.html_rlev22_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^"And you shall observe my commandments and do them. I am YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_31.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:32*](#Contents.html_rlev22_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^"And you shall not desecrate my holy name, so I shall be sanctified
among the children of Israel. I am YHWH, who makes you holy,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_32.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 22:33*](#Contents.html_rlev22_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^who brought brought you out from the land of Egypt to be God to you.
I am YHWH."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap22_33.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Lev_Chapter23.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:1*](#Contents.html_rlev23_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **23** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_01.jpg){height="22" width="230"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:2*](#Contents.html_rlev23_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Speak to the children of Israel, and you shall say to them: YHWH's
appointed times, which you shall call holy assemblies---these are my
appointed times:

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_02.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

23:2. **these are my appointed times**. The laws of the holidays as
expressed in Leviticus reflect a concern with order and arrangement as
well. YHWH tells Moses to tell the people what the nation's holidays are
to be, specifically: the Sabbath, the holiday of unleavened bread
(*ma![Image](images/sdot.jpg){.block height="11"
width="6"}![Image](images/sdot.jpg){.block height="11" width="6"}ôt*),
an unnamed holiday related to firstfruits, an unnamed holiday involving
horn blowing (known subsequently in Judaism, but not biblically, as the
New Year), the Day of Atonement, and the holiday of booths (*sukkôt*).
In each case the text gives information on how to celebrate the holiday.
In all but one case it gives the date on which the holiday is to be
celebrated. Interestingly, it does not make historical associations for
the holidays, not even connecting the feast of unleavened bread to the
exodus from Egypt, or the Sabbath to God's rest at the end of the
creation. (The single exception is an instruction in 23:39--43 that the
Israelites should live in booths during the feast of *sukkôt* in order
to remember that YHWH had Israel live in booths when He brought them out
of Egypt.) It appears that the main concern, as with the food and purity
laws, is not to explicate the background and purposes of the
holidays---these things are a concern of Genesis, Exodus, Numbers, and
Deuteronomy---but rather to instruct the people on how to observe them
properly. In the case of the holidays, the concern of Leviticus with
categorization and organization is applied to the temporal as well as
the spatial, thus institutionalizing the sanctification of time more
than in any text thus far. Organization through ritual is thus designed
to pervade the people's life.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:3*](#Contents.html_rlev23_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^Six days work shall be done, and in the seventh day is a Sabbath, a
ceasing, a holy assembly. You shall not do any work. It is a Sabbath to
YHWH in all your homes.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_03.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:4*](#Contents.html_rlev23_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^"These are YHWH's appointed times, holy assemblies that you shall
proclaim at their appointed time:

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_04.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:5*](#Contents.html_rlev23_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^In the first month, on the fourteenth of the month, 'between the two
evenings,' is YHWH's Passover.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_05.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:6*](#Contents.html_rlev23_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And on the fifteenth day of this month is YHWH's Festival of
Unleavened Bread. You shall eat unleavened bread seven days.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_06.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:7*](#Contents.html_rlev23_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^On the first day you shall have a holy assembly. You shall not do any
act of work.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_07.jpg){height="50" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:8*](#Contents.html_rlev23_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And you shall bring forward an offering by fire to YHWH for seven
days. On the seventh day you shall have a holy assembly. You shall not
do any act of work."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_08.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:9*](#Contents.html_rlev23_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_09.jpg){height="51" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:10*](#Contents.html_rlev23_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^"Speak to the children of Israel, and you shall say to them: When
you will come to the land that I am giving to you and you reap its
harvest, you shall bring the first sheaf of your harvest to the priest,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_10.jpg){height="96" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:11*](#Contents.html_rlev23_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^and he shall elevate the sheaf in front of YHWH for acceptance for
you. The priest shall elevate it on the day after the Sabbath.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_11.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:12*](#Contents.html_rlev23_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And on the day of your elevating the sheaf, you shall do an
unblemished lamb in its first year as a burnt offering to YHWH,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_12.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:13*](#Contents.html_rlev23_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^and its grain offering shall be two-tenths of a measure of fine
flour mixed with oil, an offering by fire to YHWH, a pleasant smell, and
its libation shall be wine, a fourth of a hin.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_13.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:14*](#Contents.html_rlev23_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And you shall not eat bread or parched or fresh grain until that
very day, until you have brought your God's offering. It is an eternal
law through your generations in all your homes.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_14.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:15*](#Contents.html_rlev23_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And you shall count from the day after the Sabbath, from the day of
your bringing the sheaf for an elevation offering, seven Sabbaths. They
shall be complete.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_15.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

23:15. **seven Sabbaths**. Meaning: seven full weeks.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:16*](#Contents.html_rlev23_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^You shall count until the day after the seventh Sabbath: fifty days.
And you shall bring forward a new grain offering to YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_16.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:17*](#Contents.html_rlev23_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^From your homes you shall bring bread for an elevation offering: two
of them. They shall be two-tenths of a measure of fine flour, baked with
leaven, firstfruits to YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_17.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:18*](#Contents.html_rlev23_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And you shall bring forward with the bread seven lambs, unblemished
one-year-olds, and one bull of the cattle and two rams. They shall be a
burnt offering to YHWH, with their grain offering and their libations,
an offering by fire, a pleasant smell to YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_18.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:19*](#Contents.html_rlev23_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And you shall do one goat as a sin offering and two one-year-old
lambs as a peace-offering sacrifice.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_19.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:20*](#Contents.html_rlev23_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And the priest shall elevate them with the bread of firstfruits, an
elevation offering in front of YHWH with the two sheep. They shall be a
holy thing to YHWH: for the priest.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_20.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:21*](#Contents.html_rlev23_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And you shall proclaim on that very day: you shall have a holy
assembly. You shall not do any act of work: an eternal law in all your
homes through your generations.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_21.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:22*](#Contents.html_rlev23_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And when you reap your land's harvest, you shall not finish your
field's corner when you harvest, and you shall not gather your harvest's
gleaning. You shall leave them for the poor and for the alien. I am
YHWH, your God."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_22.jpg){height="99" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:23*](#Contents.html_rlev23_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_23.jpg){height="45" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:24*](#Contents.html_rlev23_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^"Speak to the children of Israel, saying: In the seventh month, on
the first of the month you shall have a ceasing, a commemoration with
horn-blasting, a holy assembly.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_24.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

23:24. **In the seventh month, on the first of the month**. This holiday
is not named; but, coming nine days before Yom Kippur and involving the
blasting of a horn, it is clearly the holiday that is now known as the
Jewish New Year, Rosh Hashanah. It is not known by this name in the
Torah because, as this verse indicates, it came in the seventh month of
the calendar. In the Torah's calendar, the first month comes in spring,
and the first holiday is Passover (see Exod 12:2).

23:24. **blasting**. Sounding a shofar or trumpet.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:25*](#Contents.html_rlev23_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^You shall not do any act of work. And you shall bring forward an
offering by fire to YHWH."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_25.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:26*](#Contents.html_rlev23_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_26.jpg){height="43" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:27*](#Contents.html_rlev23_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^"Just: On the tenth of this seventh month, it is the Day of
Atonement. You shall have a holy assembly, and you shall degrade
yourselves. And you shall bring forward an offering by fire to YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_27.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

23:27. **Just**. This term of special emphasis (Hebrew *'ak*) introduces
commands concerning certain holidays: the Sabbath (Exod 31:13), Passover
(12:16), the Day of Atonement (Lev 23:27), and Sukkot (23:39).

23:27. **degrade yourselves**. The word "degrade" is the same term that
is used for what the Egyptians did to the Israelites (Exod 1:11--12). As
a noun or adjective, the word means "poor." As a verb, it means "to
abuse, afflict, oppress." It suggests that on the Day of Atonement one
is to be hard on oneself and not to give care to making oneself look
attractive or materially successful. It is understood to include
fasting.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:28*](#Contents.html_rlev23_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And you shall not do any work on this very day, because it is a day
of atonement, to atone for you in front of YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_28.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:29*](#Contents.html_rlev23_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^Because any person who will not be degraded on this very day will be
cut off from his people.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_29.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

23:29. **his people**. The Hebrew word for "person" or "soul" (*nepeš*)
is feminine, and so this is a case in which the feminine form stands for
both men and women---contrary to the usual practice in Hebrew (and
English). A more neutral English translation could be "I shall destroy
that soul from among its people." But the word "soul" carries a wide
range of meanings, which go beyond the point of this verse. And it is
worth noting that there are occasions when the feminine as well as the
masculine is used to stand for all humans.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:30*](#Contents.html_rlev23_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And any person who will do any work in this very day: I shall
destroy that person from among his people.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_30.jpg){height="71" width="336"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:31*](#Contents.html_rlev23_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^You shall not do any work: an eternal law through your generations
in all your homes.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_31.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:32*](#Contents.html_rlev23_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^It is a Sabbath, a ceasing, for you, and you shall degrade
yourselves: on the ninth of the month in the evening, from evening to
evening, you shall keep your Sabbath."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_32.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:33*](#Contents.html_rlev23_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_33.jpg){height="22" width="224"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:34*](#Contents.html_rlev23_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^"Speak to the children of Israel, saying: On the fifteenth day of
this seventh month is the Festival of Booths, seven days, for YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_34.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

23:34. **Booths**. Hebrew *sukkôt.*This term is used to describe the
function of the *p![Image](images/a-image.jpg){.block height="11"
width="8"}r![Image](images/o.jpg){.block height="11" width="7"}ket,* the
pavilion that covers over the ark (Exod 40:3; Ps 27:5; Lam 2:6; see my
comment on Exod 26:31). It thus reflects simultaneously both the simple,
makeshift dwellings that house the Israelites in the wilderness period
and the beautiful, most sacred structure that houses the ark.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:35*](#Contents.html_rlev23_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^On the first day is a holy assembly. You shall not do any act of
work.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_35.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:36*](#Contents.html_rlev23_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^For seven days you shall bring forward an offering by fire to YHWH.
On the eighth day you shall have a holy assembly, and you shall bring
forward an offering by fire to YHWH. It is a convocation. You shall not
do any act of work.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_36.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

23:36. **convocation**. The meaning of this term, Hebrew
*'![Image](images/a1.jpg){.block height="12"
width="8"}![Image](images/sdot.jpg){.block height="11" width="6"}eret,*
is uncertain. It occurs six times in the *Tanak,* and five of them refer
specifically to this holiday, now known as Shemini Atzeret. The sixth
(Jer 9:1) suggests that it means a gathering of people. It is thus
understood to mean a special assembly.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:37*](#Contents.html_rlev23_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^"These are YHWH's appointed times, which you shall call holy
assemblies, to bring forward an offering by fire to YHWH: burnt offering
and grain offering, sacrifice and libations, each day's thing on its
day,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_37.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:38*](#Contents.html_rlev23_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^aside from YHWH's Sabbaths and aside from your gifts and aside from
all of your vows and aside from all of your contributions that you will
give to YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_38.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:39*](#Contents.html_rlev23_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^"Just: On the fifteenth day of the seventh month when you gather the
land's produce, you shall celebrate YHWH's holiday seven days. On the
first day is a ceasing, and on the eighth day is a ceasing.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_39.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:40*](#Contents.html_rlev23_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^And on the first day you shall take fruit of appealing trees,
branches of palms, boughs of thick trees, and willows of a wadi, and you
shall be happy in front of YHWH, your God, seven days.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_40.jpg){height="73" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:41*](#Contents.html_rlev23_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^And you shall celebrate it, a holiday to YHWH, seven days in the
year, an eternal law through your generations; you shall celebrate it in
the seventh month.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_41.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:42*](#Contents.html_rlev23_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^You shall live in booths seven days. Every citizen in Israel shall
live in booths,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_42.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

23:42. **booths**. Hebrew *sukkôt.* See comment on 23:34.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:43*](#Contents.html_rlev23_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^so your generations will know that I had the children of Israel live
in booths when I brought them out from the land of Egypt. I am YHWH,
your God."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_43.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 23:44*](#Contents.html_rlev23_44)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^44^And Moses spoke YHWH's appointed times to the children of Israel.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap23_44.jpg){height="48" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Lev_Chapter24.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:1*](#Contents.html_rlev24_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **24** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_01.jpg){height="22" width="241"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:2*](#Contents.html_rlev24_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Command the children of Israel that they shall take clear pressed
olive oil to you for the light, to keep up a lamp always.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_02.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:3*](#Contents.html_rlev24_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^Outside of the pavilion of the Testimony, in the Tent of Meeting,
Aaron shall arrange it from evening until morning in front of YHWH
always, an eternal law through your generations.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_03.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:4*](#Contents.html_rlev24_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^He shall arrange the lamps on the pure menorah in front of YHWH
always.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_04.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:5*](#Contents.html_rlev24_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^"And you shall take fine flour and bake it into twelve loaves: each
loaf shall be two-tenths of a measure.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_05.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:6*](#Contents.html_rlev24_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And you shall set them in two rows, six to a row, on the pure table
in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_06.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:7*](#Contents.html_rlev24_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And you shall put clear frankincense on each row, and it will be a
representative portion for the bread, an offering by fire to YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_07.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:8*](#Contents.html_rlev24_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^On the Sabbath day, on the Sabbath day, he shall arrange it in front
of YHWH always from the children of Israel, an eternal covenant.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_08.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

24:8. **On the Sabbath day, on the Sabbath day**. Meaning: on every
Sabbath.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:9*](#Contents.html_rlev24_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And it shall be Aaron's and his sons', and they shall eat it in a
holy place, because it is the holy of holies to him from YHWH's
offerings by fire, an eternal law."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_09.jpg){height="70" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:10*](#Contents.html_rlev24_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And a son of an Israelite woman---and he was a son of an Egyptian
man---went out among the children of Israel, and the son of the
Israelite woman fought with an Israelite man in the camp.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_10.jpg){height="106" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

24:10--23. **a son of an Israelite woman\.... they battered him with
stone**. It is curious that this single story occurs here in a section
fifteen chapters long that otherwise simply states the law without any
accompanying narratives. This story's significance may lie in the fact
that it states that the man in question is the son of an Israelite woman
and an Egyptian father, and the deity's decision includes the notation
that the law applies equally to an alien and to a citizen. It therefore
appears that the offender in this story is an alien (see the comment on
Lev 24:16,22), and the account therefore has the value not only of
dramatizing the law concerning blasphemy, but also of dramatizing the
principle of equality before the law: "You shall have one judgment: it
will be the same for the alien and the citizen" (24:22). Indeed, the
group of laws that were described above as reflecting the notion of
equivalence of justice ("eye for eye ... ") is embedded in the middle of
this very story. The story may appear here, therefore, because it
illustrates several important laws and an extremely important legal
principle.

The story is also interesting in parallel with the only other narrative
in Leviticus, the story of the consecration of the priesthood. Like that
other story, which culminates in the deaths of Aaron's sons, the account
of the blasphemy expresses what is at stake in the people's young
relationship with their God. Further, in the earlier account, it is God
who deals with the offenders, Nadab and Abihu, directly; whereas in this
account of the blasphemy, humans themselves must deal justice. The two
stories thus convey together the idea that the law is both a divine and
a human concern.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:11*](#Contents.html_rlev24_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And the son of the Israelite woman profaned the name and cursed. And
they brought him to Moses. And his mother's name was Shelomith, daughter
of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_11.jpg){height="80" width="371"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:12*](#Contents.html_rlev24_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And they left him under watch, to determine it for them by YHWH's
word.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_12.jpg){height="50" width="368"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:13*](#Contents.html_rlev24_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_13.jpg){height="50" width="367"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:14*](#Contents.html_rlev24_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^"Take out the one who cursed to the outside of the camp, and let all
who heard lay their hands on his head, and all the congregation shall
batter him.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_14.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

24:14. **lay their hands on his head**. The community's elders lay their
hands on the head of a bull that is slaughtered as a sacrifice for a sin
(4:15); and Aaron and his sons lay their hands on the head of the animal
that is sacrificed as a sin offering (Lev 8:14). And so the mention of
this ritual here brings up a frightful image of what is to come for the
man who cursed, and it emphasizes the unusual solemnity of this
execution for the crime of cursing God.

24:14. **batter**. Meaning: stone to death.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:15*](#Contents.html_rlev24_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^"And you shall speak to the children of Israel, saying: Any man who
curses his God: he shall bear his sin.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_15.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:16*](#Contents.html_rlev24_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And one who profanes YHWH's name shall be *put to death.* All the
congregation shall *batter* him. The same for the alien and the citizen:
when he profanes the name he shall be put to death.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_16.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

24:16. **the same for the alien and the citizen**. This phrase occurs at
both the beginning and the end of this section on equivalence in
justice. This principle of treating an alien like a citizen comes up
often in the Torah, but its double emphasis here in the story of a man
who has an Israelite mother and an Egyptian father is significant. It
indicates that this man is an alien, because there would be no reason to
make a special point of this principle if he were a citizen (that is, a
native Israelite). It is further evidence that in biblical times a
person's identification was based on the father (patrilineal descent).
In every case we have encountered thus far, the woman and children take
the ethnic and religious identity of the woman's husband---Sarah,
Rebekah, Leah, Rachel, Zipporah, and their children. And all sons of
Levite fathers are Levites, regardless of the tribal origins of their
mothers. Likewise, all tribal identification is through the father (see
the comment on Num 36:3). Identification in the Torah is patrilineal.
This continues through the *Tanak* until the time of Ezra and Nehemiah.
At that point it appears to change, because when Nehemiah opposes
marriages of Jewish men to women from certain other groups, he instructs
that their children be excluded (Nehemiah 13). And in postbiblical
Judaism, identification came to be based on the mother (matrilineal
descent). In the Torah, however, it follows the father in every case.
Identification by patrilineal descent (in addition to matrilineal
descent, not in place of it) is at present a matter of dispute among the
movements in Judaism.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:17*](#Contents.html_rlev24_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^"And a man who will strike any human's life shall be *put to death,*

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_17.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

24:17. **strike any human's life**. Meaning: strike to death.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:18*](#Contents.html_rlev24_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^and one who strikes an animal's life shall pay for it: a life for a
life.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_18.jpg){height="43" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:19*](#Contents.html_rlev24_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And a man who will make an injury in his fellow: as he has done, so
it shall be done to him.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_19.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:20*](#Contents.html_rlev24_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^A break for a break, an eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth: as he
will make an injury in a human, so it shall be made in him.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_20.jpg){height="67" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

24:20. **an eye for an eye**. Perhaps the most perplexing of the ethical
laws is the principle of justice expressed in the formulation "an eye
for an eye." It has frequently been cited as evidence of the stern
character of YHWH, but that is a misunderstanding. In its context in
Leviticus it applies solely to human justice. YHWH Himself frequently
follows a more relenting course than that, from the golden calf event to
a series of reprieves for seemingly undeserving individuals and
communities in subsequent books of the *Tanak.* As for the meaning of
this formulation for human justice, we must read it in its context,
where the basic principle appears to be that punishment should
correspond to the crime and never exceed it:

A man who will make an injury in his fellow: as he has done, so it shall
be done to him.

A break for a break, an eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth: as he will
make an injury in a human, so it shall be made in him.

And one who strikes \[mortally\] an animal shall pay for it, and one who
strikes \[mortally\] a human shall be put to death.

You shall have one judgment: it will be the same for the alien and the
citizen.

The common principle of all the instances in this group appears to be
one of equivalence in justice. To that extent it is a prohibition
against imposing punishments that do not suit the crime; e.g., execution
as a punishment for killing an animal or for wounding a man; or more
severe punishments for lower-class defendants or foreigners than for
others. The question remains as to whether "a break for a break, an eye
for an eye" was meant literally or if it likewise referred figuratively
to equivalence of punishment: not requiring the court actually to gouge
the eye of a man convicted of gouging, but only to exact an appropriate
monetary compensation for the victim. The text certainly appears to be
speaking quite literally. It is set in the middle of a story of an
actual stoning, and the wording would not ordinarily be taken as
figurative were it not for our surprise at the severity of it in
comparison to our own current laws. Still, we must be cautious about our
ability to judge the meaning of idiomatic expressions in the Hebrew of
an ancient age, a Hebrew in which, for example, the expression "to know
God face-to-face" explicitly did *not* mean actually to see the deity's
face (Exod 33:11; cf. 33:20,23). Further, the earliest postbiblical
Jewish sources already understood "an eye for an eye" to mean monetary,
and not literal, compensation. In the present state of our knowledge,
therefore, we may be limited to saying that this text either (1) meant
equivalent monetary compensation, or else (2) initially meant literal
physical punishment but was reinterpreted as monetary equivalence at a
relatively early stage in legal history (in the early postbiblical
period at the latest).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:21*](#Contents.html_rlev24_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And one who strikes an animal shall pay for it, and one who strikes
a human shall be put to death.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_21.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:22*](#Contents.html_rlev24_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^You shall have one judgment: it will be the same for the alien and
the citizen; because I am YHWH, your God."

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_22.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 24:23*](#Contents.html_rlev24_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And Moses spoke to the children of Israel. And they brought out the
one who cursed to the outside of the camp, and they battered him with
stone.

And the children of Israel had done as YHWH commanded Moses.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap24_23.jpg){height="98" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Lev_Chapter25.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:1*](#Contents.html_rlev25_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## I[N THE]{.smallcaps1} M[OUNTAIN]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **25** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses in Mount Sinai, saying,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_01.jpg){height="25" width="312"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:2*](#Contents.html_rlev25_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Speak to the children of Israel. And you shall say to them: When you
will come to the land that I am giving to you, then the land shall have
a Sabbath for YHWH.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_02.jpg){height="73" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:3*](#Contents.html_rlev25_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^Six years you shall seed your field, and six years you shall prune
your vineyard, and you shall gather its produce.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_03.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:4*](#Contents.html_rlev25_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And in the seventh year the land shall have a Sabbath, a ceasing, a
Sabbath for YHWH: you shall not seed your field, and you shall not prune
your vineyard,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:5*](#Contents.html_rlev25_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^you shall not reap your harvest's free growth, and you shall not cut
off your untrimmed grapes. The land shall have a year of ceasing.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_05.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:5. **free growth**. Plants that sprout on their own after a harvest
rather than having been planted.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:6*](#Contents.html_rlev25_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And the land's Sabbath shall be yours for food: for you and for your
servant and for your maid and for your employee and for your visitor who
are residing with you

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_06.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:7*](#Contents.html_rlev25_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^and for your domestic animal and for the wild animal that are in your
land shall be all of its produce to eat.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_07.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:8*](#Contents.html_rlev25_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^"And you shall count seven Sabbaths of years: seven years, seven
times. And the days of the seven Sabbaths of years shall be for you
forty-nine years.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_08.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:9*](#Contents.html_rlev25_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And you shall pass a blasting horn in the seventh month on the tenth
of the month: on the Day of Atonement you shall have the horn pass
through all your land.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_09.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:10*](#Contents.html_rlev25_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And you shall consecrate the year that makes fifty years and
proclaim liberty in the land, to all its inhabitants. It shall be a
jubilee for you. And you shall go back, each to his possession; and you
shall go back, each to his family.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_10.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:10. **possession**. Meaning: each person's ancestral property.

25:10. **a jubilee: you shall go back, each to his possession**. In the
law of the jubilee, YHWH commands that every fifty years all property is
to return to the original owners. This appears to be an economic program
designed to prevent the feudal system, common in the rest of the ancient
Near East, from developing in Israel. That is, it functions to prevent
the establishment of a class of wealthy landowners at the top of the
economic scale and a mass of landless peasants at the bottom. Every
Israelite is to be apportioned some land (described in the books of
Numbers and Joshua), and the deity commands that in every fiftieth year
the system returns to where it started. If an Israelite has lost his
ancestral land as a result of debt or calamity, he regains ownership of
it in the jubilee year. *Land is unalienable.* Individuals can suffer
difficult times, but there is a divinely decreed limit to their loss,
and the nation as a whole can never degenerate into a two-tiered system
of the very rich and the very poor.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:11*](#Contents.html_rlev25_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^It, the year that makes fifty years, shall be a jubilee for you. You
shall not seed, and you shall not harvest its free growths, and you
shall not cut off its untrimmed fruits,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_11.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:12*](#Contents.html_rlev25_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^because it is a jubilee; it shall be a holy thing to you. You shall
eat its produce from the field.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_12.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:13*](#Contents.html_rlev25_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^In this jubilee year you shall go back, each to his possession.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_13.jpg){height="22" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:14*](#Contents.html_rlev25_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And when you will make a sale to your fellow or buy from your
fellow's hand: each of you, do not persecute his brother.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_14.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:15*](#Contents.html_rlev25_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^You shall buy from your fellow by the number of years after the
jubilee. He shall sell to you by the number of years of yields---

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_15.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:16*](#Contents.html_rlev25_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^corresponding to a greater amount of the years, you shall make its
price greater; and corresponding to a smaller amount of the years, you
shall make its price smaller---because it is a number of yields that he
is selling to you.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_16.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:17*](#Contents.html_rlev25_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And you shall not persecute---each of you---his brother, but you
shall fear your God, because I am YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_17.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:18*](#Contents.html_rlev25_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^"And you shall do my laws and observe my judgments and do them, so
you will live on the land in security.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_18.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:19*](#Contents.html_rlev25_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And the land will give its fruit, and you will eat to the full, and
you will live in security on it.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_19.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:20*](#Contents.html_rlev25_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And if you will say, 'What shall we eat in the seventh year? Here,
we won't seed and won't gather our produce,'

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_20.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:21*](#Contents.html_rlev25_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^I shall command my blessing for you in the sixth year, and it will
make the produce for the three years.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_21.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:21. **three years**. The sixth year, the seventh year, and the eighth
year (the first year of the next seven-year cycle, until the first
planting of that year produces a crop).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:22*](#Contents.html_rlev25_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And you will seed the eighth year and eat from the old produce until
the ninth year; you will eat the old until its produce comes.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_22.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:23*](#Contents.html_rlev25_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^But the land shall not be sold permanently, because the land is
*mine,* because you are aliens and visitors with me!

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_23.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:24*](#Contents.html_rlev25_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^So in all the land in your possession you shall give a redemption
for the land.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_24.jpg){height="53" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:25*](#Contents.html_rlev25_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^When your brother will be low so that he will sell any of his
possession, then his redeemer who is the closest relative to him shall
come and redeem his brother's sale.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_25.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:25,35,39. **when your brother will be low**. Meaning: when your
fellow Israelite will be so poor that he has difficulty holding on to
his land. It has long been noted that the three times that this phrase
occurs in this chapter reflect three stages of increasing hardship. In
the first, the person has to sell his land. In the second, he has both
lost his land and is without money. And in the third, he himself is sold
(or sells himself) into servitude. And at each stage, the man's fellow
Israelite is commanded to help him. If he has to sell his land, one must
redeem it for him and then give it back to him in the jubilee year. If
he is without money, one must lend him money without any charge or
interest. And if he is sold, one must not treat him as a slave.

Here the units of law convey the specific requirements while the
arrangement conveys the basic principle, namely, that as one's brother's
need increases, so does one's responsibility to help him. Further, one
must thus help one's fellow as a matter of law. The chapter never speaks
of charity, nor does it appeal to one's feelings of compassion or
generosity. An unfortunate Israelite need not feel degraded to be poor
nor ashamed to be pitied. Economic suffering is rather treated as a
reality of life, which one is required by law to remedy. The poor man
thus can know that his brother is helping him because the system
requires brothers to help one another and that, if the shoe were on the
other foot, he would do the same for his brother. This is not to say
that the text denies or discourages feelings of compassion, but only
that the fulfillment of the law is not made dependent upon the presence
or absence of such feelings.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:26*](#Contents.html_rlev25_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And when a man will not have a redeemer, but his hand has come to
attain it and he has found enough for his redemption,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_26.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:27*](#Contents.html_rlev25_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^then he shall figure the years of his sale and pay back what is left
over to the man to whom he sold it, and he shall go back to his
possession.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_27.jpg){height="49" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:28*](#Contents.html_rlev25_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And if his hand has not found enough to pay it back to him, then his
sale shall be in the hand of the one who bought it until the jubilee
year, and it shall come out in the jubilee, and he shall go back to his
possession.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_28.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:29*](#Contents.html_rlev25_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^"And when a man will sell a home in a walled city, then its
redemption shall be until the end of the year of its sale. Its
redemption shall be for days.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_29.jpg){height="47" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:29. **for days**. The word "days" is understood here to mean the rest
of the days of the year---up to one full year. His redemption period can
amount to days, not years.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:30*](#Contents.html_rlev25_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And if it will not be redeemed by the completion of a full year for
it, then the house that is in a city that has a wall shall be
established permanently for the one who bought it, through his
generations. It shall not come out in the jubilee.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_30.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

![Image](images/25page_405.jpg){height="26" width="69"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:31*](#Contents.html_rlev25_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^"As for houses of the villages that do not have a wall around them:
it shall be figured along with the land's field. It shall have
redemption, and it shall come out in the jubilee.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_31.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:32*](#Contents.html_rlev25_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^"As for the Levites' cities, the houses in the cities of their
possession: the Levites shall have eternal redemption.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_32.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:33*](#Contents.html_rlev25_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And that which one from the Levites will redeem: it shall come out,
a sale of a house and of a city of his possession, in the jubilee,
because that---the houses of the Levites' cities---is their possession
among the children of Israel.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_33.jpg){height="91" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:34*](#Contents.html_rlev25_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^But a field of their cities' surrounding land shall not be sold,
because it is an eternal possession for them.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_34.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:35*](#Contents.html_rlev25_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^"And when your brother will be low so that his hand is slipping with
you, then you shall take hold of him---an alien and a visitor---and he
shall live with you.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_35.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:35. **his hand is slipping**. Meaning: he does not have enough food
and money to live.

25:35. **an alien and a visitor**. Being landless, he is now like an
alien or a visitor.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:36*](#Contents.html_rlev25_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^Do not take interest or a charge from him; but you shall fear your
God, and your brother will live with you.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_36.jpg){height="47x" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:37*](#Contents.html_rlev25_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^You shall not give him your money for interest, and you shall not
give him your food for a charge.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_37.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:38*](#Contents.html_rlev25_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^I am YHWH, your God, who brought you out from the land of Egypt to
give you the land of Canaan, to be God to you.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_38.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:39*](#Contents.html_rlev25_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^"And when your brother will be low with you so that he is sold to
you, you shall not have him work a slave's work.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_39.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:40*](#Contents.html_rlev25_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^He shall be like an employee, like a visitor, with you. He shall
work with you until the jubilee year.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_40.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:41*](#Contents.html_rlev25_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^And he shall go out from you, he and his children with him, and go
back to his family, and he shall go back to his fathers' possession.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_41.jpg){height="49" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:42*](#Contents.html_rlev25_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^Because they are *my* servants, whom I brought out from the land of
Egypt; they shall not be sold like the sale of a slave.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_42.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:42. **servant, slave**. The same Hebrew root (*'bd*) is used for
"servant" and "slave." It has a wider range of meaning in Hebrew than
either word has in English. Here it results in the use of its two
different translations in the very same sentence, but this still conveys
the Torah's meaning more correctly than leveling the nuances of the
Hebrew into a single English word.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:43*](#Contents.html_rlev25_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^You shall not dominate him with harshness, but you shall fear your
God.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_43.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:43. **dominate**. "You shall not *dominate* him." This is the term
that is used for human dominion over animals in Genesis 1. Israelites
are explicitly denied the kind of power over other human beings, even a
slave, that they might exert over animals.

25:43. **with harshness**. This is the term that is used for the way the
Egyptians made the Israelites work (Exod 1:14). Eliminating slavery on
earth has been a gradual process rather than a revolution. The
Israelites are permitted to have slaves themselves, but not in the
manner of the Egyptians. They cannot overwork them. They cannot mistreat
them (so an Israelite who knocks out his slave's tooth must set the
slave free; Exod 21:2). The rules that are imposed on treatment of
slaves in the Torah involve a recognition of a slave's *humanity* and
*dignity,* and they establish that a slave has *rights.* This was a
crucial first stage in the long process leading to opposition to slavery
altogether.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:44*](#Contents.html_rlev25_44)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^44^"And your slave and your maid that you will have: from the nations
that are around you, you shall buy a slave or a maid from them.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_44.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:45*](#Contents.html_rlev25_45)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^45^And also from the children of the visitors who reside with you---you
shall buy from them and from their families that are with you---to whom
they gave birth in your land. And they shall become a possession for
you.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_45.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:46*](#Contents.html_rlev25_46)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^46^And you shall make them a legacy for your children after you to
inherit as a possession. You may have them work forever, but as for your
brothers, the children of Israel---a man toward his brother---you shall
not dominate him with harshness.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_46.jpg){height="98" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:47*](#Contents.html_rlev25_47)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^47^"And if the hand of an alien and visitor with you will attain it,
and your brother is low with him and is sold to the alien and visitor
with you or to an offshoot of an alien's family,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_47.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

25:47. **attain it**. That is, be able to provide the money to buy the
poor Israelite.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:48*](#Contents.html_rlev25_48)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^48^after he is sold he shall have redemption. One of his brothers shall
redeem him,

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_48.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:49*](#Contents.html_rlev25_49)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^49^or his uncle or his uncle's son shall redeem him, or some close
relative of his from his family shall redeem him; or if his hand can
attain it then he can redeem himself.

![Image](images/Lev_Chap25_49.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Leviticus 25:50*](#Contents.html_rlev25_50)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^50^And he shall figure with the one who buys him: from the year he was
sold to him to the jubilee year; and the price of his sale shall be by
