------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 7:84*](#Contents.html_rnum7_84)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^84^This was the dedication offering of the altar from Israel's
chieftains on the day of its being anointed: Twelve silver dishes,
twelve silver basins, twelve gold pans,

![image](images/Num_Chap07_84.jpg){height="97" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:84. **from Israel's chieftains**. Why does the text have to repeat the
detailed list of offerings from each of the twelve chieftains, when all
their offerings are exactly, word for word, the same? It is notable that
earlier the text gave detailed records of the number of persons in each
tribe. Now we learn that, even though the tribes are all different
sizes, their chieftains all bring the same offering. It would appear to
establish that all the tribes, whether large or small in population, are
equal.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 7:85*](#Contents.html_rnum7_85)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^85^each dish being a hundred thirty of silver, and each basin seventy;
all the silver of the items was two thousand four hundred by the shekel
of the Holy.

![image](images/Num_Chap07_85.jpg){height="96" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 7:86*](#Contents.html_rnum7_86)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^86^Twelve gold pans full of incense, the pans being ten each by the
shekel of the Holy; all the gold of the pans was a hundred twenty.

![image](images/Num_Chap07_86.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 7:87*](#Contents.html_rnum7_87)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^87^All the oxen for the burnt offering were twelve bulls, twelve rams,
twelve lambs a year old and their grain offering and twelve goats for a
sin offering.

![image](images/Num_Chap07_87.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 7:88*](#Contents.html_rnum7_88)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^88^And all the oxen of the peace-offering sacrifice were twenty-four
bulls, sixty rams, sixty he-goats, sixty lambs a year old. This was the
dedication offering of the altar after its being anointed.

![image](images/Num_Chap07_88.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 7:89*](#Contents.html_rnum7_89)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^89^And when Moses came into the Tent of Meeting to speak with Him, then
he heard the voice speaking to him from above the atonement dais that
was on the Ark of the Testimony, from between the two cherubs, and He
spoke to him.

![image](images/Num_Chap07_89.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers08.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:1*](#Contents.html_rnum8_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## W[HEN YOU PUT UP]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## 8 {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap08.jpg){height="34" width="95"}

![image](images/Num_Chap08_01.jpg){height="22" width="228"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:2*](#Contents.html_rnum8_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Speak to Aaron, and you shall say to him: 'When you put up the
lamps, the seven lamps shall shed light opposite the front of the
menorah.'"

![image](images/Num_Chap08_02.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:3*](#Contents.html_rnum8_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Aaron did so. He put up its lamps opposite the front of the
menorah as YHWH had commanded Moses.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_03.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:4*](#Contents.html_rnum8_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And this was the meno[]{#Numbers08.html_page_28}rah's construction:
hammered work of gold; including its shaft, including its flower, it was
hammered work. According to the appearance that YHWH had shown Moses, so
he made the menorah.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:5*](#Contents.html_rnum8_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap08_05.jpg){height="22" width="227"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:6*](#Contents.html_rnum8_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^"Take the Levites from among the children of Israel, and make them
pure.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_06.jpg){height="43" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:7*](#Contents.html_rnum8_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And this is what you shall do to them to make them pure: you shall
sprinkle water of sin expiation on them, and they shall pass a razor
over all of their flesh, and they shall wash their clothes, and so they
will be made pure.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_07.jpg){height="92" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:7. **water of sin expiation**. The nature and preparation of water of
sin expiation are described in Num 19:1--9.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:8*](#Contents.html_rnum8_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And they shall take a bull of the cattle and its grain offering, fine
flour mixed with oil; and you shall take a second bull of the cattle for
a sin offering.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_08.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:9*](#Contents.html_rnum8_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And you shall bring the Levites forward in front of the Tent of
Meeting, and you shall assemble all of the congregation of the children
of Israel.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_09.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:10*](#Contents.html_rnum8_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And you shall bring the Levites forward in front of YHWH, and the
children of Israel shall lay their hands on the Levites.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_10.jpg){height="68" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:11*](#Contents.html_rnum8_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Aaron shall make the Levites an elevation offering in front of
YHWH from the children of Israel, and they shall be to do YHWH's
service.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_11.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:12*](#Contents.html_rnum8_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And the Levites shall lay their hands on the bulls' heads. And make
one a sin offering and one a burnt offering for YHWH to make atonement
for the Levites.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_12.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:13*](#Contents.html_rnum8_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And you shall stand the Levites in front of Aaron and in front of
his sons and make them an elevation offering for YHWH.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_13.jpg){height="70" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:14*](#Contents.html_rnum8_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And you shall distinguish the Levites from among the children of
Israel, and the Levites shall be mine.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_14.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:15*](#Contents.html_rnum8_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And after that the Levites shall come to work at the Tent of
Meeting, when you have made them pure and made them an elevation
offering.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_15.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:16*](#Contents.html_rnum8_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^Because they are given, *given,* to me from among the children of
Israel. I have taken them to me in place of the first birth of every
womb, the firstborn of everyone from the children of Israel.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_16.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:17*](#Contents.html_rnum8_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^Because every firstborn of the children Israel is mine, of human and
of animal; on the day that I struck every firstborn in the land of Egypt
I consecrated them to me.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_17.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:18*](#Contents.html_rnum8_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And I took the Levites in place of every firstborn of the children
of Israel

![image](images/Num_Chap08_18.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:19*](#Contents.html_rnum8_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^and gave the Levites---they are given---to Aaron and to his sons
from among the children of Israel to do the work of the children of
Israel in the Tent of Meeting and to make atonement for the children of
Israel, so there will not be a plague in the children of Israel when the
children of Israel would come near to the Holy."

![image](images/Num_Chap08_19.jpg){height="120" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:19. **plague**. Only Levites come near to the holy zone at the
Tabernacle. This notice that a plague would occur if the children of
Israel would come near to the Holy proves to be disastrously true later
in the account of Phinehas, in which an encroachment on the
*qubb![Image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11" width="8"}h,* the inside
of the Tabernacle, is associated with a plague that kills thousands (Num
25:8--9).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:20*](#Contents.html_rnum8_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Moses and Aaron and all the congregation of the children of
Israel did to the Levites according to all that YHWH commanded Moses
about the Levites; the children of Israel did so to them.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_20.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:21*](#Contents.html_rnum8_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And the Levites did sin expiation and washed their clothes, and
Aaron made them an elevation offering in front of YHWH, and Aaron made
atonement for them to make them pure.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_21.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:21. **the Levites did sin expiation**. Meaning: they used the water of
sin expiation. See the comment on 8:7.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:22*](#Contents.html_rnum8_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And after that the Levites came to do their work in the Tent of
Meeting in front of Aaron and in front of his sons. As YHWH commanded
Moses about the Levites, so they did to them.

![image](images/Num_Chap08_22.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:23*](#Contents.html_rnum8_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap08_23.jpg){height="43" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:24*](#Contents.html_rnum8_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^"This is what the Levites have: from twenty-five years old and up
each shall come to do army service by the work of the Tent of Meeting;

![image](images/Num_Chap08_24.jpg){height="71" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:25*](#Contents.html_rnum8_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^and from fifty years old he shall go back from the army service and
shall not work anymore;

![image](images/Num_Chap08_25.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 8:26*](#Contents.html_rnum8_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^and he shall minister with his brothers in the Tent of Meeting to
keep the charge, but he shall not do work. You shall do thus to the
Levites regarding their charge."

![image](images/Num_Chap08_26.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers09.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:1*](#Contents.html_rnum9_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **9** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH had spoken to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai in the second
year of their exodus from the land of Egypt, in the first month, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap09_01.jpg){height="70" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:1. **had spoken**. This is a rare occurrence in the Torah: telling an
event out of order. The book of Numbers begins with God speaking to
Moses in the second month of the year. Now it tells of God speaking to
Moses back in the first month of the year. Why? Because it tells of a
case in which some people had been unable to observe Passover, which
falls in the first month, and so God tells Moses in this flashback that
the law in that situation is that they should observe Passover one month
later, on the evening of the fourteenth day of the second month. Their
observance on that date thus belongs here in the sequence of the
narrative. The flashback is placed here to explain it. The point is that
the Torah adheres to chronological order in its story, and exceptional
breaks in that pattern such as this one occur very rarely and for
specific narrative purposes.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:2*](#Contents.html_rnum9_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"And the children of Israel shall do the Passover at its appointed
time.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_02.jpg){height="22" width="279"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:3*](#Contents.html_rnum9_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^On the fourteenth day in this month 'between the two evenings' you
shall do it at its appointed time. You shall do it according to all of
its law and according to all of its judgments."

![image](images/Num_Chap09_03.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:3. **between the two evenings**. Understood to mean the early evening
hours: "dusk" or (The Hebrew has the dual ending, which produces the
meaning of being between two evenings.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:4*](#Contents.html_rnum9_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Moses had spoken to the children of Israel to do the Passover,

![image](images/Num_Chap09_04.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:5*](#Contents.html_rnum9_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^and they had done the Passover in the first month on the fourteenth
day of the month "between the two evenings" in the wilderness of Sinai.
According to all that YHWH had commanded Moses, so the children of
Israel had done.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_05.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:6*](#Contents.html_rnum9_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And there had been people who were impure by a human being and so had
not been able to do the Passover on that day, and they had come forward
in front of Moses and in front of Aaron on that day,

![image](images/Num_Chap09_06.jpg){height="92" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:6. **impure by a human being**. See comment on Num 5:2.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:7*](#Contents.html_rnum9_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^and those people had said to him, "We're impure by a human being. Why
should we be excluded so as not to make YHWH's offering at its appointed
time among the children of Israel?"

![image](images/Num_Chap09_07.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:8*](#Contents.html_rnum9_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Moses had said, "Stay, and let me hear what YHWH will command
about you."

![image](images/Num_Chap09_08.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:9*](#Contents.html_rnum9_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And YHWH had spoken to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap09_09.jpg){height="22" width="213"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:10*](#Contents.html_rnum9_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^"Speak to the children of Israel, saying: Any man who will be impure
by a person or on a faraway journey---among you or through your
generations---and will do the Passover for YHWH,

![image](images/Num_Chap09_10.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:11*](#Contents.html_rnum9_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^they shall do it in the second month on the fourteenth day 'between
the two evenings.' They shall eat it with unleavened bread and bitter
herbs.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_11.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:12*](#Contents.html_rnum9_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^They shall not leave any of it until morning, and they shall not
break a bone of it. They shall do it according to all of the law of the
Passover.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_12.jpg){height="50" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:13*](#Contents.html_rnum9_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^But a man who is pure and has not been on a journey and has failed
to do the Passover: that person will be cut off from his people, because
he did not make YHWH's offering at its appointed time. That man shall
bear his sin.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_13.jpg){height="95" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:13. **cut off from his people**. On the meaning of this phrase, see
the comment on Gen 17:14.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:14*](#Contents.html_rnum9_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And if an alien will reside with you and will do the Passover for
YHWH: according to the law of the Passover and according to its required
manner, so he shall do it. You shall have one law, for the alien and for
the citizen of the land."

![image](images/Num_Chap09_14.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:15*](#Contents.html_rnum9_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And on the day that the Tabernacle was set up, the cloud covered the
Tabernacle of the tent of the Testimony, and in the evening it would be
over the Tabernacle like the appearance of fire until morning.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_15.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:15. **the tent of the Testimony**. This is the first occurrence of
this phrase. The usual phrase until now has been "Tent of Meeting." The
phrase here may not refer to the entire Tent of Meeting, but rather to
the inner pavilion, the *p![Image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11"
width="8"}r![Image](images/o.jpg){height="11" width="7"}ket,* which is
associated with the Testimony (the tablets of the Ten Commandments) four
times (Exod 27:21; 30:6; Lev 24:3; Num 4:5) and is called explicitly
"the pavilion of the Testimony" (Lev 24:3).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:16*](#Contents.html_rnum9_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^So it would be always: the cloud would cover it and the appearance
of fire at night.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_16.jpg){height="22" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:17*](#Contents.html_rnum9_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And according to when the cloud was lifted from over the tent, after
that the children of Israel would travel. And in a place where the cloud
would tent, the children of Israel would camp there.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_17.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:17. **the cloud would tent**. Forms of this Hebrew verb
(*yišk![Image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11" width="8"}n*) are
frequently taken to mean "to dwell"; but, when it refers to God or to
the cloud, this verb is a denominative from the noun
*mišk![Image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11" width="8"}n:* Tabernacle.
The deity cannot be pictured as *residing* on the earth, but God rather
is understood to *tent* among humans, that is, to be present in
association with an impermanent, movable structure. For references, see
the comment on Exod 25:8.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:18*](#Contents.html_rnum9_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^By YHWH's word the children of Israel would travel, and by YHWH's
word they would camp. All the days that the cloud would tent over the
Tabernacle they would camp.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_18.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:19*](#Contents.html_rnum9_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And when the cloud would extend many days over the Tabernacle, then
the children of Israel kept YHWH's charge and would not travel.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_19.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:20*](#Contents.html_rnum9_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And there were times that the cloud would be over the Tabernacle for
a number of days: by YHWH's word they would camp, and by YHWH's word
they would travel.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_20.jpg){height="50" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:21*](#Contents.html_rnum9_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And there were times that the cloud would be there from evening
until morning, and the cloud was lifted in the morning, and then they
would travel; or for a day and a night, and the cloud was lifted, and
then they traveled.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_21.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:22*](#Contents.html_rnum9_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^Whether two days or a month or a year, when the cloud would extend
long over the Tabernacle, to tent over it, the children of Israel would
camp and would not travel, and when it was lifted they would travel.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_22.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 9:23*](#Contents.html_rnum9_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^By YHWH's word they would camp, and by YHWH's word they would
travel. They kept YHWH's charge, by YHWH's word through Moses' hand.

![image](images/Num_Chap09_23.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers10.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:1*](#Contents.html_rnum10_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **10** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap10_01.jpg){height="22" width="235"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:2*](#Contents.html_rnum10_2)

^2^"Make two trumpets of silver. You shall make them hammered work. And
you shall have them for calling the congregation and for having the
camps travel.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![image](images/Num_Chap10_02.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:3*](#Contents.html_rnum10_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And when they will blow them, then all the congregation shall be
gathered to you, to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_03.jpg){height="50" width="366"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:4*](#Contents.html_rnum10_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And if they will blow with one, then the chieftains, the heads of
thousands of Israel shall be gathered to you.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_04.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:5*](#Contents.html_rnum10_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And when you will blow a blasting sound, then the camps that are
camping on the east shall travel.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_05.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:5. **blasting**. We do not know what sort of trumpet sound this was.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:6*](#Contents.html_rnum10_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And when you will blow a second blasting sound, then the camps that
are camping on the west shall travel. They shall blow a blasting sound
for their travels.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_06.jpg){height="46" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:7*](#Contents.html_rnum10_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And for assembling the community you shall blow, but you shall not
make a blast.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_07.jpg){height="22" width="294"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:8*](#Contents.html_rnum10_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow the trumpets; and they
shall be an eternal law for you through your generations.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_08.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:9*](#Contents.html_rnum10_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And when you will go to war in your land against a foe who afflicts
you, then you shall blast the trumpets, and you will be brought to mind
in front of YHWH, your God, and you will be saved from your enemies.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_09.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:10*](#Contents.html_rnum10_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And on your happy occasion and at your appointed times and on your
new moons, you shall blow the trumpets over your burnt offerings and
over your peace-offering sacrifices, and they shall be for a
commemoration for you in front of your God. I am YHWH, your God."

![image](images/Num_Chap10_10.jpg){height="94" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:11*](#Contents.html_rnum10_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And it was in the second year, in the second month, on the twentieth
of the month that the cloud was lifted from over the Tabernacle of the
Testimony.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_11.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:12*](#Contents.html_rnum10_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And the children of Israel set out on their travels from the
wilderness of Sinai. And the cloud tented in the wilderness of Paran.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_12.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:12. **the cloud tented**. See the comment on 9:17.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:13*](#Contents.html_rnum10_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And they traveled from the first by YHWH's word through Moses' hand.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_13.jpg){height="44" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:14*](#Contents.html_rnum10_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And the flag of the camp of the children of Judah traveled first by
their armies, and over its army was Nahshon son of Amminadab.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_14.jpg){height="69" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:15*](#Contents.html_rnum10_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And over the army of the tribe of the children of Issachar was
Nethanel son of Zuar.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_15.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:16*](#Contents.html_rnum10_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And over the army of the tribe of the children of Zebulun was Eliab
son of Helon.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_16.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:17*](#Contents.html_rnum10_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And the Tabernacle was taken down; and the children of Gershon and
the children of Merari, who carried the Tabernacle, traveled.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_17.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:18*](#Contents.html_rnum10_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And the flag of Reuben's camp by their armies traveled, and over its
army was Elizur son of Shedeur.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_18.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:19*](#Contents.html_rnum10_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And over the army of the tribe of the children of Simeon was
Shelumiel son of Zurishadday.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_19.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:20*](#Contents.html_rnum10_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And over the army of the tribe of the children of Gad was Eliasaph
son of Deuel.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_20.jpg){height="22" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:21*](#Contents.html_rnum10_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And the Kohathites, who carried the holy place, traveled; and they
set up the Tabernacle by the time they came.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_21.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:21. **holy place**. The Kohathites' charge was the sacred objects
that went inside the holy place (the inte[]{#Numbers10.html_page_34}rior
of the Tabernacle), namely: the ark, the table, the menorah, and the
altars and the equipment of the Holy (Num 3:31).

10:21. **they set up the Tabernacle by the time they came**. The
Gershonite and Merarite priests traveled first and set up the Tabernacle
so that it was ready when the Kohathite priests arrived with the ark,
table, menorah, altars, and equipment.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:22*](#Contents.html_rnum10_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And the flag of the camp of the children of Ephraim by their armies
traveled, and over its army was Elishama son of Ammihud.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_22.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:23*](#Contents.html_rnum10_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And over the army of the tribe of the children of Manasseh was
Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.

[*Numbers 10:31*](#Contents.html_rnum10_31)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[*Numbers 10:32*](#Contents.html_rnum10_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And it will be, when you go with us, that we'll do good for you in

![image](images/Num_Chap10_32.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:33*](#Contents.html_rnum10_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And they traveled from the mountain of YHWH three days' journey. And
the ark of the covenant of YHWH traveled in front of them three days'
journey to scout a resting place for them.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_33.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:34*](#Contents.html_rnum10_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And YHWH's cloud was over them by day as they traveled from the
camp.

![image](images/Num_Chap10_34.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:35*](#Contents.html_rnum10_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And it was, when the ark traveled, that Moses said, "Arise, YHWH,
and let your enemies be scattered, and let those who hate you flee from
your presence,"

![image](images/Num_Chap10_35.jpg){height="69" width="309"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:35. **let your enemies be scattered**. Moses' ceremonial words that
are associated with the respective lifting and resting of the ark have
been taken as poetry in several scholarly treatments of Numbers:

And it was, when the ark traveled, that Moses said, "Arise, YHWH, and
let your enemies be scattered, and let those who hate you flee from your
presence,"

Many identify this as poetry without any explanation. One elaborates
that it is "4:4 meter." Given the complexities of speaking of meter in
biblical Hebrew poetry, and given that the lines in question here do not
add up to 4:4 by any metrical reckoning in any case, we are better
advised to be cautious of calling these lines poetry. Presumably the
element that has led some to assume that this is poetry is the parallel
of scattered enemies and fleeing adversaries, but simple parallels
within a single verse such as this may be found in both prose and poetry
in the Hebrew Bible. Some scholars have more circumspectly spoken of
these lines simply as "sayings."

The significance of this point is that it directs us to recognize the
effect of the late introduction of short poems further on in the book
(in Numbers 21--24). More broadly, it should serve as a methodological
caution in our identification of verses as poetry in the Hebrew Bible.
As a matter of method, it is precarious to call a single verse in a
prose context poetry. Biblical poetry rarely rhymes and has no obvious
meter. Its best-known feature is the use of parallels ("Hear YHWH's
word, rulers of Sodom. / Listen to our God's teaching, people of
Gomorrah," Isa 1:10), and it is also frequently characterized by puns
and alliteration. But parallels, puns, and alliteration are all to be
found in biblical prose as well. Without entering into a tedious
discussion of the respective definitions of prose and poetry in the
Bible, we can say at this point that in almost any passage that is more
than a few verses long there will be little scholarly disagreement over
the simple identification of the passage as prose or poetry. But making
a judgment of a single verse in the middle of a narrative and calling it
poetry---and sometimes even proceeding thereafter to analyze the
author's use of these poetic lines in prose contexts as an artistic
device---is a delicate and subjective practice. This is not to say that
there are no cases of single lines of poetry embedded in biblical prose.
It is rather a call for recognition, as a matter of method, that
whenever one identifies a verse as poetry one must feel obliged to
support that claim with analysis and evidence. In the case of this
passage, if I were to make such a case, it would be based on the absence
of prose particles (the definite article, the direct object marker
*'et*, the relative pronoun *'ašer*). But my point is not to enter into
this technical matter here, but rather to make the larger point about
poetry in the Torah in general.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 10:36*](#Contents.html_rnum10_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^and when it rested, he said, "Come back, YHWH, the ten thousands of
thousands of Israel."

![image](images/Num_Chap10_36.jpg){height="45" width="297"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers11.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:1*](#Contents.html_rnum11_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **11** {.h2a}

^1^And the people were like grumblers, bad in YHWH's ears, and YHWH
heard, and His anger flared, and a fire of YHWH burned among them and
consumed at the edge of the camp.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_01.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:1. **the people were like grumblers**. This is just the first of many
accounts of their complaining in Numbers, and, interestingly, it is the
only one in which the reason for their dissatisfaction is not told. The
story's function, therefore, is not to point to any of the obstacles in
the wilderness but rather to indicate the people's state of mind:
negative, volatile, unconfident.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:2*](#Contents.html_rnum11_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And the people cried out to Moses, and Moses prayed to YHWH, and the
fire subsided.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_02.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:2. **the people cried out to Moses**. The people appeal to Moses
rather than to God, and Moses acts as intercessor. They still turn to
the human leader rather than directly to the mysterious, invisible
divine source.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:3*](#Contents.html_rnum11_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And he called that place's name Taberah because a fire of YHWH burned
among them.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_03.jpg){height="78" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:4*](#Contents.html_rnum11_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And the gathered mass who were among them had a longing, and the
children of Israel, as well, went back and cried, and they said, "Who
will feed us meat?

![image](images/Num_Chap11_04.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:4. **the gathered mass**. See the comment on Num 12:2.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:5*](#Contents.html_rnum11_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^We remember the fish that we would eat in Egypt for free: the
cucumbers and the melons and the leek and the onions and the garlics.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_05.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:6*](#Contents.html_rnum11_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And now, our soul is dried up. There isn't anything---except the
manna before our eyes."

![image](images/Num_Chap11_06.jpg){height="74" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:7*](#Contents.html_rnum11_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And the manna: it was like a seed of coriander, and its appearance
was like the appearance of bdellium.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:8*](#Contents.html_rnum11_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^The people went around and collected and ground it in mills or
pounded it in a mortar and cooked it in a pot and made it into cakes.
And its taste was like the taste of something creamy made with oil.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_08.jpg){height="80" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:9*](#Contents.html_rnum11_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And when the dew descended on the camp at night, the manna would
descend on it.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_09.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:10*](#Contents.html_rnum11_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Moses heard the people crying by their families, each at his
tent entrance, and YHWH's anger flared very much, and it was bad in
Moses' eyes.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_10.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:11*](#Contents.html_rnum11_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Moses said to YHWH, "Why have you done bad to your servant, and
why have I not found favor in your eyes, to set the burden of this
entire people on me?

![image](images/Num_Chap11_11.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:11. **Why have you done bad to your servant**. Is this a way to talk
to God?! In what is the most extraordinary speech spoken by a human to
God in the Torah, Moses begins with these words and ends with: "And if
this is how you treat me, *kill me!*" We have seen a dramatic change in
the way humans speak to God: Adam and Cain speak like children caught in
a naughty act. Abraham dares to question a divine decision (the
destruction of Sodom) and says, "Far be it from you to do a thing like
this\.... Will the judge of all the earth not do justice?" (Gen 18:25).
Moses pleads for the people after the golden calf event and says that if
God will not forgive them, then "wipe me out from your scroll that
you've written" (Exod 32:32). And now Moses, pleading for himself,
speaks to the creator in a way that we would never imagine Adam or
Abraham or Jacob or even Moses himself speaking until now. It can only
be understood in the light of everything that has happened up to this
moment: God's speaking to Moses in a personal way from the beginning
("I'm your father's God"), Moses' spending long hours in conversation
with God, Moses' performance of the miracles. The same Moses whose first
reaction to the deity was to "hide his face, because he was afraid of
looking at God" (Exod 3:6) now dares to speak forcefully. It is the
opposite of when an atheist uses some audacious or outrageous language
about gods. Moses is pictured as the human who has come the closest to
God, the one person who has seen God, and it is precisely Moses who
speaks the most audaciously of all. And Moses in turn thus epitomizes
humankind: it is precisely the wilderness generation, the generation
that is closest to God, who hear God's voice and see miracles daily, who
are the most rebellious generation in the Bible. This in turn may remind
us of the Bible's first story: when humans live in the garden where God
walks, they make the initial act of rebellion. An ongoing point of the
Torah is thus that humans and their creator have an underlying basis of
conflict, something in the very nature of the divine-human condition.
And the relations between God and Abraham's descendants are aimed at
eventually resolving this. Thus Moses is not seen as bad for speaking
this way to God, and he is not punished. It is rather part of his close
relationship with God, and part of the Torah's central story.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:12*](#Contents.html_rnum11_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^Did *I* conceive this entire people? Did *I* give birth to it, that
you should say to me, 'Carry it in your bosom,' the way a nurse carries
a suckling, to the land that you swore to its fathers?

![image](images/Num_Chap11_12.jpg){height="101" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:12. **by myself, to carry**. Moses complains that he has to bear the
burden of the people alone. This is in explicit contrast to the report
about Jethro's advice to Moses in Exod 18:22, in which Jethro tells him
to pick judges to help him with the weight of his task, "and they'll
bear it with you." Moses takes Jethro's advice there and appoints judges
to help him, so why does he say now that he is carrying the burden
alone? Notably, that earlier matter did not come from God, and the text
never says that God sanctioned it. It was specifically Moses' own
arrangement, on the advice of his father-in-law. He still complains now
to God that God has left so much to him.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:13*](#Contents.html_rnum11_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^From where do I have meat to give to this entire people, that they
cry at me, saying, 'Give us meat, and let's eat'?

![image](images/Num_Chap11_13.jpg){height="44" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:14*](#Contents.html_rnum11_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^I'm not able, I, by myself, to carry this entire people, because
it's too heavy for me.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_14.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:14. **by myself, to carry**. Moses complains that he has to bear the
burden of the people alone. This is in explicit contrast to the report
about Jethro's advice to Moses in Exod 18:22, in which Jethro tells him
to pick judges to help him with the weight of his task, "and they'll
bear it with you." Moses takes Jethro's advice there and appoints judges
to help him, so why does he say now that he is carrying the burden
alone? Notably, that earlier matter did not come from God, and the text
never says that God sanctioned it. It was specifically Moses' own
arrangement, on the advice of his father-in-law. He still complains now
to God that God has left so much to him.

11:14. **too heavy for me**. The recurring adjective "heavy," which
persisted through the exodus account and culminated with the heavy cloud
and the divine glory (*k![image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11"
width="8"}bôd*), now returns, with Moses using it to describe the
impossible burden on him, just as Jethro had told Moses earlier (Exod
18:18). Note the parallels in Jethro's words there and Moses' words
here, which indicate that Moses' father-in-law indeed had much influence
on him. Moses' use of the term "heavy" now recalls the exodus, arguably
to remind God of Moses' past service.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:15*](#Contents.html_rnum11_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And if this is how you treat me, *kill me,* if I've found favor in
your eyes, and let me not see my suffering."

![image](images/Num_Chap11_15.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:16*](#Contents.html_rnum11_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And YHWH said to Moses, "Gather to me seventy men from Israel's
elders whom you've known because they're the people's elders and its
officers, and you'll take them to the Tent of Meeting, and they'll stand
up there with you.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_16.jpg){height="99" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:17*](#Contents.html_rnum11_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And I'll come down and speak with you there, and I'll take some of
the spirit that is on you, and I'll set it on them, and they'll carry
the burden of the people with you, and you won't bear it, you, by
yourself.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_17.jpg){height="75" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:18*](#Contents.html_rnum11_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And you shall say to the people, 'Consecrate yourselves for
tomorrow, and you'll eat meat, because you cried in YHWH's ears saying:
Who will feed us meat, because we had it good in Egypt. And YHWH will
give you meat, and you'll eat.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_18.jpg){height="98" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:19*](#Contents.html_rnum11_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^You'll eat not for one day and not two days and not five days and
not ten days and not ten days and not twenty days.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_19.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:20*](#Contents.html_rnum11_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^Until a month of days! Until it will come out of your nose, and it
will be a revulsion to you! []{#Numbers11.html_page_36}Because you
rejected YHWH, who was among you, and you cried in front of him, saying:
Why is this that we went out from Egypt?'"

![image](images/Num_Chap11_20.jpg){height="105" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:21*](#Contents.html_rnum11_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Moses said, "The people among whom I am are six hundred thousand
on foot, and you've said, 'I'll give them meat, and they'll eat for a
month of days!'

![image](images/Num_Chap11_21.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:22*](#Contents.html_rnum11_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^Will flock and herd be slaughtered for them, and would it provide
for them? Or will all the sea's fish be gathered for them, and would it
provide for them?"

![image](images/Num_Chap11_22.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:23*](#Contents.html_rnum11_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And YHWH said to Moses, "Is YHWH's hand getting short?! Now you'll
see if my word happens to you or not."

![image](images/Num_Chap11_23.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:24*](#Contents.html_rnum11_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And Moses went out and spoke YHWH's words to the people. And he
gathered seventy men from the people's elders, and he stood them around
the tent.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_24.jpg){height="100" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:25*](#Contents.html_rnum11_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And YHWH came down in a cloud and spoke to him and took some of the
spirit that was on him and put it on the seventy men of the elders. And
it was when the spirit rested on them: and they prophesied. Then they
did not do it anymore.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_25.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:25. **they prophesied**. What exactly do they do? Prophecy in the
Bible does not primarily involve prediction. It is not that they are
going about telling the future. What is happening that makes it obvious
to Moses, Joshua, and apparently everyone that they are doing something
that is associated with prophets? Some suggest that they are in some
sort of trance, but trance behavior is not generally part of what is
pictured in the fifteen books of the prophets in the *Tanak* or in the
cases of prophecy in the *Tanak*'s narrative books. What is typical of
prophecy, as opposed to historical narrative, in the Bible is that
*prophecy is in poetry* (or in combinations of poetry and prose).
Biblical prophecy has the characteristics of oral formulaic poetry. That
is, the prophet composes it on the spot, using lines that he or she has
already composed and memorized, and mixing these "formulas" with new
lines that occur to him or her spontaneously. When such poems occurred
to the ancient poets, the poets must have truly felt inspired. And the
people who watched and listened to them must have perceived them to be
inspired as well. When a man *spontaneously* says:

They'll beat their swords into plowshares

and their spears into pruning hooks.

A nation won't lift a sword against a nation,

and they won't learn war anymore.

we can easily imagine him and his audience feeling that it is God moving
through him. Further evidence that they are doing something musical or
poetic comes from the other most famous biblical instance of such group
prophecy: King Saul joins in a group of prophets, and he prophesies with
them, which astounds everyone. And in that case the text notes that
these prophets are accompanied by musical instruments (1 Sam 10:5).

See also the story of Aaron's and Miriam's challenge to Moses in the
coming chapter. They have experienced prophecy, and they say, "Has YHWH
only just spoken through Moses? Hasn't He also spoken through us?" God
responds with a declaration about the nature of prophecy, saying that
Moses' experience is different from all others, and the important thing
to note here is that the words of God are in poetry (see Num 12:6--8).

Some think that biblical prophecy involved some sort of ecstatic state.
That is possible, but it may be more of an *inspired* state, both in an
Isaiah and in a case like Saul's or the seventy elders here, in which
one who does not normally speak in poetry is now moved and enabled to do
so.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:26*](#Contents.html_rnum11_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And two men had been left in the camp. One's name was Eldad, and the
second's name was Medad. And the spirit rested on them. And they were
among the ones who were written, but they did not go out to the tent,
[]{#Numbers11.html_page_37}and they prophesied in the camp.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_26.jpg){height="73" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:26. **they were among the ones who were written**. Meaning: they were
among the seventy who were written about in the preceding verse.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:27*](#Contents.html_rnum11_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And a boy ran and told Moses, and he said, "Eldad and Medad are
prophesying in the camp!"

![image](images/Num_Chap11_27.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:28*](#Contents.html_rnum11_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And Joshua, son of Nun, Moses' attendant from his chosen ones,
answered, and he said, "My lord Moses, restrain them!"

![image](images/Num_Chap11_28.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:28. **his chosen ones**. The seventy whom Moses had chosen earlier on
Jethro's advice (Exod 18:25). The Aramaic translation (Targum Onkelos)
and others read different vowels here and understand this to say: "from
his youth." The Greek (Septuagint) takes it as "chosen," as does Ibn
Ezra. The explicit reference to Moses' "choosing" a group of seventy in
the Jethro episode seems to me to make this the more probable reading
here. It also makes Joshua's concern now more intriguing, as he
questions the behavior of members of the second group of seventy. They
go beyond what the seventy whom Moses himself had chosen had done, and
he wants them to stop. But Moses says, Don't be jealous for me.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:29*](#Contents.html_rnum11_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And Moses said to him, "Are you jealous for me? And who would make
it so that *all* of YHWH's people were prophets, that YHWH would put His
spirit on them!"

![image](images/Num_Chap11_29.jpg){height="73" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:29. **who would make it so**. Literally, "who would give" or "who
would put it." Meaning: "if only it were so" or "I wish it were so."
This is the only time that Moses uses this expression. The people use it
once (Exod 16:3); it occurs once as part of a curse (Deut 28:67); and,
most extraordinarily, Moses reports that God used it after the
revelation at Sinai (Deut 5:29).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:30*](#Contents.html_rnum11_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And Moses was gathered back to the camp, he and Israel's elders.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_30.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:31*](#Contents.html_rnum11_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And a wind traveled from YHWH and transported quails from the sea
and left them on the camp, about a day's journey in one direction and
about a day's journey in the other around the camp, and about two cubits
on the face of the earth.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_31.jpg){height="103" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:31. **a wind from YHWH**. The wording plays exquisitely on the double
meaning of the Hebrew word *rûa![image](images/hdot.jpg){height="15"
width="7"},* which means both spirit and wind. (See the comment on Gen
1:2.) Moses has just said, "If only YHWH would put His *spirit*" on all
the people; and now YHWH brings His *wind,* carrying food for the
people. It appears that, in the divine wisdom, the provision of
sustenance is of more immediate importance than the bestowing of the
spirit. It reminds one of the later rabbinic expression
![image](images/inl-page90.jpg){height="25" width="122"} (*'ên
qema![image](images/hdot.jpg){height="15" width="7"} 'ên
tôr![Image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11" width="8"}h;* No flour, no
Torah!).

11:31. **two cubits on the face of the earth**. Piled three feet high.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:32*](#Contents.html_rnum11_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And the people got up all that day and all night and all the next
day and gathered the quail. The one who had the least gathered ten
homers. And they spread them out, spreading around the camp.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_32.jpg){height="97" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:34. **Kibroth Hattaavah**. Meaning "Graves of Longing."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:33*](#Contents.html_rnum11_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And the meat was still between their teeth, not yet chewed, and
YHWH's anger flared at the people, and YHWH struck at the people, a very
great strike.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_33.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:34*](#Contents.html_rnum11_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And he called that place's name Kibroth Hattaavah, because they
buried the people who were longing there.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_34.jpg){height="45" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 11:35*](#Contents.html_rnum11_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^From Kibroth Hattaavah the people traveled to Hazeroth, and they
were in Hazeroth.

![image](images/Num_Chap11_35.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers12.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:1*](#Contents.html_rnum12_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **12** {.h2a}

^1^And Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses about the Cushite wife whom
he had taken---because he had taken a Cushite wife.

![image](images/Num_Chap12_01.jpg){height="51" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:1. **the Cushite wife**. Cush is usually understood to be Ethiopia.
Its people are identified as descendants of Noah's son Ham (Gen
10:6--7). On this understanding, Moses has taken an Ethiopian wife in
addition to his first, Midianite wife, Zipporah. This has been confused
somewhat by the fact that the prophet Habakkuk refers to a place called
Cushan in parallel with Midian (Hab 3:7). Some scholars, therefore, have
concluded that the Cushite wife is Zipporah herself (so Ibn Ezra). But
this latter view does not explain why the text should suddenly refer to
her here as a Cushite. Also, the words "because he had taken a Cushite
wife" certainly appear to be here in the verse in order to inform us of
an essential *new* fact, but we already knew that he was married to
Zipporah (so Rashbam). This story, therefore, is about a second,
probably Ethiopian wife.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:2*](#Contents.html_rnum12_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And they said, "Has YHWH only just spoken through Moses? Hasn't He
also spoken through us?"

And YHWH heard.

![image](images/Num_Chap12_02.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:2. **only just spoken**. Aaron and Miriam say, "Has YHWH only just
spoken through Moses?" "Only just" sounds redundant. The redundant word
"only," though, is Hebrew *raq,* and the story concludes with YHWH
demanding a measure of punishment of Miriam comparable to the shame she
would have experienced if her father "had *spit* in her face," which is
Hebrew *y![image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11"
width="8"}r![image](images/o.jpg){height="11" width="7"}q
y![image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11" width="8"}raq* (12:14), an
intensive form that doubles the verb---and doubly emphasizes the pun.
And the pun confirms that Miriam's punishment is meant to fit the crime:
see the comment on v. 10.

One might regard this last pun as a relatively minor item of wordplay,
but it is in fact part of an elaborate chain of puns that are stitched
through this section of Numbers. The chain encompasses this story and
the one that precedes it, the story of Moses' anguished complaint and
the appointment of the seventy elders to share his load. The first word
of that story is the extremely unusual and uncertain term *'sapsup*
(spelled with an unvocalized *aleph* at the beginning because it is
preceded by the definite article), meaning a "gathered mass"
(classically translated as a "mixed multitude") among the Israelites.
The root letters of this strange word (*'SP*) then resurface in a
showcase of forms through this story and the next one:

![image](images/page89_1.jpg){height="405" width="420"}

Through fourteen variations on the root letters, we are brought from the
crisis that is generated by the "gathered mass" to resolution when
Miriam is "gathered back" (*h![image](images/e1.jpg){height="11"
width="7"}'![image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11"
width="8"}s![image](images/e1.jpg){height="11" width="7"}p*). Moreover,
this chain of paronomasia may serve to stitch these two stories more
tightly together and thus suggest a relationship between the seventy
elders' prophesying in Numbers 11 and Aaron's and Miriam's complaint in
Numbers 12 ("Has YHWH only just spoken through Moses?").

12:2. **they said, "Has YHWH only just spoken through Moses?"** What
does this complaint have to do with Moses' wife?! Answer: nothing. Like
someone who has had a bad day and comes home and complains about dinner,
Aaron and Miriam do not voice what is really bothering them. They
redirect their annoyance at Moses himself. Aaron and Miriam have been
identified as prophets (Exod 7:1; 15:20). God has spoken to Aaron, and
Aaron was part of the group that had a vision of God on Sinai (Exodus
24). And so they question Moses' position: is he the only one close to
God? It is common for people to redirect anger, but it has risks in
every case, especially when it occurs within a family. This episode
dramatizes that danger: Miriam and Aaron bring God into what was a
family dispute. This is a mistake! God appears, and they suffer.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:3*](#Contents.html_rnum12_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And the man Moses was very humble, more than every human who was on
the face of the earth.

![image](images/Num_Chap12_03.jpg){height="47" width="340"}

![image](images/Num_Chap12_03a.jpg){height="26" width="71"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:3. **humble, more than every human who was on the face of the
earth**. Is this the humblest man on earth? But he is the man who has
just said things to *God* like "Why have you done bad to your servant?"
and "If this is how you treat me, *kill me!*" We might say that his
closeness to God enables him to speak this way to the deity while still
being humble when dealing with other human beings. But then how are we
to account for the way he spoke to the Pharaoh before the last plague in
Egypt: "All these servants of yours will come down to *me,* and they'll
bow to *me*" (Exod 11:8)? Perhaps it is precisely his experiences in
Egypt and at Sinai that have made him *now* the humblest of humans.
Having seen the extent of God's power, he feels humble. Having seen his
inability to handle the people and their complaints on his own, he feels
humbler still. Thus, even when he speaks to God with his audacious
closeness, he says, "I'm not able, I, by myself, to carry this entire
people, because it's too heavy for me" (11:14).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:4*](#Contents.html_rnum12_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And YHWH said suddenly to Moses and to Aaron and to Miriam, "Go out,
the three of you, to the Tent of Meeting." And the three of them went
out.

![image](images/Num_Chap12_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:5*](#Contents.html_rnum12_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And YHWH came down in a column of cloud and stood at the entrance of
the tent. And He called, "Aaron and Miriam." And the two of them went
out.

![image](images/Num_Chap12_05.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:6*](#Contents.html_rnum12_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And He said, "Hear my words:

::: hanging
If there will be a prophet among you,

I, YHWH, shall be known to him in a vision;

in a dream I shall speak through him.
:::

![image](images/Num_Chap12_06.jpg){height="69" width="272"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:6. **in a vision; in a dream**. All prophetic experience in the
*Tanak* is understood to be through visions and dreams---except Moses'.
The fifteen books of the Hebrew Bible that are named for prophets either
identify the prophets' experiences as visions or else leave the form of
the experiences undescribed (Ezek 12:27; 40:2; Hos 12:11; Hab 2:2; Mic
3:6). Many begin by identifying the book's contents as the prophet's
vision: "The vision of Isaiah" (Isa 1:1; cf. 2 Chr 32:32); "The vision
of Obadiah" (Oba 1); "The book of the vision of Nahum" (Nah 1:1); "The
words of Amos ... which he envisioned" (Amos 1:1); "The word of YHWH
that came to Micah ... which he envisioned" (Mic 1:1); "The oracle that
Habakkuk the prophet envisioned" (Hab 1:1).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:7*](#Contents.html_rnum12_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^Not so is my servant Moses;

in all my house he is faithful.

![image](images/Num_Chap12_07.jpg){height="22" width="284"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:7. **Not so is my servant Moses**. Some experiences are impressive or
inspiring or powerful in themselves; and some experiences change a
person's life ever after. The moment at which Moses sees the form of God
from the back on Sinai separates him from all other prophets (Exod
33:21--23). It is understood that no other prophet has a standing that
is equal to that of Moses.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:8*](#Contents.html_rnum12_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^Mouth to mouth I shall speak through him

and vision and not in enigmas,

and he will see the form of YHWH.

And why did you not fear to speak against my servant,

against Moses?"

![image](images/Num_Chap12_08.jpg){height="70" width="294"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:9*](#Contents.html_rnum12_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And YHWH's anger flared against them, and He went.

![image](images/Num_Chap12_09.jpg){height="25" width="199"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:10*](#Contents.html_rnum12_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And the cloud turned from over the tent; and, here, Miriam was
