leprous, like snow. And Aaron turned to Miriam, and, here, she was
leprous.

![image](images/Num_Chap12_10.jpg){height="79" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:10. **leprous, like snow**. Miriam is left with a kind of leprosy in
which her flesh is devoid of pigment. If we understand correctly that
the Cushite wife is Ethiopian, then this is a provocative case of
punishment to suit the crime and a powerful biblical statement regarding
racism. It is []{#Numbers12.html_page_45}as if God says to Miriam: "You
don't like a woman with dark pigmentation? Then you don't have to have
*any* pigmentation!" Or: "You don't like a woman who is black? Try being
completely white!"

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:11*](#Contents.html_rnum12_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Aaron said to Moses, "In me, my lord. Don't set a sin on us,
which we did foolishly and which we sinned.

![image](images/Num_Chap12_11.jpg){height="80" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:11. **my lord**. Aaron addressed Moses as "my lord" one other time:
in the golden calf episode. On each of the two occasions on which he has
acted inappropriately toward Moses, he has ended up having to
acknowledge Moses' status humbly and politely.

12:11. **Don't set a sin on us**. Aaron acknowledges that both he and
Miriam are guilty. He says, "Don't set a sin on *us*, which *we* did
foolishly and which *we* sinned." Why is there no consequence here for
Aaron? Why does Miriam suffer while Aaron does not? As I commented in
the case of Aaron's making the golden calf, the answer may be that Aaron
is the high priest. In that position he cannot suffer any direct
punishment from God, which would both disqualify him and demean the
office of high priest. And so what is left is for Aaron to make amends
through personal atonement. What is impressive here is that he frames
his atonement entirely in terms of Miriam. He acknowledges his own equal
culpability, and he pleads with Moses to intercede for his sister.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:12*](#Contents.html_rnum12_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^Let her not be like the dead who, when he comes out of his mother's
womb, half of his flesh is eaten up!"

![image](images/Num_Chap12_12.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:13*](#Contents.html_rnum12_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^[]{#Numbers12.html_page_40}13^And Moses cried out to YHWH, saying, "Oh,
God, heal her!"

![image](images/Num_Chap12_13.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:13. **Oh, God, heal her**! The untranslatable Hebrew
*n![image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11" width="8"}'* particle occurs
twice in Moses' words, conveying that this is a fervent plea, something
like: "God, please, heal her, please!"

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:14*](#Contents.html_rnum12_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And YHWH said to Moses, "And if her father had *spit* in her face,
wouldn't she be humiliated seven days? Let her be closed up seven days
outside the camp, and after that let her be gathered back."

![image](images/Num_Chap12_14.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:15*](#Contents.html_rnum12_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Miriam was closed up outside the camp seven days. And the people
did not travel until Miriam was gathered back, and after that the people
traveled from Hazeroth. And they camped in the Paran wilderness.

![image](images/Num_Chap12_15.jpg){height="70" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 12:16*](#Contents.html_rnum12_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![image](images/Num_Chap12_16.jpg){height="45" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers13.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:1*](#Contents.html_rnum13_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## S[**END**]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **13** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap13.jpg){height="34" width="93"}

![image](images/Num_Chap13_01.jpg){height="22" width="236"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:2*](#Contents.html_rnum13_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Send men and let them scout the land of Canaan that I'm giving to
the children of Israel. You shall send one man for each tribe of his
fathers, every one of them a chieftain."

![image](images/Num_Chap13_02.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:2. **send one man for each tribe**. One scout is named for each tribe
*except* *Levi.* Why is the priestly tribe not represented? Perhaps no
one from Levi is sent to scout the territory because Levi is the one
tribe that will not be assigned any territory in the land. They derive
their support from the sacrifices, tithes, and donations of the other
tribes.

13:2. **every one of them a chieftain**. This story is commonly referred
to as the episode of the *spies,* but the men whom Moses sends are
tribal leaders, not ordinary scouts. The function of their mission is
not espionage but observation, to bring back a description of the land
to their people. They are leaders. That is why their negativity when
they return is so devastating. It is not just a disappointing bit of
intelligence. It is a failure to lead and encourage.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:3*](#Contents.html_rnum13_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Moses sent them from the wilderness of Paran by YHWH's word. They
were all men who were heads of the children of Israel.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_03.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:4*](#Contents.html_rnum13_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And these are their names: For the tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of
Zaccur.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_04.jpg){height="22" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:5*](#Contents.html_rnum13_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^For the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_05.jpg){height="49" width="221"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:6*](#Contents.html_rnum13_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^For the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_06.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:7*](#Contents.html_rnum13_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^For the tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_07.jpg){height="22" width="222"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:8*](#Contents.html_rnum13_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^For the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Nun.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_08.jpg){height="22" width="196"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:9*](#Contents.html_rnum13_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^For the tribe of Benjamin, Palti son of Raphu.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_09.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:10*](#Contents.html_rnum13_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^For the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel son of Sodi.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_10.jpg){height="22" width="253"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:11*](#Contents.html_rnum13_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^For the tribe of Joseph: of the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi son of
Susi.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_11.jpg){height="22" width="278"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:12*](#Contents.html_rnum13_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^For the tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_12.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:13*](#Contents.html_rnum13_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^For the tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_13.jpg){height="52" width="347"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:14*](#Contents.html_rnum13_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^For the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi son of Vophsi.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_14.jpg){height="23" width="214"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:15*](#Contents.html_rnum13_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^For the tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Machi.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_15.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:16*](#Contents.html_rnum13_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^These are the names of the men whom Moses sent to scout the land.
And Moses called Hoshea son of Nun Joshua.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_16.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:17*](#Contents.html_rnum13_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And Moses sent them to scout the land of Canaan. And he said to
them, "Go up there in the Negeb, and you shall go up the mountain

![image](images/Num_Chap13_17.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:18*](#Contents.html_rnum13_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^and see the land, how it is; and the people who live on it, are they
strong or weak, are they few or many;

![image](images/Num_Chap13_18.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:19*](#Contents.html_rnum13_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^and how is the land in which they live, is it good or bad; and how
are the cities in which they live, are they in camps or in fortified
places;

![image](images/Num_Chap13_19.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:20*](#Contents.html_rnum13_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^and how is the land, is it fat or meager; does it have trees or not;
and exert strength and take some fruit of the land."

And it was the days of the first grapes.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_20.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:21*](#Contents.html_rnum13_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And they went up and scouted the land from the wilderness of Zin to
Rehob at the entrance of Hamath.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_21.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:22*](#Contents.html_rnum13_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And they went up in the Negeb and came to Hebron. And Ahiman,
Sheshai, and Talmai, the offspring of the giants, were there. (And
Hebron was built seven years before Zoan of Egypt.)

![image](images/Num_Chap13_22.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:22. **came to Hebron**. They return to a place where Abraham lived
(Gen 13:18), where he met the three visitors and first questioned God
(Genesis 18; Mamre is at Hebron), and where the patriarchs and
matriarchs are buried (Gen 23:19). It is also the place from which Jacob
sent Joseph to find his brothers, which, in a sense, initiated the
sequence of events that led to Israel's being in Egypt. It is therefore,
meaningful in a variety of ways that the scouts are especially noted to
come to Hebron. It particularly symbolizes that this is a return home.
It might have been the perfect place to begin Israel's return to the
land, but the people's resistance will change all that.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:23*](#Contents.html_rnum13_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And they came to the Wadi Eshcol, and they cut a branch and one
cluster of grapes from there---and carried it on a pole by two
people---and some pomegranates and some figs.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_23.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:24*](#Contents.html_rnum13_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^That place was called Wadi Eshcol on account of the cluster that the
children of Israel cut from there.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_24.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:25*](#Contents.html_rnum13_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And they came back from scouting the land at the end of forty days.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_25.jpg){height="44" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:26*](#Contents.html_rnum13_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And they went and came to Moses and to Aaron and to all the
congregation of the children of Israel, to the wilderness of Paran, at
Kadesh; and they brought back word to them and all the congregation and
showed them the land's fruit.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_26.jpg){height="95" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:27*](#Contents.html_rnum13_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And they told him and said, "We came to the land where you sent us,
and also it's flowing with milk and honey, and this is its fruit.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_27.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:28*](#Contents.html_rnum13_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^Nonetheless: the people who live in the land are strong. And the
cities are fortified, very big. And also we saw the offspring of the
giants there.

![image](images/Num_Chap13_28.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:29*](#Contents.html_rnum13_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^Amalek lives in the land of the Negeb, and the Hittite and the
Jebusite and the Amorite live in the mountains, and the Canaanite lives
by the sea and along the Jordan."

![image](images/Num_Chap13_29.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:30*](#Contents.html_rnum13_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And Caleb quieted the people toward Moses and said, *"Let's go up,*
and we'll take possession of it, because *we'll be able* to handle it."

![image](images/Num_Chap13_30.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:31*](#Contents.html_rnum13_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And the men who went up with him said, "We won't be able to go up
against the people, because they're stronger than we are."

![image](images/Num_Chap13_31.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:32*](#Contents.html_rnum13_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And they brought out a report of the land that they had scouted to
the children of Israel, saying, "The land through which we passed to
scout it: it's a land that eats those who live in it, and all the people
whom we saw in it were people of *size!*

![image](images/Num_Chap13_32.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 13:33*](#Contents.html_rnum13_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And we saw the Nephilim there, sons of giants from the Nephilim, and
we were like grasshoppers in our eyes, and so were we in *their* eyes."

![image](images/Num_Chap13_33.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:33. **sons of giants from the Nephilim**. The huge creatures known as
the Nephilim come up in a chain of references that are spread far apart
through several books of the *Tanak.* Their origin is explained near the
beginning of the Torah, in the story of the "sons of God" who have
relations with human women who then give birth to giants (Gen 6:1--4).
Now in the spies episode, the Israelite spies see the descendants of
these giants (Numbers 13). Later, Joshua eliminates the giants from all
of the land except from the Philistine cities, including Gath (Josh
11:21--22). And later still, the Philistine giant Goliath comes from
*Gath* (1 Sam 17:4). These widespread references are among the many
stitches that bind the *Tanak* together as a continuing story. Those who
see the Bible as a loose collection of stories fail to take proper
account of the substantial number of such widely distributed binding
elements.

[]{#Numbers14.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:1*](#Contents.html_rnum14_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **14** {.h2a}

^1^And all the congregation raised and let out their voices! And the
people wept that night.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_01.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:2*](#Contents.html_rnum14_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And all the children of Israel complained at Moses and at Aaron, and
all the congregation said to them, "If only we had died in the land of
Egypt! Or in this wilderness, if only we had died!

![image](images/Num_Chap14_02.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:3*](#Contents.html_rnum14_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And why is YHWH bringing us to this land to fall by the sword? Our
wives and our infants will become a spoil! Isn't it better for us to go
back to Egypt?"

![image](images/Num_Chap14_03.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:4*](#Contents.html_rnum14_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And they said, each man to his brother, "Let's appoint a chief and go
back to Egypt."

![image](images/Num_Chap14_04.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:5*](#Contents.html_rnum14_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Moses and Aaron fell on their faces in front of all the community
of the congregation of the children of Israel.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_05.jpg){height="50" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:6*](#Contents.html_rnum14_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh, from those who had
scouted the land, had torn their clothes.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_06.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:7*](#Contents.html_rnum14_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And they said to all the congregation of the children of Israel,
saying, "The land through which we passed to scout it: the land is very,
very good!

![image](images/Num_Chap14_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:8*](#Contents.html_rnum14_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^If YHWH desires us, then He'll bring us to this land and give it to
us, a land that flows with milk and honey!

![image](images/Num_Chap14_08.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:9*](#Contents.html_rnum14_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^Just don't revolt against YHWH. And you, don't fear the people of the
land, because they're our bread! Their protection has turned from them,
while YHWH is with us. Don't fear them."

![image](images/Num_Chap14_09.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:10*](#Contents.html_rnum14_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And all the congregation said to batter them with stones.

And YHWH's glory appeared at the Tent of Meeting to all the children of
Israel!

![image](images/Num_Chap14_10.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:11*](#Contents.html_rnum14_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And YHWH said to Moses, "How long will this people reject me, and
how long will they not trust in me, with all the signs that I've done
among them?

![image](images/Num_Chap14_11.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:11. **how long will they not trust in me, with all the signs that
I've done among them?** Why in fact do the people not trust? Why are
they still afraid and doubtful? They have seen their God handle *Egypt,*
the major power of the world. Why would they doubt that He can handle
the Canaanites? They have seen Him manipulate the whole of nature with
the ten plagues and the splitting of the sea. They have seen water come
from a mountain crag. They have heard God's voice from the sky at Sinai.
They are eating miraculous manna every day. They have just miraculously
been provided with quail to eat. Even as they complain in this moment,
there is a miraculous column of cloud and fire right in front of them.
Why are they afraid? Recall that the reason they are directed to the Red
Sea when they leave Egypt, rather than through Philistine territory, is
that YHWH is concerned that they will be afraid of facing war with the
Philistines. As I commented there (Exod 13:18), the Red Sea required no
decisions or action on their part, but facing the Philistines would have
required them to take up arms. Likewise, in every case so far, from the
plagues to the quail, God and Moses have acted for them, and they have
had to do very little. Even in the battle against the Amalekites, it was
the Amalekites who attacked them, and then Joshua chose a select group
to fight on their behalf (Exod 17:8--13). But now, for the first time,
the people perceive that *they* will have to act. The
[]{#Numbers14.html_page_49}scouts speak in terms of the people of Israel
themselves having to fight: "*We* won't be able to go up against the
people, because they're stronger than *we* are" (13:31). The scouts do
not even mention God. What was true when they left Egypt is still true:
They are still slaves in their hearts. They are not yet capable of
independence, responsibility, or action. And so God's decision is
precisely to leave the entry into the land for their children. See the
comment on Num 14:29.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:12*](#Contents.html_rnum14_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^I'll strike them with an epidemic and dispossess them, and I'll make
you into a bigger and more powerful nation than they are."

![image](images/Num_Chap14_12.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:12. **I'll make you**. The Septuagint and Samaritan texts have "I'll
make you *and your father's house.*" The extra words appear to have been
lost by haplography. (That is, in the Hebrew, the word for "make you"
and the phrase for "your father's house" both end in the same letter,
*kaf,* so a scribe's eye skipped from one *kap* to the next and left out
the words in between.) The addition of the reference to Moses' father's
house may suggest that Aaron's family is to be spared as well, or it may
mean more broadly that the Levites as a whole are to survive.
Alternatively, it may still mean that only Moses is to survive to start
over a new people, but it is still described as the survival of his
father's house because God's relationship with Moses has focused on that
link from the beginning---God's self-introduction to Moses at the bush
is: "I am your father's God" (Exod 3:6).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:13*](#Contents.html_rnum14_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Moses said to YHWH, "And Egypt will hear it, for you brought
this people up from among them with your power,

![image](images/Num_Chap14_13.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:14*](#Contents.html_rnum14_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^and they'll say it to those who live in this land. They've heard
that you, YHWH, are among this people; that you, YHWH, have appeared
eye-to-eye; and your cloud stands over them; and you go in front of them
in a column of cloud by day and in a column of fire by night.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_14.jpg){height="95" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:15*](#Contents.html_rnum14_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And if you kill this people as one man, then the nations that have
heard about you will say it, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap14_15.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:16*](#Contents.html_rnum14_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^'Because YHWH wasn't able to bring this people to the land that He
swore to them, He slaughtered them in the wilderness.'

![image](images/Num_Chap14_16.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:17*](#Contents.html_rnum14_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And now, let my Lord's power be big, as you spoke, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap14_17.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:17. **let my Lord's power be big**. Moses suggests that it is more
powerful to be merciful than to punish. In some circumstances,
compassion is weakness. In others, it requires enormous strength.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:18*](#Contents.html_rnum14_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^'YHWH is slow to anger and abounding in kindness, bearing crime and
offense; though not making one *innocent:* reckoning fathers' crime on
children, on third generations and on fourth generations.'

![image](images/Num_Chap14_18.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:18. **slow to anger ...** Moses quotes God's own words from the
moment of the great revelation, when Moses saw God at Sinai (Exod
34:6--7). So he is not only appealing to God's mercy; he is citing God's
own description of Himself as if to say that God must be true to
Himself.

14:18. **on children, on third generations and on fourth generations**.
The passage that Moses is quoting says: "on children and on children's
children, on third generations and on fourth generations" (Exod 34:7).
It looks like "and on children's children" has been lost, presumably by
another haplography: a scribe's eye skipped from one occurrence of the
word "children" to the next. It is the easiest kind of scribal error to
make, and in fact this same omission occurs in the Ten Commandments
(Exod 20:5 and Deut 5:9). As a less banal and more homiletical
explanation of this case, we might suggest that the text pictures Moses
as shortening the long formula so as to keep focused on the larger
point. Or perhaps, by leaving it out, he subtly presses God to make the
correction and thus to be reminded of the extent of His compassion
according to His own words. Deliberately misquoting someone may actually
be an effective tool of persuasion.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:19*](#Contents.html_rnum14_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^Forgive this people's crime in proportion to the magnitude of your
kindness and as you've borne this people from Egypt to here."

![image](images/Num_Chap14_19.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:19. **Forgive this people's crime**. Moses' four-fold defense of the
people is brilliantly merged: What will the Egyptians say! What will the
nations say! You swore the land to them (namely, in the covenant)! You
are merciful.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:20*](#Contents.html_rnum14_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And YHWH said, "I've forgiven according to your word.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_20.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:20. **I've forgiven according to your word**. Abraham questioned God
regarding Sodom and Gomorrah, but his conversation did not change
anything. Ten virtuous persons were not found, and the cities were
destroyed. But Moses is pictured as actually making a difference in a
divine decision and in the fate of the people. This is another case in
which there is growth in the human stance relative to God.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:21*](#Contents.html_rnum14_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^But indeed, as I live, and as YHWH's glory has filled all the earth,

![image](images/Num_Chap14_21.jpg){height="47" width="302"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:22*](#Contents.html_rnum14_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^I swear that all of these people, who have seen my glory and my
signs that I did in Egypt and in the wilderness and who have tested me
ten times now and haven't listened to my voice,

![image](images/Num_Chap14_22.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:23*](#Contents.html_rnum14_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^won't see the land that I swore to their fathers, and all those who
rejected me won't see it.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_23.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:24*](#Contents.html_rnum14_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And my servant Caleb, because a different spirit was with him, and
he went after me completely, I'll bring him to the land where he went,
and his seed will possess it.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_24.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:25*](#Contents.html_rnum14_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And the Amalekite and the Canaanite live in the valley. Turn and
travel tomorrow to the wilderness by the way of the Red Sea."

![image](images/Num_Chap14_25.jpg){height="50" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:26*](#Contents.html_rnum14_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And YHWH spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap14_26.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:27*](#Contents.html_rnum14_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^"How much further for this bad congregation, that they're
complaining against me? I've heard the complaints of the children of
Israel that they're making against me.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_27.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:28*](#Contents.html_rnum14_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^Say to them: As I live---word of YHWH---what you have spoken in my
ears, that is what I'll do to you!

![image](images/Num_Chap14_28.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:28. **what you have spoken in my ears, that is what I'll do to you!**
Another case of ironic divine punishment to suit the crime: They could
have had the land and lived, but they were afraid and said that they
would die and their children would be made captives. So now they will in
fact die, but their children will live and one day be free in the land.
This is the most dramatic demonstration yet of the people's persistent
slave mentality. God says that He has forgiven them, and they are not
stricken with an epidemic as God had originally said; but they are also
not ready for the life of free people in a land of their own. And so
this is left for their children, who grow up not knowing the experience
of having been slaves. An entire generation's experience is not easily
reversed. In our own age we see the impact on the lives of survivors of
the holocaust. Some become more religious, some reject religion. Some
cannot speak of it, and some speak of it constantly. Some return to the
sites where they suffered, and some will never go back. It affects their
medical decisions, their ways of raising their children, their trust of
other human beings, their work, their thoughts, their opinions, and
their memories. Their children carry tremendous loads of guilt and
obligation, but they have a chance of knowing a kind of freedom that
their parents cannot know.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:29*](#Contents.html_rnum14_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^In this wilderness your carcasses will fall; and all of you who were
counted, for all your number, from twenty years old and up, who
complained against me,

![image](images/Num_Chap14_29.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:30*](#Contents.html_rnum14_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^I swear that you won't come to the land that I raised my hand to
have you reside there---except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of
Nun.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_30.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:31*](#Contents.html_rnum14_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And your infants, whom you said would become a spoil: I'll bring
*them,* and they will know the land that you rejected!

![image](images/Num_Chap14_31.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:32*](#Contents.html_rnum14_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And *you:* your carcasses will fall in this wilderness.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_32.jpg){height="25" width="293"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:33*](#Contents.html_rnum14_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And your children will be roving in the wilderness forty years, and
they'll bear your whoring until the end of your carcasses in the
wilderness.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_33.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:34*](#Contents.html_rnum14_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^For the number of days that you scouted the land, forty days, you
shall bear your crimes a day for each year, forty years, and you shall
know my frustration!

![image](images/Num_Chap14_34.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:35*](#Contents.html_rnum14_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^I, YHWH, have spoken: If I shall not do this to all this bad
congregation who are gathered against me: in this wilderness they shall
end, and they shall die there!"

![image](images/Num_Chap14_35.jpg){height="74" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:35. **If I shall not do this**. This wording is an oath formulation.
It means, "I shall surely do this."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:36*](#Contents.html_rnum14_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And the men whom Moses sent to scout the land and came back and
caused all the congregation to complain against him, bringing out a
report about the land:

![image](images/Num_Chap14_36.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![image](images/Num_Chap14_36a.jpg){height="26" width="87"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:37*](#Contents.html_rnum14_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^the men who brought out the bad report of the land died in a plague
in front of YHWH.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_37.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:38*](#Contents.html_rnum14_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^But, out of those men who went to scout the land, Joshua son of Nun
and Caleb son of Jephunneh lived.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_38.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^And Moses spoke these things to all the children of Israel, and the
people mourned very much.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_39.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:40*](#Contents.html_rnum14_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^And they got up in the morning and went up to the top of the
mountain, saying, "Here we are, and we'll go up to the place that YHWH
said, because we've sinned."

![image](images/Num_Chap14_40.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:41*](#Contents.html_rnum14_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^And Moses said, "Why are you violating YHWH's word? And it won't
succeed.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_41.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:42*](#Contents.html_rnum14_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^Don't go up, so you won't be stricken in front of your enemies,
because YHWH isn't among you.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_42.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:43*](#Contents.html_rnum14_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^Because the Amalekite and the Canaanite are there in front of you,
and you'll fall by the sword because of the fact that you've gone back
from following YHWH, and YHWH won't be with you."

![image](images/Num_Chap14_43.jpg){height="94" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:44*](#Contents.html_rnum14_44)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^44^And they acted heedlessly, going up to the top of the mountain. And
the ark of the covenant of YHWH and Moses did not draw away from the
camp.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_44.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 14:45*](#Contents.html_rnum14_45)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^45^And the Amalekite and the Canaanite who lived in that mountain came
down, and they struck them and crushed them as far as Hormah.

![image](images/Num_Chap14_45.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers15.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:1*](#Contents.html_rnum15_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **15** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap15_01.jpg){height="22" width="230"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:2*](#Contents.html_rnum15_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Speak to the children of Israel, and you shall say to them: When you
will come to the land of your homes that I am giving to you,

![image](images/Num_Chap15_02.jpg){height="71" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:3*](#Contents.html_rnum15_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^and you will make a fire offering to YHWH, a burnt offering or a
sacrifice, to express a vow or as a contribution or at your appointed
times, to make a pleasant smell to YHWH from the cattle or from the
flock,

![image](images/Num_Chap15_03.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:4*](#Contents.html_rnum15_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^then the one bringing his offering to YHWH shall bring forward a
grain offering, fine flour, one-tenth of a measure, mixed with a fourth
of a hin of oil.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_04.jpg){height="50" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:5*](#Contents.html_rnum15_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And you shall make wine for a libation with the burnt offering or the
sacrifice, a fourth of a hin for one lamb.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_05.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:6*](#Contents.html_rnum15_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^Or for a ram, you shall make a grain offering, fine flour, two
tenths, mixed with oil, a third of a hin,

![image](images/Num_Chap15_06.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:7*](#Contents.html_rnum15_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^and you shall bring forward wine for a libation, a third of a hin, a
pleasant smell to YHWH.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_07.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:8*](#Contents.html_rnum15_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And if you will make a bull a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to
express a vow, or a peace offering to YHWH,

![image](images/Num_Chap15_08.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:9*](#Contents.html_rnum15_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^then he shall bring forward with the bull a grain offering, fine
flour, three tenths, mixed with oil, half of a hin,

![image](images/Num_Chap15_09.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:10*](#Contents.html_rnum15_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^and you shall bring forward wine for a libation, half of a hin, an
offering by fire of a pleasant smell to YHWH.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_10.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:11*](#Contents.html_rnum15_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^It shall be done this way for one ox or for one ram or for a lamb of
the sheep or of the goats.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_11.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:12*](#Contents.html_rnum15_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^By the number that you will do, you shall do each one of their
number this way.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_12.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:13*](#Contents.html_rnum15_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^Every citizen shall do these this way when bringing forward an
offering by fire of a pleasant smell to YHWH.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_13.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:14*](#Contents.html_rnum15_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And when an alien will reside with you---or someone who is among
you---through your generations and will make an offering by fire of a
pleasant smell to YHWH, as you will do so he shall do.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_14.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:15*](#Contents.html_rnum15_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^You, congregation, and the resident alien have one law, an eternal
law through your generations: it will be the same for you and for the
alien in front of YHWH.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_15.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:16*](#Contents.html_rnum15_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^You and the alien who resides with you shall have one instruction
and one judgment."

![image](images/Num_Chap15_16.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:16. **You and the alien**. In these verses (14--16) the text says in
four different ways that the law is the same for a citizen and a
resident alien. Thus it again emphasizes that this is an essential
principle of the Torah: Israelites are not privileged over anyone else.
A country must treat everyone who lives in it fairly, with equality
under the law.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:17*](#Contents.html_rnum15_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap15_17.jpg){height="47" width="165"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:18*](#Contents.html_rnum15_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^"Speak to the children of Israel, and you shall say to them: When
you come to the land where I'm bringing you,

![image](images/Num_Chap15_18.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:19*](#Contents.html_rnum15_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^then it will be that when you eat some of the land's bread you shall
make a donation to YHWH.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_19.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:20*](#Contents.html_rnum15_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^The first of your dough: you shall make a loaf as a donation. Like a
donation from the threshing floor, so you shall donate it.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_20.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:21*](#Contents.html_rnum15_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^From the first of your dough you shall give a donation to YHWH
through your generations.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_21.jpg){height="46" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:22*](#Contents.html_rnum15_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^"And when you will make a mistake and not do all of these
commandments that YHWH has spoken to Moses,

![image](images/Num_Chap15_22.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:23*](#Contents.html_rnum15_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^everything that YHWH has commanded you by Moses' hand, from the day
that YHWH commanded and on through your generations,

![image](images/Num_Chap15_23.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:24*](#Contents.html_rnum15_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^then it will be that, if it was done by mistake, out of the
congregation's sight, then all the congregation shall do one bull of the
cattle as a burnt offering for a pleasant smell to
[]{#Numbers15.html_page_52}YHWH and its grain offering and its libation
according to the required manner and one goat for a sin offering.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_24.jpg){height="96" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:25*](#Contents.html_rnum15_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And the priest shall make atonement for all the congregation of the
children of Israel, and it will be forgiven for them, because it was a
mistake, and they brought their offering, an offering by fire to YHWH,
and their sin offering in front of YHWH for their mistake.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_25.jpg){height="95" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:26*](#Contents.html_rnum15_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And it will be forgiven for all the congregation of the children of
Israel and the alien who resides among them, because it was by mistake
for all the people.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_26.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:27*](#Contents.html_rnum15_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^"And if one person will sin by mistake, then he shall bring a
she-goat in its first year for a sin offering.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_27.jpg){height="43" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:28*](#Contents.html_rnum15_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And the priest shall make atonement for the person who made the
mistake, sinning by mistake, in front of YHWH to make atonement for him,
and it will be forgiven for him.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_28.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:29*](#Contents.html_rnum15_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^The citizen among the children of Israel and for the alien who
resides among them: you shall have one instruction for one who acts by
mistake.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_29.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:30*](#Contents.html_rnum15_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^"But the person who will act with a high hand, a citizen or an
alien, he is blaspheming YHWH, and that person will be cut off from
among his people,

![image](images/Num_Chap15_30.jpg){height="92" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:31*](#Contents.html_rnum15_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^because he disdained YHWH's word and broke His commandment. That
person will be *cut off.* His crime is in him."

![image](images/Num_Chap15_31.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:32*](#Contents.html_rnum15_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And the children of Israel were in the wilderness, and they found a
man collecting wood on the Sabbath day.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_32.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:33*](#Contents.html_rnum15_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And those who found him collecting wood brought him forward to Moses
and to Aaron and to all the congregation.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_33.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:34*](#Contents.html_rnum15_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And they left him under watch because it had not been determined
what should be done to him.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_34.jpg){height="41" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:34. **it had not been determined**. There had already been
commandments to observe the Sabbath, not to work on that day, and not to
make a fire on that day; and it had already been stated that one who
violates the Sabbath is to be executed and cut off. But it was yet to be
determined whether this particular case of collecting wood constituted
work or whether (if the collection of wood was for building a fire) the
collection itself constituted a violation. This account thus not only
teaches a law about the Sabbath but also demonstrates the relationship
between a legal *principle* and the application of that principle to
each individual *case.* No case is to be prejudged. Every case deserves
an inquiry and a decision.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:35*](#Contents.html_rnum15_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And YHWH said to Moses, "The man shall be *put to death!* All the
congregation is to batter him with stones outside the camp."

![image](images/Num_Chap15_35.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:36*](#Contents.html_rnum15_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And all the congregation brought him outside the camp and battered
him with stones, and he died, as YHWH commanded Moses.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_36.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:37*](#Contents.html_rnum15_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^And YHWH said to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap15_37.jpg){height="22" width="223"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:38*](#Contents.html_rnum15_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^"Speak to the children of Israel, and you shall say to them that
they []{#Numbers15.html_page_53}shall make fringe on the corners of
their clothes through their generations. And they shall put a blue
string on the fringe of the corner.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_38.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:38. **blue string**. Blue string is used in the garments of the high
priest (Exod 28:28,37). The blue string in the fringe of every
Israelite's clothing is an element of the holy in every one's daily
life. The fringe in general is a reminder to keep all the commandments;
the blue string is a reminder of holiness. (See the next comment.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:39*](#Contents.html_rnum15_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^And you shall have the fringe so you will see it and bring to mind
all of YHWH's commandments and will do them, and you will not go around
after your heart and after your eyes, because you whore after them.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_39.jpg){height="94" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:39. **see it and bring to mind**. Most of the commandments in the
Torah are given without any reason being stated. We are not told why we
are not allowed to eat a pig or to steal, or why the Tabernacle must
have twenty frames on each side. If we want to know the reasons, we have
to ponder and try to determine them ourselves. For some commandments the
reason is obvious, and for some it is extremely difficult to know. The
commandment to wear fringes stands out, therefore, as being one for
which the reason is stated explicitly: the fringe is a reminder; it is
always present on one's clothing so that one will see it and be reminded
of the existence of all the commandments, so one will do them, and so
the people will be holy.

15:39. **go around**. Hebrew *tûr.* This is the same word that is used
for the scouting of the land by the spies in the preceding chapters.
This both helps us understand the word---meaning to go looking all over,
here with the sense of looking for trouble---and helps to connect the
laws of this chapter with the story of the spies that just precedes it.
This chapter of law is often seen as intrusive between a series of
stories, but the language used in the laws bring the stories to mind,
and vice versa.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:40*](#Contents.html_rnum15_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^So you will bring to mind and do all my commandments, and you will
be holy to your God.

![image](images/Num_Chap15_40.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 15:41*](#Contents.html_rnum15_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^I am YHWH, your God, who brought you out from the land of Egypt to
be God to you. I am YHWH, your God."

![image](images/Num_Chap15_41.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers16.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:1*](#Contents.html_rnum16_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## K[ORAH]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **16** {.h2a}

^1^And Korah son of Izhar son of Kohath son of Levi, and Dathan and
Abiram, sons of Eliab, and On, son of Peleth, sons of Reuben, took

![image](images/Num_Chap16.jpg){height="28" width="48"}

![image](images/Num_Chap16_01.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:1. **Korah**. He is a first cousin of Moses and Aaron (Exod
6:18--21).

16:1. **Korah ... took**. He assembles a group with him, and he frames
his argument in terms of the people ("all of them are holy"). He is the
prototype of some politicians, religious leaders, and others, who act as
though they represent a group and are working for the people---when
their motives are suspect.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:2*](#Contents.html_rnum16_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^and got up in front of Moses---and two hundred fifty people from the
children of Israel, chieftains of the congregation, prominent ones of
the assembly, people of repute.

![image](images/Num_Chap16_02.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:3*](#Contents.html_rnum16_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And they assembled against Moses and against Aaron and said to them,
"You have much! Because all of the congregation, all of them, are holy,
and YHWH is among them. And why do you raise yourselves up over YHWH's
community?"

![image](images/Num_Chap16_03.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:3. **all of the congregation, all of them, are holy**. It is true
that all the people have been described as a holy nation. Korah uses
this now to enhance his claim that he has as much right to the
priesthood as Aaron. But it is a deception, mixing two different
meanings and degrees of holiness. It is like telling a child that he or
she is now "big" or "grown up"---and then the child demands all the
rights and privileges of grownups.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:4*](#Contents.html_rnum16_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Moses listened, and he fell on his face.

![image](images/Num_Chap16_04.jpg){height="42" width="141"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:5*](#Contents.html_rnum16_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And he spoke to Korah and to all of his congregation, saying, "In the
morning YHWH will make known who is His and who is holy, and He will
bring him close to Him. And He will bring the one He chooses close to
Him.

![image](images/Num_Chap16_05.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:5. **who is holy**. Moses sets up a test of who is holy, to establish
what it means to be holy in the priestly sense, as opposed to the
general concept that the people should strive to be holy. The test will
be the use of incense, because only the holy---namely, priests---are
permitted to burn incense. The test will establish this, and it will be
declared formally following this episode (Num 17:5). And Korah and his
company cannot be unaware of the significance of the correct burning of
incense, because it was precisely a misuse of incense that led to the
death of Aaron's sons, Nadab and Abihu (Lev 10:1--2).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:6*](#Contents.html_rnum16_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^Do this: Take incense burners, Korah and all his congregation,

![image](images/Num_Chap16_06.jpg){height="49" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:6. **Do this**. Why does Korah go along with the test? Do he and his
followers really believe that God would support them? Answer: they have
no choice. Moses does not *propose* a test. He *commands* Korah and his
group. His verbs are four imperatives in a row: do, take, put, set! And
Korah cannot refuse. The whole point, after all, is that Korah is
seeking priesthood (Num 16:10). What Moses challenges him to do is to
perform one of the acts that he would commonly have to do if he were to
become a priest: burn incense. Korah's claim on the priesthood would
evaporate if he were to decline the opportunity he has been offered to
do a priestly task.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:7*](#Contents.html_rnum16_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^and put fire in them and set incense on them in front of YHWH
tomorrow. And it will be that the man whom YHWH will choose, he will be
the holy one. *You* have much, sons of Levi!"

![image](images/Num_Chap16_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:8*](#Contents.html_rnum16_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Moses said to Korah, "Listen, sons of Levi,

![image](images/Num_Chap16_08.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:9*](#Contents.html_rnum16_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^is it too small a thing for you that Israel's God has distinguished
you from the congregation of []{#Numbers16.html_page_54}Israel to bring
you close to him, to do the work of YHWH's Tabernacle and to stand in
front of the congregation to minister for them,

![image](images/Num_Chap16_09.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:10*](#Contents.html_rnum16_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^and that He has brought you and all your brothers, the sons of Levi,
with you close to Him? And you seek priesthood as well?!

![image](images/Num_Chap16_10.jpg){height="46" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:11*](#Contents.html_rnum16_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^Therefore you and all your congregation who are gathering are
against YHWH! And Aaron, what is he that you complain against him?"

![image](images/Num_Chap16_11.jpg){height="73" width="341"}

![image](images/Num_Chap16_11a.jpg){height="27" width="86"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:12*](#Contents.html_rnum16_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And Moses sent to call Dathan and Abiram, sons of Eliab, and they
said, "We won't come up.

![image](images/Num_Chap16_12.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:12. **Moses sent to call Dathan and Abiram**. Two groups with two
different complaints have joined together to challenge Moses. Korah's
company challenge Aaron's hold on the priesthood; Dathan and Abiram
challenge Moses' hold on the leadership of Israel. This political union
of two agendas is presumably arranged to strengthen the positions of
both. Moses, however, keeps them separate. He has responded first to
Korah and his company who are challenging the priestly prerogatives of
Moses and Aaron. Now he turns to Dathan and Abiram and summons them. By
separating them, he weakens their position.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:13*](#Contents.html_rnum16_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^Is it a small thing that you brought us up from a land flowing with
milk and honey to kill us in the wilderness, that you lord it over us as
well?

![image](images/Num_Chap16_13.jpg){height="64" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:13. **you brought us up from a land flowing with milk and honey**.
They are referring to Egypt! They use the rhetorical device of reversing
their opponent's position: they say that Moses has taken them *out* of a
land flowing with milk and honey. It is a clever device (common in
political debate). And it is outrageous: accusing the liberator of being
the source of their problems, picturing the land of slavery as the land
of milk and honey, and, as usual, focusing on Moses rather than on God.
And then they say, "Will you put out those people's eyes?"---accusing
Moses of being the one who is deceiving the people, when it is they who
are the deceivers. This is a battle for leadership, which is to say it
is a political battle, which is to say it is a battle for power; and it
dramatizes the danger of the power-seeking politician who is a skillful
speaker. Moses defeats his opponents because God intervenes with a
miracle. We who cannot count on earthquakes to identify the corrupt ones
among us must be thoughtful, sensitive, and diligent when we listen to
political argument.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:14*](#Contents.html_rnum16_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^Besides, you haven't brought us to a land flowing with milk and
honey or given us possession of field or vineyard. Will you put out
those people's eyes? We won't come up."

![image](images/Num_Chap16_14.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:14. **you haven't brought us to a land flowing with milk and honey or
given us possession of field or vineyard**. This is true. Part of a
persuasive lie is to mix in something that is true.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:15*](#Contents.html_rnum16_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Moses was very angry, and he said to YHWH, "Don't turn to their
offering. Not one ass of theirs have I taken away, and I haven't wronged
one of them."

![image](images/Num_Chap16_15.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:16*](#Contents.html_rnum16_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And Moses said to Korah, "You and all your congregation, be in front
of YHWH---you and they and Aaron---tomorrow.

![image](images/Num_Chap16_16.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:17*](#Contents.html_rnum16_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And each man take his fire-holder, and put incense on them, and each
man bring his fire-holder forward in front of YHWH, two hundred fifty
fire-holders, and you and Aaron, each man his fire-holder."

![image](images/Num_Chap16_17.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:18*](#Contents.html_rnum16_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And each man took his fire-holder, and they put fire on them and set
incense on them, and they stood at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting,
and Moses and Aaron.

![image](images/Num_Chap16_18.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:19*](#Contents.html_rnum16_19)
