------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And Korah assembled all the congregation against them, to the
entrance of the Tent of Meeting. And YHWH's glory appeared to all the
congregation.

![image](images/Num_Chap16_19.jpg){height="65" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:20*](#Contents.html_rnum16_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And YHWH spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap16_20.jpg){height="22" width="305"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:21*](#Contents.html_rnum16_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^"Separate from among this congregation, and I'll finish them in an
instant!"

![image](images/Num_Chap16_21.jpg){height="22" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:22*](#Contents.html_rnum16_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And they fell on their faces and said, "God, the God of the spirits
of all flesh, will one man sin and you be angry at all the
congregation?"

![image](images/Num_Chap16_22.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:22. **will one man sin and you be angry at all the congregation**.
Korah has brought the whole congregation of Israel along (16:19), and
God has threatened to destroy all of them (16:21), and so Moses and
Aaron plead that only those who have done something wrong should suffer.
"One man" here cannot mean literally only a single person; it must refer
to every individual in Korah's congregation, as opposed to the entire
congregation of Israel. It is reminiscent of Abraham's plea in the case
of Sodom and Gomorrah ("Will you also annihilate the virtuous with the
wicked?" Gen 18:23). The connection to the case of Sodom is highlighted
by the fact that Moses then tells the people to get away "or else you'll
be annihilated," which are the words that the angels say at Sodom---and
these words occur nowhere else in the Torah (see Gen 18:23,24;
19:15,17).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:23*](#Contents.html_rnum16_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap16_23.jpg){height="22" width="229"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:24*](#Contents.html_rnum16_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^"Speak to the congregation, saying, 'Get up from around the
tabernacle of Korah, Dathan and Abiram.'"

![image](images/Num_Chap16_24.jpg){height="53" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:25*](#Contents.html_rnum16_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And Moses got up and went to Dathan and Abiram, and Israel's elders
went after him.

![image](images/Num_Chap16_25.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:26*](#Contents.html_rnum16_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And he spoke to the congregation, saying, "Turn away from the tents
of these wicked men and don't touch anything that is theirs, or else
you'll be annihilated through all their sins."

![image](images/Num_Chap16_26.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:27*](#Contents.html_rnum16_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And they got up from around the tabernacle of Korah, Dathan and
Abiram. And Dathan and Abiram came out, standing at the entrance of
their tents, and their wives and their children and their infants.

![image](images/Num_Chap16_27.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:28*](#Contents.html_rnum16_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And Moses said, "By this you'll know that YHWH sent me to do all
these things, because it's not from my own heart.

![image](images/Num_Chap16_28.jpg){height="70" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:29*](#Contents.html_rnum16_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^If these die like the death of every human, and the event of every
human happens to them, then YHWH hasn't sent me.

![image](images/Num_Chap16_29.jpg){height="73" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:30*](#Contents.html_rnum16_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^But if YHWH will create something, and the ground will open its
mouth and swallow them and all that they have, and they'll go down alive
to Sheol, then you'll know that these people have rejected YHWH."

![image](images/Num_Chap16_30.jpg){height="101" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:31*](#Contents.html_rnum16_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And it was as he was finishing speaking all these things, and the
ground that was under them was broken up,

![image](images/Num_Chap16_31.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:32*](#Contents.html_rnum16_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^and the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them and their
households and all the people who were with Korah and all the property,

![image](images/Num_Chap16_32.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:32. **them and their households**. Meaning: their entire families die
with them. Why does the entire household suffer? Elsewhere in the law we
are told that each person is responsible for his or her own crimes.
Children are not to suffer for their parents' acts (Deut 24:16). But
that principle applies only to ethical law: crimes between one human and
another. In ritual law---violations against God, such as blasphemy or,
in this case, openly opposing an instruction from God ("these people
have rejected YHWH")---anyone who comes in contact with the source of
the offense is tainted by it, regardless of that person's own intent.
Thus, for example, in the book of Joshua, Achan keeps some of the
forbidden spoils of Jericho, which are designated for complete
destruction (*![image](images/hdot.jpg){height="15" width="7"}erem*). He
conceals them in his tent. When his offense is revealed he and the
spoils "and his sons and his daughters and his ox and his ass and his
flock and his tent and everything he had" are stoned and burned (Josh
7:24). It is difficult to understand in the postbiblical age, but the
concepts of ritual, sacred realms, purity and impurity, and violation of
sacred zones are fundamental to Israel's life as pictured in the Bible's
early books. It may be that the advance in psychology and especially of
psychoanalysis in this century can help us to appreciate the mind-set of
those who conceived of this notion of guilt by contact. We can recognize
that people feel guilt even about things that are not their fault
morally. Victims of childhood incest frequently feel guilt through their
adult lives. As a closer example: as a child I observed another boy tear
a Torah scroll, and I recall how upsetting this was to him and others
even though he did not necessarily do it out of lack of care. I also
recall, from my student days, knocking a pious rabbi's black hat from a
hook onto the floor and feeling terrible even though it was an accident.
(He was wise; he just said lightly, "Praise God that my head wasn't in
it at the time.") The point is that we do have feelings of guilt in
connection with things that we hold in awe. And this is multiplied a
thousandfold in the narrative of the age of the Torah: when the divine
is believed to be close at hand.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:33*](#Contents.html_rnum16_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^and they went down, they and all that they had, alive to Sheol. And
the earth covered them over, and they perished from among the community.

![image](images/Num_Chap16_33.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:34*](#Contents.html_rnum16_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And all Israel that was around them fled at the sound of them, for
they said, "Or else the earth will swallow us."

![image](images/Num_Chap16_34.jpg){height="50" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 16:35*](#Contents.html_rnum16_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And fire had gone out from YHWH and consumed the two hundred fifty
people offering the incense.

![image](images/Num_Chap16_35.jpg){height="68" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:35. **fire had gone out**. This is worded in the past perfect. (The
noun precedes the verb in the Hebrew.) That is, this must be understood
to have happened to Korah's incense-burning group before the earthquake
swallowed them along with Dathan's and Abiram's households.

[]{#Numbers17.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:1*](#Contents.html_rnum17_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **17** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap17_01.jpg){height="22" width="230"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:2*](#Contents.html_rnum17_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Say to Eleazar son of Aaron, the priest, that he should pick up the
fire-holders from the burning and disperse the fire, because they have
become holy.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_02.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:3*](#Contents.html_rnum17_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And the fire-holders of these who sinned at the cost of their lives:
let them make them into hammered plates as plating for the altar,
because they brought them forward in front of YHWH, and they became
holy, and let them become a sign to the children of Israel."

![image](images/Num_Chap16_03.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:4*](#Contents.html_rnum17_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Eleazar, the priest, took the fire-holders of bronze that those
who were burned had brought forward, and they hammered them into a
plating for the altar,

![image](images/Num_Chap17_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:5*](#Contents.html_rnum17_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^a commemoration for the children of Israel so that no outsider, one
who is not from Aaron's seed, will come forward to burn incense in front
of YHWH, so he will not be like Korah and like his congregation, as YHWH
spoke to him by Moses' hand.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_05.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:6*](#Contents.html_rnum17_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And all the congregation of the children of Israel complained the
next day against Moses and against Aaron, saying, "*You* killed YHWH's
people!"

![image](images/Num_Chap17_06.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:6. **all the congregation of the children of Israel complained the
next day**. Sometimes a solution may give rise to worse conditions than
the original problem. Korah, Dathan, and Abiram had 250 persons in their
congregation challenging Moses and Aaron. They are all killed, but now
the *entire* congregation of Israel challenges them.

17:6. ***You*** **killed YHWH's people!** Not only do they focus, once
again, on Moses and Aaron instead of on their God, they say that Moses
and Aaron caused the deaths of Korah and his group. And they turn the
truth around even further by saying, "*You* killed YHWH's
people!"---seemingly placing Moses and Aaron on the *opposite* side from
God. Their attack turns the truth on its head, and so God's response is
ironic: they have said that Moses and Aaron have killed God's people, so
God now threatens to kill His people Himself for their attack on Moses
and Aaron! And the irony is extended because Moses and Aaron are the
ones who *save* the people from the rage and plague that come.

One might look at a summary of these episodes of challenges to Moses and
be inclined to think that the book of Numbers ultimately has only one
story to tell and simply keeps repeating it with slight changes of
detail. However, that is not likely to be the sense one gets while
reading through the stories themselves. The stories act more like
variations on a theme. They are each attractive and instructive in
themselves, while the recurring elements of theme and language provide
sufficient commonality to relate them sensibly to one another and thus
give shape to Numbers as a book. When one reads that the people make the
same error again and again, directing their complaints toward their
human leaders rather than toward the deity who is in fact responsible
for their fortunes, one errs to see in this mere repetition. More
fruitful would be a consideration of what is to be learned from this
picture of a human community who will not learn from experience on this
point---or who apparently refuse to accept the reality of it. As in
Exodus, closeness to the divine, witnessing the intervention of the
cosmic into the mundane, is pictured more as frightening than as
uplifting. Despite all that they have seen, they resist coming to terms
with the invisible God who has gotten them their freedom and made a
covenant with them. And so each new episode of conflict and divine
response does not resolve the problem but only leads to another stage of
it. But they will finally begin to recognize that their conflicts are
also with their God in one of their last rebellions (see Num 21:4--9 and
the comments on 21:5).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:7*](#Contents.html_rnum17_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And it was when the congregation was assembled against Moses and
against Aaron, and they turned to the Tent of Meeting; and, here, the
cloud had covered it, and YHWH's glory appeared.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_07.jpg){height="70" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:8*](#Contents.html_rnum17_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Moses and Aaron came to the front of the Tent of Meeting.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_08.jpg){height="22" width="293"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:9*](#Contents.html_rnum17_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap17_09.jpg){height="22" width="225"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:10*](#Contents.html_rnum17_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^"Move away from among this congregation, and I'll finish them in an
instant!" And they fell on their faces.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_10.jpg){height="69" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:11*](#Contents.html_rnum17_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Moses said to Aaron, "Take a fire-holder and put fire from the
altar on it and set incense and carry it quickly to the congregation and
make atonement for them, because a rage has come out from in front of
YHWH! The plague has begun!"

![image](images/Num_Chap17_11.jpg){height="98" width="376"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:12*](#Contents.html_rnum17_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And Aaron took it as Moses had spoken, and he ran among the
community. And, here, the plague had begun among the people. And he put
in incense and made atonement for the people.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_12.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:13*](#Contents.html_rnum17_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And he stood between the dead and the living. And the plague was
halted.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_13.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:14*](#Contents.html_rnum17_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And the dead in the plague were fourteen thousand seven hundred,
apart from the dead over the matter of Korah.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_14.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:15*](#Contents.html_rnum17_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Aaron went back to Moses, to the entrance of the Tent of
Meeting, when the plague had been halted.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_15.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:16*](#Contents.html_rnum17_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap17_16.jpg){height="22" width="217"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:17*](#Contents.html_rnum17_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^"Speak to the children of Israel and take from them a staff for each
father's house, from all their chieftains by their fathers' house,
twelve staffs. You shall write each man's name on his staff,

![image](images/Num_Chap17_17.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:18*](#Contents.html_rnum17_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^and you shall write Aaron's name on the staff of Levi, because there
shall be one staff for the head of their fathers' house.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_18.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:19*](#Contents.html_rnum17_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And you shall leave them in the Tent of Meeting in front of the
Testimony where I shall meet with you.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_19.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:20*](#Contents.html_rnum17_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And it will be that the man I shall choose: his staff will bloom.
And I'll decrease from me the complaints of the children of Israel that
they're making against you."

![image](images/Num_Chap17_20.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:21*](#Contents.html_rnum17_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Moses spoke to the children of Israel, and all their chieftains
gave him a staff for each chieftain by their fathers' house, twelve
staffs, and Aaron's staff was among their staffs.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_21.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:22*](#Contents.html_rnum17_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Moses left the staffs in front of YHWH in the Tent of the
Testimony.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_22.jpg){height="22" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:23*](#Contents.html_rnum17_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And it was the next day, and Moses came to the Tent of the
Testimony; and, here, Aaron's staff, of the house of Levi, had bloomed;
and it brought out a bloom, and it made a blossom, and it produced
almonds!

![image](images/Num_Chap17_23.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:24*](#Contents.html_rnum17_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And Moses brought out all the staffs from in front of YHWH to the
children of Israel. And they saw, and each took his staff.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_24.jpg){height="65" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:25*](#Contents.html_rnum17_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And YHWH said to Moses, "Put back Aaron's staff in front of the
Testimony for watching over, for a sign to rebels, and you'll end their
complaints against me so they won't die!"

![image](images/Num_Chap17_25.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:25. **Put back Aaron's staff in front of the Testimony for watching
over, for a sign to rebels**. Aaron's staff that miraculously blossomed
is now saved by the ark as a reminder when people will rebel in the
future. This will be crucial to the event that will determine Aaron's
fate later, when the people will rebel at Meribah. See the comment on
Num 20:8.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:26*](#Contents.html_rnum17_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And Moses did it. As YHWH had commanded him, he did so.

![image](images/Num_Chap17_26.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:27*](#Contents.html_rnum17_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And the children of Israel said to Moses, saying, "Here, we're
expiring, we're perishing, we're all perishing!

![image](images/Num_Chap17_27.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 17:28*](#Contents.html_rnum17_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^Everyone who comes close---who comes close to YHWH's
Tabernacle---will die. Have we come to the end of expiring?!"

![image](images/Num_Chap17_28.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers18.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:1*](#Contents.html_rnum18_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **18** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH said to Aaron, "You and your sons and your father's house
with you shall bear any crime of the holy place, and you and your sons
with you shall bear any crime of your priesthood.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_01.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:2*](#Contents.html_rnum18_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And bring forward your brothers, the tribe of Levi, your father's
tribe, with you as well, and they shall be connected to you and shall
minister to you, and you and your sons with you shall be in front of the
Tent of the Testimony.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_02.jpg){height="76" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:2. **connected**. The word is the verbal form of the word "Levite."
Some would say that it might shed light on the original meaning of the
term Levite as being someone who is joined or attached to the sacred in
order to perform service; in other words, a priest. In this verse, it is
used to mean that these persons are rather attached to the priests, in
order to perform service to the priests; in other words, Levites are
designated as secondary clergy, whose task is to assist the priests.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:3*](#Contents.html_rnum18_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And they shall keep your charge, the charge of all of the tent, but
they shall not come close to the equipment of the holy and to the altar,
so they will not die, both they and you.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_03.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:4*](#Contents.html_rnum18_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And they shall be connected to you and shall keep the charge of the
Tent of Meeting, for all the work of the tent, but an outsider shall not
come close to you.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_04.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:5*](#Contents.html_rnum18_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And you shall keep the charge of the holy and the charge of the
altar, so there will not be any more rage at the children of Israel.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_05.jpg){height="70" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:6*](#Contents.html_rnum18_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And I, here, I have taken your brothers, the Levites from among the
children of Israel as a gift to you, given for YHWH, to do the work of
the Tent of Meeting.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_06.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:7*](#Contents.html_rnum18_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And you and your sons with you shall watch over your priesthood for
everything of the altar's and for inside the pavilion, and you shall
serve. I give your priesthood as a gift of service. And the outsider who
comes close shall be put to death."

![image](images/Num_Chap18_07.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:8*](#Contents.html_rnum18_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And YHWH said to Aaron, "And I, here, I have given charge of my
donations to you for all the holy things of the children of Israel. I
have given them to you as an anointing and to your sons as an eternal
law.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_08.jpg){height="95" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:8. **anointing**. Meaning the donations that they acquire by virtue
of being anointed as Israel's priests (see Exod 29:27--28; Lev
7:35--36).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:9*](#Contents.html_rnum18_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^This shall be yours from the holy of holies, from the fire: every
offering of theirs, every grain offering of theirs and every sin
offering of theirs and every guilt offering of theirs that they will pay
back to me. It is holy of holies for you and for your sons.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_09.jpg){height="101" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:9. **guilt offering that they will pay back**. See the case in Num
5:7--8.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:10*](#Contents.html_rnum18_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^You shall eat it in the holy of holies. Every male shall eat it. It
shall be holy to you.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_10.jpg){height="47" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:10. **in the holy of holies**. Meaning in the holiest of holy
conditions.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:11*](#Contents.html_rnum18_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^"And this is yours: their gift donation, all the elevation offerings
of the children of Israel. I have given them to you and to your sons and
to your daughters with you as an eternal law. Everyone who is pure in
your house shall eat it.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_11.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:12*](#Contents.html_rnum18_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^"All the best of the oil and all the best of the wine and grain, the
first of them that they will give to YHWH: I have given them to you.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_12.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:13*](#Contents.html_rnum18_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^The firstfruits of everything that is in their land that they will
bring to YHWH shall be yours. Everyone who is pure in your house shall
eat it.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_13.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:14*](#Contents.html_rnum18_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^Every devoted thing in Israel shall be yours.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_14.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:14. **devoted thing**. Anything that one formally devotes to God
cannot ever be sold or redeemed, and it becomes the priests' property.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:15*](#Contents.html_rnum18_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^Every first birth of a womb of all flesh that they will bring
forward to YHWH, human and animal, shall be yours. Just: you shall
*redeem* the human firstborn, and you shall redeem the firstborn of an
impure animal.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_15.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:16*](#Contents.html_rnum18_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And their redemption price: from one month old you shall redeem it
at your appraisal, five shekels of silver by the shekel of the Holy. It
is twenty gerah.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_16.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:17*](#Contents.html_rnum18_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^Just: you shall not redeem a firstborn of an ox or firstborn of a
sheep or firstborn of a goat. They are holy. You shall fling their blood
on the altar and burn their fat to smoke, a fire offering for a pleasant
smell to YHWH.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_17.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:18*](#Contents.html_rnum18_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And their meat shall be yours; like the breast of the elevation
offering and the right thigh it shall be yours.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_18.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:19*](#Contents.html_rnum18_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^All the donations of the holy things that the children of Israel
will donate to YHWH I have given to you and to your sons and to your
daughters with you as an eternal law. It is an eternal covenant of salt
in front of YHWH for you and for your seed with you."

![image](images/Num_Chap18_19.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:19. **covenant of salt**. Salt is associated with the offerings that
are given to the priests. Lev 2:13 emphasizes, "You shall sprinkle every
offering of a grain offering with salt. And you shall not let the salt
of your God's covenant cease from on your grain offering; you shall
bring salt on all your offerings." (Some take the fact that salt is a
preservative to imply that "covenant of salt" means a long-lasting
covenant.) It is a custom to this day to sprinkle some salt on the bread
at the Sabbath meal as a memorial of this practice.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:20*](#Contents.html_rnum18_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And YHWH said to Aaron, "You shall not have a legacy in their land,
and you shall not have a portion among them. I am your portion and your
legacy among the children of Israel.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_20.jpg){height="78" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:21*](#Contents.html_rnum18_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And to the children of Levi, here, I've given every tithe in Israel
as a legacy in exchange for their work that they're doing, the service
of the Tent of Meeting.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_21.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:22*](#Contents.html_rnum18_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And the children of Israel shall not come near to the Tent of
Meeting anymore so as to bear sin and die.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_22.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:23*](#Contents.html_rnum18_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^But he, the Levite, shall do the service of the Tent of Meeting, and
they shall bear their crime. It is an eternal law through your
generations. And they shall not have a legacy among the children of
Israel,

![image](images/Num_Chap18_23.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:24*](#Contents.html_rnum18_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^because I've given to the Levites as a legacy the tithe of the
children of Israel that they will give to YHWH as a donation. On account
of this I've said to them: they shall not have a legacy among the
children of Israel."

![image](images/Num_Chap18_24.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:25*](#Contents.html_rnum18_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap18_25.jpg){height="44" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:26*](#Contents.html_rnum18_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^"And you shall speak to the Levites and say to them: 'When you will
take the tithe from the children of Israel that I have given you from
them as your legacy, then you shall make a donation for YHWH from it, a
tithe of the tithe.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_26.jpg){height="99" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:27*](#Contents.html_rnum18_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And it will be counted for you as your donation, like grain from the
threshing floor and like the fill of the winepress.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_27.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:28*](#Contents.html_rnum18_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^So you shall donate, you too, a donation for YHWH from all your
tithes that you will take from the children of Israel, and you shall
give a donation for YHWH to Aaron, the priest, from them.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_28.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:29*](#Contents.html_rnum18_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^From all your gifts you shall make every donation for YHWH, from all
the best of it, its holy part from it.'

![image](images/Num_Chap18_29.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:30*](#Contents.html_rnum18_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And you shall say to them: 'When you donate the best of it from it,
then it shall be counted to the Levites like the produce of the
threshing floor and like the produce of the winepress.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_30.jpg){height="79" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:31*](#Contents.html_rnum18_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And you shall eat it in every place, you and your house, because it
is compensation for you in exchange for your work in the Tent of
Meeting.

![image](images/Num_Chap18_31.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 18:32*](#Contents.html_rnum18_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And you will not bear sin over it, when you have donated its best
from it, so you will not desecrate the holy things of the children of
Israel, and you will not die.'"

![image](images/Num_Chap18_32.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers19.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:1*](#Contents.html_rnum19_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## L[AW]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **19** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap19.jpg){height="28" width="49"}

![image](images/Num_Chap19_01.jpg){height="22" width="310"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:2*](#Contents.html_rnum19_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"This is the law of the instruction that YHWH commanded, saying:
Speak to the children of Israel that they should take to you an
unblemished red cow that has no injury, on which a yoke has not gone.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_02.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:2. **law**. Legal texts are worked in at various points of the
wilderness narrative, thus conveying that not all of the law has been
revealed at Sinai, but that the law rather continues to be elaborated
upon and added to as the people move closer to the land. Also through
this construction the revelation of the law comes to be pictured as an
ongoing part of the people's life, and it is understood to be part of
the new, post--exodus generation's experience---and thus the possession
of the people who will carry it into the promised land.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:3*](#Contents.html_rnum19_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And you shall give it to Eleazar, the priest, and one shall bring it
outside the camp and slaughter it in front of him.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_03.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:3. **Eleazar**. Here and in the matter of the fire-holders after the
Korah rebellion (17:2), Eleazar, Aaron's oldest surviving son, is
directly commanded for the first time. It is as if the text is
anticipating the events of the next chapter (20), in which Aaron will
suffer the same fate as Moses: to die before reaching the promised land.
The conclusion of that story recounts the succession of Eleazar to
Aaron's place as high priest (20:23--28). These first commands to
Eleazar thus appear to be a preparation for the moment of succession.
Part of the task of a wise leader is to prepare a successor.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:4*](#Contents.html_rnum19_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And Eleazar, the priest, shall take some of its blood with his finger
and sprinkle some of its blood toward the front of the Tent of Meeting
seven times.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_04.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:5*](#Contents.html_rnum19_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And one shall burn the cow before his eyes. He shall burn its skin
and its meat and its blood and its dung.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_05.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:6*](#Contents.html_rnum19_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And the priest shall take cedarwood and hyssop and scarlet and throw
them into the fire of the cow.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_06.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:7*](#Contents.html_rnum19_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And the priest shall wash his clothes and wash his flesh with water,
and after that he shall come to the camp, and the priest shall be impure
until evening.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_07.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:8*](#Contents.html_rnum19_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And the one who burns it shall wash his clothes with water and wash
his flesh with water and be impure until evening.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_08.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:9*](#Contents.html_rnum19_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And a man who is pure shall gather the cow's ashes and leave them
outside the camp in a pure place, and it shall be for the congregation
of the children of Israel to be kept for water of impurity, for sin
expiation.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_09.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:10*](#Contents.html_rnum19_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And the one who gathers the cow's ashes shall wash his clothes and
be impure until evening. And it will be for the children of Israel and
for the alien who resides among them as an eternal law.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_10.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:11*](#Contents.html_rnum19_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^"One who touches a dead body of any human being will be impure seven
days.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_11.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:12*](#Contents.html_rnum19_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^He shall do sin expiation with it on the third day, and on the
seventh day he will be pure. And if he will not do sin expiation on the
third day, then on the seventh day he will not be pure.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_12.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:12. **with it**. With the ashes of the red cow.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:13*](#Contents.html_rnum19_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^Anyone who touches a dead body of a human being who has died and
does not do sin expiation has made YHWH's Tabernacle impure, and that
person will be cut off from Israel. Because the water of impurity was
not flung on him, he shall be impure. His impurity is still in him.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_13.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:14*](#Contents.html_rnum19_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^"This is the instruction: When a human will die in a tent, anyone
who comes into the tent and everything that is in the tent will be
impure seven days.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_14.jpg){height="69" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:15*](#Contents.html_rnum19_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And any open container, on which there is not a fastened cover: it
is impure.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_15.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:16*](#Contents.html_rnum19_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And anyone who, at an open field, will touch a corpse slain by the
sword or a dead body or a human bone or a grave will be impure seven
days.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_16.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:17*](#Contents.html_rnum19_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And they shall take for the one who is impure some of the ashes of
the fire of sin expiation, and one shall put living water on it in a
container.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_17.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:17. **living water**. Freshwater flowing from a well or other natural
source. See the comment on Lev 14:5.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:18*](#Contents.html_rnum19_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And a man who is pure shall take hyssop and dip it in the water and
sprinkle it on the tent and on all the items and on the persons who were
there and on the one who touched the bone or the slain corpse or the
dead body or the grave.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_18.jpg){height="96" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:19*](#Contents.html_rnum19_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And the one who is pure shall sprinkle it on the impure on the third
day and on the seventh day, so he shall expiate sin from him on the
seventh day, and he shall wash his clothes and wash in water and be pure
in the evening.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_19.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:20*](#Contents.html_rnum19_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And if a man will be impure and will not do sin expiation, then that
person will be cut off from among the community, because he has made
YHWH's holy place impure. The water of impurity has not been flung on
him: he is impure.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_20.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:21*](#Contents.html_rnum19_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And it will become an eternal law for them. And one who sprinkles
the water of impurity shall wash his clothes, and one who touches the
water of impurity will be impure until evening.

![image](images/Num_Chap19_21.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 19:22*](#Contents.html_rnum19_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And whatever the impure one will touch will be impure, and the
person who touches it will be impure until evening."

![image](images/Num_Chap19_22.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers20.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:1*](#Contents.html_rnum20_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **20** {.h2a}

^1^And the children of Israel, all the congregation, came to the
wilderness of Zin in the first month, and the people stayed in Kadesh.
And Miriam died there and was buried there.

![image](images/Num_Chap20_01.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:2*](#Contents.html_rnum20_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And there was no water for the congregation, and they assembled
against Moses and against Aaron.

![image](images/Num_Chap20_02.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:3*](#Contents.html_rnum20_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And the people quarreled with Moses, and they said, saying, "If only
we had expired when our brothers expired in front of YHWH!

![image](images/Num_Chap20_03.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:4*](#Contents.html_rnum20_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And why have you brought YHWH's community to this wilderness to die
there, we and our cattle?

![image](images/Num_Chap20_04.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:5*](#Contents.html_rnum20_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And why did you bring us up from Egypt to bring us to this bad place?
It's not a place of seed and fig and vine and pomegranate, and there's
no water to drink!"

![image](images/Num_Chap20_05.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:6*](#Contents.html_rnum20_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Moses and Aaron came from in front of the community to the
entrance of the Tent of Meeting, and they fell on their faces. And
YHWH's glory appeared to them.

![image](images/Num_Chap20_06.jpg){height="65" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:7*](#Contents.html_rnum20_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap20_07.jpg){height="22" width="205"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:8*](#Contents.html_rnum20_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^"Take the staff and assemble the congregation, you and Aaron, your
brother. And you shall speak to the rock before their eyes, and it will
give its water. So you shall bring water out of the rock for them and
give a drink to the congregation and their cattle."

![image](images/Num_Chap20_08.jpg){height="97" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

20:8. **Take the staff and ... speak to the rock**. Why does he need the
staff if he is only supposed to speak to the rock?! Some might say that
God is testing Moses. But the reason for taking the staff is already
given in the story of Aaron's blossoming staff. Moses is told there to
"put back Aaron's staff in front of the Testimony ... *for a sign to
rebels*" (Num 17:25). The text now says that Moses "took the staff from
in front of YHWH" (20:9). This expression would normally be expected to
mean that he took a staff that was located at the ark in the Tent of
Meeting, "in front of YHWH." William Propp has pointed out that this
connects back to the staff of Aaron that miraculously blossomed and was
placed before the ark in the preceding episode. As quoted above, its
purpose, explicitly, was to be "a sign to rebels." That is why Moses is
supposed to carry it in his hand in the people's sight while dealing
with their rebellion. As he holds it, his first words to the people are
in fact "Listen, rebels" (19:10). And that is why Aaron is implicated by
its misuse even though Aaron does not say or do anything at the rock. It
is his staff, with his name on it, to be kept in a holy place and used
in specific circumstances. As we have seen in Leviticus, intent is not
the issue in the realm of ritual. Rather, actions relating to sacred
objects and boundaries bring necessary consequences of themselves.
Whether or not Aaron has thought or done anything improper, he is tied
to what has happened.

20:8. **you shall speak**. It is a plural: both Moses and Aaron are
supposed to speak to the rock.

20:8. **rock**. This is a different word from the one in the other story
of Moses striking a rock to get water (Exod 17:1--7). There it is a crag
(*![image](images/sdot.jpg){height="11" width="6"}ûr*) of Mount Horeb
(Sinai). Here it is a rock (*sela'*) in the wilderness. In Moses' last
blessings to the people before he dies, he will speak of God as Israel's
*![image](images/sdot.jpg){height="11" width="6"}ûr* (Deut
32:4,15,18,30,31,37); i.e., he uses the word that recalls the
water-from-the-rock episode that came out well, not the present episode,
which comes out disastrously for himself and Aaron.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:9*](#Contents.html_rnum20_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Moses took the staff from in front of YHWH as He commanded him.

![image](images/Num_Chap20_09.jpg){height="44" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:10*](#Contents.html_rnum20_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And Moses and Aaron assembled the community opposite the rock. And
he said to them, "Listen, rebels, shall we bring water out of this rock
for you?"

![image](images/Num_Chap20_10.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:11*](#Contents.html_rnum20_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Moses lifted his hand and struck the rock with his staff twice.
And much water came out! And the congregation and their cattle drank.

![image](images/Num_Chap20_11.jpg){height="68" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

20:11. **water came out!** Moses is supposed to speak to the rock, but
he hits it. So why does the miracle still work?! We might surmise that
it means that God makes it happen so as not to humiliate Moses. Or the
explanation may be that the miraculous power is in the staff, which is
the one that miraculously blossomed. Whatever the reason, though, the
fact remains: for the first time in the Bible, a human has changed a
miracle. This is an all-important step in a gradual shift in the balance
of control of miraculous phenomena in the *Tanak.* Adam, Noah, Abraham,
and Isaac perform no miracles. But Jacob manipulates the coloring of
Laban's flock. Then Joseph interprets dreams. Then Moses and Aaron
perform God's miracles. And now Moses changes a miracle. This shift will
continue in the biblical books that follow the Torah, and it is one of
the central developments of the Bible: Joshua will call for the sun to
stand still in the skies. By calling for a miracle on his own, without
direction from God, he goes even further than Moses. Later, Samson has
powers implanted in him at birth, so that he is free to use them as he
wishes all his life. Later still, Elijah and Elisha use miracles for a
variety of personal purposes. It appears that, starting with Moses, God
is entrusting humans with ever more responsibility and control of their
destiny. By the end of the *Tanak,* miracles cease. There are no visible
miracles in the late books of Ezra, Nehemiah, and Esther. Humans rather
must direct their destiny with their own natural human powers, not with
endowments of miraculous powers from God. Through the course of the
*Tanak,* humans are forced to grow up and become more self-reliant.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:12*](#Contents.html_rnum20_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And YHWH said to Moses and to Aaron, "Because you did not trust in
me, to make me holy before the eyes of the children of Israel, therefore
you shall not bring this community to the land that I have given them!"

![image](images/Num_Chap20_12.jpg){height="93" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

20:12. **you did not trust in me, to make me holy**. What is Moses'
offense? What has he done that is so horrible as to deserve what is
probably the worst possible thing that could have been done to him: that
he will not live to enter the land? Many answers have been proposed to
the question of what the sin of Moses is. He calls the people rebels. He
says, "Shall *we* bring water out of this rock" instead of "Shall *God
...* " He strikes the rock instead of speaking to it. The answer may lie
in the wording of YHWH's reprimand: "You did not trust in me, to make me
holy." By hitting the rock Moses makes it appear more as if it is his
own agency that is []{#Numbers20.html_page_70}producing the water than
if he had just spoken to the rock and watched the water flow out. His
saying "we" has the same effect verbally that his physical action has
visually. True, YHWH had said to him in his instructions, "So *you*
shall bring water out," but Moses' saying it to the people before
hitting the rock still makes those words appear to mean something quite
different from their meaning in the original instructions. By word and
act Moses is thus appropriating to himself an act of God. In doing this
he is undoing the message that God and Moses himself have been conveying
to the people up to this point. The people have continuously directed
their attention to Moses instead of to God. In this story, as in the
others so far, they say to Moses and Aaron, "Why have *you* brought
YHWH's community to this wilderness ... ? Why did *you* bring us up from
Egypt?" Until this episode Moses has repeatedly told the people, "It is
not from my own heart" and "You are congregating against YHWH," but now
his words and actions confirm the people's own perception. He has come a
considerable way from his timid attempts to avoid the assignment at the
burning bush. At Meribah Moses oversteps a divine limit, and he pays a
maximal price. The severity of the punishment demonstrates the
seriousness of the offense.

20:12. **you shall not bring this community to the land**. In a way, the
sin of Moses is like the sin of Adam and Eve: stepping over the line,
taking a power from the divine realm. His punishment is not death. He
lives to be 120 years old, the maximum (as decreed in Gen 6:3); his
punishment is exile---he is not permitted to enter the land---which is
also like the punishment of Adam and Eve, who are prevented from
reentering Eden.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:13*](#Contents.html_rnum20_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^They are the waters of Meribah, over which the children of Israel
quarreled with YHWH, and He was made holy among them.

![image](images/Num_Chap20_13.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:14*](#Contents.html_rnum20_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Moses sent messengers from Kadesh to the king of Edom. "Your
brother, Israel, says this: You know all the hardship that has found us:

![image](images/Num_Chap20_14.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:15*](#Contents.html_rnum20_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And our fathers went down to Egypt, and we lived in Egypt many days.
And Egypt was bad to us and to our fathers,

![image](images/Num_Chap20_15.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:16*](#Contents.html_rnum20_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^and we cried to YHWH, and He heard our voice and sent an angel and
brought us from Egypt. And here we are in Kadesh, a city at the edge of
your border.

![image](images/Num_Chap20_16.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:17*](#Contents.html_rnum20_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^Let us pass through your land. We won't pass through a field or
through a vineyard, and we won't drink the water of a well. We'll go by
the king's road, we won't turn right or left, until we pass your
border."

![image](images/Num_Chap20_17.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:18*](#Contents.html_rnum20_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And Edom said to him, "You won't pass through me, or else I'll go
out to you with the sword."

![image](images/Num_Chap20_18.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:19*](#Contents.html_rnum20_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And the children of Israel said to him, "We'll go up by the highway,
and if we drink your water, I and my cattle, then I'll pay its price.
Only, it's nothing: let me pass through by foot."

![image](images/Num_Chap20_19.jpg){height="75" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:20*](#Contents.html_rnum20_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And he said, "You won't pass." And Edom went out to him with a heavy
mass of people and a strong hand,

![image](images/Num_Chap20_20.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:21*](#Contents.html_rnum20_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^and Edom refused to allow Israel to pass through his border. And
Israel turned away from him.

![image](images/Num_Chap20_21.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:22*](#Contents.html_rnum20_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And they traveled from Kadesh, and the children of Israel, all the
congregation, came to Mount Hor.

![image](images/Num_Chap20_22.jpg){height="43" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:23*](#Contents.html_rnum20_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And YHWH said to Moses and to Aaron at Mount Hor, on the border of
the land of Edom, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap20_23.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:24*](#Contents.html_rnum20_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^"Let Aaron be gathered to his people, because he shall not come to
the land that I have given to the children of Israel, because you
rebelled against my word at the waters of Meribah.

![image](images/Num_Chap20_24.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

20:24. **you rebelled**. *You* rebelled---meaning Moses and Aaron! God
tells them that they themselves did exactly what they were supposed to
stop the people from doing. It is especially painful for Moses, who said
"Listen, rebels," to hear his God apply that word now to him. Leaders of
a congregation cannot violate the very instruction that they uphold and
teach to others.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:25*](#Contents.html_rnum20_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^Take Aaron and Eleazar, his son, and take them up Mount Hor,

![image](images/Num_Chap20_25.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:26*](#Contents.html_rnum20_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^and take off Aaron's clothes, and you shall put them on Eleazar, his
son. And Aaron will be gathered and die there."

![image](images/Num_Chap20_26.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:27*](#Contents.html_rnum20_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And Moses did as YHWH commanded him. And they went up to Mount Hor
before the eyes of all the congregation.

![image](images/Num_Chap20_27.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:28*](#Contents.html_rnum20_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And Moses took off Aaron's clothes and put them on Eleazar, his son.
And Aaron died there on the top of the mountain. And Moses and Eleazar
came down from the mountain.

![image](images/Num_Chap20_28.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 20:29*](#Contents.html_rnum20_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And all the congregation saw that Aaron had expired, and all the
house of Israel mourned Aaron thirty days.

![image](images/Num_Chap20_29.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers21.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 21:1*](#Contents.html_rnum21_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **21** {.h2a}

^1^And the Canaanite, the king of Arad, who lived in the Negeb, heard
that Israel was coming by the way of Atharim, and he fought against
Israel and took some of them prisoners.

![image](images/Num_Chap21_01.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 21:2*](#Contents.html_rnum21_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Israel made a vow to YHWH and said, "If you will *deliver* this
people into my hand, then I shall completely destroy their cities."

![image](images/Num_Chap21_02.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 21:3*](#Contents.html_rnum21_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And YHWH listened to Israel's voice and delivered the Canaanite, and
they completely destroyed them and their cities. And the name of the
place was called Hormah.

![image](images/Num_Chap21_03.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 21:4*](#Contents.html_rnum21_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And they traveled from Mount Hor by way of the Red Sea road to go
around the land of Edom. And the people's soul was getting short on the
way.

![image](images/Num_Chap21_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

21:4. **the Red Sea**. Hebrew *yam sûp.* People argue that the sea that
splits in Exodus 14 is not the Red Sea but rather the "Reed Sea," an
otherwise unknown body of water. But the reference to the *yam sûp*
here, when the Israelites are no longer anywhere near Egypt, must refer
to the eastern arm of the Red Sea, which is the only body of water that
extends both up into Egypt and to a location far away in the Sinai. See
my comment on Exod 13:8.

21:4. **the people's soul was getting short**. The image is of getting
short of breath or short of temper. The word here stands in contrast to
its last occurrence in the Torah: when the people cried for meat, and
Moses doubted whether so much meat could be provided, God said, "Is
YHWH's hand getting short?!" (Num 11:23). Now they are crying for food
again, and it is the people's soul or breath that is "getting short."
And all of this is doubly ironic because back in Egypt the people were
unable to respond to Moses' announcement of their coming freedom owing
to "shortage of spirit" (![image](images/Num_Chap21_24a.jpg){height="19"
width="58"}---Exod 6:9). Freedom has not produced a speedy
transformation of their nature. When faced with stress they think of
returning to Egypt, which now looks attractive even though their
condition there was described with the same term.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 21:5*](#Contents.html_rnum21_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And the people spoke against God and against Moses: "Why did you
bring us up from Egypt to die in the wilderness? Because there's no
bread, and there's no water, and our soul is disgusted with the cursed
bread."

![image](images/Num_Chap21_05.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

21:5. **the people spoke against God and against Moses**. The notable
new element in their complaint this []{#Numbers21.html_page_72}time is
that they finally seem to have assimilated the fact that it is not
merely Moses but God who is directing them. What made them finally see
this? It certainly appears to be the aftermath of the events at Meribah.
When Moses stopped saying "It's God, not I," and just one time said
instead "Shall *we* bring water out," he paid such a terrible price that
the people have now seen the proof that it is God, and not just Moses,
who controls their fortunes.

21:5. **Why did you bring us up**. The word "you" here is plural,
further reflecting that the people are, for once, not directing their
entire complaint against Moses. They are at least beginning to
acknowledge the divine power in what happened in Egypt. Even though it
comes in the context of a complaint, this is still a step for the
people, who have, all along, been unable to accept the terrifying wonder
of the closeness of divinity.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 21:6*](#Contents.html_rnum21_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And YHWH let fiery snakes go among the people, and they bit the
people, and a great many people from Israel died.

![image](images/Num_Chap21_06.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 21:7*](#Contents.html_rnum21_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And the people came to Moses, and they said, "We've sinned, because
we spoke against YHWH and against you. Pray to YHWH, that He will turn
the snake away from us." And Moses prayed on behalf of the people.

![image](images/Num_Chap21_07.jpg){height="96" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 21:8*](#Contents.html_rnum21_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
