Zur. He was head of the people of a father's house in Midian.

![image](images/Num_Chap25_15.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 25:16*](#Contents.html_rnum25_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap25_16.jpg){height="44" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 25:17*](#Contents.html_rnum25_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^"Afflict the Midianites---and you shall strike them---

![image](images/Num_Chap25_17.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 25:18*](#Contents.html_rnum25_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^because they have been afflicting you with their conspiracies that
they made against you over the matter of Peor and over the matter of
Cozbi, the daughter of a chieftain of Midian, their sister who was
struck on the day of the plague over the matter of Peor."

![image](images/Num_Chap25_18.jpg){height="90" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 25:19*](#Contents.html_rnum25_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And it was after the plague.

![image](images/Num_Chap25_19.jpg){height="22" width="177"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers26.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:1*](#Contents.html_rnum26_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **26** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH said to Moses and to Eleazar son of Aaron, the priest,
saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap26_01.jpg){height="46" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:2*](#Contents.html_rnum26_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Add up the heads of all of the congregation of Israel from twenty
years old and up, by their fathers' house, everyone going out to
Israel's army."

![image](images/Num_Chap26_02.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:3*](#Contents.html_rnum26_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Moses and Eleazar, the priest, spoke with them in the plains of
Moab by the Jordan toward Jericho, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap26_03.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:4*](#Contents.html_rnum26_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^"From twenty years old and up," as YHWH commanded Moses and the
children of Israel who had gone out from the land of Egypt.

![image](images/Num_Chap26_04.jpg){height="68" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:5*](#Contents.html_rnum26_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^Reuben, Israel's firstborn; the children of Reuben:

Hanoch: the family of the Hanochites. Of Pallu: the family of the
Palluites.

![image](images/Num_Chap26_05.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:6*](#Contents.html_rnum26_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^Of Hezron: the family of the Hezronites. Of Carmi: the family of the
Carmites.

![image](images/Num_Chap26_06.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:7*](#Contents.html_rnum26_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^These are the families of the Reubenites, and their counts were
forty-three thousand seven hundred thirty.

![image](images/Num_Chap26_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:8*](#Contents.html_rnum26_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And Pallu's sons: Eliab.

![image](images/Num_Chap26_08.jpg){height="22" width="138"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:9*](#Contents.html_rnum26_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Eliab's sons: Nemuel and Dathan and Abiram. That is the Dathan
and Abiram, prominent ones of the congregation, who fought against Moses
and against Aaron in Korah's congregation when they fought against YHWH,

![image](images/Num_Chap26_09.jpg){height="92" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:10*](#Contents.html_rnum26_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^and the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them and Korah when the
congregation died, when the fire consumed the two hundred fifty men, and
they became a sign.

![image](images/Num_Chap26_10.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:11*](#Contents.html_rnum26_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^But Korah's sons did not die.

![image](images/Num_Chap26_11.jpg){height="22" width="164"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

26:11. **Korah's sons did not die**. Indeed, their descendants later
become the composers or singers of psalms in the Temple (see Pss 42; 44;
45; 46; 47; 48; 49; 84; 85; 87; 88), and they are mentioned in an
inscription on a bowl from a temple excavated at Arad that functioned
during the preexilic biblical period. So, if they were known in Israel,
why was it necessary to mention the obvious fact that they did not die?
It may be to distinguish them from the children of Dathan and Abiram.
The wives and children of Dathan and Abiram die with them (Num
16:27,32). Why do Korah's children not die with Korah? Korah's ritual
offense is the improper burning of incense. His 250 followers commit
this offense as well. And so all those who have had contact with the
forbidden incense are killed. There is no suggestion that their sons
play any part or come into contact with the incense. But Dathan and
Abiram's ritual offense is different. It is the direct rejection of
Moses' and God's authority: "These people have rejected YHWH" (Num
16:30). All those who stand with them in their refusal to respond to
Moses (Num 16:27b) participate in the sin and suffer the consequence.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:63*](#Contents.html_rnum26_63)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^63^These are the counts of Moses and Eleazar, the priest, who counted
the children of Israel in the plains of Moab by the Jordan toward
Jericho;

![image](images/Num_Chap26_63.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:64*](#Contents.html_rnum26_64)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^64^and there was not a man among these from the counts of Moses and
Aaron, the priest, who counted the children of Israel in the wilderness
of Sinai,

![image](images/Num_Chap26_64.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

26:64. **there was not a man among these from the counts of Moses and
Aaron**. The main result of this second census is the confirmation that
YHWH's condemnation of the exodus generation has come true. Because of
their rejection of the promised land in the spies episode, all the
adults of that generation have died. The exceptions are Joshua and
Caleb, the two scouts who remained faithful, and Moses. Moses' death
will be told at the end of the Torah, and only Caleb and Joshua will
live to lead the new generation into the promised land.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 26:65*](#Contents.html_rnum26_65)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^65^because YHWH said of them, "They shall *die* in the wilderness," and
not a man was left of them except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son
of Nun.

![image](images/Num_Chap26_65.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers27.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:1*](#Contents.html_rnum27_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **27** {.h2a}

^1^And the daughters of Zelophehad son of Hepher son of Gilead son of
Machir son of Manasseh, of the families of Manasseh son of Joseph, came
forward. And these are his daughters' names: Mahlah, Noah, and Hoglah
and Milcah and Tirzah.

![image](images/Num_Chap27_01.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:2*](#Contents.html_rnum27_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And they stood in front of Moses and in front of Eleazar, the priest,
and in front of the chieftains and all the congregation at the entrance
of the Tent of Meeting, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap27_02.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:3*](#Contents.html_rnum27_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^"Our father died in the wilder[]{#Numbers27.html_page_91}ness. And he
wasn't among the congregation who were gathered against YHWH: in Korah's
congregation. Rather, he died through his own sin. And he had no sons.

![image](images/Num_Chap27_03.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:4*](#Contents.html_rnum27_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^Why should our father's name be subtracted from among his family
because he didn't have a son? Give us a possession among our father's
brothers."

![image](images/Num_Chap27_04.jpg){height="75" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

27:4. **Why should our father's name be subtracted**. They do not make
their case in terms of their own rights or of women's rights in general.
They put it in terms of their father, that his name would be eliminated
from the territorial holdings of Israel. The issue is the one that
arises in the law of the jubilee. Land is unalienable. It is to be
distributed among the Israelites when they arrive in the land. If a man
ever loses his land, it is returned to him in the jubilee year. If he
has died by then, it is returned to his sons. But the property in his
name stays in the family forever. Zelophehad's daughters now protest
that the property that would have gone to their father will go to his
brothers instead, and the family holding in his name will be lost.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:5*](#Contents.html_rnum27_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Moses brought their case forward in front of YHWH.

![image](images/Num_Chap27_05.jpg){height="45" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:6*](#Contents.html_rnum27_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And YHWH said to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap27_06.jpg){height="22" width="213"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:7*](#Contents.html_rnum27_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^"Zelophehad's daughters speak right. You *shall* give them a
possession for a legacy among their father's brothers, and you shall
pass their father's legacy to them.

![image](images/Num_Chap27_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

27:7. **Zelophehad's daughters speak right**. See the comment on Num
36:5.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:8*](#Contents.html_rnum27_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And you shall speak to the children of Israel, saying, 'A man who
will die and not have a son: you shall pass his legacy to his daughter.

![image](images/Num_Chap27_08.jpg){height="49" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

27:8. **A man who will die and not have a son: you shall pass his legacy
to his daughter**. This judgment is an important step in the development
of women's rights, but its message is mixed. On one hand, it says that
women can inherit property, and their right of inheritance precedes the
rights of their father's male siblings or any other male relatives who
are more distantly related to their father than the women themselves. On
the other hand, this applies only if their father had no sons. If a
father has even one son and ten daughters, the son inherits the family
land. The daughters are dependent on that brother or on their husbands
for property. (This matter of women's inheritance will become even more
complex in the last chapter of Numbers; see the comments on Num 36:3;
36:5; and 36:6.) There is little point in debating whether this step
means that the Torah is supportive of women, on the grounds that it
provides for them to inherit, or whether it means that the Torah is
unfair to women, on the grounds that sons still precede daughters. The
fact is that social transformations take time: generations, centuries,
even millennia. The Torah does not command a revolution in the status of
women. It provides for steps such as this one, which in the short run
established that women do have rights, and which ultimately participated
in the development of women's rights more generally. We can praise the
Bible for how far it went, or we can be critical that it did not go
farther. But we would do better to examine how far it went in its age,
and how much this contributed to the transformation in the balance
between men and women in the millennia that followed. The larger point
is the same that I made with regard to slavery: The Torah does not
forbid it and attempt to bring it to an end overnight. It rather gives
laws of treatment of slaves---which involved granting respect, rights,
and compassion for slaves. And this eventually undermined slavery as an
institution. The diminution of slavery and the increase of women's
rights are two of the major developments of the past century. The
Torah's laws played an early and determinative part in birthing and
nurturing both of these revolutions.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:9*](#Contents.html_rnum27_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And if he does not have a daughter, then you shall give his legacy to
his brothers.

![image](images/Num_Chap27_09.jpg){height="22" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:10*](#Contents.html_rnum27_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And if he does not have brothers, then you shall give his legacy to
his father's brothers.

![image](images/Num_Chap27_10.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:11*](#Contents.html_rnum27_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And if there are no brothers of his father, then you shall give his
legacy to his relative who is closest to him of his family, and he shall
possess it.' And it shall become a law of judgment for the children of
Israel, as YHWH commanded Moses."

![image](images/Num_Chap27_11.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

27:11. **law of judgment**. This term is used twice in the Torah: here
for cases of inheritance, and later for cases involving cities of refuge
(Num 35:29). In both occurrences it involves the stating of a legal
procedure for deciding cases---civil procedure in this instance, and
criminal procedure in the later instance---which judges will then apply
to the particular cases before them.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:12*](#Contents.html_rnum27_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And YHWH said to Moses, "Go up to this mountain of Abarim and see
the land that I've given to the children of Israel.

![image](images/Num_Chap27_12.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:13*](#Contents.html_rnum27_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And you'll see it, and then you'll be gathered to your people, you
as well, as Aaron, your brother, was gathered,

![image](images/Num_Chap27_13.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:14*](#Contents.html_rnum27_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^because you rebelled against my word in the wilderness of Zin in the
congregation's quarrel to make me holy with water before their eyes.
That is the water of Meribah of Kadesh at the wilderness of Zin."

![image](images/Num_Chap27_14.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:15*](#Contents.html_rnum27_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Moses spoke to YHWH, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap27_15.jpg){height="22" width="230"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

27:15. **Moses spoke to YHWH**. This is the last time that Moses is
quoted as saying anything to God. In Deuteronomy Moses will report to
the people his *past* conversations with God, but *these are the last
words of Moses to God in the Torah.* And his last concern is: the
*people*---that they should have a leader. And note that
[]{#Numbers27.html_page_95}all of this draws us back to Moses' first
meeting with God, at the burning bush. God begins the charge to Moses
there by expressing concern for the people: "I've *seen* the degradation
of my people" (Exod 3:7). So God's first charge to Moses and Moses' last
request of God are both about the good of the people. And the connection
to Moses' origins is underscored by Moses' choice of metaphor here. The
episode at the bush begins: "Moses had been shepherding the flock" (Exod
3:1); and now Moses asks for a leader so the people won't be "like sheep
that don't have a shepherd" (Num 27:17).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:16*](#Contents.html_rnum27_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^"Let YHWH, God of the spirits of all flesh, appoint a man over the
congregation

![image](images/Num_Chap27_16.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:17*](#Contents.html_rnum27_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^who will go out in front of them and who will come in in front of
them and who will bring them out and who will bring them in, so YHWH's
congregation won't be like sheep that don't have a shepherd."

![image](images/Num_Chap27_17.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:18*](#Contents.html_rnum27_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And YHWH said to Moses, "Take Joshua son of Nun, a man with spirit
in him, and lay your hand on him.

![image](images/Num_Chap27_18.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:19*](#Contents.html_rnum27_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And you shall stand him in front of Eleazar, the priest, and in
front of all the congregation, and you shall command him before their
eyes.

![image](images/Num_Chap27_19.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:20*](#Contents.html_rnum27_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And you shall put some of your eminence on him so that all the
congrega[]{#Numbers27.html_page_92}tion of the children of Israel will
hear.

![image](images/Num_Chap27_20.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:21*](#Contents.html_rnum27_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And he shall stand in front of Eleazar, the priest; and he shall ask
him in judgment of the Urim in front of YHWH. By his word they shall go
out, and by his word they shall come in, he and all the children of
Israel with him and all the congregation."

![image](images/Num_Chap27_21.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:22*](#Contents.html_rnum27_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And Moses did as YHWH commanded him, and he took Joshua and stood
him in front of Eleazar, the priest, and in front of all the
congregation.

![image](images/Num_Chap27_22.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 27:23*](#Contents.html_rnum27_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And he laid his hands on him and commanded him, as YHWH had spoken
by Moses' hand.

![image](images/Num_Chap27_23.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

27:23. **he laid his hands**. God used the singular: "Lay your *hand.*"
But Moses lays both hands on Joshua. It is as if he wants to make the
appointment of his successor as visible and unmistakable as possible.
And he indicates in this way that this appointment is his own choice as
well. He fulfills the divine command with one hand. The second hand is
his own affirmation of Joshua, which bolsters Joshua's appointment and
preempts anyone from criticizing Joshua later as not having been Moses'
choice. The successor to a great leader is always in a vulnerable
position, and it is a gracious act by the great leader to support that
successor.

[]{#Numbers28.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 28:1*](#Contents.html_rnum28_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **28** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap28_01.jpg){height="23" width="241"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 28:2*](#Contents.html_rnum28_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Command the children of Israel, and you shall say to them: You shall
observe to bring forward my sacrifice to me, my bread, for my offering
by fire, my pleasant smell, at its appointed time.

![image](images/Num_Chap28_02.jpg){height="70" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:2. **Command the children of Israel**. Numbers 28 and 29 contain
commands concerning sacrifices and holidays. Much of this has been
treated already. There is a notable difference here, though: nowhere in
these two chapters is there a mention of the Tabernacle (or Tent of
Meeting). Other laws clearly indicate that sacrifice can be performed
only at the entrance of the Tabernacle. If I was correct in my
commentary on the Tabernacle (Exodus 26)---that the Tabernacle was
actually located in the First Temple---then it is critically important
that these laws come now picturing sacrifice even without reference to
the Tabernacle. By no reckoning was the Tabernacle in the Second Temple.
(Nor were the ark, the tablets, the cherubs, or the Urim and Tummin.) It
is these two chapters (and Num 15:1--31, which likewise does not mention
the Tabernacle) that make it possible for there to be sacrificial
worship at the Second Temple.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 28:3*](#Contents.html_rnum28_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^"And you shall say to them: This is the offering by fire that you
shall bring forward to YHWH: two unblemished one-year-old lambs per day
as a continual burnt offering.

![image](images/Num_Chap28_03.jpg){height="71" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 28:4*](#Contents.html_rnum28_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^You shall do one lamb in the morning, and you shall do the second
lamb 'between the two evenings.'

![image](images/Num_Chap28_04.jpg){height="66" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 28:5*](#Contents.html_rnum28_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And a tenth of an ephah of fine flour for a grain offering, mixed
with a fourth of a hin of pressed oil,

![image](images/Num_Chap28_05.jpg){height="47" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 28:6*](#Contents.html_rnum28_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^the continual burnt offering that was done at Mount Sinai for a
pleasant smell, an offering by fire to YHWH.

![image](images/Num_Chap28_06.jpg){height="51" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 28:7*](#Contents.html_rnum28_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And its libation shall be a fourth of a hin for one lamb. Pour a
libation of beer in the holy place for YHWH.

![image](images/Num_Chap28_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers28.html_page_96}28:7. **beer**. I have consistently
translated Hebrew *yayin* and *š![Image](images/e1.jpg){height="11"
width="7"}k![Image](images/a-image.jpg){height="11" width="8"}r* as
"wine" and "beer," respectively, because wine and beer are the alcoholic
beverages of the ancient Near East. Still, I admit that a libation of
beer here presents a difficulty because beer involves fermentation, and
normally fermented substances are not used in the holy place. Thus the
priests eat the meat of their sacrifices on unleavened bread. The
translation "beer" is therefore uncertain in this passage (and possibly
in other occurrences as well).

[]{#Numbers29.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 29:1*](#Contents.html_rnum29_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **29** {.h2a}

^1^"And in the seventh month on the first of the month you shall have a
holy assembly. You shall not do any act of work. You shall have a day of
horn-blasting.

![image](images/Num_Chap29_01.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:1. **seventh month on the first of the month**. This is the holiday
that later came to be known as Rosh Hashanah, the New Year. It is never
called by that name in the Torah, where it is rather counted as the
seventh month.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 29:2*](#Contents.html_rnum29_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

bull of the cattle, one ram, seven unblemished one-year-old lambs,

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

a bull, two tenths for a ram,

^27^and their grain offering and their libations for the bulls, for the
rams, and for the lambs by their number as required,

[*Numbers 29:28*](#Contents.html_rnum29_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

offering and its grain offering and its libation.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 29:29*](#Contents.html_rnum29_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

one-year-old lambs

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 29:31*](#Contents.html_rnum29_31)

[*Numbers 29:32*](#Contents.html_rnum29_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^"And on the seventh day: seven bulls, two rams, fourteen unblemished
one-year-old lambs

![image](images/Num_Chap29_32.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 29:33*](#Contents.html_rnum29_33)

^33^and their grain offering and their libations for the bulls, for the
rams, and for the lambs by their number according to their requirement,

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 29:34*](#Contents.html_rnum29_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^and one goat for a sin offering, aside from the continual burnt

[*Numbers 29:35*](#Contents.html_rnum29_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^"On the eighth day you shall have a convocation. You shall not do

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 29:36*](#Contents.html_rnum29_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And you shall bring forward a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a

![image](images/Num_Chap29_36.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^their grain offering and their libations for the bull, for the ram,
and for the lambs by their number as required,

[]{#Numbers30.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:1*](#Contents.html_rnum30_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **30** {.h2a}

^1^And Moses said it to the children of Israel, according to everything
that YHWH commanded Moses.

![image](images/Num_Chap30_01.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:2*](#Contents.html_rnum30_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## T[RIBES]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

^2^And Moses spoke to the heads of the tribes of the children of Israel,
saying, "This is the thing that YHWH commanded:

![image](images/Num_Chap30.jpg){height="22" width="60"}

![image](images/Num_Chap30_02.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:3*](#Contents.html_rnum30_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^A man who will make a vow to YHWH or has sworn an oath to make a
restriction on himself shall not desecrate his word. He shall do it
according to everything that comes out of his mouth.

![image](images/Num_Chap30_03.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:4*](#Contents.html_rnum30_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^"And a woman who will make a vow to YHWH and made a restriction in
her father's house in her youth,

![image](images/Num_Chap30_04.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:5*](#Contents.html_rnum30_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^and her father hears her vow and her restriction that she made on
herself, and her father keeps quiet to her, then all her vows shall
stand, and every restriction that she made on herself shall stand.

![image](images/Num_Chap30_05.jpg){height="94" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:6*](#Contents.html_rnum30_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^But if her father held her back on the day he heard all her vows and
her restrictions that she made on herself, it will not stand. And YHWH
will forgive her because her father held her back.

![image](images/Num_Chap30_06.jpg){height="75" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:7*](#Contents.html_rnum30_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^"And if she will have a husband while her vows or the thing she let
out of her lips with which she restricted herself are on her,

![image](images/Num_Chap30_07.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:8*](#Contents.html_rnum30_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^and her husband hears and keeps quiet on the day he heard it, then
her vows will stand, and her restrictions that she made on herself will
stand.

![image](images/Num_Chap30_08.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:9*](#Contents.html_rnum30_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^But if, on the day her husband hears it, he will hold her back and
will break her vow that is on her and what was let out of her lips by
which she restricted herself, then YHWH will forgive her.

![image](images/Num_Chap30_09.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:10*](#Contents.html_rnum30_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^"And a widow's or divorced woman's vow, everything by which she has
restricted herself, shall stand regarding her.

![image](images/Num_Chap30_10.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:11*](#Contents.html_rnum30_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^"And if she vowed or made a restriction on herself by an oath at her
husband's house,

![image](images/Num_Chap30_11.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:12*](#Contents.html_rnum30_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^and her husband heard it and kept quiet to her---he did not hold her
back---then all her vows shall stand, and every restriction that she
made on herself shall stand.

![image](images/Num_Chap30_12.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:13*](#Contents.html_rnum30_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^But if her husband broke them on the day he heard all that came out
of her lips for her vows and for restricting herself, it shall not
stand. Her husband had broken them, and YHWH will forgive her.

![image](images/Num_Chap30_13.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:14*](#Contents.html_rnum30_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^Every vow and every oath of restriction to degrade oneself: her
husband shall make it stand, and her husband shall break it.

![image](images/Num_Chap30_14.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:15*](#Contents.html_rnum30_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And if her husband will keep *quiet* in regard to her from that day
to the next, then he has made all her vows and all her restrictions that
are on her stand. He has made them stand because he kept quiet to her on
the day he heard it.

![image](images/Num_Chap30_15.jpg){height="93" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:16*](#Contents.html_rnum30_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And if he will *break* them after he has heard them, then he shall
bear her crime."

![image](images/Num_Chap30_16.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:16. **he shall bear her crime**. Having power over another person
also means taking responsibility for that person's actions. If a man
causes his wife to violate a vow she made, then it is he who must live
with the consequences.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 30:17*](#Contents.html_rnum30_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^These are the laws that YHWH commanded Moses between a man and his
wife, between a father and his daughter in her youth at her father's
house.

![image](images/Num_Chap30_17.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:17. **between a man and his wife, between a father and his
daughter**. This law, like several others in the Torah, stands at a
juncture in the development of the balance between women and men. On one
hand, it accepts the fact that men are in a position of such power over
women that they can prevent their daughters and
[]{#Numbers30.html_page_97}wives from keeping vows that they have made
to their God---and it gives this power the force of law. But, on the
other hand, it places a tremendous burden of responsibility on the men.
If they dare to prevent their daughters and wives from keeping these
vows, they bear the guilt themselves for whatever comes of it.

[]{#Numbers31.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:1*](#Contents.html_rnum31_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **31** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_01.jpg){height="22" width="261"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:2*](#Contents.html_rnum31_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Get revenge for the children of Israel from the Midianites. After
that you'll be gathered to your people."

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_02.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:3*](#Contents.html_rnum31_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And Moses spoke to the people, saying, "Let people from you be
equipped for the army, so they'll be against Midian to put YHWH's
revenge in Midian.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_03.jpg){height="71" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:3. **YHWH's revenge**. God had said "*Israel*'s revenge \[revenge for
the children of Israel\]" (31:2), but Moses tells the people to get
*YHWH*'s revenge. Perhaps this is because the Israelites willingly
participated in the matter of Baal Peor, and so in their minds there is
no reason for revenge; therefore Moses tells them that it is for God.
But the deity sees the event at Baal Peor as an injury to the people,
not to God.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:4*](#Contents.html_rnum31_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^You shall send a thousand per tribe, a thousand
[]{#Numbers31.html_page_99}per tribe, for all Israel's tribes, to the
army."

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_04.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:5*](#Contents.html_rnum31_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And, from Israel's thousands, a thousand per tribe were delivered,
twelve thousand equipped for the army.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_05.jpg){height="71" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:6*](#Contents.html_rnum31_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Moses sent them, a thousand per tribe, to the army; them and
Phinehas son of Eleazar, the priest, to the army, with the equipment of
the Holy and the trumpets for blasting in his hand.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_06.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:7*](#Contents.html_rnum31_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And they made war on Midian as YHWH had commanded Moses. And they
killed every male.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_07.jpg){height="73" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:8*](#Contents.html_rnum31_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And they killed the kings of Midian---Evi and Rekem and Zur and Hur
and Reba, the five kings of Midian---over their corpses. And they killed
Balaam son of Beor by the sword.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_08.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:8. **they killed Balaam**. This is a shock. Balaam has been pictured
as a prophet who faithfully pronounces YHWH's words, who blesses Israel
and thus offends the Moabite king who hires him. Why is he killed? Moses
relates a few verses later that the Midianite women who seduced the
Israelites at Baal Peor did so at Balaam's direction: "Here, they came
to bring about a breach against YHWH by the children of Israel, at
Balaam's word, over the matter of Peor" (31:16). But there is no
indication of why Balaam would have done that. The ambiguity prods us to
imagine possibilities and conceive connections. One possibility is that
he gives the advice to the Moabites and Midianites, thinking that it
will do them no good, since he himself has
prophe[]{#Numbers31.html_page_98}sied God's words promising a bright
future for Israel. Another is that he himself has no affection for
Israel; he pronounces blessings for them because he is a true prophet
and faithfully says what he is told, but he still acts on his own to
injure them. These are mere speculations. The text only lets us know
that human motives are complex. And so we should be circumspect in
judging and admiring persons whom we think, by their words, to be great
benefactors of humankind.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:9*](#Contents.html_rnum31_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And the children of Israel took the women of Midian and their infants
prisoner, and they despoiled all their animals and all their livestock
and all their wealth.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_09.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:10*](#Contents.html_rnum31_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And they burned all their cities with their homes and all their
encampments in fire.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_10.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:11*](#Contents.html_rnum31_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And they took all the spoil and all the prey, human and animal.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_11.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:12*](#Contents.html_rnum31_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And they brought the prisoners and the prey and the spoil to Moses
and to Eleazar, the priest, and to the congregation of the children of
Israel, to the camp, to the plains of Moab, which is by the Jordan
toward Jericho.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_12.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:13*](#Contents.html_rnum31_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Moses and Eleazar, the priest, and all the congregation's
chieftains went out to them, outside the camp.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_13.jpg){height="78" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:14*](#Contents.html_rnum31_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And Moses was angry at the officers of the army, the chiefs of
thousands and the chiefs of hundreds who were coming from the army of
the war.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_14.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:15*](#Contents.html_rnum31_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And Moses said to them, "Did you keep every female alive?!

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_15.jpg){height="50" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:16*](#Contents.html_rnum31_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^Here, they came to bring about a breach against YHWH by the children
of Israel, at Balaam's word, over the matter of Peor, so there was the
plague in YHWH's congregation!

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_16.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:16. **plague**. The extraordinary severity of the command---to kill
all males, even infants, and all females who have had sexual relations
with men is due to the fact that this is about a *ritual* crime. The
plague is the indication of this. Plague is a divine response to ritual
crimes, such as trespass on the Holy (Num 8:19) and the events of the
Korah rebellion and its aftermath (Num 17:11--12). The harshness of
treatment for ritual crimes, including the execution of persons who had
no evil intent (including babies in this case), has been one of the
hardest elements of the Torah for people to comprehend in the past two
millennia. Since the destruction of the last Temple, we have lost the
feeling for holy zones and sacred boundaries. Whether we are better off
or worse, the fact remains that we are mystified and horrified by the
seeming barbarism of a massacre of women and children that Moses
commands here. On the crucial distinction between treatment of ritual
and ethical offenses, see the comments on Num 4:15; 16:32; 25:8.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:17*](#Contents.html_rnum31_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^So now, kill every male among the infants, and kill every woman who
has known a man for male intercourse.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_17.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:17. **kill every woman** ... If Moses' anger over the fact that they
have not killed the women and children is difficult to accept, how much
more amazing is the fact that the women in question are Midianite---like
Moses' wife! The command to attack the Midianites comes from God, and
the text does not state what Moses' personal reaction is. He must order
a war against his wife's people, with whom he once lived, whose priest
was his father-in-law and adviser. He married a Midianite woman, but now
he has all the other married Midianite women killed. The lack of any
comment about Moses' thoughts and feelings here is the most powerful
silence since the story of the near-sacrifice of Isaac, in which we were
told nothing of Abraham's heart. The Torah's way is to leave these
things unspoken, and thus to leave us to ponder them.

Remarkably, the fearful order to kill the women and children comes from
Moses himself. The text does not say whether it originates from God. To
conceive of Moses' thoughts, perhaps we must go back to the point of the
Midianite seductions at Baal Peor and start with the question of what
Moses might feel when he learns that women of his wife's people are
seducing the Israelites into heresy: shock, embarrassment, betrayal,
fury. What conversation can we imagine between Moses and Zipporah? What
humiliation might Zipporah suffer from the Israelites in the aftermath
of Baal Peor? How much are both Moses and Zipporah undermined? Moses'
command to eliminate the Midianite women can be conceived of as coming
from the depth of his outrage and pain.

Another point: The text never reports that Moses' order was carried out!
There is a mention of retaining the virgin women as captives (31:35) but
no mention of the execution of the women who have known men or of the
male infants. It is possible to imagine that they are released or
allowed to escape---and that Moses acquiesces in this. Alternatively,
perhaps it is not reported simply because it is so horrible to describe.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:18*](#Contents.html_rnum31_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^But all infants among the women who have not known a male's
inter-course: keep alive.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_18.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:19*](#Contents.html_rnum31_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And you, camp outside the camp for seven days: everyone who killed a
life and everyone who touched a corpse, do sin expiation on the third
day and on the seventh day, you and your prisoners.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_19.jpg){height="74" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:19. **do sin expiation**. Moses is directing them to follow the
procedure for persons who have had contact with the dead, which God had
instructed him earlier (Num 19:11--13).

31:19. **on the third day and on the seventh day**. Based on the law in
Num 19:12, the text here should say: "Do sin expiation on the third day,
and on the seventh day be pure." The term for "be pure" may have been
lost here by a scribe.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:20*](#Contents.html_rnum31_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And for every piece of clothing and every leather item and
everything made from goats and every wood item you shall do expiation."

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_20.jpg){height="47" width="336"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:21*](#Contents.html_rnum31_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And Eleazar, the priest, said to the men of the army who had come to
the war, "This is the law of the instruction that YHWH commanded Moses:

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_21.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:22*](#Contents.html_rnum31_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^Just the gold and the silver, the bronze, the iron, the tin, and the
[]{#Numbers31.html_page_100}lead,

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_22.jpg){height="43" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:23*](#Contents.html_rnum31_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^every thing that will go in fire, you shall pass through fire and
purify. It shall be expiated from sin just in water of impurity. And
everything that will not go in fire, you shall pass through water.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_23.jpg){height="74" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

31:23. **water of impurity**. See Num 19:9.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:24*](#Contents.html_rnum31_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And you shall wash your clothes on the seventh day, and you will be
pure, and after that you shall come to the camp."

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_24.jpg){height="51" width="346"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:25*](#Contents.html_rnum31_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And YHWH said to Moses, saying,

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_25.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:26*](#Contents.html_rnum31_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^"Add up the heads of the prey, the prisoners, human and animal, you
and Eleazar, the priest, and the heads of the fathers of the
congregation.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_26.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:27*](#Contents.html_rnum31_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And you shall split the prey between the warriors who went out to
the army and all the congregation.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_27.jpg){height="49" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:28*](#Contents.html_rnum31_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And you shall levy a tax for YHWH from the men of war who went out
to the army, one individual out of five hundred, from humans and from
cattle and from asses and from sheep.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_28.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:29*](#Contents.html_rnum31_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^You shall take from their half and you shall give to Eleazar, the
priest, YHWH's donation.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_29.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:30*](#Contents.html_rnum31_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^From the half of the children of Israel you shall take one share out
of fifty, from humans, from cattle, from asses, and from sheep, from all
the animals, and you shall give them to the Levites, who keep the watch
of YHWH's Tabernacle."

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_30.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:31*](#Contents.html_rnum31_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And Moses and Eleazar, the priest, did as YHWH commanded Moses.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_31.jpg){height="48" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:32*](#Contents.html_rnum31_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And the prey, over and above the spoil that the people of the army
had taken, was: six hundred seventy-five thousand sheep

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_32.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:33*](#Contents.html_rnum31_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^and seventy-two thousand cattle

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_33.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:34*](#Contents.html_rnum31_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^and sixty-one thousand asses

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_34.jpg){height="24" width="218"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:35*](#Contents.html_rnum31_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^and human beings---from the women who had not known male
intercourse---all the persons were thirty-two thousand.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_35.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:36*](#Contents.html_rnum31_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^So the half, the portion of those who went out in the army was: the
number of sheep was three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_36.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:37*](#Contents.html_rnum31_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^And the tax for YHWH from the sheep was six hundred seventy-five.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_37.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:38*](#Contents.html_rnum31_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^And the cattle were thirty-six thousand, and their tax for YHWH was
seventy-two.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_38.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:39*](#Contents.html_rnum31_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^And asses were thirty thousand five hundred, and their tax for YHWH
was sixty-one.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_39.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:40*](#Contents.html_rnum31_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^And human beings were sixteen thousand, and their tax for YHWH was
thirty-two persons.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_40.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:41*](#Contents.html_rnum31_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^And Moses gave the tax, a donation for YHWH, to Eleazar, the priest,
as YHWH commanded Moses.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_41.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:42*](#Contents.html_rnum31_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^And of the half for the children of Israel, which Moses had split
from the people who were serving in the army:

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_42.jpg){height="50" width="344"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:43*](#Contents.html_rnum31_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^and the congregation's half of the sheep was three hundred
thirty-seven thousand five hundred.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_43.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:44*](#Contents.html_rnum31_44)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^44^And the cattle were thirty-six thousand.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_44.jpg){height="22" width="224"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:45*](#Contents.html_rnum31_45)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^45^And asses were thirty thousand five hundred.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_45.jpg){height="22" width="249"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:46*](#Contents.html_rnum31_46)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^46^And human beings were sixteen thousand.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_46.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:47*](#Contents.html_rnum31_47)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^47^And Moses took from the half of the children of Israel one share out
of fifty, from humans and from animals, and gave them to the Levites,
who kept the watch of YHWH's Tabernacle, as YHWH commanded Moses.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_47.jpg){height="95" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:48*](#Contents.html_rnum31_48)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^48^And the officers who were over the thousands of the army came
forward to Moses, the chiefs of the
thou[]{#Numbers31.html_page_101}sands and the chiefs of the hundreds.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_48.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:49*](#Contents.html_rnum31_49)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^49^And they said to Moses, "Your servants have added up the heads of
the men of war who are in our hands, and not a man of them is lacking.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_49.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:50*](#Contents.html_rnum31_50)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^50^And we have made an offering for YHWH---whatever each man found: an
item of gold, an armlet or bracelet, a ring, an earring, or an
ornament---to make atonement for ourselves in front of YHWH."

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_50.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:51*](#Contents.html_rnum31_51)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^51^And Moses and Eleazar, the priest, took the gold from them, every
item of handiwork.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_51.jpg){height="69" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:52*](#Contents.html_rnum31_52)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^52^And all the gold of the donation that they donated to YHWH was
sixteen thousand seven hundred fifty shekels, from the chiefs of the
thousands and from the chiefs of the hundreds.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_52.jpg){height="74" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:53*](#Contents.html_rnum31_53)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^53^The men of the army each had kept as spoil what was his.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_53.jpg){height="45" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 31:54*](#Contents.html_rnum31_54)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^54^And Moses and Eleazar, the priest, took the gold from the chiefs of
the thousands and the hundreds and brought it to the Tent of Meeting, a
commemoration for the children of Israel in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Num_Chap31_54.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers32.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:1*](#Contents.html_rnum32_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **32** {.h2a}

^1^And the children of Reuben and the children of Gad had a great amount
of livestock, very substantial. And they saw the land of Jazer and the
land of Gilead, and here: the place was a place for livestock.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_01.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:2*](#Contents.html_rnum32_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And the children of Gad and the children of Reuben came and said to
Moses and to Eleazar, the priest, and to the chieftains of the
congregation, saying,

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_02.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:3*](#Contents.html_rnum32_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^"Ataroth and Dibon and Jazer and Nimrah and Heshbon and Elealeh and
Sebam and Nebo and Beon,

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_03.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:4*](#Contents.html_rnum32_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^the land that YHWH struck in front of the congregation of Israel:
it's livestock land, and your servants have livestock."

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:5*](#Contents.html_rnum32_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And they said, "If we've found favor in your eyes, let this land be
given to your servants for a possession. Don't have us cross the
Jordan."

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_05.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:6*](#Contents.html_rnum32_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And Moses said to the children of Gad and to the children of Reuben,
"Will your brothers go to war while you sit here?!

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_06.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:7*](#Contents.html_rnum32_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And why do you hold back the heart of the children of Israel from
crossing to the land that YHWH has given them?

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_07.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:8*](#Contents.html_rnum32_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^That's what your fathers did when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to
see the land.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_08.jpg){height="68" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:9*](#Contents.html_rnum32_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And they went up to the Wadi Eshcol and saw the land, but they held
back the heart of the children of Israel so as not to go to the land
that YHWH had given them.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_09.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:10*](#Contents.html_rnum32_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And YHWH's anger flared on that day, and he swore saying,

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_10.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:11*](#Contents.html_rnum32_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^'The people who came up from Egypt, from twenty years old and up,
won't see the land that I swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob,
because they didn't go after me completely,

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_11.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:12*](#Contents.html_rnum32_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^except Caleb son of Jephun[]{#Numbers32.html_page_102}neh, the
Kenizzite, and Joshua son of Nun because they went after YHWH
completely.'

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_12.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:13*](#Contents.html_rnum32_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And YHWH's anger flared at Israel, and he made them roam in the
wilderness forty years, until the end of all the generation who were
doing bad in YHWH's eyes.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_13.jpg){height="70" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:14*](#Contents.html_rnum32_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And, here, you've gotten up in your fathers' place, a group of
sinning people, to add more onto YHWH's flaring anger at Israel.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_14.jpg){height="74" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:15*](#Contents.html_rnum32_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^If you go back from behind Him, then He'll add more to leave them in
the wilderness, and you'll have destroyed all of this people!"

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_15.jpg){height="69" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:16*](#Contents.html_rnum32_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And they came over to him and said, "We'll build walls for our
livestock here, and cities for our infants.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_16.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:17*](#Contents.html_rnum32_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And *we* shall be equipped, ready in front of the children of Israel
until we've brought them to their place, and our infants will live in
fortified cities because of the residents of the land.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_17.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:18*](#Contents.html_rnum32_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^We won't go back to our houses until the children of Israel take
possession, each of his legacy.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_18.jpg){height="49" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:19*](#Contents.html_rnum32_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^Because we won't have a possession with them from the farther side
of the Jordan, because our possession has come to us from the eastward
side of the Jordan."

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_19.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:20*](#Contents.html_rnum32_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And Moses said to them, "If you'll do this thing: if you'll get
equipped for war in front of YHWH,

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_20.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:21*](#Contents.html_rnum32_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^and every one of you will cross the Jordan equipped in front of YHWH
until He dispossesses his enemies in front of him,

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_21.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:22*](#Contents.html_rnum32_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^and the land will be subdued in front of YHWH, then, after that,
you'll go back, and, from YHWH and from Israel, you'll be free, and this
land will become yours for a possession in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_22.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:23*](#Contents.html_rnum32_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^But if you won't do this, here, you've sinned to YHWH, and know that
your sin will find you.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_23.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------
