[*Numbers 32:24*](#Contents.html_rnum32_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^Build cities for your infants and fences for your flocks, and do
what has come out of your mouths."

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_24.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:25*](#Contents.html_rnum32_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And the children of Gad and the children of Reuben said to Moses,
saying, "Your servants will do as my lord commands.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_25.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:26*](#Contents.html_rnum32_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^Our infants, our women, our livestock, and all our animals will be
there in the cities of Gilead.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_26.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:27*](#Contents.html_rnum32_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And your servants will cross, everyone equipped for the army, in
front of YHWH for war as my lord speaks."

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_27.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:28*](#Contents.html_rnum32_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And Moses commanded Eleazar, the priest, and Joshua son of Nun and
the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the children of Israel for
them.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_28.jpg){height="71" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

32:28. **Moses commanded Eleazar and Joshua** ... Moses is now directing
his successors to handle a matter that will not arise until after Moses
himself is dead. This is the first indication that Moses has accepted
the reality of his condemnation to die and never set foot in the
promised land. So, even though this fact is never mentioned explicitly
in this episode, it stands as a powerful background to what is going on
in the foreground. And, at the same time, it is a lesson about the
preparation of a successor---and the humble acceptance that one's own
time of leadership must end and that someone else will take one's place.
It is a natural inclination to hope that one's successor will not be as
good as oneself. But a wise leader understands that it is in his or her
own interest to have a good successor. Thus one's accomplishments live
on and grow.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:29*](#Contents.html_rnum32_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And Moses said to them, "If the children of Gad and the children of
Reuben will cross the Jordan with you, everyone equipped for war, in
front of YHWH, and the land will be subdued in front of you, then you
shall give them the land of Gilead for a possession.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_29.jpg){height="117" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:30*](#Contents.html_rnum32_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^But if they will not cross equipped with you, then they will have
possessions among you in the land of Canaan."

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_30.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:31*](#Contents.html_rnum32_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And the children of Gad and the children of Reuben answered, saying,
"What YHWH has spoken to your servants: we shall do so.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_31.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:32*](#Contents.html_rnum32_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^We'll cross equipped in front of YHWH to the land of Canaan while
we'll have our legacy possession across the Jordan."

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_32.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:33*](#Contents.html_rnum32_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And Moses gave them, the children of Gad and the children of Reuben
and half of the tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph, the kingdom of Sihon,
king of the Amorites, and the kingdom of Og, king of Bashan, the land
with its cities, with the borders of the land's cities all around.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_33.jpg){height="116" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

32:33. **half of the tribe of Manasseh**. Some understand and translate
this as "the half-tribe of Manasseh,"
[]{#Numbers32.html_page_105}apparently thinking that Manasseh and
Ephraim are merely half-tribes because they are both traced to one
ancestor, Joseph. But that is wrong, because there is later a reference
to the nine and a half tribes on the west side of the Jordan (Num
34:13). Thus, as is commonly pictured on maps, Manasseh was split, half
on each side of the Jordan.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:34*](#Contents.html_rnum32_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And the children of Gad built Dibon and Ataroth and Aroer

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_34.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:35*](#Contents.html_rnum32_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^and Atroth-shophan and Jazer and Jogbehah

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_35.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:36*](#Contents.html_rnum32_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^and Beth-Nimrah and Beth-Haran, fortified cities and fences for
flocks.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_36.jpg){height="43" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:37*](#Contents.html_rnum32_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^And the children of Reuben built Heshbon and Elealeh and Kiriathaim

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_37.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:38*](#Contents.html_rnum32_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^and Nebo and Baal-meon---with changes of name---and Sibmah, and they
called the names of the cities that they built by new names.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_38.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:39*](#Contents.html_rnum32_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^And the children of Machir son of Manasseh went to Gilead and
captured it and dispossessed the Amorite who was in it.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_39.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:40*](#Contents.html_rnum32_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^And Moses gave Gilead to Machir son of Manasseh, and they lived in
it.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_40.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:41*](#Contents.html_rnum32_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^And Jair son of Manasseh went and captured their villages, and he
called them Havvoth-Jair.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_41.jpg){height="52" width="248"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 32:42*](#Contents.html_rnum32_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^And Nobah went and captured Kenath and its towns, and he called it
Nobah after his own name.

![Image](images/Num_Chap32_42.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers33.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:1*](#Contents.html_rnum33_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## T[RAVELS]{.smallcaps1} {.h201}

## **33** {.h2a}

^1^These are the travels of the children of Israel who went out from the
land of Egypt by their armies by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_01.jpg){height="48" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:2*](#Contents.html_rnum33_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And Moses wrote their stops for their travels by YHWH's word, and
these are their travels and their stops.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_02.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:3*](#Contents.html_rnum33_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And they traveled from Rameses in the first month on the fifteenth
day of the first month. On the day after the Passover, the children of
Israel went out with a high hand before the eyes of all Egypt.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_03.jpg){height="96" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:4*](#Contents.html_rnum33_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And the Egyptians were burying those whom YHWH had struck among them:
every firstborn. And YHWH had made judgments on their gods.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_04.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:5*](#Contents.html_rnum33_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And the children of Israel traveled from Rameses and camped in
Succoth.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_05.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:6*](#Contents.html_rnum33_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And they traveled from Succoth and camped in Etham, which is at the
edge of the wilderness.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_06.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:7*](#Contents.html_rnum33_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And they traveled from Etham and turned back toward Pi-hahiroth,
which is in front of Baal-Zephon: and they camped in front of Migdol.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_07.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:8*](#Contents.html_rnum33_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And they traveled from before Pi-hahiroth and passed through the sea
to the wilderness. And they went three days' journey in the wilderness
of Etham and camped in Marah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_08.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:9*](#Contents.html_rnum33_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And they traveled from Marah and came to Elim. And in Elim were
twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees. And they camped there.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_09.jpg){height="73" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:10*](#Contents.html_rnum33_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And they traveled from Elim and camped by the Red Sea.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_10.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:11*](#Contents.html_rnum33_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And they traveled from the Red Sea and camped in the wilderness of
Sin.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_11.jpg){height="43" width="185"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:12*](#Contents.html_rnum33_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And they traveled from the wilderness of Sin and camped in Dophkah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_12.jpg){height="21" width="244"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:13*](#Contents.html_rnum33_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And they traveled from Dophkah and camped in Alush.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_13.jpg){height="23" width="219"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:14*](#Contents.html_rnum33_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And they traveled from Alush and camped at Rephidim. And there was
no water there for the people to drink.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_14.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:15*](#Contents.html_rnum33_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And they traveled from Rephidim and camped in the wilderness of
Sinai.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_15.jpg){height="22" width="275"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:16*](#Contents.html_rnum33_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And they traveled from the wilderness of Sinai and camped at Kibroth
Hattaavah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_16.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:17*](#Contents.html_rnum33_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And they traveled from Kibroth Hattaavah and camped at Hazeroth.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_17.jpg){height="45" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:18*](#Contents.html_rnum33_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And they traveled from Hazeroth and camped in Rithmah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_18.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:19*](#Contents.html_rnum33_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And they traveled from Rithmah and camped at Rimmon-parez.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_19.jpg){height="23" width="234"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:20*](#Contents.html_rnum33_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And they traveled from Rimmon-parez and camped in Libnah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_20.jpg){height="23" width="228"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:21*](#Contents.html_rnum33_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And they traveled from Libnah and camped at Rissah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_21.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:22*](#Contents.html_rnum33_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And they traveled from Rissah and camped in Kehelathah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_22.jpg){height="24" width="231"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:23*](#Contents.html_rnum33_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And they traveled from Kehelathah and camped at Mount Shapher.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_23.jpg){height="22" width="237"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:24*](#Contents.html_rnum33_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And they traveled from Mount Shapher and camped in Haradah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_24.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:25*](#Contents.html_rnum33_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And they traveled from Haradah and camped in Makheloth.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_25.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:26*](#Contents.html_rnum33_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And they traveled from Makheloth and camped at Tahath.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_26.jpg){height="23" width="250"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:27*](#Contents.html_rnum33_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And they traveled from Tahath and camped at Tarah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_27.jpg){height="22" width="191"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:28*](#Contents.html_rnum33_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And they traveled from Tarah and camped in Mithkah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_28.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:29*](#Contents.html_rnum33_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And they went from Mithkah and camped in Hashmonah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_29.jpg){height="22" width="217"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:30*](#Contents.html_rnum33_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^And they traveled from Hashmonah and camped at Moseroth.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_30.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:31*](#Contents.html_rnum33_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And they traveled from Moseroth and camped in Bene-jaakan.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_31.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:32*](#Contents.html_rnum33_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And they traveled from Bene-jaakan and camped at Hor-haggidgad.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_32.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:33*](#Contents.html_rnum33_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^And they went from Hor-haggidgad and camped in Jotbathah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_33.jpg){height="22" width="274"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:34*](#Contents.html_rnum33_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And they traveled from Jotbathah and camped at Ebronah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_34.jpg){height="22" width="267"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:35*](#Contents.html_rnum33_35)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^35^And they traveled from Ebronah and camped at Ezion-geber.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_35.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:36*](#Contents.html_rnum33_36)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^36^And they traveled from Ezion-geber and camped in the wilderness of
Zin. That is Kadesh.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_36.jpg){height="47" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:37*](#Contents.html_rnum33_37)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^37^And they traveled from Kadesh and camped in Mount Hor, at the edge
of the land of Edom.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_37.jpg){height="48" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:38*](#Contents.html_rnum33_38)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^38^And Aaron, the priest, went up into Mount Hor at the word of YHWH
and died there in the fortieth year after the children of Israel came
out of the land of Egypt, in the fifth month, on the first day of the
month.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_38.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:39*](#Contents.html_rnum33_39)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^39^And Aaron was a hundred twenty-three years old when he died in Mount
Hor.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_39.jpg){height="47" width="278"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:40*](#Contents.html_rnum33_40)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^40^And the Canaanite, the king of Arad, who lived in the Negeb in the
land of Canaan, heard of the coming of the children of Israel.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_40.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:41*](#Contents.html_rnum33_41)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^41^And they traveled from Mount Hor and camped in Zalmonah.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_41.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:42*](#Contents.html_rnum33_42)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^42^And they traveled from Zalmonah and camped in Punon.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_42.jpg){height="22" width="208"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:43*](#Contents.html_rnum33_43)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^43^And they traveled from Punon and camped in Oboth.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_43.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:44*](#Contents.html_rnum33_44)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^44^And they traveled from Oboth and camped in Iyye-abarim, in the
border of Moab.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_44.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:45*](#Contents.html_rnum33_45)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^45^And they traveled from Iyyim and camped in Dibon-gad.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_45.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:46*](#Contents.html_rnum33_46)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^46^And they traveled from Dibon-gad and camped in Almon-diblathaim.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_46.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:47*](#Contents.html_rnum33_47)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^47^And they traveled from Almon-diblathaim and camped in the mountains
of Abarim in front of Nebo.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_47.jpg){height="48" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:48*](#Contents.html_rnum33_48)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^48^And they traveled from the mountains of Abarim and camped in the
plains of Moab by the Jordan toward Jericho.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_48.jpg){height="49" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:49*](#Contents.html_rnum33_49)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^49^And they camped by the Jordan from Beth-jeshimoth to Abel-shittim in
the plains of Moab.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_49.jpg){height="48" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:50*](#Contents.html_rnum33_50)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^50^And YHWH spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan toward
Jericho, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap33_50.jpg){height="49" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:51*](#Contents.html_rnum33_51)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^51^"Speak to the children of Israel, and you shall say to them: When
you cross the Jordan to the land of Canaan,

![image](images/Num_Chap33_51.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:52*](#Contents.html_rnum33_52)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^52^you shall dispossess all the residents of the land in front of you,
and destroy all their carved figures, and you shall destroy all their
molten images and demolish all their high places.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_52.jpg){height="73" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:53*](#Contents.html_rnum33_53)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^53^And you shall take possession of the land and live in it, because
I've given the land to you to possess it.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_53.jpg){height="72" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:54*](#Contents.html_rnum33_54)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^54^And you shall give legacies of the land by lot, by your families:
for the large you shall make its legacy larger, and for the small you
shall make its legacy smaller. Wherever the lot indicates for it shall
belong to it. You shall give legacies to the tribes of your fathers.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_54.jpg){height="98" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:55*](#Contents.html_rnum33_55)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^55^And if you don't dispossess the residents of the land in front of
you, then those whom you leave of them will become sticks in your eyes
and thorns in your sides, and they'll afflict you on the land in which
you're living.

![image](images/Num_Chap33_55.jpg){height="98" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 33:56*](#Contents.html_rnum33_56)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^56^And it will be that, as I meant to do to them, I'll do to you!"

![image](images/Num_Chap33_56.jpg){height="23" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers34.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:1*](#Contents.html_rnum34_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **34** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap34_01.jpg){height="22" width="268"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:2*](#Contents.html_rnum34_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Command the children of Israel, and you shall say to them: When you
come to the land of Canaan, this is the land that will fall to you as a
legacy, the land of Canaan by its borders:

![image](images/Num_Chap34_02.jpg){height="96" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:3*](#Contents.html_rnum34_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And the southern side for you shall be from the wilderness of Zin
next to Edom, and the southern border for you shall be from the edge of
the Dead Sea to the east.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_03.jpg){height="70" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:4*](#Contents.html_rnum34_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And the border for you shall turn from the south to the ascent of
Akrabim and will pass to Zin, and its extent shall be from the south to
Kadesh-barnea, and it shall go out to Hazar-addar and pass to Azmon.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:5*](#Contents.html_rnum34_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And the border shall turn from Azmon to the Wadi of Egypt, and its
extent shall be to the sea.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_05.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

34:5. **the wadi of Egypt**. This refers to the Wadi el-'Arîsh.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:6*](#Contents.html_rnum34_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And the western border: and the big sea shall be a border for you.
This shall be the western border for you.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_06.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

34:6. **the big sea**. The Mediterranean Sea.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:7*](#Contents.html_rnum34_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And this shall be the northern border for you: you shall mark from
the big sea to Mount Hor,

![image](images/Num_Chap34_07.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:8*](#Contents.html_rnum34_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^from Mount Hor you shall mark to the entrance of Hamath, and the
border's extent shall be to Zedad,

![image](images/Num_Chap34_08.jpg){height="45" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:9*](#Contents.html_rnum34_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^and the border shall go out to Ziphron, and its extent shall be to
Hazar-enan. This shall be the northern border for you.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_09.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:10*](#Contents.html_rnum34_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And you shall mark for the border to the east from Hazar-enan to
Shepham,

![image](images/Num_Chap34_10.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:11*](#Contents.html_rnum34_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^and the border shall go down from Shepham to Riblah at the east of
Ain, and the border shall go down and rub on the shoulder of the Sea of
Kinneret on the east,

![image](images/Num_Chap34_11.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

34:11. **Sea of Kinneret**. The Sea of Galilee.

34:11. **shoulder ... on the east**. On the east side of the Sea of
Galilee is a mountain wall, a "shoulder," known as the Golan Heights.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:12*](#Contents.html_rnum34_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^and the border shall go down to the Jordan, and its extent will be
to the Dead Sea. This shall be the land for you by its borders all
around."

![image](images/Num_Chap34_12.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

34:12. **the Dead Sea**. In Hebrew it is called "the Salt Sea."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:13*](#Contents.html_rnum34_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And Moses commanded the children of Israel, saying, "This is the
land that you shall give as legacies by lot, which YHWH commanded to
give to the nine tribes and the half of a tribe."

![image](images/Num_Chap34_13.jpg){height="95" width="339"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

34:13. **nine tribes and the half of a tribe**. The half of a tribe is
half of the tribe of Manasseh. The other half of Manasseh took land on
the east side of the Jordan along with the tribes of Reuben and Gad.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:14*](#Contents.html_rnum34_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^Because the tribe of the children of the Reubenites by their
fathers' house and the tribe of the children of the Gadites by their
fathers' house had taken and half of the tribe of Manasseh had taken
their legacy.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_14.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:15*](#Contents.html_rnum34_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^The two tribes and the half of a tribe had taken their legacy from
across the Jordan toward Jericho on the east side---to the east.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_15.jpg){height="63" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:16*](#Contents.html_rnum34_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap34_16.jpg){height="22" width="216"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:17*](#Contents.html_rnum34_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^"These are the names of the people who will give the land as
legacies to you: Eleazar, the priest, and Joshua son of Nun;

![image](images/Num_Chap34_17.jpg){height="71" width="340"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

34:17. **the names of the people**. Over and over, the tribes and their
leaders are named in the book of Numbers. From the wilderness period to
the time of the judges to the time of the monarchy, Israel is never
ruled by an individual political authority. There are always tribal
chiefs, councils of leaders, priests, judges, and military officers. An
individual human's rule is never absolute---not even for Moses, not even
for David.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:18*](#Contents.html_rnum34_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^and you shall take one chieftain for each tribe to give the land as
legacies.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_18.jpg){height="43" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:19*](#Contents.html_rnum34_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And these are the people's names: For the tribe of Judah, Caleb son
of Jephunneh.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_19.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:20*](#Contents.html_rnum34_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And for the tribe of the children of Simeon, Samuel son of Ammihud.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_20.jpg){height="46" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:21*](#Contents.html_rnum34_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^For the tribe of Benjamin, Elidad son of Chislon.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_21.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:22*](#Contents.html_rnum34_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And for the tribe of the children of Dan, a chieftain, Bukki son of
Jogli.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_22.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:23*](#Contents.html_rnum34_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^For the children of Joseph: for the tribe of the children of
Manasseh, a chieftain, Hanniel son of Ephod;

![image](images/Num_Chap34_23.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:24*](#Contents.html_rnum34_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^and for the tribe of the children of Ephraim, a chieftain, Kemuel
son of Shiphtan.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_23.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:25*](#Contents.html_rnum34_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And for the tribe of the children of Zebulun, a chieftain, Elizaphan
son of Parnach.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_24.jpg){height="46" width="336"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:26*](#Contents.html_rnum34_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And for the tribe of the children of Issachar, a chieftain, Paltiel
son of Azzan.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_25.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:27*](#Contents.html_rnum34_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And for the tribe of the children of Asher, a chieftain, Ahihud son
of Shelomi.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_27.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:28*](#Contents.html_rnum34_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^And for the tribe of the children of Naphtali, a chieftain, Pedahel
son of Ammihud."

![image](images/Num_Chap34_28.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 34:29*](#Contents.html_rnum34_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^These are the ones whom YHWH commanded to give the children of
Israel legacies in the land of Canaan.

![image](images/Num_Chap34_29.jpg){height="46" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Numbers35.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:1*](#Contents.html_rnum35_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **35** {.h2a}

^1^And YHWH spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan toward
Jericho, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap35_01.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:2*](#Contents.html_rnum35_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"Command the children of Israel that, from the legacy of their
possession, they shall give the Levites cities in which to live, and you
shall give the Levites surrounding land for the cities all around them.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_02.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:3*](#Contents.html_rnum35_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And the cities shall be theirs to live in, and their surrounding
lands shall be for their cattle and for their property and for their
animals.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_03.jpg){height="67" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:4*](#Contents.html_rnum35_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And the surrounding lands of the cities that you shall give to the
Levites: from the city wall outward, a thousand cubits all around.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_04.jpg){height="47" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:5*](#Contents.html_rnum35_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And you shall measure outside the city: on the eastern side two
thousand in cubits, and on the southern side two thousand in cubits, and
on the western side two thousand in cubits, and on the northern side two
thousand in cubits, and the city in the middle. This shall be the
surrounding lands of the cities for them.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_05.jpg){height="120" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:6*](#Contents.html_rnum35_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And the cities that you shall give to the Levites: six cities of
refuge that you shall give for the manslayer to flee there, and you
shall give in addition to them forty-two cities.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_06.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

35:6. **manslayer ... murderer**. These two words are both used to
translate the same Hebrew word throughout this chapter. In Hebrew the
word is the same for an unintentional manslayer and for a murderer. We
determine which is meant in each sentence by the context. In English we
have two words available, and so the appropriate word is used for each
case.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:7*](#Contents.html_rnum35_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^All the cities that you shall give to the Levites: forty-eight
cities, them and their surrounding lands.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_07.jpg){height="46" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:8*](#Contents.html_rnum35_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And the cities that you shall give from the possession of the
children of Israel: from the larger you shall give more, and from the
smaller you shall give fewer. Each shall give some of his cities to the
Levites according to his legacy that they will give."

![image](images/Num_Chap35_08.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:9*](#Contents.html_rnum35_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

![image](images/Num_Chap35_09.jpg){height="22" width="205"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:10*](#Contents.html_rnum35_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^"Speak to the children of Israel, and you shall say to them: When
you cross the Jordan to the land of Canaan,

![image](images/Num_Chap35_10.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:11*](#Contents.html_rnum35_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^you shall establish cities; they shall be cities of refuge for you,
and a manslayer who strikes a life by mistake shall flee there.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_11.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:12*](#Contents.html_rnum35_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And you shall have the cities for refuge from an avenger so the
manslayer will not die until he stands in front of the congregation for
judgment.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_12.jpg){height="47" width="341"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:13*](#Contents.html_rnum35_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And the cities that you shall give: You shall have six cities of
refuge.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_13.jpg){height="54" width="417"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:14*](#Contents.html_rnum35_14)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^You shall put three of the cities across the Jordan, and you shall
put three of the cities in the land of Canaan. They shall be cities of
refuge.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_14.jpg){height="76" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:15*](#Contents.html_rnum35_15)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^"These six cities shall be for refuge for the children of Israel and
for the alien and for the visitor among
[]{#Numbers35.html_page_111}them, for anyone who strikes a life by
mistake to flee there.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_15.jpg){height="86" width="417"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:16*](#Contents.html_rnum35_16)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^But if he struck him with an iron item so that he died, he is a
murderer. The murderer shall be put to *death.*

![image](images/Num_Chap35_16.jpg){height="57" width="415"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:17*](#Contents.html_rnum35_17)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And if he struck him with a stone in the hand by which one could
die, so that he died, he is a murderer. The murderer shall be put to
*death.*

![image](images/Num_Chap35_17.jpg){height="56" width="417"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:18*](#Contents.html_rnum35_18)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^Or if he struck him with a wooden object in the hand by which one
could die, so that he died, he is a murderer. The murderer shall be put
to *death.*

![image](images/Num_Chap35_18.jpg){height="86" width="417"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:19*](#Contents.html_rnum35_19)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^The blood avenger, he shall kill the murderer. When he comes upon
him, *he* shall kill him.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_19.jpg){height="56" width="416"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:20*](#Contents.html_rnum35_20)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And if he pushed him in hatred or threw something on him through
scheming so that he died,

![image](images/Num_Chap35_20.jpg){height="55" width="416"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:21*](#Contents.html_rnum35_21)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^or if he struck him with his hand in enmity so that he died, the one
who struck shall be put to *death.* He is a murderer. The blood avenger
shall kill the murderer when he comes upon him.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_21.jpg){height="85" width="417"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:22*](#Contents.html_rnum35_22)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^But if by chance, not in enmity, he pushed him or threw any object
on him not through scheming,

![image](images/Num_Chap35_22.jpg){height="83" width="417"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:23*](#Contents.html_rnum35_23)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^or with any stone by which one could die, without seeing, so that he
dropped it on him so that he died, and he was not an enemy to him and
was not seeking to harm him,

![image](images/Num_Chap35_23.jpg){height="86" width="416"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:24*](#Contents.html_rnum35_24)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^then the congregation shall judge between the one who struck and the
blood avenger on the basis of these judgments.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_24.jpg){height="56" width="417"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:25*](#Contents.html_rnum35_25)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And the congregation shall rescue the manslayer from the blood
avenger's hand, and the congregation shall bring him back to his city of
refuge to which he fled, and he shall live in it until the death of the
high priest whom one anointed with the holy oil.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_25.jpg){height="117" width="419"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

35:25. **the death of the high priest**. Why does the high priest's
death release him? It makes atonement for the crime. After that, the
person who killed by accident is innocent, and if a blood avenger would
kill him it would be murder.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:26*](#Contents.html_rnum35_26)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^But if the murderer will go *out* of the border of his city of
refuge to which he will flee,

![image](images/Num_Chap35_26.jpg){height="56" width="417"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:27*](#Contents.html_rnum35_27)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^and the blood avenger will find him outside of the border of his
city of refuge, and the blood avenger will murder the murderer, he does
not have blood,

![image](images/Num_Chap35_27.jpg){height="86" width="416"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

35:27. **he does not have blood**. Meaning: he does not have guilt for
shedding the blood of the person who killed someone by accident.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:28*](#Contents.html_rnum35_28)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^because he shall live in his city of refuge until the high priest's
death, and after the high priest's death the murderer shall go back to
the land of his possession.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_28.jpg){height="86" width="416"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:29*](#Contents.html_rnum35_29)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^"And these shall become a law of judgment for you through your
generations in all your homes.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_29.jpg){height="56" width="416"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

35:29. **law of judgment**. See the comment on Num 27:11.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:30*](#Contents.html_rnum35_30)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^Anyone who strikes a life, the murderer shall be murdered by the
mouth of witnesses. And one witness shall not testify against a person
so as to die.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_30.jpg){height="86" width="416"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

35:30. **one witness**. A person cannot be executed on the testimony of
only one witness. There must be two or more.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:31*](#Contents.html_rnum35_31)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^And you shall not take a ransom for a murderer's life, because he
did wrong so as to die, but he shall be put to *death.*

![image](images/Num_Chap35_31.jpg){height="56" width="417"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:32*](#Contents.html_rnum35_32)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^And you shall not take a ransom to flee to his city of refuge, to go
back to live in the land until the priest's death.

![image](images/Num_Chap35_32.jpg){height="86" width="416"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:33*](#Contents.html_rnum35_33)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^So you shall not pollute the land in which you are. Because blood:
it will pollute the land, and the land will not have expiation for blood
that is spilled in it except by the blood of the one who spilled it!

![image](images/Num_Chap35_33.jpg){height="78" width="337"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

35:33. **blood will pollute the land**. As conveyed at the beginning of
the Torah from the stories of Eden, Cain and Abel, and the flood, the
earth's environment suffers from human wrongdoing: "The ground is cursed
on your account." "You're cursed from the ground that opened its mouth
to take your brother's blood." "The earth was corrupted, because all
flesh had corrupted its way on the earth." Human violence and corruption
have consequences not only for the immediate victims, and not even only
for other humans, but for the condition of the earth and nature. Murder
is not only a sin against the victim. It is a sin against the earth.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 35:34*](#Contents.html_rnum35_34)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And you shall not make the land impure in which you are living, in
which I tent, because I, YHWH, tent among the children of Israel."

![image](images/Num_Chap35_34.jpg){height="116" width="417"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

35:34. **you shall not make the land impure**. As we have seen in other
instances, blood can make impure (for example, Lev 15:33), and it can
expiate impurity (for example, Lev 14:49--53). It can be contaminating,
and it can be protective. As blood can make a person impure, so
blood(shed) can render the *land* impure.

35:34. **I tent**. On God's tenting presence among humans, see the
comments on Exod 25:8 and Num 9:17.

[]{#Numbers36.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 36:1*](#Contents.html_rnum36_1)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **36** {.h2a}

^1^And the heads of the fathers of the family of the children of Gilead
son of Machir son of Manasseh, from the families of the children of
Joseph, came forward and spoke in front of Moses and in front of the
chieftains, heads of fathers of the children of Israel.

![image](images/Num_Chap36_01.jpg){height="95" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 36:2*](#Contents.html_rnum36_2)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And they said, "YHWH commanded my lord to give the land as legacy by
lot to the children of Israel, and my lord was commanded by YHWH to give
the legacy of Zelophehad, our brother, to his daughters.

![image](images/Num_Chap36_02.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 36:3*](#Contents.html_rnum36_3)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^If they will become wives to one of the sons of the tribes of the
children of Israel, then their legacy will be subtracted from our
fathers' legacy and added onto the legacy of the tribe to whom they will
belong, and it will be subtracted from our legacy by the lot.

![image](images/Num_Chap36_03.jpg){height="71" width="342"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

36:3. **their legacy will be subtracted**. Whenever a new law arises
that makes a social change, there will be consequences that will make
the matter complex. Earlier (27:4), Zelophehad's daughters protested
that since their father had no sons, his land would not be passed on.
And so, they said, "Why should our father's name be subtracted?" It was
therefore determined that, in such a situation, women can inherit their
father's land. But now their tribe (Manasseh) realizes that this leads
to a new problem. If these women marry men from other tribes, then their
land will pass by inheritance to the sons whom they will have. But
tribal identification goes by the father, not the mother.
(Identification in the Torah is always patrilineal; see the comment on
Lev 24:16,22.) And so their sons will be members of their fathers'
tribes, not members of Manasseh. And so some of the territory of
Manasseh would be subtracted from Manasseh and passed to another tribe.
(A contemporary analogy would be if parts of a state or country could be
transferred away to another state or country without the former having
any say in the matter.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 36:4*](#Contents.html_rnum36_4)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And if the children of []{#Numbers36.html_page_112}Israel will have
the jubilee, then their legacy will be added onto the legacy of the
tribe to whom they will belong, and their legacy will be subtracted from
the legacy of our fathers' tribe."

![image](images/Num_Chap36_04.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 36:5*](#Contents.html_rnum36_5)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And Moses commanded the children of Israel by YHWH's word, saying,
"The children of Joseph speak right.

![image](images/Num_Chap36_05.jpg){height="48" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

36:5. **The children of Joseph speak right**. These are the exact words
that Moses had said about the case that Zelophehad's daughters made:
"Zelophehad's daughters speak right" (Num 27:7). This case thus involves
a conflict between two legitimate positions. And so we must rely on
human judgment on questions of new consequences of laws. This requires
turning to those who have both learning and wisdom.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 36:6*](#Contents.html_rnum36_6)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^This is the thing that YHWH has commanded Zelophehad's daughters,
saying: They shall become wives for any in whose eyes it is good,
except: they shall become wives to the family of their father's tribe.

![image](images/Num_Chap36_06.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

36:6. **they shall become wives to the family of their father's tribe**.
The decision on the conflicting claims of Zelophehad's daughters and the
leaders of their tribe (Manasseh) results in a limitation on the rights
of women that were established in the original case. If there are no
sons, daughters may still inherit their father's property, but they must
then marry only men from their own tribe. If they choose to marry
outside their tribe, they lose their legacy, and it passes to their
father's brothers instead.

Beside the immediate issues of this case, it teaches a broader principle
of law as well. When any law is instituted, there may be situations and
consequences later that require a modification of the law. There must be
a mechanism for dealing with such situations. In the wilderness Moses
can turn directly to God for an answer. But later that is no longer
possible. And so we must rely on human judgment on questions of new
consequences of laws. This requires turning to those who have both
learning and wisdom.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 36:7*](#Contents.html_rnum36_7)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^So a legacy of the children of Israel will not turn from tribe to
tribe, but the children of Israel shall each cling to the legacy of his
fathers' tribe.

![image](images/Num_Chap36_07.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 36:8*](#Contents.html_rnum36_8)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And any daughter who inherits a legacy from the tribes of the
children of Israel shall become a wife to someone from a family of her
father's tribe, so that the children of Israel will each inherit his
fathers' legacy,

![image](images/Num_Chap36_08.jpg){height="94" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 36:9*](#Contents.html_rnum36_9)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^and a legacy will not turn from a tribe to another tribe, but the
tribes of the children of Israel shall each cling to its legacy."

![image](images/Num_Chap36_09.jpg){height="47" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 36:10*](#Contents.html_rnum36_10)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^As YHWH commanded Moses, so Zelophehad's daughters did.

![image](images/Num_Chap36_10.jpg){height="44" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

36:10. **Zelophehad's daughters**. Numbers concludes with this account
of a ruling on a point of law that has arisen out of an earlier ruling
on women's property rights in the case of the daughters of Zelophehad.
It is a strange ending, not only because it is anticlimactic but because
it is not remotely a denouement to all that has been narrated so far in
the book. The slave mentality of the people, their rebellions, their
rejection of the land, the development of Moses' character and of his
relationship with God, the challenges to his authority, the great
miraculous events and the continuous divine nurturing, the entire theme
of the journey from Sinai to the promised land itself---all of these
things seem to be left without a finish at the end of Numbers. This,
however, is consistent with what we have already observed: that Numbers
is less self-contained than the other books of the Five Books of Moses.
The natural culmination and conclusion to all these things is the death
of Moses, which is pictured as resulting from the spiral of all these
elements. But Moses' death is to be preceded by an exceptionally long
address by the prophet, which would have been too long to include in
Numbers. It would have doubled the length of the book. And so the
division of the originally continuous biblical text into five books has
left Numbers with a finish that is unsatisfactory in itself but that
serves to point us to a coming climax and resolution in the book of
Deuteronomy.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 36:11*](#Contents.html_rnum36_11)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Mahlah, Tirzah, and Hoglah and Milcah and Noah, Zelophehad's
daughters, became wives to their uncles' sons.

![image](images/Num_Chap36_11.jpg){height="50" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 36:12*](#Contents.html_rnum36_12)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^They became wives within the families of the children of Manasseh
son of Joseph, and their legacy was on the tribe of their father's
family.

![image](images/Num_Chap36_12.jpg){height="69" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[*Numbers 36:13*](#Contents.html_rnum36_13)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^These are the commandments and the judgments that YHWH commanded by
Moses' hand to the children of Israel in the plains of Moab by the
Jordan toward Jericho.

![image](images/Num_Chap36_13.jpg){height="71" width="338"}

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

36:13. **by Moses' hand**. Numbers develops what was begun in Exodus in
the matter of the man Moses himself. Exodus began to present the complex
and conflicting sides of his personality. Now in Numbers we see these
elements of Moses' character operating and metamorphosing through a
variety of circumstances. There are fewer glimpses of him alone in
conversation with God and more portrayals of his deepening connection
with the people to whom he has been bound not by choice but by divine
decision. Through the course of Numbers he emerges as the most textured
personality in the Hebrew Bible, the closest to God and yet perhaps the
most empathetically human. His humility is depicted in conflict with his
anger; his love of his fellow humans is set in tension with his repeated
disappointments over their rebelliousness and lack of wisdom. Precisely
in his closeness to God he speaks to the deity in language that appears
audacious by usual human conceptions of reverence before the divine,
even more audacious than his own words at Sinai in Exodus (32:32;
33:12--16). And, despite his protests to the people that it is not
against him but God that they are complaining, after years of closeness
to the divine---human boundary of authority he comes to infringe on that
boundary and thus brings upon himself the condemnation of death. Whether
or not one regards this depiction as tragic in the Greek sense, one must
appreciate its power and its irony. One can also appreciate that it
exemplifies the issue of the danger of coming close to the divine, which
is present through the course of the book of Numbers. The cosmic and the
historical are bound in a particularly pervasive way in Numbers: in the
record of the people, who witness and are sustained by constant miracles
and whose closeness to the cosmic occasionally brings them into contact
with fearful phenomena; in the life of the priesthood, where closeness
to the cosmic realm is an ongoing and threatening concern; and in the
life of Moses himself, where his extraordinary position on that boundary
is at the heart of the episodes about him in this book.

A closing comment on the book of Numbers

Scholars have often described the book of Numbers as a hodgepodge of
relatively unconnected narratives, laws, and poems. They say it is
heterogeneous and the least unified composition in the Torah. But, on
the contrary, the book is coherent and tightly woven. The *journey* is
the unifying element. It functions both through time (by its chronology
of events) and space (by the geographical placement of the events along
the route of travel) to organize the episodes into a related story.
Elements of language and recurring themes contribute to this unity. Most
of all, though, the events of the journey themselves bind the story, for
they are not a collection of unrelated episodes. A connecting line of
cause and effect runs through them so that they can be read both as
individual stories and as a meaningful chain of events. Indeed, the
episodes of the journey described in the book of Numbers are much more
clearly related to one another than those of the epic of *Gilgamesh* or
*The Odyssey.*

Here is a review of the book with attention to the narrative's
progression:

The story of the journey begins with an account of preparations for the
departure. There is a census, the Levites are numbered and assigned to
care for the Tabernacle, YHWH gives a group of laws relating to
maintaining purity in the camp, a number of ritual matters are described
(the priestly blessing, the menorah, the Passover sacrifice), the tribal
offerings for the Tabernacle are enumerated, and instructions for travel
are given: travel is to follow the movements of the cloud and fire over
the Tabernacle and is to be signaled by blasts on two silver trumpets.

This takes up the great majority of the first ten chapters of the book.
Then the moment of departure arrives. The journey begins with the people
complaining (11:1--3). The reason for their grumbling is not told. The
story's function, therefore, is rather to establish the people's state
of mind as they set out. With the usual biblical economy of wording, the
text, which is only three verses long, conveys the dynamic of the
triangle of God, the people, and Moses. The people anger YHWH, He
plagues them (in this case with fire), they cry to Moses, Moses pleads
on their behalf, and the plague stops. Most notably about this dynamic:
the people still address Moses rather than God. They still focus on the
human leader.

This role for Moses has direct implications for the episode that
immediately follows (11:4--34). The people's complaint in this case is
over food. They find the manna that they have been eating to be
unsatisfactory compared to the food that they received---"free"!---in
Egypt (11:5). They cry for meat. Moses, apparently unable to bear the
burden of being the sole leader any longer, pours out his heart to YHWH
in one of the most remarkable speeches in the Scriptures. Having come
closer to God than any other human being has, Moses speaks to God as no
other human being in the Hebrew Bible does: "Why have you done bad to
your servant ... if this is how you treat me, kill me" (11:11--15).

God's response to this anguished yet audacious speech is seemingly to
ignore its emotional content and to go directly to the solution of the
problem. This mode of divine response is akin to earlier accounts we
have observed, in which the deity addresses what is in the person's
heart rather than what the person says. YHWH instructs Moses to gather
seventy of the elders of Israel and to inform the people that they will
receive meat the next day. The meat comes in the form of birds that a
divine wind carries to the camp. The assistance to Moses comes as God
takes some of the spirit that is on Moses and sets it on the seventy
elders, who then prophesy. Despite the uncertainty about the terms, it
is understood that Moses' special role as the people's prophet is now
shared in some way with others.

This in turn sets the stage for the episode that follows, the story of
"snow-white" Miriam in Numbers 362. Miriam and Aaron speak against
Moses. On the heels of the story of the sharing of Moses' prophetic
status, they ask, "Has YHWH only just spoken through Moses? Hasn't He
also spoken through us?" Their concern is a personal matter, but they
involve God. This error of judgment draws God personally into the matter
and proves costly. Moses does not defend himself, but YHWH defends him
to Aaron and Miriam, declaring that Moses' experience of the divine is
indeed qualitatively different from the prophetic experience of
others---Moses has actually seen God---and the deity concludes with the
ominous words, "And why did you not fear to speak against my servant,
against Moses?" (12:8). Miriam is left with the leprosy. Aaron, like the
people in the first episode, appeals to Moses, whose superiority he now
appears to acknowledge by addressing him as "my lord." Moses in turn
intercedes with God, and Miriam is healed after a week passes. The story
thus establishes by word and action that Moses' singular experience of
God at Sinai has left him with a singular relationship with God.

This unique status of Moses out of all the people of Israel then plays a
