[]{#Deuteronomy06.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:1](#Contents.html_rDeu6_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **6** {.h2a}

^1^"And this is the commandment, the laws, and the judgments that YHWH,
your God, commanded to teach you to do in the land to which you're
crossing to take possession of it,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:2](#Contents.html_rDeu6_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^so that you'll fear YHWH, your God, to observe all His laws and His
commandments that I'm commanding you: you and your child and your
child's child, all the days of your life, and so that your days will be
extended.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:3](#Contents.html_rDeu6_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And you shall listen, Israel, and be watchful to do it, that it will
be good for you and that you'll multiply very much, as YHWH, your
fathers' God, spoke to you: a land flowing with milk and honey.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:4](#Contents.html_rDeu6_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^"Listen, Israel: YHWH is our God. YHWH is one.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

6:4. **Listen**. Forms of this word (Hebrew
*![image](images/s-image.jpg)![image](images/e-image.jpg)ma'*) occur
about eighty times in Deuteronomy. This emphasis on listening goes
together with the title and theme of the book: words, Moses' speech. The
great events of creation, patriarchs, exodus, Sinai, and the wilderness
are at an end. What remains now is the words: the story of what
happened, and the commandments to be fulfilled. Israel's task now---and
in its future generations---is to *listen,* to learn the story and to do
the commandments. And so this passage, probably the most commonly known
and frequently repeated words in the Bible by Jews, begins with the
commandment that necessarily precedes all others: Listen.

[]{#Deuteronomy06.html_page_28}6:4. **YHWH is one**. This is a supremely
monotheistic statement, especially when taken together with the
preceding statements: "He is God" and "There is no other outside of Him"
and "There isn't another." More evidence that monotheism is essential in
Deuteronomy, in its early (preexilic) layers, will follow. Monotheism
was an early concept in biblical Israel.

Some take these words to mean, "YHWH is our God, YHWH *alone.*" But
"alone" is an extremely uncommon meaning for the Hebrew word here:
*'e![image](images/hdot.jpg)![image](images/a-image.jpg)d.*

6:4. **YHWH is one**. In comparing Israel's monotheism to pagan
religion, we must appreciate that the difference between one and many is
not the same sort of thing as the difference between two and three or
between six and twenty. It is not numerical. It is a different concept
of what a god is. A God who is outside of nature, known through acts in
history, a creator, unseeable, without a mate, who makes legal covenants
with humans, who is one, is a revolution in religious conception.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:5](#Contents.html_rDeu6_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And you shall love YHWH, your God, with all your heart and with all
your soul and with all your might.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:6](#Contents.html_rDeu6_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And these words that I command you today shall be on your heart.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:7](#Contents.html_rDeu6_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And you shall impart them to your children, and you shall speak about
them when you sit in your house and when you go in the road and when you
lie down and when you get up.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:8](#Contents.html_rDeu6_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And you shall bind them for a sign on your hand, and they shall
become bands between your eyes.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

6:8. **bind them on your hand**. This came to be taken literally,
requiring one to wear boxes (*tephillin*) on one's arm and head
containing passages from the Torah. In the *Tanak,* however, this
expression is meant figuratively, meaning to keep these teachings at
hand. Thus, elsewhere it refers to binding religious teachings to one's
neck (Prov 3:3), one's heart (6:21), and one's fingers (7:3).

6:8. **bands between your eyes**. This, too, is meant figuratively,
meaning to keep these teachings right before one's eyes. Elsewhere it is
used to refer to the law of redemption of the firstborn (Exod 13:16),
where there is no connection to *tephillin.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:9](#Contents.html_rDeu6_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And you shall write them on the doorposts of your house and in your
gates.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:10](#Contents.html_rDeu6_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^"And it will be when YHWH, your God, will bring you to the land that
He swore to your fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give
you big and good cities that you didn't build,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:11](#Contents.html_rDeu6_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^and houses filled with everything good that you didn't fill, and
cisterns hewed that you didn't hew, vineyards and olives that you did't
plant, and you'll eat and be satisfied,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:12](#Contents.html_rDeu6_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^watch yourself in case you'll forget YHWH, who brought you out from
the land of Egypt, from a house of slaves.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:13](#Contents.html_rDeu6_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^It's YHWH, your God, whom you'll fear, and it's He whom you'll
serve, and it's in His name that you'll swear.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:14](#Contents.html_rDeu6_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^You shall not go after other gods, from the gods of the peoples who
are around you,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:15](#Contents.html_rDeu6_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^because YHWH, your God, is a jealous God among you, in case the
anger of YHWH, your God, will flare at you, and He'll destroy you from
the face of the earth.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:16](#Contents.html_rDeu6_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^You shall not test YHWH, your God, as you tested at Massah.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:17](#Contents.html_rDeu6_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^You *shall* observe the commandments of YHWH, your God, and His
testimonies and His laws that He commanded you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:18](#Contents.html_rDeu6_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And you shall do what is right and good in YHWH's eyes so it will be
good for you, and you'll come and take possession of the good land that
YHWH swore to your fathers,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:19](#Contents.html_rDeu6_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^to push all your enemies from in front of you, as YHWH has spoken.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:20](#Contents.html_rDeu6_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^"When your child will ask you tomorrow, saying, 'What are the
testimonies and the laws and the judgments that YHWH, our God, commanded
you?'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:21](#Contents.html_rDeu6_21)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^then you shall say to your child, 'We were slaves to Pharaoh in
Egypt, and YHWH brought us out from Egypt with a strong hand.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_21.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:22](#Contents.html_rDeu6_22)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And YHWH put great and harsh signs and wonders in Egypt at Pharaoh
and at all of his household before our eyes.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_22.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:23](#Contents.html_rDeu6_23)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And He brought us out from there in order to bring us to give us the
land that He swore to our fathers.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_23.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:24](#Contents.html_rDeu6_24)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And YHWH commanded us to do all these laws, to fear YHWH, our God,
for our good every day, to keep us alive as we are this day.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_24.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 6:25](#Contents.html_rDeu6_25)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And we'll have virtue when we are watchful to do all of this
commandment in front of YHWH, our God, as He commanded us.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap6_25.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

6:25. **we'll have virtue**. Again an issue from Genesis returns in
Moses' farewell in Deuteronomy. The initial covenant with Abraham is
made on the premise of Abraham's virtue
(*![image](images/sdot.jpg)![image](images/e-image.jpg)d![image](images/a-image.jpg)q![image](images/a-image.jpg)h,*
Gen 15:6), and God later says of Abraham that "I've known him for the
purpose that he'll command his children and his house after him, and
they'll observe YHWH's way, to do *virtue* ... " (18:19). His grandson
Jacob later says that "my *vir[]{#Deuteronomy06.html_page_29}tue* will
answer for me" (30:33). And then the word does not occur again in the
Torah until this speech in Deuteronomy, where Moses repeatedly tells the
people how they can come to have virtue.

[]{#Deuteronomy07.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:1](#Contents.html_rDeu7_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **7** {.h2a}

^1^"When YHWH, your God, will bring you to the land to which you're
coming to take possession of it and will eject numerous nations from in
front of you---the Hittite and the Girgashite and the Amorite and the
Canaanite and the Perizzite and the Hivite and the Jebusite---seven
nations more numerous and powerful than you,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:1. **more numerous and powerful than you**. Ironically, these are the
words that the Pharaoh who enslaved Israel used to describe the
Israelites: "Here, the people of the children of Israel is more numerous
and powerful than we" (Exod 1:9). But the situation is reversed. The
Pharaoh tells his people that they should fear Israel on account of its
great size. But God now tells the Israelites *not* to fear the Canaanite
peoples on account of their size.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:2](#Contents.html_rDeu7_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^and YHWH, your God, will put them in front of you, and you'll strike
them: you shall completely destroy them! You shall not make a covenant
with them, and you shall not show grace to them.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:2. **completely destroy**. See the comment on Deut 2:34. Many people
have been troubled by the idea of commanding the annihilation of the
Canaanite residents of the land. The archaeological evidence is that
such a destruction never took place. This passage in the Torah was
written long after the period of the Israelites' settlement in the land,
and so it is ironic that the author of this text conceived of a degree
of violence that appears never in fact to have happened, and then people
are troubled by this degree of violence in Israel's history.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:3](#Contents.html_rDeu7_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And you shall not marry with them. You shall not give your daughter
to his son, and you shall not take his daughter for your son,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:3. **you shall not marry with them**. Moses tells the Israelites that
they are no longer free to marry persons from the seven peoples of
Canaan. Their ancestors, like Judah (Gen 38:2), could marry Canaanite
women because, biblically, a woman takes her husband's religion. Thus
Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, Joseph, Moses, and Samson all marry women who
come from other communities but who are understood to take on their
husbands' religion. But Canaanite women are no longer included.

There is certainly the possibility that wives will not completely turn
to their husbands' religion. This is what happens in the case of Solomon
(1 Kings 11) and in the case of Ahab and Jezebel (1 Kings 16:30--33).
And this possibility is given as the reason for the ban on marriage to
Canaanites, as stated in the next verse (Deut 7:4).

This command is curious because in the preceding verse Moses has already
said that the Canaanites are to be completely destroyed. The ban on
marrying them appears to come from the recognition that the Israelites
may fail to eliminate the Canaanites from the land or that it may be a
very long process---which is in fact what happens (see Judg 2:1--3).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:4](#Contents.html_rDeu7_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^because he'll turn your son from after me, and they'll serve other
gods, and YHWH's anger will flare at you, and He'll destroy you quickly.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:5](#Contents.html_rDeu7_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^But this is what you shall do to them: you shall demolish their
altars and shatter their pillars and cut down their Asherahs and burn
their statues in fire.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:5. **Asherahs**. See the comment on Deut 16:21.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:6](#Contents.html_rDeu7_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^Because you are a holy people to YHWH, your God. YHWH, your God,
chose you to become a treasured people to Him out of all the peoples who
are on the face of the earth.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:6. **chose you to become a treasured people**. As we see in an earlier
occurrence of the term "treasured" for Israel, it applies only if they
listen and fulfill their covenant (see the comment on Exod 19:5). The
same applies to the term "chosen." It does not refer to any superiority
of Israel. The reference to being chosen here is immediately followed by
the statement that it is not because of size. And a little farther on,
Moses will declare in vivid terms that it is not because of any special
merit of Israel's (Deuteronomy 9). It is because of (*a*) YHWH's
affection for Israel and (*b*) YHWH's oath to the patriarchs. Its
practical consequence is that YHWH and Israel have a covenant. That is,
being chosen has many sides: it is inspiring, uplifting, a burden, a
challenge, a threat, an opportunity.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:7](#Contents.html_rDeu7_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^It wasn't because of your being more numerous than all the peoples
that YHWH was attracted to you so that He chose you, because you're the
smallest of all the peoples.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:7. **attracted**. This is the same word that is used to describe
Shechem's longing for Dinah (Gen 34:8) and a soldier's attraction to a
female captive (Deut 21:11). It conveys a feeling of attachment that one
soul has to another.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:8](#Contents.html_rDeu7_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^But because of YHWH's loving you and because of His keeping the oath
that He swore to your fathers, YHWH brought you out with a strong hand
and redeemed you from a house of slaves, from the hand of Pharaoh, king
of Egypt.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:9](#Contents.html_rDeu7_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^Therefore you shall know that YHWH, your God, *He* is God, the
faithful God, keeping the covenant and kindness for those who love Him
and who observe His commandments to the thousandth generation

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_09.jpg)

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_09a.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:10](#Contents.html_rDeu7_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^and paying back to those who hate Him to their faces to destroy
them. He won't delay toward one who hates Him. He'll pay him back to his
face.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:11](#Contents.html_rDeu7_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^So you shall observe the commandment and the laws and the judgments
that I command you today, to do them.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:12](#Contents.html_rDeu7_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**B[ECAUSE]{.small}**

^12^"And it will be because you'll listen to these judgments and observe
and do them that YHWH, your God, will keep the covenant and kindness for
you that he swore to your fathers.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:13](#Contents.html_rDeu7_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And He'll love you and bless you and multiply you and bless the
fruit of your womb and the fruit of your land: your grain and your wine
and your oil, your cattle's offspring and your flock's young, on the
land that He swore to your fathers to give to you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:13. **your womb**. The word "your" in this phrase is masculine, and so
some have interpreted it as meaning that the woman's womb was thought to
belong to her husband. But that is questionable because the entire list
of blessings in these verses (13--24) is formulated in the masculine
singular, so the reference to the womb was just understood as taking a
masculine possessive pronoun like the rest of the list. No conclusions
should be drawn from this technical point of grammar regarding ancient
Israelite conceptions of male-female ownership of the womb.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:14](#Contents.html_rDeu7_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^You'll be more blessed than all the peoples. There won't be an
infertile male or female among you or among your animals.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:14. **blessed**. This word in the Torah generally refers to being well
off. Here it is explicitly connected with fertility and good health. So
when it says that Israel will be more blessed than other peoples if they
keep their covenant, it does not mean that Israel will have some special
status, but rather it means that God will bless them with well-being.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:15](#Contents.html_rDeu7_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And YHWH will turn away from you every illness. And all the bad
diseases of Egypt that you knew: He won't set them among you but will
put them among all who hate you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:16](#Contents.html_rDeu7_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And you shall eat up all the peoples that YHWH, your God, is giving
you. Your eye shall not pity them. And you shall not serve their gods,
because that's a trap for you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:17](#Contents.html_rDeu7_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^"If you say in your heart, 'These nations are more numerous than I;
how shall I be able to dispossess them?'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:18](#Contents.html_rDeu7_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^you shall not fear them. You shall *remember* what YHWH, your God,
did to Pharaoh and to all Egypt:

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:19](#Contents.html_rDeu7_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^the big tests that your eyes saw and the
[]{#Deuteronomy07.html_page_32}signs and the wonders and the strong hand
and the outstretched arm by which YHWH, your God, brought you out. So
YHWH, your God, will do to the peoples before whom you fear!

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:20](#Contents.html_rDeu7_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And YHWH, your God, will send the hornet against them as well until
those who remain and those who are hidden perish from in front of you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:21](#Contents.html_rDeu7_21)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^Don't be scared in front of them, because YHWH, your God, is among
you, a great and fearful God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_21.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:22](#Contents.html_rDeu7_22)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And YHWH, your God, will eject these nations from in front of you
little by little. You won't be able to finish them quickly, or else the
animal of the field will become many at you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_22.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:23](#Contents.html_rDeu7_23)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And YHWH, your God, will put them in front of you and put them into
a big tumult until they're destroyed.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_23.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

7:23. **a big tumult**. This is the term for what God does to the
Egyptians at the Red Sea. A few verses earlier Moses says that God will
do to the Canaanites what was done to the Egyptians, and so he now
describes what will happen to the Canaanites in the language of the
defeat of the Egyptian army.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:24](#Contents.html_rDeu7_24)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And He'll put their kings in your hand, and you'll destroy their
name from under the skies. Not a man will stand in front of you, until
you've destroyed them.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_24.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:25](#Contents.html_rDeu7_25)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^You shall burn the statues of their gods in fire. You shall not
covet the silver and gold on them and take them for yourself, in case
you'll be trapped through it, because that is an offensive thing to
YHWH, your God,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_25.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 7:26](#Contents.html_rDeu7_26)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^and you shall not bring an offensive thing to your house, so that
you'll be a thing to be completely destroyed like it. You shall *detest*
it and find it *abhorrent,* because it is a thing to be completely
destroyed.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap7_26.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy08.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:1](#Contents.html_rDeu8_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **8** {.h2a}

^1^"All of the commandment that I command you today you shall be
watchful to do, so that you'll live, and you'll multiply, and you'll
come and take possession of the land that YHWH swore to your fathers.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:2](#Contents.html_rDeu8_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And you shall remember all the way that YHWH, your God, had you go
these forty years in the wilderness in order to degrade you, to test
you, to know what was in your heart: would you observe His commandments
or not.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_02.jpg)

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_02a.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:2. **to degrade you, to test you, to know**. This sequence of verbs is
powerful because of the ways that they are used before this. The word
"degrade" is used to describe what the Egyptians did to the Israelites
as slaves (Exod 1:11). (It is also used for what Shechem did to Dinah,
and for what the people of Israel are supposed to do to themselves on
Yom Kippur.) It means to bring down, to humble, to lower someone. The
difference is that the Egyptians do it to weaken the Israelites, whereas
God does it to test their strength. The second word here, "test," is the
word for what God does in commanding Abraham to sacrifice Isaac (Gen
22:1). The result of that test is that God says, "Now I *know* that you
fear God" (22:12). And so the third word here, likewise, is "know,"
conveying that the purpose of a divine test is still to establish what
is in a human's heart. Moses thus []{#Deuteronomy08.html_page_33}now
tells the people that the reason for many of the tribulations that they
experienced for forty years was to test them. We, the readers, were
informed of this at the beginning of the account of the years in the
wilderness, where testing is mentioned twice (Exod 15:25; 16:4), but
this is the first time that Moses reveals it to the people.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:3](#Contents.html_rDeu8_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^So He degraded you and made you hunger and then fed you the manna,
which you had not known and your fathers had not known, to let you know
that a human doesn't live by bread alone, but a human lives by every
product of YHWH's mouth.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:4](#Contents.html_rDeu8_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^Your garment didn't wear out on you, and your foot didn't swell these
forty years.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:5](#Contents.html_rDeu8_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^So you shall know in your heart that, the way a man disciplines his
child, so YHWH, your God, disciplines you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:6](#Contents.html_rDeu8_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And you shall observe the commandments of YHWH, your God, to go in
His ways and to fear Him.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:6. **fear**. Some may find the idea of being afraid of God to be
strange or unattractive. But the fact remains that to experience the
power of the creator of the universe is fearful. Thus Moses hides his
face at the burning bush because he is afraid to look at God. Thus the
people are terrified when they hear the divine voice at Sinai. Thus
Nadab and Abihu die because of a misstep in closeness to the holy. When
one appreciates what is at stake, one can understand why the idea of
*fear* of God---literally, profoundly---is so significant here.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:7](#Contents.html_rDeu8_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^Because YHWH, your God, is bringing you to a good land, a land of
wadis of water, springs, and deeps coming out in valleys and in hills,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:8](#Contents.html_rDeu8_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^a land of wheat and barley and vine and fig and pomegranate, a land
of olive, oil, and honey,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:9](#Contents.html_rDeu8_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^a land in which you won't eat bread in scarcity, in which you won't
lack anything, a land whose stones are iron and from whose hills you'll
hew bronze.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:9. **you'll hew bronze**. Copper and tin.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:10](#Contents.html_rDeu8_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And you'll eat and be full and bless YHWH, your God, for the good
land that He has given you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:10. **eat and be full and bless**. The Jewish practice of saying grace
after meals, *birk![image](images/a-image.jpg)t
hamm![image](images/a-image.jpg)z![image](images/o.jpg)n,* is associated
with this verse.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:11](#Contents.html_rDeu8_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^"Watch yourself in case you'll forget YHWH, your God, so as not to
observe His commandments and His judgments and His laws that I command
you today,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:12](#Contents.html_rDeu8_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^in case you'll eat, and you'll be full, and you'll build good
houses, and you'll live there,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:13](#Contents.html_rDeu8_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^and your oxen and your flock will multiply, and silver and gold will
multiply for you, and everything that you'll have will multiply,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:14](#Contents.html_rDeu8_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^and your heart will be lifted, and you'll forget YHWH, your God, who
brought []{#Deuteronomy08.html_page_34}you out from the land of Egypt,
from a house of slaves,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:15](#Contents.html_rDeu8_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^who led you in the big and fearful wilderness of fiery snake and
scorpion and thirst, where there isn't water, who brought out water for
you from the flint rock,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:16](#Contents.html_rDeu8_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^who fed you manna in the wilderness, which your fathers had not
known, in order to degrade you and in order to test you to be good to
you in your future;

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:17](#Contents.html_rDeu8_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^and you'll say in your heart: '*My* power and *my* hand's strength
made this wealth for me.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

8:17. ***My*** **power**. The entire passage from v. 11 to v. 17 is one
sentence. Moses uses this extraordinarily long sentence to convey the
weight of the situation against which he is warning. So much will go
well that the people can be drawn to forget the God who has done so much
for them, so they think that they have achieved it all on their own. And
then Moses answers in a simple sentence of ordinary length: You shall
remember that *He* is the one who gives ...

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:18](#Contents.html_rDeu8_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^Then you shall remember YHWH, your God, because *He* is the one who
gave you power to make wealth so as to uphold His covenant that He swore
to your fathers---as it is this day.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:19](#Contents.html_rDeu8_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^"And it will be, if you'll *forget* YHWH, your God, and you'll go
after other gods and serve them and bow to them, I call witness
regarding you today that you'll *perish!*

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 8:20](#Contents.html_rDeu8_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^Like the nations that YHWH is making to perish in front of you, so
will you perish, because you wouldn't listen to the voice of YHWH, your
God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap8_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy09.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:1](#Contents.html_rDeu9_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **9** {.h2a}

^1^"Listen, Israel: you're crossing the Jordan today to come to
dispossess nations bigger and stronger than you, big cities and
fortified to the skies,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:2](#Contents.html_rDeu9_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^a people big and tall, giants, of whom you have known, and of whom
you have heard it said: 'Who can stand in front of the giants?!'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:3](#Contents.html_rDeu9_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^So you shall know today that YHWH, your God: *He* is the one who is
crossing in front of you, a consuming fire. *He* will destroy them, and
*He* will subdue them in front of you, so you'll dispossess them and
destroy them quickly as YHWH has spoken to you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:4](#Contents.html_rDeu9_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^"Don't say in your heart when YHWH pushes them from in front of you,
saying, 'Because of my virtue YHWH has brought me to take possession of
this land,' when it is because of these nations' wickedness that YHWH
dispossesses them from before you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:4. **take possession**. Forms of this term occur more than sixty times
in Deuteronomy. Here it is linked specifically to the promise to the
patriarchs. This brings back to mind Abraham's exchange with YHWH just
before their covenant, where this word occurs three times. Abraham says
that he is childless, so someone else will "take possession" of what is
his, but God assures him that his own offspring will "take possession"
from him (Gen 15:3--4). The numerous repetitions of the term convey the
central importance of the link and continuity between the episodes of
the patriarchs' lives and the fate of their descendants.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:5](#Contents.html_rDeu9_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^It's not because of your virtue and your heart's integrity that
you're coming to take possession of their land, but it is because of
these nations' wickedness that YHWH, your God, dispossesses them from
before you, and in order to uphold the thing that YHWH swore to your
fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:5. **not because of your virtue**. The point is made three times, in
three verses in a row (9:4--6): it is not because of your virtue but
rather because of these nations' wickedness. Some think that the
repetition is just a scribe's error, repeating a line by mistake. (Such
errors are known as dittography.) That is possible, but the repetition
itself is not sufficient reason to make that conclusion. On the
contrary, the text seems to me precisely to be making the point as
emphatically as possible. Moses notes the people's own lack of virtue
even more strongly in the next verse (7) and he begins with the words
"Remember---don't forget!" which is redundant as well but is certainly
done on purpose for emphasis. And he then goes on for the rest of the
chapter listing the people's record of rebellions. Moses thus gives a
powerful warning against chauvinism and self-congratulation. And this
also provides a profound balance to the declaration that Israel was
chosen to become a treasured people, which came just two chapters
earlier (7:6). Possession of the land is the result of a promise to
Israel's ancestors. Status as a treasured people depends on actions:
faithfulness to the covenant. Israel is not intrinsically better than
anyone. What is special about Israel is rather that it has been given a
singular opportunity to follow a path that will ultimately bring
blessing to all the families of the earth.

9:5. **take possession**. Forms of this term occur more than sixty times
in Deuteronomy. Here it is linked specifically to the promise to the
patriarchs. This brings back to mind Abraham's exchange with YHWH just
before their covenant, where this word occurs three times. Abraham says
that he is childless, so someone else will "take possession" of what is
his, but God assures him that his own offspring will "take possession"
from him (Gen 15:3--4). The numerous repetitions of the term convey the
central importance of the link and continuity between the episodes of
the patriarchs' lives and the fate of their descendants.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:6](#Contents.html_rDeu9_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^So you shall know that it is not because of your virtue that YHWH,
your God, gives you this good land to take possession of it, because
you're a hardnecked people.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:6. **take possession**. Forms of this term occur more than sixty times
in Deuteronomy. Here it is linked specifically to the promise to the
patriarchs. This brings back to mind Abraham's exchange with YHWH just
before their covenant, where this word occurs three times. Abraham says
that he is childless, so someone else will "take possession" of what is
his, but God assures him that his own offspring will "take possession"
from him (Gen 15:3--4). The numerous repetitions of the term convey the
central importance of the link and continuity between the episodes of
the patriarchs' lives and the fate of their descendants.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:7](#Contents.html_rDeu9_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^Remember---don't forget!---that you made YHWH, your God, angry in the
wilderness. From the day that you went out from the land of Egypt until
you came to this place, you've been rebellious toward YHWH.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:8](#Contents.html_rDeu9_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And you made YHWH angry at Horeb, and YHWH was so incensed at you as
to destroy you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:9](#Contents.html_rDeu9_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^When I went up to the mountain to get the tablets of stones, the
tablets of the covenant that YHWH made with you, and I stayed in the
mountain forty days and forty nights, I didn't eat bread and didn't
drink water.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:9. **didn't eat bread and didn't drink water**. This is the first
mention of this miracle that Moses personally experiences at Horeb. It
is not reported in Exodus. Moses tells the people about it now, in his
list of their rebellions, seemingly to convey to them that they rebelled
in the golden calf episode precisely while something awesome was going
on. While they were saying, "This man Moses, we don't know what's become
of him," Moses was in fact experiencing a kind of physical
transformation.

Another explanation: The people's rebellions up to that point (and
frequently thereafter) were about food and water: the bitter water at
Marah (Exod 22--26), the manna (16:2--36), and the water from the rock
(17:1--7). And so Moses conveys to them that their worries and
complaints were utterly unnecessary: in God's hands a man could even
live without food or water for forty days. Indeed, this follows Moses'
admonition in the preceding chapter that "a human doesn't live by bread
alone" (Deut 8:3).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:10](#Contents.html_rDeu9_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And YHWH gave me the two tablets of stones, written with God's
finger, and on them were all the words that YHWH had spoken with you at
the mountain from inside the fire in the day of the assembly.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:11](#Contents.html_rDeu9_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And it was: at the end of forty days and forty nights YHWH gave me
the two tablets of stones, the tablets of the covenant.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:12](#Contents.html_rDeu9_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^"And YHWH said to me, 'Get up. Go down quickly from here, because
your people whom you brought out from Egypt has become corrupt. They've
turned quickly from the way that I commanded them. They've made
themselves a molten thing.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:13](#Contents.html_rDeu9_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And YHWH said to me, saying, 'I've seen this people; and, here, it's
a hardnecked people.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:14](#Contents.html_rDeu9_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^Hold back from me, and I'll destroy them and wipe out their name
from under the skies, and I'll make you into a more powerful and
numerous people than they.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:15](#Contents.html_rDeu9_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^"And I turned and went down from the mountain---and the mountain was
burning in fire---and the two tablets of the covenant were on my two
hands.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:16](#Contents.html_rDeu9_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And I saw! And, here, you'd sinned against YHWH, your God. You'd
made yourselves a molten calf. You'd turned quickly from the way that
YHWH had commanded you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:17](#Contents.html_rDeu9_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And I grasped the two tablets and threw them from on my two hands
and shattered them before your eyes.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:18](#Contents.html_rDeu9_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^"And I prostrated myself in front of YHWH like the first time, forty
days and forty nights---I didn't eat bread and didn't drink water---over
all your sin that you did, to do what was bad in YHWH's eyes, to provoke
Him,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:19](#Contents.html_rDeu9_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^because I was dreading on account of the anger and the fury, that
YHWH was so angry at you as to destroy you. But YHWH listened to me that
time as well.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:20](#Contents.html_rDeu9_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And YHWH was very incensed at Aaron so as to destroy him, and I
prayed for Aaron at that time as well.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:20. **incensed at Aaron**. The text of the golden calf event (Exodus
32) never says that God was specifically angry at Aaron. We might
understand the fact that Moses only makes it known now for the first
time as indicating that he would not mention it until after Aaron's
death. Thus he spares Aaron the humiliation. And he has thus deferred
any doubts that this might have raised about the status of the high
priest until now, when they can be considered in the light of the man's
entire life and career as high priest.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:21](#Contents.html_rDeu9_21)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And your sin that you []{#Deuteronomy09.html_page_37}made, the calf:
I took it and burned it in fire and crushed it, grinding it well until
it was thin as dust, and I threw its dust into the wadi that comes down
from the mountain.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_21.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:21. **burned it ... thin as dust**. The parallels between Aaron's
golden calf and those of Jeroboam I, king of Israel, are unmistakable.
Jeroboam erects golden calves at Dan and Beth-El (1 Kings 12:26--30). On
that occasion he says, "Here are your gods, Israel, which brought you
out from the land of Egypt"---which are the same words that the people
say at the golden calf in Exodus. Jeroboam's sons are Nadab and Abiyah;
Aaron's sons are Nadab and Abihu. And King Josiah destroys the high
place where Jeroboam's golden calf stood at Beth-El, and, like Moses,
"he burned it thin as dust" (2 Kings 23:15). Also like Moses, Josiah
casts the dust of pagan altars into a wadi (23:6,12). A
literary-historical analysis of this parallel appears in my *Who Wrote
the Bible?* (pp. 111--116). In the terms of the full *Tanak* as it now
stands, the parallel would suggest that history repeats itself. This is
also one of many parallels between Josiah and Moses that serve to single
out Josiah as the best of the kings of Israel, the one who comes closest
to the standards that Moses taught by commandment and example. Thus the
Torah says at the end of Moses' life: "And a prophet did not rise again
in Israel like Moses" (Deut 34:10); and the *Tanak* says at the end of
Josiah's life: "And after him one did not rise like him" (2 Kings
23:25)---and these are the only two occurrences of the expression "did
not rise like him" in the Bible. Again the embroidery of connections
through the *Tanak* is apparent.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:22](#Contents.html_rDeu9_22)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^"And at Taberah and at Massah and at Kibroth Hattaavah you were
making YHWH angry.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_22.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:23](#Contents.html_rDeu9_23)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And when YHWH sent you from Kadesh-barnea, saying, 'Go up and take
possession of the land that I've given you,' then you rebelled at the
word of YHWH, your God, and you didn't trust Him and didn't listen to
His voice.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_23.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:24](#Contents.html_rDeu9_24)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^You've been rebelling toward YHWH from the day I knew you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_24.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:25](#Contents.html_rDeu9_25)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^"So I prostrated myself in front of YHWH for the forty days and
forty nights that I had fallen down because YHWH had said He would
destroy you,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_25.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:26](#Contents.html_rDeu9_26)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^and I prayed to YHWH and said, 'My Lord YHWH, don't destroy your
people and your legacy whom you redeemed by your greatness, whom you
brought out from Egypt with a strong hand.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_26.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

9:26. **destroy**. The word in Hebrew,
*ta![image](images/s-image.jpg)![image](images/hdot.jpg)![image](images/e1.jpg)t,*
has the same root meaning as the word for what the people have done:
become corrupt,
*hi![image](images/s-image.jpg)![image](images/hdot.jpg)ît* (9:12). The
same play on this root is used in the flood story (Gen 6:12--13). There
and here it is not just wordplay for its own sake. It conveys the
principle that punishment is meant to be in proportion to the crime. The
repetition of the pun here in Deuteronomy conveys that what was true for
all humankind in the early generations of creation is still true in
relations between God and humans in the Torah's final book.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:27](#Contents.html_rDeu9_27)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^Remember your servants Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Don't look to this
people's hardness and to its wickedness and to its sin,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_27.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:28](#Contents.html_rDeu9_28)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^or else the land from which you brought us out will say: Because
YHWH wasn't *able* to bring them to the land of which He spoke to them,
and because He *hated* them, He brought them out to kill them in the
wilderness!

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_28.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 9:29](#Contents.html_rDeu9_29)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And they're your people and []{#Deuteronomy09.html_page_38}your
legacy, whom you brought out by your great power and by your
outstretched arm.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap9_29.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy10.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:1](#Contents.html_rDeu10_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **10** {.h2a}

^1^"At that time YHWH said to me, 'Carve two tablets of stones like the
first ones, and come up to me at the mountain. And you shall make an ark
of wood.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:1. **you shall make an ark of wood**. It says "*you* shall make," but
in Exodus it was Bezalel, not Moses, who made the ark. Rashi and Ramban
and others therefore say that there must have been two arks. In current
critical scholarship, the apparent contradiction would generally be
taken to be a result of the fact that this text in Deuteronomy and the
text in Exodus were written by two different authors. But, even without
raising such solutions that are arrived at through critical approaches,
we can understand Moses' words in Deuteronomy to be a brief account of
what happened at Sinai, and so he speaks of himself as making the ark
when he means that he directed Bezalel to do it. Likewise, he speaks of
the ark as if it were finished and ready when he came down from the
mountain when he knows that actually some time passed before the ark was
made. J. H. Hertz proposed such an understanding as an alternative to
Rashi's and Ramban's view even though Hertz harshly rejected
literary-critical scholarship. This is important because an almost
fundamentalist view of Rashi is growing at present in some communities,
wherein it is practically heresy to question Rashi. This gives Rashi a
status that I do not think he would have wanted for himself. And
certainly the commentators of the generations that followed him,
including his own grandson Rashbam, were prepared to question his
comments. We respect and admire the great commentators of past
generations, but we should never forget to distinguish between Torah and
commentary. The goal of refining our knowledge of the Torah and shedding
new light from it in each new age is never-ending; and our
commentaries---including my own---are always subject to criticism and
improvement.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:2](#Contents.html_rDeu10_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And I'll write on the tablets the words that were on the first
tablets, which you shattered, and you shall set them in the ark.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:3](#Contents.html_rDeu10_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^"And I made an ark of acacia wood, and I carved two tablets of stones
like the first ones, and I went up the mountain with the two tablets in
my hands.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:4](#Contents.html_rDeu10_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And He wrote on the tablets like the first writing: the Ten
Commandments that YHWH spoke to you at the mountain from inside the fire
in the day of the assembly, and YHWH gave them to me.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:4. **the Ten Commandments**. Hebrew
*'![image](images/a1.jpg)![image](images/s.jpg)eret
hadd![image](images/e-image.jpg)b![image](images/a-image.jpg)rîm,*
literally, the ten things*.* This is what the Ten Commandments are
called in Hebrew.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:5](#Contents.html_rDeu10_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And I turned and went down from the mountain, and I set the tablets
in the ark that I had made, and they have been there, as YHWH commanded
me.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:6](#Contents.html_rDeu10_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^"And the children of Israel had traveled from Beeroth-bene-Jaakan to
Moserah. There Aaron died, and he was buried there; and Eleazar, his
son, functioned as priest in his place.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:7](#Contents.html_rDeu10_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^From there they traveled to Gudgod, and from Gudgod to Jotbah, a land
of wadis of water.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:8](#Contents.html_rDeu10_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^At that time YHWH distinguished the tribe of Levi to carry the ark of
YHWH's covenant, to stand in front of YHWH to serve Him, and to bless in
His name to this day.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:9](#Contents.html_rDeu10_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^Therefore Levi has not had a portion and a legacy with its brothers.
YHWH: *He* is its legacy, as YHWH, your God, spoke to it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:10](#Contents.html_rDeu10_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^"And I: I stood in the mountain as in the first days: forty days and
forty nights. And YHWH listened to me that time as well. YHWH was not
willing to destroy you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:10. **And I**. Moses now resumes his account of his second forty days
on Horeb, which had left off at 9:29. He had interrupted his story with
mention of the ark and other matters.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:11](#Contents.html_rDeu10_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And YHWH said to me, 'Get up. Set out on the journey in front of the
people, and they'll come and take possession of the land that I swore to
their fathers to give to them.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:12](#Contents.html_rDeu10_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^"And now, Israel, what is YHWH, your God, asking from you except to
fear YHWH, your God, to go in []{#Deuteronomy10.html_page_39}all His
ways, and to love Him and to serve YHWH, your God, with all your heart
and all your soul,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:12. **what is God asking from you**. All the things that follow in
this verse have been mentioned already in Moses' speech. They come now,
therefore, as a summary, a statement of the basic things that underlie
everything else: fear, love, deeds, service, complete commitment.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:13](#Contents.html_rDeu10_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^to observe YHWH's commandments and His laws that I command you today
to be good for you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:14](#Contents.html_rDeu10_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^Here, YHWH, your God, has the skies---and the skies of the
skies!---the earth and everything that's in it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:14. **the skies of the skies**. Meaning, presumably, that if one were
in the sky and looked up, one would see a farther portion of the sky. So
the phrase means the farthest reaches of the sky.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:15](#Contents.html_rDeu10_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^Only, YHWH was attracted to your fathers, to love them, and He chose
their seed after them: you, out of all the peoples, as it is this day.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:16](#Contents.html_rDeu10_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^So you shall circumcise the foreskin of your *heart,* and you shall
not harden your necks anymore.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:16. **the foreskin of your *heart***. This concept occurs in two
books of the Torah (Deut 10:16; 30:6; Lev 26:41) and in two of the
prophets (Jer 4:4; Ezek 44:7,9). The sign of the Abrahamic covenant, and
probably the most commonly observed of the commandments by Jewish
families for centuries, is circumcision. But the Torah *commands* the
circumcision of one's heart as well. It establishes that outward
fulfillment of practices without also feeling it in one's heart is
insufficient. The concept of circumcising one's heart, moreover, unlike
physical circumcision, applies to both women and men.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:17](#Contents.html_rDeu10_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^Because YHWH, your God: He is the God of gods and the Lord of lords,
the great, the mighty, and the awesome God, who won't be partial and
won't take a bribe,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

10:17. **the God of gods and the Lord of lords**. Admittedly, this
sounds unmonotheistic, seemingly acknowledging the existence of other
gods. But, earlier, Moses has said, "YHWH: *He* is God. There is no
other outside of Him" (4:35) and "YHWH is one" (6:4). And, later, YHWH
says, "There is no god with me" (32:39). How are we to reconcile the
many monotheistic passages in Deuteronomy with "God of gods and Lord of
lords"? As in the case of not having "other gods before my face," this
is a linguistic matter. "God of gods" is an expression for conveying
that YHWH is something that all other gods in whom people believe are
not. It need not presume that such other gods exist.

10:17. **won't take a bribe**. What could it mean to bribe God?! A
sacrifice, a donation, a kindness, charity, prayer: one might do any of
these to win the deity's favor, rather than doing them as fulfillment of
the commandments or as free acts. Moses informs the people that this
will not work.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:18](#Contents.html_rDeu10_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^doing judgment for an orphan and a widow and loving an alien, to
give him bread and a garment.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:19](#Contents.html_rDeu10_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^So you shall love the alien, because you were aliens in the land of
Egypt.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:20](#Contents.html_rDeu10_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^You shall fear YHWH, your God, you shall serve Him, and you shall
cling to Him, and you shall swear by His name.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:21](#Contents.html_rDeu10_21)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^He is your splendor, and He is your God, who did these great and
awesome things for you that your eyes have seen. Your fathers went down
to Egypt with seventy persons, and now YHWH, your God, has made you like
the stars of the skies for multitude.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_21.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 10:22](#Contents.html_rDeu10_22)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/Deu_Chap10_22.jpg)

[]{#Deuteronomy11.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:1](#Contents.html_rDeu11_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **11** {.h2a}

^1^"And you shall love YHWH, your God, and keep His charge and His laws
and His judgments and His commandments every day.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:2](#Contents.html_rDeu11_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And you shall know today that it's not with your children, who didn't
know and who didn't see the discipline of YHWH, your God, His greatness
and His strong hand and His outstretched arm

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:3](#Contents.html_rDeu11_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^and His signs and His deeds that He did in Egypt to Pharaoh, king of
Egypt, and to all his land,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:4](#Contents.html_rDeu11_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^and what He did to Egypt's army and to his horses and his chariots,
that He flowed the waters of the Red Sea over their faces when they
pursued you so YHWH destroyed them to this day,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

*[Deuteronomy 11:5](#Contents.html_rDeu11_5)*

place,

so that []{#Deuteronomy11.html_page_41}you'll be strong and you'll come
and take possession of the land that you're crossing there to take

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_08.jpg)
