------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:9](#Contents.html_rDeu11_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^and so that you'll extend days on the land that YHWH swore to your
fathers to give to them and to their seed, a land flowing with milk and
honey.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:10](#Contents.html_rDeu11_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^"Because the land to which you're coming to take possession of it:
it's not like the land of Egypt from which you've come out, where you
plant your seed and water it at your feet like a garden of plants.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:10. **water it at your feet like a garden**. Because the Nile's water
is commonly present, the human's role is simply to get it to the plants,
by conveying or irrigation. But in Israel much of the land is dependent
on rain as its source of water; it "drinks water by the skies' showers"
(11:11). And so one feels more dependent on God. But, Moses assures the
people, God "cares about" the land, and God's "eyes are always on it"
(11:12).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:11](#Contents.html_rDeu11_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^But the land to which you're crossing to take possession of it is a
land of hills and valleys. It drinks water by the skies' showers.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:12](#Contents.html_rDeu11_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^A land that YHWH, your God, cares about; YHWH's eyes are always on
it, from the year's beginning to year's end.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:13](#Contents.html_rDeu11_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^So it will be, if you'll *listen* to my commandments that I command
you today, to love YHWH, your God, and to serve Him with all your heart
and with all your soul,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:14](#Contents.html_rDeu11_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^then I'll give your land's showers at their time, early rain and
late rain, and you'll gather your grain and your wine and your oil.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:14. **I'll give**. The subject of "I'll give" must be God, and so
Moses has apparently shifted in the middle of his discussion and is now
quoting God when until now he has been speaking in his own name and
referring to God in third person. It may convey a kind of intimacy in
which Moses is now so close to the deity and so used to speaking in
God's name and declaring the commandments that he moves into the divine
first-person mode with ease.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:15](#Contents.html_rDeu11_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^And I'll give vegetation in your field for your animals, and you'll
eat and be full.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:14,15. **I'll give**. The subject of "I'll give" must be God, and so
Moses has apparently shifted in the middle of his discussion and is now
quoting God when until now he has been speaking in his own name and
referring to God in third person. It may convey a kind of intimacy in
which Moses is now so close to the deity and so used to speaking in
God's name and declaring the commandments that he moves into the divine
first-person mode with ease.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:16](#Contents.html_rDeu11_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^"Watch yourselves in case your heart will be deceived so you'll turn
and serve other gods and bow to them,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:17](#Contents.html_rDeu11_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

and there won't be showers, and the earth won't give its crop, and
you'll perish quickly from the good land that YHWH is giving you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

*[Deuteronomy 11:18](#Contents.html_rDeu11_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

and when you get up,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_19.jpg)

*[Deuteronomy 11:20](#Contents.html_rDeu11_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^and you shall write them on the doorposts of your house and in your
gates,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:21](#Contents.html_rDeu11_21)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^so that your days and your children's days will be many on the land
that YHWH swore to your fathers to give them, like the days of the skies
over the earth.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_21.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:22](#Contents.html_rDeu11_22)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^Because, if you *will* observe all of this commandment that I
command you, to do it, to love YHWH, your God, to go in all His ways and
to cling to Him,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_22.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:23](#Contents.html_rDeu11_23)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^then YHWH will dispossess all these nations in front of you, and
you'll dispossess bigger and more powerful nations than you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_23.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:24](#Contents.html_rDeu11_24)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^Every place in which your foot will step shall be yours, from the
wilderness and Lebanon, from the river, the Euphrates River, to the far
sea shall be your border.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_24.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:24. **foot**. Hebrew *kap regel* is often taken to mean "the sole of
the foot," but that is not correct. *regel* refers to the leg in hebrew
(although we often translate it as "foot" in English because of the
conventions of usage of the two words "leg" and "foot" in English), and
*kap regel* refers to the portion of the leg from the ankle down, i.e.,
the foot. See Ezek 1:7, which says: "Their *kap regel* was like the *kap
regel* of a calf"---which must mean the appearance of a foot and not of
the sole of a foot.

The same goes for *kap y![image](images/a-image.jpg)d,* which is often
taken to mean "the palm of the hand," but which means the portion of the
*y![image](images/a-image.jpg)d* ("forearm," although, again, we often
translate it as "hand" because of English usage) from the wrist down,
i.e., the hand. See 2 Kings 9:35, where dogs have eaten Jezebel's body,
and nothing can be found but her skull, legs, and *kappôt
hayy![image](images/a-image.jpg)d![image](images/a-image.jpg)yîm*---which
must mean "hands" and not "palms." (See also the comment on Deut 4:34.)

11:24. **the far sea**. The Mediterranean Sea.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:25](#Contents.html_rDeu11_25)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^Not a man will stand up in front of you. YHWH, your God, will put
awe of you and fear of you on the face of all the land on which you'll
step, as He spoke to you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_25.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:26](#Contents.html_rDeu11_26)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**S[EE]{.small}**

^26^"See: I'm putting in front of you today a blessing and a curse:

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_26.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:27](#Contents.html_rDeu11_27)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^the blessing when you'll listen to the commandments of YHWH, your
God, that I command you today,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_27.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:28](#Contents.html_rDeu11_28)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^and the curse if you won't listen to the commandments of YHWH, your
God, and you'll turn from the way that I command you today, to go after
oth[]{#Deuteronomy11.html_page_43}er gods, whom you haven't known.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_28.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:29](#Contents.html_rDeu11_29)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And it shall be, when YHWH, your God, will bring you to the land to
which you're coming to take possession of it, that you shall put the
blessing on Mount Gerizim and the curse on Mount Ebal.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_29.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:30](#Contents.html_rDeu11_30)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^Aren't they across the Jordan, beyond the way of the sun's setting,
in the land of the Canaanite who lives in the plain, opposite Gilgal,
near the oaks of Moreh?

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_30.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

11:30. **oaks of Moreh**. The first place to which Abraham comes when he
moves to Canaan is the oak of Moreh (Gen 12:6). There YHWH is said to
appear to him for the first time (which is also the first time that God
is said to have appeared to *anyone* in the Bible). There YHWH says for
the first time that He will give the land to Abraham's descendants. And
there Abraham builds the first altar to YHWH in Canaan. Now the oaks (or
oak; the Septuagint has the singular) of Moreh are mentioned just before
a statement that those descendants are now about "to come to take
possession of the land." It is thus another signal that the merit of the
ancestors is a source of protection and well-being for Israel many
generations later. In this case, because Abraham listened to God's first
command and left his home for a new land, his descendants now come to
the land.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:31](#Contents.html_rDeu11_31)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^Because you're crossing the Jordan to come to take possession of the
land that YHWH, your God, is giving you; and you shall take possession
of it and live in it

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_31.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 11:32](#Contents.html_rDeu11_32)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^and be watchful to do all the laws and the judgments that I'm
putting in front of you today.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap11_32.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy12.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:1](#Contents.html_rDeu12_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **12** {.h2a}

^1^"These are the laws and the judgments that you shall be watchful to
do in the land that YHWH, your fathers' God, has given you to take
possession of it, every day that you're living on the land:

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:2](#Contents.html_rDeu12_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"You shall *destroy* all the places where the nations that you're
dispossessing worshiped their gods there: on the high mountains and on
the hills and under every lush tree.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:3](#Contents.html_rDeu12_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And you shall demolish their altars and shatter their pillars and
burn their Asherahs in fire and cut down the statues of their gods in
fire and destroy their name from that place.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:4](#Contents.html_rDeu12_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^"You shall not do that for YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:4. **You shall not do that**. Meaning: you shall not worship your God
*in many places.* Moses has just told the people that the nations
worshiped their gods all over the land---on hills, in valleys, and under
every tree---and that the Israelites are to destroy "all the places." In
the next verse Moses will tell them that they are to worship their God
in *only one place.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:5](#Contents.html_rDeu12_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^But, rather, you shall inquire at the place that YHWH, your God, will
choose from all your tribes to set His name there, to tent it. And you
shall come there,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:5. **the place that YHWH, your God, will choose**. This is the
central point: There are not to be Temples of YHWH all over the world,
or even all over Israel. There is to be only one place. I understand
this to be a physical, visible expression of the principle of
monotheism: one God, one place of worship. (This does not preclude the
postbiblical Jewish practice of having synagogues, nor the Christian
practice of having churches, as places of prayer, study, and gathering.
It just means that worship involving *sacrifices* can be done only in
the one sanctioned place.)

12:5. **to set His name there**. This is known as the Deuteronomistic
Name Theology. The concept is that God cannot be contained in all of the
universe, so how can a structure built on earth by humans possibly house
God? Answer: God causes His name to be contained in the structure. The
divine name becomes almost a tangible entity, and it is connected to the
structure; that is, to the Tent of Meeting. The visible symbol of the
presence of the divine name is the ark. And so every occurrence of this
expression in Deuteronomy refers to the place where the ark is. Later
the ark and the Tent of Meeting will be moved to the Temple that Solomon
builds in Jerusalem (1 Kings 8:4), and on that occasion Solomon will use
some of these same terms to convey that the Name is now housed in the
Temple (8:12--30). For prayer and sacrifice, Israelites are to focus
their attention in the direction of the place where the name of YHWH is
housed.

12:5. **to tent it**. I follow Frank Moore Cross in the understanding of
this word (Hebrew root *![image](images/s-image.jpg)kn*) as denominative
from the word
*mi![image](images/s-image.jpg)k![image](images/a-image.jpg)n,* a tent
or tabernacle, so that it means that YHWH tents His name there. That is,
the Tent of Meeting houses the ark and the divine name.

One can *pray* anywhere, but a great many of the commandments can be
fulfilled only at that one place. Most notably, all sacrifices must be
made there, and nowhere else on earth.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:6](#Contents.html_rDeu12_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^and bring there your burnt offerings and your sacrifices and your
tithes and your hand's donation and your vows and your contributions and
the firstborn of your herd and your flock.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:7](#Contents.html_rDeu12_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And you shall eat there in front of YHWH, your God. And you shall
rejoice about everything your hand has taken on, you and your
households, in that YHWH, your God, has blessed you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:7. **you shall eat there in front of YHWH**. A sacrifice is a sacred
meal eaten in the presence of God. The commandments of Leviticus 17 are
conveyed again here. The language is different, but the principle is the
same: Animals' lives cannot be taken in a thoughtless way. If one wishes
to eat meat, the animal must be killed at the one central place of
worship, called the Tabernacle in Leviticus and "the place where YHWH
sets His name" in Deuteronomy. Then, instead of slaughter, one
participates in holiness when one eats the meal. The meal then includes
a component of reverence for the animal's life and affirms a belief in
the unity of God.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:8](#Contents.html_rDeu12_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^You shall not do it like everything that we're doing here today, each
one, everything that's right in his own eyes,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:9](#Contents.html_rDeu12_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^because up to now you haven't come to the resting place and to the
legacy that YHWH, your God, is giving you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:10](#Contents.html_rDeu12_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

your God, is giving you as a legacy, and He'll give you rest from all
your enemies all around, and you'll live securely,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

your burnt offerings and your sacrifices, your tithes and your hand's
donation and every choice one of your vows that you'll make to YHWH.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:12](#Contents.html_rDeu12_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And you shall rejoice in front of YHWH, your God: you and your sons
and your daughters and your servants and your maids and the Levite who
is in your gates because he doesn't have a portion or a legacy with you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:13](#Contents.html_rDeu12_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^"Watch yourself in case you would make your burnt offerings in any
place that you'll see.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:14](#Contents.html_rDeu12_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^But, rather, in the place that YHWH will choose in one of your
tribes: there you shall make your burnt offerings, and there you shall
do everything that I command you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:15](#Contents.html_rDeu12_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^Only, as much as your soul desires you may slaughter and may eat
meat according to the blessing of YHWH, your God, that He has given you
in all your gates. The impure and the pure may eat it: like a gazelle,
like a deer.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy12.html_page_47}12:15. **like a gazelle, like a deer**.
Israelites may kill an animal as a *sacrifice* only at the one place
that will be chosen (which will ultimately be the Temple). But they may
slaughter an animal in a nonsacrificial way (that is, secular rather
than ritual slaughter) at any other place. Then the same laws apply to
this slaughter as would apply to animals that are killed by hunting,
such as gazelles and deer: (1) the blood must be spilled off and not
eaten; (2) only people who are in a condition of ritual purity may eat
sacrificed meat, but this secularly slaughtered meat may be eaten by
anyone, pure or impure.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:16](#Contents.html_rDeu12_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^Only, you shall not eat the blood. You shall spill it like water on
the earth.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:17](#Contents.html_rDeu12_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^You may not eat within your gates the tithe of your grain or your
wine or your oil or the firstborn of your herd or your flock or all your
vows that you'll make or your contributions or your hand's donation.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:18](#Contents.html_rDeu12_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^But, rather, you shall eat them in front of YHWH, your God, in the
place that YHWH, your God, will choose: you and your son and your
daughter and your servant and your maid and the Levite who is in your
gates. And you shall rejoice in front of YHWH, your God, about
everything your hand has taken on.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:19](#Contents.html_rDeu12_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^Watch yourself in case you would leave the Levite, all your days in
your land.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:20](#Contents.html_rDeu12_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^"When YHWH, your God, will widen your border as He spoke to you, and
you'll say, 'Let me eat meat,' because your soul will desire to eat

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:21](#Contents.html_rDeu12_21)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^When the place that YHWH, your God, will choose to put His name

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_21.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_22.jpg)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:23](#Contents.html_rDeu12_23)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^Only, be strong not to eat the blood, because the blood: it's the

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_23.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:24](#Contents.html_rDeu12_24)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^You shall not eat it. You shall spill it like water on the earth.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_24.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:25](#Contents.html_rDeu12_25)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^You shall not eat it, so it will be good for you and for your
children after you when you do what is right in YHWH's eyes.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_25.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:26](#Contents.html_rDeu12_26)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^"Only, you shall carry your holy things that you'll have and your
vows and shall come to the place that YHWH will choose.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_26.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:27](#Contents.html_rDeu12_27)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And you shall do your burnt offerings, the meat and the blood, on
the altar of YHWH, your God. And the blood of your sacrifices shall be
spilled on the altar of YHWH, your God, and you shall eat the meat.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_27.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

12:27. **sacrifices**. This refers to sacrifices that are for food (as
opposed to burnt offerings, which the donor does not eat). The blood is
to be poured off at the altar, and then one may eat the meat of the
sacrifice.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:28](#Contents.html_rDeu12_28)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^Be watchful that you listen to all these things that I command you
so that it will be good for you and for your children after you forever
when you'll do what is good and right in the eyes of YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_28.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:29](#Contents.html_rDeu12_29)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^"When YHWH, your God, will cut off the nations that you're coming
there to dispossess from in front of you, and you'll dispossess them and
live in their land,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_29.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:30](#Contents.html_rDeu12_30)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^watch yourself in case you'll be trapped after them, after their
destruction from in front of you, and in case you'll inquire about their
gods, saying, 'How did these nations serve their gods? And I'll do
that---I, too.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_30.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 12:31](#Contents.html_rDeu12_31)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^You shall *not* do this for YHWH, your God, because they did every
offensive thing of YHWH that He hates for their gods, because they would
also burn their sons and their daughters in fire to their gods!

![Image](images/Deu_Chap12_31.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy13.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:1](#Contents.html_rDeu13_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **13** {.h2a}

^1^"Everything that I command you: you shall be watchful to do it. You
shall not add onto it, and you shall not subtract from it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:1. **You shall not add onto it, and you shall not subtract from it**.
Moses repeats the principle of not adding to or taking away from the
law. This repetition emphasizes its extreme importance. It is a command
that affects all other commandments. See the comment on Deut 4:2.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:2](#Contents.html_rDeu13_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"When a prophet or one who has a dream will get up among you and will
give you a sign or a wonder,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:3](#Contents.html_rDeu13_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^and the sign or the wonder of which he spoke to you---saying, 'Let's
go after other gods,' whom you haven't known, 'and let's serve
them'---will come to pass,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:4](#Contents.html_rDeu13_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^you shall not listen to that prophet's words or to that one who has
the dream, because YHWH, your God, is testing you, to know whether you
are loving YHWH, your God, with all your heart and with all your soul.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:5](#Contents.html_rDeu13_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^You shall go after YHWH, your God, and you shall fear *Him,* and you
shall observe *His* commandments and listen to *His* voice and serve
*Him* and cling to *Him.*

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:6](#Contents.html_rDeu13_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And that prophet or that one who has the dream shall be put to death,
because he spoke a misrepresentation about YHWH, your God, who brought
you out from the land of Egypt and who redeemed you from a house of
slaves, to drive you from the way in which YHWH, your God, commanded you
to go. So you shall burn away what is bad from among you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:6. **burn away what is bad**. This is the first of nine occurrences
of this expression in Deuteronomy. Until now Moses has used the word
literally to describe the burning fire on Mount Sinai (see the comment
on 4:11). Now he turns it from describing what God has done to conveying
figuratively what the people of Israel must do themselves. This device
of using a word in two ways so as to compare what God does and what
humans do occurs elsewhere in Deuteronomy as well. Notably, in this very
verse, the false prophet or dreamer is described as "driving" Israel
from their God. Later God will be described as "driving" Israel from its
land because of their apostasy (see the comment on Deut 30:1). In this
way, Deuteronomy is an exquisitely woven tapestry of language, pointing
to connections and to causes and effects in human behavior.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:7](#Contents.html_rDeu13_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^"When your brother, your mother's son or your father's son, or the
wife of your bosom, or your friend who is as your own self will entice
you in secret, saying, 'Let's go and serve other gods,' whom you haven't
known, you and your fathers,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:8](#Contents.html_rDeu13_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^from the gods of the peoples who are all around you, those close to
you or those far from you, from one end of the earth to the other end of
the earth,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:9](#Contents.html_rDeu13_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^you shall not consent to him, and you shall not listen to him, and
your eye shall not pity him, and you shall not have compassion and shall
not cover it up for him.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:10](#Contents.html_rDeu13_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^But you shall *kill* him. Your hand shall be on him first to put him
to death, and all the people's hand thereafter.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:11](#Contents.html_rDeu13_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And you shall stone him with stones so he dies because he sought to
drive you away from YHWH, your God, who brought you out from the land of
Egypt, from a house of slaves.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:12](#Contents.html_rDeu13_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And all Israel will hear and fear and won't continue to do a bad
thing like this among you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:13](#Contents.html_rDeu13_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^"When you'll hear in one of your cities that YHWH, your God, is
giving you to live there, saying,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:14](#Contents.html_rDeu13_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^'Good-for-nothing people have gone out from among you and driven
away their city's residents, saying: "Let's go and serve other gods,"
whom you haven't known,'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:14. **Good-for-nothing**. The Hebrew
*b![image](images/e-image.jpg)lîya'al* appears to be a compound of two
words meaning "without value" or "without benefit." This corresponds to
the English expression "good-for-nothing," which fits with every
occurrence of the expression in the *Tanak*'s narrative books.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:15](#Contents.html_rDeu13_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^and you'll inquire and investigate and ask well, and, here, the
thing is true---this offensive thing was done among you---

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:16](#Contents.html_rDeu13_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^you shall strike that city's residents by the sword, completely
destroying it and everything in it and its animals with the sword.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_16.jpg)

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_16a.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:16. **completely destroying it and everything in it**. On the extreme
violence that is commanded in destroying those (Israelites) who would
lead one to worship other gods, or in destroying an entire Israelite
town that worships other gods (including any righteous; cf. Sodom):
again the Torah distinguishes between ethical sins and ritual sins.
Ethical sins (those that occur between a human and one's fellow humans;
theft, for example) are judged in terms of each individual person's
actions, and the person's intent is taken into account. Ritual sins
(those that are between a human and God; blasphemy, for example) are
judged in terms of *groups* and *zones,* and intent is not an issue.
Thus, when Achan violates the law of *![image](images/hdot.jpg)erem* in
Joshua 7, the text says that "*the children of Israel*" violated the
*![image](images/hdot.jpg)erem* (7:1)---and all of Israel suffers a
defeat. Achan takes items that were forbidden to the Israelites, and he
hides them in his tent; and the result is that not only is Achan stoned
and burned, but his family, his animals, everything that was
[]{#Deuteronomy13.html_page_51}in the tent, and also the tent itself are
stoned and burned.

Questions about the harsh response to Israelites who turn to (or seduce
others to) pagan worship must be addressed in this light. Worship of
other gods is the ultimate ritual sin in Deuteronomy. As with all ritual
infringements, the perpetrators must be utterly eliminated from Israel.
If it is an entire city, then that city must be eliminated from
Israel---including any righteous members of the community and even the
animals.

All of this may seem foreign and barbaric in our time. That is because
(1) Israel has not been a religious state for 2000 years; and (2) after
the destruction of the Second Temple, ritual became less central in
Judaism, and emphasis on ethics rose. Also, (3) monotheism has defeated
pagan religion in Western civilization, so we no longer feel the degree
of threat that authors of the Bible felt toward it.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:17](#Contents.html_rDeu13_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And you shall gather all its spoil into the middle of its square,
and you shall burn the city and all its spoil in fire entirely to YHWH,
your God, and it shall be a tell eternally. It shall not be rebuilt
again.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

13:17. **a tell**. A tell is a hill that came to exist as a result of
successive layers of cities being built on top of one another. Because a
tell is composed of sand and rock and debris rather than soil, very
little grass or vegetation grows on it. Such a mound is a treasure to an
archaeologist, but it is a curse here in the text: not only is an
apostate city to be destroyed, but it is to become a tell permanently,
never to be rebuilt.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:18](#Contents.html_rDeu13_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And let nothing from the complete destruction cling in your hand, so
that YHWH will turn back from His flaring anger and will give you mercy.
And He'll be merciful to you and multiply you as He swore to your
fathers

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 13:19](#Contents.html_rDeu13_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^when you'll listen to the voice of YHWH, your God, to observe all
His commandments that I command you today, to do what is right in the
eyes of YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap13_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy14.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:1](#Contents.html_rDeu14_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **14** {.h2a}

^1^"You are children of YHWH, your God. You shall not cut yourselves and
shall not make a bald place between your eyes for the dead.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:1. **cut yourselves**. This commandment is already given, in
different language, in Lev 19:28. Now the commandment not to "make a
bald place" is added to it. In Leviticus this latter commandment refers
only to priests (21:5). In some matters, such as entry into the
Tabernacle, priests are different from the rest of the people. But in
certain other matters, all Israelites are to behave like priests,
reflecting the idea that "you are a holy people to YHWH," which is
stated in the next verse (Deut 14:2). These cases of imitation of the
priests act as a sign and a reminder of what Israel was told at the
beginning of the covenant at Sinai: if they will listen and observe the
covenant, they will be a "kingdom of priests" (Exod 19:6).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:2](#Contents.html_rDeu14_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^Because you are a holy people to YHWH, your God, and YHWH has chosen
you to become a treasured people to Him out of all the peoples who are
on the face of the earth.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:3](#Contents.html_rDeu14_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^"You shall not eat any offensive thing.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:4](#Contents.html_rDeu14_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^This is the animal that you shall eat: ox, lamb of sheep, and lamb of
goats,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:4. **This is the animal that you shall eat**. On the dietary laws,
cf. the comments on Leviticus 11.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:5](#Contents.html_rDeu14_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^deer and gazelle and roebuck and wild goat and bison and antelope and
mountain sheep

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:6](#Contents.html_rDeu14_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^and every animal that has a hoof and that has a split of hooves in
two, that regurgitates cud, among animals, you shall eat it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:7](#Contents.html_rDeu14_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^Except you shall not eat this out of those that regurgitate the cud
and out of those that have a hoof: the camel and the rock-badger and the
hare, because they regurgitate cud and do not have a hoof; they are
impure to you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:8](#Contents.html_rDeu14_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

You shall not eat from their meat, and you shall not touch their
carcass.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:9](#Contents.html_rDeu14_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^"You shall eat this out of all that are in the water: you shall eat
every one that has fins and scales.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:10](#Contents.html_rDeu14_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And you shall not eat any one that does not have fins and scales; it
is impure to you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:11](#Contents.html_rDeu14_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^"And you shall eat any pure bird.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:12](#Contents.html_rDeu14_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And this is what you shall not eat from them: the eagle and the
vulture and the black vulture

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:13](#Contents.html_rDeu14_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^and the kite and the falcon by its kind

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

14:13. **kite and the falcon**. There are problems, apparently scribal,
in this verse. The first word,
*r![image](images/a-image.jpg)'![image](images/a-image.jpg)h,* is an
apparent *resh*/*dalet* scribal error for
*d![image](images/a-image.jpg)'![image](images/a-image.jpg)h,* as it
appears in Lev 11:14. The third word,
*dayy![image](images/a-image.jpg)h,* may be a further confusion of
*d![image](images/a-image.jpg)'![image](images/a-image.jpg)h.* It does
not appear in the Septuagint.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:14](#Contents.html_rDeu14_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^and every raven by its kind

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^and the eagle owl and the nighthawk and the seagull and the hawk by

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

*[Deuteronomy 14:16](#Contents.html_rDeu14_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^the little owl and the great owl and the white owl

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^and the pelican and the fish hawk and the cormorant

*[Deuteronomy 14:18](#Contents.html_rDeu14_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^and the stork and the heron by its kind and the hoopoe and the bat.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:19](#Contents.html_rDeu14_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^"And every swarming thing of flying creatures; it is impure to you.
It shall not be eaten.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:20](#Contents.html_rDeu14_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^You shall eat every pure bird.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:21](#Contents.html_rDeu14_21)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^"You shall not eat any carcass. You shall give it to the alien who
is in your gates, and he will eat it, or sell to a foreigner. Because
you are a holy people to YHWH, your God.

"You shall not cook a kid in its mother's milk.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_21.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy14.html_page_52}14:21. **a kid in its mother's milk**.
This is the third occurrence of this command (Exod 23:19; 34:26), and it
is the first time that it is grouped with the laws of forbidden animals.
It does not appear in the corresponding list in Leviticus 11. Its origin
and purpose are thus different from the other dietary laws. Its original
meaning may have been ritual or moral (see the comment on Exod 23:19),
but it belongs to a different category of law from the prohibitions of
eating pigs and other animals.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:22](#Contents.html_rDeu14_22)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^"You shall *tithe* all of your seed's produce that comes out of the
field year by year.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_22.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:23](#Contents.html_rDeu14_23)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And you shall eat the tithe of your grain, your wine, and your oil
and the firstborn of your herd and your flock in front of YHWH, your
God, in the place that He will choose to tent His name there, so that
you'll learn to fear YHWH, your God, all the days.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_23.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:24](#Contents.html_rDeu14_24)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^And if the way will be too long for you because you won't be able to
carry it because the place that YHWH, your God, will choose to set His
name there will be far from you, because YHWH, your God, will bless you,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_24.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:25](#Contents.html_rDeu14_25)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^then you shall give it by money. And you shall enclose the money in
your hand and go to the place that YHWH, your God, will choose.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_25.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:26](#Contents.html_rDeu14_26)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And you shall spend the money for anything that your soul will
desire: for herd and for flock and for wine and for beer and for
anything that your soul will ask of you. And you shall eat there in
front of YHWH, your God, and you shall rejoice, you and your household.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_26.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:27](#Contents.html_rDeu14_27)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And the Levite who is in your gates: you shall not leave him,
because he doesn't have a portion and legacy with you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_27.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:28](#Contents.html_rDeu14_28)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^"At the end of three years you shall bring out all the tithe of your
produce in that year and leave it within your gates,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_28.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 14:29](#Contents.html_rDeu14_29)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/Deu_Chap14_29.jpg)

[]{#Deuteronomy15.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 15:1](#Contents.html_rDeu15_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **15** {.h2a}

^1^"At the end of seven years you shall make a remission.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap15_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 15:2](#Contents.html_rDeu15_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And this is the matter of the remission: every holder of a loan is to
remit what he has lent his neighbor. He shall not demand it of his
neighbor and his brother, because a remission for YHWH has been called.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap15_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:2. **demand**. This is the same root that is understood in its noun
form to mean taskmasters in the story of the enslavement in Egypt (Exod
3:7; 5:10). As a verb it means to require, demand, or exact something
from someone.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 15:3](#Contents.html_rDeu15_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^You may demand it of a foreigner, but your hand shall remit whatever
of yours is with your brother.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap15_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 15:4](#Contents.html_rDeu15_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^Nonetheless, there won't be an indigent one among you, because YHWH
will *bless* you in the land that YHWH, your God, is giving you as a
legacy, to take possession of it---

![Image](images/Deu_Chap15_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 15:5](#Contents.html_rDeu15_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^only if you'll *listen* to the voice of YHWH, your God, to be
watchful to do all of this commandment that I command you today.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap15_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 15:6](#Contents.html_rDeu15_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^When YHWH, your God, will have blessed you as He spoke to you, then
you'll lend to many nations, but you won't borrow; and you'll dominate
many nations, but they won't dominate you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap15_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 15:7](#Contents.html_rDeu15_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^"When there will be an indigent one among you from one of your
brothers within one of your gates in your land that YHWH, your God, is
giving you, you shall not fortify your heart and shall not shut your
hand from your brother who is indigent.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap15_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 15:8](#Contents.html_rDeu15_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^But you shall *open* your hand to him and shall *lend* to him, enough
for his shortage that he has.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap15_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 15:9](#Contents.html_rDeu15_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^Watch yourself in case there will be something good-for-nothing in
your heart, saying, 'The seventh year, the remission year, is getting
close,' and your eye will be bad toward your brother who is indigent,
and you won't give to him; and he'll call to YHWH about you, and it will
be a sin in you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap15_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 15:10](#Contents.html_rDeu15_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^You *shall* give to him, and your heart shall not be bad when you're
giving to him, because on account of this thing YHWH, your God, will
bless you in everything you do and in everything your hand has taken on.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap15_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 15:11](#Contents.html_rDeu15_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^Because there won't stop being an indigent in the land. On account
of this I command you, saying: you shall *open* your hand to your
brother, to your poor, and to your indigent in your land.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap15_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

15:11. **there won't stop being an indigent in the land**. This is
generally understood to mean that there will never cease to be poor
people. And the problem is that just a few verses earlier the text says
the opposite: "there won't be an indigent one among you" (15:4). Ibn
Ezra and others have explained this apparent contradiction by noting
that the statement that "there won't be an indigent one among you" goes
on to say "only if you'll *lis[]{#Deuteronomy15.html_page_53}ten*\.... "
Since Israel will not, in fact, listen in the future, therefore "there
won't stop being an indigent in the land." Ramban criticizes this view,
saying that it is unimaginable that the Torah would say that the Jewish
people will never come to listen to the Torah's commandments. It seems
to me that the problem arises because everyone takes this verse to mean
that there will *never* stop being indigent people. But it simply says
"there won't stop." I take that to mean that poverty will not just come
to a stop on its own one day---without any action by humans. That is one
of the ways in which this word is used elsewhere in the Torah. Sarah's
menopause is described as "Sarah had *stopped* having the way of women"
(Gen 18:11). The end of the plague of hail in Egypt is described as "the
shower and the hail *stopped*" (Exod 9:34). So here, too, the full verse
says that poverty will not simply stop---the poor will not just
disappear---and "*On account of this* I command you, saying: you shall
open your hand to your brother, to your poor, and to your indigent in
your land." The statements here and in v. 4 are consistent: there will
be no poverty only if people act to end it.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 15:12](#Contents.html_rDeu15_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^"When your Hebrew brother or sister will be sold to you, then he
shall work for you six years, and in the seventh year you shall let him
go liberated from you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap15_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy15.html_page_54}15:12. **let him go**. This is the same
term that was used for Pharaoh's release of the Israelite slaves: "Let
my people go." The Israelite should do no less than Pharaoh! The
Israelite should let his slave go, without the coercion that Pharaoh
needed, and after no more than six years.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 15:13](#Contents.html_rDeu15_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

empty-handed.

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

## **16** {.h2a}

^1^"Observe the month of Abib, and you shall make Passover for YHWH,
your God, because in the month of Abib YHWH, your God, brought you out
from Egypt at night.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap16_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:1. **the month of Abib**. See the comment on Exod 13:4.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 16:2](#Contents.html_rDeu16_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And you shall make a Passover sacrifice to YHWH, your God, of the
flock and herd, in the place that YHWH will choose to tent His name
there.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap16_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

*[Deuteronomy 16:3](#Contents.html_rDeu16_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/Deu_Chap16_03.jpg)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[]{#Deuteronomy16.html_page_55}count seven weeks.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And you shall rejoice on your festival---you and your son and your

*[Deuteronomy 16:15](#Contents.html_rDeu16_15)*

^15^Seven days you shall celebrate for YHWH, your God, in the place that

![Image](images/Deu_Chap16_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 16:16](#Contents.html_rDeu16_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^"Three times in the year every male of yours shall appear in front
of YHWH, your God, in the place that He will choose: on the Festival of

Booths. And he shall not appear in front of YHWH empty-handed:

![Image](images/Deu_Chap16_16.jpg)
