------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 16:17](#Contents.html_rDeu16_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^each according to what his hand can give, according to the blessing
of YHWH, your God, that He has given you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap16_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 16:18](#Contents.html_rDeu16_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**J[UDGES]{.small}**

^18^"You shall put judges and officers in all your gates that YHWH, your
God, is giving you, for your tribes, and they shall judge the people:
judgment with justice.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap16_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:18. **judgment with justice**. It should be obvious, but,
regrettably, it needs to be said: judgment and justice are not the same
thing. Judges and lawyers can be part of one of the noblest endeavors of
humankind: the law. When they fail to pursue justice in their
performance of judgment, they pervert and degrade the law, and thus they
demean humankind---nothing less. The same goes for clerks, bailiffs, law
enforcement officers, and everyone else involved in the execution of
justice. They elevate or lower themselves---and all of us---by the
degree of their commitment to justice.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 16:19](#Contents.html_rDeu16_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^You shall not bend judgment, you shall not recognize a face, and you
shall not take a bribe, because bribery will blind the eyes of the wise
and undermine the words of the virtuous.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap16_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 16:20](#Contents.html_rDeu16_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^Justice, justice you shall pursue, so that you'll *live,* and you'll
take possession of the land that YHWH, your God, is giving you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap16_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:20. **Justice, justice**. Many interpretations have been given for
the repetition of the word *justice* in this famous verse, from taking
it to mean that one should go to a good court (Siphre; Rashi) to
midrashic and Kabbalistic meanings (Ramban), as well as the most basic
explanation: that it is repeated for emphasis. It has also been taken to
mean "justice alone" (Jeffrey Tigay). And it is often said to mean that
one must pursue justice in a just way: one cannot use improper means to
achieve it. For example, police and prosecutors must not bend the law in
order to get a conviction. The repetition might also be taken to mean
that one must pursue justice for both sides: both the plaintiff and the
defense in a civil matter, and both the defendant and the prosecution
("the people") in a criminal matter. Most probably, though, we should
understand it with those who take it to be a matter of emphasis. Lacking
italics or exclamation points, Biblical Hebrew has few ways other than
repetition to express emphasis. That is the function of placing an
infinitive before a cognate verb (see the comment on Exod 19:12), and
here it is achieved by repeating the noun.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 16:21](#Contents.html_rDeu16_21)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^"You shall not plant an Asherah of any wood near the altar of YHWH,
your God, that you will make.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap16_21.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:21. Asherah. The word is used for the goddess of that name or for a
tree or wooden pole that was associated with the worship of Asherah (or
possibly other deities as well).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 16:22](#Contents.html_rDeu16_22)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^And you shall not set up a pillar, which YHWH, your God, hates.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap16_22.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

16:22. **you shall not set up a pillar**. Moses set up pillars himself
(Exod 24:4). So did Jacob (Gen 28:18; 32:45; 35:14,20). So why does
Moses forbid it now? Rashi and some modern commentators have thought
that pillars were originally acceptable but came to be associated with
pagan worship and so were abandoned. That may or may not be the actual
historical development behind these changes, but that does not seem to
be what is presented here in the text, because Moses moves from making
them to saying that God hates them, all within the forty
[]{#Deuteronomy16.html_page_59}years in the wilderness. Perhaps we
should understand it to mean that Moses is prohibiting the people from
making pillars after the formal altar and Temple are built in the land,
because then an altar might be taken as an alternative locus of God.
Here in Deuteronomy Moses repeatedly emphasizes that there can be only
one place of worship, one structure in which God's name is housed. Thus,
even Joshua sets up a large stone, which seems like a pillar (although
the word is not used there; Josh 24:26), but this may be before the
establishment of an official central place of worship.

[]{#Deuteronomy17.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:1](#Contents.html_rDeu17_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **17** {.h2a}

^1^"You shall not sacrifice to YHWH, your God, a bull or a sheep in
which will be any injury, any bad thing, because that is an offensive
thing of YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:2](#Contents.html_rDeu17_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^"If there will be found among you, in one of your gates that YHWH,
your God, is giving you, a man or woman who will do what is bad in the
eyes of YHWH, your God, to violate His covenant,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:3](#Contents.html_rDeu17_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^and will go and serve other gods and bow to them and to the sun or to
the moon or to any of the array of the skies, which I did not command,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:4](#Contents.html_rDeu17_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^and it will be told to you and you will hear it, and you will inquire
well, and, here, it is true, the thing is right, this offensive thing
has been done in Israel,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:5](#Contents.html_rDeu17_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^then you shall bring that man or that woman who did this bad thing
out to your gates, the man or the woman, and you shall stone them with
stones so they die.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:6](#Contents.html_rDeu17_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^"On the word of two witnesses or three witnesses shall the one who is
to die be put to death. One shall not be put to death on the word of one
witness.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:7](#Contents.html_rDeu17_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^The witnesses' hand shall be on him first to put him to death, and
all the people's hand after that. So you shall burn away what is bad
from among you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:7. **witnesses first ... all the people after**. Numerous
commentators discuss the first half of this verse but not the second. It
is of course significant that witnesses must know that, if they testify
against someone in a capital case, they will have to strike the first
blows against the defendant themselves. That conveys vividly how serious
their testimony is. But it is at least as significant that *all the
people* participate in the execution as well. Capital punishment is the
act of a community---in ancient Israel and even more so in democracies.
In biblical law, the community must know that if they are going to have
capital punishment they must understand that this means that the
community as a whole is taking lives. So that there can be no mistaking
this point, the entire community must participate in the execution. In
postbiblical societies where the entire people does not participate
personally, this point may easily be forgotten, but it is true all the
same: a community that has capital punishment is responsible *as a
community* for the lives it takes. It is not just the executioner or
just the witnesses or just the courts.

Perhaps this fact---that in ancient Israel all the people had to carry
out the execution---was one of the forces that led Israel to make
capital punishment almost impossible in rabbinic law.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:8](#Contents.html_rDeu17_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^"If a matter for judgment will be too daunting for you, between blood
and blood, between law and law, and between injury and injury, matters
of disputes in your gates, then you shall get up and go up to the place
that []{#Deuteronomy17.html_page_57}YHWH, your God, will choose.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:8. **If a matter for judgment will be too daunting for you**. In
every law code there must be a mechanism for change and for application
to new and difficult situations. Even if the law is divine law, there
must be such a mechanism, and *the Torah recognizes this* and *provides
for it.* It directs that in such difficult questions, the authorities
(judges and priests; i.e., authorities in law and religion) in each age
shall determine what to do. This has always been done in Judaism. The
most obvious distinction among the movements in Judaism has been their
different views of how the law changes. The primary consideration when
authorities, including scholars and rabbis, determine that a law is
changed is that they do so with wisdom and reverence, and not with
arrogance.

17:8. **blood and blood**. Cases involving the taking of life.

17:8. **between law and law**. Cases that are complex because more than
one law applies.

17:8. **between injury and injury**. Cases involving physical hurt.

17:8. **disputes**. The term (Hebrew *rîb*) meant a quarrel, as in the
quarreling at the rock that leads to the name Meribah (Exodus 17;
Numbers 20), and it came to refer to a lawsuit, a legal dispute.
Israel's prophets use it to describe God's judgment of Israel in legal
terms (Hos 12:3; Mic 6:2). This is known in biblical scholarship as a
"covenant lawsuit." The covenant between God and Israel is a legal
relationship, formulated in the legal terminology of the ancient Near
East (see the comment on Exod 20:1). In a *rîb* oracle, the prophet
describes God as bringing a case against His people for violating the
covenant, which can end in judgment and punishment.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:9](#Contents.html_rDeu17_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And you shall come to the Levite priests and to the judge who will be
in those days, and you shall inquire, and they will tell you the matter
of judgment.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:9. **the Levite priests**. Historically, originally all Levites were
priests. Later the Levite group that identified itself as descendants of
Aaron took exclusive hold of the priesthood and limited all the other
Levites to a secondary role. Thereafter a distinction was made between
priests and Levites. Deuteronomy reflects the original status of all
Levites and does not make this distinction between priests and Levites.
(I have discussed this history of priesthood in *Who Wrote the Bible?*)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:10](#Contents.html_rDeu17_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And you shall do according to the word on the matter that they will
tell you from that place that YHWH will choose, and you shall be
watchful to do according to everything that they will instruct you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:11](#Contents.html_rDeu17_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^You shall do it according to the word of the instruction that they
will give you and according to the judgment that they will say to you.
You shall not turn from the thing that they will tell you, right or
left.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:12](#Contents.html_rDeu17_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And the man who will act presumptuously, not listening to the priest
who is standing to serve YHWH, your God, there, or to the judge: that
man shall die. So you shall burn away what is bad from Israel.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:13](#Contents.html_rDeu17_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And all the people will listen and fear and won't act presumptuously
anymore.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:14](#Contents.html_rDeu17_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^"When you'll come to the land that YHWH, your God, is giving you,
and you'll take possession of it and live in it, and you'll say, 'Let me
set a king over me like all the nations that are around me,'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:15](#Contents.html_rDeu17_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^you *shall* set a king over you whom YHWH, your God, will choose!
You shall set a king from among your brothers over you. You may not put
a foreign man, who is not your brother, over you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:15. **you** ***shall*** **set a king over you**. The Torah permits
Israel to have a king if they wish. It is placed in their hands to
decide if they want one: "When ... you'll say, 'Let me set a king over
me ... ,' you *shall* set a king over you." But the king must be chosen
by God (see the next comment). Later, Gideon and Samuel will question
whether it is right for Israel to have a king, saying that God should be
Israel's king (Judg 8:23; 1 Sam 8:6). But they do not base their
opposition to monarchy on the Torah. Both Gideon and Samuel are judges
and also priests (Judg 8:24--27), and they naturally think of God's will
as being properly expressed through priests, and so they see kings as a
threat to the order that they know. But the Torah permits the people to
have kings if they wish. And so God tells Samuel to give the people a
king, even though their wish for a king, in that particular case,
involves a rejection of God (1 Sam 8:7,22).

17:15. **whom God will choose**. This means that a prophet must
designate the king as chosen by God. Thus Samuel designates Saul and
David, Nathan designates Solomon, and Ahijah designates Jeroboam. The
stories of the kings of Israel and Judah in the books of Samuel, Kings,
and Chronicles convey that kings are acceptable if the people want them,
but their power is limited and balanced by the power of prophets. And
kings who do not listen to prophets, or who imprison or execute them, do
so at their peril.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:16](#Contents.html_rDeu17_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^Only, he shall not get himself many horses, and he shall not bring
the people back to Egypt in order to get many horses, when YHWH has said
to you, 'You shall not go back this way ever again.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

17:16. **not get himself many horses**. Maintaining forces with a great
many horses means the development of a small upper class plus a large
class of serfs or servants to support and serve them. This is one of
several laws in the Torah that work to counter the development of such a
two-tiered feudal system. And so, when King David defeats Hadadezer and
captures 1,700 chariots and horsemen, he hamstrings all but a small
number of the horses (2 Sam 8:4). Why would he deliberately give up a
prize of such tremendous value? Because he is obeying this Law of the
King.

17:16. **YHWH has said to you, 'You shall not go back.'** No such words
from God are reported prior to this. This may appear to be a problem,
but it may simply be that this *is* Moses' report of God's words on this
matter.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:17](#Contents.html_rDeu17_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^And he shall not get himself many wives, so his heart will not turn
away. And he shall not get himself very much silver and gold.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:18](#Contents.html_rDeu17_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And it will be, when he sits on his kingdom's throne, that he shall
write himself a copy of this instruction on a scroll from in front of
the Levite priests.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy17.html_page_62}17:18. **a copy of this instruction**. It
is unclear whether this means a copy of this Law of the King or a copy
of the full law code of Deuteronomy in which it is now contained. In
either case, this means that Israel has a constitutional monarchy.
Baruch Halpern has shown substantial evidence that Israel's kings were
bound by this Law of the King from the time of the first king, Saul
(Halpern, *The Constitution of the Monarchy in Israel*). That means that
Israel was historically a constitutional monarchy, with a written
constitution, more than a thousand years [B.C.E]{.smallcaps}.

17:18. **a copy ... from in front of the Levite priests**. That is, the
Levites have the text of the law, and the king makes himself a copy from
the text that they have before them.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:19](#Contents.html_rDeu17_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And it shall be with him, and he shall read it all the days of his
life, so that he will learn to fear YHWH, his God, to observe all the
words of this instruction and these laws, to do them,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 17:20](#Contents.html_rDeu17_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^so his heart will not be elevated above his brothers, and so he will
not turn from the commandment, right or left, and so he will extend days
over his kingdom, he and his sons, within Israel.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap17_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy18.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:1](#Contents.html_rDeu18_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **18** {.h2a}

^1^"The Levite priests, all the tribe of Levi, shall not have a portion
and legacy with Israel. They shall eat YHWH's offerings by fire and
*His* legacy.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:2](#Contents.html_rDeu18_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^But he shall not have a legacy among his brothers. YHWH: *He* is his
legacy, as He spoke to him.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:3](#Contents.html_rDeu18_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And this shall be the rule for the priests from the people, from
those who make a sacrifice, whether an ox or a sheep: he shall give the
shoulder, the cheeks, and the stomach.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:4](#Contents.html_rDeu18_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^You shall give him the first of your grain, your wine, and your oil,
and the first shearing of your sheep.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:5](#Contents.html_rDeu18_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^Because YHWH, your God, has chosen him from all your tribes to stand
to serve in YHWH's name, he and his sons, for all time.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:6](#Contents.html_rDeu18_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^[]{#Deuteronomy18.html_page_58}6^"And when a Levite will come from one
of your gates, from all of Israel, where he lives, then he shall come as
much as his soul desires to the place that YHWH will choose

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:7](#Contents.html_rDeu18_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^and serve in the name of YHWH, his God, like all of his Levite
brothers who are standing there in front of YHWH.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:8](#Contents.html_rDeu18_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^They shall eat, portion for portion, aside from his sales of
patrimony.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:8. **portion for portion**. Any Levite who comes to serve at the
central place of worship is supposed to receive the same share of the
priestly food as every other Levite. And this share is not affected by
whether he has any other income from family property, i.e., "from his
sales of patrimony."

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:9](#Contents.html_rDeu18_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^"When you come to the land that YHWH, your God, is giving you, you
shall not learn to do like the offensive things of those nations.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:10](#Contents.html_rDeu18_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^There shall not be found among you someone who passes his son or his
daughter through fire, one who practices enchantment, a soothsayer or a
diviner or a sorcerer

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:11](#Contents.html_rDeu18_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^or one who casts spells or who asks of a ghost or of a spirit of an
acquaintance or inquires of the dead,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:12](#Contents.html_rDeu18_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^because everyone who does these is an offensive thing of YHWH, and
because of these offensive things YHWH, your God, is dispossessing them
from in front of you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:13](#Contents.html_rDeu18_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^You shall be unblemished with YHWH, your God,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:13. **unblemished**. This word is the mark of the recipients of the
first two covenants with God. It is the word that is used to describe
the merit of Noah; and God tells Abraham to be unblemished as well in
the opening words of the covenant (Gen 6:9; 17:1). (The only other
person in the *Tanak* to be described by these words is Job.) Now *all*
the people of Israel are recipients of the third covenant, and so all of
them are instructed to be like Noah and Abraham. They may receive the
covenant initially on the merit of Abraham, but they must maintain it on
their own integrity.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:14](#Contents.html_rDeu18_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^because these nations whom you are dispossessing listen to
soothsayers and enchanters, but you: YHWH, your God, has not permitted
such for you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:15](#Contents.html_rDeu18_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^YHWH, your God, will raise up for you a prophet from among you, from
your brothers, like me. You shall listen to him---

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:15. **God will raise up a prophet like me**. But the end of the Torah
says: "A prophet did *not* rise again in Israel like Moses" (Deut
34:10)! Still, this need not be a contradiction. The context of the
passage here in Deuteronomy 18 is that Israel should not listen to
soothsayers and enchanters, but only to a prophet from God, a "prophet
like me." Moses reminds the people that this is what they asked for at
Sinai: that they not hear God's voice directly anymore, but that they
hear from God only through prophets. The passage at the end of the Torah
visibly means that no other prophet was as great as Moses. It is simply
a linguistic matter: the range of the expression "to be like someone" is
wide enough to have these two meanings (and several more).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:16](#Contents.html_rDeu18_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^in accordance with everything that you asked from YHWH, your God, at
Horeb in the day of the assembly, saying, 'Let me not continue to hear
the voice of YHWH, my God, and let me not see this big fire anymore, so
I won't die!'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:17](#Contents.html_rDeu18_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^"And YHWH said to me, 'They've been good in what they've spoken.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:18](#Contents.html_rDeu18_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^I'll raise up a prophet for them from among their brothers, like
you, and I'll put my words in his mouth, and he'll speak to them
everything that I'll command him.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:18. **God will raise up a prophet like me**. But the end of the Torah
says: "A prophet did *not* rise again in Israel like Moses" (Deut
34:10)! Still, this need not be a contradiction. The context of the
passage here in Deuteronomy 18 is that Israel should not listen to
soothsayers and enchanters, but only to a prophet from God, a "prophet
like me." Moses reminds the people that this is what they asked for at
Sinai: that they not hear God's voice directly anymore, but that they
hear from God only through prophets. The passage at the end of the Torah
visibly means that no other prophet was as great as Moses. It is simply
a linguistic matter: the range of the expression "to be like someone" is
wide enough to have these two meanings (and several more).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:19](#Contents.html_rDeu18_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^And it will be: the man who won't listen to my words that he'll
speak in my name, *I* shall require it from him!

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:20](#Contents.html_rDeu18_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^Just: the prophet who will presume to speak a thing in my name that
I didn't command him to speak, and who will speak in the name of other
gods---that prophet shall die.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:21](#Contents.html_rDeu18_21)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^"And if you'll say in your heart, 'How shall we know the thing, that
YHWH didn't speak it?!'---

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_21.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

18:21. **How shall we know**. It is one of the Bible's central and most
difficult questions: How does one tell a true prophet from a false one?
Moses tells the people that the way to tell a false prophet is by seeing
whether his prophecy comes true or not. But that is a little late, is it
not? The question was how to know *at the time of the prophecy* whether
it is from God. Moses' instruction appears to mean that one should go by
the prophet's past record. Even then, people's inclination seems to be
to disbelieve the true prophets. Even after Jeremiah's prophecies of
Jerusalem's fall come to pass (and the prophecies of those who oppose
[]{#Deuteronomy18.html_page_64}him fail), as soon as he gives a prophecy
that the people do not like they say, "You're speaking a lie. YHWH
hasn't sent you" (Jer 43:2)! So Moses' criterion for identifying false
prophets may seem simple and obvious, but the psychological point is
that people miss the obvious and turn instead to the comfortable.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 18:22](#Contents.html_rDeu18_22)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^when the prophet will speak in YHWH's name, and the thing won't be
and won't come to pass: that is the thing that YHWH did *not* say. The
prophet spoke it presumptuously. You shall not be fearful of him.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap18_22.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy19.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:1](#Contents.html_rDeu19_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **19** {.h2a}

^1^"When YHWH, your God, will cut off the nations whose land YHWH, your
God, is giving you, and you'll dispossess them and live in their cities
and in their houses,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:2](#Contents.html_rDeu19_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^you shall distinguish three cities within your land that YHWH, your
God, is giving you to take possession of it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:3](#Contents.html_rDeu19_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^You shall prepare the way and divide the border of your land that
YHWH, your God, will give you as a legacy into three sections, and it
will be for any murderer to flee there.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:4](#Contents.html_rDeu19_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And this is the case of the manslayer who will flee there and live:
one who will strike his neighbor without knowing, when he did not hate
him from the day before yesterday;

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:4. **from the day before yesterday**. Meaning: he had no prior malice
toward the person whom he killed.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:5](#Contents.html_rDeu19_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^and one who will come with his neighbor into the forest to cut down
trees, and his hand will be moved with the axe to cut down the tree, and
the axe head will come off the wood and find his neighbor, and he dies.
He shall flee to one of these cities and live.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:6](#Contents.html_rDeu19_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^In case an avenger of blood will pursue the manslayer when his heart
will be hot and will catch up to him because the way will be long, and
he'll strike him mortally though he does not have a sentence of death,
because he did not hate him from the day before yesterday,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:7](#Contents.html_rDeu19_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^on account of this I command you, saying, 'You shall distinguish
three cities.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:8](#Contents.html_rDeu19_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And if YHWH, your God, will widen your border as He swore to your
fathers and will give you all the land that He spoke to give to your
fathers,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:9](#Contents.html_rDeu19_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^when you'll observe all of this commandment to do it, that which I
command you today, to love YHWH, your God, and to go in His ways every
day, then you shall add three more cities to these three.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:10](#Contents.html_rDeu19_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^So innocent blood will not be spilled within your land that YHWH,
your God, is giving you as a legacy, and blood would be on you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:10. **blood would be on you**. Meaning: you would incur guilt for
spilled blood.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:11](#Contents.html_rDeu19_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^"But if there will be a man who hates his neighbor and will lie in
wait for him and get up against him and strike him mortally, and he
dies, and he will flee to one of these cities,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:12](#Contents.html_rDeu19_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^then his city's elders shall send and take him from there and put
him in the hand of the avenger of blood, so he will die.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:13](#Contents.html_rDeu19_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^Your eye shall not pity him. And you shall burn away the innocent
blood from Israel, and it will be well with you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:14](#Contents.html_rDeu19_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^"You shall not move your neighbor's landmark that the first ones
set, in your legacy that you will have in the land that YHWH, your God,
is giving you to take possession of it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:14. **your legacy that you will have in the land**. This chapter
gives laws protecting lives and property, which will apply once the
people arrive in the land. Most of the Torah thus looks toward the
well-being of the land from outside it. Only about 29 of the Torah's 187
chapters take place in the promised land. Moses never sets foot in the
land. And the phrase "Land of Israel" never occurs in the Torah. (The
land acquires this name only after the people of Israel have settled
there. It first occurs in 1 Sam 13:19.) Yet the Torah's story is focused
on and directed toward Israel constantly. It is the promise of the
covenant with Abraham. The promise is renewed to Isaac and Jacob.
Jacob's body is returned from Egypt to be buried there. Joseph asks to
be taken back for burial there []{#Deuteronomy19.html_page_65}someday as
well. And almost the entire content of Exodus through Numbers is the
story of the people's liberation and journey home. The Torah thus
already relates to the experience of the Jewish people in the
postbiblical world: For the last two millennia most of the Jews have
lived outside the promised land. They have lived all over the world and
have been good citizens of the countries where they settled while having
affection for the land of Israel, a consciousness of their origin there,
and a desire to contribute to it, travel there, and seek its well-being.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:15](#Contents.html_rDeu19_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^"One witness shall not get up against a man for any crime or for any
sin, in any sin that one will commit. On the word of two witnesses or on
the word of three witnesses a case shall stand up.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:16](#Contents.html_rDeu19_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^If a malicious witness will get up against a man to testify a
misrepresentation against him,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:17](#Contents.html_rDeu19_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^and the two people who have the dispute shall stand in front of
YHWH, in front of the priests and the judges who will be in those days,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:18](#Contents.html_rDeu19_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^and the judges will inquire well, and, here, the witness is a lying
witness, he testified a lie against his brother,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:19](#Contents.html_rDeu19_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^then you shall do to him as he schemed to do to his brother. So you
shall burn away what is bad from among you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:20](#Contents.html_rDeu19_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And those who remain will listen and fear and won't continue to do
anything like this bad thing anymore among you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 19:21](#Contents.html_rDeu19_21)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^And your eye shall not pity: life for life, eye for eye, tooth for
tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap19_21.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

19:21. **life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth**. See the comments
on Exod 21:23 and Lev 24:20.

[]{#Deuteronomy20.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:1](#Contents.html_rDeu20_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **20** {.h2a}

^1^"When you'll go out to war against your enemies, and you'll see
horses and chariots, a people more numerous than you, you shall not fear
them, because YHWH, your God, is with you, who brought you up from the
land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:2](#Contents.html_rDeu20_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And it will be, when you approach the war, that the priest shall go
over and speak to the people.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:3](#Contents.html_rDeu20_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And he shall say to them, 'Listen, Israel, you're approaching war
against your enemies today. Let your heart not be weak. Don't be afraid
and don't panic and don't be scared in front of them,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:4](#Contents.html_rDeu20_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^because YHWH, your God, is the one going with you to fight for you
with your enemies, to save you.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:5](#Contents.html_rDeu20_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^"And the officers shall speak to the people, saying, 'Who is a man
who has built a new house and has not dedicated it? Let him go, and let
him go back to his house, in case he would die in the war and another
man would dedicate it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:6](#Contents.html_rDeu20_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And who is a man who has planted a vineyard and has not desanctified
it? Let him go, and let him go back to his house, in case he would die
in the war and another man would desanctify it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

20:6. **desanctified**. This may relate to a law such as that in Lev
19:23--25, whereby a newly planted tree goes through a period during
which its fruit may not be eaten, followed by a period of being holy
("all of its fruit shall be holy"), and then a third period in which its
period of sanctification ends and one may eat it. In the present
context, then, it would refer to a man who has planted a vineyard but
has not yet eaten any of its fruit.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:7](#Contents.html_rDeu20_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And who is a man who has betrothed a woman and has not taken her? Let
him go, and let him go back to his house, in case he would die in the
war and another man would take her.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:8](#Contents.html_rDeu20_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And the officers shall continue to speak to the people and shall say,
'Who is a man who is afraid and weakhearted? Let him go, and go back to
his house, so he won't melt his brothers' heart like his heart.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:9](#Contents.html_rDeu20_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And it will be, when the officers finish speaking to the people, that
they shall appoint officers of the armies at the head of the people.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:10](#Contents.html_rDeu20_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^"When you'll approach a city to fight against it, then you shall
call to it for peace.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:11](#Contents.html_rDeu20_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And it will be, if it will answer you with peace and open up to you,
then it will be that all the people who are found in it shall become
yours for a work-company and shall serve you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:12](#Contents.html_rDeu20_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^And if it will not make peace with you, but it will make war with
you, then you shall besiege it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:13](#Contents.html_rDeu20_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And YHWH, your God, will put it in your hand, and you shall strike
all its males by the sword.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:14](#Contents.html_rDeu20_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^Only, you shall take as spoil the women and the infants and the
animals and everything that will be in the city, all its spoil, and you
shall eat your enemies' spoil that YHWH, your God, has given you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:15](#Contents.html_rDeu20_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^That is what you shall do to all the cities that are very distant
from you, those that are not from these nations' cities.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:16](#Contents.html_rDeu20_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^Only from these peoples' cities that YHWH, your God, is giving you
as a legacy shall you not let any soul live.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:17](#Contents.html_rDeu20_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^But you shall *completely* destroy them---the Hittite and the
Amorite, the Canaanite and the Perizzite, the Hivite and the
Jebusite---as YHWH, your God, has commanded you,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:18](#Contents.html_rDeu20_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^so they won't teach you to do things like all their offensive things
that they did for their gods, and you'll sin to YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:19](#Contents.html_rDeu20_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^"When you'll besiege a city many days, fighting against it to
capture it, you shall not destroy a tree of it, moving an axe at it,
because you'll eat from it, so you shall not cut it down; because is a
tree of the field a human, to go from in front of you in a siege?!

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 20:20](#Contents.html_rDeu20_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^Only a tree that you'll know that it isn't a tree for eating: that
one you may destroy and cut down so you may build a siege-work against
the city that is making war with you until its fall.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap20_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy21.html}
