![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 25:18](#Contents.html_rDeu25_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^how he fell upon you on the way and cut off all the weak ones at
your rear, when you were exhausted and tired, and he didn't fear God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap25_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 25:19](#Contents.html_rDeu25_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^So it shall be, when YHWH, your God, will give you rest from all
your enemies all around in the land that YHWH, your God, is giving you
as a legacy to take possession of it, you shall wipe out the memory of
Amalek from under the skies. You shall not forget.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap25_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy26.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:1](#Contents.html_rDeu26_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**W[HEN YOU'LL COME]{.small}**

## **26** {.h2a}

^1^"And it shall be, when you'll come to the land that YHWH, your God,
is giving you as a legacy, and you'll take possession of it and live in
it,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:2](#Contents.html_rDeu26_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^that you shall take from the first of all the land's fruit that
you'll bring in from your land that YHWH, your God, is giving you, and
set it in a basket and go to the place that YHWH, your God, will choose
to tent His name there.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:3](#Contents.html_rDeu26_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And you shall come to the priest who will be in those days and say to
him:

I declare today to YHWH, your God, that I've come to the land that YHWH
swore to our fathers to give to us.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:4](#Contents.html_rDeu26_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And the priest shall take the basket from your hand and set it down
in front of the altar of YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:5](#Contents.html_rDeu26_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And you shall answer and say in front of YHWH, your God:

My father was a perishing Aramean, so he went down to Egypt and resided
there with few persons and became a big, powerful, and numerous nation
there.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:6](#Contents.html_rDeu26_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And the Egyptians were bad to us and degraded us and imposed hard
work on us.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:7](#Contents.html_rDeu26_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And we cried out to YHWH, our fathers' God, and YHWH listened to our
voice and saw our degradation and our trouble and our oppression.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:8](#Contents.html_rDeu26_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And YHWH brought us out from Egypt with a strong hand and an
outstretched arm and with great fear and with signs and with wonders.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:9](#Contents.html_rDeu26_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And He brought us to this place and gave us this land, a land flowing
with milk and honey.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:10](#Contents.html_rDeu26_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And now, here, I've brought the first of the fruit of the land that
you've given me, YHWH.

And you shall set it down in front of YHWH, your God, and bow in front
of YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:11](#Contents.html_rDeu26_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And you shall rejoice in all the good that YHWH, your God, has given
to you and to your house, you and the Levite and the alien who is among
you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:12](#Contents.html_rDeu26_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^"When you'll finish doing all the tithe of your produce in the third
year, the year of the tithe, and you'll give it to the Levite, to the
alien, to the orphan, and to the widow, and they'll eat in your gates
and be full,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:13](#Contents.html_rDeu26_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^then you shall say in front of YHWH, your God:

I have taken away what was holy from the house, and I have also given it
to the Levite and to the alien, to the orphan, and to the widow
according to all your commandment that you've commanded me. I have not
violated and have not forgotten any of your commandments.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:14](#Contents.html_rDeu26_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^I have not eaten any of it while I was mourning, and I have not
taken any of it away while impure, and I have not given any of it to the
dead. I have listened to the voice of YHWH, my God. I have done
according to everything that you commanded me.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

26:14. **not given any of it to the dead**. The person declares that he
has not misused his tithed produce in any way that associates it with
death or impurity. This disallows offering the dead *tithed* food; but,
as many scholars have observed, this prohibition is not against making
*other* offerings of food to the dead. Giving food (and drink) for dead
ancestors was practiced in Israel and Judah at least until the reign of
King Hezekiah (c. 700 [B.C.E.]{.smallcaps}). Tombs have been excavated
(at Megiddo, Hazor, Gezer, Beth-Shemesh, Dothan) that had apertures cut
into their ceilings through which it would be possible to give offerings
to the dead, or that had storage jars placed directly over the heads of
the corpses. Beginning in the tenth century [B.C.E.]{.smallcaps}, open
ceramic bowls and flasks and jars for liquids, store-jars with dipper
juglets, plates, cooking pots, and wine decanters were placed in tombs
in Judah. The destruction of much of the countryside by the Assyrians,
followed by the centralization of worship under Hezekiah, dramatically
cut back on this practice.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:15](#Contents.html_rDeu26_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^Gaze from your holy abode, from the skies, and bless your people,
Israel, and the land that you've given us as you swore to our fathers, a
land flowing with milk and honey.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:16](#Contents.html_rDeu26_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^"This day YHWH, your God, commands you to do these laws and
judgments. And you shall be watchful and do them with all your heart and
all your soul.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

26:16. **This day**. This passage, vv. 16--19, concludes Moses' speech
that began at the start of chapter 5. In it, Moses formally pronounces
that the people and God have each made a commitment. The people have
declared that YHWH is to be their God and that they will keep His laws
and listen to Him. YHWH has declared that Israel is to be His treasured
people and that, if they will keep His laws, He will make them a
renowned, holy people. The performance of the lengthy corpus of law that
Moses has just taught (chapters 12--26, as well as the Decalogue in
chapter 5) is thus said to be mutually confirmed by God and the people.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:17](#Contents.html_rDeu26_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^You have proclaimed YHWH today to be God to you, and to go in His
ways and to observe His laws and His commandments and His judgments and
to listen to His voice.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:18](#Contents.html_rDeu26_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^And YHWH proclaimed you today to be a treasured people to Him as He
spoke to you---and to observe all His commandments---

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 26:19](#Contents.html_rDeu26_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^and to set you high above all the nations that He has made in praise
and name and beauty, and for you to be a holy people to YHWH, your God,
as He spoke."

![Image](images/Deu_Chap26_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy27.html}

*[Deuteronomy 27:1](#Contents.html_rDeu27_1)*

## **27** {.h2a}

^1^And Moses and Israel's elders commanded the people, saying, "Observe
all the commandment that I command you today.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:2](#Contents.html_rDeu27_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And it shall be, in the day that you'll cross the Jordan to the land
that YHWH, your God, is giving you, that you shall set up big stones and
cover them with plaster.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:3](#Contents.html_rDeu27_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^And you shall write on them all the words of this instruction when
you cross so that you'll come to the land that YHWH, your God, is giving
you, a land flowing with milk and honey, as YHWH, your fathers' God,
spoke to you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:4](#Contents.html_rDeu27_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^And it shall be, when you cross the Jordan, that you shall set up
these stones that I command you today in Mount Ebal and cover them with
plaster.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:5](#Contents.html_rDeu27_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And you shall build an altar to YHWH, your God, there, an altar of
stones. You shall not lift iron over them.

*[Deuteronomy 27:6](#Contents.html_rDeu27_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

shall offer burnt offerings on it to YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_06.jpg)

27:6. **whole stones**. Unhewn stones, not cut or shaped with tools.

*[Deuteronomy 27:7](#Contents.html_rDeu27_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And you shall sacrifice peace offerings and eat there and rejoice in
front of YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_07.jpg)

*[Deuteronomy 27:8](#Contents.html_rDeu27_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And you shall write on the stones all the words of this instruction
very clearly."

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

*[Deuteronomy 27:9](#Contents.html_rDeu27_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And Moses and the Levite priests spoke to all Israel, saying, "Be
silent, and listen, Israel: this day you have become a people to YHWH,
your God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:10](#Contents.html_rDeu27_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^And you shall listen to the voice of YHWH, your God, and do His
commandments and His laws that I command you today."

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:11](#Contents.html_rDeu27_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And Moses commanded the people on that day, saying,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:12](#Contents.html_rDeu27_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^"These shall stand to bless the people on Mount Gerizim when you
cross the Jordan: Simeon and Levi and Judah and Issachar and Joseph and
Benjamin.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:13](#Contents.html_rDeu27_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And these shall stand for the curse on Mount Ebal: Reuben, Gad, and
Asher and Zebulun, Dan, and Naphtali.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:14](#Contents.html_rDeu27_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^And the Levites shall answer, and they shall say in a loud voice to
every man of Israel:

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:15](#Contents.html_rDeu27_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^'Cursed be the man who will make a statue or molten thing, an
offensive thing of YHWH, a stone engraver's work, and set it up in
secret.'

And all the people shall answer, and they shall say: 'Amen.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:16](#Contents.html_rDeu27_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^'Cursed be one who disrespects one's father and his mother.'

And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:17](#Contents.html_rDeu27_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^'Cursed be one who moves his neighbor's landmark.'

And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:18](#Contents.html_rDeu27_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^'Cursed be one who misleads a blind person on the way.'

And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:19](#Contents.html_rDeu27_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^'Cursed be one who bends the judgment of an alien, an orphan, or a
widow.'

And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:20](#Contents.html_rDeu27_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^'Cursed be one who lies with his father's wife, because he has
exposed his father's hem.'

And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:21](#Contents.html_rDeu27_21)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^'Cursed be one who lies with any animal.'

And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_21.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:22](#Contents.html_rDeu27_22)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^'Cursed be one who lies with his sister ---his father's daughter or
his mother's daughter.'

And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_22.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:23](#Contents.html_rDeu27_23)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^'Cursed be one who lies with his mother-in-law.'

And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_23.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:24](#Contents.html_rDeu27_24)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^'Cursed be one who strikes his neighbor in secret.'

And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_24.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:25](#Contents.html_rDeu27_25)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^'Cursed be one who takes a bribe to strike a person---innocent
blood.'

And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_25.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 27:26](#Contents.html_rDeu27_26)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^'Cursed be one who will not uphold the words of this instruction, to
do them.'

And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap27_26.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

27:26. **who will not uphold the words of this instruction**. The curses
seem like a list of sundry examples of the laws that Moses has given
until now. Most of the things that are forbidden are uncommon and easy
to avoid doing. So the people as a whole will readily say the "Amen" to
them. But then the final curse is on anyone who will not support and
perform "the words of this Torah"---in other words: the Torah as a
whole. The people are led to a point at which they must say "Amen" to
the full Torah.

[]{#Deuteronomy28.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:1](#Contents.html_rDeu28_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **28** {.h2a}

^1^"And it will be, if you'll *listen* to the voice of YHWH, your God,
to be watchful to do all His commandments that I command you today, that
YHWH, your God, will set you high above all the nations of the earth.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:2](#Contents.html_rDeu28_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^And all these blessings will come on you and catch up with you when
you'll listen to the voice of YHWH, your God:

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:2. **blessings ... curses**. The curses are four times the length of
the blessings. Like the blessings and curses list in Leviticus 26 (where
the curses are three times longer than the blessings), this list may
convey that threats of punishment were thought to be more effective than
promises of reward. Or it may convey the opposite: that threats are
*less* effective, and therefore more are required. The remarkable thing
is that, following all these blessings and curses, Moses speaks
beautifully for two chapters about why the people should keep the
covenant for *itself.* The blessings and curses are there out of a
realistic recognition of human psychology:
re[]{#Deuteronomy28.html_page_85}wards and punishments are effective
tools of instruction from childhood and up. But the aim is higher: that
humans should come to see that what is being put in their hands is
"life" and "good" and "love" (Deut 30:15--16).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:3](#Contents.html_rDeu28_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^You'll be blessed in the city, and you'll be blessed in the field.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:4](#Contents.html_rDeu28_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^The fruit of your womb and the fruit of your land and the fruit of
your animals, your cattle's offspring and your flock's young, will be
blessed.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:5](#Contents.html_rDeu28_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^Your basket and your bowl will be blessed.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:6](#Contents.html_rDeu28_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^You'll be blessed when you come in, and you'll be blessed when you go
out.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:7](#Contents.html_rDeu28_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

of you. By one road they'll come out at you, and by seven roads they'll
flee in front of you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

if you'll keep the commandments of YHWH, your God, and go in His ways.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:11](#Contents.html_rDeu28_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^And YHWH will give you a surplus of good in the fruit of your womb
and in the fruit of your animals and in the fruit of your land, on the

*[Deuteronomy 28:12](#Contents.html_rDeu28_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^YHWH will open His good treasure, the skies, to you, to give your
lands showers at their time and to bless all your hand's work. And
you'll lend to many nations, and you won't borrow;

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:13](#Contents.html_rDeu28_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^and YHWH will put you at the head and not at the tail; and you'll
only be above, and you won't be below---if you'll listen to the
commandments of YHWH, your God, that I command you today, to observe and
to do,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:14](#Contents.html_rDeu28_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^and you won't turn from all the things that I command you today,
right or left, to go after other gods, to serve them.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:15](#Contents.html_rDeu28_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^"And it will be, if you won't listen to the voice of YHWH, your God,
to be watchful to do all His commandments and His laws that I command
you today, that all these curses will come on you and catch up with you:

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:15. **blessings ... curses**. The curses are four times the length of
the blessings. Like the blessings and curses list in Leviticus 26 (where
the curses are three times longer than the blessings), this list may
convey that threats of punishment were thought to be more effective than
promises of reward. Or it may convey the opposite: that threats are
*less* effective, and therefore more are required. The remarkable thing
is that, following all these blessings and curses, Moses speaks
beautifully for two chapters about why the people should keep the
covenant for *itself.* The blessings and curses are there out of a
realistic recognition of human psychology:
re[]{#Deuteronomy28.html_page_86}wards and punishments are effective
tools of instruction from childhood and up. But the aim is higher: that
humans should come to see that what is being put in their hands is
"life" and "good" and "love" (Deut 30:15--16).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^You'll be cursed in the city, and you'll be cursed in the field.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:18](#Contents.html_rDeu28_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^The fruit of your womb and the fruit of your land, your cattle's
offspring and your flock's young, will be cursed.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:19](#Contents.html_rDeu28_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^You'll be cursed when you come in, and you'll be cursed when you go
out.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:20](#Contents.html_rDeu28_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^YHWH will send curse and tumult and annoyance at you in everything
your hand takes on that you'll do, until you're destroyed and until you
perish quickly because of your bad practices, in that you left me.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:21](#Contents.html_rDeu28_21)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^YHWH will make an epidemic cling to you until He finishes you from
the land to which you're coming to take possession of it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_21.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:22](#Contents.html_rDeu28_22)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^YHWH will strike you with consumption and with fever and with
inflammation and with burning and with the sword and with blight and
with mildew. And they'll pursue you until you perish.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_22.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:23](#Contents.html_rDeu28_23)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^And your skies that are over your head will be bronze, and the land
that is under you iron.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_23.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:24](#Contents.html_rDeu28_24)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^YHWH will make your land's showers powder and dust; it will fall on
you from the skies until you're destroyed.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_24.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:25](#Contents.html_rDeu28_25)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^YHWH will make you stricken in front of your enemies. By one road
you'll go out at him, and by seven roads you'll flee in front of him.
And you'll be a horrifying thing to all the earth's kingdoms.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_25.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:26](#Contents.html_rDeu28_26)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And your carcass will become food for every bird of the skies and
for the animals of the earth, with no one making them afraid.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_26.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:27](#Contents.html_rDeu28_27)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^YHWH will strike you with the boils of Egypt and with hemorrhoids
and with scabs and with itches, from which you won't be able to be
healed.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_27.jpg)

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_27a.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:27. **the boils of Egypt**. The horror of the curses is multiplied by
the fact that this and other curses are among the ten plagues that were
imposed on Egypt. The thought that God would impose on Israel the very
plagues that were used on Egypt to make YHWH known and set Israel free
is terrifying. The reminder of Egypt will culminate in the last curse
(v. 68 below).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:28](#Contents.html_rDeu28_28)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^YHWH will strike you with madness and with blindness and with
amazement of heart.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_28.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:29](#Contents.html_rDeu28_29)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^29^And you'll be feeling around at noon the way the blind would feel
around, in the dark. And you won't make your ways successful, but you'll
just be exploited and robbed every day, and there will be no one to save
you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_29.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:29. **feeling around at noon the way the blind would feel around, in
the dark.** This curse, too, reflects the plagues on Egypt. In Exod
10:22, the darkness is described with this same term, Hebrew
*'![image](images/a1.jpg)p![image](images/e1.jpg)l![image](images/a-image.jpg)h,*
which occurs only in these two places in the Torah. Also, the related
verb for "feeling" or "groping" is used there (10:21).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:30](#Contents.html_rDeu28_30)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^30^You'll betroth a woman, and another man will ravish her. You'll
build a house, and you won't live in it. You'll plant a vineyard, and
you won't desanctify it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_30.jpg)

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_30a.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:30. **ravish her**. The written text of the Torah (the *ketib*) uses
this strong term. When it is read (the *qere'*), a milder term, "lie
with her"
(*yi![image](images/s-image.jpg)kaben![image](images/a-image.jpg)h*), is
substituted.

28:30. **desanctify.** See the comment on Deut 20:6.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:31](#Contents.html_rDeu28_31)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^31^Your ox slaughtered before your eyes---and you won't eat any of it.
Your ass stolen from in front of you---and it won't come back to you.
Your sheep given to your enemies---and you'll have no one to save you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_31.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:32](#Contents.html_rDeu28_32)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^32^Your sons and your daughters given to another people---while your
eyes are looking for them all day and giving out, and there's no God at
your hand.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_32.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:32. **no God at your hand**. Laban uses this expression to convey to
Jacob that he has power ("The god at my hand has the means to do bad to
you," Gen 31:29). Here in the curse list of Deuteronomy it is formulated
in the negative, conveying that the people will be powerless. Similarly,
the curse in v. 29 uses the expression "you won't make your ways
successful," which is a negative formulation of the words that are used
to describe Abraham's servant's internal question ("to know if YHWH had
made his trip successful or not," Gen 24:21). Thus there are
reminiscences of the first book of the Torah that are reversed here in
the curse list in the last book of the Torah---which doubles the force
of these horrible threats.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:33](#Contents.html_rDeu28_33)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^33^A people whom you haven't known will eat your land's fruit and all
the product of your exhaustion, and you'll only be exploited and crushed
every day.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_33.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:34](#Contents.html_rDeu28_34)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^34^And you'll be driven mad from the sight before your eyes that you'll

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

*[Deuteronomy 28:35](#Contents.html_rDeu28_35)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_35.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

*[Deuteronomy 28:36](#Contents.html_rDeu28_36)*

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

*[Deuteronomy 28:46](#Contents.html_rDeu28_46)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:47](#Contents.html_rDeu28_47)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:49](#Contents.html_rDeu28_49)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^49^YHWH will fetch a nation from far, from the end of the earth, over
you the way an eagle soars, a nation whose language you won't
understand,

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:50](#Contents.html_rDeu28_50)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^50^a fierce-faced nation who won't be partial to the old and won't show

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:51](#Contents.html_rDeu28_51)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^51^and it will eat the fruit of your animals and the fruit of your land
until you are destroyed---who won't leave you grain, wine, and oil, your
cattle's offspring and your flock's young until it has made you perish.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_51.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:52](#Contents.html_rDeu28_52)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^52^And it will close you in, in all your gates, until your high and
fortified walls in which you trust come down in all your land; and it
will close you in, in all your gates, in all your land that YHWH, your
God, has given you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_52.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:53](#Contents.html_rDeu28_53)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^53^And you'll eat the fruit of your womb, the flesh of your sons
[]{#Deuteronomy28.html_page_87}and your daughters whom YHWH, your God,
has given you, in the siege and in the constraint that your enemy will
put on you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_53.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:53. **you'll eat ... the flesh of your sons**. This horrid curse
comes true four centuries later during the Aramean siege of Samaria (2
Kings 6:25--29).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:54](#Contents.html_rDeu28_54)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^54^The tenderest man among you and the very delicate: his eye will look
with evil intent at his brother and the wife of his bosom and the rest
of his children that he'll have left

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_54.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:55](#Contents.html_rDeu28_55)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^55^from giving to one of them any of the flesh of his children that
he'll eat, because nothing will be left to him in the siege and in the
constraint that your enemy will put on you in all your gates.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_55.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:56](#Contents.html_rDeu28_56)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^56^The tenderest and the most delicate woman among you, who wouldn't
risk setting her foot on the ground out of delicacy and tenderness: her
eye will look with evil intent at the man of her bosom and her son and
her daughter

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_56.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:57](#Contents.html_rDeu28_57)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^57^and her afterbirth that comes out from between her legs and her
children to whom she'll give birth, because she'll eat them in secret
due to the lack of everything in the siege and in the constraint that
your enemy will put on you in your gates.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_57.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:58](#Contents.html_rDeu28_58)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^58^If you won't be watchful to do the words of this instruction that
are written in this scroll, to fear this honored and awesome name: YHWH,
your God,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_58.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:59](#Contents.html_rDeu28_59)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^59^then YHWH will make your plagues and your seed's plagues
astonishing, great and enduring plagues, and great and enduring
illnesses.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_59.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:60](#Contents.html_rDeu28_60)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^60^And He'll bring back among you every disease of Egypt, which you
were dreading, and they'll cling to you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_60.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:61](#Contents.html_rDeu28_61)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^61^YHWH will bring over you every illness and every plague that is not
written in this scroll of instruction, as well, until you are destroyed.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_61.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:62](#Contents.html_rDeu28_62)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^62^And you'll be left with few persons when you had been like the stars
of the skies for multitude, because you didn't listen to the voice of
YHWH, your God.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_62.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:63](#Contents.html_rDeu28_63)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^63^And it will be: as YHWH had satisfaction over you to do good to you
and to multiply you, so YHWH will have satisfaction over you to make you
perish and to destroy you, and you'll be torn away from the land to
which you're coming to take possession of it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_63.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:64](#Contents.html_rDeu28_64)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^64^And YHWH will scatter you among all the peoples from one end of the
earth to the other end of the earth. And you'll serve other gods, whom
you haven't known, you and your fathers, there, wood and stone.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_64.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:65](#Contents.html_rDeu28_65)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^65^And among those nations you won't have a respite, and there won't be
a resting place []{#Deuteronomy28.html_page_88}for your foot, and YHWH
will give you there a trembling heart and a failing of eyes and a
fainting of soul.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_65.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:65. **a resting place for your foot**. Again an expression from the
early part of Genesis reappears at the end of the Torah. The dove that
Noah lets go "did not find a resting place for its foot" (Gen 8:9). The
helplessness of the dove, with no place on earth to go, now turns out to
have been a metaphoric image of the condition of the people of Israel if
they do not keep their covenant.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:66](#Contents.html_rDeu28_66)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^66^And your life will be hanging opposite you, and you'll fear night
and day, and you won't trust in your life.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_66.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:67](#Contents.html_rDeu28_67)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^67^In the morning you'll say, 'Who would make it evening,' and in the
evening you'll say, 'Who would make it morning,' because of your heart's
fear that you'll have and because of the sight before your eyes that
you'll see.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_67.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:68](#Contents.html_rDeu28_68)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^68^And YHWH will bring you back to Egypt in boats, by the way that I
said to you: 'You won't go on to see it anymore.' And you'll sell
yourselves there to your enemies as slaves and as maids, and none will
buy!"

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_68.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

28:68. **back to Egypt**. For the last curse of this list of horrors,
what would be the worst threat specifically for the people of Israel:
back to Egypt! This nightmare comes true seven hundred years later,
after the Babylonian destruction of Jerusalem, described at the end of
the book of Kings: "All the people, from the smallest to the biggest ...
got up and came to Egypt" (2 Kings 25:26; Jer 43:5--7). The last curse
is that they will go back to Egypt, and the last page of the book of
Kings reports that the entire people go back to Egypt. Yet, incredibly,
this fact is almost never mentioned in commentaries on Deuteronomy or
Kings or in biblical scholarship in general. The focus has been on the
small portion of the nation who go into exile in Babylon, not on the
mass of the people, who go as refugees to Egypt. It is time that we
recognized, first, the full horror of the final curse of the covenant.
Second, we must be sensitive to what it means to *Moses* to pronounce
this curse: the heartbreak of what it would mean for his people to be
back in Egypt, even worse off than before, the failure, the humiliation.
Third, we must give due attention to the fate of the Jews who ended up
back in Egypt. And, fourth, we should appreciate the significance of the
fact that it was the small portion of the community who were taken to
Babylon who produced the kernel who returned to Israel a generation
later and rebuilt the Temple, Jerusalem, and the country---a second life
for Israel in its land that lasted six hundred years.

28:68. **in boats**. The reference to boats has never been understood.
It may be an *alef*/*ayin* scribal error for *'nywt* (afflictions) or a
plural of *'![image](images/a1.jpg)niyy![image](images/a-image.jpg)h*
(mourning; Isa 29:2; Lam 2:5), although these forms of the words are
unattested in the *Tanak;* or it may refer to an historical event that
is no longer known.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 28:69](#Contents.html_rDeu28_69)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^69^These are the words of the covenant that YHWH commanded Moses to
make with the children of Israel in the land of Moab, aside from the
covenant that He made with them at Horeb.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap28_69.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

[]{#Deuteronomy29.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:1](#Contents.html_rDeu29_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **29** {.h2a}

^1^And Moses called all of Israel and said to them, "You've seen
everything that YHWH did before your eyes in the land of Egypt to
Pharaoh and to all his servants and to all his land,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:2](#Contents.html_rDeu29_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^the great tests that your eyes saw, those great signs and wonders.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:3](#Contents.html_rDeu29_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^But YHWH did not give you a heart to know and eyes to see and ears to
hear until this day.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:3. **did not give you ... until this day**. This is an old enigma:
have they not been able to understand the meaning of all the great
wonders they have witnessed until now? I understand this to mean just
that. Moses has just finished the long speech that takes up nearly all
of Deuteronomy. Now he is beginning a much shorter speech, and he is
telling them that he is about to reveal to them, in his last message to
them, the key to understanding what their experience means. What follows
is a speech like no other he has ever made in the Torah. Until now he
has focused on facts: points of history, specific laws. But, starting in
a few verses, he will turn philosophical, moral, and spiritual---and his
message is formulated in some of the most beautiful words in the Torah.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:4](#Contents.html_rDeu29_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^As I led you forty years in the wilderness, your clothing did not
become worn on you, and your shoe did not become worn on your foot,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:5](#Contents.html_rDeu29_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^you didn't eat bread, and you didn't drink wine and beer, so you
would know that 'I am YHWH your God.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:6](#Contents.html_rDeu29_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And you came to this place. (And Sihon, king of Heshbon, and Og, king
of Bashan, came out at us for war, and we struck them.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:7](#Contents.html_rDeu29_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And we took their land and gave it as a legacy to the Reubenite and
to the Gadite and to half of the Manassite tribe.)

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:8](#Contents.html_rDeu29_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And you shall observe the words of this covenant and do them so that
you'll *understand* all that you'll do.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:8. ***understand***. Yet again the words of the beginning of the
Torah return in the Torah's closing portions. In the garden of Eden, the
woman is attracted to the fruit of the tree of knowledge of good and bad
because it will "bring about understanding" (Hebrew
*ha![image](images/s.jpg)kîl;* 3:6). Humans acquire that
ability---understanding---when they eat the forbidden fruit. The word
then does not occur again in the Torah until here. Now Moses tells them
that they can use the divine power of understanding for a purpose:
comprehending the meaning of what they have experienced and of what they
will do. The past and the future, the miracles and the performance of
the covenant's commandments, are all within humans' power to grasp. What
began as something taken in violation of a divine command now becomes
something positive. Humans are not limited to following the commandments
without comprehension. They can use what they took from the divine realm
for their own good and for their relationship with their God.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:9](#Contents.html_rDeu29_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**S[TANDING]{.small}**

^9^"You're standing today, all of you, in front of YHWH, your God---your
heads, your tribes, your elders, and your officers, every man of Israel,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:10](#Contents.html_rDeu29_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^your infants, your women, and your alien who is in your camps, from
one who cuts your wood to one who draws your water---

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:11](#Contents.html_rDeu29_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^for you to enter into the covenant of YHWH, your God, and into His
oath, which YHWH, your God, is making with you today,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:12](#Contents.html_rDeu29_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^in order to establish you today for Him as a people, and *He* will
be a God to you, as He spoke to you and as He swore to your fathers: to
Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:13](#Contents.html_rDeu29_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And I am not making this covenant and this oath with you alone,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:14](#Contents.html_rDeu29_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^but with the one who is here standing with us today in front of
YHWH, our God, and with the one who isn't here with us today.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:15](#Contents.html_rDeu29_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^Because *you* know that we lived in the land of Egypt and that we
passed among the nations that you passed.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:16](#Contents.html_rDeu29_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^And you've seen their disgraces and their idols, wood and stone,
silver and gold, that were with them.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:17](#Contents.html_rDeu29_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^In case there will be among you a man or woman or family or tribe
whose heart is turning today from YHWH, our God, to go to serve those
nations' gods; in case there is among you a root bearing poison and
wormwood,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:18](#Contents.html_rDeu29_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^and it will be when he hears the words of this oath that he'll feel
himself blessed in his heart, saying, 'I'll have peace, though I'll go
on in my heart's obstinacy,' so as to annihilate the wet with the dry:

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:18. **oath**. The word can mean either "oath" or "curse." Here it can
refer to the covenant oath mentioned in v. 11 or to the covenant curses;
or it can mean the two together, since the covenant oath implicitly
invokes the curses on the one who takes it.

29:18. **annihilate the wet with the dry**. Commentators and translators
struggle with this phrase, which may have been an idiomatic expression
whose context is no longer known. The answer may lie in the phenomenon
that we have seen a number of times whereby expressions from Genesis
recur here in Deuteronomy. The verb here ("annihilate" or "sweep away")
occurs four times in the matter of Sodom and Gomorrah (Gen 18:23,24;
19:15,17) and in one other place in the Torah, the destruction of Korah
and his group (Num 16:26). In all five occurrences there is an issue of
whether good people will be annihilated along with the bad. And they all
involve fire coming from God and destroying the offenders. The meaning
of this expression here, therefore, may be that as a strong fire burns
up what is dry and evaporates what is wet (or as a forest fire destroys
both the dry and fresh trees), so the obstinate person, or group in this
case, may bring down the community with them. This understanding is
reinforced by the fact that Sodom and Gomorrah are referred to just four
verses later (29:22).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:19](#Contents.html_rDeu29_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^YHWH will not be willing to forgive him, because YHWH's anger and
His jealousy will then smoke against that man, and every curse that is
written in this scroll will weigh on him, and YHWH will wipe out his
name from under the skies.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:20](#Contents.html_rDeu29_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^20^And YHWH will separate him from all of Israel's tribes
[]{#Deuteronomy29.html_page_90}for bad, according to all the curses of
the covenant that is written in this scroll of instruction.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_20.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:21](#Contents.html_rDeu29_21)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^21^"And a later generation will say---your children who will come up
after you, and the foreigner who will come from a far land, when they'll
see that land's plagues and its illnesses that YHWH put in it,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_21.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:22](#Contents.html_rDeu29_22)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^22^brimstone and salt, all the land a burning, it won't be seeded and
won't grow, and not any vegetation will come up in it, like the
overturning of Sodom and Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboiim, which YHWH
overturned in His anger and His fury---

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_22.jpg)

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_22a.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:23](#Contents.html_rDeu29_23)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^23^and all the nations will say, 'For *what* did YHWH do something like
this to this land? What is this big flaring of anger?'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_23.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:24](#Contents.html_rDeu29_24)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^24^"And they'll say, 'For the fact that they left the covenant of YHWH,
their fathers' God, which He made with them when He brought them out
from the land of Egypt.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_24.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:25](#Contents.html_rDeu29_25)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^25^And they went and served other gods and bowed to them, gods whom
they hadn't known and He hadn't allocated to them.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_25.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:26](#Contents.html_rDeu29_26)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^26^And YHWH's anger flared at that land, to bring over it every curse
that was written in this scroll.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_26.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:27](#Contents.html_rDeu29_27)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^27^And YHWH plucked them from their land in anger and in fury and in
great rage, and He threw them into another land, as it is this day.'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_27.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 29:28](#Contents.html_rDeu29_28)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^28^"The hidden things belong to YHWH, our God, and the revealed things
belong to us and to our children forever, to do all the words of this
instruction.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap29_28.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

29:28. **The hidden things belong to YHWH**. Moses tells the people that
there are things that are solely in the divine realm, hidden from
humans. Humans' task is here in this world: to live a certain kind of
life. He breaks off from this matter and turns to a discussion of future
exile and return to God (30:1--10). Then he returns to this picture of
what humans must do, and how it is not something enigmatic, in v. 11.

[]{#Deuteronomy30.html}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:1](#Contents.html_rDeu30_1)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **30** {.h2a}

^1^And it will be, when all these things, the blessing and the curse
that I've put in front of you, will come upon you, and you'll store it
in your heart among the nations to which YHWH, your God, has driven you,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_01.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:1. **your God has driven you**. The word "driven" is ironic here. It
has been used three times until now, always referring to persons who
would drive Israelites away from their God (Deut 13:6,11,14). But now it
describes how YHWH drives Israel away to other lands because of Israel's
apostasy. The term is thus another allusion to the fact of divine
punishment to fit the crime, which is to say: there is justice.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:2](#Contents.html_rDeu30_2)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^2^and you'll come back to YHWH, your God, and listen to His voice,
according to everything that I command you today, you and your children,
with all your heart and with all your soul,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_02.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:3](#Contents.html_rDeu30_3)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^3^that YHWH, your God, will bring back your captivity and be merciful
to you. And He'll come back and gather you from all the peoples to which
YHWH, your God, has scattered you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_03.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:3. **bring back your captivity**. This expression occurs about
twenty-five times in the *Tanak.* It is grammatically unclear. The verb
(*![image](images/s-image.jpg)![image](images/a-image.jpg)b*) would
normally be in the *Hiphil,* but here (and usually) it is in the
intransitive *Qal.* The noun may reflect the root
*![image](images/s-image.jpg)wb,* "bring back" (making it a cognate
accusative with the verb), or it may reflect the root
*![image](images/s-image.jpg)bh,* "captive." On the former root, the
phrase would mean "to produce a coming back." On the latter root, it
would mean "to bring back a captivity." These two meanings are very
close to each other in any case. Those who take this phrase to mean
figuratively "to restore your fortunes" have a good parallel in Job
42:10; but all the occurrences in Jeremiah, which is linked closely with
Deuteronomy, fit the meaning of "coming back" or "captivity" far better.
Note especially Jer 30:16--23 and 48:46--47, in which this expression
occurs in proximity to the related word for captivity:
*![image](images/s-image.jpg)ebî.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:4](#Contents.html_rDeu30_4)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^4^If you'll be driven to the end of the skies, YHWH, your God, will
gather you from there, and He'll take you from there.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_04.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:5](#Contents.html_rDeu30_5)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^5^And YHWH, your God, will bring you to the land that your fathers
possessed, and you'll take possession of it, and He'll be good to you
and multiply you more than your fathers.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_05.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:6](#Contents.html_rDeu30_6)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^6^And YHWH, your God, will circumcise your heart and your seed's heart
so as to love YHWH, your God, with all your heart and with all your soul
so that you'll live.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_06.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:6. **God will circumcise your heart**. Earlier, Moses directs the
people to circumcise their hearts themselves (see Deut 10:16 and the
comment there), but now he speaks of God circumcising their hearts for
them. The difference is that now he is speaking of a future condition:
the people will have violated the covenant, suffered the curses, and
turned back to their God; and God, in response, will bring them back and
then will circumcise their hearts. This in turn will enable them to love
the deity with all their hearts and souls---which Moses had commanded
them to do earlier as well. The divine-human relationship is pictured as
mutual. Humans require the experience of being away from their God, on
their own. And then, more experienced, more understanding, having
suffered and grown, humans look to God anew. And then the new encounter
with the divine transforms them.

30:6. **your seed's heart**. Meaning: the hearts of your progeny.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:7](#Contents.html_rDeu30_7)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^7^And YHWH, your God, will put all these curses on your enemies and on
those who hate you who have pursued you.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_07.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:8](#Contents.html_rDeu30_8)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^8^And you'll come back and listen to YHWH's voice and do all His
commandments that I command you today.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_08.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:9](#Contents.html_rDeu30_9)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^9^And YHWH, your God, will give you extra of all your hand's work, of
the fruit of your womb and of the fruit of your animals and the fruit of
your land for good. Because YHWH will come back to have satisfaction
over you for good as He had satisfaction over your fathers,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_09.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:10](#Contents.html_rDeu30_10)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^10^when you'll listen to the voice of YHWH, your God, to observe His
commandments and His laws, written in this scroll of instruction, when
you'll come back to YHWH, your God, with all your heart and with all
your soul.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_10.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:11](#Contents.html_rDeu30_11)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^11^"Because this commandment that I command you today: it's not too
wondrous for you, and it's not too far.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_11.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:11. **it's not too wondrous for you**. Now Moses finishes what he
started to say ten verses earlier about the hidden things belonging to
God and the revealed things belonging to humans (29:28). In one of the
most beautiful passages in the Torah (vv. 11--14), he explains what this
meant: The commandments are not enigmatic, they do not reside in a
distant realm, and they do not require an intermediary. They are already
made known. And they are within a human's ability to do.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:12](#Contents.html_rDeu30_12)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^12^It's not in the skies, that one would say, 'Who will go up for us to
the skies and get it for us and enable us to hear it so we'll do it?'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_12.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:13](#Contents.html_rDeu30_13)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^13^And it's not across the sea, that one would say, 'Who will cross for
us, across the sea, and get it for us and enable us to hear it so we'll
do it?'

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_13.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:14](#Contents.html_rDeu30_14)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^14^But the thing is very close to you, in your mouth, and in your
heart, to do it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_14.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:15](#Contents.html_rDeu30_15)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^15^"See: I've put in front of you today life and good, and death and
bad,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_15.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:16](#Contents.html_rDeu30_16)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^16^in that I command you today to love YHWH, your God, to go in His
ways and to observe His commandments and His laws and His judgments so
you'll live and multiply and bless YHWH, your God, in the land to which
you're coming to take possession of it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_16.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:16. **so you'll live and multiply**. This is a change of wording from
the beginning of the Torah, where the command is to "be fruitful and
multiply" (Gen 1:28). The command to grow in numbers is for the purpose
of *life.* As the human population grows to the point that its number is
a threat to life, humankind and its leaders need enormous wisdom to
determine what to do. The wording there in Genesis is: "And God
*blessed* them, and God said to them, 'Be fruitful and multiply ... '"
(Gen 1:28). That first commandment of the Torah is thus presented not as
a strict order, but as a blessing. And here at the end of the Torah as
well, the text is: "so you'll live and multiply and *bless* YHWH." In
the Torah, the creator blesses humans with the opportunity to grow, and
humans will bless their creator for it once it is fulfilled. The Bible's
first commandment is now becoming humankind's greatest challenge.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:17](#Contents.html_rDeu30_17)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^17^But if your heart will turn away, and you won't listen, and you'll
be driven so that you'll bow to other gods and serve them,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_17.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:18](#Contents.html_rDeu30_18)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^18^I've told you today that you'll *perish.* You won't extend days on
the land to which you're crossing the Jordan to come to take possession
of it.

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_18.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:19](#Contents.html_rDeu30_19)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

^19^I call the skies and the earth to witness regarding you today: I've
put life and death in front of you, blessing and curse. And you shall
choose life, so you'll live, you and your seed,

![Image](images/Deu_Chap30_19.jpg)

![Image](images/hr.jpg){height="17" width="550"}

30:19. **choose life**. The theme of the path to life began early in
Moses' speech (Deut 4:1) and culminates here in the last words of the
speech. This focus at the conclusion of the Torah returns us to the
Torah's opening: the loss of the tree of life. Humans lose access to the
tree of life as the price of having gained access to the tree of
knowledge of *good* and *bad.* Now the people are told, "I've put in
front of you life and good, blessing and curse." Using the knowledge of
good and bad, and choosing to do good, is the path back to life---not
necessarily eternal life (though who knows?), but *meaningful* life,
*fulfilling* life. As the book of Proverbs says about knowledge and
wisdom: "It is a tree of life." And Jews sing this verse from Proverbs
each Sabbath after reading the Torah and returning it to the ark.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

*[Deuteronomy 30:20](#Contents.html_rDeu30_20)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------
